PT P
PN CN111592025-A
TI Preparing Chrysanthemum-shaped micron calcium            carbonate, comprises e.g. mixing aqueous phase, oil            phase, co-surfactant and surfactant to form clear            reversed phase micro-emulsion, introducing carbon            dioxide gas, aging, separating and drying.
AU WANG N
   ZHAO D
   ZENG Y
AE UNIV LANZHOU JIAOTONG (UYLJ-C)
   UNIV HEZHOU (UHEZ-C)
GA 202085781M
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing Chrysanthemum-shaped micron calcium                carbonate, comprises (i) fully mixing aqueous                phase, oil phase, co-surfactant and surfactant to                prepare clear reversed phase micro-emulsion, where                the aqueous phase is calcium chloride aqueous                solution, the oil phase is cyclohexane, the                co-surfactant is n-butanol, and the surfactant is                cetyltrimethylammonium bromide, and (ii)                introducing carbon dioxide gas into the reverse                micro-emulsion of step (i) under stirring condition                for a certain time until no precipitate is                generated, then stopping to introduce carbon                dioxide gas and stirring, allowing to stand at room                temperature, aging, centrifuging and separating,                and then drying the obtained precipitate to obtain                final product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing                Chrysanthemum-shaped micron calcium                carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The micron calcium carbonate: has multiple                folds on the surface; and resembles blooming                Chrysanthemumwith uniform appearance and excellent                dispersion.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ratio between                  aqueous phase, oil phase, co-surfactant and                  surfactant is 4 ml:100 ml:30 ml:16 g. Preferred                  Method: In step (ii), the reaction time of                  passing carbon dioxide into the reverse                  microemulsion of step (i) is 30 minutes. In step                  (ii): the aging time is 24 hours; the drying is                  carried out in the oven at 80&#176; C for 24                  hours. The calcium chloride aqueous solution is                  prepared by dissolving calcium chloride in 25%                  aqueous ammonia and less amount of water, and                  then adding water to make 0.5 mol/l solution. The                  carbon dioxide is prepared by measuring                  concentrated hydrochloric acid and placing in an                  Erlenmeyer flask, weighing equal mole of marble                  and adding to Erlenmeyer flask and                  reacting.

TI Preparing slow-release drug-loaded porous membrane            based on supercritical fluid comprises taking polymer            and solid drug particles, mixing and dissolving in            solvent, spreading solution, and fully contacting thin            layer, cleaning and drying.

   MA L
   CUI H
AE UNIV HEBEI SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYKJ-C)
GA 2020340922
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing slow-release drug-loaded porous                membrane based on supercritical fluid comprises (a)                taking polymer and solid drug particles, mixing and                dissolving in the solvent to make solution or                suspension with concentration of 15-50%, (b)                spreading the solution or suspension obtained in                the step (a) into a thin layer, and fully                contacting the thin layer with supercritical carbon                dioxide with carrier, and (c) circulating                supercritical carbon dioxide, cleaning and drying                the thin layer, and where the solvent continuously                conveys supercritical carbon dioxide and taking out                of the system to obtain porous film.

TI Obtaining calcium oxide-magnesium oxide binders            and construction products with reuse of by-products            and/or residues involves performing curing and            hardening process with carbon dioxide absorption.
AU DE CASTRO GOMES J P
   SILVA HUMBERT P
   HUMBERT P S
   DE CASTRO G J P
   SILVA H P
   GOMES J P D C
AE UNIV DA BEIRA INTERIOR (UYBE-Non-standard)
   UNIV DA BEIRA INTERIOR (UYBE-Non-standard)
   UNIV DA BEIRA INTERIOR (UYBE-Non-standard)
   UNIV DA BEIRA INTERIOR (UYBE-Non-standard)
GA 202011030X
AB    NOVELTY - Obtaining calcium oxide-magnesium oxide                binders and construction products with reuse of                by-products and/or residues involves performing                curing and hardening process with carbon dioxide                absorption.
   USE - Method for obtaining calcium oxide-magnesium                oxide binders and construction products with reuse                of by-products and/or residues.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to obtain calcium                oxide-magnesium oxide binders and construction                products with reuse of by-products and/or residues                which is useful for the entire construction                industry by reducing the need to use cement, sand                and natural gravel, and reduces consumption of                drinking water in the construction industry,                reduces the disposal of by-products, waste and                carbon dioxide in the environment, accelerates the                speed of production and increases the mechanical                resistance of civil construction materials, in                addition to contributing to the Circular Economy                and Sustainable Development objectives, and applied                in the production of all types of civil                construction products.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The by-products and/or                  residues are rich in calcium and magnesium. The                  curing and hardening is carried out under                  constant conditions of humidity, temperature and                  pressure. The binders are obtained by crushing                  and grinding. The products are obtained by mixing                  the binders with by-products and/or residues of                  different particle sizes, preferably mixing with                  non-potable residual water. The mixture additives                  are such as alcohol, sodium chloride and sodium                  bicarbonate sodium. The construction products                  includes other residues and materials containing                  silica and alumina. The construction products are                  obtained by molding with compaction. The curing                  and hardening process is carried out with a                  carbon dioxide recirculation system closed                  circuit. Preferred Process: The production                  process of calcium oxide-magnesium oxide binders                  includes Crushing and fine grinding of                  by-products and/or residues, separation and                  sieving of by-products and/or waste less than                  250pm, adding calcium oxide-magnesium oxide                  binders, other fine residues, materials rich in                  silica and alumina and/or Portland cement, and                  then adding sodium bicarbonate, sodium chloride                  or alcohol to the calcium oxide-magnesium oxide                  binders.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN109645394-A
TI Preparation of water soluble ginger powder used            for preparing brown sugar ginger tea, involves drying            fresh ginger slice, crushing and sieving, supercritical            carbon dioxide extracting, adding auxiliary material,            spray drying, and sieving.
AU MENG J
   LI X
   ZENG X
   QIN H
AE QIANJIN PHARM CO LTD ZHUZHOU (QJPM-C)
   UNIV SOUTH CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UYSC-C)
GA 201939518G
AB    NOVELTY - A water soluble ginger powder is prepared by                drying fresh ginger slice, crushing and sieving to                obtain ginger powder, supercritical carbon dioxide                extracting to obtain ginger extract, adding                auxiliary material, spray drying, and sieving.                Extraction is done at 38-48&#176; C for 120-240                minutes at pressure of 26-45 MPa and carbon dioxide                flow rate of 20-40 L/hour.
   USE - The method is used for water soluble ginger                powder preparation used for preparing brown sugar                ginger tea (claimed) for strengthening spleen,                warming stomach, and relieving exterior syndrome by                diaphoresis and clearing away cold.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has high extraction rate, improves                ginger material utilization rate, reduces                production cost, optimizes ginger extracting                technique and preparing step, and completely keeps                pure natural feature of plant raw material to                obtain high purity. The product has anti-oxidizing                function.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: The brown sugar ginger                  tea comprises 60-78 pts. wt. brown sugar, 1-10                  pts. wt. ginger powder, 1-10 pts. wt.                  Chrysanthemumpowder, 1-8 pts. wt.                  xylooligosaccharide and 0.1-5 pts. wt. Ampelopsis                  grossedentataleaf powder.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: Sieving is done through                  30-60 meshes. Extraction is done at                  40-45&#176; C for 150-210 minutes at pressure                  of 30-40 MPa and carbon dioxide flow rate of                  24-34 L/hour. Spray drying is done at head speed                  of 350-380 revolutions/minute (rpm), air inlet                  temperature of 120-140&#176; C, air outlet                  temperature of 80-95&#176; C and feeding flow                  rate of 30-40 rpm. Preferred Components: The                  auxiliary material is dextrin. The mass                  percentage of accessories and ginger extract is                  1:3-8.
   ACTIVITY - Dermatological; Virucide.

PN CN109569238-A
TI Method for sequestration of carbon, involves crushing electric arc furnace steel slag, sieving, drying and cooling followed by placing treated steel slag in reaction vessel, followed by discharging gas in kettle, filtering, and drying.
AU LI J
   ZENG Y
   WANG Y
AE UNIV NORTH CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UNNS-C)
GA 201934409F
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon sequestration method involves crushing the electric arc furnace steel slag, sieving, drying and cooling. The above treated steel slag is placed in the reaction vessel, and deionized water is added, where the ratio of the deionized water to the steel slag is 6-8 l/kg. Carbon dioxide is introduced into the reaction kettle, and closing the valve at a partial pressure of carbon dioxide at 7-9 bar and raising the temperature to 120-140 degrees C to start the reaction followed by after completion of the reaction, discharging gas in the kettle and cooling at room temperature, pouring slurry after the reaction, filtering, and drying filter residue in a dry box.
   USE - Method for sequestration of carbon.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables reducing the carbon dioxide emission, and fully realizing secondary utilization of the discarded steel slag, and resource recycling.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The composition of the electric arc furnace slag comprises 38-42% calcium oxide, 22-24% silicon dioxide, 12-14% magnesium oxide, 9-10% aluminum oxide, 1.5-2% iron oxide, 1.6-2.1% titanium oxide, 0.8-0.95% manganese oxide, 4.2-4.6% chromium, and 0.13-0.16% vanadium.

PN CN109437275-A
TI Preparing calcium carbonate-calcium hydroxide by shell powder comprises e.g. subjecting shell to alkali cleaning and acid cleaning, crushing and subjecting to vacuum negative pressure low-temperature calcination.
AU WANG P
   XIE L
   LI Z
   QI X
   ZHOU Y
   LI X
AE QINGDAO RONGCHUANG NEW MATERIAL ENG RES (QING-Non-standard)
GA 201924915Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing calcium carbonate-calcium hydroxide by shell powder comprises subjecting shell to alkali cleaning and acid cleaning, crushing to obtain shell powder, subjecting to vacuum negative pressure low-temperature calcination, heating for 1-5 hours, stopping heating and vacuuming and delivering air or oxygen in the furnace to obtain calcium carbonate- calcium oxide powder and placing carbonate- calcium oxide powder in a closed space without carbon dioxide , where carbon dioxide free water after ultrasonic atomization makes the humidity of the enclosed space reach 90-95% and carrying out flexible slow reaction of calcium carbonate- calcium oxide powder for 6-48 hours in the carbon dioxide -free closed space reaction system.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing calcium carbonate-calcium hydroxide by shell powder.
   ADVANTAGE - The method saves energy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for calcium carbonate-calcium hydroxide prepared as mentioned above.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method of alkali washing comprises taking shell and alkali in a weight ratio of 100:(1-3) are weighed, dissolving in water, where weight ratio of shell and water is 1:(2-4), and heating to the inner wall of the shell, destroying membrane of phosphorus, washing the shell, centrifuging and drying. The acid cleaning comprises placing alkali-washed shell is placed in an acid solution, where the weight ratio of the shell and acid solution is 1: (1.5-2), washing shell until the impurities on the outer wall of the shell are removed, centrifuging and drying. The shell after acid cleaning and alkali cleaning iscrushed to 325 mesh or less as needed. The calcination temperature in the vacuum negative pressure low-temperature calcination process is 600750 degrees C and degree of vacuum is -80 KPa and -50 KPa. Preferred Components: The alkali in the alkali washing process is potassium hydroxide or sodium hydroxide. The acid in acid cleaning process is hydrochloric acid solution having a mass concentration of 1-5%, sulfuric acid having a mass concentration of 1-5% or nitric acidsolution having a mass concentration of 1-5%. The whiteness of calcium carbonate-calcium hydroxide is greater than 80 and the BET is greater than 15 m2/g.

TI Conversion of feedstock comprising cyclic alkylene            urea to alkyleneamine e.g. ethyleneamine, involves            reacting cyclic alkylene urea with water, removing            carbon dioxide and reacting cyclic alkylene urea with            amine compound e.g. primary amine.
AU TEN KATE A J B
   RAAIJMAKERS M J T
   LAKE K F
   KANTZER E N
   JOVIC S
   EDVINSSON R K
   EHLERS I
   VAN DAM H
   VENEMAN R
   TENKATE A J B
   VANDAM H
   KATE A J B T
   DAM H V
   RAAIYMAKERS M Y T
AE AKZO NOBEL CHEM INT BV (ALKU-C)
   NOURYON CHEM INT BV (NOUR-Non-standard)
   AKZO NOBEL CHEM INT BV (ALKU-C)
   NOURYON CHEM INT BV (ALKU-C)
GA 201915326J
AB    NOVELTY - Conversion of feedstock comprising cyclic                alkylene urea into alkyleneamine involves (a)                reacting cyclic alkylene ureas in the liquid phase                with water to convert into their corresponding                alkyleneamines and removing carbon dioxide, and (b)                converting the cyclic alkylene ureas by reactive                separation into their corresponding alkyleneamines                by reacting with an amine compound comprising                primary and/or secondary amines having a higher                boiling point than the alkyleneamine formed during                the process, and removing alkyleneamine.
   USE - Conversion of feedstock comprising cyclic                alkylene urea into alkyleneamine e.g.                ethyleneamine, preferably diethylenetriamine,                linear triethylenetetramine and                tetraethylenepentamine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method efficiently converts cyclic                alkylene urea into alkyleneamine with high yield                without adding adjuvants, and avoids degradation of                alkyleneamines. The method can be carried out under                relatively mild conditions.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view explaining                the method for converting feedstock comprising                cyclic alkylene urea into alkyleneamine.
   Carbon dioxide removal unit (2)
   Intermediate separation (8)
   Amine removal unit (10)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method involves                  providing feedstock with carbon dioxide loading                  of at least 0.2, before the step (a), providing                  feedstock with carbon dioxide loading of between                  0.05 and 0.8, before the step (b), and providing                  at least a portion of the obtained product after                  the step (b) to the step (a). The step (a)                  involves reacting cyclic alkylene urea in the                  liquid phase with water in an amount of 0.1-20                  mol water per mol urea moiety, at a temperature                  of at least 230 degrees C. The amine is removed                  by reactive distillation. Preferred Components:                  The carbon dioxide conversion is carried out in                  the presence of an amine compound chosen from                  primary amine, cyclic secondary amine, and                  bicyclic tertiary amine. The cyclic alkylene urea                  is compound of formula (I). The alkyleneamine is                  compound of formula (II). In the step (b), when                  the cyclic alkylene urea is ethylene urea, the                  amine compound is chosen from diethylenetriamine                  (DETA), triethylenetetramine (TETA),                  tetraethylenepentamine (TEPA),                  pentaethylenehexamine (PEHA),                  aminoethylethanolamine (AEEA),                  hydroxyethyldiethylenetriamine (HE-DETA),                  hydroxyethyltriethylenetetramine (HE-TETA), urea                  of triethylenetetramine (UTETA), urea of                  pentaethylenehexamine (UPEHA), urea of                  tetraethylenepentamine (UTEPA) and urea of                  hydroxyethyl triethylenetetramine (HE-UTETA),                  when the cyclic alkylene urea is urea of                  diethylenetriamine (UDETA), the amine compound is                  chosen from TETA, TEPA, PEHA, AEEA, HE-DETA,                  HE-TETA, UTETA, UTEPA and HE-UTETA, when the                  cyclic alkylene urea is urea of                  aminoethylethanolamine (UAEEA), the amine                  compound is chosen from TETA, TEPA, PEHA,                  HE-DETA, HE-TETA, UTETA, UTEPA and HE-UTETA, when                  the cyclic alkylene urea is UTETA or diurea of                  triethylenetetramine (DUTETA), the amine compound                  is chosen from TEPA, PEHA, HE-DETA, HE-TETA,                  UTETA, UTEPA, UPEHA and HE-UTETA, when the cyclic                  alkylene urea is urea of                  hydroxyethyldiethylenetriamine (HE-UDETA), the                  amine compound is chosen from TEPA, PEHA,                  HE-TETA, UTEPA, UPEHA and HE-UTETA, when the                  cyclic alkylene urea is UTEPA or diurea of                  tetraethylenepentamine (DUTEPA), the amine                  compound is chosen from PEHA, HE-TETA, UTEPA,                  UPEHA and HE-UTETA, and when the cyclic alkylene                  urea is UPEHA, diurea of pentaethylenehexamine                  (DUPEHA) or triurea of pentaethylenehexamine                  (TUPEHA), the amine compound is UPEHA or                  HE-UTETA.R1 = H, X-R3-(NH-R3-)p-, or X-R3-(O-R3-)n-,                  or group combining alkyleneamino and                  alkoxy;p,n = 1 or more, preferably 2-20;-N-R3-N- = group of formula (i) or                  (ii);R2 = H;X = hydroxy, amino, linear or branched 1-20C                  hydroxyalkyl or 1-20C aminoalkyl;andR3 = optionally substituted alkylene.(i) When n or p is 2-20, R2 optionally                  contains piperazinyl and/or alkyleneurea; and                  (ii) When p or n is 0, R2 is 1-20C hydroxyalkyl                  or 1-20C aminoalkyl. Preferred Conditions: In                  steps (a,b), 5-95% alkylene urea moieties are                  converted into amine moieties. The step (a) is                  carried out at a temperature of at least 150                  degrees C, preferably 250-320 degrees C. The step                  (b) is carried out at a temperature of at least                  150 degrees C, preferably 350 degrees C and a                  pressure of 127 bar or less, preferably less than                  5 bar. The feed in the step (a) has carbon                  dioxide loading of at least 0.2, preferably                  0.6-1. The feed in the step (b) has carbon                  dioxide loading of at most 0.8, preferably                  0.2-0.6. The water present in the step (b) is                  less than 10 wt.%, preferably less than 5                  wt.%.

AU HU H

   ZHENGZHOU FULONG NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY (CGSU-C)
GA 201904954V
AB    NOVELTY - A high performance TC-5A molecular sieve adsorbent is prepared by taking 8-15 wt.% kaolin, and 2-3 mu m of D50 2.5 m small and medium crystal 4A zeolite, mixing, granulating and sieving into diameter of 0.5-2.5 mm, coating with 10-50 wt.% 4A zeolite and 50-90 wt.% kaolin mixture, drying, calcining, carrying out pre-absorption, performing alkalization treatment, washing, performing calcium exchange method, drying, primary calcining, pumping high purity nitrogen, and secondary calcining to obtain product with high abrasion loss of 0.005%, dust of 40 ppm and carbon dioxide adsorption capacity of 6% under 4 torr at 25 degrees C.
   USE - Method for preparing high performance TC-5A molecular sieve adsorbent.

Preferred Component: The kaolin is halloysite.

PN CN108339373-A; CN108339373-B
TI Preparation of alumina-molecular sieve catalyst            used for catalytic carbon dioxide desorption, involves            dissolving soluble metal aluminum salt in water, adding            acidic molecular sieve powder, ultrasonically treating,            and increasing pH value.
AU LIANG Z
   ZHANG X
   LIU H
   TONG B
AE UNIV HUNAN (UYHU-C)
GA 201861932G
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation method of alumina-molecular                sieve catalyst involves (1) dissolving soluble                metal aluminum salt in water, and adding an acidic                molecular sieve powder to obtain mixed solution                (A), (2) ultrasonically treating the mixed solution                (A) to obtain mixed solution (B), (3) adding                alkaline substance to increase the pH value of the                mixed solution (B), precipitating the metal                aluminum salt, and aging to obtain mixed solution                (C), and (4) solid-liquid separating the mixed                solution (C), washing the separated solid with                water, drying, and calcining.
   USE - Preparation method of alumina-molecular sieve                catalyst used for catalytic carbon dioxide                desorption.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of                alumina-molecular sieve catalyst with excellent                catalytic desorption capability and small effect on                the carbon dioxide absorption performance of the                amine solution, and which is easy to separate,                stable and recyclable, by simple and economical                processes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: In the step (1), the                  mass ratio of the soluble metal aluminum salt to                  the acidic molecular sieve is 1:2-3:1.Preferred Components: In the step (3), the                  alkaline substance is aqueous ammonia or sodium                  hydroxide. In the step (1), the acidic molecular                  sieve is an HZSM series, SAPO series, MCM series,                  SBA series, H-( beta ), HY and/or HUSY-6                  molecular sieve.Preferred Conditions: In the step (2), the                  ultrasonically treating time is 0.5-2 hours. In                  the step (3), the alkaline substance is added to                  hydrolyze and precipitate the metal aluminum salt                  for 2-8 hours. In the step (3), the aging time is                  6-24 hours. In the step (4), the drying                  temperature is 60-200 degrees C and the drying                  time is 8-36 hours. In the step (4), the                  calcining temperature is 300-800 degrees C and                  the calcining time is 2-12 hours.Preferred Properties: In the step (3), the                  alkaline substance is added to improve the pH                  value of the mixed solution (B) to 8-12.

TI Hydrogenation of carbon dioxide and removing alkyne comprises introducing effluent from the top of deethanizer to adiabatic reactor for selective hydrogenation.
AU GOU G
   LI H
   HU X
   YU L
   HAN W
   QIAN Y
   LIU M
   ZHANG F
   LIANG Y
   CHE C
   LIU J
   XIE P
   WANG B
AE PETROCHINA CO LTD (CNPC-C)
GA 201854534Q
AB    NOVELTY - Hydrogenation of carbon dioxide and removing alkyne comprises introducing effluent from the top of deethanizer in carbon two post-hydrogenation ethylene plant to adiabatic reactor for selective hydrogenation. The reactor is equipped with iron-based selective hydrogenation catalyst. The carrier is high temperature resistant inorganic oxide. The catalyst comprises 3-8%, preferably 4-6% iron and 0.5-1.5%, preferably 0.8-1.2% silver. The specific surface of catalyst is 10-200 m2/g, preferably 30-100 m2/g. The pore volume is 0.2-0.63 ml/g, preferably 0.35-0.49 ml/g. The catalyst is carried on the carrier by impregnation, roasting and hydrogen atmosphere reduction. The reactor inlet temperature is 40-100 degrees C. The reaction pressure is 1.5-2.5 MPa. The gas airspeed is 2000-10000 h-1. The hydrogen/acetylene volume ratio is 0.5-3.
   USE - Method of hydrogenating carbon dioxide and removing alkyne.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has good operation elasticity and good ethylene selectivity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The active component iron is alpha -iron oxide. The catalyst carrier is alumina or alumina carrier. The alumina carrier comprises composite carrier of alumina and other oxides. The alumina comprises more than 50% of carrier. The alumina carrier is alumina, silica, zirconia or magnesium oxide. The alumina-zirconia composite carrier comprises 60% alumina. The alumina is theta , alpha , gamma type or crystal form, preferably alpha -aluminum oxide or mixed crystal alumina containing alpha -aluminum oxide. The volume content of hydrogen is 10-50%. The top effluent comprises 0.98-2.2% acetylene, 11.2-30.3% ethane and 65-85% ethylene. Preferred Method: The reduction temperature is 300-450 degrees C, preferably 350-400 degrees C. The reduction time is 240-360 minutes. The airspeed is 60-350 h-1. The reduction pressure is 0.1-0.5 MPa.

PN CN113073343-A
TI Synthesis of copper-zinc bimetallic catalyst used for electrocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction, involves depositing zinc oxide on conductive substrate with copper oxide nanowires, by atomic layer deposition, wrapping layer of ZIF-8, and performing high-temperature calcination under inert atmosphere.
AU LUO J
   WAN L
AE UNIV NANKAI (UNAN-C)
GA 202185123T
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesis of copper-zinc bimetallic catalyst involves depositing zinc oxide with a controllable thickness on surface of a conductive substrate with copper oxide nanowires grown in-situ, by atomic layer deposition process to form core-shell structure copper oxide-doped zinc oxide nanowires, wrapping a layer of ZIF-8 on the surface by hydrothermal method, to form ZIF-8 coated copper oxide-doped zinc oxide nanowires, and performing high-temperature calcination under the protection of an inert atmosphere. The copper-zinc bimetallic nanowire material has a phase-separated structure.
   USE - Synthesis of copper-zinc bimetallic catalyst used for electrocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction.
   ADVANTAGE - The phase separation copper-zinc double-metal nanowires have high efficiency performance and stability of electrocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction. The synthesis method is simple and operation is easy.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The atomic layer deposition technology involves depositing zinc oxide on a substrate with copper oxide nanowires to obtain copper oxide and oxide zinc nanowires with a core-shell structure, and the deposition thickness of zinc oxide is 30-80 nm. The hydrothermal method involves coating a layer of ZIF-8 on the surface of the copper oxide-doped zinc oxide nanowires with a core-shell structure to obtain ZIF-8-coated copper oxide-doped zinc oxide nanowires, using 0.8-1.6 mM dimethylimidazole in dimethylformamide/water solution in volume ratio of 3:1 as the precursor solution of the hydrothermal reaction at temperature of 50-100 degrees C, and the reaction time is 4-10 hours. The copper oxide-doped zinc oxide nanowires coated with ZIF8 is calcined at a high temperature under the protection of an inert atmosphere to obtain a copper-zinc bimetallic nanowires with a phase-separated structure, and the calcination temperature is 600-1000 degrees C and the calcination time is 0.5-4 hours. The electrocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction performance is efficient and stable in the reduction potential -1.0 to -1.6V vs. RHE range in the carbon dioxide saturated 0.1 MKHCO3 electrolyte to obtain the copper-zinc bimetallic nanowires with a phase-separated structure.

PN CN111825877-A
TI Preparing polypropylene foam material comprises foaming polypropylene and small molecular alcohol under carbon dioxide conditions.
AU HU D
   ZHAO L
   QIANG W
   GAO X
   LIU T
AE UNIV EAST CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYEC-C)
GA 2020A7443U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing polypropylene foam material comprises foaming polypropylene and small molecular alcohol under carbon dioxide conditions, where the small molecular alcohol is an alcohol with a carbon chain of 1-10C, when the small molecular alcohol is an alcohol with a carbon chain of 1-4C, the foaming temperature is greater than 125 degrees C and less than 140 degrees C, when the small molecular alcohol is an alcohol with a carbon chain of 5-10C, the foaming temperature is 125-140 degrees C, the addition amount of the small molecular alcohol is 5-10 wt.% of carbon dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing polypropylene foam material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple; and broadens the lower limit of polypropylene foaming temperature, thus allows polypropylene to be foamed at lower temperatures; reduces energy consumption and lowering the requirements for equipment sealing. The foaming material: has high foaming ratio, small pore size and high cell density.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for polypropylene foam material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The small molecular alcohol is an alcohol with a carbon chain of 2-10C, preferably 2-4C. The small molecular alcohol is ethanol, butanol, hexanol, and/or decanol. The content of small alcohol is 5 wt.% of carbon dioxide. Preferred Method: In the process, when the small alcohol is an alcohol with a carbon chain of 1-4C, the foaming temperature is 135-140 degrees C, and when the small molecular alcohol is an alcohol with a carbon chain of 5-10C, and the foaming temperature is 125-135 degrees C. The preparation further comprises compressing polypropylene into tablets; or, adding the small molecular alcohol, purging with CO2 three times, exhausting the air in the kettle, heating to the foaming temperature, keeping the temperature, and filling with high-pressure CO2, setting the pressure to 15-20 MPa, making the tablets as saturated, and relieving the pressure, cooling and shaping to obtain microcellular foam material, the heating rate is a constant rate, preferably, the heating rate is 5-10 degrees C/minute.

   X=Br, Cl, I, or F; and
   n=0-5.

PN CN110373893-A; CN110373893-B
TI Preparing graphene conductive fabric comprises            e.g. mixing fabric with alkali solution, treating,            subjecting alkali treated fabric to supercritical            carbon dioxide treatment to obtain pretreated fabric,            and adhering graphene slurry coating.
AU WANG H
   LAI C
   CHEN Y
   LIU Z
AE NINGBO GRAPHENE INNOVATION CENT CO LTD (NING-Non-standard)
GA 201990239W
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing graphene conductive fabric comprises                mixing fabric with alkali solution having mass                concentration of 20-40 g/l, treating at 50-75                degrees C for 20-30 minutes, subjecting the alkali                treated fabric to supercritical carbon dioxide                treatment to obtain pretreated fabric, and adhering                graphene slurry coating to the surface of                pretreated fabric to obtain the product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing graphene                conductive fabric.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: prepares the fabric having                excellent swelling effect, through synergistic                action of alkali treatment, and supercritical                carbon dioxide treatment; increases the roughness                of surface of fabric for entering graphene sheet                structure in graphene paste to the interior of                fabric and swollen voids; greatly improves water                washing performance of graphene conductive fabric;                has stronger conductive property of fabric, and                better antistatic effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                graphene conductive fabric prepared by the above                mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The alkali solution                  comprises sodium hydroxide solution, and/or                  potassium hydroxide solution. The alkali solution                  has a mass concentration of 20-25 g/l. The                  graphene has a sheet size of 0.34-5 nm, and a                  sheet diameter of 200 nm-50 mu m, optionally, the                  graphene has a sheet size of 3-5 nm, and a sheet                  diameter of 5-10 mu m. The dispersant in the                  graphene slurry comprises anions, and non-ions,                  optionally the dispersant comprises methylene                  bisbenzylnaphthalene di sodium salt, alkyl                  diphenyl ether sulfonate, naphthalene sulfonate                  condensate, methylene dimethyl naphthalene                  sulfonate sodium salt, and/or fatty alcohol                  polyvinyl ether silane. The fabric is cotton,                  polyester fabric, or blend of polyester and                  cellulosic fibers, optionally the fabric is                  cotton fabric, cotton and linen blend fabric,                  polyester cotton, and polyester-viscose blend                  fabric. Preferred Method: The conditions for the                  supercritical carbon dioxide treatment comprises                  treating at 32-40 degrees C, and at the pressure                  of 73-80 atm for 30 seconds-5 minutes,                  optionally, the conditions for the supercritical                  carbon dioxide treatment comprises treating at                  33-35 degrees C, and at the pressure of 75-77 atm                  for 2-4 minutes. The method further comprises                  calendering the fabric after alkali treatment,                  and before supercritical carbon dioxide                  treatment. The conditions of the calendering                  process comprises the pressure of 6-10 MPa,                  temperature of 100-140 degrees C, and has the                  speed of 20-30 m/minute. The method further                  comprises coating of graphene paste, then hot                  pressing, water washing, and shaping the graphene                  conductive fabric.

PN CN110218190-A; CN110218190-B
TI Preparing tetrahydropyrimidine derivative useful            in synthesis and medicinal chemistry fields, comprises            e.g. introducing carbon dioxide gas, reacting aromatic            amine compounds, reducing agent, and then reacting with            enamine compounds.
AU ZHAO Y
   LIU X
   ZHENG L
   GUO X
   DU Y
   SHI X
   WAN Z
   CHEN T
   DING X
   ZHOU Z
AE UNIV QUFU NORMAL (UNQN-C)
GA 201979557A
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing tetrahydropyrimidine derivative (I),                comprises introducing carbon dioxide gas and                reacting aromatic amine compounds (II), reducing                agent, organic solvent and first catalyst at                corresponding temperature and time, adding enamine                compounds (III) and second catalyst, and then                reacting to obtain final product.
   USE - (I) is useful in synthesis and medicinal                chemistry fields (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes easily available and                simple raw materials; is environmentally friendly;                has simple post treatment and high product                yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing tetrahydropyrimidine derivative of                formula (I), comprises introducing carbon dioxide                gas and reacting aromatic amine compounds of                formula (II), reducing agent, organic solvent and                first catalyst at corresponding temperature and                time, adding enamine compounds of formula (III) and                second catalyst, and then reacting to obtain final                product.
   R1 = ketone carbonyl group or ester carbonyl                substituent;
   R2 = aryl substituent; and
   R3 and R4 = alkyl or aryl substituent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: (III) is aminoketene                  or urethane ester based compound, and (II) is                  electron rich aromatic amine or electron                  deficient aromatic amine. The first catalyst is                  1,5,7-triazabicyclo(4.4.0)dec-5-ene (TBD) and the                  second catalyst is combination of boron                  trifluoride etherate, ferric chloride or zinc                  chloride. The reducing agent is hydrosilane-based                  reducing agent, and the purity of carbon dioxide                  is greater than 99.9%. The molar ratio between                  (III) and (II) is 1:1-1:5, the molar ratio                  between (III) and carbon dioxide is 1:2-1:10, the                  molar ratio between (III) and reducing agent is                  1:4-1:10, the molar ratio between (III) and first                  catalyst is 1:0.1-1:0.9, and the molar ratio                  between (III) and second-catalyst is 1:0.1-1:0.9.                  The organic solvent is acetonitrile,                  N,N-dimethylacetamide or N,N-dimethylformamide.                  Preferred Method: The reaction is a one-pot                  reaction without intermediate separation and is                  added in portions. The method comprises, reaction                  is carry out at 90-115 degrees C under carbon                  dioxide gas atmosphere, then detecting (III) by                  thin layer chromatography, and then purifying to                  obtain (I) having yield of 45-99%, where the                  reaction time for first step is 6-12 hours and                  second step is 12-24 hours.

PT P
PN WO2018189366-A1; CN110475813-A; KR2019134647-A; EP3609952-A1; US2020071484-A1; JP2020513049-W; US11124621-B2
TI Preparing porous material e.g. as thermal insulation material or for vacuum insulation panels by providing gel containing solvent, pressurizing the gel with carbon dioxide, removing supernatant liquid, and drying the gel.
AU WEINRICH D
   LOELSBERG W
   FRICKE M
   FERNANDEZ RAMIREZ G A
   VOGELSANG V
   MORALES A B
   SUBRAHMANYAM R
   GURIKOV P
   SELMER I
   SMIRNOVA I
   LOLSBERG W
   FERNANDEZ R G A
AE BASF SE (BADI-C)
   BASF SE (BADI-C)
   BASF SE (BADI-C)
   BASF SE (BADI-C)
GA 201881185V
AB    NOVELTY - Porous material is prepared by (a) providing gel containing solvent having a volume (V1), (b) pressurizing the gel with carbon dioxide at temperature and pressure at which carbon dioxide solubilizes in solvent forming gas-expanded liquid (EL) having volume (V2) that is greater than V1, (c) removing supernatant liquid, and (d) drying the gel.
   USE - Preparation of porous material as thermal insulation material or for vacuum insulation panels (all claimed). Can also be used for insulation in interior or exterior of building.
   ADVANTAGE - Substantive amount of solvent in a given gel can be removed before actual drying step. Large proportion of solvent can be expelled with the liquid phase from the gel. Lower pore size of the gel leads to slower expulsion of liquid phase from the gel. Lower thickness of the gel leads to faster expulsion of liquid phase from the gel.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Gel is organic gel. Solvent is chosen from alcohols, ketones, esters, aldehydes, alkyl alkanoates, amides, sulfoxides, aliphatic and cycloaliphatic halogenated hydrocarbons, halogenated aromatic compounds, dialkyl ethers, cyclic ethers and fluorine-containing ethers, and acetals. Preferred Process: Gel is provided in the form of monolithic block. Step (b) is carried out at temperature in the range between critical point of carbon dioxide (T1) and critical point of solvent (T2). Drying according to step (d) is carried out by converting the liquid in the gel into gaseous state at temperature and pressure below the critical point of liquid or gas-expanded liquid in the gel. Preferred Conditions: Step (b) is carried out at 31-90 degrees C and at pressure of 0.1-0.99 Pc,mix where Pc,mix is pressure of critical point of solvent-carbon dioxide system at temperature T. Step (b) is carried out at pressure 50-200 bar. Pressure is gradually increased from 1-150 bar for 30-90 minutes. Drying according to step (d) is carried out under supercritical conditions.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); Q46 (Building aids, special structures, ladders (E04G,H, E06C))

PT P
PN CN108285733-A
TI Preparation of polyurethane involves adding e.g. epoxidized soybean oil into reactor, introducing carbon dioxide gas, reacting, preparing polyaminoorganosiloxane solution using e.g. ethanol, adding e.g. cyclic carbonate and mixing.
AU WANG X
AE FUJIAN LONGSHANG MICROFIBER CO LTD (FUJI-Non-standard)
GA 2018570297
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of polyurethane for coating film formation, involves adding epoxidized soybean oil and catalyst in an amount of 1-5 %mass of epoxidized soybean oil into reactor, introducing carbon dioxide gas, reacting at 100-140 degrees C at 1-10 MPa for 30-40 hours under stirring, stopping the carbon dioxide gas at the end of the reaction to obtain a cyclic carbonate, preparing polyaminoorganosiloxane solution using 20 %mass aminopropyltriethoxysilane, 10 %mass water, and 70 %mass ethanol, adding polyaminoorganosiloxane solution in drops to the cyclic carbonate, stirring and mixing the mixture at room temperature for 20-24 hours. The catalyst is quaternary ammonium salt or metal halide or transition metal oxide.
   USE - Preparation of polyurethane.
   ADVANTAGE - The prepared polyurethane replaces isocyanate polyurethane as film-forming substance for surface coating of synthetic leather, textile, and furniture, and solves the problem that the isocyanate is more toxic in the preparation process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The amount of carbon dioxide gas is 99.99 vol.%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The catalyst is prepared from sodium iodide and dimethylformamide or dimethyl sulfoxide solvent in the ratio of 1-5:100 to obtain a homogeneous solution, discharging redundant gas after the reaction is completed, distilling and releasing the solvent under negative pressure state. Preferred Property: The progress of the reaction is judged by monitoring the infrared spectrum during the reaction. When the infrared spectrum of the reactants appears at 1805 cm-1, characteristic peak corresponding to cyclic carbonate appears and increases. The characteristic peak corresponding to epoxy carbonyl appears in the wavelength of 823 cm-1 and 845 cm-1, and reduces and disappears indicating the reaction completion. When the characteristic peak of the cyclic carbonate at 1805 cm-1 reduces and disappears, the peak corresponding to carbamate is observed at 1704 cm-1 and 1545 cm-1 and addition of polyaminoorganosiloxane solution in drops is stopped to prepare a silica hybrid non-isocyanate polyurethane. Preferred Condition: The epoxidized soybean oil and catalyst are reacted under carbon dioxide gas at 120-125 degrees C at 8-10 MPa.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The catalyst comprises zinc chloride-zinc bromide-tetrabutylammonium bromide in a mass ratio of 1.8:1:2 with respect to epoxidized soybean oil.

PN CN115160038-A
TI Preparing sludge by composite microbial inoculum,            involves inoculating composite microbial inoculum to            culture medium, culturing, centrifuging, collecting            thallus precipitate, adding sterile defatted milk            powder into the thallus, freeze-drying and storing            followed by finishing fermentation.
AU WANG F
   LI H
   WANG R
AE YOUYOU INTELLIGENT ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECT (YOUY-Non-standard)
GA 2022D0123U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing sludge involves inoculating the                composite microbial inoculum to the culture medium                and culturing for 18-24 h at 5% carbon dioxide at                37 &#176;C. The inoculum is centrifuged for                30-45 min at 12000 rpm and collected thallus                precipitate. The 10-20% sterile defatted milk                powder is added into the thallus, freeze-dried and                stored. The mixture is stirred uniformly according                to 1 g strain to ferment 1 ton amount. The strain                is mixed with sludge uniformly. The fermentation                period is 60 d and stirred once every 15 days                followed by finishing fermentation after 60 d to                obtain fermentation product with fertilizer                effect.
   USE - Method for preparing sludge by composite                microbial inoculum.
   ADVANTAGE - The method carries out large-scale                fermentation, has broad market prospects and                greater economic benefits, and effectively solves                the technical problems of low reaction efficiency,                poor sludge degradability and long residence time                in the existing sludge treatment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The preparation process of                  the culture medium comprises preparing raw                  materials of 20-25 g casein pancreatin digestion                  product, 0.9-1.5 g starch, 5-8 g sodium chloride,                  0.9-1.2 g dipotassium hydrogen phosphate, 9-13 g                  lactose, 5-8 g yeast extract, 10 g vitamin                  B12-28, 900-1300 ml distilled water, uniformly                  mixing and dissolving the raw materials,                  sterilizing at 120-130 &#176;C under high                  pressure for 15-25 min, and cooling to 40-50                  &#176;C.

PN WO2022203985-A1
TI Conversion of methane in methane-containing feed            to 2C or more hydrocarbon and hydrogen, involves            providing methane-containing feed to methane conversion            reactor operating under specific methane conversion            conditions.
AU PETERSON A
   GOLDSMITH F
   BAE J
   YUN D
   IM J
   KIM D
AE UNIV BROWN (UYBW-C)
   SK INNOVATION CO LTD (SKSK-C)
GA 2022C5347M
AB    NOVELTY - Conversion of methane in methane-containing                feed involves providing the methane-containing feed                to a methane conversion reactor operating under                methane conversion conditions to obtain product                comprising 2C or more hydrocarbons. The methane                conversion conditions include an absolute pressure                of less than 1 MPa and a residence time of less                than 60 seconds.
   USE - Conversion of methane in methane-containing                feed to 2C or more hydrocarbon such as ethane,                ethylene, and acetylene, and aromatic hydrocarbons                e.g. benzene and naphthalene and hydrogen, used as                fuel components, specialty chemicals, or                intermediates in manufacture of petrochemicals                including plastics e.g. polyolefin, polyamide,                polyaromatics such as polyesters and                polystyrene.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables conversion of methane in                methane-containing feed without requiring oxidant                to form carbon monoxide as intermediate. Since                direct N-doped ordered mesoporous carbons is                converted to 2C or more hydrocarbons, in methane                conversion reactor operating under methane                conversion conditions, thereby reducing complexity                and unwanted side reactions that reduce hydrocarbon                yields and lead to carbon dioxide production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The methane conversion                  active metal is chosen from iron, chromium,                  manganese, vanadium, molybdenum, and                  tungsten.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The methane conversion                  is performed at 750-2000&#176; C and hydrogen                  content in the methane conversion reactor is at                  least 10 vol.%. Preferred Components: The                  methane-containing feed comprises at least 20                  vol.%, preferably 90 vol.% or more methane and                  less than 5 vol.% combined amount of water,                  carbon dioxide, oxygen, and hydrosulfide. The 2C                  or more hydrocarbons include one or more 2C or                  more hydrocarbons and one or more aromatic                  hydrocarbons. The 2C hydrocarbons include ethane,                  ethylene, or acetylene. The aromatic hydrocarbon                  includes benzene, 1-2C substituted benzene or                  fused ring aromatic hydrocarbons. The conversion                  of methane in the methane-containing feed is at                  least 15 wt.%. The conversion of methane in the                  methane-containing feed is converted with                  selectivity to 2C or more hydrocarbons of at                  least 35 wt.%. The methane in the                  methane-containing feed is converted with a                  selectivity to single ring aromatic hydrocarbons                  of at least 45 wt.%. Methane in the                  methane-containing feed is converted with                  selectivity to coke of less than 3 wt.%. The                  methane conversion reactor contains a catalyst                  comprising a methane conversion active metal, or                  compound of methane conversion active metal.                  Preferred Process: The method involves providing                  the methane-containing feed to a methane                  conversion reactor operating under methane                  conversion conditions to obtain a product                  comprising the 2C or more hydrocarbons, including                  one or more 2C hydrocarbons and one or more                  aromatic hydrocarbons, and further comprising                  hydrogen and unconverted methane and separating                  the product by condensing from the product a                  liquid being enriched in the one or more aromatic                  hydrocarbons. The separation provides a vapor,                  which is enriched in the hydrogen and the                  unconverted methane. The vapor is further                  enriched in the one or more 2C hydrocarbons. The                  method further involves recycling at least a                  portion of the vapor to the methane conversion                  reactor. The process further involves, in                  response to a performance parameter of the                  process that is below a target value, increasing                  a hydrogen concentration or hydrogen partial                  pressure in the methane conversion reactor. The                  performance parameter is a conversion of methane                  in the methane-containing feed or a yield of the                  2C or more hydrocarbons.

PN CN114873690-A
TI Treating waste water containing dimethyl            formamide, involves carrying out electrolytic            degradation on waste water containing dimethyl            formamide serving as electrolyte, inert polar plate            serving as anode and titanium serving as cathode under            action of direct current electric field and            catalyst.
AU XING M
   YU Y
   YAN Q
   ZHANG Y
   SHAO S
AE HEBEI HAILI FRAGRANCES CO LTD (HEBE-Non-standard)
GA 2022A5673H
AB    NOVELTY - Treating waste water containing dimethyl                formamide, involves carrying out electrolytic                degradation on waste water containing dimethyl                formamide serving as electrolyte, an inert polar                plate serving as an anode and titanium serving as a                cathode under the action of a direct current                electric field and a catalyst. The catalyst                comprises particulate carbon and cobalt supported                on the particulate carbon. The catalyst is                dispersed in the waste water containing dimethyl                formamide.
   USE - Method for treating waste water containing                dimethyl formamide.
   ADVANTAGE - The wastewater electrolyzes and degrades the                dimethylformamide in the wastewater containing the                dimethylformamide into carbon dioxide and water                under the action of the catalyst (comprises                granular carbon and cobalt loaded on the granular                carbon) and the direct current electric field, and                further avoids generation of secondary pollutants.                The primarily purified wastewater obtained after                electrolytic degradation is directly subjected to                biochemical treatment, so that to realize discharge                of the wastewater.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The DC electric field                  has a current density of 100-250 A/cm2. Preferred                  Compositions: The mass ratio of catalyst and                  dimethylformamide-containing wastewater is 1:                  1-2. Preferred Process: The catalyst is prepared                  by adding granular carbon into a soluble cobalt                  salt water solution for adsorption, and then                  adding a reducing agent for reduction reaction to                  obtain target product. The mass ratio of the                  soluble cobalt salt and granular carbon in                  soluble cobalt salt aqueous solution is 1:                  50-100, preferably 1: 1.5-2.5. The adsorption                  time is 1-4 hours. The inert plate is a ruthenium                  plate or a platinum plate. The pH value at the                  end point of the electrolytic degradation is                  10-12. The obtained preliminary purified                  wastewater is subjected to biochemical treatment                  after the electrolytic degradation.

TI Comprehensive recovery method of arsenic and            antimony soot involves adding arsenic and anti-smoke            dust into leaching solution for section of oxidation            leaching, filtering, and obtaining filtrate A and            filter residue A.
AU CAO B
   CHEN W
AE HUNAN RES INST NONFERROUS METALS (HUNA-Non-standard)
GA 202216984G
AB    NOVELTY - Comprehensive recovery method of arsenic and                antimony soot comprises adding arsenic and                anti-antimony smog dust to leaching solution for                oxidation leaching, filtering to obtain filtrate                and filter residue, adding filter residue A to                leaching liquid for two-stage oxidation-leaching,                filtering, obtaining filtrates B and filter residue                B, electrolyzing filtrated A to obtain electrolytic                arsenic and electrolytic waste liquid, roasting                filter residues B at high temperature to obtain                low-arsenic high-antimony slag, where method                involves adding arsenic and antimony smoke dust to                lye solution for two stages of oxidative leaching,                and filtering.
   USE - The method is used for carrying out                comprehensive recovery of arsenic and antimony                soot.
   ADVANTAGE - The arsenic and antimony smoke dust can be                recovered and processed effectively. The                arsenic-containing filter liquid obtained in the                recycling process can be used for electrolytic                production of electrolytic arsenic. The leaching                generated carbon dioxide is bubbler in the                electrolytic waste liquid to regenerate sodium                carbonate, realizing zero medicine cost and zero                discharge of waste water.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The leachate contains                  sodium carbonate solution.Preferred Method: The first stage oxidative                  leaching is done at 75-100degreesC for 2-3 hours.                  The pH value of reaction end point of the first                  stage of oxidative leaching is controlled to 8-9.                  The second-stage oxidative leaching is done at                  75-100degreesC for 2-3 hours. The first-stage                  oxidative leaching is carried out under stirring                  conditions, and the second-stage oxidative                  leaching is carried out under stirring and                  microwave irradiation conditions, and oxidant                  used in oxidative leaching is air and/or oxygen.                  The frequency of microwaves in microwave                  radiation is controlled to 2450 MHz. The                  electrolysis described is diaphragm electrolysis,                  and diaphragm is homogeneous cationic membrane.                  The oxidative leaching produces waste gas, and                  the waste gas is introduced into electrolytic                  waste liquid to regenerate sodium carbonate. The                  high-temperature roasting treatment is carried                  out at 900-930degreesC for 0.5-1 hour. The                  filtrate B is returned to participate in one                  stage oxidative leaching.

The M1 is alkali metal element. The catalyst comprises 0.1-2 wt.% platinum element, and 1-10 wt.% M1 element.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for oxidizing carbon monoxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has high carbon monoxide catalytic oxidation activity and stability in various characteristic atmospheres (e.g. hydrogen-rich, carbon dioxide-rich, sulfur-containing), and excellent application potential in carbon monoxide elimination scenarios e.g. fuel cells, automobile exhaust and low-temperature methanol wash exhaust.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the catalyst, comprising co-precipitation method comprises mixing the platinum precursor solution and M2 precursor solution to obtain the mixed solution A, adding the mixed solution A dropwise to the alkali solution containing M1 element, adjusting pH to obtain mixture B after the dropwise addition, crystallizing the mixture B, washing, and treating at high temperature to obtain catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The M2 is manganese, iron and/or nickel, and the mass of platinum is 0.15-3 wt.% of M2 oxide. The transition metal element M2 is manganese element. The M1 is sodium and/or potassium elements. The ratio of the amount of M1 element in the solution of M1 element to the sum of the amount of platinum in the platinum precursor solution and the amount of M2 in the M2 precursor solution is 2:1-4:1. The platinum precursor is water-soluble platinum-containing salt solution, preferably chloroplatinic acid or platinum chloride. The M2 precursor is water-soluble salt solution containing M2 element, preferably nitrate or chloride containing M2 element. The solution containing M1 element includes M1-hydroxide or M12-carbon trioxide. The concentration of the solution of M1 element is 0.1-0.5 M.Preferred Method: The high temperature treatment is heating at temperature of 150-400degrees Celsius, preferably150-250degrees Celsius. The high temperature treatment process is carried out in air atmosphere or in mixed gas containing hydrogen. The high temperature treatment is first performed in air atmosphere, and performed in hydrogen-containing mixed gas. The drying step is performed before the high temperature treatment, and the drying temperature is lower than 100degrees Celsius, preferably 70-100degrees Celsius. The preparation method specifically comprises mixing the platinum precursor solution, M2 precursor solution and the alumina powder, obtaining mixed solution A1, mixing the solution containing the M1 element with the mixed solution A1, adjusting the pH to 7-10 to obtain mixture B1, crystallizing, washing and drying the mixture B1 to obtain catalyst powder, mixing the obtained catalyst powder, surfactant and water to prepare catalyst slurry, mixing the ceramic carrier with pores and the catalyst slurry to obtain mixture C1, and treating the mixture C1 at high temperature to obtain coated catalyst, where the mass of the platinum element is 0.5-2 wt.% of the alumina.

AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of metal phthalocyanine complex-titanium dioxide composite photocatalyst comprises adding hydrofluoric acid to tetrabutyl titanate, stirring for 20-60 minutes, hydrothermally reacting in an autoclave at 180degrees Celsius for 12-24 hours, washing with ethanol and deionized water for 3 times, drying and grinding to obtain titanium dioxide nanosheet; and adding titanium dioxide nanosheet and metal phthalocyanine complex to N,N-dimethylformamide, and heat-refluxing for 2-5 hours while stirring. PN CN113426994-A; CN113426994-B
TI Passivation treatment process of soft magnetic            metal powder useful for e.g. inductance forming,            involves using phosphate as passivating agent to            passivate soft magnetic powder, drying to obtain            primary passivation, and passivating primary            product.
AU PENG M
   ZHANG H
AE HOTLAND ELECTRONICS SHENZHEN CO LTD (HOTL-Non-standard)
GA 2021B4111V
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves using phosphate as a                passivating agent to passivate a soft magnetic                metal powder and drying to obtain a primary                passivation. The preliminary passivation is placed                in carbon dioxide gas to react to obtain the                primary product. The phosphate and a nano adsorbent                are mixed according to a weight ratio of 1:0.1-0.5.                A mixture is obtained. The mixture is used as the                passivant. The primary product is passivated by                using the mixture. The dried product is dried to                obtain finished product powder.
   USE - Process of passivation treatment of soft                magnetic metal powder for inductance forming, flat                computer, notebook computer, vehicle device, smart                phone, YWS earphone, smart watch and other high-end                consumer electronics and intelligent hardware field                expanding application.
   ADVANTAGE - The finished product soft magnetic metal                powder has good magnetic conductivity and high                resistivity. The inner structure of the phosphating                film on the surface of the primary product is                uniform and compact, eliminating internal stress                and air hole.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Components: The nano adsorbent is                  composed of nano silicon nitride and nano silicon                  dioxide in a weight ratio of 1:0.8-1.6. The raw                  material of the nano adsorbent further comprises                  silicone, and the weight ratio of nano silicon                  nitride, nano silicon dioxide and silicone is                  1:0.8-1.6:0.1-0.5. The nano silicon nitride is                  aminated modified nano silicon nitride, and the                  nano silicon dioxide is aminated modified nano                  silicon dioxide. The aminated modified nano                  silicon nitride is prepared by using 2-6 pts. wt.                  polyethylene glycol aqueous solution, 5-10 pts.                  wt. nano silicon nitride, and 5-12 pts. wt.                  aminopropyltriethoxysilane. The aminated modified                  nano silicon dioxide is prepared by using 0.2-0.7                  pts. wt. nano silica microsphere, 20-30 pts. wt.                  anhydrous toluene, and 0.2-0.8 pts. wt.                  aminopropyltriethoxysilane. The method utilizes                  carbon monoxide gas.

PN CN113398937-A; CN113398937-B
TI Preparing nickel-zinc-zinc oxide catalyst            comprises e.g. stirring and dispersing nickel precursor            and zinc precursor in water to obtain nickel zinc            solution, adding organic alkaline substance, performing            hydrothermal reaction and heating.
AU ZHENG N
   LIU N
   LIU K
   LI K
   ZHANG Y
   LIU S
   CHEN J
AE UNIV XIAMEN (UYXI-C)
GA 2021B0837X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nickel-zinc-zinc oxide catalyst                comprises (i) adding nickel precursor and zinc                precursor into water, fully stirring and dispersing                to obtain nickel zinc solution; (ii) adding organic                alkaline substance in nickel-zinc solution of step                (i), stirring until the organic alkaline substance                is dissolved, performing hydrothermal reaction,                after finishing hydrothermal reaction, reducing                temperature, washing, drying, grinding, obtaining                powder precursor; and (iii) heating and reducing                the powder precursor obtained in step (ii) under                hydrogen environment, obtaining the catalyst.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing                nickel-zinc-zinc oxide catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: can be directly applied to the                conversion reaction of water gas, converts carbon                monoxide and water into carbon dioxide and                hydrogen; and has high catalytic rate, high carbon                monoxide conversion rate, and less by-product                methane and carbon deposits.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                catalyst, prepared by the preparation method to the                catalysis of water gas shift reaction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The nickel precursor                  in the step (i) is water soluble nickel salt,                  nickel nitrate, nickel nitrate hexahydrate,                  nickel chloride, nickel chloride hexahydrate,                  nickel sulfate and nickel sulfate hexahydrate,                  where the zinc precursor is water-soluble zinc                  salt, zinc nitrate, zinc nitrate hexahydrate,                  zinc chloride, zinc chloride hexahydrate, zinc                  sulfate and zinc sulfate hexahydrate and the mol                  ratio of the nickel precursor to the zinc                  precursor is 0.4-3. The added amount of water                  added in the step (i) is: concentration of nickel                  precursor is 0.1-5 mol/l. The shift reaction of                  the water gas is: conversion of carbon dioxide                  and water to carbon dioxide and hydrogen.                  Preferred Method: The hydrothermal reaction in                  the step (ii) is carried out at 80-150 degrees C                  for 3-8 hours. The hydrogen environment in step                  the (iii) is 1-10 atmospheres and heat reduction                  treatment is carried out at 200-500 degrees C for                  1-4 hours. The catalyst is placed in a reactor,                  heated to 150-350 degrees C, and water gas is                  preheated to 150-250 degrees C and introduced for                  reaction. The inlet pressure of the water gas is                  0.5-10 atmospheric pressure.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic alkaline                  substance in the step (ii) urea, dicyandiamide                  and melamine, where the molar amount of the                  organic alkaline substance is 1.5-6 times the sum                  of the molar amounts of the nickel precursor and                  the zinc precursor in step (i).

PN CN113371704-A
TI Preparing highly conductive and high dispersion graphene comprises e.g. reacting deionized water, grapheme, buffer solution and persulfate, heating and then subjecting to ultrasonic treatment and centrifugal washing.
AU HAN L
   ZHONG Y
AE QINGDAO OLEFINIC CARBON RUIER NEW MATERI (QING-Non-standard)
GA 2021A8157F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing highly conductive and high dispersion graphene comprises (i) adding deionized water and graphene to a round bottom flask equipped with condenser, then magnetically stirring at room temperature for certain time, adding buffer solution to adjusting the pH of the reaction solution, and adding persulfate, dissolving, replacing with ultrapure nitrogen to remove oxygen and carbon dioxide dissolved in deionized water, (ii) heating to make the reaction solution reach the reaction temperature and taking persulfate anion (S2O82-) continuously generates sulfate radicals to oxidizing the edges of the graphene, and (iii) reacting, subjecting the obtained edge graphene oxide to ultrasonic treatment and centrifugal washing in sequence to obtain stable dispersed graphene dispersion liquid.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing highly conductive and high dispersion graphene.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: does not seriously damage the graphite structure of the substrate; and is simple, environmentally friendly and suitable for industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for highly conductive and high dispersion graphene obtained by above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (i), the graphene is a single-layer, double-layer or few-layer graphene with high conductivity. The buffer solution is acetic acid-sodium acetate buffer, sodium carbonate-sodium bicarbonate buffer, disodium hydrogen phosphate-sodium dihydrogen phosphate buffer, citric acid-sodium citrate buffer or ammonia-ammonium chloride buffer, the persulfate is potassium persulfate, sodium persulfate or ammonium persulfate. In step (i), the mass ratio between graphene and deionized water in the reaction solution is 0.1-1%, the pH of the reaction solution is 2-11, and the molar ratio between persulfate and graphene is 1:1-10:1. Preferred Method: In step (ii), the reaction temperature is 40-90 degrees C. In step (iii), the reaction time is 1-24 hours.

PT P
PN CN113201320-A; CN113201320-B
TI Preparing high-purity modified nano-drying agent for dense gas reservoirs by using high-energy ball milling method to prepare nano aluminum carbide powder, purifying it, mixing and modifying nano aluminum carbide powder and nano-sodium ethoxide, mixing it in solvent, and fully mixing.
AU XIONG Y
   SUN Y
   FU X
AE UNIV SOUTHWEST PETROLEUM (USWP-C)
GA 2021926209
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation method of a high-purity modified nano-drying agent for dense gas reservoirs, involves (a) using a high-energy ball milling method to prepare nano aluminum carbide powder under the protection of an inert atmosphere, (b) using supercritical carbon dioxide dissolution to purify the nano aluminum carbide powder obtained in step (a), (c) mixing and modifying the nano aluminum carbide powder and nano-sodium ethoxide at a molar ratio of 1:(3-5), and (d) mixing the mixture obtained in step (c) in a solvent, and fully mixing to obtain the desired nano-drying agent.
   USE - Preparation method of a high-purity modified nano-drying agent for dense gas reservoirs.
   ADVANTAGE - The drying agent obtained by the method has high dispersibility, is not easy to agglomerate, does not need to use toxic solvents such as tetrahydrofuran, and has higher safety.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The nano aluminum carbide powder obtained in step (b) is mixed with nano sodium ethoxide and then subjected to surface modification. The modification process includes: adding polyvinyl alcohol to absolute ethanol, and ultrasonically dispersing at 50 degrees C for 30 minutes; adding nano aluminum carbide powder and nano sodium ethoxide; and ultrasonically dispersing for 2-3 hours, then filtering under suction, and drying to obtain the desired modified nano-drying agent powder. The method includes firstly using magnetic stirring and ethanol as the dispersion carrier to disperse the aluminum carbide powder obtained after ball milling, where the molar ratio of ethanol to aluminum carbide powder is (3-5):1, charging carbon dioxide gas for 5 times the number of moles of ethanol and increasing the pressure to 8 MPa, dissolving aluminum carbide under magnetic stirring at a temperature above 40 degrees C, after leaving still for 1 hour, letting the supercritical carbon dioxide fluid spray into the nitrogen gas environment through the short sections of the nano-sieve mesh to make nano-drying agent mixed particles. Preferred Conditions: After mixing in the step (c), a synergistic agent needs to be added, and the synergistic agent is potassium carbonate. The high-energy ball milling parameters in step (a) are the rotation speed at 300-500 rpm, and the ball milling time of 20-50 hours. The mixing in step (d) adopts ultrasonic dispersion with the ultrasonic dispersion power of 50 W for 2-3 hours. Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of aluminum powder and graphite powder in step (a) is (2-3):1. The mass ratio of the nano-drying agent and polyvinyl alcohol is 1:(0.05-0.1).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (d), the dispersion carrier is ethanol.
