PT P
PN CN113120937-A
TI Utilization method of fluorosilicic acid wastewater involves carbonizing calcium-containing solution to form calcium carbonate, mixing with fluorine-containing wastewater of desiliconization treatment and separating to form calcium fluoride.
AU CAO S
   ZHANG Y
   ZHANG F
AE INST PROCESS ENG CHINESE ACAD SCI (CAGC-C)
GA 202184150U
AB    NOVELTY - Utilization method of fluorosilicic acid wastewater involves (1) mixing calcium-containing raw material with dissolution medium to carry out decomposition reaction to obtain calcium-containing material liquid, separating to obtain calcium-containing solution and insoluble residue, (2) contacting the calcium-containing solution with gas containing carbon dioxide to carry out carbonization conversion reaction to obtain carbonized feed liquid, separating to obtain calcium carbonate solids and carbonized mother liquor, mixing fluorosilicic acid wastewater and the desiliconization reaction medium and subjecting to desiliconization reaction, separating to obtain fluorine-containing wastewater and white carbon black after the desiliconization treatment, and (3) reacting calcium carbonate solids with the fluorine-containing wastewater after desiliconization treatment and additive, and separating to obtain calcium fluoride solids and reaction mother liquor.
   USE - Utilization method of fluorosilicic acid wastewater.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is carried out under mild reaction condition to produce calcium fluoride solids with controllable morphology, particle size and purity of more than 99.9%, and produce white carbon black by-products with fluorine resources recovery rate of more than 90%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Composition: The fluorosilicic acid wastewater comprises 0.05-35 %mass hexafluorosilicic acid. The ratio of total amount of ammonia and ammonium ion in desiliconization reaction medium to amount of fluorine in fluorosilicic acid wastewater is 0.9-1.2:1. The ratio of amount of ammonium ion in dissolution medium to amount of calcium oxide in calcium-containing raw material is 1.6-2.4:1. The ratio of calcium carbonate in the calcium carbonate solids to fluorine in the fluorine-containing wastewater after desiliconization is 0.4-0.75:1. The amount of additive added is 0-20% of the amount of the fluorine in fluorine-containing wastewater Preferred Condition: The step (1) is carried out by performing decomposition reaction at 20-100 degrees C for 0.05-3 hours, separating and mixing washing water obtained from washing the insoluble residue with calcium-containing solution. The step (2) is carried out by performing he carbonization conversion reaction at 25-100 degrees C for 0.1-5 hours, separating and mixing washing water obtained by washing the calcium carbonate solid with carbonized mother liquor, evaporating carbonized mother liquor and returning to decomposition reaction as dissolution medium, performing desiliconization reaction at 5-95 degrees C for 0.5-15 hours, separating and mixing washing water obtained by washing white carbon black and fluorine-containing wastewater after the desiliconization treatment. The step (3) is carried out by reacting at 5-100 degrees C for 1-50 hours, separating, washing calcium fluoride solid and drying, mixing washing water obtained by washing the calcium fluoride solid in step (3) with reaction mother liquor, collecting gas generated by the reaction in step (3), pressurizing and returning to desiliconization reaction for recycling, subjecting reaction mother liquor to evaporation treatment to obtain evaporation condensate and evaporation mother liquor, and returning evaporation condensate to desiliconization reaction as circulating liquid.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The desiliconization reaction medium is one or more of ammonia, ammonium bicarbonate and ammonium carbonate, when if ammonium bicarbonate and/or ammonium carbonate are used as the desiliconization reaction medium, the carbon dioxide gas generated by the reaction is collected, pressurized, and then returned to the carbonization conversion reaction for recycling. The calcium-containing raw material is one or more of industrial lime, lime milk, calcium carbide slag and calcium hydroxide. The dissolution medium is ammonium chloride and/or ammonium nitrate. The additive is one or more of alkali metal hydroxide, alkali metal carbonate and basic ammonium salts, preferably one or more of sodium hydroxide, sodium carbonate, potassium hydroxide, potassium carbonate, ammonia, ammonium carbonate and ammonium bicarbonate.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.)

PN CN113019374-A; CN113019374-B
TI Preparing nickel oxide/zirconium dioxide nano            composite photocatalyst comprises adding sodium            borohydride solution into zirconium oxychloride            octahydrate solution to form white sol, stirring to            generate precipitate, and filtering.
AU GUO C
   GUO F
   WANG Y
AE SHANDONG VOCATIONAL COLLEGE LIGHT IND (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202176863S
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nickel oxide/zirconium dioxide nano                composite photocatalyst comprises adding the sodium                borohydride solution into zirconium (IV)                oxychloride octahydrate solution to form white sol,                stirring to generate precipitate, filtering, after                drying, adding ammonium carbonate solution and                nickel (II) nitrate hexahydrate solution,                ultrasonically dispersing, drying, calcining,                grinding to obtain nickel oxide/zirconium dioxide                nano composite photocatalyst.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing nickel                oxide/zirconium dioxide nano composite                photocatalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The nano composite photocatalyst: has high                catalytic activity and good dispersibility; can                directly degrades toxic and harmful substance into                water and carbon dioxide; reaches the degradation                rate of more than 99.8%, and realizes the                degradation treatment of organic substance in the                organic waste water.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for use                method of nickel oxide/zirconium dioxide nano                composite photocatalyst comprising adding nano                composite photocatalyst into the organic waste                water under the illumination condition, the                degradation reaction occurs, realizing the                degradation treatment of organic substance in the                organic waste water.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The volume ratio of                  sodium borohydride solution to zirconium (IV)                  oxychloride octahydrate solution is 1-1.5:1. The                  concentration of the sodium borohydride solution                  is 0.1-0.2 mol/l. The concentration of zirconium                  (IV) oxychloride octahydrate solution is 0.1-0.2                  mol/l. The volume ratio of ammonium carbonate                  solution, nickel (II) nitrate hexahydrate                  solution and zirconium (IV) oxychloride                  octahydrate solution is 1-5:0.5-1:4-10. The                  concentration of (ammonium carbonate solution is                  0.5-1.0 mol/l. The concentration of nickel (II)                  nitrate hexahydrate is 0.1-0.2 mol/l. Preferred                  Method: In the process, after ultrasonic                  dispersion, using vacuum drying, and carrying out                  vacuum drying at 80-90 degrees C for 8-10 hours.                  The calcining temperature is 350-400 degrees C,                  the calcining time is 2-4 hours. In the process,                  adding ammonium carbonate solution and nickel                  (II) nitrate hexahydrate solution, stirring for                  20-30 minutes, ultrasonically dispersing for                  20-25 minutes, allowing to stand for 2-4 hours,                  drying, calcining, grinding to obtain nickel                  oxide/zirconium dioxide nano composite                  photocatalyst. The ratio of nickel                  oxide/zirconium dioxide nanocomposite                  photocatalyst to organic wastewater is 0.4-0.6:1,                  where the nanocomposite photocatalyst is                  calculated in g, the organic wastewater is                  calculated in L, and the degradation reaction                  time is 1.0-2.5 hours.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) application method of the zinc-MOF material for adsorption of greenhouse gases, which involves mixing the crystals of zinc-molecular organic framework material with methanol or acetone solution, centrifuging and activating at 50-60 degrees C for 10-14 hours and using the activated crystals to adsorb greenhouse gases; and
   (2) application method of zinc-MOF material for preparation of mechanically stressed luminescent material, which involves irradiating the zinc-MOF material crystal at excitation wavelength of 460-490 nm, grinding, and irradiating the crystal powder at excitation wavelength of 500-530 nm.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The zinc salt is one or more of zinc nitrate hexahydrate, zinc chloride, and zinc acetate dihydrate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The preparation involves dissolving 0.2-2.0 mmol zinc salt in solvent to obtain zinc salt solution, dissolving 0.1-1.0 mmol ligand in solvent to obtain ligand solution, mixing the zinc salt solution and the ligand solution, stirring for 30-120 minutes or ultrasonically processing for 20-50 minutes, adding to reactor, reacting at 90-150 degrees C for 24-120 hours, and cooling to room temperature at cooling rate of 0.9-2 degrees C/minute to obtain crystals, washing the crystals with water or ethanol and drying at 50-70 degrees C for 12-24 hours. The ligand is prepared by mixing 1,4-dibromobenzene or 4,4-dibromobiphenyl with 4-pinacol ethyl benzoate or diethyl 4'-bromo-(1,1'-biphenyl)-3,5-dicarboxylate, subjecting to Suzuki coupling reaction under catalysis of palladium catalyst to obtain brominated compound (A), coupling compound (A) and pinacol diboronic acid under catalysis of a palladium catalyst to obtain borate compound (B), subjecting the compound (B) and tetrakis(4-bromobenzene)ethylene to Suzuki coupling reaction under catalysis of palladium catalyst and hydrolyzing. Preferred Components: The solvent comprises N,N-dimethylformamide and deionized water in ratio of 1-20:1. The ligand is one or more of 4'',4''''',4'''''''',4'''''''''''-(ethylene-1,1,2,2-tetrayl)tetrakis((1,1',4',1''-terphenyl)-4-carboxylic acid) of formula (I), 4''',4''''''',4''''''''''',4''''''''''''''-(ethylene-1,1,2,2-tetrayl)tetrakis((1,1':4',1'':4'',1'''-tetraphenyl)-4-carboxylic acid) of formula (II) and 4'',4''''',4'''''''',4'''''''''''-(ethylene-1,1,2,2-tetrayl)tetrakis((1,1':4',1''-terphenyl)-3,5-dicarboxylic acid) of formula (III). The palladium catalyst is tetrakis(triphenylphosphine)palladium catalyst and/or ferrocene palladium chloride.A = group of formula (i);B' = group of formula (ii);andC' = group of formula (iii).Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of zinc salt and the ligand is 2-5:1.

PN CN112760169-A
TI Preparing essential oil of Callicarpa longissima seeds useful in preparation of antibacterial and antiinflammatory medicine, by freeze-drying Callicarpa longissima seeds, pulverizing, sieving, extracting, purifying and collecting product.
AU PAN Z
   YU L
   NING D
   LI H
AE UNIV GUILIN TECHNOLOGY (UYGI-C)
   CHINESE ACAD SCI GUANGXI BOTANY INST (CHSC-Non-standard)
GA 2021541752
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing essential oil of Callicarpa longissima seeds involves (a) taking C.longissima seeds, freeze-drying, pulverizing and sieving to obtain coarse powder and (b) taking the coarse powder, performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, classifying and purifying through separation kettle I and separation kettle II, and collecting the essential oil in the separation kettle II as product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing essential oil of C.longissima seeds which is used in preparation of antibacterial and antiinflammatory medicine in form of solid preparation, semi-solid preparation or liquid preparation (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares essential oil of C.longissima seeds under mild and controllable operating condition with high yield of 4.9-5.24%, good purity and relative content of the active ingredient alpha -bisabolol of 71.6-81%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for use of essential oil of C.longissima seeds prepared by the above-mentioned method, in preparation of antibacterial and antiinflammatory medicine.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method involves (a) freeze-drying the C.longissima seeds at -55 degrees C to -60 degrees C for 24-48 hours, pulverizing, sieving using a sieve of 40 mesh size and obtaining coarse powder with water content of 4-6 wt.%, and (b) performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction at 35-37 degrees C and 12-14 MPa for 1-2 hours using supercritical carbon dioxide as a solvent and 95-100 vol.% ethanol as an entrainer, where the flow ratio of the entrainer to the solvent is 10-15% and the carbon dioxide flow rate is 20-30 l/hour, and classifying and purifying through separation kettle I at 35-37 degrees C for 8-10 MPa and separation kettle II at 31-33 degrees C for 5-7 MPa.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Composition: The medicine comprises the essential oil of C.longissima seeds and carrier. Preferred Components: The carrier is one or more of diluents, absorption enhancers, excipients, surfactants, binders, wetting agents, lubricants and flavoring agents.
   ACTIVITY - Antibacterial; Antiinflammatory.

PN CN112745265-A; CN112745265-B
TI Preparing            3-(difluoromethyl)-1-methyl-1H-pyrazole-4-carboxylic            acid comprises e.g. dissolving N-methyl-3-aminopyrazole            in water and reacting with halogen to obtain            4-halo-1-methyl-1H-pyrazol-3-amine, and reacting.
AU CHEN H
   YU W
AE XUZHOU SHENGYUAN CHEM CO LTD (XUZH-Non-standard)
GA 202153952P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing                3-(difluoromethyl)-1-methyl-1H-pyrazole-4-carboxylic                acid comprises (i) dissolving                N-methyl-3-aminopyrazole in water and reacting with                halogen to obtain                4-halo-1-methyl-1H-pyrazol-3-amine, (ii) reacting                4-halo-1-methyl-1H-pyrazol-3-amine with an aqueous                solution of sodium nitrite to obtain a diazonium                salt, and then reacting with potassium                difluoromethyl trifluoroborate in the presence of                cuprous oxide to obtain                4-halo-3-(difluoromethyl)-1-methyl-1H-pyrazole, and                (iii) exchanging                4-halo-3-(difluoromethyl)-1-methyl-1H-pyrazole with                Grignard reagent, after cooling, reacting with                carbon dioxide, quenching and recrystallizing to                obtain                3-(difluoromethyl)-1-methyl-1H-pyrazole-4-carboxylic                acid.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing                3-(difluoromethyl)-1-methyl-1H-pyrazole-4-carboxylic                acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple to operate; obtains                three-step total yield as high as 64%; reaches the                product purity more than 99.5%; avoids the presence                of isomers in the traditional process; and has                potential process amplification prospects.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the                  halogen is bromine and iodine, and when iodine is                  used, hydrogen peroxide is added to react. In the                  step (i), the molar ratio of the                  N-methyl-3-aminopyrazole and halogen is                  1:0.5-1.05, the elemental iodine is nearly 0.5                  equivalent, and bromine is the nearly equivalent                  reaction. In the step (ii), the molar ratio of                  the added ketone oxide and                  4-halo-1-methyl-1H-pyrazol-3-amine is                  0.05-0.10:1. In the step (ii), the molar ratio of                  the 4-halo-1-methyl-1H-pyrazol-3-amine, potassium                  difluoromethyl trifluoroborate and sodium nitrite                  is 1:0.98-1.3:2.0-2.5. In the step (iii), the                  Grignard reagent is isopropylmagnesium chloride,                  isopropylmagnesium chloride-lithium chloride or                  sec-butylmagnesium chloride-lithium chloride. In                  the step (iii), the reaction with carbon dioxide                  is carried out by introducing carbon dioxide gas                  or a saturated solution of carbon dioxide under                  the condition of a catalytic amount of DBU. In                  the step (iii), the molar ratio of                  4-halo-3-(difluoromethyl)-1-methyl-1H-pyrazole,                  Grignard reagent and carbon dioxide is                  1:1.01-1.15:1-2.5. In the step (iii), mixture of                  alcohol solvent and water is used for                  crystallization. In the step (iii), 40% ethanol                  aqueous solution is used for the                  recrystallization.

TI Producing calcium hydroxide from eggshell used in effluent treatment system of shell-generating egg farm itself, involves adding sample of dried and crushed eggshells to solution of hydrochloric acid under stirring on magnetic stirrer.
AU AZEVEDO MELO L C
   RIBEIRO I C A
   GUIMARAES GUILHERME L R
   TEODORO J C
AE UNIV FEDERAL LAVRAS (UYLA-Non-standard)
   FUNDACAO AMPARO A PESQUISA MINAS GERAIS (AMPA-Non-standard)
GA 202145845G
AB    NOVELTY - Producing calcium hydroxide from eggshell involves adding a sample of dried and crushed eggshells to a solution of hydrochloric acid (HCl) and keeping under stirring on a magnetic stirrer, the system must be kept under stirring, until all the carbonate is converted to carbon dioxide (CO2), water (H2O), and calcium chloride (CaCl2), after the system comes into equilibrium, adding a concentrated solution of sodium hydroxide (NaOH), until all the calcium chloride present in the solution is converted to calcium hydroxide.
   USE - Method for producing calcium hydroxide from eggshell used in the effluent treatment system of the shell-generating egg farm itself (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is a unique material in national and international literature, of high added value, produced from eggshells, without necessarily the use of specialized labor, expensive chemical reagents and sophisticated equipment, and also provides economic advantages with the extension of the useful life of the product, besides facilities in the transportation and conservation of the food.

PN CN112535206-A
TI Preserving Tricholoma matsutake by modified atmosphere dormancy, involves pre-processing Tricholoma matsutake, packaging by packaging material, obtaining packing material, sealing by gas adjusting film, and storing at low temperature.
AU XIAO K
   LIN M
   WU Y
AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UYSC-C)
   GUANGDONG HUAKAI MINGXIN TECHNOLOGY CO (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021310902
AB    NOVELTY - Preserving Tricholoma matsutake by modified atmosphere dormancy involves pre-processing Tricholoma matsutake, packaging by packaging material, obtaining packing material, sealing surface of package by amidated graphene oxide (AGO)/sulfonated poly(ether-ether ketone) (SPEEK) gas adjusting film, storing at 0-8 degrees C at low temperature, where AGO/SPEEK gas adjusting film comprises functionalized graphene oxide/sulfonated polyether ether ketone gas adjusting film which comprises functionalized graphene oxide (GO) and SPEEK reaction to obtain film, where functionalized graphene oxide is obtained by ethylene diamine modified graphene oxide powder.
   USE - Method for preserving Tricholoma matsutake by modified atmosphere dormancy.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is green and safe, automatically reduces oxygen concentration and improves carbon dioxide concentration, reduces breathing time of Tricholoma matsutake, promotes Tricholoma matsutake to reach sleep state in short time, and at same time, shelf life of method is prolonged to 15 days, where Tricholoma matsutake is fresh, pulp is full, color is bright, is free from microorganism spoilage phenomenon.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method for preparing AGO/SPEEK atmosphere-modified membrane involves dissolving SPEEK in organic solvent to obtain 1 wt.% SPEEK solution; dispersing functionalized GO in SPEEK solution, where concentration of functionalized GO in organic solvent is 0.1-1 wt.%; reacting at 55-65 degrees C for 24-36 hours to obtain casting solution, forming film, and drying. Preferred Components: The functionalized GO is obtained from GO powder modified by ethylenediamine with dosage of ethylenediamine and graphene oxide is 250-400 mL:200mg; reacting ethylenediamine and graphene oxide at 20-40 degrees C for 24-48 hours under protective atmosphere, diluting, filtering, washing, and drying, where reaction is carried out in organic solvent.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The packaging material is polypropylene (PP); the pretreatment refers to immersing matsutake mushroom with food grade hypochlorous acid, rinsing with water, and wiping water on surface of matsutake mushroom. The gas-phase sustained-release bactericide is prepared from natural volatile antibacterial materials. The natural volatile antibacterial material is eugenol or cinnamaldehyde.

PN CN112441605-A; CN112441605-B
TI Preparing vermicular precipitated calcium carbonate comprises subjecting quicklime to digestion reaction to obtain the lime milk and modulating to obtain calcium hydroxide suspension, adding magnesium in the calcium hydroxide suspension.
AU LI R
   ZHU Y
   WEN Q
   YANG A
   LIAO H
   MA J
AE ANHUI XUANCHENG HUANA NEW MATERIAL TECHN (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 2021244189
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing vermicular precipitated calcium carbonate comprises (1) subjecting quicklime to digestion reaction to obtain the lime milk and modulating specific gravity to 1.045-1.065 to obtain calcium hydroxide suspension, (2) adding magnesium in the calcium hydroxide suspension, salt of strontium or barium element or compound as crystal control agent, stirring and mixing uniformly, adding into the carbonization tower, introducing mixed gas containing carbon dioxide for carbonation reaction, stop carbonation until the pH is reduced to 9 to obtain precipitated calcium carbonate suspension, (3) slowly adding phosphoric acid solution to the precipitated calcium carbonate suspension, until the pH is reduced to 7, stop the reaction, allowing to stand for 10-30 minutes, filtering the suspension according to the common method, drying, crushing, grading to obtain the product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing vermicular precipitated calcium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The prepared vermicular precipitated calcium carbonate has high purity, high whiteness and high specific surface area.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The crystal form control agent is magnesium chloride, magnesium oxide, magnesium hydroxide, calcium magnesium carbonate, strontium chloride, strontium oxide, strontium nitrate, strontium cyanide, barium chloride, barium oxide, barium nitrate and barium acetate. The dosage of the crystal form control agent is 0.1-0.8 mol/15kg calcium hydroxide suspension and the concentration of water is 1-8 mol/l. The crystal form control agent is composed of 0.1 mol magnesium chloride, 0.2 mol strontium chloride, 0.4 mol magnesium bicarbonate, 0.1 mol barium chloride, 0.5 mol strontium nitrate and 0.3 mol strontium cyanide. The mass concentration of the phosphoric acid is 5%. Preferred Method: The adding speed of the phosphoric acid is 200-300 ml/minutes. The carbonization temperature of the calcium hydroxide suspension in the carbonation reaction process is 20-24 degrees C, the concentration of the carbon dioxide mixed gas is 30-33%; the air inlet flow rate is 2.0-2.4 m3/h, the stirring frequency is 20 Hz.

   USE - The heteroligand iron complex catalyst is useful for producing cyclic carbonate (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The heteroligand iron complex catalyst produces value-added carbon compounds.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Heteroligand iron complex catalyst comprises bipyridine compounds of formulae ((Fe(BIP-R)(dhbpy)X)X) (I), ((Fe(tpy)(bpy)X)X) (II) and ((Fe(tpy)(dhbpy)X)) (III), and cocatalyst including tetrabutylammonium bromide (TBAB), tetrabutylammonium iodide (TBAI), tetraphenylphosphonium iodide (TPPI) and 1,5,7-triazabicyclo(4,4,0)dec-5-ene (TBD).
   BIP = disubstituted-2,6-bisiminopyridine;
   tpy = terpyridine;
   bpy = bipyridine;
   dhbpy = dihydroxybipyridine;
   R = ethanol, propanol, 2-propanol or propyl-1H-imidazole; and
   X = Cl, Br or I.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for producing cyclic carbonate having 3-4C atoms, comprises performing cyclization addition reaction of mono- or di-substituent oxide and carbon dioxide under the hetero-ligand iron complex catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mono-substituent oxide is styrene oxide, p-chloro styrene oxide, p-bromo styrene oxide, alkyl or alkylene oxide having 1-10C atoms, glycidol, phenyl glycidyl ether, allyl glycidyl ether, methacrylate oxide and chloromethyl oxide. The disubstituted oxide group is cyclopentene oxide or cyclohexene oxide.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN111732986-A
TI Biogas purification method for treating kitchen waste involves fermenting kitchen waste in anaerobic tank, filtering generated biogas, boosting, absorbing hydrogen sulfide, condensing, separating biogas, and reducing water content.
AU CAO S
   TIAN C
   HAN S
   CHEN G
   YANG Y
   LI G
AE JINAN SHANYUAN ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (JINA-Non-standard)
GA 202099259L
AB    NOVELTY - Biogas purification method for treating kitchen waste involves subjecting kitchen waste to anaerobic fermentation in an anaerobic tank, storing generated biogas in a double-membrane gas tank, introducing the biogas into a bag filter, filtering to control the particle size and content of dust in the biogas based on the air intake requirements of the generator set, boosting the filtered biogas by Roots blower, then introducing into a desulfurization device provided with a sprayer, spraying absorbent through the sprayer, absorbing hydrogen sulfide in the biogas, introducing resultant biogas into a heat exchanger for condensation and dehydration, controlling dew point temperature of the biogas at the rear end of the heat exchanger, condensing and precipitating the internal saturated water vapor, defogging and separating the biogas using a demister, reducing the water content of the biogas, and introducing qualified biogas to cyclic operation.
   USE - Biogas purification method for treating kitchen waste.
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively, efficiently and rapidly absorbs hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide using N-methyldiethanolamine or ethanolamine as absorbent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: The double-membrane gas tank is fixed on the top of the anaerobic tank by steel rails. The main component of the double-membrane gas tank is made of polyester material, and includes an outer membrane, an inner membrane, a bottom membrane and an auxiliary equipment. The filter bag in the bag filter is made of woven filter cloth or nonwoven felt. The demister is a fiber demister. The filter material of the fiber demister is polytetrafluoroethylene fiber felt used to remove small droplets contained in the reaction product. Preferred Process: When the dust-containing biogas is introduced into the bag filter, the dust with large particles and high specific gravity settles down due to gravity and falls into an ash hopper. The biogas containing fine dust is blocked when passing through the filter bag. The absorbent can absorb carbon dioxide while absorbing hydrogen sulfide. Hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide concentration detectors are installed in the desulfurization device. When the concentration of the hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide gases is detected to increase, absorption saturation is obtained. When the absorption liquid is saturated, the saturated absorption liquid is transported to the saturated liquid storage tank through a transfer pump, and fresh absorbent is injected through the sprayer. When the absorbent after absorbing hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide is not saturated, then the absorbent is pumped into the sprayer through a circulating pump to absorb hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide. Preferred Composition: The dust concentration of the outlet biogas of the bag filter is 10 mg/m3 or less. Preferred Condition: The dew point temperature of the biogas at the rear end of the heat exchanger is controlled at 15-20 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The absorbent is N-methyldiethanolamine or ethanolamine. The total sulfur content in the biogas is reduced to less than 200 mg/m3, after the absorbent is absorbed.

TI Isolating and primary culture of zebrafish            intestinal mucosal epithelial cells, by placing            zebrafish in water containing antibiotics, adding            D-Hanks solution, centrifuging, adding primary cell            culture medium, and culturing medium.
AU LIU Y
   LIU W

   FANG C

GA 2020953551
AB    NOVELTY - Method for isolating and primary culture of                zebrafish intestinal mucosal epithelial cells,                involves selecting healthy zebrafish, placing                zebrafish in water containing antibiotics for 12-24                hours fasting, obtaining sample of the zebrafish's                intestine and removing surface attachments, cutting                the intestinal sample, washing with D-Hank's                solution containing antibiotics for 2-3 times,                adding D-Hank's solution containing collagenase,                shaking and digesting in water bath of                27-29&#176; C for 15-20 minutes, adding stop                solution to stop digestion, centrifuging,                discarding supernatant, adding Dulbecco's modified                Eagle's medium (DMEM) medium to obtain cell                suspension, centrifuging cell suspension,                discarding the supernatant, adding primary cell                culture medium to resuspend the cells, and                performing primary culture at 23-29&#176; C and                6-12% carbon dioxide concentration.
   USE - The method is useful for isolating and primary                culture of zebrafish intestinal mucosal epithelial                cells (claimed), and as fish cell line for                genetics, neurobiology, development biology,                immunology and human disease model, and screening                drug.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides high purity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The antibiotics                  include penicillin and gentamicin, where the                  concentration of penicillin is 80000 U/l and the                  concentration of the gentamicin is 80000 U/l.                  Preferred Method: The method involves obtaining                  zebrafish intestinal sample, and removing surface                  attachments, where the intestinal sample is the                  foregut of the zebrafish intestine. The method                  involves cutting removed intestinal sample into                  pieces, washing with D-Hank's solution containing                  antibiotics for 2-3 times, adding Dank's solution                  containing collagenase and shake in a water bath                  of 27-29&#176; C for 15-20 minutes, adding                  stop solution to terminate the digestion,                  centrifuging, discarding the supernatant, adding                  DMEM medium to make cell suspension, where the                  D-Hank's solution comprises collagenase, the                  collagenase includes collagenase I and                  collagenase IV, the concentration of the                  collagenase I is 0.15-0.25 g/l, and the                  concentration of collagen enzyme IV is 0.15-0.25                  g/l. The method involves cutting removed                  intestinal sample into pieces, washing with                  D-Hank's solution containing antibiotics for 2-3                  times, adding Dank's solution containing                  collagenase in a water bath at 27-29&#176; C,                  shaking for 15-20 minutes, adding stop solution                  to terminate the digestion, centrifuging,                  discarding the supernatant, and adding DMEM                  medium to make cell suspension, where every 10 ml                  stop solution comprises 0.5 ml fetal bovine serum                  (FBS) and 9.5 mL DMEM medium. The method involves                  centrifuging cell suspension, discarding the                  supernatant, adding primary cell culture medium                  to resuspend the cells, culturing primary culture                  at 23-29&#176; C and 6-12% carbon dioxide                  concentration, where the primary cell culture                  medium includes basal medium, FBS, bFGF, EGF,                  &#946; -mercaptoethanol, penicillin and                  gentamicin, the base medium includes DMEM-low                  sugar medium, DMEM-high sugar medium and M199                  medium, and in the primary cell culture medium,                  the volume fraction of the FBS is 10-20%. In the                  primary cell culture medium, the base medium is                  M199 medium, and the volume fraction of FBS is                  15%, the concentration of bFGF is 20 ng/ml, the                  concentration of EGF is 20 ng/ml, the                  concentration of &#946; -mercaptoethanol is                  0.5 mmol/l, the concentration of the penicillin                  is 80000 U/l, and the concentration of the                  gentamicin is 80000 U/l. The method involves                  centrifuging the cell suspension, discarding the                  supernatant, and adding primary cell culture                  medium to resuspend the cells. The centrifugation                  rate of the centrifugation is 350-500 revolutions                  per minute (rpm), and the centrifugation time is                  4-8 minutes.

PT P
PN WO2020130735-A1; KR2020078389-A; CA3121629-A1; CN113056330-A; EP3900830-A1; US2022023850-A1; KR2404440-B1; JP2022509661-W; JP7128965-B2
TI Regeneration of waste organozinc catalyst for            producing polyalkylene carbonate resin, involves mixing            waste organozinc catalyst with dicarboxylic acid,            stirring mixture with zinc compound, and alternately            repeating steps.
AU KIM S K
   KIM S
   JIN C
AE LG CHEM LTD (GLDS-C)
   LG CHEM LTD (GLDS-C)
   LG CHEM LTD (GLDS-C)
GA 2020571972
AB    NOVELTY - Method for regeneration of waste organozinc                catalyst, involves (a) mixing waste organozinc                catalyst with dicarboxylic acid and stirring to                produce a mixture, and (b) mixing and stirring the                mixture with a zinc compound, where the                dicarboxylic acid and zinc compounds are solid                phases, and alternately repeating steps (a) and (b)                greater than or equal to 2 times,                respectively.
   USE - The method is useful for regeneration of waste                organozinc catalyst for producing polyalkylene                carbonate resin (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method regenerates an organozinc catalyst                by a simple and cost-effective method, eliminates                the use of a separate solvent when the organozinc                catalyst is regenerated, reduces the cost of the                regeneration process to increase the economic                efficiency and the advantage of no additional                solvent treatment, and restores the activity of the                waste organozinc catalyst to a high level, so that                the organozinc catalyst is easily reused.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for producing polyalkylene carbonate resin,                involving polymerizing a monomer comprising epoxide                and carbon dioxide in the presence of a regenerated                organozinc catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The zinc compound                  comprises zinc oxide, zinc nitrate, zinc acetate,                  zinc hydroxide, zinc sulfate and/or zinc                  chlorate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (a), the zinc ion                  on the surface of the waste organozinc catalyst                  and the oxygen atom derived from the dicarboxylic                  acid mixed in step (a) cause a coordination bond.                  In step (b), the oxygen atom derived from the                  dicarboxylic acid mixed in step (a) and the zinc                  ion of the zinc compound mixed in step (b) cause                  a coordination bond. The steps (a) and (b) are                  each performed independently using a stirring                  mixer, a V-type mixer, a W-type mixer, a ribbon                  mixer, a drum mixer or a ball mill. Preferred                  Components: The dicarboxylic acid comprises                  malonic acid, succinic acid, glutaric acid,                  adipic acid, pimelic acid, terephthalic acid,                  isophthalic acid, homophthalic acid and/or phenyl                  glutaric acid. Preferred Conditions: The steps                  (a) and (b) are each independently stirred for                  3-20 minutes. The steps (a) and (b) are                  alternately repeated 3-5 times, respectively. The                  dicarboxylic acid content of step (a) is 0.5-20                  pts. wt. based on 100 pts. wt. of the waste                  organozinc catalyst. The zinc compound content in                  step (b) is 0.5-20 pts. wt. based on 100 pts. wt.                  of the waste organozinc catalyst. The molar ratio                  of the dicarboxylic acid and the zinc compound is                  (1:0.5)-(1:1.5). The catalyst regenerated by the                  above method has a catalytic activity of greater                  than or equal to 50% of the catalytic activity                  before regeneration.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN WO2020127098-A1; IN202147031561-A; CN113166284-A; KR2021104784-A; EP3898709-A1; US2021332167-A1; BR112021010757-A2; RU2770427-C1; JP2022515052-W
TI Preparing ethylene polymer involves forming an            ethylene polymer in particle form in a gas-phase            polymerization unit which comprises one gas-phase            polymerization reactor by homopolymerizing ethylene or            copolymerizing ethylene and alkenes.
AU PENZO G
   MEIER G
   SCHUELLER U
   BAITA P
   MEI G
   DAMM E
AE BASELL POLYOLEFINE GMBH (BASE-C)
   BASELL POLYOLEFINE GMBH (BASE-C)
   BASELL POLYOLEFINE GMBH (BASE-C)
GA 202057266G
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing ethylene polymer involves forming an                ethylene polymer in particle form in a gas-phase                polymerization unit which comprises one gas-phase                polymerization reactor by homopolymerizing ethylene                or copolymerizing ethylene and 4-12C alkenes at                temperatures from 20 to 200 degrees C and pressures                of from 0.5 to 10 mega pascal in a reaction gas and                using propane as polymerization diluent in the                presence of a pre-activated polymerization                catalyst. The purified propane feed stream                comprises 99 mol.% propane and not more than 100                parts per million-mol propylene which is fed to the                gas-phase polymerization unit. The purification is                carried out by passing a hydrocarbon stream through                hydrocarbon purification units which reduce at                least the concentration of propylene, acetylene,                cartoon monoxide, carbon dioxide, oxygen and water                contained in the hydrocarbon stream.
   USE - Method for preparing ethylene polymer.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare ethylene polymer                that increases the catalyst feed and catalyst                productivity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The purified propane feed                  stream fed to the gas-phase polymerization unit                  comprises not more than 0.03 parts per                  million-mol carbon monoxide, not more than 0.4                  parts per million-mol carbon dioxide, not more                  than 2.0 parts per million-mol oxygen, not more                  than 3.0 parts per million-mol acetylene, and not                  more than 2.0 parts per million-mol water. The                  pre-activation of the polymerization catalyst is                  carried out by contacting, in liquid propane as                  diluent, a solid catalyst component with an or-                  ganometallic compound and optionally an electron                  donor compound to form a pre-activated                  polymerization catalyst. The propane used as                  liquid diluent for the pre-activation of the                  polymerization catalyst is taken from the                  purified propane feed stream. The hydrocarbon                  purification units are used for preparing the                  purified propane stream is a catalytic                  hydrogenation unit. Preferred Components: The                  catalytic hydrogenation unit comprises a                  hydrogenation catalyst which is a platinum- or                  palladium-based composition on an alumina                  support. The ethylene polymer is a multimodal                  ethylene polymer and the gas-phase polymerization                  unit comprises a reactor cascade of two or more                  gas-phase polymerization reactors which comprises                  a first polymerization reactor, into which the                  pre-activated polymerization catalyst is fed, and                  one or more subsequent polymerization reactors,                  which receive the polymerization catalyst in                  active form incorporated in ethylene polymer                  particles formed in the preceding reactors. The                  first reactor of the reactor cascade is a                  fluidized-bed reactor. The fluidized-bed reactor                  comprises a polymer discharge pipe, which                  contains a bed of ethylene polymer particles                  which moves from top to bottom of the discharge                  pipe, and propane is introduced into the                  discharge pipe in an amount that an upward stream                  of the propane is induced in the bed of ethylene                  polymer particles above the propane introduction                  point. The subsequent polymerization reactors is                  a multizone circulating reactor in which one                  polymerization zone is a riser, in which growing                  polyolefin particles flow upwards under fast                  fluidization or transport conditions, and another                  polymerization zone is a downcomer, in which the                  growing polyolefin particles flow downward in a                  densified form.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Numerical simulation of liquid rocket reignition reaction calculation involves establishing e.g. three-dimensional geometric model of gas impact deflector during launch phase of liquid carrier rocket, and performing parallel calculations.
AU ZHOU Z
   YUE G
AE UNIV NANJING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UNSC-C)
GA 2020501540
AB    NOVELTY - Numerical simulation of liquid rocket reignition reaction calculation involves (1) establishing three-dimensional geometric model of gas impact deflector during launch phase of liquid carrier rocket, (2) using grid division scheme to divide liquid rocket impact deflector into a structural grid, (3) establishing rocket gas shock model based on three-dimensional compressible Reynolds average Navier-Stokes equation and realizable k- epsilon double equation turbulence model, (4) establishing rocket gas chemical kinetics model based on the finite-rate chemical kinetics model for numerical simulation of the rocket gas reburning reaction, (5) performing parallel calculations on liquid impingement gas flow field of liquid carrier rocket and obtaining Mach number cloud diagram, temperature cloud diagram, carbon monoxide mole fraction cloud diagram, carbon dioxide mole fraction cloud diagram, hydrogen mole fraction cloud diagram and water mole fraction cloud diagram.
   USE - Numerical simulation of liquid rocket reignition reaction calculation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides results with high accuracy, and has low computational cost.

PN WO2020068539-A1
TI Reducing alkylene carbonate content in a crude polyether polycarbonate polyol effluent involves mixing the crude polyether polycarbonate polyol effluent with an aqueous wash solution comprising an inorganic salt.
AU BIGI M A
   NIKBIN N
AE DOW GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES LLC (DOWC-C)
GA 2020251570
AB    NOVELTY - Reducing alkylene carbonate content in a crude polyether polycarbonate polyol effluent, which comprises the alkylene carbonate and polyether polycarbonate polyol, which is the polymerized reaction product of alkylene oxide and carbon dioxide in the presence of a catalyst, involves mixing the crude polyether polycarbonate polyol effluent with an aqueous wash solution comprising an inorganic salt, where the alkylene carbonate mixes with the aqueous wash solution. The mixture of crude polyether polycarbonate polyol effluent and aqueous wash solution is allowed to separate into a first aqueous phase comprising alkylene carbonate. A first organic phase comprising the polyether polycarbonate polyol and an amount of alkylene carbonate less than the amount of alkylene carbonate in the crude polyether polycarbonate polyol effluent.
   USE - Method for reducing alkylene carbonate content in a crude polyether polycarbonate polyol effluent.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to reduced alkylene carbonate content in a crude polyether polycarbonate polyol effluent, where enhances phase separation between the organic phase and the aqueous phase dissolving the alkylene carbonate, hence reducing the alkylene carbonate byproduct in the organic phase.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: The method for reducing alkylene carbonate content in a crude polyether polycarbonate polyol effluent further involves removing the first aqueous phase and mixing a second aqueous wash solution with the first organic phase. The mixing comprises mixing at a temperature from 25-150 degrees C. The inorganic salt is present in an amount greater than or equal to 5 wt.% based on a total weight of the aqueous wash solution.The inorganic salt is present in the aqueous wash solution in an amount of from 5-20 wt.% based on a total weight of the aqueous wash solution. The pH of the aqueous wash solution is from 4-11. The mixing comprises mixing the crude polyether polycarbonate polyol effluent with the aqueous wash solution at a weight ratio (crude polyether polycarbonate polyol effluent and aqueous wash solution) of from 0.1-10. The process further comprising recovering the alkylene carbonate from the aqueous layer. Preferred Components: The inorganic salt comprises sodium chloride (NaCl), potassium chloride (KCl), sodium bicarbonate (NaHCO3), potassium bicarbonate (KHCO3), magnesium sulfate (MgSO4), monosodium phosphate (NaH2PO4) or trisodium citrate.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN110702586-A; CN110702586-B
TI Rock penetrant rate measuring method, involves calculating penetrant rate of rock core by using early measured upstream pressure data, and correcting errors caused by coupling of gas pressure changes and sliding effects in calculation.
AU WANG M
   WANG Y
AE UNIV TSINGHUA (UYQI-C)
GA 202008316E
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves applying an unstable pressure pulse to a rock core with nitrogen, carbon dioxide, helium or argon. Changes in pressure are recorded across core over time. A penetrant rate of the rock core is calculated by using early measured upstream pressure data. Errors caused by coupling of gas pressure changes and sliding effects in the calculation are corrected. The rock core sample is put in a sample chamber. Two ends of a sample cavity are connected with an upstream gas chamber and a downstream gas chamber. An upper surface of the rock core sample is connected to the upstream gas chamber.
   USE - Rock penetrant rate measuring method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables realizing high index precision and efficient and accurate non-stable penetrant rate measuring process and reducing measuring time.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a rock penetrant rate measuring device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a front view of a rock penetrant rate measuring device.

PN CN110668439-A
TI Preparing coconut shell activated carbon for extracting gold, by activating coconut shell carbonization material using SLEP furnace, adding to externally-heated converter for activating, post-processing and refining.
AU LIU S
AE HAINAN XINGGUANG ACTIVATED CARBON CO LTD (HAIN-Non-standard)
GA 202006198B
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing coconut shell activated carbon involves (i) selecting coconut shell carbonization material, where the coconut shell carbonization material has a fixed carbon content greater than 70%, (ii) activating the coconut shell carbonization material using SLEP furnace, using water vapor and flue gas as activator and activating at 850-900 degrees C to obtain primary coconut shell activated carbon, (iii) adding the primary coconut shell activated carbon to an externally-heated converter for activating, using water vapor and canned carbon dioxide as activator, and activating at 850-900?C to obtain secondary activated coconut shell activated carbon, and (iv) post-processing the secondary activated coconut shell activated carbon and refining to obtain the product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing coconut shell activated carbon for extracting gold.
   ADVANTAGE - The coconut shell activated carbon has high specific surface area, adsorbs high amount of gold, ensures high strength, reduces abrasion, reduces black water production, and is economical.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: In step (i), the particle size of the coconut shell carbonization material is 3-8 mesh, the bulk specific gravity is greater than 0.55 g/ml, the volatile content is 12-20% and the moisture is less than 10%. Preferred Conditions: In step (ii), the pressure of the water vapor is 0.2 plus minus 0.02 MPa, and the flow rate is 900 plus minus 50 kg/h, the amount of flue gas used is a natural recovery amount, the furnace pressure of the SLEP furnace is a slight positive pressure of 20 plus minus 5 Pa, and the activation time of the primary activation is 12 plus minus 0.5 hours. In step (iii), the pressure of the water vapor is 0.2 plus minus 0.02 MPa, the flow rate is 400 plus minus 50 kg/h, the amount of carbon dioxide introduced is one half the amount of water vapor, and the furnace pressure of the externally-heated converter is slightly positive pressure of 10 plus minus 5 Pa, and the activation time of the secondary activation is 8 plus minus 0.5 hours. Preferred Method: The post-processing involves crushing, screening, dust removal, water washing, grinding and performing fluidized bed drying.

TI Method for controlling temperature distribution in fixed bed reactor during regeneration of catalyst, involves circulating oxygen-containing gas with respect to catalyst, and removing carbonaceous material deposited on catalyst.
AU NAKASHIGE M
   SAWANO M
   KURIYA M
AE TOSOH CORP (TOYJ-C)
GA 201984686M
AB    NOVELTY - Method for controlling temperature distribution in fixed bed reactor involves controlling temperature distribution in a fixed bed reactor during regeneration of a catalyst by circulating an oxygen-containing gas with respect to the catalyst in the fixed bed reactor, combustion-removing carbonaceous material deposited on the catalyst, calculating the amount and the distribution of carbonaceous material adhering to the catalyst due to the reaction of multi-component raw material in advance, estimating the residual amount of carbonaceous material adhering to the catalyst by simulation, and adjusting the oxygen concentration, the regeneration gas temperature, and the regeneration gas flow rate, such that the carbon dioxide concentration at the outlet of the reactor becomes lower than a predetermined amount.
   USE - Method for controlling temperature distribution in fixed bed reactor during regeneration of catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient control of temperature distribution in fixed bed reactor with suppressed temperature rise in the fixed bed reactor and deactivation of catalyst, without affecting the regeneration conditions, by fast process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Component: The fixed bed reactor has a jacket structure.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: Method for controlling temperature distribution in fixed bed reactor further involves calculating the amount and the distribution of carbonaceous material adhering to the catalyst experimentally, estimating the residual amount of carbonaceous material adhering to the catalyst by simulation based on the measurement results of the carbon dioxide concentration at the reactor outlet after the start of regeneration or the temperature inside the reactor and at the outlet of the reactor, controlling the temperature distribution in the fixed bed reactor by increasing the oxygen concentration in the regeneration gas and the regeneration gas temperature in stepwise, and setting the operating conditions of the fixed bed reactor based on the results of a simulation program which causes the controller to analyze the combustion behavior in the fixed bed reactor and the temperature behavior of a portion(s) of the reactor.

The signal transmitted to the ancillary control unit comprises patient data i.e. displayed on the display. The control unit is configured to receive a signal from at least one or more sensing units, where the signal comprises patient analyte data and transmit instructions to at least one or more sensing units to measure analytes data on a fixed or adjustable schedule. The sensing units are removable and replaceable. The sensor further comprises a treatment system for providing medication to the patient, where the treatment system is housed within the housing or located remotely from a site where the sensor is implanted. The control unit is configured to receive a signal from at least one or more sensing units, where the signal comprises patient analyte data mid transmit instructions to the treatment system to deliver medication to the patient based on the patient analyte data included in at least one or more sensing units signal. The energy source is chosen from one or more replaceable batteries or a self generating system which uses a process such as motion or vascular flow, body heat, or chemical reaction. The sensor is implantable in a pelvic region of a patient.

TI Oil binder useful as a cleaning agent or cleaning agent additive comprises open-cell polymer foam with specific pore sizes.
AU OBERHOFFER R
   MUELLER A
AE SUMTEQ GMBH (SUMT-Non-standard)
   SUMTEQ GMBH (SUMT-Non-standard)
GA 201971912B
AB    NOVELTY - Oil binder comprises an open-cell polymer foam with pore sizes of 0.01-10 mu m, preferably 0.02-5 mu m, preferably 0.03-2 mu m.
   USE - The oil binder is useful as a cleaning agent or cleaning agent additive (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The oil binder provides nanoporous polymer foam with high thermal insulation, ideally pore size, homogeneity of the pores, and uniform the foam density.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the oil binder, comprising (a) addition of crosslinker to monomer or a mixture of monomers to obtain a monomer mixture, (b) polymerizing the monomer mixture, (c) drying the obtained polymer, (d) swelling the polymer with a swelling agent, (e) the swollen polymer is placed in carbon dioxide atmosphere with a pressure of 50-500 bar and a temperature of 0-120 degrees C, (f) lowering the pressure after a waiting time of 10-180 minutes to the atmospheric ambient pressure, where the polymer foam is obtained, and (g) obtaining resulting polymer foam.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The binder becomes translucent by absorbing oil. The oil binder is contacted with oil. The oil is expelled from the oil binder.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The foam is a polymer foam, preferably partially crosslinked polymer foam. The density of the foam is 30-300 kg/m3. The binder has specific surface area of 3-500 m2/g, preferably 20-400 m2/g; and bulk density of 20-250 kg/m3, preferably 30-150 kg/m3.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN110128693-A; CN110128693-B
TI Preparing polyolefine foamed material involves weighing polyolefin, inorganic flame retardant, synergist, after mixing, adding molten extruding in extruder to obtain premix, and adding premix to single screw extruder.
AU PANG Y
   ZHENG W
   HUANG P
   LIU W
   WU M
AE NINGBO INST MATERIALS TECHNOLOGY & ENG C (CANM-C)
GA 201972740B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing polyolefine foamed material involves weighing 80-120 pts. wt. polyolefin, 40-220 pts. wt. inorganic flame retardant, 0-20 pts. wt. synergist, after mixing, adding molten extruding in the extruder to obtain premix, adding the premix to the single screw extruder, extruding and foaming to obtain polyolefin foaming material, where the single screw extruder includes a upper stage screw rod and the lower stage screw, where the upper stage screw temperature is 140-230 degrees C, the temperature of the lower stage screw is 90-180 degrees C, the extrusion temperature is 90-175 degrees C, and the upper stage screw injecting supercritical carbon dioxide at 1/5-1/3.
   USE - Method for preparing polyolefine foamed material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare polyolefine foamed material, which improves the dispersing effect of the flame retardant, polyolefin foaming material cellular structure to obtain uniform, high foaming multiplying power, the degree of orientation is controllable, it has excellent flame retardant property and mechanical property.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The polyolefin is one or nore of polyethylene, polypropylene, ethylene propylene rubber, and ethylene octylene copolymer. The inorganic flame retardant is one or more of magnesium hydrate, aluminum hydroxide, expanded graphite, red phosphorus, zinc oxide, titanium oxide, aluminum oxide, silicon dioxide, zinc borate, zinc stannate, zinc silicate, ammonium salt, phosphate, and hypophosphite. The synergist is one or more of antimony trioxide, montmorillonite, calcium carbonate, potassium carbonate, magnesium carbonate, dolomite, magnesite, brucite, seaweed stone, and kaolin. Preferred Conditions: The total mass of the inorganic flame retardant and the synergist accounts for 40-70 wt.% of the mass of the premix. The amount of supercritical carbon dioxide used is from 0.5-12 wt.% of the mass of the premix. The injection pressure of the supercritical carbon dioxide is 7-25 mega pasclas. The temperature of the lower step screw is lower than the temperature of the upper screw, and the difference is T, 20 degrees C less than or equal to T less than or equal to 80 degrees C. The drawing device has a draw ratio of 0-80 and a drawing speed of 0.1-3.0m/s. The cell density of the polyolefin foaming material is 104-1012cell/cm3, and the expansion ratio is 1.05-25.

PN CN110124035-A; CN110124035-B
TI Gold nano-rod/calcium carbonate nano-particle material useful for preparing therapeutic agent and imaging agent comprises polymer modified gold nano-rods and polymer modified calcium carbonate nano-particles.
AU TIAN H
   XU C
   WANG Y
   CHEN J
   GUO Z
   LIN L
   CHEN X
AE CHANGCHUN APPLIED CHEM INST CHINESE ACAD (CAAC-C)
GA 201972842E
AB    NOVELTY - Gold nanorod/calcium carbonate nano-particle material comprises polymer modified gold nan-orods and polymer modified calcium carbonate nanoparticles, where the polymers are polyethyleneimine and polyethylene glycol.
   USE - The nano-particle material is useful for preparing therapeutic agent e.g. photothermotherapy agent and preparing an imaging agent e.g. photoacoustic imaging agent (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The nano-particle material: has substantially no toxicity and side effects, and has good photoacoustic imaging effect. The method: generates carbon dioxide for the purpose of enhancing photoacoustic imaging and realizes the integration of diagnosis and treatment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing a gold nano-rod /calcium carbonate nanoparticle material comprising (A) mixing a polyethyleneimine-modified gold nano-rod with an aldehyde-modified polyethylene glycol in water to obtain a polymer-modified gold nano-rod, and (B) mixing calcium carbonate nanoparticles, polyethyleneimine and aldehyde-modified polyethylene glycol in water to obtain polymer-modified calcium carbonate nanoparticles, where the steps (A) and (B) are not sequentially limited.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: In the polymer-modified gold nano-rod, the molar ratio of the gold nano-rod, polyethyleneimine, and polyethylene glycol is 1:0.1-50:0.1-50. In the polymer modified calcium carbonate nanoparticles, the molar ratio of polyethyleneimine, polyethylene glycol and calcium carbonate nanoparticles is 1:0.1-50:1-100. The polyethyleneimine has a weight average molecular weight of 500-35000, and the polyethylene glycol has a weight average molecular weight of 500-35000. The solvent of the calcium chloride solution and the solvent of the alkali metal carbonate solution are both an anionic surfactant solution. The molar ratio of the calcium chloride in the calcium chloride solution to the alkali metal carbonate in the alkali metal carbonate solution is 1:1. The mixing time of the calcium chloride solution and the alkali metal carbonate solution is 0.5-24 hours and the ultrasonic dispersion time is 1-30 minutes. Preferred Method: In the step (B), the calcium carbonate nanoparticles are prepared by: mixing the calcium chloride solution and the alkali metal carbonate solution, centrifuging, and ultrasonically dispersing to obtain calcium carbonate nanoparticles. The polyethyleneimine modified gold nano-rod and the aldehyde-modified polyethylene glycol are mixing and stirring in water for 0.5-24 hours. The calcium carbonate nanoparticles, polyethyleneimine and aldehyde-modified polyethylene glycol are mixing and stirring in water for 0.5-24 hours.

PT P
PN CN110127668-A
TI Preparation of carbon nanomaterial with high graft ratio involves adding carbon nano material, water, and lithium hydroxide monohydrate in reaction container, ultrasonically irradiating, heating, cooling, hydrating, filtering, and drying.
AU WU T
   ZHANG Y
   ZHAO Z
AE SHANGHAI LEVSON NANOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201972762X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of carbon nanomaterial with high graft ratio involves selecting carbon nano material component, adding selected carbon nano material, and water in reaction container, mixing, adding lithium hydroxide monohydrate, placing the reaction container in ultrasonic generator having power of 250W or more, ultrasonically irradiating at room temperature for 1-2 hours with continuous supply of carbon dioxide, heating the reaction container at 95-105 degrees C for 6-12 hours, cooling naturally at room temperature, subjecting the material in reaction container to hydration reaction using 2.5 kPa water vapor for 10-60 minutes, filtering, washing, and vacuum drying at 80-100 degrees C. The concentration of the carbon nano material component is 0.01-3%. The mass ratio of lithium hydroxide monohydrate to the carbon nanomaterial component is 0.5-1:1.
   USE - Preparation of carbon nanomaterial with high graft ratio.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon nanomaterial having average graft ratio of 90% or more is prepared by simple process under mild reaction conditions.

AE LI S (LISS-Individual)

GA 201958498J
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dot-loaded porous organic framework nanofibers comprising 2,4,6-tris(aminomethylene)cyclohexane-1,3,5-trione repeating unit (I), is new.
   USE - New carbon dot-loaded porous organic framework nanofibers for formaldehyde removal (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The new compound has excellent performance and stable electron-transporting ability, rapidly decomposes the released toxic formaldehyde gas into non-toxic carbon dioxide and water, and enables formaldehyde removal reaching as high as 91% under closed illumination condition for 5 hours, and can be prepared by simple method with high yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A carbon dot-loaded porous organic framework nanofibers comprising 2,4,6-tris(aminomethylene)cyclohexane-1,3,5-trione repeating unit of formula (I), is new.
   A = group of formula (i).
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) preparation of the carbon dot-loaded porous organic framework nanofibers; and
   (2) removal of formaldehyde using the carbon dot-loaded porous organic framework nanofibers, which involves spraying the nano-dimensional carbon dot-loaded porous organic framework on a formaldehyde-containing substance under illumination. The formaldehyde removal rate is 90-95%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Property: The repeating unit (I) has a pore size of 1.5-4.0 nm. Preferred Components: The organic acid is acetic acid, lauric acid, and/or stearic acid. The alkali solution is sodium hydroxide aqueous solution, potassium hydroxide aqueous solution, and/or barium hydroxide aqueous solution. The organic solvent is anhydrous N-N-dimethylformamide, tetrahydrofuran, and anhydrous acetone.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preparation (claimed): Preparation of the carbon dot-loaded porous organic framework nanofibers involves ultrasonically mixing 0.5-5 g organic acid and 0.1-6 mol/L alkali solution for 10-30 minutes, transferring to a high-pressure reaction vessel with PTFE, reacting at 150-250 degrees C for 5-24 hours while heating, cooling, separating, washing (A), drying to obtain 2-10 nm carbon quantum dots, adding 2,4,6-triformylphloroglucinol, 1,4-p-phenylenediamine, and 1-10 mL mixed solution of mesitylene and 1,4-dioxane in a volume ratio of 1:1-5, sequentially to a reactor, adding an organic acid aqueous solution, mixing, packaging, vacuumizing and injecting inert gas, reacting at 100-150 degrees C, obtaining solid powder, washing repeatedly using an organic solvent, air-drying, obtaining porous organic framework nanofibers, dissolving 1-3 g carbon quantum dots to 100-150 mL acetone, obtaining a carbon quantum dot-acetone solution, spraying the carbon quantum dot-acetone solution into the porous organic framework nanofibers, leaving still, and drying.

TI Treating printing and dyeing wastewater comprises passing printing and dyeing wastewater to flocculation tank, adjusting pH by adding calcium hydroxide, stirring, allowing to stand, passing supernatant to neutralization tank and discharging.
AU YU P
   WANG X
AE ZHONGKE CHUANGXIE ENVIRONMENT TECHNOLOGY (ZHON-Non-standard)
GA 201956309W
AB    NOVELTY - Treating printing and dyeing wastewater comprises (a) passing the printing and dyeing wastewater into flocculation sedimentation tank, and adjusting the pH to 11-14 by adding calcium hydroxide or calcium bicarbonate, (b) firstly stirring at a rate of 1500-2000 r for 2-4 hours, and then stirring at a rate of 200-400 revolutions/minute for 4-6 hours, (c) allowing to stand for 24-48 hours, (d) passing the supernatant to neutralization tank, then neutralizing the pH by introducing carbon dioxide to obtain calcium carbonate precipitate, and (e) passing the supernatant liquid to the flocculation tank, then adding 100-500 g flocculating agent per 1 l of sewage, stirring at a rate of 500-800 revolutions/minute for 4-8 hours, and discharging.
   USE - The method is useful for treating printing and dyeing wastewater.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can greatly improves the water treatment effect; can reduce the secondary pollution; and can improve the quality of the treated water.

TI Composite air purifying agent comprises acid-modified attapulgite, titanium dioxide, chlorine dioxide, tourmaline stone, zeolite and activator.
AU CHEN W
AE CHEN W (CHEN-Individual)
GA 201947480E
AB    NOVELTY - Composite air purifying agent comprises 40-45 pts. wt. acid-modified attapulgite, 5-10 pts. wt. titanium dioxide, 3-8 pts. wt. chlorine dioxide, 10-20 pts. wt. tourmaline stone, 10-20 pts. wt. zeolite and 0.5-1 pts. wt. activator.
   USE - Used as composite air purifying agent.
   ADVANTAGE - The purifying agent: has large amount of adsorption of harmful gas molecules in the air (including formaldehyde and ammonia), polar molecules (including carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide), or contain harmful molecules (including irritating and cytotoxic substances); does not lock firmly; and removes harmful substances by chlorine dioxide reaction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing composite air purifying agent comprising (i) adding acid-modified attapulgite and water into the zeolite, and granulating, (ii) preparing titanium dioxide into a titania colloidal solution, and dissolving with chlorine dioxide and tourmaline stone in the colloidal solution, and evenly spraying onto the core to obtain a purifying ball, and (iii) dissolving the activator in ethanol, uniformly spraying onto the purification ball to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The acid-modified attapulgite is prepared by adding attapulgite into a saturated silicotungstic acid solution, refluxing the temperature at 110-120 degrees C for 8-10 hours, filtering the acid-treated attapulgite and drying under reduced pressure. The attapulgite is subjected to purification treatment before being modified comprises (i) adding acidic attapulgite into 0.1 mol/l hydrochloric acid solution, carrying out ultrasonic treatment for 1-2 hours, stirring at 150-200 revolutions per minute for 12 hours at room temperature, and allowing to stand for 24 hours, and removing the supernatant and the lowest impurities, (ii) adding the attapulgite in the middle layer after pickling to the water, stirring at 150-200 revolutions per minute for 24-36 hours, and allowing to stand for 24 hours, and pouring off the supernatant and the lower layer of impurities to obtain water-washed attapulgite. The washed attapulgite is dried under reduced pressure. The activator is ammonium hydrogencarbonate, sodium hydrogencarbonate and/or aluminum sulfate. The method comprises calcining the air purifying agent and activating. The baking is activated by a flat type electric furnace, and the activation temperature is 200-300 degrees C. Preferred Composition: The composite air purifying agent comprises 40 pts. wt. acid-modified attapulgite, 5 pts. wt. titanium dioxide, 3 pts. wt. chlorine dioxide, 10 pts. wt. tourmaline stone, 10 pts. wt. zeolite and 0.5 pts. wt. activator.

PN CN109647323-A
TI Preparing amino-modified layered double hydroxide comprises e.g. dissolving anionic surfactant and aminosilane in aqueous alcohol solution, mixing both solutions, ultrasonic cell disintegrating, filtering, washing and drying.
AU AI N
   ZHAO Y
   LIU Q
   WANG Q
   JIANG Y
   LOU S
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG TECHNOLOGY (UYZT-C)
GA 201939473M
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing amino-modified layered double hydroxide comprises (i) dissolving 2-5.0 g anionic surfactant and 5-7.0 g aminosilane in 150 ml aqueous alcohol solution to obtain solution A, where the volume ratio between anhydrous ethanol and deionized water in the aqueous alcohol solution is 1:2, taking magnesium nitrate hexahydrate and aluminum nitrate nonahydrate in a molar ratio of 3:1 to obtain 50-100 ml solution B, (ii) adding solution B into solution A dropwise at rate of 0.5-1.0 ml/minute, starting the ultrasonic cell disintegrator, working ultrasonic frequency for 5 seconds, stop for 5 seconds, stirring continuously during the dropwise addition, and maintaining the pH at 10 plus minus 0.1, and (iii) after finishing the dropwise addition of solution B, turn off the ultrasonic cell disintegrator, aging at 60-80 degrees C for 4-6 hours, filtering the mixture, washing and drying to obtain final product.
   USE - The amino-modified layered double hydroxide is useful in carbon dioxide adsorption.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The anionic surfactant used in the modification process is sodium dodecyl sulfate, sodium dodecyl sulfate or Sodium dodecyl benzene sulfonate. The aminosilane used in the modification is 3-aminopropyltrimethoxyaminosilane or N-(2-aminoethyl)-3-aminopropyltrimethoxysilane. During the modification process, the ratio between solution A and solution B is such that n magnesium:n aluminum:n anionic surfactant :n aminosilane of 3:1:2:6. The ultrasonic auxiliary strength during modification process is 0 W, 65 W, 195 W and 325 W.

TI Long afterglow luminescent aerogel comprises zinc            gallate crystal material and chromium(iii) ion doped in            zinc gallate crystal material.
AU DU A
   ZHANG T
   ZHOU B
AE UNIV TONGJI (UYTJ-C)
GA 201935796Y
AB    NOVELTY - Long afterglow luminescent aerogel comprises                zinc gallate (ZnGa2O4) crystal material and                chromium(iii) ion (Cr3+) doped in the zinc gallate                crystal material, where the molar ratio of the zinc                gallate crystal material to chromium(iii) ion is                1:(0.005-0.01).
   USE - Used as long-afterglow luminescent                aerogel.
   ADVANTAGE - The long-afterglow luminescent aerogel has                high specific surface area, good absorption                performance, can still emit light after the                ultraviolet light stops irradiating, has higher                research value in the field of biological                medicine.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing long afterglow luminescent aerogel,                comprising (i) dissolving zinc nitrate, gallium                nitrate and chromium nitrate in ethanol, mixing                uniformly, then adding de-ionized water and                polyacrylic acid, and stirring uniformly to obtain                mixed solution, (ii) adding a gel enhancer into the                mixed solution, stirring and allowing to stand to                convert into a gel, (iii) aging on the surface of                the gel with a layer of ethanol, changing the                ethanol at intervals, and performing carbon dioxide                supercritical drying to obtain crude aerogel, and                (iv) calcining the crude aerogel to obtain long                afterglow luminescent aerogel.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The molar ratio of the                  zinc nitrate, gallium nitrate and chromium                  nitrate is 1:1:0.005-0.01. The molar ratio of the                  de-ionized water to zinc nitrate added is                  (10-13):1, the molar ratio of the polyacrylic                  acid to zinc nitrate added is (3-4.5):1, and in                  the step (i), the stirring speed is 300-1000                  revolutions/minute, and the stirring time is                  15-30 minutes. The gel enhancer is propylene                  oxide, the molar ratio of the propylene oxide to                  de-ionized water is (0-1.5): 0.15, and the amount                  of propylene oxide added is not 0, and in the                  step (ii), the stirring speed is 300-1000                  revolutions/minute, and the stirring time is                  15-30 minutes. In the step (iii), the ethanol is                  replaced every 8-12 hours, and the replacement is                  performed 3-5 times. In the method, the carbon                  dioxide supercritical drying temperature is 38-43                  degrees C, the pressure is 7.5-9.5 MPa, and the                  time is 3-4 hours. In the method, the calcination                  temperature is 600-1200 degrees C, and the                  calcination time is 1-6 hours.

   HU J

   ZHU L

The preparation of F-CM conditioned                  culture medium comprises culturing 3T3 cells to                  80-90% confluence, washing with phosphate                  buffered saline (PBS) cleaning liquid,                  continuously culturing for 24 hours, collecting                  upper layer culture medium, after 10 minutes,                  centrifuging at 3000 g, removing the cell debris                  to obtain the conditioned medium and storing at -                  20 degrees C.
