PN CN109320566-A; CN109320566-B
TI Separating and purifying ganglioside, involves placing fresh pig brain tissue in mixed system of sodium chloride and ethanol aqueous solution, heating, stirring and reflowing, ultrasonically stirring, extracting, precipitating and filtering.
AU XIANG J
   ZHU L
   QIN S
   ZHANG Y
   WANG L
AE SICHUAN XINGJIEXIANG PHARM CO LTD (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 201918559A
AB    NOVELTY - The method for separating and purifying ganglioside involves (1) taking fresh pig brain tissue as raw material, placing in a mixed system of sodium chloride and ethanol aqueous solution, heating, stirring and reflowing to obtain crude liquid containing ganglioside, where the concentration of the sodium chloride in the mixed system is 1-3 %mass, (2) ultrasonically stirring the crude liquid with 40-50 kHz for 10-15 minutes, (3) extracting the product using supercritical carbon dioxide at 50-60 degrees C and 30-50 MPa for 20-25 minutes, and (4) adding the product to acetone for low temperature precipitation, filtering the resulting precipitate, washing with absolute ethyl alcohol, and drying under reduced pressure.
   USE - Method for separating and purifying ganglioside.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables separation and purification of ganglioside with high yield and purity, and short extraction and purifying time, which does not use toxic organic solvent, by simple and economical process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: In step (1), the heating temperature is 65-68 degrees C, and the processing time is 10-15 minutes. The stirring speed of the heating, stirring and reflowing treatment is 120-150 rpm. The ultrasonically stirring speed in step (2) is 300-350 rpm. The low-temperature precipitation temperature is 5-10 degrees C, and the precipitation reaction time is 15-20 minutes. In step (4), the washing process with absolute ethyl alcohol is performed one or two times. The pressure of drying process is -0.01 to -0.05 MPa, and the drying temperature is 40-60 degrees C. Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of the brain tissue and mixed system is 1:(10-12). The concentration of the ethanol in the ethanol aqueous solution is 50-55 %mass. The content of acetone in step (4) is 1.5-3 times of weight of fresh brain tissue.

GA 201905486N
AB    NOVELTY - Oxidized biodegradable film composition comprises 86-90 wt.% polypropylene, 1-3 wt.% slow release agent, 6-10 wt.% oxidative degradation composite masterbatch and 1-3 wt.% photosensitizer.
   USE - Used as oxidized biodegradable film composition.
   ADVANTAGE - The oxidized biodegradable film composition utilizes oxidative degradation composite masterbatch which induces the oxidative cleavage and decomposition of polypropylene chain, and then decomposes into carbon dioxide and water under the action of microorganisms, moist heat and oxygen; has self-basic capability, and is environmentally friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing oxidized biodegradable film composition comprising (i) mixing polypropylene, slow release agent, oxidative degradation composite masterbatch, and photosensitizer, and then plasticizing through twin-screw extruder, (ii) feeding the molten melt into die and forming molten sheet at the die opening, (iii) attaching the sheet to the chill roll with air knife to quench to form unshaped sheet, and then cooling the sheet to form slab, (iv) drawing cast piece into film by longitudinal drawing machine and transverse stretching machine respectively, (v) performing thickness detection on the film, and (vi) cooling and wounding tested product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The slow release agent is dibutylhydroxytoluene. The photosensitizer is graft copolymer of alkyl ketone with polyethylene or polypropylene. The oxidative degradation composite masterbatch is composed of 60-70 wt.% iron stearate and 30-40 wt.% titanium dioxide.

PT P
PN CN108975815-A
TI Producing regenerated concrete involves preparing            regenerated aggregate, crushing waste concrete, and            then carrying out primary sieving and washing to obtain            regenerated coarse aggregate and regenerated fine            aggregate.
AU LIU H
   LI S
   DU L
   ZHANG W
AE WUHAN HUAQIANG NEW CONSTR MATERIALS CO (WUHA-Non-standard)
GA 2018A2974A
AB    NOVELTY - Producing regenerated concrete involves                preparing regenerated aggregate, crushing waste                concrete, and then carrying out primary sieving and                washing to obtain regenerated coarse aggregate and                regenerated fine aggregate. The regenerated                aggregate is pre-treated, regenerated coarse                aggregate and regenerated fine aggregate is heated                to 300-400 degrees C, crushed again, and then                secondary screening is performed to obtain refined                coarse aggregate and regenerated fine aggregate.                The regenerated aggregate is subjected to secondary                treatment, refined coarse aggregate and regenerated                fine aggregate are placed in a closed container for                carbonization, relative humidity is controlled to                40-85%, and carbon dioxide concentration is 30-75%                until weight of the recycled aggregate without                carbonization is not more than 5%. The regenerated                concrete is prepared by mixing treated recycled                aggregate with original aggregate, adhesive                material, filler and admixture to obtain a finished                product.
   USE - Method for producing regenerated                concrete.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to produce regenerated                concrete, which obtains more qualified recycled                aggregate, improves mechanical property, reduces                dry shrinkage, and improves reinforced concrete                stress, impermeability and service life.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Conditions: The waste concrete is                  crushed to obtain crushed concrete block, which                  is subjected to magnetic separation screening to                  remove metal impurities from it. The obtained                  recycled coarse aggregate has a particle size of                  5-40mm, and recycled fine aggregate has a                  particle size of less than 5mm. The refined                  coarse aggregate obtained by secondary screening                  has a particle size of 5-20mm, recycled fine                  aggregate has a particle size of less than 5mm,                  and regenerated fine aggregate is sieved three                  times to obtain a refined fine aggregate having a                  particle size of 0.15-5mm. The secondary                  treatment of regenerated aggregate is completed,                  and partially regenerated aggregate is grounded                  into a powder and spread uniformly. The                  phenolphthalein indicator is sprayed uniformly to                  observe that red color is present, and if volume                  of red color is more than 5 vol.%, and recycled                  aggregate is charged until weight of                  unconsolidated recycled aggregate is less than or                  equal to 5%. The water reducing agent comprises                  sodium methylene dimethyl naphthalene sulfonate                  polymer and beta-naphthalene sulfonate                  formaldehyde condensate in a weight ratio of                  1:1.5-3.5. The pore regulator comprises sustained                  release agent capable of releasing carbon dioxide                  and swelling agent in a weight ratio of                  1:0.5-0.8. The sustained release agent is an                  aluminosilicate subjected to carbon dioxide                  immersion treatment. The swelling agent comprises                  calcium oxide, calcium sulfoaluminate and                  magnesium oxide in a weight ratio of 2-3:1:1. The                  refined coarse aggregate, refined fine aggregate,                  cement and water are mixed and stirred, and                  remaining components are added and components are                  kept in a positive pressure environment, and then                  mixed. Preferred Compositions: The recycled                  concrete comprises 963-1328 pts. wt. primary                  aggregate, 331-528 pts. wt. refined coarse                  aggregate, 268-488 pts. wt. refined fine                  aggregate, 566-720 pts. wt. cement, 231-322 pts.                  wt. coal ash, 118-266 pts. wt. furnace slag,                  25-55 pts. wt. water reducing agent, 221-308 pts.                  wt. water and 18-45 pts. wt. pore regulator. The                  recycled concrete further comprises air                  entraining agent, which is 43-66 pts. wt. of                  saponin.
DC A93 (Roads, building, construction flooring.); L02 (Refractories, ceramics, cement - includes manufacturing methods, limes, soil preparation for (road) building, magnesias and slags, cements, mortars, concretes, abrasives, thermal or acoustic insulation (non)oxide ceramics and ceramic composites, but not brick making, concrete mixers or casting or pottersâ€™ wheels (C04).)

TI Preparation of aluminum-base compound fertilizer involves mixing phosphogypsum, red mud, additives, and modifier, roasting, reacting with vanadium pentoxide, mixing with sulfuric acid, adding e.g. urea, granulating, drying, and sieving.
AU LI J
   GAO G
   LUO X
   ZHOU D
   LU F
   CHEN X
AE GUIZHOU GUIFU ECOLOGICAL FERTILIZER IND (GUIZ-Non-standard)
   GUIZHOU TECHNOLOGY INST (GZTI-C)
   GUIZHOU KEXIN CHEM & METALLURGY CO LTD (GUIZ-Non-standard)
GA 2018995057
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of aluminum-base compound fertilizer comprises mixing phosphogypsum, red mud, additives, and modifier, grinding, and roasting to obtain clinker; dissolving and solid-liquid separating; adding carbon dioxide to solution, filtering, washing white precipitate, drying, burning to obtain alumina, and separating to obtain sulfide; putting into 30-50% oxygen-enriched environment, roasting at 800-1200 degrees C for 3-5 hours, catalytic reacting with vanadium pentoxide, and absorbing with concentrated sulfuric acid; mixing with concentrated sulfuric acid, urea, and zeolite at molar ratio of 1-2:0.5-1; adding potassium chloride, reacting for 60-90 minutes at 120-140 degrees C; feeding to reactor, inleting ammonia, neutralizing, adding urea, ammonium phosphate, calcium magnesium phosphate fertilizer, and melted phosphorus fertilizer, mixing, and granulating; and drying at 80-90 degrees C, cooling, and sieving.
   USE - Method for preparing aluminum-base compound fertilizer.
   ADVANTAGE - The fertilizer has low cost, high utilization ratio of phosphogypsum and red mud, high recovery ratio, high purity, and high quality.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The additive is sodium carbonate or sodium hydroxide. The modifier is anthracite, carbon, or coal gangue. The ratio of phosphogypsum and red mud is 1:0.8-2. The amount of modifier is 10-25 wt.%. The ratio of clinker to water is 4-6:1. The amount of zeolite powder is 0.2 wt.%. The molar ratio of sulfuric acid solution with potassium chloride is 1.5-3:1. The material in eight step comprises 230-250 pts. wt. acidic slurry, 130-150 pts. wt. urea, 30-40 pts. wt. ammonium phosphate, 20-30 pts. wt. calcium magnesium phosphate fertilizer, and 30-40 pts. wt. melted phosphorus fertilizer. Preferred Method: The roasting is done for 1-2 hours at 1000-1350 degrees C. The burning is done for 2-5 hours at temperature of 800-1200 degrees C.
   ACTIVITY - Fertilizer.

TI Production of composite of zinc oxide and calcium carbonate involves mixing and grinding zinc-containing ore and leaching agent, filtering, adding calcium oxide, stirring, filtering to obtain solid and filtrate, and calcining solid.
AU YUE H
   LI S
   LONG Z
   JIANG T
   LIU Q
AE CHONGQING DONGQUN TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (CHON-Non-standard)
GA 201897182J
AB    NOVELTY - Production of composite of zinc oxide and calcium carbonate involves mixing and grinding zinc-containing ore and leaching agent, filtering to obtain leachate, adding 100-130 %mass, preferably 100-110 degrees C calcium oxide and/or calcium hydroxide to leachate, stirring, filtering to obtain solid product and filtrate, passing carbon dioxide to filtrate, rinsing solid with water, drying and calcining the solid product at 150 degrees C or more, preferably 250-350 degrees C. The leaching agent is mixed aqueous solution of ammonia and ammonium bicarbonate or mixed aqueous solution of ammonia and ammonium carbonate, or mixed aqueous solution of ammonia, ammonium bicarbonate and ammonium carbonate.
   USE - Production of composite of zinc oxide and calcium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, convenient and easy to operate, energy saving, economical and environmentally-friendly, and utilizes ultra-low-grade zinc ore, does not require steaming, and solves the ammonia cycle. The composite can be directly used as product or as raw material for other industrial processes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method further involves performing pretreatment by adding calcium hydroxide and/or calcium oxide to leachate, stirring, filtering to obtain solid product (a) and intermediate filtrate, and performing zinc immersion by adding calcium hydroxide and/or calcium oxide to the intermediate filtrate, stirring, filtering to obtain solid product (b) and final filtrate, drying and calcining solid product (b), mixing solid product (b) with solid product (a). Preferred Composition: The concentration of total ammonia in leaching agent is 5-15 %mass, preferably 6-8 %mass. The zinc ion concentration in the leachate is 10 g/L or more, preferably 20-50 g/L. The zinc oxide and calcium carbonate composite have a core-shell composite structure.

PN CN108823049-A
TI Preparing cocktail comprises preprocessing water, sterilizing, adding raw materials e.g. pure water, F42 syrup, rum, edible alcohol and citric acid into heating tank, performing heat exchange treatment, filtering, sterilizing and filling.
AU CUI J
   SHI P
AE QINGDAO YIBO FOOD TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (QING-Non-standard)
GA 201912451J
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing cocktail comprises (i) preprocessing of pure water, screening other raw materials, sterilizing the bottle and bottle cap, rinsing with filtered water and extracting the auxiliary materials by edible alcohol; (ii) adding the filtered water into the heating tank, adding raw materials including fructose syrup, heating to 80-90 degrees C for 15-20 minutes and stirring; (iii) performing heat exchange treatment on heated and stirred syrup solution, mixing the extracted auxiliary materials and heat exchange treated syrup solution according to the weight fraction and filtering; (iv) adding citric acid, sodium citrate, potassium sorbate, food flavor and food color, mixing and stirring; and (v) stirring uniformly, filtering, cooling, sterilizing, filling in bottle, filling carbon dioxide, pressing the lid and covering.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing cocktail.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is suitable for mass production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for cocktail prepared by the above mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the extraction comprises placing auxiliary material and 95% edible alcohol in a leaching tank according to specific ratio and leaching at room temperature, where the leaching time is determined according to the leaching month. In the step (i), the bottle is disinfected with 100 ppm sodium hypochlorite solution for 6-10 seconds and the cap is disinfected with 10 ppm/m3 ozone for 25-40 minutes. In the step (ii), the high temperature sterilization is performed at 85-95 degrees C for 15-25 minutes. The step (v) further comprises spraying the product after pressing, washing bottle and checking the product status by using the light to determine the qualified product for outer packaging process. Preferred Components: The raw materials are as follows: 70-85 pts. wt. pure water, 6-15 pts. wt. F 42 syrup, 2-4 pts. wt. 45% rum, 1-3 pts. wt. 95% edible alcohol, 0.1-0.5 pts. wt. citric acid, 0.1-0.5 pts. wt. sodium citrate, 0.01-0.02 pts. wt. potassium sorbate, 0-0.002 pts. wt. food color, 0.01-0.04 pts. wt. food flavor, 6-8 pts. wt. auxiliary materials and 0.5-1 pts. wt. carbon dioxide. The food color is fancy red, brilliant blue pigment, lemon yellow pigment and/or caramel colorant. The food flavor is ethyl maltol, pacifiers, passion fruit flavor, caffeine and taurine flavor, blueberry flavor, herb flavor, cranberry flavor, raspberry flavor, lychee flavor, honey peach flavor and/or edible flavor MOJITO flavor spice. The auxiliary material is dried passion fruit extract, coffee bean extract, dried blueberry extract, Vanilla extract, dried cranberry extract, dried lemon extract, dried yellow peach extract and/or dried lychee extract.

PN CN108707060-A; CN108707060-B
TI Reduction of acetic acid to ethanol involves mixing methanol and acetic acid, adding to reactor, reacting with catalyst to obtain reaction product, and separating reaction product to liquid mixture and gas mixture.
AU SHEN S
   CHANG S
   XU C
   SHAN W
   LI H
   GUO C
   LI J
   WU T
AE UNIV TAIYUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYTL-C)
GA 201886127M
AB    NOVELTY - In-situ reduction of acetic acid to ethanol involves mixing methanol and acetic acid, adding to reactor, reacting with catalyst at 80-400 degrees C and 0.5-8 MPa without carrier gas to obtain reaction product, separating reaction product by gas-liquid separation, separating the liquid mixture and gas mixture, and separating the liquid mixture. The remaining product after the separation of ethanol from the liquid mixture comprises methanol, acetic acid, methyl acetate and ethyl acetate. The gas mixture comprises carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrogen gas. The molar ratio of methanol to acetic acid is 1-10:1. The reactor has liquid space velocity of 0.1-3 hour-1.
   USE - Reduction of acetic acid to ethanol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, economical, efficient, inhibits the trans esterification reaction and prevents the conversion of methyl acetate to ethyl acetate, thereby overcoming the problems of low conversion of acetic acid and low selectivity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Apparatus: The reactor is fixed bed reactor, fluidized bed reactor and/or slurry bed reactor. The device used in process comprises mixer, vaporizer, reactor, gas-liquid separator, rectification separator, condenser, valve, pipe and feed pump connected to gas compressor.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method further involves recycling the separated gas mixture, further separating ethanol to methanol and adding methanol and acetic acid as a raw material to reactor and carrying out the reaction. The separation of liquid mixture is carried out by azeotropic distillation, extractive distillation, membrane separation or adsorption. The catalyst comprises copper/silica, copper/ceria, copper/zinc oxide, copper/alumina, platinum/ferric oxide and/or nickel/alumina, and is obtained by impregnation, ion exchange or co-precipitation. Preferred Components: The carrier gas comprises nitrogen, hydrogen, argon and/or helium, preferably hydrogen as supplemental hydrogen source. The carrier gas has space velocity of 0-200 hour-1. The usage amount of catalyst is 10-50 wt.%.

TI Motorcycle fork welding technique parameter optimizing method, involves analyzing welding residual stress and strain field simulation result, selecting residual stress peak level, and determining optimal welding technique parameter.
AU YAN C
   WU L
   LIU J
   ZHANG L
   JI X
   ZHAO J
AE UNIV HOHAI CHANGZHOU CAMPUS (UYHO-C)
GA 201881509Q
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves determining modeling parameters. An entity model of the half fork is established according to actual size. A heat source model is selected according to a heat source model and model parameter. Number of total welding seam connections between an end pipe and a side pipe are determined. Straight welding seam is welded. Predetermined time is set. Material thermal physical property parameter is obtained. Different direction thermal conductivity and linear expansion coefficient are constructed. Welding residual stress and strain field simulation result are analyzed. Residual stress peak level is selected. Optimal welding technique parameter is determined.
   USE - Motorcycle fork welding technique parameter optimizing method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables reducing residual stress and deformation of a motorcycle through SYSWELD numerical simulation software under different welding technique parameter so as to find welding technique parameter of carbon dioxide gas.

PN WO2018172813-A2; WO2018172813-A3; EP3601167-A2; CN110869320-A; US2020109060-A1; IN201917041929-A; EP3601167-A4
TI Preparation of zinc sulfide particles used as e.g.            semiconductor material, involves preparing mixture of            zinc mineral and elemental sulfur, milling obtained            mixture, and annealing obtained mixture at preset            conditions.
AU ALHASSAN S
   LONKAR S P
AE PETROLEUM INST (PETR-Non-standard)
   UNIV KHALIFA SCI & TECHNOLOGY & RES              KUST (UYKH-Non-standard)
   UNIV KHALIFA SCI & TECHNOLOGY & RES              KUST (UYKH-Non-standard)
GA 201876510Q
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of zinc sulfide particles involves                preparing mixture of zinc mineral and elemental                sulfur, milling the obtained mixture, and annealing                the obtained mixture at 300-500 degrees C.
   USE - Preparation of zinc sulfide particles                (claimed) e.g. zinc sulfide nanoparticles. Uses                include but are not limited to II-VI semiconductor                material, material for production of UV/blue light                device, infrared window material in thermal imaging                and aerospace applications, electronic                applications, active photocatalyst for                photocatalytic water splitting in hydrogen energy                generation and photoreduction of carbon dioxide and                dehalogenation of halogenated aromatics.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is non-toxic,                environmentally-friendly, solvent-free                mechano-thermal synthesis, is economical, does not                require further purification and can be scaled-up                easily with minimal complexity. The process uses                natural abundant low cost starting materials. The                process is operable without the necessity for                solvents or surface capping agents to control the                particle size. Since the method is carried out in                the absence of solvent, the formation of clumps is                prevented.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The zinc mineral is                  hydrozincite. Preferred Process: The milling                  process is carried out till the mixture of zinc                  mineral and elemental sulfur is homogenized. The                  milling process is carried out using a vortex                  ball mill. The method is carried out in the                  absence of any solvent including water so that it                  is a dry method, and in the absence of                  surfactant, stabilizers or other additives.                  Preferred Properties: The weight ratio of zinc                  mineral to sulfur is 1:0.25-1:1. The zinc sulfide                  particles have an average particle size of 20 nm                  or less. Preferred Conditions: The annealing                  process is carried out in inert atmosphere,                  preferably under nitrogen atmosphere or argon                  atmosphere.
DC E32 (Compounds of Ti, Zr, Hf, Cu, Ag, Au, Zn, Cd, Hg, Ga, In, Te, Ge, Sn, Pb, As, Sb, Bi.); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); U11 (Semiconductor Materials and Processes)
MC E11-W; E31-U01; E35-C04; L04-A05; U11-A14; U11-C03A; U11-C03J2A; U11-C03J8B

TI High-efficiency asymmetric rotary engine suitable for all drive applications, achieves significant expansion of expansion volume, expansion ratio and efficiency by constructive asymmetric angle division.
AU 
AE MELLERT H (MELL-Individual)
GA 201872868W
AB    NOVELTY - The engine achieves significant expansion of expansion volume, expansion ratio and efficiency by constructive asymmetric angle division. The wear-free hollow cylinder, housing cover and combustion chamber are constructed in an entire ceramic insulated expansion area, which minimizes the loss of heat energy and increases the efficiency. A rotary piston is arranged eccentrically on a drive shaft rotates which the circular hollow cylinder, and radially sealing out from motor housing corresponding to piston rotation and sliding shutters, thus sealing the compression space, expansion space and suction space from each other.
   USE - High-efficiency asymmetric rotary engine suitable for all drive applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The internal efficiency can be maximized to achieve minimal carbon dioxide emissions. The efficiency of the combustion chamber and expansion chamber can be increased so that the heat energy is largely converted into mechanical work. The high liter performance is achieved.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view illustrating the operating chart within the compression and clock rate range for all lifting cylinder engines. (Drawing includes non-English language text)

PT P
PN CN108485209-A
TI Preparing phosphate buffer saline/chloroform (PBS/CF) microcellular foamed material used in electronic and electrical, construction, and furniture, involves dissolving polybutylene succinate in chloroform to get PBS clear solution.
AU KUANG T
   PENG X
   JU J
   CHEN B
   YANG Z
AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UYSC-C)
GA 2018712884
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing phosphate buffer saline/chloroform (PBS/CF) microcellular foamed material involves dissolving polybutylene succinate in chloroform to obtain PBS clear solution. The carbon fiber is added to chloroform, then ultrasonically stirring to fully wet the carbon fiber in chloroform to obtain chloroform solution containing CF. The chloroform solution containing CF is added to the PBS clear solution, and uniformly mixing by ultrasonic stirring, and dried under vacuum to obtain PBS/CF blend. The PBS/CF blend is injection molded to obtain PBS/CF composite material. The PBS/CF composite is intermittently foamed with supercritical carbon dioxide as a foaming agent to obtain PBS/CF microcellular foamed material.
   USE - Method for preparing phosphate buffer saline/chloroform (PBS/CF) microcellular foamed material used in electronic and electrical, construction, and furniture.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare phosphate buffer saline/chloroform (PBS/CF) microcellular foamed material, which has small cell size, high foaming intensity, good conductivity and has wide application prospect.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: The carbon fiber is immersed in acetone and dried before use to carry out impurity removal pretreatment. The carbon fibers have an average length of 2 mm. The injection molding employs a plunger type injection molding machine, and the temperature of the injection molding machine barrel is maintained to 150-180 degrees C. The intermittent foaming is performed at 100 degrees C, under pressure of 18 megapascal (MPa), then PBS/CF composite is soaked in supercritical carbon dioxide to form homogeneous system of supercritical carbon dioxide and composite materials, and then cooled to foaming temperature of 50-53 degrees C and after a period of heat preservation, the pressure is released. The atmospheric pressure causes supercritical carbon dioxide to form micropores in the matrix while opening cooling water to shape the cells to obtain PBS/CF microcellular foaming material. Preferred Composition: The PBS/CF blend comprises 80-90 wt.% PBS, and 10-20 wt.% CF.
DC A35 (Other processing and general - including vulcanisation, welding of plastics and adhesive processes. Testing.); A23 (Polyamides; polyesters. (including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.); A84 (Household and office fittings - including carpets and carbon paper.); A85 (Electrical applications.); A93 (Roads, building, construction flooring.); 
MC A05-E02A; A08-B02; A08-M10; A08-S02; A11-A03; A11-B06; A12-D01; A12-E01; A12-R01; A12-S04; L03-J02
IP C08J-009/12; C08K-007/06; C08L-067/02

TI Wine-making wastewater processing system includes anaerobic pool, aerobic pool, coagulating basin, sedimentation tank, filtering tank, and boiler.
AU FANG T
AE LUZHOU TANZISHAN LIQUOR IND CO LTD (LUZH-Non-standard)
GA 2018686070
AB    NOVELTY - A wine-making wastewater processing system comprises an anaerobic pool (11), an aerobic pool (12), a coagulating basin (13), a sedimentation tank (14), a filtering tank (15), and a boiler (17).
   USE - A wine-making wastewater processing system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system reduces processing steps in the wastewater treatment process, is inexpensive, and reduces dosage of fresh water in the process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method of processing the wine-making wastewater, comprising:
   (A) inoculating anaerobic microorganisms in a brewing wastewater, and carrying out anaerobic decomposition to treat an organic matter in the wastewater, where the organic matter is further decomposed to carbon dioxide and water;
   (B) adding a coagulant in the wastewater to agglomerate and flocculate colloidal substance in the wastewater, and adding a chemical softener;
   (C) standing to precipitate;
   (D) filtering to separate insoluble matter from the wastewater; and
   (E) using the wastewater as boiler water to produce stream for wine-making.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing is a flow diagram of wastewater processing.
   Anaerobic pool (11)
   Aerobic pool (12)
   Coagulating basin (13)
   Sedimentation tank (14)
   Filtering tank (15)
   Boiler (17)

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing heat-resistant and corrosion-resistant magnesium alloy, comprising (i) placing magnesium, aluminum, zinc, manganese, lanthanum and cerium under the protection of a mixed gas of carbon dioxide and sulfur hexafluoride, melting, when the melt temperature reaches 690-700 degrees C, adding intermediate alloy magnesium vanadium, and intermediate alloy magnesium strontium, maintaining at 690-700 degrees C for 10-12 minutes, heating t to 720-730 degrees C for 8-10 minutes, cooling to 680-690 degrees C, and casting to obtain cast alloy, and (ii) subjecting the cast alloy to solution treatment and aging treatment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the casting uses a steel mold. The magnesium, aluminum, zinc, manganese, lanthanum, cerium, intermediate alloy magnesium vanadium and intermediate alloy magnesium lanthanum are preheated to 200-250 degrees C for 1-3 hours before use. In the step (ii), the temperature for the solution treatment is 420-430 degrees C and the time is 10-12 hours. The temperature of the aging treatment is 210-230 degrees C and the time is 14-16 hours.

   SHANXI LUAETMAN MINING GROUP CO LTD (SHLU-C)
GA 2018271702
AB    NOVELTY - A cobalt catalyst comprises cobalt, manganese, additive and carrier. The molar ratio of cobalt to manganese is 1:30-30:1, and weight ratio of carrier to cobalt catalyst is 10-90 wt.%.
   USE - Cobalt catalyst used for reaction of direct conversion of syngas into olefins in slurry-bed reactor (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is low-cost, simple, has high production efficiency and suitable for industrialization. The catalyst has low temperature high activity, low methane and carbon dioxide selectivity, and has high olefin selectivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) preparation of cobalt catalyst comprising preparing mixed salt solution, dissolving precipitant in water to prepare precipitant solution, dispersing carrier in water to obtain carrier dispersion preparation, mixing prepared solutions, carrying out co-precipitation reaction, aging, filtering, washing obtained filter cake, adding carrier and water, stirring to obtain slurry, spray drying, sintering, adding impregnation aid, evaporating, drying, and sintering; and
   (2) reaction of direct conversion of syngas into olefins in slurry-bed reactor comprising reacting at 150-300 degrees C under 0.1-10 MPa at reaction volume space velocity of 100-20000/hour.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of additive to cobalt and manganese is 0-10. The additive is alkali metal, alkaline earth metal, transition metal or rare earth metal. The alkali metal is sodium, potassium or rubidium; alkaline earth metal is magnesium, calcium or barium; transition metal is zirconium, zinc, vanadium, molybdenum or yttrium; and rare earth metal is lanthanum or cerium. The carrier is alumina powder, silica powder, titania powder, alumina sol, silica sol or titania sol. The precipitant is sodium carbonate, sodium bicarbonate, sodium hydroxide, potassium carbonate, potassium bicarbonate or potassium hydroxide. The syngas comprises hydrogen and carbon monoxide at molar ratio of 0.1-10. Preferred Methods: The preparation of cobalt catalyst comprises preparing 0.01-5 mol/L mixed salt solution comprising cobalt, and manganese inorganic or organic salts, dissolving precipitant in water to prepare 0.01-5 mol/L precipitant solution, dispersing carrier in water to obtain carrier dispersion preparation, mixing prepared solutions, carrying out co-precipitation reaction at 10-100 degrees C at pH value of 6-12, aging at 10-100 degrees C for 0.5-50 hours, filtering, washing obtained filter cake, adding carrier and water, stirring to obtain slurry, spray drying at 100-350 degrees C, sintering at 200-800 degrees C for 0.5-24 hours, adding impregnation aid, evaporating, drying at 10-200 degrees C for 0.5-24 hours, and sintering at 200-800 degrees C for 0.5-24 hours. The reaction of direct conversion of syngas into olefins in slurry-bed reactor further comprises activating at 150-600 degrees C for 1-24 hours.

TI Method for assessment, management and monitoring sequestration of carbon dioxide, involves identifying and obtaining samples of media, evaluating in situ microbial self limitation conditions followed by determining effect of biosurfactants.
AU VANCE D B
AE ARCADIS CORP SERVICES INC (ARCA-Non-standard)
GA 201876511W
AB    NOVELTY - A sequestration of carbon dioxide assessment, management and monitoring method involves identifying and obtaining samples of media from residual oil zone, and quantitatively and qualitatively evaluating in situ microbial self limitation conditions. The effects of microbial processes of the microbial consortia on mineral species is determined within the samples obtained from the ROZ followed by determining in situ MSL conditions govern the microbial processing of petroleum by the microbial consortia in the samples taken from the ROZ to effect biogenic mineral production of amorphous, nano-crystalline and chaotic mineral, determining whether modification of the native hydrocarbon species generates charged or polar species that interact with mineral surface charges to stimulate adsorption of petroleum hydrocarbons to mineral surfaces within samples, determining the effect of biosurfactants on the samples obtained from the ROZ, and monitoring the sequestration of injected carbon dioxide.
   USE - Method for assessment, management and monitoring sequestration of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient identification and assessment of residual oil zones that significantly increases the geographic footprint and target locations into which carbon dioxide can be injected and ultimately utilized and permanently sequestered in a commercial fashion generating value to partially offset the parasitic costs associated with the capture of anthropogenic carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A sequestration of carbon dioxide assessment, management and monitoring method involves identifying and obtaining samples of media from residual oil zone, and quantitatively and qualitatively evaluating in situ microbial self limitation conditions govern the microbial processing of petroleum in the samples obtained from the residual oil zone by a process of identifying specific microbial consortia and associated metabolic pathways within the samples obtained from the ROZ by identifying domain and species and by heterotrophic and autotrophic metabolic pathways, where the carbon dioxide assessment is associated with carbon dioxide enhanced oil recovery (EOR) operations by characterizing conditions within a residual oil zone (ROZ) to assess if it is a viable commercial carbon dioxide enhanced oil recovery target. The effects of microbial processes of the microbial consortia on mineral species is determined within the samples obtained from the ROZ followed by determining in situ MSL conditions govern the microbial processing of petroleum by the microbial consortia in the samples taken from the ROZ to effect biogenic mineral production of amorphous, nano-crystalline and chaotic mineral forms having enhanced reactivity towards carbon dioxide by determining the activity of the indigenous or introduced microbial consortia to the presence of petroleum hydrocarbons based on selective degradation and modification of native hydrocarbons species in the one or more samples, determining whether the modification of the native hydrocarbon species generates charged or polar species that interact with mineral surface charges to stimulate adsorption of petroleum hydrocarbons to mineral surfaces within the samples, determining the effect of biosurfactants on the samples obtained from the ROZ, determining the capacity for stimulation of iron reduction by the microbial consortia within samples, determining the effect of sulfate reduction, methanogenic and labile elemental systems, and processes and effects of microbial inhibition that prevent the substantially complete degradation of petroleum in the one or more samples, determining the ability of the amorphous, nano-crystalline and chaotic mineral forms having enhanced reactivity towards carbon dioxide that are effected by the processing of petroleum by the microbial consortia in the samples taken from the ROZ to sequester and store injected carbon dioxide, and determining if the sampled ROZ is economically developed using carbon dioxide EOR techniques based in part on the determination of the effects of the microbial consortia on mineral species and the determination of how in situ MSL conditions govern the microbial processing of petroleum by the microbial consortia to effect biogenic mineral production of amorphous, nano-crystalline and chaotic mineral forms having enhanced reactivity towards carbon dioxide within the samples obtained from the ROZ, if it is determined that the ROZ is economically developed using carbon dioxide EOR techniques, and injecting carbon dioxide into the ROZ and monitoring the sequestration of injected carbon dioxide associated with the carbon dioxide EOR operation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The native hydrocarbons that generate charged or polar species that interact with mineral surface charges comprise organic acids, ketones, and/residual oil zone or alcohols.

PN CN112521703-A; CN112521703-B
TI Preparing modified aramid            pulp/polytetrafluoroethylene composite material by            supercritical carbon dioxide comprises using            supercritical carbon dioxide, initiating polymerization            of fluorine-containing monomer, and compounding.
AU KONG H
   LI B
   DU X
   XU Q
   JIANG F
   ZENG J
   HAO Y
   SUN X
   SONG J
   YU M
   YU Z
   ZHANG P
AE UNIV SHANGHAI ENG & TECHNOLOGY (USES-C)
GA 202131518J
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing modified aramid                pulp/polytetrafluoroethylene composite material by                supercritical carbon dioxide comprises using                supercritical carbon dioxide for carrying out                fluorine-containing monomer and its corresponding                initiator into aramid pulp, initiating the                polymerization of fluorine-containing monomer to                obtain modified aramid pulp, and compounding the                modified aramid pulp and polytetrafluoroethylene to                prepare a composite material, where the friction                coefficient of composite material is 0.231-0.268,                and the volume wear rate is 0.112-0.243x 10-6                cm3/N.m.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing modified                aramid pulp/polytetrafluoroethylene composite                material by supercritical carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The material: uses supercritical carbon                dioxide, fluorine-containing monomers and                initiators to modify aramid pulp, which improves                its dispersion effect in the                polytetrafluoroethylene matrix and its interface                bonding performance with polytetrafluoroethylene;                and has excellent wear resistance and tearing                performance. The method: is simple; can realize                continuous production; uses supercritical carbon                dioxide as a solvent; and eliminates the necessary                dispersant, suspending agent and other chemical                reagents, which is environmentally friendly and                efficient.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The method specifically                  comprises (a) adding dried aramid pulp into                  reaction device at room temperature, and then                  adding fluorine-containing monomer and its                  corresponding initiator for closing the reaction                  device, (b) exhausting the air in the reaction                  device and pipeline, (c) filling the reaction                  device with carbon dioxide gas, increasing the                  temperature in the reaction device to the set                  value at a certain rate, so that the carbon                  dioxide in the reaction device reaches                  supercritical state, and insulating to obtain                  modified aromatic fiber pulp, (d) mixing the                  modified aramid pulp and polytetrafluoroethylene                  powder uniformly to obtain a mixture, (e) adding                  the mixture into a mold for cold pressing and                  demolding to obtain preformed sample, and (f)                  placing the preformed sample in an electric                  furnace, heating, insulating, and naturally                  cooling to room temperature along with the                  furnace to obtain a composite material. The step                  (b) comprises exhausting air in the reaction                  device and pipeline by filling carbon dioxide gas                  to a certain pressure, then vacuuming, and                  repeating the charging-vacuuming operation, where                  the pressure is 0.5-0.8 MPa. The step (c)                  comprises certain pressure is 10-25 MPa, certain                  rate is 5-20 degrees C/minute, set value is 70-80                  degrees C for 20-24 hours. The method comprises                  mixing and stirring uniformly at a rate of 10-15                  revolutions/minute for 5-10 minutes. The step (e)                  comprises pressing by cold pressing under the                  pressure of 60-100 MPa for 15-25 minutes at a                  rate of 3-5 MPa.minute-1. The step (f) comprises                  certain rate of 50-60 degrees C.hour-1, and                  temperature of 375-385 degrees C for 4-6 hours.                  Preferred Components: The aramid pulp is                  Kevlar29, Kevlar49, Kevlar129, Twaron, or                  domestic 1414 pulp. The fluorine-containing                  monomer is dodecafluoroheptyl acrylate. The                  initiator corresponding to fluorine-containing                  monomer is more than one of                  azobisisobutyronitrile, dibenzoyl peroxide,                  lauryl peroxide, potassium persulfate, and                  ammonium persulfate. The mass ratio of                  fluorine-containing monomer to aramid pulp is                  1:3-3:2. The mass ratio of initiator                  corresponding to fluorine-containing monomer to                  fluorine-containing monomer is 1:20-100. The                  content of modified aramid pulp in the mixture is                  5-25 wt.%.
DC A87 (Textile auxiliaries.); A14 (Polymers of other substituted monoolefins; including PVC, PTFE.); ; F06 (Chemical-type treatment of textiles (D06B, L, M, P, Q).); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).)

PT P
PN US2021017108-A1; US10961173-B2
TI Conversion of carbon dioxide in formation of e.g. methanol used as feedstock, involves reacting and combining epoxide, hydrogenation catalyst, hydrogen, and carbon dioxide with condensed phase comprising amine to form methanol and glycol.
AU KOTHANDARAMAN J
   HELDEBRANT D J
   JIANG Y
   DAGLE R A
AE BATTELLE MEMORIAL INST (BATT-C)
GA 202108616L
AB    NOVELTY - Conversion of carbon dioxide involves reacting and combining epoxide, a hydrogenation catalyst, hydrogen, and carbon dioxide with a condensed phase comprising an amine (220) under effective conditions to form methanol (250) and glycol in which at least 10 mol% epoxide is consumed in the reaction.
   USE - Conversion of carbon dioxide from landfill gases, wastewater treatment gases, manure gas sources, and industrial process used for producing methanol and glycol used as feedstock. Uses include but are not limited to olefin, ether, fuel blend, acetic acid, automotive antifreeze, and chemical feedstock for polyester production.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and economical, and enables conversion of carbon dioxide with improved atom efficiency, eliminates separations of excess reagent and/or by-product, using simplified equipment, with reduced energy consumption, reduced waste, and reduced usage amount of the water.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram of conversion of carbon dioxide to methanol.
   Condensed phase amine (202,220,240)
   Solvent (204)
   Hydrogenation catalyst (210)
   Water (230)
   Methanol (250)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The condensed phase is a condensed phase solution further comprising carbon dioxide. The epoxide is of formula (I). The glycol is of formula: R-CH(OH)-CH(OR')-(OH). Amine is primary amine, secondary amine, and tertiary amine, polyamine, and amine compound of formula: R1(R2)N-L1-NH-R3, preferably polyethylenimine. Carbon dioxide is adsorbed, absorbed, covalently bond, or ionically bond to amine. The condensed phase solution comprises a nonaqueous solvent (204), and/or 0-10 wt.% water (230). The catalyst comprises (a) ruthenium, platinum, rhodium, iron, nickel, cobalt, iron, copper, zinc, palladium, iridium, nickel, a catalyst supported on alumina, carbon, silica, chromite, and/or magnesium aluminum spinel, and/or (b) pincer ligand, and further comprises ruthenium compounds of formula (II) or (III). The hydrogenation catalyst is positioned in a fixed bed or fluidized bed.R,R' = H or aliphatic group, in which R is preferably methyl and R' is H;or R+R'+C = cycloaliphatic ring;R1,R2,L1 = aliphatic group or cycloaliphatic group;or R1+R2+N,L1+R2+N = heterocyclyl;andR3 = aliphatic group, cycloaliphatic group, alkoxyalkyl, or cycloalkylalkyl.Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of hydrogen to carbon dioxide of 2:1-10:1. Preferred Process: The method further involves preparing the condensed phase solution by contacting a gas stream comprising carbon dioxide with an amine-containing solvent or solution prior to combining reaction, and combining the epoxide, the carbon dioxide, and the condensed phase under cycloaddition conditions effective to form a cyclic carbonate, producing a cyclic carbonate-containing mixture, and combining the cyclic carbonate-containing mixture with the hydrogenation catalyst and hydrogen under conditions effective to form methanol and the glycol, or forming a mixture by combining the epoxide, the hydrogen catalyst, and carbon dioxide with the condensed phase, exposing the mixture to cycloaddition conditions effective to form a cyclic carbonate, producing a cyclic carbonate-containing mixture, and combining the cyclic carbonate-containing mixture with hydrogen under conditions effective to form methanol and the glycol, cooling the methanol and the glycol at (-100)-(30) degrees C, or (ii) separating the methanol and the glycol from the condensed phase, or (iii) separating the methanol and glycol from the condensed phase, and separating the methanol and the glycol. The method involves producing methanol by combining a hydrogenation catalyst, hydrogen, and carbon dioxide with a condensed phase comprising an amine under conditions (EC1) to provide a reaction between the hydrogen and carbon dioxide to form methanol and water, separating the methanol and water from the amine, and separating the methanol and water. Preferred Condition: The contacting step of the gas stream with the amine-containing solvent or solution is performed: (i) at a temperature (T') of 20-60 degrees C, or (ii) at an initial pressure (P') of 0.1-5 MPa, or (iii) both (i) and (ii). The effective conditions are (i-1) temperature (Tmg) of 50-170 degrees C, (i-2) initial pressure of 3-10 MPa, and/or (i-3) time of 3 seconds to 36 hours. The cycloaddition conditions effective to form a cyclic carbonate are (i) a temperature (Tc) of 25-180 degrees C, (ii) an initial pressure (Pc) of 0.1-2 MPa, or (iii) a time (tc) of 3 seconds-36 hours. The condition (EC1) are temperature of 50-180 degrees C or an initial pressure of 1-10 MPa, and/or time of 3 seconds to 36 hours.

PN CN109915804-A
TI Method for producing steam by using carbon dioxide, involves generating high temperature medium pressure steam, and absorbing heat of heat-conducting water tank when high temperature heat conduction oil is heated.
AU MENG F
AE HEFEI SIMULEI BRAND MANAGEMENT CO LTD (HEFE-Non-standard)
GA 201957191G
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves filling high-temperature and high-pressure vaporous carbon dioxide in a high-temperature heat-conducting oil tank from a carbon dioxide vaporization device. The high-temperature and high-pressure vaporous carbon dioxide and heat-conducting oil are carried out in a heat-conducting oil pipe. Normal temperature water is filled in the heat conduction water tank through an electromagnetic valve. Water vapor of a medium temperature steam pipe is rapidly heated in the high temperature oil heat exchange tank. High temperature medium pressure steam is generated and is pressurized by a pressurizing pump. Heat of the heat-conducting water tank is completely absorbed when the high temperature heat conduction oil is heated.
   USE - Method for producing steam by using carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a device for producing steam by using carbon dioxide.

PN CN206946112-U
TI Visible carbon dioxide laser device, has rotating base whose front end is connected with laser light, where adjusting visible laser is emitted by laser emission with carbon dioxide laser that is reflected by mirror.
AU LU X
   ZHU S
   LI Y
AE SHANGHAI KASU INTELLIGENT TECHNOLOGY CO (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201810987C
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a visible laser device suitable for carbon dioxide laser, comprising a rotating base, a visible laser and the laser output end of the turning base fixedly connected with the carbon dioxide laser. the visible laser and the mirror is adjustably set in the rotating base, the rotating base is provided with a front end connected with laser light, the visible light emitting point of the laser faces to the front end of the carbon dioxide laser of laser output end opposite to the back end; adjusting a visible laser or/and the visible visible laser emitted by the laser emitter and the carbon dioxide laser after the laser reflected by said mirror through said transmission beams overlap and emitted from the laser outlet, the technical solution solves the problem that the carbon dioxide laser dotting inconvenience in debugging, operation process and the technical problem of easy to burn the operator in the process of light.
DC P81 (Optics (G02).); V08 (Lasers and Masers)

PT P
PN CN111268670-A
TI Device useful for breaking and disassembling graphite under ultrasonic carbon dioxide suspension state of graphite powder, comprises sealing cover, stirring device, and kettle unit, which is fixed to ring-shaped sealing interface.
AU DONG L
   WANG Z
   DONG T
AE DONG T (DONG-Individual)
GA 202057764A
AB    NOVELTY - A device comprises a kettle unit, a sealing cover, and a stirring device. The kettle unit is fixed to a ring-shaped sealing interface by three layers of inner wall, middle wall and outer wall that are parallel to each other and curved at the bottom. A closed liquid nitrogen chamber is formed between the middle wall interval and the inner wall. A three-layer double-cavity barrel-shaped structure with a closed vacuum chamber is formed between the outer wall and the inner wall, where the internal space is a reaction chamber with an open upper port, and the center of the bottom surface of the reaction chamber is provided with a stable support with a funnel-shaped upper port. A discharge port is provided to set on the side of the stable support. A longitudinal liquid level detector is provided on the side wall of the reaction chamber. A liquid carbon dioxide input port is set near the upper edge.
   USE - The device is useful for breaking and disassembling graphite under ultrasonic carbon dioxide suspension state of graphite powder.
   ADVANTAGE - The device has high production efficiency, provides large output, and is low in cost, safe and environmentally-friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A device comprises a kettle unit, a sealing cover, and a stirring device. The kettle unit is fixed to a ring-shaped sealing interface by three layers of inner wall, middle wall and outer wall that are parallel to each other and curved at the bottom. A closed liquid nitrogen chamber is formed between the middle wall interval and the inner wall. A three-layer double-cavity barrel-shaped structure with a closed vacuum chamber is formed between the outer wall and the inner wall, where the internal space is a reaction chamber with an open upper port, and the center of the bottom surface of the reaction chamber is provided with a stable support with a funnel-shaped upper port. A discharge port is provided to set on the side of the stable support. A longitudinal liquid level detector is provided on the side wall of the reaction chamber. A liquid carbon dioxide input port is provided to set near the upper edge, and several ultrasonic probes protruding into the reaction chamber are provided near the bottom. A liquid nitrogen input port is provided in the upper portion of the liquid nitrogen chamber. A pressure gauge and a safety valve are provided between the liquid nitrogen input port and the sealed interface. The longitudinal liquid level detector is provided in the middle and upper portions of the side wall of the liquid nitrogen chamber. The sealing cover is composed of two mutually parallel dome arc pieces fixedly connected to the sealing interface matched with the kettle unit. A vacuum chamber is provided between the two dome arcs. A shaft hole of a stirring shaft of stirring device is arranged in the center of the dome. The pressure gauge and the safety valve of the reaction chamber are set on the side of the shaft hole. The stirring device consists of a motor, a stirring shaft and stirring blades. The stirring shaft passes through the shaft hole of the sealing cover. The stirring blades are installed at the lower end, which is inserted into the center of the funnel-shaped slot of the stable support. The vacuum chamber of the kettle unit and the sealing cover is a fully enclosed dead chamber, which is permanently isolated from the outside after vacuuming. The liquid nitrogen chamber is filled with liquid nitrogen at one time through a liquid nitrogen inlet to the control level of the liquid level detector before the device is applied. The liquid level detector reading is dynamically supplemented to maintain the liquid nitrogen capacity. During production, a high-mesh graphite powder and a liquid carbon dioxide are strictly and accurately measured into the reaction chamber at one time, the sealing cover is closed, the stirring device and ultrasonic generator are started successively to obtain the loss of liquid carbon dioxide volatilization through a barometer and a safety valve liquid level detector, and the liquid carbon dioxide is dynamically supplemented through a liquid carbon dioxide input port to maintain the stability of the liquid level in the reactor. The liquid carbon dioxide suspension of carbon atoms after dismantling is discharged several times through the discharge port. Regardless of the discharge method or intermittent multiple discharges, the carbon atoms and liquid carbon dioxide are fully mixed under the normal operation of the stirring device.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)
MC J04-X03
IP B01J-019/00; B01J-019/10; B01J-003/00; B01J-003/04; C01B-032/19

PN EP3819259-A1; WO2021089737-A1; CN114599635-A; KR2022098137-A; EP4054975-A1
TI Production of isocyanate and polyurethane for e.g.            foam, involves providing carbon dioxide gas through            reverse water gas shift reaction, converting to            isocyanate through phosgene, and reacting diisocyanate            with polyol to form polyurethane.
AU VOR D E L
   BULAN A
   WEBER R
AE COVESTRO DEUT AG (CVST-C)
   COVESTRO INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY GMBH & CO (CVST-C)
   COVESTRO INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY GMBH & CO (CVST-C)
GA 202149338W
AB    NOVELTY - Production of isocyanate and polyurethane                involves providing hydrogen stream (29a) and                feeding with the purified carbon dioxide gas stream                (31a) in a reverse water gas shift reaction zone                and converting (6) to a product gas mixture (39) of                water vapor (26b), carbon monoxide (21), separating                (7) water of the steam (26b) into the water                electrolysis (5), separating (8) unconverted carbon                dioxide (31b), recycling of the unconverted carbon                dioxide (31b) into the reaction (6), separating (9)                hydrogen (29a), recycling hydrogen (29c) into the                reaction (6), feeding the remaining carbon monoxide                (21a) from the separation (9) into the phosgene                synthesis (1), feeding of the hydrogen (29) from                the water electrolysis (5), with unreacted hydrogen                (29c), separating and purifying (11) hydrogen                chloride (25), oxidative converting (18) hydrogen                chloride in a thermocatalytic gas phase oxidation                (16) with oxygen (27) to form chlorine (22a) and                water (26), and feeding chlorine into the phosgene                synthesis.
   USE - Production of isocyanate and polyurethane used                for molded component, elastic fibers, polyurethane                varnish, polyurethane adhesive, and foam. Uses                include but are not limited to used in building,                cooling device, heat and cold storage, plastic                jacket composite pipe, flexible composite pipe,                steering wheel, arm rest, soft coating of handle,                interior trim, dashboard, sound insulation, rattle                protection, seal, and transparent coating of wood                decors.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables production of isocyanate                and polyurethane using reduced amount of fossil                material as starting material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Production of isocyanate and polyurethane                involves synthesizing (1) phosgene from carbon                monoxide (21a) and chlorine (22), reacting (2)                phosgene with diamines (23) to diisocyanates (24)                and hydrogen chloride (25), reacting (3)                diisocyanate with polyether polyol (35a) and/or                polyester polyol (35b) to form polyurethanes (37),                providing carbon dioxide gas flow (31), purifying                (4) stream (31) of secondary components, preferably                nitrogen oxide, sulfur compound, dust, water,                oxygen, and hydrochloric acid, optionally by                adsorption, gas scrubbing or catalytic treatment to                obtain a purified carbon dioxide (31a),                electrolyzing (5) water (26) to hydrogen (29) and                oxygen (27), providing hydrogen stream (29a) and                feeding together with the purified carbon dioxide                gas stream (31a) in a reverse water gas shift                reaction (RWGS) reaction zone and converting (6) of                the starting materials according to the RWGS                principle to a product gas mixture (39) of water                vapor (26b), carbon monoxide (21) and optionally                by-products (32), preferably methane, separating                (7) water of the steam (26b) from the product gas                mixture (39) and recycling of the water into the                water electrolysis (5), separating (8) unconverted                carbon dioxide (31b) from the gas mixture (39a) of                the RWGS reaction (6) obtained from the separation                (7), preferably by amine scrubbing, and recycling                of the unconverted carbon dioxide (31b) into the                reaction (6), separating (9) the hydrogen (29a) not                converted in the reaction (6) from the gas mixture                (39b) of carbon monoxide (21a) and hydrogen (29c)                obtained after the separation (8), preferably using                a cold box, and optionally recycling of the                hydrogen (29c) into the reaction (6) or feeding of                the hydrogen (29c) into the hydrogenation (34) of                dinitro compounds for the production of diamines                (23) as material for the diisocyanate (24), feeding                the remaining carbon monoxide (21a) from the                separation (9) into the phosgene synthesis (1),                feeding of the hydrogen (29) from the water                electrolysis (5), optionally together with                unreacted hydrogen (29c) from the RWGS reaction (6)                in the hydrogenation of nitro compounds for the                production (2) of diamines (23), separating and                purifying (11) hydrogen chloride (25), and                oxidative converting (18) hydrogen chloride in a                thermocatalytic gas phase oxidation (16) with                oxygen (27) to form chlorine (22a) and water (26),                and/or as electrochemical oxidizing (12) chloride                (25) to chlorine (22b), and/or as electrochemical                oxidizing (17) hydrogen chloride (25) to chlorine                (22c) and hydrogen (29) by hydrochloric acid                diaphragm electrolysis (17), feeding chlorine (22a,                22b, 22c) into the phosgene synthesis (1),                optionally adding fresh chlorine (22d) from a                chloralkali electrolysis (14).
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic overview of                illustrating production of isocyanate and                polyurethane.
   Synthesis of phosgene (1)
   Reaction of phosgene with diamine (2)
   Reaction of diisocyanate with polyether                polyol and/or polyester polyol (3)
   Purification of stream of secondary                components (4)
   Water electrolysis (5)
   Reverse water gas shift reaction (6)
   Separation of water of the steam (7)
   Separation of unconverted carbon dioxide                (8)
   Separation of hydrogen (9)
   Recycling of polyurethane material waste                (10)
   Purification of hydrogen chloride (11)
   Chloralkali electrolysis (14)
   Thermocatalytic gas phase oxidation                (16)
   Electrochemical oxidation of hydrogen                chloride (17)
   Conversion of hydrogen chloride (18)
   Carbon monoxide (21)
   Chlorine (22)
   Diamine (23)
   Diisocyanate (24)
   Hydrogen chloride (25)
   Water (26)
   Oxygen (27)
   Electrical power (28)
   Hydrogen (29)
   By-product (32)
   Polyurethane material waste (38)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The RWGS synthesis is                  performed using carbon dioxide which is provided                  from the recycling (10) of polyurethane material                  waste (38) by incineration (10b) and/or by                  pyrolysis (10a). The incineration is performed                  using gas with at least 30 vol.%, preferably at                  least 99.5 wt.% oxygen gas content, preferably                  using oxygen gas (27a) obtained from the water                  electrolysis (5). The electrolysis (5) and/or the                  electrochemical oxidation (12,17) is carried out                  using electrical power generated from                  regenerative energy, preferably from regenerative                  energy in the form of wind power, solar energy or                  water power, and is carried out using electrical                  current from fed-back energy, which is obtained                  from the incineration of polyurethane material                  waste and/or the implementation of the RWGS                  reaction. The RWGS reaction (6) is carried out by                  using electrical power (28) generated from                  regenerative energy, preferably electrical power                  optionally obtained through the use of wind                  power, solar energy, or water power. The heating                  of the RWGS reaction (6) is carried out by using                  energy fed back, which is obtained from the                  incineration of polyurethane material waste (38).                  The RWGS reaction (6) takes place by burning                  hydrocarbons of regenerative origin, preferably                  burning biomethane. The method involves recycling                  polyurethane material (37) after its use as                  polyurethane material waste (38), incinerating                  the waste (38) to carbon dioxide, and using                  carbon dioxide as an input material in the                  cleaning (4).

GA 2022C7674W
AB    NOVELTY - The system (10) has a combustion assembly (11)                operatively engaged with an air intake, where the                air intake performs air enrichment. The combustion                assembly is coupled to a separation assembly (14).                The separation assembly is connected to a                liquefaction assembly (16). A flow controller is                connected with a rich stream to a mixing assembly                to receive the rich stream and mix the stream with                air to provide enriched air to a burner of the                combustion assembly that is configured as a                combustion boiler assembly to utilize hydrocarbon                fuel. The air intake comprises a membrane assembly                to provide a nitrogen-rich stream and another rich                stream.
   USE - Combustion system for separating carbon                dioxide from combustion products and air i.e. flue                gas, from a combustion boiler in a building i.e.                large metropolitan area.
   ADVANTAGE - The system effectively separates the carbon                dioxide from the flue gas generated from the                combustion boiler within the building and provides                heat and power to the building. The system reduces                generation of the carbon dioxide in an effort to                address climate change.

   a method for enriching air to a combustion                assembly; and
   a system for separating carbon dioxide from                flue gas; and

   a method for separating carbon dioxide from                flue gas.

PN WO2020053752-A1; EP3849699-A1
TI Biomass-based activated carbon used as adsorbent for separating carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide from blast furnace gas, has porous structure and is prepared from olive stones and is impregnated with copper chloride.
AU PEVIDA GARCIA C
   RUBIERA GONZALEZ F
   ALVAREZ GUTIERREZ N
   REYES RODRIGUEZ J
AE ARCELORMITTAL (ARCM-C)
   ARCELORMITTAL (ARCM-C)
GA 202021991J
AB    NOVELTY - A biomass-based activated carbon having a porous structure is prepared from olive stones and is impregnated with copper chloride.
   USE - Biomass-based activated carbon used in adsorbent for separating carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide from gas such as blast furnace gas containing carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide and inert components such as nitrogen and hydrogen (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The activated carbon is prepared from inexpensive, easily accessible and widely available raw materials. The separation process is carried out easily and carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide can be recovered efficiently from the gas mixture.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) adsorbent for separation of carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide from a gas containing at least carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide and inert components such as nitrogen and hydrogen, which comprises the biomass-based activated carbon;
   (2) preparation of the biomass-based activated carbon, which involves supplying particles of olive stones, activating particles to obtain activated particles of olive stones, mixing activated particles of olive stones with dissolved copper chloride, and drying and heat treating mixture;
   (3) separation of carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide from the gas, which involves flowing gas through a carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide adsorbent layer comprising the biomass-based activated carbon; and
   (4) apparatus, which consists of at least one adsorption unit comprising at least carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide adsorbent layer arranged in a single layered bed.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide adsorbent layer, and the carbon dioxide adsorbent layer are arranged in a single layered bed. Preferred Properties: The particles of olive stones have size of 1-3 mm. The height of the carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide adsorbent layer and the height of the carbon dioxide adsorbent layer in the single layered bed are equal. Preferred Process: The activation process is carried out in a single activation procedure under carbon dioxide flow. The separation process involves flowing the gas through a carbon dioxide adsorbent and flowing through the carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide adsorbent layer in order under a vacuum swing separation method. The vacuum swing separation method is a three-step method involves pressurizing with feed, adsorption and evacuation, four-step method involves pressurizing with feed, adsorption, rinse and evacuation, or five-step method involves pressurizing with feed, adsorption, rinse, evacuation and purge. The rinsing step is carried out with a mixture of carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide.

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PT P
PN WO2018197047-A1; EP3614840-A1; US2020138036-A1
TI Fumigant formulation used as pesticide or biocide comprises a mixture of a fumigant active ingredient dissolved in supercritical carbon dioxide, which is acting as diluent for the active ingredient.
AU MIKLOS E
   BAYERL W
   HENKE S
   GARCIA ALBA L
   HUBER S
   GARCIA A L
AE LINDE AG (LINM-C)
   LINDE AG (LINM-C)
   LINDE AG (LINM-C)
GA 2018857039
AB    NOVELTY - Fumigant formulation comprises a mixture of a fumigant active ingredient and carbon dioxide, where the active ingredient is dissolved in supercritical carbon dioxide, which is acting as diluent for the active ingredient.
   USE - The formulation is useful as a pesticide or a biocide, and in the gaseous state is useful for treatment of infested matter and/or for pest treatment (all claimed). No biological data given.
   ADVANTAGE - The formulation: does not allow to change the concentration of the active ingredient when a portion of the mixture is withdrawn, where preferably the active ingredient will not be enriched with time; avoids, when ethyl formate is used as active ingredient, an increase of flammability due to enrichment of ethyl formate in carbon dioxide; does not damage crop by over-doses of fumigant since using constant concentration of fumigant; is user-friendly and easy to use; and does not require a sophisticated vaporizer to vaporize the mixture.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) providing a mixture of the active ingredient and carbon dioxide, comprising heating the mixture of the active ingredient and carbon dioxide in a closed container to a temperature at which the carbon dioxide becomes supercritical; and
   (2) a device for providing a mixture of the active ingredient and supercritical carbon dioxide comprising a container to receive the mixture of the active ingredient and carbon dioxide, where the container is in heat transfer contact with heating means, preferably with a heating jacket.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The container is heated by electrical means, preferably using a heating jacket.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The active ingredient is ethyl formate, 1,3-dichloropropene, dazomet (methyl isothiocyanate precursor), chloropicrin, iodoform, methyl isocyanate or sulfuryl fluoride. The mixture consists of 3-15 (preferably 5-10) vol.% ethyl formate dissolved in carbon dioxide. Preferred Method: The mixture of the active ingredient and carbon dioxide is heated to a temperature of greater than or equal to 31-55 degrees C, preferably 35-50 degrees C. The mixture of the active ingredient and carbon dioxide is heated until the active ingredient is completely dissolved in the supercritical carbon dioxide. The mixture of the active ingredient and carbon dioxide is heated in a container, preferably in a gas cylinder, and that the pressure of a portion of the heated mixture of the active ingredient and carbon dioxide is reduced and that the mixture is thus transferred into its gaseous state.
   ACTIVITY - Insecticide.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC C07 (Apparatus, formulation, general. including veterinary syringes, general formulations where the active compound is not central to the invention (e.g. wettable powders) and analysis.); C02 (Heterocyclic.); C03 (Other organic compounds, inorganic compounds and multicomponent mixtures. Polymers and proteins.)
