PN JP2018065068-A
TI Manufacture of adsorption agent used for removing carbon dioxide, involves preparing reaction liquid containing liquid phase comprising cerium solution, and liquid phase comprising basic solution, and generating cerium oxide in cerium phase.
AU AOSHIMA M
   SHIMAZAKI T
   NAKAMURA H
   YOSHIKAWA K
   KANAEDA M
AE HITACHI CHEM CO LTD (HITB-C)
GA 201833284V
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of adsorption agent (80) involves preparing a reaction liquid comprising a liquid phase (l1) comprising cerium solution containing tetravalent cerium, a liquid phase (l2) comprising basic solution, and an intermediate phase (l3) which separates the phase (l1) and phase (l2), and generating cerium oxide in the phase (l1).
   USE - Manufacture of adsorption agent used for removing carbon dioxide in air-conditioning target space of air conditioner (all claimed). Uses include but are not limited to manufacturing plant of foodstuff and chemical, motor vehicle, space station, midget submarine and building.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides adsorption agent having excellent carbon dioxide adsorption rate even when the content of carbon dioxide in the process target gas is low.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) removal of carbon dioxide, which involves contacting a process target gas containing carbon dioxide with the adsorption agent, such that carbon dioxide is adsorbed to the adsorption agent; and
   (2) air conditioner (100), which comprises a flow path (10) connected to an air-conditioning target space (R) containing the process target gas containing carbon dioxide, a removal unit (10c) which removes the carbon dioxide contained in the process target gas, and arranged at flow path, and the adsorption agent arranged at the removal unit.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the air conditioner.
   Flow path (10)
   Removal unit (10c)
   Adsorption agent (80)
   Air conditioner (100)
   Air-conditioning target space (R)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The cerium solution and basic solution are contacted with each other to form the intermediate phase. Preferred Composition: The basic solution is an aqueous ammonia solution. The content of carbon dioxide in the process target gas is 5000 ppm or less, preferably 1000 ppm or less. The cerium solution is an aqueous solution containing cerium(IV) hydroxide, cerium(IV) ammonium nitrate and/or cerium(IV) ammonium sulfate.

PT P
PN IN201911001838-A
TI Enrichment and recovery of hydrogen and carbon dioxide from low hydrogen bearing gas mixture, involves pressurizing adsorber column with feed gas mixture at preset pressure, and withdrawing hydrogen rich gas from adsorber column.
AU DASGUPTA S
   SWAPNIL D
   AARTI A
   GUPTA P
   NANOTI A
AE COUNCIL SCI & IND RES INDIA (COUI-C)
GA 2020876938
AB    NOVELTY - Enrichment and recovery of hydrogen (H2) and carbon dioxide (CO2) involves (i) pressurizing a adsorber column (C1) with a feed gas mixture at absolute pressure of 1.1-4.0 bar and at adsorber bed temperature of 15-55 degrees C, (ii) withdrawing the hydrogen rich gas from the adsorber column (C1) under back pressure control, (iii) terminating the feeding of the adsorber column with feed gas mixture and then equalizing the pressure of the adsorber column (C1) with adsorber column (C2) at pressure of 0.55-2 bar, (iv) purging adsorber column (C1) with the carbon dioxide rich evacuation product from a evacuation product storage vessel, (v) withdrawing void gases co-currently to the feed direction, (vi) drawing vacuum counter-currently to obtain a carbon dioxide rich product from the adsorber column, and (vii) pressure equalizing the adsorber column (C1) with adsorber column (C2) for next cycle.
   USE - Enrichment and recovery of hydrogen and carbon dioxide from low hydrogen bearing gas mixture of hydrogen, carbon monoxide (CO), carbon dioxide and methane-ethane (1-2C) hydrocarbon such as tail gas of pressure swing adsorption (PSA) plant for producing hydrogen and other chemical, and merchant application.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables enrichment and recovery of hydrogen and carbon dioxide with improved hydrogen economy of refinery, high productivity, and high purity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Enrichment and recovery of hydrogen (H2) and carbon dioxide (CO2) involves (i) pressurizing a adsorber column (C1) with a feed gas mixture at absolute pressure of 1.1-4.0 bar and at adsorber bed temperature of 15-55 degrees C, (ii) withdrawing the hydrogen rich gas from the adsorber column (C1) under back pressure control, (iii) terminating the feeding of the adsorber column with feed gas mixture and then equalizing the pressure of the adsorber column (C1) with adsorber column (C2) at pressure of 0.55-2 bar, (iv) purging adsorber column (C1) with the carbon dioxide rich evacuation product from a evacuation product storage vessel, (v) withdrawing void gases co-currently to the feed direction, (vi) drawing vacuum counter-currently to obtain a carbon dioxide rich product from the adsorber column, and (vii) pressure equalizing the adsorber column (C1) with adsorber column (C2) for next cycle. The hydrogen (H2) and carbon dioxide (CO2) are recovered from low hydrogen (H2) bearing gas mixture of hydrogen, carbon monoxide (CO), carbon dioxide and methane-ethane (1-2C) hydrocarbon.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The feed gas mixture composition comprises 25-40 mol% hydrogen, 45-65 mol% carbon dioxide, 5-30 mol% methane, 0-2 mol% carbon monoxide, and 0-2 mol% ethane hydrocarbons. The steps (i) and (ii) are performed by using 20-120% methane. Preferred Components: The adsorber columns (C1) and (C2) are identical with respect to adsorbent layer configuration comprising porous adsorbent chosen from zeolite, activated carbon, silica gel, alumina placed in a layered bed configuration. The hydrogen purity is enriched in 70-99.99 mol%, preferably 97-99.9 mol% at a recovery rate of 50-100 mol%, 83-92 mol% by fixing the pressurization of the gas mixture. The carbon dioxide purity is enriched in 82-99 mol%, preferably 98-99 mol% at a recovery rate of 85-95 mol%, preferably 85-91 mol% by fixing the pressurization of the gas mixture.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)
MC E11-Q01B; E11-Q02B; E31-A02C; E31-N04C; E31-N05C; E31-P02B; E31-P03; E34-C02; J01-E02; J01-E02B; J01-E03C; J01-E03D

PN CN114965898-A
TI Method for realizing remote monitoring of nitrogen            oxide and carbon dioxide discharged from heavy motor            vehicle, involves performing remote monitoring to            actual operation discharge of target heavy vehicle            according to comprehensive discharge data of nitrogen            oxide and carbon-dioxide.
AU SHEN X
   LU Y
   YANG Y
   FENG Q
   LIU B
AE BEIJING MUNICIPAL ECOLOGICAL & ENVIRONME (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 2022B4962P
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves collecting actual                operation data of a target heavy vehicle in a                preset period in real-time (S110). Discharge data                of nitrogen oxide and carbon-dioxide of the target                heavy vehicle is determined (S120) under different                working condition models using the actual operation                data. Comprehensive discharge data of the nitrogen                oxide of the target heavy vehicle is calculated                (S130) in the preset period according to the                discharge data of the nitrogen oxide under                different working condition models. Comprehensive                discharge data of carbon-dioxide of the target                heavy vehicle is calculated (S140) in the preset                period according to the discharge data of the                carbon-dioxide under different working condition                models. Remote monitoring is performed (S150) to                actual operation discharge of the target heavy                vehicle according to the comprehensive discharge                data of nitrogen oxide and the                carbon-dioxide.
   USE - Method for realizing remote monitoring of                nitrogen oxide and carbon-dioxide discharged from a                heavy motor vehicle.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables solving heavy vehicle                actual operation condition of nitrogen oxide and                carbon-dioxide emission standard monitoring so as                to realize performance evaluation of the heavy                vehicle in actual operation conditions, thus                realizing objective evaluation of energy-saving and                environment-friendly performance of the heavy                vehicle.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The actual operation data is included with                vehicle speed, atmospheric pressure, engine maximum                reference torque, engine net output torque,                friction torque, engine speed, engine fuel flow,                nitrogen oxide emission data output by a flow                sensor, intake air quantity read by the flow                sensor, reaction balance information, oil tank                liquid level, engine cooling liquid temperature and                latitude and longitude information.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating                a method for realizing remote monitoring of                nitrogen oxide and carbon-dioxide discharged from a                heavy motor vehicle. (Drawing includes non-English                language text).

TI Producing solid carbon and gaseous oxygen from carbon dioxide via electrolysis, comprises supplying electrolyte to chamber via inlet and discharging electrolyte from chamber via outlet with electrolyte flowing from inlet to outlet.
AU ZAVABETI A
   LI G
   BATTERHAM R J
   DRY R J
   DRY M J
AE CARBELEC PTY LTD (CARB-Non-standard)
GA 2022811169
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of solid carbon and gaseous oxygen from carbon dioxide involves using an electrolysis apparatus having a chamber (101) with an electrolyte inlet, an electrolyte outlet, a liquid electrolyte (105) containing carbon dioxide (103) in the chamber, at least one cathode-anode pair with the cathode (108) including a liquid metal (110) which is capable of catalyzing reduction of carbon dioxide to solid carbon, supplying the electrolyte to the chamber through the inlet and discharging the electrolyte from the chamber through the outlet with the electrolyte flowing from the inlet to the outlet in fluid communication with the cathode-anode pair, applying a voltage between the cathode-anode pair and causing solid carbon to form on the cathode from carbon dioxide in the electrolyte and evolving gaseous oxygen at the anode from carbon dioxide in the electrolyte.
   USE - Manufacture of solid carbon and gaseous oxygen used for manufacturing iron for manufacturing steel (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The process produces solid carbon and gaseous oxygen from carbon dioxide that is not subject to the cathode-fouling problem. The faradic efficiency will be similar to that of a typical water hydrogen electrolysis cell (around 70%).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Manufacture of solid carbon and gaseous oxygen from carbon dioxide involves using an electrolysis apparatus having a chamber (101) with an electrolyte inlet, an electrolyte outlet, a liquid electrolyte (105) containing carbon dioxide (103) in the chamber, at least one cathode-anode pair with the cathode (108) including a liquid metal (110) which is capable of catalyzing reduction of carbon dioxide to solid carbon, supplying the electrolyte to the chamber through the inlet and discharging the electrolyte from the chamber through the outlet with the electrolyte flowing from the inlet to the outlet in fluid communication with the cathode-anode pair, applying a voltage between the cathode-anode pair and causing solid carbon to form on the cathode from carbon dioxide in the electrolyte and evolving gaseous oxygen at the anode from carbon dioxide in the electrolyte, discharging solid carbon by transporting solid carbon from the cathode in the electrolyte to the electrolyte outlet and from the chamber through the outlet and discharging gaseous oxygen from the chamber. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) electrolysis apparatus; and
   (2) manufacture of iron.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the process and apparatus for manufacturing sold carbon and gaseous oxygen.
   Electrolysis chamber (101)
   Carbon dioxide (103)
   Electrolyte (105)
   Cathode (108)
   Liquid metal (110)

PN CN114388872-A
TI Flexible lithium-carbon dioxide soft pack battery comprises packaging material and battery core, where the packaging material is aluminum-plastic film, and battery core is sequentially provided with a metal lithium strip.
AU CHEN L
   WANG B
AE UNIV CHINA ELECTRONIC SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UEST-C)
GA 202269242M
AB    NOVELTY - Flexible lithium-carbon dioxide soft pack battery comprises a laminated structure obtained by sequentially stacking a metal lithium tape, a glass fiber separator, and a carbon cloth loaded with copper indium sulfur, and an aluminum-plastic film covered on the laminated structure. Through holes are uniformly distributed on the aluminum-plastic film on the contact surface with the copper indium sulfide-loaded carbon cloth, where the copper indium sulfide-loaded carbon cloth is used as a positive air electrode. The metal lithium strip is used as a negative electrode.
   USE - Flexible lithium-carbon dioxide soft pack battery.
   ADVANTAGE - The flexible lithium-carbon dioxide soft pack battery has a charging voltage as low as about 3.4V, a stable charge-discharge cycle of about 400 weeks, high efficiency and energy saving, simple material synthesis method, fast soft pack manufacturing process, low cost, and wide application prospects.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing flexible lithium-carbon dioxide soft pack battery, which involves heating the flexible base carbon cloth at 200-400degrees Celsius for 20-30 minutes, ultrasonically washing with acetone and water respectively for 0.5-1 hour, putting 5% potassium persulfate and 10% sulfuric acid solution, soaking at 60-80degrees Celsius for 8-10 hours, and finally ultrasonically washing with deionized water for 10-30 minutes, put into a blast drying oven at 60-80degrees Celsius for drying, mixing lithium bistrifluoromethanesulfonimide and tetraethylene glycol dimethyl ether in a glove box with both oxygen and water content below 1 ppm in a mass ratio of (1.8-3.0):(9.2-10.8) to form electrolyte. 90% copper indium sulfide powder and 10% polyvinylidene fluoride binder are added in N-methyl pyrrolidone solution to form a uniform slurry, applying the obtained slurry on carbon cloth to form an air electrode to obtain a positive electrode active material substrate, dissolving and reshaping the lithium sheet into a lithium tape with a length of 4-6 cm and a width of 0.5-1.5 cm in the glove box, cutting the glass fiber separator to an appropriate length and width, cutting carbon cloth with a length of 5- 7cm and a width of 1-2 cm, coating the copper indium sulfur active material, stacking the lithium tape and the glass fiber diaphragm in sequence, adding 400-800 microL electrolyte on the diaphragm contacting the carbon cloth end, putting the obtained positive active material substrate, finally putting the whole into the aluminum plastic film, sealing the aluminum-plastic film covered with holes on one side with transparent tape before putting into the package, placing the mentioned soft-pack battery to be packaged in an argon bag, taking out the glove box, quickly putting the whole of the soft-pack battery to be packaged into the hot-pressing splint room of the vacuum hot-pressing packaging machine to equip the soft-pack battery, and removing the transparent tape and placing in a pure carbon dioxide atmosphere to obtain low charging voltage flexible lithium-carbon dioxide pouch battery.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows an X-ray powder diffraction pattern of the prepared copper-indium-sulfur solid powder. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon cloth loaded with copper indium sulfur is a flexible carbon cloth with a thickness of 0.5-1 mm. The air electrode comprises flexible carbon cloth and copper indium sulfur.

PN CN114275736-A
TI Chemical chain carbon dioxide classification            regeneration process with red mud as oxygen carrier            involves reacting fuel with red mud oxygen carrier in            fuel reactor, so that red mud oxygen carrier is reduced            to solid red mud oxygen carrier.
AU LIU G
   MAO X
   ZHANG B
   YANG B
   ZHANG H
   WU Z
AE UNIV XIAN JIAOTONG (UYXJ-C)
GA 202259081H
AB    NOVELTY - Chemical chain carbon dioxide classification                regeneration process with red mud as oxygen carrier                involves reacting fuel with the red mud oxygen                carrier in the fuel reactor (R1), so that the red                mud oxygen carrier is reduced to the solid red mud                oxygen carrier, subjecting fuel to oxidation                reaction to generate carbon monoxide, hydrogen,                water, coke and carbon dioxide (CO2), reacting                solid red mud oxygen carrier and coke obtained from                CO2 in the primary regeneration reactor (R2) to                generate carbon monoxide and red mud oxygen                carrier.
   USE - Method for chemical chain carbon dioxide                classification regeneration process with red mud as                oxygen carrier.
   ADVANTAGE - The method adopts the CO2 classification                regeneration process and system, divides the                chemical chain regeneration process into two                portion, primary regeneration and complete                regeneration, realizes the conversion of CO2, and                realizes zero emission or negative emission of                CO2.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Chemical chain carbon dioxide classification                regeneration process with red mud as oxygen carrier                involves reacting fuel CmHn with the red mud oxygen                carrier Fe2O3-BR in the fuel reactor (R1), so that                the red mud oxygen carrier Fe2O3-BR is reduced to                the solid red mud oxygen carrier Fe/FeO-BR,                subjecting fuel to oxidation reaction to generate                carbon monoxide, hydrogen, water, coke and carbon                dioxide (CO2), reacting solid red mud oxygen                carrier Fe2O3-BR and coke obtained from CO2 in the                primary regeneration reactor (R2) to generate                carbon monoxide and red mud oxygen carrier                Fe3O4-BR, oxidizing oxygen carrier, regenerating                for the first time, reacting air or oxygen with the                regenerated red mud oxygen carrier in the complete                regeneration reactor (R3) to obtain the red mud                oxygen carrier Fe2O3-BR, regenerating oxygen                carrier by the second oxidation, and making the red                mud oxygen carrier Fe2O3-BR regenerated by the                second oxidation enters the fuel reactor (R1) for                the next cycle.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                chemical chain method CO2 classification                regeneration system with red mud as oxygen carrier,                which comprises: fuel reactor (R1), primary                regeneration reactor (R2), full regeneration                reactor (R3) and CO2 separation unit, where the gas                outlet of the fuel reactor (R1), the gas outlet of                the primary regeneration reactor (R2) and the gas                outlet of the complete regeneration reactor (R3)                are respectively connected with the inlet of the                CO2 separation device, the solid outlet of the fuel                reactor (R1) is connected to the inlet of the                primary regeneration reactor (R2), the solid outlet                of the primary regeneration reactor (R2) is                connected to the inlet of the complete regeneration                reactor (R3), the solid outlet of the complete                regeneration reactor (R3) is connected to the inlet                of the fuel reactor (R1), and the CO2 outlet of the                CO2 separation device is connected to the inlet of                the primary regeneration reactor (R2).
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                method for chemical chain carbon dioxide                classification regeneration process with red mud as                oxygen carrier. (Drawing includes non-English                language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The preparation method of                  the red mud oxygen carrier Fe2O3-BR involves                  pulverizing the industrial red mud, drying at                  110-150degrees Celsius for 12-24 hours, measuring                  Fe2O3 content by X-ray fluorescence spectroscopy                  (XRF) characterization method, if it is                  determined that Fe2O3 is greater than or equal to                  40 wt.%, calcining directly at 1200degrees                  Celsius for 6 hours, preparing red mud oxygen                  carrier Fe2O3-BR by grinding, screening to 60-80                  mesh, if it is measured that Fe2O3 is less than                  40 wt.%, modifying the red mud, loading the                  oxygen-carrying component Fe2O3 and the additive                  calcium oxide (CaO) by dispersing uniformly the                  red mud in deionized water with mass ratio of 1:4                  at room temperature to obtain a red mud aqueous                  solution, heating the obtained red mud aqueous                  solution to 85degrees Celsius, adding 3 mol/L                  sodium metasilicate solution to the red mud                  aqueous solution under magnetic stirring,                  stirring continuously to obtain a turbid liquid,                  where the volume ratio of hydrochloric acid                  solution and deionized water is 3:2, cooling the                  obtained turbid liquid to room temperature,                  dropping slowly aqueous ammonia under vigorous                  stirring to adjust its pH to 8, heating                  hydrothermally at 150degrees Celsius for 960                  minutes, performing ultrasonic treatment at a                  frequency of 40 kilohertz for 15 minutes,                  centrifuging at a speed of 10,000 revolutions per                  minute, centrifuging, washing with distilled                  water for multiple times until neutral to obtain                  a solid product, washing with anhydrous ethanol                  for 15 minutes to obtain a precipitate, drying                  the obtained precipitate at 110degrees Celsius                  for 12 hours, grinding to obtain a powder                  modified red mud, preparing specific different                  mass fractions of Fe2O3 and CaO of the red mud                  oxygen carrier, taking the mass of iron nitrate                  and calcium chloride, calculating, weighing,                  dissolving in absolute ethanol, preparing an                  ethanol solution by taking the modified red mud,                  dispersing the modified red mud in the prepared                  ethanol solution by dipping method, processing                  ultrasonically at a frequency of 40 kilohertz for                  20 minutes to make it uniformly dispersed, drying                  at 110-150degrees Celsius for 12-24 hours,                  calcining at 1200degrees Celsius for 12 hours,                  taking out, cooling to room temperature,                  subjecting to grinding and screening treatment,                  and screening to 60-80 mesh to prepare the red                  mud oxygen carrier Fe2O3-BR. Preferred                  Conditions: The fuel reactor (R1) is a                  countercurrent moving bed reactor, and the                  reaction temperature is 800-1000degrees Celsius.                  The gas-solid molar ratio of the fuel CmHn to the                  red mud oxygen carrier Fe2O3-BR is (1-10):1. The                  primary regeneration reactor (R2) adopts a riser                  reactor, and the reaction temperature is                  700-900degrees Celsius. The gas-solid molar ratio                  of the carbon monoxide to the solid red mud                  oxygen carrier Fe/FeO-BR is (0.2-5):1. The                  complete regeneration reactor (R3) adopts a fast                  circulating fluidized bed reactor, and the                  reaction temperature is 900-1100degrees Celsius.                  The mass content of iron oxide (Fe2O3) in the red                  mud oxygen carrier Fe2O3-BR is 40-75%.

PT P
PN CN113197934-A; CN113197934-B
TI Preparation of extract of radix Aquilinum leaf by placing dried and crushed black medicine leaves in supercritical extraction device for extraction, adding ethanol, extracting, eluting, and collecting eluate.
AU PENG X
   WANG J
   JI T
   SUN C
AE UNIV NINGBOTECH (NBLG-C)
GA 2021950307
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of extract of radix Aquilinum leaf comprises drying and pulverizing black medicine leaves, placing in extraction kettle in supercritical extraction device, using carbon dioxide as solvent, placing dried and crushed black medicine leaves in supercritical extraction device, and carrying out supercritical extraction; adding residue after supercritical extraction of volatile oil, adding ethanol containing hydrochloric acid to reflux and extract, extracting for 2-3 times, combining filtrates, adjusting pH with sodium hydroxide solution, filtering, collecting filtrate, concentrating, and dissolving with water to obtain crude extract; and loading crude extract, adding to macroporous resin column, using water and ethanol, washing, eluting with ethanol and ammonia-containing ethanol, and collecting combined eluate to obtain mixed extract of flavonoids and alkaloids.
   USE - The method is used for preparing extract of radix Aquilinum leaf.
   ADVANTAGE - The research shows that total alkaloid, total flavone and volatile oil and mixture have functions of promoting blood circulation and relieving pain. The method is simple. The quality controllability is strong. The extraction rate is high.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of extract of radix Aquilinum leaf comprises drying and pulverizing black medicine leaves at 30-40 degrees C, placing in extraction kettle in supercritical extraction device, using carbon dioxide as solvent, placing dried and crushed black medicine leaves in supercritical extraction device, and carrying out supercritical extraction of carbon dioxide by cooling carbon dioxide introduced by condenser until carbon dioxide is in liquid state, pumping carbon dioxide into extraction kettle through high-pressure pump, heating extraction kettle in supercritical extraction device, pressurizing, controlling flow of carbon dioxide at 30 L/hour, performing cyclic extraction at 22 plus minus 5 MPa and 40 plus minus 5 degrees C for 2-3 hours, carrying out two-stage separation in separation kettle where first-stage separation pressure is 4 plus minus 0.5 MPa and separation temperature is 40 plus minus 5 degrees C, and the second-stage separation pressure is 4 plus minus 0.5 MPa and the separation temperature is 35 plus minus 5 degrees C, taking yellow-green viscous extract, and removing water in extract to become volatile oil of black medicine leaf; adding residue after supercritical extraction of volatile oil of Wuyao leaves at material-to-liquid ratio of 1:10-15 g/mL, adding 80% ethanol containing 0.1 wt.% hydrochloric acid to reflux and extract for 1-2 hours at 60 degrees C, extracting for 2-3 times, combining filtrates, adjusting pH to 7.0 with sodium hydroxide solution, filtering, collecting filtrate, concentrating by rotary evaporation to volatilize ethanol, and dissolving with distilled water to obtain crude extract; and loading crude extract at flow rate of 2 BV/hour with concentration of sample solution at 2 mg/mL and pH of sample is 6.0, adding to macroporous resin column, using 2 BV water and 1 BV 20% ethanol, washing once each, eluting with 3 BV 60% ethanol and 2 BV 2% ammonia-containing 80% ethanol at flow rate of 2 BV/hour, and collecting combined eluate to obtain mixed extract of flavonoids and alkaloids.

PN CN108743721-A
TI Radix scrophulariae total phenol extract useful in preparing medicine having neuroprotective function, comprises an extract of phenolic component extracted from Chinese medicine radix scrophulariae.
AU CHEN N
   ZHONG Z
   ZHOU X
   LI L
   WU S
AE YAOSHENGTANG HUNAN PHARM CO LTD (YAOS-Non-standard)
GA 2018887436
AB    NOVELTY - Radix scrophulariae total phenol extract comprises an extract of phenolic component extracted from Chinese medicine radix scrophulariae. The extract includes total phenol content of more than 52.16%. The radix scrophulariae total phenol extract is prepared by: (i) slicing radix scrophulariae, adding 6-12 times amount of 30-60% ethanol, extracting 1-3 times for 0.5-3 hours every time, filtering and mixing the extracts; (ii) subjecting the mixed extract to ultrasonic treatment to obtain treated slurry; (iii) extracting the slurry by supercritical carbon dioxide, added with potassium lauryl sulfate, ethyl acetate and polysorbate (weight ratio of (1.6-2.3):(0.7-1.8): (1.2-2.8)), where the total amount of potassium lauryl sulfate, ethyl acetate and polysorbate is 18-25% of the carbon dioxide fluid, extracting to obtain a crude product-A, separating the crude-B, mixing the crude product-A and the crude product-B, eluting, concentrating and drying.
   USE - The radix scrophulariae total phenol extract is useful in preparing medicine having neuroprotective function (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Radix scrophulariae total phenol extract comprises an extract of phenolic component extracted from Chinese medicine radix scrophulariae. The extract includes total phenol content of more than 52.16%. The radix scrophulariae total phenol extract is prepared by: (i) slicing radix scrophulariae, adding 6-12 times amount of 30-60% ethanol, extracting 1-3 times for 0.5-3 hours every time, filtering and mixing the extracts; (ii) subjecting the mixed extract to ultrasonic treatment to obtain treated slurry; (iii) extracting the slurry by supercritical carbon dioxide, added with potassium lauryl sulfate, ethyl acetate and polysorbate (weight ratio of (1.6-2.3):(0.7-1.8): (1.2-2.8)), where the total amount of potassium lauryl sulfate, ethyl acetate and polysorbate is 18-25% of the carbon dioxide fluid, extracting at 50-56 degrees C for 2-3 hours with a pressure of 20-30 MPa and carbon dioxide flow rate of 20-25 l/hour to obtain a crude product-A, separating the crude-A from the slurry at 42-48 degrees C with a pressure of 8-10 MPa, where the remaining slurry is again extracted by supercritical carbon dioxide added with 10-16% methyl ethyl ketone and potassium lauryl sulfate (weight ratio of (1.2-2.6):(0.5-0.9)), extracting at 52-58 degrees C for 1.2-2 hour with a pressure of 25-35 MPa and carbon dioxide flow rate of 20-25 l/hour to obtain crude product-B, separating the crude-B from the slurry at 42-48 degrees C with a pressure of 8-10 MPa, mixing the crude product-A and the crude product-B at 42-48 degrees C, adjusting its pH to 3.6-4.8 using 8-15% hydrochloric acid, then centrifuging the separation liquid; and (iv) adsorbing the separation liquid via macroporous resin for more than 2 hours, eluting with water, discarding the water eluate to remove large polarity impurities, then eluting 50-70% ethanol, collecting the eluate, recovering ethanol, concentrating and drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The extract includes total phenol content of greater than or equal to 80%. The radix scrophulariae total phenol extract is used for preparing medicine having neuroprotective function. The dosage form of the medicine includes powder, dropping pill, tablet, hard capsule, soft capsule and/or granule. In the step (iii), the concentration of hydrochloric acid is 22-26%. The macroporous adsorption resin is D201 macroporous adsorption resin, AB-8 macroporous adsorption resin or D101 macroporous adsorption resin. Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the ultrasonic process is carried out with a frequency of 35-42 KHz for 6-9 minutes. In the step (iv), the total phenol extract of radix scrophulariae is prepared by eluting with ethanol, further purifying by n-butanol; and the drying process adopts vacuum drying, spray drying and/or microwave drying.
   ACTIVITY - Neuroprotective.

Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. PN CN108543502-A; CN108543502-B
TI Alkali reactor used for preparing ammonium chloride comprises actuator having telescopic rod, cylindrical baffle bracket, cylindrical baffle, and elastic mechanism, where upper end of telescopic rod is provided with conical plug.
AU LI C
AE DALIAN SHENGDUO EDUCATION CONSULTING CO (DALI-Non-standard)
   SHANDONG SANYUE CHEM CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201878503A
AB    NOVELTY - Alkali reactor comprises actuator having telescopic rod (1), cylindrical baffle bracket (2), cylindrical baffle (3), and elastic mechanism (4). The upper end of telescopic rod is provided with conical plug (5). A sodium chloride inlet pipe is connected to sodium chloride cavity. The middle of kettle body is provided with inverted cylindrical structure connected to outer wall of kettle body. A heating device is set below reaction chamber. The upper portion of inverted buckled cylindrical structure is provided with first passage comprising left side passage and right side passage. The left side passage is connected to carbon dioxide inlet pipe. The right side passage is provided with filtering device. A carbon dioxide inlet passage is connected to telescopic rod movable cavity. The bottom of telescopic rod movable cavity is connected to ammonium chloride outlet conduit.
   USE - The reactor is used for preparing ammonium chloride (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The reactor effectively controls reaction rate and can complete multi-step reaction in one device.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Alkali reactor comprises actuator having telescopic rod (1), cylindrical baffle bracket (2), cylindrical baffle (3), and elastic mechanism (4). The elastic mechanism is set below kettle body. The cylindrical baffle bracket is set on upper portion of elastic mechanism. The cylindrical baffle is set on upper portion of cylindrical baffle bracket. The lower end of telescopic rod is set below kettle body. The upper end of telescopic rod is provided with conical plug (5). A sodium chloride inlet pipe is connected to sodium chloride cavity. The middle of kettle body is provided with inverted cylindrical structure connected to outer wall of kettle body. A heating device is set below reaction chamber. The upper portion of inverted buckled cylindrical structure is provided with first passage which penetrates to reaction chamber and telescopic rod movable chamber. The first passage comprises left side passage and right side passage. The left side passage is connected to carbon dioxide inlet pipe. The right side passage is provided with filtering device. A carbon dioxide inlet passage is connected to telescopic rod movable cavity. The bottom of telescopic rod movable cavity is connected to ammonium chloride outlet conduit. The control method of reactor comprises placing aqueous solution of ammonia gas into reaction chamber, injecting sodium chloride solution into sodium chloride cavity, opening fourth valve, closing first valve, third valve, second valve, fifth valve and sixth valve, introducing carbon dioxide slowly into telescopic rod movable cavity to react with aqueous solution of ammonia gas in reaction chamber to form ammonium carbonate, closing fourth valve, compressing elastic mechanism to descend cylindrical baffle and let sodium chloride solution flow into reaction chamber to react ammonium carbonate with sodium chloride solution, opening first valve and fifth valve, filtering out ammonium chloride into telescopic rod movable cavity, discharging from ammonium chloride outlet, decomposing sodium bicarbonate by-product in reaction chamber by heating, and opening second valve and third valve to transfer carbon dioxide generate to carbon dioxide storage tank for use in next reaction.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows structural schematic diagram of alkali reactor.
   Telescopic rod (1)
   Cylindrical baffle bracket (2)
   Cylindrical baffle (3)
   Elastic mechanism (4)
   Conical plug (5)

PT P
PN GB2604047-A
TI Producing hydrogen gas and carbon dioxide gas            involves providing an electrolyser comprising anodic            compartment comprising aqueous solution of acid,            cathodic compartment, and middle compartment, applying            voltage between anodic and cathodic compartments.
AU BENTON D J
AE CARBON NEUTRAL PETROL LTD (CARB-Non-standard)
GA 2022A80089
AB    NOVELTY - Producing hydrogen gas and carbon dioxide gas                involves providing an electrolyser comprising an                anodic compartment comprising an aqueous solution                of acid, a cathodic compartment, and a middle                compartment. The cathodic and middle compartments                comprise an aqueous solution of first and second                electrolytes. The first electrolyte comprises a                metal carbonate. The anodic and cathodic                compartments are separated from the middle                compartment by first and second semi-permeable                membranes. Voltage is applied between the anodic                and cathodic compartments to generate oxygen gas in                the anodic compartment. Hydrogen ions from the                aqueous solution of acid pass through the first                semi-permeable membrane and enter the middle                compartment. Carbon dioxide gas and water in the                middle compartment are generated from the reaction                of carbonate or bicarbonate ions with the hydrogen                ions.
   USE - Method for producing hydrogen gas and carbon                dioxide gas in H2:CO2 ratio greater than 1,                optionally at least 3:1.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is able to lower the voltage                required for electrolysis to occur (i.e. the                voltage needed to decompose the carbonate) and so                minimize the electrical energy required by the                process. The presence of the second electrolyte in                the middle compartment advantageously allow the                carbonate to be decomposed (to form H2:CO2in a 1:1                ratio) by the available current without having any                serious effect on the conductivity that might                require a large use of electricity (i.e. an unduly                high voltage), leaving a proportion of the                available current to be used to decompose some                water in the cathodic compartment to make more                hydrogen, enabling hydrogen gas and carbon dioxide                gas to be produced in a H2:CO2ratio of greater than                one. The need for second electrolyser is omitted                which avoids the additional capital costs                associated with the running of a second                electrolyser.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing hydrogen gas and carbon dioxide gas                involves providing an electrolyser comprising an                anodic compartment comprising an aqueous solution                of acid, an anodic compartment comprising an                aqueous solution of acid, a cathodic compartment,                and a middle compartment. The cathodic and middle                compartments comprise an aqueous solution of first                and second electrolytes. The first electrolyte                comprises a metal carbonate. The anodic and                cathodic compartments are separated from the middle                compartment by first and second semi-permeable                membranes. Voltage is applied between the anodic                and cathodic compartments to generate oxygen gas in                the anodic compartment. Hydrogen ions from the                aqueous solution of acid pass through the first                semi-permeable membrane and enter the middle                compartment. Carbon dioxide gas and water in the                middle compartment are generated from the reaction                of carbonate or bicarbonate ions with the hydrogen                ions. The metal ions pass through the second                semi-permeable membrane and enter the cathodic                compartment. Hydrogen gas and hydroxide ions are                generated in the cathodic compartment. The water in                the cathodic compartment is decomposed to generate                additional hydrogen gas, once the carbonate or                bicarbonate ions in the middle compartment have                decomposed, by maintaining conductivity across the                middle compartment via the second                electrolyte.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method for producing                  hydrogen gas and carbon dioxide gas further                  involves providing a gas/liquid separator in                  fluid connection with the middle compartment,                  separating carbon dioxide and water in the                  gas/liquid separator, delivering the water to the                  cathodic compartment, recovering an aqueous                  solution comprising metal ions and hydroxide ions                  from the cathodic compartment, and reacting the                  aqueous solution comprising metal ions and                  hydroxide ions with carbon dioxide to form an                  aqueous solution comprising metal carbonate. The                  hydrogen gas and carbon dioxide gas are reacted                  in the presence of a catalyst to form methanol.                  The voltage between the anodic and cathodic                  compartments is applied using renewable                  electricity.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).)

PN WO2022018068-A1
TI Use of surface-reacted calcium carbonate as            stabilizing agent for probiotic composition e.g.            cosmetic probiotic composition, where surface-reacted            calcium carbonate is reaction product of natural ground            calcium carbonate or precipitated calcium carbonate            with carbon dioxide and hydronium ion donors.
AU SHARMA L
   BUDDE T
AE OMYA INT AG (OMYA-C)
GA 202216427E
AB    NOVELTY - Use of surface-reacted calcium carbonate as                stabilizing agent for a probiotic composition is                claimed, where the surface-reacted calcium                carbonate is a reaction product of natural ground                calcium carbonate or precipitated calcium carbonate                with carbon dioxide and one or more hydronium ion                donors, and the carbon dioxide is formed in situ by                the hydronium ion donors treatment and/or is                supplied from an external source.
   USE - The surface-reacted calcium carbonate is used                as stabilizing agent for a probiotic composition,                preferably a pharmaceutical probiotic composition,                a nutritional probiotic composition, or a cosmetic                probiotic composition, and/or a tablet, a capsule,                a chewable tablet, a chewable gum, a chewable                pastille, a lozenge, a powder, a granulate, a                pellet, a paste, a cream, a food, a feed, or a                beverage, a dry stabilized probiotic composition,                and in a product, and in pharmaceutical,                nutritional or cosmetic applications (all claimed),                and for special dietary requirements, such as                infants, young children, elderly people, or                diabetic patients, and for plant milk beverages or                fermented plant milk beverages are oat milk, rice                milk, soy milk, almond milk, hemp milk, coconut                milk, lupine milk, pea milk, barley milk, or                hazelnut milk, and cosmetic probiotic compositions                are probiotic moisturizer, probiotic cream,                probiotic gel, probiotic serum, probiotic shower                gel, probiotic soap, probiotic shampoo, probiotic                face wash, probiotic skin mask, or probiotic skin                spray.
   ADVANTAGE - The surface-reacted calcium carbonate reduces                or prevents degradation of probiotic microorganisms                during drying of a probiotic composition, extends                shelf-life, is environmentally safe, and easy to                degradable.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a method for stabilizing a probiotic                microorganism culture, comprising mixing a                probiotic microorganism culture with a                surface-reacted calcium carbonate in an aqueous                medium, where the surface-reacted calcium carbonate                is a reaction product of natural ground calcium                carbonate or precipitated calcium carbonate with                carbon dioxide and hydronium ion donors, the carbon                dioxide is formed in situ by the hydronium ion                donors treatment and/or is supplied from an                external source, and drying the obtained mixture,                preferably carrying-out the drying by spray drying,                freeze drying, spray freeze drying, flash drying,                fluidized bed drying, jet drying, vacuum drying, or                their combination; and
   (2) a process for preparing a dry stabilized                probiotic composition comprising (i) providing an                aqueous probiotic composition comprising the                probiotic microorganism culture, (ii) providing                10-30 wt.% aqueous suspension of surface-reacted                calcium carbonate, where the surface-reacted                calcium carbonate is a reaction product of natural                ground calcium carbonate or precipitated calcium                carbonate with carbon dioxide and hydronium ion                donors chosen from hydrochloric acid, sulfuric                acid, sulfurous acid, phosphoric acid, oxalic acid,                or their mixtures, the carbon dioxide is formed in                situ by the hydronium ion donors treatment and/or                is supplied from an external source, (c) mixing the                probiotic composition of step (a) and the                surface-reacted calcium carbonate of step (b), and                (d) spray drying the mixture obtained in step (c)                at an inlet temperature of 130-210 degrees C and an                outlet temperature of 50-130 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Microorganisms: The probiotic                  composition comprises a probiotic microorganism                  culture chosen from Bifidobacterium adolescentis,                  B.lactis, B.infantis, B.longum, B.bifidum,                  B.breve, Lactobacillus acidophilus, L.johnsonii,                  L.casei, L.reuteri, L.rhamnosus, L.plantarum,                  L.lactis, Enterococcus faecium, Escherichia coli                  Nissle 1917, E.coli criodesiccata(083:K24:H31),                  Saccharomyces boulardii, S.cerevisiae, and/or                  their mixtures. Preferred Composition: The                  probiotic composition comprises at least 50 wt.%,                  preferably 95 wt.% probiotic microorganism                  culture. The probiotic composition is a dry                  composition or an aqueous suspension, and                  preferably the probiotic composition is a dry                  composition.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The natural ground                  calcium carbonate is chosen from marble, chalk,                  limestone, and/or their mixtures. The                  precipitated calcium carbonate is chosen from                  precipitated calcium carbonates having an                  aragonitic, vateritic or calcitic crystal form                  and their mixtures, and/or the hydronium ion                  donor is chosen from hydrochloric acid, sulfuric                  acid, sulfurous acid, phosphoric acid, citric                  acid, oxalic acid, an acidic salt, acetic acid,                  formic acid and their mixtures, preferably                  hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid, sulfurous acid,                  phosphoric acid, oxalic acid and phosphoric acid,                  being at least partially neutralized by a cation                  chosen from lithium ion, sodium ion and/or                  potassium ion, phosphoric acid is at least                  partially neutralized by a cation chosen from                  lithium ion, sodium ion and/or potassium ion,                  magnesium ion, and/or calcium ion. The hydronium                  ion donor is phosphoric acid. The weight ratio of                  probiotic microorganism culture, and                  surface-reacted calcium carbonate is 5:95-40:60,                  preferably 20:80-25:75. The stabilizing agent is                  a drying stabilizer and/or a shelf live                  preservative. The concentration of viable                  probiotic microorganism culture after drying of                  the probiotic composition is increased by at                  least 5%, preferably at least 20%, compared to a                  probiotic composition comprising maltodextrin as                  stabilizing agent. Preferred Properties: The                  surface-reacted calcium carbonate has a volume                  median particle size of 0.1-75 mu m, preferably                  1.5-15 mu m, and/or a volume top cut particle                  size of 0.2-150 mu m, preferably 3-30 mu m,                  and/or a specific surface area of 15-200 m2/g,                  preferably 30-100 m2/g, measured using nitrogen                  and the BET method, and/or an intra-particle                  intruded specific pore volume of 0.1-2.3 cm3/g,                  preferably 0.35-1.6 cm3/g, calculated from                  mercury porosimetry measurement.
DC B07 (General - tablets, dispensers, catheters (excluding drainage and angioplasty), encapsulation etc, but not systems for administration of blood or saline or IV feeding etc.); D21 (Preparations for dental or toilet purposes - including filling alloys, compositions for dentures or dental impressions, anti-caries chewing gum, plaque disclosing compositions, toothpastes, cosmetics, shampoos, topical anti-sunburn compositions and toilet soaps (A61K).); D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); ; E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.)

PT P
PN CN112517012-A; CN112517012-B
TI Preparing catalyst for preparing carbon dioxide            hydrogenation to methanol comprises e.g. uniformly            mixing pseudo-boehmite and ethanol solution of            1,3,5-trimellitic acid to form suspension, heating            suspension to remove solvent, and grinding.
AU ZHANG Y
   QITIAN Q
   CHEN S
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 2021316018
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing catalyst for preparing carbon                dioxide hydrogenation to methanol comprises (1)                uniformly mixing pseudo-boehmite and the ethanol                solution of 1,3,5-trimellitic acid to form                suspension, where the mass ratio of trimesic acid                and pseudo-boehmite is 9-125, and the concentration                of trimellitic acid in ethanol solution is 0.1-4                mol/l, (2) heating the suspension in the step (1)                to remove the solvent, and grinding the solid to                obtain modified pseudo-boehmite, (3) adding                modified pseudo-boehmite obtained in the step (2)                to the ethanol solution of copper nitrate and zinc                nitrate and stirring, and obtaining blue                precipitate after allowing to stand, where the                molar ratio of copper nitrate and zinc nitrate is                7.86:(0.62-4.91), and the molar ratio of copper                nitrate, zinc nitrate and pseudo-boehmite used is                7.86:(0.62-4.91):(0-3.79), and (4) subjecting blue                precipitate in the step (3) to hydrothermal                treatment to obtain precursor precipitate.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing catalyst                for preparing carbon dioxide hydrogenation to                methanol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is easy to control, and                convenient; has simple preparation process;                recycles the mother liquor after washing and used                as the re-synthesized stock solution; and reduces                the loss of raw materials.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing catalyst for preparing carbon                dioxide hydrogenation to methanol comprises (1)                uniformly mixing pseudo-boehmite and the ethanol                solution of 1,3,5-trimellitic acid to form                suspension, where the mass ratio of trimesic acid                and pseudo-boehmite is 9-125, and the concentration                of trimellitic acid in ethanol solution is 0.1-4                mol/l, (2) heating the suspension in the step (1)                to remove the solvent, and grinding the solid to                obtain modified pseudo-boehmite, (3) adding                modified pseudo-boehmite obtained in the step (2)                to the ethanol solution of copper nitrate and zinc                nitrate and stirring, and obtaining blue                precipitate after allowing to stand, where the                molar ratio of copper nitrate and zinc nitrate is                7.86:(0.62-4.91), and the molar ratio of copper                nitrate, zinc nitrate and pseudo-boehmite used is                7.86:(0.62-4.91):(0-3.79), (4) subjecting blue                precipitate in the step (3) to hydrothermal                treatment to obtain precursor precipitate, where                the hydrothermal conditions hydrothermal time at                hydrothermal temperature of 80-150 degrees C for                12-48 hours, (5) subjecting precursor precipitate                in the step (4) to suction filtration, washing,                drying, roasting, tableting and sieving to prepare                catalyst precursor, (6) reducing catalyst precursor                in the step (5) under certain ratio of                hydrogen/argon mixed gas to obtain catalyst for                carbon dioxide hydrogenation to methanol, and (7)                using filtrate obtained in the step (5) as the                stock solution in the step (3) in the re-synthesis.                An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for methanol                catalyst prepared by the preparation method is used                for the reaction of carbon dioxide hydrogenation to                methanol, comprises (1) reaction temperature of                carbon dioxide hydrogenation to methanol in 200-300                degrees C, (2) reaction weight hourly space                velocity is 7200-28800 mlg-1/hour, (3) reaction                pressure is 2-4 MPa, and (4) hydrogen to carbon                ratio of the mixed gas is 2-4.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (2), the                  heating conditions are the temperature is 70-100                  degrees C for 3-8 hours. In the step (5), the                  number of washings in the suction filtration                  process is 3-6 times, the drying temperature is                  60-90, the roasting temperature is 300-600                  degrees C, and the sieving mesh number is 40-60                  mesh. In the step (6), the conditions for                  reduction in mixed atmosphere of hydrogen and                  argon with volume ratio of 5-20%, the reduction                  temperature is controlled to be 100-400 degrees                  C. Preferred Components: In the step (3), the                  total concentration of copper ion and zinc ion is                  0.1-50 mol/l. The ethanol solution is replaced                  with mixed solution of water and ethanol with                  volume ratio of 1:1.

PT P
PN CN109112467-A; CN109112467-B
TI Soft nitriding treatment process useful for fixing ring comprises e.g. immersing retaining ring in ethanol solution, placing fixing ring and tooling in heating furnace, introducing nitrogen, heating, introducing ammonia and carbon dioxide.
AU YE Z
AE WENLING DASHAN HEAT TREATMENT FACTORY (WENL-Non-standard)
   WENLING DASHAN HEAT TREATMENT FACTORY (WENL-Non-standard)
GA 2019036864
AB    NOVELTY - Soft nitriding treatment process for fixing ring comprises e.g. (i) immersing a retaining ring in 75% ethanol solution for 0.1-0.2 hours to remove oil stain on the surface of the retaining ring, (ii) evenly placing a fixing ring and a tooling, and placing the tooling and the fixing ring together in a heating furnace to seal the heating furnace, (iii) continuously introducing nitrogen into the heating furnace in an amount of 100-120 l/ hour, until the air in the heating furnace is completely discharged, (iv) heating the furnace until the temperature in the furnace reaches 540-550 degrees C, then introducing ammonia gas and carbon dioxide, where the amount of ammonia gas is 2000 plus minus 10 l/hour and the amount of carbon dioxide is 5 plus minus 0.5 l/hour, stopping heating after the temperature reaches 580-590 degrees C, keeping ammonia gas and carbon dioxide unchanged, and keeping the temperature for 3-4 hours, (v) taking out the fixing ring from heating furnace, and quickly cooling the fixing ring and (vi) cooling.
   USE - The process is useful for fixing ring.
   ADVANTAGE - The tool improves the quality and gloss of the fixed ring after treatment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Soft nitriding treatment process for fixing ring comprises (i) immersing a retaining ring in 75% ethanol solution for 0.1-0.2 hours to remove oil stain on the surface of the retaining ring, (ii) evenly placing a fixing ring and a tooling, and placing the tooling and the fixing ring together in a heating furnace to seal the heating furnace, (iii) continuously introducing nitrogen into the heating furnace in an amount of 100-120 l/ hour, until the air in the heating furnace is completely discharged, (iv) heating the furnace until the temperature in the furnace reaches 540-550 degrees C, then introducing ammonia gas and carbon dioxide, where the amount of ammonia gas is 2000 plus minus 10 l/hour and the amount of carbon dioxide is 5 plus minus 0.5 l/hour, stopping heating after the temperature reaches 580-590 degrees C, keeping the ammonia gas and carbon dioxide unchanged, and keeping the temperature for 3-4 hours, (v) taking out the fixing ring from the heating furnace, and quickly cooling the fixing ring, (vi) cooling the heating furnace to 550-570 degrees C, reinstalling the cooled fixed ring, and continuously supplying steam into the heating furnace for 2-2.5 hours, and (vii) removing the retaining ring from the furnace and cooling. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for fixing ring soft nitriding processing tool, comprising multiple bottom frame (1), a top frame (2), where the outer edges of the top frame and a support rod (3) are set between multiple trays (4) uniformly set along the height direction of the support rods, and the multiple trays are located on the bottom frame between the top frame.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic representation of the soft nitriding processing tool.
   Bottom frame (1)
   Top frame (2)
   Support rod (3)
   Tray (4)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), citric acid solution is added to the ethanol solution. In the step (ii), the fixing ring is immersed in the treatment liquid for 0.2-0.3 hours before the fixed ring furnace is installed, and the treatment is performed, and the liquid is a mixed solution of salicylic acid and glycolic acid. In the step (iv), the decomposition rate of ammonia gas is 20-30%. In the step (v), cooling method is air cooling.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Method: In the step (vi), the air pressure in the heating furnace is 0.7 plus minus 0.1 MPa. In the step (vii), cooling method is air cooling.
DC M13 (Coating material with metals, diffusion processes, enamelling and vitreous coatings - including coating from liquid metal or solution, spraying, cementation, cathodic sputtering, enamelling and oilfree lubricant coatings, but not coatings for the production of semiconductors (C23C, D).)

PN CN113074381-A
TI Dry method carbon dioxide trapping low-temperature waste heat generating system, has heat energy comprehensive collecting unit utilized for collecting heat energy in dry carbon dioxide collecting system unit.
AU LIU X
   ZHU R
AE JIANGSU JINGNENG ZERO CARBON TECHNOLOGY (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021850311
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a dry collecting carbon dioxide system unit provided with a secondary cooler (1), a primary cooler (2), a carbon dioxide dust remover (3), a regeneration tower (4), an absorption tower (5), a cyclone separator (6), a bag-type dust remover (7), a cooler (12) and a discharging chute (14). A heat energy comprehensive collecting unit is utilized for collecting heat energy in the dry collecting carbon dioxide system unit. The heat utilization unit utilizes the heat collected by the heat energy comprehensive collecting unit in the system.
   USE - Dry method carbon dioxide trapping low-temperature waste heat generating system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system can systematically use the heat of the reaction in the CO2 capturing process and the step utilization of the residual heat resource in the CO2 absorbing agent regeneration process. The circulating cooling water which needs extra energy consumption is replaced by the circulating heat working substance. The heat energy grade of the heat working substance is improved as much as possible. The obtained heat is used for low-temperature working medium power generation, reducing the energy consumption of the original system, at the same time, generating the effect of the energy product.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a circuit diagram of a dry method carbon dioxide trapping low-temperature waste heat generating system. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   Secondary cooler (1)
   Primary cooler (2)
   Carbon dioxide dust remover (3)
   Regeneration tower (4)
   Absorption tower (5)
   Cyclone separator (6)
   Bag-type dust remover (7)
   Cooler (12)
   Discharging chute (14)

PN US2022306468-A1; WO2022213054-A1
TI Producing hydrogen-enriched product and recovering            carbon dioxide from hydrogen production process unit            used as clean-burning fuel, involves processing feed            stream comprising hydrocarbons or carbonaceous            feedstock in hydrogen production process unit to obtain            synthesis gas mixture including hydrogen.
AU KONDA N V S
   KAYODE O
   CUELLAR K
   BIETTO S
   KRAUS K
   BENNETT E J
   CRUZ A
   WEXLER J T
   RUSSELL B
   CARTER E
   CADY W
   VENKATA S M
AE UOP LLC (UNVO-C)
   UOP LLC (UNVO-C)
GA 2022C44741
AB    NOVELTY - Producing hydrogen-enriched product and                recovering carbon dioxide from hydrogen production                process unit involves processing a feed stream                comprising hydrocarbons or a carbonaceous feedstock                in a hydrogen production process unit to produce a                synthesis gas mixture comprising hydrogen, carbon                dioxide, water, and at least one of methane, carbon                monoxide, nitrogen, and argon, separating an                effluent stream from the hydrogen production                process unit in a first pressure swing adsorption                (PSA) system that produces at least two product                streams into at least a first high-pressure                hydrogen stream enriched in hydrogen and a hydrogen                depleted tail gas stream, and compressing the                hydrogen depleted tail gas stream in a compressor                to form a compressed tail gas stream.
   USE - Method for producing hydrogen-enriched product                and recovering carbon dioxide from hydrogen                production process unit used as clean-burning                fuel.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has improved hydrogen production                processes with increased hydrogen production and                carbon dioxide recovery, cost-effective, reduced                net carbon dioxide emissions, and avoided                contamination of the carbon dioxide liquid product                with hydrocarbons.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing hydrogen-enriched product and                recovering carbon dioxide from hydrogen production                process unit involves processing a feed stream                comprising hydrocarbons or a carbonaceous feedstock                in a hydrogen production process unit to produce a                synthesis gas mixture comprising hydrogen, carbon                dioxide, water, and at least one of methane, carbon                monoxide, nitrogen, and argon, separating an                effluent stream from the hydrogen production                process unit in a first pressure swing adsorption                (PSA) system that produces at least two product                streams into at least a first high-pressure                hydrogen stream enriched in hydrogen and a hydrogen                depleted tail gas stream, compressing the hydrogen                depleted tail gas stream in a compressor to form a                compressed tail gas stream, separating the                compressed tail gas stream in a carbon dioxide                recovery system producing a carbon dioxide-enriched                product stream and a second gas stream comprising                hydrogen, introducing at least a portion of a purge                gas stream comprising hydrogen from a bio-renewable                conversion process for making a hydrocarbon product                from a bio-renewable feedstock into a process unit                used in the method of producing hydrogen, and                recovering carbon dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                three-product PSA unit for use in the PSA                system.
   310PSA adsorption vessel
   330First opening
   335First end
   340Second opening
   345Second end
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method for producing                  hydrogen-enriched product and recovering carbon                  dioxide from hydrogen production process unit                  further involves separating the purge gas stream                  comprising hydrogen from the bio-renewable                  conversion process in a membrane separation unit                  into a permeate stream comprising hydrogen and                  carbon dioxide (CO2), and a residue stream                  comprising at least one of methane, ethane,                  propane, C4+ hydrocarbons, and carbon monoxide,                  and where the process unit comprises the                  compressor, and the permeate stream is compressed                  in the compressor.

TI Covalently bonded titanium dioxide doped covalent            triazine framework heterojunction material used in            photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction reaction,            comprises titanium dioxide nanosheets and triazine            frame, where titanium dioxide nanosheets are wrapped            inside triazine frame.
AU LI H
   XU Z
AE UNIV SOOCHOW (USWZ-C)
   UNIV SOOCHOW (USWZ-C)
GA 202180032R
AB    NOVELTY - Covalently bonded titanium dioxide doped                covalent triazine framework (CTF-Py) heterojunction                material comprises titanium dioxide nanosheets and                triazine frame, where the titanium dioxide                nanosheets are wrapped inside the triazine frame                and titanium dioxide nanosheets forms covalent bond                with the triazine framework.
   USE - The heterojunction material is used in the                photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction reaction                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The heterojunction material catalyzes the                carbon dioxide reduction reaction under light                conditions, has high activity and high selectivity,                is environmentally-friendly and mild and remains                stable.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a method for preparing covalently bonded                titanium dioxide doped covalent triazine framework                heterojunction material involving adding                amine-titanium dioxide, 2,6-pyridine dimethanol,                terephthalamidine and cesium carbonate to an                organic solvent, and reacting to obtain titanium                dioxide doped covalent triazine framework                heterojunction material;
   (2) use of heterojunction material in the                photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction reaction;                and
   (3) a method for photocatalytic carbon dioxide                reduction reaction involving (i) adding                amine-titanium dioxide, 2,6-pyridine dimethanol,                terephthalamidine and cesium carbonate to an                organic solvent, and reacting to obtain titanium                dioxide doped covalent triazine framework                heterojunction material and (ii) mixing the                titanium dioxide doped covalent triazine framework                heterojunction material, metal salt, and water in                the step (i), completing the photocatalytic carbon                dioxide reduction reaction by light reaction in a                carbon dioxide atmosphere.
EA    (WO2022217521-A1)   NOVELTY - Titanium dioxide-covalently bonded triazine                framework (CTF-Py) heterojunction material                comprises titanium dioxide nanosheets and triazine                frame, where the titanium dioxide nanosheets are                wrapped inside the triazine frame. The titanium                dioxide nanosheets form covalent bonds with the                triazine frame.
   USE - The heterojunction material is useful in                photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction reaction                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The heterojunction material has improved                photocatalytic activity; high nitrogen content; and                high carbon dioxide adsorption capacity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic solvent                  comprises dimethyl sulfoxide. The amine-titanium                  dioxide comprises titanium dioxide with amino                  groups on the surface. The metal salt comprises                  aqueous-soluble metal chloride salt. Preferred                  Composition: The dosage ratio of amine-titanium                  dioxide, 2,6-pyridine dimethanol,                  terephthalamidine and cesium carbonate is 20-100                  mg:0.5 mmol:1 mmol:2.2 mmol. Preferred Method:                  The method involves adding amine-titanium dioxide                  to the organic solvent, adding portion of                  2,6-pyridine dimethanol, portion of                  terephthalamide and portion of cesium carbonate,                  reacting, adding the remaining 2,6-pyridine                  dimethanol, terephthalamidine, and cesium                  carbonate and using second reaction to obtain the                  heterojunction material. Preferred Conditions:                  The temperature of the first reaction is                  controlled at 90-110 degrees C for 10-15 hours                  and the second reaction is 115-125 degrees C for                  10-15 hours and 150-170 degrees C for 40-55                  hours, where the portion is 1/5 of the reaction                  amount.The temperature of light irradiation                  reaction is normal temperature.
   EXAMPLE - 60 mg Amine-titanium dioxide was dispersed in                30 ml dimethyl sulfoxide solution and sonicated for                30 min to form homogeneous suspension. Then, 1/5                part of 0.1 mmol 2,6-pyridinedimethanol, 0.2 mmol                terephthalamide and 0.44 mmol cesium carbonate were                added and reacted at 100&#176; C for 12 hours                to obtain prepolymer. Then, the remaining amount of                0.4 mmol 2,6-pyridinedimethanol, 0.8 mmol                terephthalamide, and 1.76 mmol cesium carbonate                were added to the reaction system and reacted at                120&#176; C for 12 hours and then heated to                160&#176; C for 48 hours. After the reaction                was completed, centrifuged and washed three times                with dilute hydrochloric acid, water, ethanol and                tetrahydrofuran in turn, and then vacuum-dried to                obtain titanium dioxide-covalently bonded triazine                framework heterojunction material.

PN CN110748341-A
TI Method for detecting the radius of influence of a            controllable seismic source based on carbon dioxide            blasting, involves construction of drilling at a            designated position on the cross section of a roadway            of a coal seam.
AU CAO Y
   ZHANG J
   TIAN L
   JIA M
   SHI B
   YANG B
AE UNIV HENAN POLYTECHNIC (UYHP-C)
   HENAN SHENHUA ENERGY ENG CO LTD (HENA-Non-standard)
GA 202012223U
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves construction of drilling                at a designated position on the cross section of a                roadway of a coal seam. The azimuth of the borehole                is equal to the azimuth of the tunneling direction.                The relationship is determined between the serial                length of the carbon dioxide blasting tube and the                drilling depth. A certain number of carbon dioxide                blasting tubes is pushed into the borehole in                series. A certain number of components is arranged                on the vertical ground corresponding to the                borehole according to a certain layout and                distance. The blasting tube detonates the preset                carbon dioxide blasting tube and takes data. The                impact range of the carbon dioxide blasting source                is calculated.
   USE - Method for detecting the radius of influence                of a controllable seismic source based on carbon                dioxide blasting.
   ADVANTAGE - Improves the practicality and reliability of                the method for detecting the radius of influence of                the controllable seismic source based on carbon                dioxide blasting, reduces the impact of accidental                errors, and guarantees the safety of the sealing                process and the safety of other subsequent                operations.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart of a method for                detecting the radius of influence of a controllable                seismic source based on carbon dioxide blasting.                (Drawing includes non-English language                text).
