PN CN110404425-A; CN110404425-B
TI Graft type ionic liquid polyimide film used in            carbon dioxide gas separation, used for separating the            mixed gas system of carbon dioxide/nitrogen, carbon            dioxide/methane and carbondioxide/oxygen.
AU LIU H
   BEI P
   YAO H
   GAO Y
   ZHANG Y
AE UNIV SHENYANG TECHNOLOGY (UYTE-C)
GA 201994373F
AB    NOVELTY - Graft type ionic liquid polyimide film is                represented by (I).
   USE - Graft type ionic liquid polyimide film used in                carbon dioxide gas separation, used for separating                the mixed gas system of carbon dioxide/nitrogen,                carbon dioxide/methane and carbondioxide/oxygen                (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The graft type ionic liquid polyimide film has                increases the carbon dioxide permeation flux, and                has improves selectivity of carbon dioxide                gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Graft type ionic liquid polyimide film is                represented by structural formula (I).
   n=100 to 200;
   R1=methylimidazole or butylimidazole or octyl                imidazole; and
   R2=represented by group of formulae                (II-IV).
   . An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                method for preparing graft type ionic liquid                polyimide film, which involves:
   (A) adding an aminated functional ionic liquid                to a casting solution formed of polyimide;
   (B) heating and stirring the water bath                grafting the aminated functional ionic liquid to                the polymer main chain in the form of a side group                through the characteristic structure of the Schiff                base to obtain an aminated casting liquid;
   (C) knife-coating the aluminized casting                solution into a film;
   (D) obtaining the modified film was obtained                by a solvent evaporation method;
   (E) drying to solidify the film; and
   (F) freezing to remove the film to obtain a                graft type ionic liquid polyimide film.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The amination                  functional ionic liquid is                  1-(aminoethyl)-3-methylimidazolium                  hexafluorophosphate,                  1-(aminoethyl)-3-butylimidazolium                  hexafluorophosphate, and 1-(aminoethyl)-3-octyl                  imidazolium hexafluorophosphate. The amination                  functional ionic liquid is 1-(aminoethyl)-                  3-butylimidazolium hexafluorophosphate. Preferred                  Conditions: The amination functional ionic liquid                  is prepared by one or more of N-methylimidazole,                  N-butylimidazole and N-octyl imidazole and                  bromoethylamine hydrobromide. The acid salt is                  heated, refluxed with ethanol, and dried to                  obtain an intermediate, further, potassium                  hydroxide is added to remove potassium bromide                  (KBr), and then ion exchange reaction is carried                  out with potassium hexafluorophosphate (KPF6),                  after ethanol dissolution, the mixture is heated                  in a water bath to remove ethanol to obtain an                  aminated functional ionic liquid. The prepartion                  method includes the mass ratio of the polyimide                  to the aminated functional ionic liquid is 1:                  (0.02-0.2), the heating condition in the water                  bath is 60-80 degrees C, and the water bath is                  stirred and heated to 8-16 hour. The graft                  structure is a Schiff base characteristic                  structure of C=N double bond, the curing                  condition is 40-60 degrees C, and the uncovering                  condition after freezing is a freezing                  temperature of -4 to -15 degrees C, and freezing                  The time is 15-30 minutes. The grafted ionic                  liquid polyimide membrane is subjected to                  separation of carbon dioxide gas under a                  condition that the transmembrane pressure                  difference is 0.1-1.6 megapascal and the                  operating temperature is 30-70 degrees C.

PN CN114797932-A
TI Preparing bimetallic three-dimensional unique            honeycomb-shaped carbon dioxide reduction catalyst used            for reducing carbon dioxide, involves dissolving            cobalt-iron alloy nanoparticle and carbon material of            three-dimensional honeycomb-shaped porous            nitrogen-doped graphitic carbon frame in water.
AU ZHAO Y
   HE L
AE UNIV WUHAN (UYWU-C)
GA 2022A1165M
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing bimetallic three-dimensional unique                honeycomb-shaped carbon dioxide reduction catalyst                involves dissolving cobalt-iron alloy nanoparticle                and carbon material of three-dimensional (3D)                honeycomb-shaped porous nitrogen-doped graphitic                carbon frame in water, stirring, mixing at room                temperature, heating, stirring the mixture to dry                to form a reddish-brown powder to obtain the                precursor of cobalt-iron (CoFe) nanoparticle                embedded in 3D honeycomb porous nitrogen-doped                graphitic carbon framework composite.
   USE - Method for preparing bimetallic                three-dimensional unique honeycomb-shaped carbon                dioxide reduction catalyst used for reducing carbon                dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple in operation,                cost-effective, and convenient for                popularization.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                bimetallic 3D unique honeycomb-shaped carbon                dioxide reduction catalyst is prepared by                preparation method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon material of                  the 3D honeycomb porous nitrogen-doped graphitic                  carbon framework is melamine, urea and                  polyvinylpyrrolidone. The water is distilled                  water, tap water, drinking water, and/or                  deionized water.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The cobalt-iron alloy                  nanoparticle comprises cobalt salt and iron salt.                  The cobalt salt is cobalt acetate and cobalt                  nitrate. The iron salt is ferric chloride and                  ferric nitrate. The inert gas is carbon dioxide                  gas, nitrogen gas, or argon gas.Preferred Conditions: The mass ratio of the                  cobalt salt and the iron salt is 3-1:1-3. The                  mass ratio of the cobalt-iron alloy nanoparticle                  to the carbon material is 4-1:1-4. The heating                  and stirring temperature is 95&#8451;. The                  calcination is performed at temperature of                  500-1100&#8451;, the heating rate is                  1-10&#8451;/minutes, and the constant                  temperature time is 0.5-2 hours.

PT P
PN CN112430472-A
TI Direct preparation of low-carbon alcohol and coproduction of gasoline by carbon dioxide hydrogenation, involves reducing catalyst containing copper oxide, activating in carbon dioxide/hydrogen atmosphere and performing catalytic reaction.
AU HONG X
   XU D
   LIU G
AE UNIV WUHAN (UYWU-C)
GA 2021230858
AB    NOVELTY - Direct preparation of low-carbon alcohol and coproduction of gasoline by carbon dioxide hydrogenation, involves reducing catalyst in hydrogen-containing atmosphere, activating in carbon dioxide/hydrogen-containing atmosphere to obtain pretreated catalyst, subjecting pretreated catalyst to catalytic reaction to obtain low-carbon alcohol and gasoline. The catalyst is potassium oxide-copper oxide-magnesium oxide-zinc oxide-ferric oxide.
   USE - Direct preparation of low-carbon alcohol and coproduction of gasoline by carbon dioxide hydrogenation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, and effectively shortens the reaction steps and improves the catalytic efficiency to produce gasoline and low-carbon alcohol with space-time yield of more than 114.3 mg/g catalyst.hour and single-pass carbon dioxide conversion rate of more than 40%, and is suitable for industrial production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The catalyst comprises 0.1-17.6 %mass potassium and 10-90 %mass potassium oxide-copper oxide-magnesium oxide-zinc oxide-ferric oxide. The molar ratio of magnesium and zinc is 3-9:1. Preferred Component: The catalyst further comprises copper oxide-zinc oxide-aluminum oxide. The hydrogen-containing atmosphere is hydrogen gas or mixture of hydrogen and argon. The carbon dioxide/hydrogen-containing atmosphere is carbon dioxide/hydrogen or mixture of carbon dioxide/hydrogen and nitrogen.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: The reduction is carried out at 300-400 degrees C under heating rate of 1-10 degrees C/minute and gas flow rate of 20-50 mL/minute for 1-5 hours. The activation method is carried out at 300-400 degrees C under pressure of 2-5 MPa for 1-5 hours. The packing mode of catalyst is bed packing, particle mixing and packing, powder mixing and filling or grinding-mixing-packing. The catalytic reaction is carried out at 260-320 degrees C under pressure of 2-5 MPa and gas mass space velocity of 3000-9000 mL/g catalyst.hour to form lower alcohol and gasoline. Preferred Process: The bed packing involves packing copper oxide-zinc oxide-aluminum oxide as layer (A) and packing potassium oxide-copper oxide-magnesium oxide-zinc oxide-ferric oxide as layer (B) on layer (A) or vice versa and separating packed bed layer with inert materials. The particle mixing and packing method involves compressing copper oxide-zinc oxide-aluminum oxide and potassium oxide-copper oxide-magnesium oxide-zinc oxide-ferric oxide into tablets, crushing, sieving to form catalyst particles with particle size of 40-60 mesh, shaking and mixing the catalyst particles and inert materials together. The powder mixing and filling method involves shaking and mixing copper oxide-zinc oxide-aluminum oxide and potassium oxide-copper oxide-magnesium oxide-zinc oxide-ferric oxide, adding inert materials, mixing and filling. The grinding-mixing-packing method involves grinding copper oxide-zinc oxide-aluminum oxide and potassium oxide-copper oxide-magnesium oxide-zinc oxide-ferric oxide for 5-30 minutes, adding inert material to collected catalyst powder, shaking and mixing, and packing.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).)

PN CN111111765-A; CN111111765-B
TI Catalyst for directly producing low-carbon            hydrocarbons by hydrogenation of carbon dioxide            comprises oxide and carbon-containing molecular            sieve.
AU SU J
   WANG Y
   LIU S
   ZHOU H
   LIU C
   JIAO W
   ZHANG L
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC SHANGHAI RES INST PETROCHEMICAL (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP SINOPEC (SNPC-C)
GA 2020415077
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst for directly producing low-carbon                hydrocarbons by hydrogenation of carbon dioxide                comprises an oxide and a carbon-containing                molecular sieve. The oxide and the                carbon-containing molecular sieve exist in a form                independent of each other, e.g. a separate package                or a mechanical mixture of each other.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for directly producing                low-carbon hydrocarbons by hydrogenation of carbon                dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                directly preparing low-carbon hydrocarbons by                hydrogenation of carbon dioxide comprising                contacting raw material containing carbon dioxide                and hydrogen with a catalyst to obtain a low-carbon                hydrocarbon-containing product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon content in                  the carbon-containing molecular sieve is 5-25%.                  The oxide is a composite oxide, including                  XYa(O)e, where X is at least one of zinc and                  indium, and Y is at least one of zirconium,                  chromium, cerium, manganese, gallium and                  aluminum. The value range of a is 0-4, preferably                  0.3-3) and e is the total number of oxygen atoms                  required to satisfy the valence of each element                  in the catalyst. The molecular sieve is                  silico-phosphoaluminum molecular sieve (SAPO                  molecular sieve) and/or phosphoroaluminum                  molecular sieve (AlPO molecular sieve),                  preferably silicophosphorus aluminum molecular                  sieve or phosphoroaluminum molecular sieve. The                  molecular screening is at least one of SAPO-5,                  SAPO-11, SAPO-18, SAPO-34, AlPO-34 and AlPO-18,                  preferably AlPO-34 or AlPO-18. The selected                  oxygen-containing organic compounds is at least                  one of methanol, ethanol, propanol, formaldehyde,                  acetaldehyde, propionaldehyde, formic acid,                  acetic acid, dimethyl ether and methyl formate.                  The hydrocarbon gas mixture includes hydrocarbon                  components and other components. The hydrocarbon                  components account for 50-100%, and other                  components account for 0-50%. The hydrocarbon                  component is at least one of ethylene, propylene,                  butene, methane, ethane, propane, butane,                  acetylene, propyne and butadiene and/or other                  components is at least one of hydrogen, nitrogen,                  argon and helium. Preferred Method: The                  carbon-containing molecular sieve is obtained by                  pre-carbonizing the oxygen-containing organic                  matter or hydrocarbon mixture. The processing                  conditions are: temperature 350-450 degrees C and                  pressure 0.1-4Mpa. The process conditions for                  carbon dioxide hydrogenation are: reaction                  temperature 340-460 degrees C, reaction pressure                  0.5-7.0Mpa, carbon dioxide: hydrogen (molar                  ratio) of 1:(0.4-3), GHSV 1000-8000h-1.

PN KR2018077972-A; KR1919556-B1
TI Producing formate by converting carbon monoxide contained in gas into carbon dioxide, contacting carbon dioxide with Acetobacterium woodii-derived formate dehydrogenase, and inhibiting synthesis of sodium-dependent ATPase.
AU KIM Y H
   HWANG H W
AE C1CHEM CO LTD (CONE-Non-standard)
GA 201855844F
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing formate involves (a) converting carbon monoxide contained in gas into carbon dioxide, (b) contacting the carbon dioxide with a formate dehydrogenase (FDR) derived from Acetobacterium woodii to form formate, and (c) inhibiting synthesis of sodium-dependent ATPase of the A.woodii.
   USE - The method is useful for producing formate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The step (a) involves converting carbon monoxide contained in gas into carbon dioxide by contacting carbon monoxide dehydrogenase derived from A.woodii with the substrate, where a volume ratio of the carbon monoxide is 40-60%, and the gas is generated in a steel manufacturing process. The step (c) involves inhibiting synthesis of sodium-dependent ATPase by removing sodium ions using a sodium ionophore. The method further involves adding potassium bicarbonate before inhibiting synthesis of sodium-dependent ATPase.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The sodium ionophore is chosen from ETH157, ETH227, ETH2120 and ETH4120.
   EXAMPLE - None given.

PN CN109220593-A; CN109220593-B
TI Saffron intelligent cultivation system comprises greenhouse, soil on ground of greenhouse for cultivating saffron or saffron, main controller for automatic control of system, soil temperature sensor for collecting soil temperature data.
AU LV W
AE HANGZHOU VOCATIONAL & TECH COLLEGE (HZVT-C)
   HANGZHOU VOCATIONAL & TECH COLLEGE (HZVT-C)
GA 2019135315
AB    NOVELTY - Saffron intelligent cultivation system comprises a greenhouse (18), a soil on ground of greenhouse for cultivating saffron or saffron, main controller (33) for automatic control of the system, soil temperature sensor (17) for collecting soil temperature data and transmitting soil temperature data to main controller, soil moisture sensor for collecting soil moisture data and transmitting soil moisture data to main controller, soil pH detecting sensor for collecting soil pH data and transmitting soil pH data to main controller. A soil nutrient detection sensor for collecting soil nutrient data and transmitting soil nutrient data to main controller for collecting air temperature data in greenhouse and transmitting air temperature data to main controller air temperature sensor, and air humidity sensor for collecting air humidity data in greenhouse and transmitting air humidity data to main controller for collecting carbon dioxide content data in air in greenhouse and carbon dioxide.
   USE - Used as saffron intelligent cultivation system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system has ventilation effect in greenhouse; improves accuracy; has reasonable design; high control precision; and has high use value.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Saffron intelligent cultivation system comprises a greenhouse (18), a soil on the ground of a greenhouse for cultivating saffron or saffron, a main controller (33) for automatic control of the system, soil temperature sensor (17) for collecting soil temperature data and transmitting soil temperature data to the main controller, soil moisture sensor for collecting soil moisture data and transmitting soil moisture data to the main controller, a soil pH detecting sensor for collecting soil pH data and transmitting soil pH data to the main controller. A soil nutrient detection sensor for collecting soil nutrient data and transmitting soil nutrient data to the main controller for collecting air temperature data in the greenhouse and transmitting the air temperature data to the main controller air temperature sensor, and air humidity sensor for collecting air humidity data in the greenhouse and transmitting the air humidity data to the main controller for collecting carbon dioxide content data in the air in the greenhouse and carbon dioxide. The content data is transmitted to the carbon dioxide detecting sensor of the main controller. An air pressure sensor for collecting air pressure data in the greenhouse and transmitting the air pressure data to the main controller, a wind turbine for generating wind power to the air in the greenhouse, a wind speed sensor for detecting wind generated by the wind turbine. An illumination intensity sensor for collecting light intensity data in the greenhouse for imaging the growth state during the cultivation of the saffron or the saffron, and transmitting the growth state image data obtained by the camera acquisition to the main control. The camera of the device and the background monitoring center connected to the main controller. The soil temperature sensor, the soil moisture sensor, the soil pH detecting sensor, and the soil nutrient detecting sensor are all disposed in the soil, air temperature sensor, air humidity sensor, carbon dioxide detection sensor, air pressure sensor, fan, wind speed sensor, light intensity sensor, camera and they are all located in the greenhouse, soil temperature sensor, soil moisture sensor, soil pH detection sensor, soil nutrient detection sensor, air temperature sensor, air humidity sensor, carbon dioxide detection sensor, the air pressure sensor, the fan, the wind speed sensor, the light intensity sensor, and the camera are all connected to the main controller. The main controller uses a dynamic sampling algorithm to preprocess the received data and obtain preprocessed data. The main controller performs data processing on the preprocessed data through a weighted averaging algorithm, and the background monitoring center controls through the main controller. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for intelligently cultivating saffron, comprising (i) setting respectively weight W1 of the soil temperature data, soil moisture data, soil PH value data, and carbon dioxide content data combination to 40%, where the soil nutrient data has a weight W2 of 30%, and the growth state image data has a weight W3 of 30%; (ii) calculating the weighted average P based on the real-time collected soil temperature data, soil moisture data, soil pH data, carbon dioxide content data combination data X1, soil nutrient data X2, growth state image data X3 and their weights. P is equal to (X1x W1+X2x W2+X3x W3)/(W1+W2+W3); (iii) determining whether there is an abnormality according to the measured weighted average value P and the normal weighted average value P0, if the positive and negative deviation 20 is exceeded, and the judgment result is abnormal Y1, otherwise it is Y0; (iv) calculating the calculated average value PP obtained by dividing the sum of the weighted average values P obtained by collecting N times in a unit time and dividing by N according to the current working state;(v) calculating the average PP again and comparing it with the normal weighted average P0 to determine the final result, where the abnormality is YY, otherwise it is Y, where the soil temperature sensor collects soil temperature data, and transmits the soil temperature data to the main controller, collecting the soil moisture sensor to soil moisture data and transmitting the soil moisture data to the main controller; (vi) collecting the soil pH detecting sensor to the soil pH data and transmits the soil pH data to the main controller; and (vii) collecting the soil nutrient detection sensor to soil nutrient data and transmitting the soil nutrient data to the main controller, collecting the carbon dioxide detecting sensor of the carbon dioxide content data in the air in the greenhouse, and transmitting the carbon dioxide content data to the main controller, where the camera images the growth state during the cultivation of the saffron or the saffron, and transmitting the growth state image data obtained by the camera acquisition to the main controller.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the control schematic functional block diagram of intelligent greenhouse monitoring control system.

   Greenhouse (18)
   Main controller (33)

PN CN109502856-A
TI Device useful for treating limestone wet desulfurization wastewater comprises e.g. wastewater collection tank, electroflocculation treatment unit, softening treatment unit, microfiltration treatment unit, and nanofiltration treatment unit.
AU WANG K
AE SHANGHAI JIANGZHE ENVIRONMENT ENG TECHNO (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201928670X
AB    NOVELTY - Device comprises e.g. a wastewater collection tank (1), an electroflocculation treatment unit (2), a softening treatment unit (3), a microfiltration treatment unit (4), a carbon dioxide blowing treatment unit (5), a nanofiltration treatment unit, a reverse osmosis treatment unit, a material membrane separation unit, a membrane distillation treatment unit, and an mechanical vapor recompressor (MVR) evaporation treatment unit. The wastewater water outlet of collecting box is connected to the water inlet of electric flocculation processing unit. The electric flocculation processing unit is used for sedimentation of suspended solids and heavy metals in the wastewater collection tank. The water outlet of the electro flocculation processing unit is connected to the water inlet of the softening treatment unit. The softening treatment unit is used for treating supernatant and alkali metal by electro flocculation processing unit, carbon dioxide blowing treatment unit, and softening treatment.
   USE - The device is useful for treating limestone wet desulfurization wastewater (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is less affected by the composition of the desulfurization wastewater; has sustained and stable treatment effect; realizes the resource utilization of the wastewater; obtains sodium sulfate and sodium chloride having purity of 98.5% and 98% respectively; and forms small impurities contained in the second fresh water, the third fresh water, the fourth fresh water and the fifth fresh water to achieve the standard of industrial water for recycling.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Device comprises a wastewater collection tank (1), an electroflocculation treatment unit (2), a softening treatment unit (3), a microfiltration treatment unit (4), a carbon dioxide blowing treatment unit (5), a nanofiltration treatment unit, a reverse osmosis treatment unit, a material membrane separation unit, a membrane distillation treatment unit, and an mechanical vapor recompressors (MVR) evaporation treatment unit. The wastewater water outlet of the collecting box is connected to the water inlet of the electric flocculation processing unit. The electric flocculation processing unit is used for sedimentation of suspended solids and heavy metals in the wastewater collection tank. The water outlet of the electro flocculation processing unit is connected to the water inlet of the softening treatment unit. The softening treatment unit is used for treating the supernatant and alkali metal by the electro flocculation processing unit, carbon dioxide blowing treatment unit, and softening treatment. The water outlet of the softening treatment unit is connected to the water inlet of the microfiltration processing unit for microfiltration treatment of the softened desulfurization wastewater. The water outlet of the microfiltration processing unit is connected to the water inlet of the carbon dioxide stripping treatment unit. The carbon dioxide stripping treatment unit is used for the carbonate in the desulfurized wastewater after softening in the form of carbon dioxide. The water outlet of the carbon dioxide stripping treatment unit is connected to the water inlet of the nanofiltration processing unit, and is used for nanofiltration treatment of the supernatant treated by the carbon dioxide stripping treatment unit to obtain the first rich sulfuric acid brine and hydrochloric acid rich brine. The first water outlet of the nanofiltration processing unit is connected to the water inlet of the reverse osmosis treatment unit for performing reverse osmosis treatment of the hydrochloric acid-rich brine to obtain the first chlorine-rich concentrated water and the first fresh water. The membrane distillation treatment unit is composed of a first membrane distillation treatment unit and a second membrane distillation treatment unit for using the first rich chlorine concentration unit. The membrane is subjected to membrane distillation treatment to obtain a second chlorine-rich concentrated water and a second fresh water. The second water outlet of the nanofiltration processing unit is connected to the water inlet of the material membrane separation unit for filtering the first sulfuric acid-rich brine to obtain a second sulfuric acid-rich brine and a sulfuric acid-poor brine. The second membrane distillation treatment unit is configured to perform a membrane distillation treatment on the second sulfur-rich brine to obtain a third sulfuric acid-rich brine and a third fresh water. The water outlet of the first membrane distillation processing unit is connected to the water inlet of the MVR evaporation processing unit for performing MVR evaporation treatment on the second rich chlorine-rich concentrated water to obtain a third rich chlorine-rich water and a fourth freshwater. The third sulfur-rich brine and the third rich chlorine-rich water are separately centrifuged by a centrifuge to obtain sodium sulfate and sodium chloride. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for treating limestone wet desulfurization wastewater by using the device comprising (i) passing desulfurization waste water in the waste water collection tank into an electric flocculation treatment unit, and performing electroflocculation treatment to remove suspended solids and heavy metals, (ii) softening the desulfurization wastewater, and removing calcium and magnesium ions, (iii) subjecting the softened effluent wastewater to microfiltration treatment, (iv) subjecting the micro-filtered desulfurization wastewater to carbon dioxide stripping treatment, where the carbonate in the desulfurization wastewater escapes in the form of carbon dioxide, (v) subjecting the wastewater to nanofiltration treatment to obtain a first sulfuric acid-rich brine and a hydrochloric acid-rich brine, (vi) subjecting the hydrochloric acid-rich brine to reverse osmosis treatment to obtain a first rich chlorine-rich concentrated water and a first fresh water, (vii) subjecting the first rich chlorine-rich concentrated water to membrane distillation treatment to obtain a second rich chlorine-rich concentrated water and a second fresh water, (viii) subjecting the first sulfuric acid-rich brine to a membrane filtration treatment to obtain a second sulfate-rich brine and a sulfuric acid-depleted brine, and (ix) subjecting the second sulfur-rich brine to membrane distillation treatment to obtain a third sulfuric acid-rich brine and a third fresh water.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic representation of the device for treating limestone wet desulfurization wastewater.
   Wastewater collection tank (1)
   Electroflocculation treatment unit (2)
   Softening treatment unit (3)
   Microfiltration treatment unit (4)
   Carbon dioxide blowing treatment unit (5)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The electro flocculation treatment unit comprises a reaction tank, a water inlet is arranged at the bottom of one side of the reaction tank, a water outlet is provided at the upper part of the other side of the reaction tank, and a sewage outlet is provided at the bottom. The anode and the cathode plate are arranged inside the pool. The anode plate and the cathode plate are both aluminum electrode plates. The anode plates and cathode plates are connected to the anode of the power supply and the negative pole of the power supply through a wire. A conductivity meter is provided between the anode plate and the cathode plate. The conductivity meter comprising the first control device is connected by wires. The first control device comprises a processor, a bridge, an operational amplifier and a commutator. The processor passing through a wire or through a commutator is connected with the bridge. The bridge is connected to an operational amplifier. The processor is connected to a conductivity meter. The operational amplifier is connected to an anode of the power supply. The processor is connected to the cathode of the power supply. The electro flocculation processing unit and the microfiltration processing unit are respectively connected to a sludge dewatering machine. The reverse osmosis treatment unit comprises a first reverse osmosis membrane and a second reverse osmosis membrane. The first reverse osmosis membrane is a high pressure reverse osmosis membrane. The second reverse osmosis membrane is a low pressure reverse osmosis membrane. The first water outlet of the nanofiltration processing unit is connected to the water inlet of the first reverse osmosis membrane to obtain the first chlorine-rich concentrated water and the first fresh water, and the first fresh water flows into the second reverse osmosis membrane to obtain a fourth fresh chlorine-rich water and a fifth fresh water. The fourth fresh chlorine-rich concentrated water flows into the first reverse osmosis membrane for cyclic concentration. Preferred Method: The method further comprises (x) subjecting the second chlorine-rich concentrated water to MVR evaporation treatment to obtain a third rich chlorine-rich concentrated water and a fourth fresh water, and (xi) centrifuging the third sulfur-rich brine and the third rich chlorine-rich concentrated water by using the centrifuge to obtain sodium sulfate and sodium chloride. The method further comprises electrocoagulation treatment of a lean sulfuric acid brine.

PN WO2022074591-A1
TI Chemical complex for oxidative dehydrogenation of alkanes, comprises at least one oxidative dehydrogenation reactor comprising mixed metal oxide catalyst, quench tower and amine wash tower adapted to remove carbon dioxide from quenched product stream to provide washed product stream.
AU SIMANZHENKOV V
   OLAYIWOLA B
   GOODARZNIA S
AE NOVA CHEM INT SA (NVCH-C)
GA 202250962A
AB    NOVELTY - A chemical complex (100) comprises at least one oxidative dehydrogenation reactor (102), a quench tower (104), an amine wash tower (108), a dryer (132), a distillation tower (110), a combustion chamber (106), and an oxygen separation module (148). At least one reactor comprises a mixed metal oxide catalyst and is designed to accept, optionally in the presence of a heat removal diluent gas, an oxygen containing gas and 2-4C alkane containing gas, and to produce a product stream comprising a corresponding 2-4C alkene and one or more unreacted 2-4C alkane, oxygen, a heat removal diluent gas, one or more carbon oxides chosen from carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide, one or more oxygenates chosen from acetic acid, acrylic acid and maleic acid, and water. The quench tower is adapted to quench the product stream and remove water and soluble oxygenates from the product stream to provide a quenched product stream. The amine wash tower is adapted to remove carbon dioxide from the quenched stream.
   USE - Chemical complex used for oxidative dehydrogenation of 2-4C alkanes e.g. ethane (claimed) to ethylene.
   ADVANTAGE - The chemical complex reduces or eliminates the amount of acetylene produced in the oxidative dehydrogenation process in combination with other processes, such as thermal cracking and eliminate downstream unit operations in oxidative dehydrogenation process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A chemical complex comprises at least one oxidative dehydrogenation reactor, a quench tower, an amine wash tower, a dryer, a distillation tower, a combustion chamber, and an oxygen separation module. At least one oxidative dehydrogenation reactor comprises a mixed metal oxide catalyst and is designed to accept, optionally in the presence of a heat removal diluent gas, an oxygen containing gas and 2-4C alkane containing gas, and to produce a product stream comprising a corresponding 2-4C alkene and one or more of an unreacted 2-4C alkane, oxygen, a heat removal diluent gas, one or more carbon oxides chosen from carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide,one or more oxygenates chosen from acetic acid, acrylic acid and maleic acid, and water. The quench tower is adapted to quench the product stream and remove water and soluble oxygenates from the product stream to provide a quenched product stream. The amine wash tower is adapted to remove carbon dioxide from the quenched product stream to provide a washed product stream. The dryer is adapted to remove water from the washed product stream to provide a dried product stream. The distillation tower is adapted to remove 2C/2C or more hydrocarbons from the dried product stream to produce an overhead stream comprising 1C hydrocarbons. The combustion chamber is adapted to receive the overhead stream and at least one fuel stream and optionally at least one stream comprising oxygen, and combust the overhead stream, for producing heat and flue gas at 850-1500 degrees C. The flue gas is used to provide heat to the oxygen separation module either by introducing the flue gas to the oxygen separation module or by using the flue gas to heat an oxygen containing stream that is introduced to the oxygen separation module. The oxygen separation module comprises an oxygen transport membrane housed inside a sealed vessel and having a retentate side and a permeate side, an inlet (I1) for introducing the flue gas or the oxygen containing stream, or both into the retentate side, an inlet (I2) for introducing the flue gas or the oxygen containing stream, or both into the permeate side, an air inlet for introducing air into the retentate side, an exhaust stream outlet for discharge of oxygen depleted air and combustion products from the retentate side, an outlet stream for removing oxygen enriched gas and combustion products from the permeate side. The oxygen enriched gas from the permeate side is directed back to the oxidative dehydrogenation reactor to make up at least portion of the oxygen containing gas introduced into the at least one oxidative dehydrogenation reactor.

PT P
PN CN111838379-A
TI Preparing strawberry-shaped chocolate products            involves selecting fresh ripe strawberries, removing            stalk and calyx, and washing them, dehydrating by            carbon dioxide at room temperature and pressure            difference or vacuum freeze-dry.
AU ZHU J
   DONG X
   QI H
   ZHU B
   QIN L
   WEN C
   LIU Y
AE JIANGSU PALARICH FOOD CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020A95805
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing strawberry-shaped chocolate products                involves selecting fresh ripe strawberries,                removing the stalk and calyx, and washing them,                dehydrating by carbon dioxide at room temperature                and pressure difference or vacuum freeze-dry,                dehydrating or drying, until the moisture content                of strawberry is 10-25 wt. wt.%, and reserving,                configuring the sugar solution with a temperature                of 35-95 degrees C and a concentration of 25-45Brix                for use, combining 1 pts. wt. strawberry from step                a-1 and 0.3-0.5 pts. wt. sugar solution, spraying                the sugar liquid on the surface of the strawberry                and passing the osmotic pressure for 4-6 hours, and                then setting aside. The prepared material is put                into the carbon dioxide pressure difference device                for dehydration, the temperature is normal                temperature, the pressure is 0.1-2 mega Pascal, the                holding time is 0.7-2 hours, and it is taken out                for use.
   USE - Method for preparing strawberry-shaped                chocolate products.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare                strawberry-shaped chocolate products is simple to                process, the strawberry and the sugar liquid, the                strawberry and the chocolate are blended with each                other, the prepared strawberry chocolate has a good                taste, and changes the taste of the strawberry over                sour.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing strawberry-shaped chocolate products                involves selecting fresh ripe strawberries,                removing the stalk and calyx, and washing them,                dehydrating by carbon dioxide at room temperature                and pressure difference or vacuum freeze-dry,                dehydrate or dry, until the moisture content of                strawberry is 10-25 wt. wt.%, and reserving,                configuring the sugar solution with a temperature                of 35-95 degrees C and a concentration of 25-45Brix                for use, combining 1 pts. wt. strawberry from step                a-1 and 0.3-0.5 pts. wt. sugar solution, spraying                the sugar liquid on the surface of the strawberry                and passing the osmotic pressure for 4-6 hours, and                then setting aside. The prepared material is put                into the carbon dioxide pressure difference device                for dehydration, the temperature is normal                temperature, the pressure is 0.1-2 mega Pascal, the                holding time is 0.7-2 hours, and it is taken out                for use. The material is quickly freezed, until the                internal center temperature is (-)20-(-)30 degrees                C, and then put the material into a drying oven to                dry, control the surface temperature of the                material to be lower than 60 degrees C and the                vacuum degree to be less than 120Pa, the                temperature at the end of drying is 50 degrees C,                and the moisture at the end of drying is less than                5 wt. wt.%. The preparation of the chocolate                includes adding cocoa solids, white sugar, milk                powder, and emulsifier are mixed and then to the                refiner to be refined into chocolate with a                particle size of 7-20 mu m. The refined chocolate                is refined. The chocolate is transferred to the                tempering machine to adjust the temperature, and                then packaged, after cooling for use. The raw                material ratio includes 48-80 wt.% cocoa solids,                8-30 wt.% white sugar, 8-22 wt.% milk powder, and                0.1-0.5 wt.% emulsifier. The mold is made into a                strawberry-shaped cavity, and the cavity is                symmetrically divided into two halves. The                chocolate is put into the chocolate melting tank,                and set the melting temperature of the melting tank                to 40-45 degrees C, stirred and set aside, then put                the melted chocolate into the tempering machine to                adjust the temperature. The temperature of the                tempering machine is set to (42-45 degrees                C)-(17-22 degrees C)-(32-33 degrees C), and the                viscosity of the obtained chocolate is                2000-3500cps. The strawberry is put into half of                the mold, and added prepared chocolate to the mold                to fill the cavity for use, then poured prepared                chocolate into the corresponding other half of the                mold and set it to over-pouring, and poured                chocolate to overflow. The mold is put into a cold                tunnel at 0 plus minus 5 degrees C for 3-5 minutes,                then quickly close the two mold halves and continue                cooling for 4-8 hours, until the chocolate is                completely solidified. The solidified mold is                separated to obtain a strawberry-shaped chocolate                product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Conditions: The material is                  operated twice, and then the material is dried in                  a vacuum drying oven. Preferred Components: The                  emulsifier is one of phospholipids or a mixture                  of phospholipids and polyglycerol ricinoleate in                  mixing ratio of 1:1 or 3:2 or 4:1. Preferred                  Process: The carbon dioxide dehydration at normal                  temperature and pressure includes removing the                  stalk and calyx, and washing the strawberry with                  free water, putting it into the carbon dioxide                  pressure difference device. The temperature is                  normal temperature, the pressure is 0.1-2Mpa, the                  holding time is 2 hours, and the strawberry is                  dehydrated by the pressure difference. The washed                  strawberries is frozen, after removing the stalks                  and calyx, until the inner core temperature of                  the strawberry is (-)20-(-)30 degrees C, then put                  the strawberries into the vacuum drying chamber,                  and controlled the vacuum degree to be less than                  120 Pascal. The vacuum drying chamber is heated                  the strawberry, so that the surface temperature                  and the inner core temperature of the strawberry                  are both less than 60 degrees C, and it is dried                  for 20-28 hours.

AU LIANG X
   ZENG H
AE SUZHOU YUANYI MEDICAL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 202241559N
AB    NOVELTY - Culture solution comprises tissue culture medium (TCM) 199, 1-20 microM Forskolin (coleonol), 10% sodium polyanethole sulfonate (SPS), 100 IU/ml penicillin and 100 g/ml streptomycin.
   USE - The culture solution is useful for in vitro maturation of human oocyte-cumulus particle cell complex.
   ADVANTAGE - The culture solution and culture method: significantly promote maturation at a rate of 76.1% and inhibit aging of human ovum; improves blastocyst formation at a rate of 64.1% and the transplanting rate of embryos; and can achieve a fertility rate of 78.9%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for in vitro maturation culturing of human oocyte-cumulus particle cell complex, comprising: (i) promoting in vitro ovulation when the dominant follicular reaches 18 mm, injecting 6000-10000 IU human chorionic gonadotropin, obtaining follicular fluid and cumulus oocyte complex (COC) after 36-38 hours, picking out COC, and washing using follicular washing liquid to obtain COC, (ii) culturing before fertilization by transferring the obtained COC to the in vitro maturation culture solution, culturing at 37degrees Celsius and 100% humidity for 4 hours, where the mixed gas is 88% nitrogen, 7% oxygen and 5% carbon dioxide, adding 100 mul of in vitro maturation culture solution in microdroplets for every two COCs, and before culturing, washing COC using in vitro maturation culture solution for 2-3 times, (iii) adding the washed COC of pre-fertilized culture in microdroplets into 50-200 mul semen containing sperms, and fertilizing at 37degrees Celsius for 14-18 hours in the presence of carbon dioxide to obtain the fertilized egg, where the sperm density in the microdroplets is 5x106 per ml and 2-3 COCs are present in each 100 mul of microdroplets; and (iv) washing the fertilized egg obtained in step (iii), transferring 50-100 mul embryo culture liquid in microdroplets, and culturing at 37degrees Celsius and 100% humidity for 2-4 days to obtain the embryo, where the mixed gas is 88% nitrogen, 7% oxygen and 5% carbon dioxide, the follicular washing liquid in step (i) is added with 10% SPS, (4-(2-hydroxyethyl)-1-piperazineethanesulfonic acid) (HEPES) buffered-human tubal fluid medium (HTF) culture medium and the in vitro maturation culture solution used in step (ii), solution comprises TCM199 culture medium, 4-10 muM Forskolin (coleonol), 10% SPS, 100 IU/ml penicillin and 100 g/ml streptomycin.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The culture solution comprises TCM199 culture medium, 4-10 muM Forskolin (coleonol), 10% SPS, 100 IU/ml penicillin and 100 g/ml streptomycin.Preferred Method: The follicular fluid and COC in step (i) are obtained by ultrasonic-guided follicle vacuum suction extraction. The follicular washing liquid in step (i) is placed overnight in a mixed gas comprising 88% nitrogen, 7% oxygen and 5% carbon dioxide, a day before egg retrieval and then on a thermostat console at 37degrees Celsius. The in vitro maturation culture solution microdroplets in step (ii) were covered with mineral oil before culturing, and placed at 37degrees Celsius, 100% humidity and mixed gas of 88% nitrogen, 7% oxygen and 5% carbon dioxide in an embryo incubator. The semen in step (iii) is washed for 2-3 times and is placed overnight in a mixed gas comprising 88% nitrogen, 7% oxygen and 5% carbon dioxide, a day before egg retrieval at 37degrees Celsius and 100% humidity. The washing process in step (iv) comprises repeatedly blowing the fertilized eggs in microdroplets of pre-balanced embryo culture liquid for several times, where the embryo culture liquid on the fertilization day is placed overnight in a mixed gas comprising 88% nitrogen, 7% oxygen and 5% carbon dioxide at 37degrees Celsius and 100% humidity.

TI Preparing nano-calcium carbonate by aging calcium            hydroxide slurry, adding calcium hydroxide crystal form            control agent, passing carbon dioxide gas for bubbling            carbonization reaction, adding calcium hydroxide            modifier, and modifying.
AU ZHAO X
   AO Z
   LIAO J
AE JIANGXI HUAMING NANO CALCIUM CARBONATE (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021211197
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nano-calcium carbonate, involves (a)                aging refined calcium hydroxide slurry for 24-48                hours, removing using a vibrating screen and a                6-stage hydrocyclone, and adjusting concentration                to 10-20 %mass and temperature to 20-35 degrees C,                (b) adding 0.5-1.5 %mass calcium hydroxide crystal                form control agent, stirring, and passing 20-40                vol.% carbon dioxide gas for bubbling carbonization                reaction until the slurry pH is 6.5-7 at the                carbonization end point to obtain 10-20 %mass                cooked slurry, (c) removing the residue of the                cooked slurry, increasing the temperature, adding                5-8 %mass calcium hydroxide modifier, after the                temperature reaches 75-85 degrees C, adjusting the                pH to 7-9, stirring, and modifying for 60-90                minutes, and (d) dehydrating the obtained modified                nano-calcium carbonate suspension by pressure                filtration, drying, and depolymerizing to obtain                the nano-calcium carbonate.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing                nano-calcium carbonate used as filler for producing                ink.
   ADVANTAGE - The prepared calcium carbonate has excellent                compatibility with polymer materials and base                materials in the ink, excellent dispersibility,                stable ink system, and high gloss, and does not                agglomerate, and exhibits excellent storage                stability, fluidity, transparency and printing                adaptability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing nano-calcium carbonate, involves (a)                aging refined calcium hydroxide slurry for 24-48                hours, removing using a vibrating screen and a                6-stage hydrocyclone, and adjusting concentration                to 10-20 %mass and temperature to 20-35 degrees C,                (b) adding 0.5-1.5 %mass calcium hydroxide crystal                form control agent, stirring, and passing 20-40                vol.% carbon dioxide gas for bubbling carbonization                reaction until the slurry pH is 6.5-7 at the                carbonization end point to obtain 10-20 %mass                cooked slurry, (c) removing the residue of the                cooked slurry, increasing the temperature, adding                5-8 %mass calcium hydroxide modifier, after the                temperature reaches 75-85 degrees C, adjusting the                pH to 7-9, stirring, and modifying for 60-90                minutes, and (d) dehydrating the obtained modified                nano-calcium carbonate suspension by pressure                filtration, drying, and depolymerizing to obtain                the nano-calcium carbonate, where the mass ratio of                modifier sodium stearate, rosin-based                monoquaternary ammonium salt and polyacrylamide is                1:0.5:0.2, and the mass ratio of the crystal form                control agents sodium tripolyphosphate, sodium                dimer, zinc sulfate and sucrose is 1:2:1:1.

   MAUST C
   KOHL J
   BITTNER J
AE CARMEUSE NORTH AMERICA (CARM-Non-standard)
   CARMEUSE NORTH AMERICA (CARM-Non-standard)
   CARMEUSE LIME INC (CARM-Non-standard)
   CARMEUSE LIME INC (CARM-Non-standard)
GA 2020B3492E
AB    NOVELTY - Making quicklime in a calciner, comprises:                preheating a solid carbonate material in a                preheating cyclone stage operating at a temperature                less than a calcination temperature of the solid                carbonate material; heating the preheated solid                carbonate material in a calcination cyclone stage                operating at a temperature of at least the                calcination temperature to generate a solid oxide                material and carbon dioxide gas; cooling the solid                oxide material in a cooling cyclone stage operating                at a temperature to cool the solid oxide material                to ambient temperature; extracting a first gas from                an outlet of the calcination cyclone stage and a                second gas from the cooling cyclone stage; and                recirculating the first gas to an inlet of the                calcination cyclone stage and the second gas to the                preheating cyclone stage.
   USE - The method is useful for making quicklime in                calciner.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: provides calciner provides                calciner smaller particles which beneficial for                plant efficiency; privides calciner system require                less energy and time to preheat and/or cool down                system; and privides calciner system have better                heat transfer inside equipment and better quality                distribution because of particles reaction surface                relative to conventional calciner systems.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) system for making quicklime, comprising a                calciner comprising preheating cyclone stage for                receiving a solid carbonate material and operating                at a temperature less than a calcination                temperature of the solid calcium carbonate                material, a calcination cyclone stage for heating                the preheated solid calcium carbonate material and                operating at a temperature of at least the                calcination temperature in which the solid calcium                carbonate material is converted to solid calcium                oxide and carbon dioxide gas, a cooling cyclone                stage for cooling the solid calcium oxide and                operating at a temperature in which the solid                calcium oxide is cooled to ambient temperature, a                first recirculating system to extract and                recirculate a first gas from an outlet of the                calcination cyclone stage to an inlet of the                calcination cyclone stage, and a second                recirculating system to extract and recirculate a                second gas from the cooling cyclone stage to the                preheating cyclone stage; and
   (2) system for recovering heat and/or carbon                dioxide from an indirect-fired calciner for making                quicklime.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises                  filtering, cooling, compressing, and heating the                  first gas prior to inserting the first gas into                  the inlet of the calcination cyclone stage. The                  method comprises filtering and exhausting at                  least a portion of the first gas after extracting                  the first gas from the preheating cyclone stage.                  In the method at least a portion of 3.20-4.50                  GJ/ton of solid carbonate material for heating                  the preheated solid carbonate material in the                  calcination cyclone stage is provided by the                  first gas. The first gas provides at least                  60-100% of the 3.20-4.50 GJ/ton of solid                  carbonate material for heating the preheated                  solid carbonate material in the calcination                  cyclone stage. The portion of 0.4-1.2 GJ/ton of                  solid carbonate material for preheating the solid                  carbonate material in the preheating cyclone                  stage is provided by the second gas. The second                  gas provides at least 10-50% of the 0.4-1.2                  GJ/ton for preheating a solid carbonate material                  in the preheating cyclone stage. The method                  comprises generating a hot flue gas stream to                  contact at least one of the solid carbonate                  materials and preheated solid carbonate material,                  where the hot flue gas stream comprises, based on                  total volume of the hot flue gas stream, at least                  99-100% of first gas and second gas. The method                  comprises generating a hot flue gas stream to                  contact at least one of the solid carbonate                  material and preheated solid carbonate material,                  where the hot flue gas stream comprises a ratio                  of the first gas to the second gas of 40:1.                  Preferred Components: The calciner comprises an                  indirect-fired vertical kiln comprising an                  electric kiln. The solid carbonate material                  comprises calcium carbonate and dolomitic stone                  and sodium carbonate, potassium carbonate,                  ferrous carbonate, manganese carbonate, cobalt                  carbonate, nickel carbonate, cupric carbonate,                  zinc carbonate, or aluminum carbonate. The solid                  carbonate material comprises, based on weight                  percent of the solid carbonate material, calcium                  carbonate and a balance of incidental impurities,                  and a particle size is 0.01-10 mm. The solid                  carbonate material is preheated to a temperature                  of 0-900 degrees C in the preheating cyclone                  stage, where the solid carbonate material is                  heated to a temperature of 700-1000 degrees C in                  calcination cyclone stage and the solid carbonate                  material is cooled to a temperature of 20-300                  degrees C in the cooling cyclone stage. The first                  gas has a temperature of 700-1100 degrees C, when                  extracted from the outlet of the calcination                  cyclone stage and a temperature of 900-2000                  degrees C, when inserted into the inlet of the                  calcination cyclone stage; and where the second                  gas has a temperature of 500-1000 degrees C, when                  extracted from cooling cyclone stage and a                  temperature of 500-1000 degrees C. when inserted                  into the preheating cyclone stage. The first gas                  and second gas comprises carbon dioxide and                  incidental impurities and steam. The first gas                  and second gas each consist of carbon dioxide and                  incidental impurities and steam. The first                  recirculating system comprises an electric heater                  to heat the calcination cyclone stage using at                  least a portion of the first gas to achieve the                  calcination temperature. In the system an energy                  requirement of the calcination heater to heat the                  calcination cyclone stage relative to a                  temperature of the first gas to achieve the                  calcination temperature is reduced by at least                  30-50% relative to a system lacking the first                  recirculating system and the second recirculating                  system.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN US2020290988-A1
TI Modifying cannabinoid molecule by providing composition comprising cannabinoids, converting into carbon dioxide and modified cannabinoid molecule in gas phase using energy, contacting with heat sink, and collecting liquid distillate.
AU THOMAS C R
   DEPALO M M
AE NATURAL EXTRACTION SYSTEMS LLC (DUPO-C)
GA 202090988G
AB    NOVELTY - Method for chemically-modifying a cannabinoid molecule involves (a) providing a composition comprising cannabinoids, (b) contacting the composition with 100 kJ or less energy/gram composition energy to convert the native cannabinoid molecule into (i) a carbon dioxide molecule and (ii) a modified cannabinoid molecule in a gas phase, (c) contacting the modified cannabinoid molecule with a heat sink to condense the modified cannabinoid molecule into a condensed cannabinoid molecule in a liquid distillate, and (d) collecting the liquid distillate, where the composition has a surface area to volume area being 1000 per meter or more, the cannabinoids comprise a native cannabinoid molecule, the native cannabinoid molecule comprises a carboxyl group, and the native cannabinoid molecule is in either a liquid phase or a solid phase.
   USE - The method is useful for chemically-modifying cannabinoid molecule.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces viscosity of liquid distillate using ethanol, which improves flow of liquid distillate in an automated system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for chemically-modifying a cannabinoid molecule involves (a) providing a composition comprising cannabinoids, (b) contacting the composition with 100 kJ or less energy/gram composition energy to convert the native cannabinoid molecule into (i) a carbon dioxide molecule and (ii) a modified cannabinoid molecule in a gas phase, (c) contacting the modified cannabinoid molecule with a heat sink to condense the modified cannabinoid molecule into a condensed cannabinoid molecule in a liquid distillate, and (d) collecting the liquid distillate, where the composition has a surface area to volume area being 1000 per meter or more, the cannabinoids comprise a native cannabinoid molecule, the native cannabinoid molecule comprises a carboxyl group, and the native cannabinoid molecule is in either a liquid phase or a solid phase. The composition comprises a particle comprising the native cannabinoid molecule. The composition comprises a non-volatile molecule. The method involves separating the modified cannabinoid molecule from the non-volatile molecule, contacting the modified cannabinoid molecule with the heat sink to condense the modified cannabinoid molecule into a condensed cannabinoid molecule in a liquid distillate, collecting the liquid distillate, and separating the non-cannabinoid molecule from the condensed cannabinoid molecule to produce a product comprising 55 wt.% or more condensed cannabinoid molecule. The liquid distillate comprises the condensed cannabinoid molecule and a non-cannabinoid molecule. The native cannabinoid molecule is cannabigerolic acid. The condensed cannabinoid molecule is cannabigerol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method involves suspending the particle in the gas phase, and performing step (b) while the particle is suspended in the gas phase. The method involves suspending the droplet in the gas phase, and performing step (b) while the droplet is suspended in the gas phase. The step (b) involves contacting the composition with 100 kJ or less energy/gram composition and 1-100 kW power/g composition for 200 milliseconds to 20 seconds. The method involves contacting the composition with energy by irradiating the composition, convectively heating the composition or conductively heating the composition. The method involves directing the composition along a path having a length of at least 4 m at a rate of at least 2 m/second, where the path comprises one or more surfaces. The method involves heating one or more surfaces at 190-250 degrees C. The method involves contacting the modified cannabinoid molecule with the heat sink for 240 seconds or less. The liquid distillate comprises one or more cannabidiol and tetrahydrocannabinol at a combined concentration of 6 wt.% or less, and the liquid distillate comprises cannabinol at a concentration of 0.8 wt.% or less. The method involves separating the non-cannabinoid molecule from the condensed cannabinoid molecule to produce a product comprising 55 wt.% or more condensed cannabinoid molecule. Preferred Composition: The composition comprises a particle comprising the native cannabinoid molecule. The composition comprises a droplet comprising the native cannabinoid molecule. The composition comprises a non-volatile molecule. Preferred Components: The non-volatile molecule is chlorophyll, cellulose, nucleic acid, protein, carbohydrate, sugar, triglyceride or phospholipid. The liquid distillate comprises each of cannabidiol, tetrahydrocannabinol, and cannabigerol. The liquid distillate comprises 50 wt.% or more ethanol. The liquid distillate comprises a non-cannabinoid molecule and the condensed cannabinoid molecule.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC B05 (Other organics - aromatics, aliphatic, organo-metallics, compounds whose substituents vary such that they would be classified in several of B01 - B05.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

PN CN111607433-A; CN111607433-B
TI Method for cooling and slagging coal ash slag, involves opening sub slag discharge valve and flushing valve, and smoothly taking coal ash and slag away by high-pressure jet water and flow to storage device along slag discharge main pipe.
AU LIU H
   WANG G
   SUN F
   MA L
   WU S
   DUAN H
   TAN X
AE XINNENG ENERGY CO LTD (XINA-C)
   XINNENG ENERGY CO LTD (XINA-C)
GA 202087226S
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves sending a closing signal to a water injection valve (18) by a controller (25), closing water injection valve, and opening a first slag discharge valve (14) when a slag discharge level sensor (17) detects that a water injection level reaches a preset value of the controller. A high-pressure gas at a top of slag discharge tank (3) moves upward into a slag collecting tank (2) and coal ash at a lower portion of the slag collecting tank is disturbed and moved into the slag discharge tank. The first slag discharge valve is closed and pressure relief valve (15) is opened after the slag discharge tank is finished feeding. A second slag discharge valve (19) and slag flushing valve (23) are opened and the coal ash and slag are smoothly taken away by a high-pressure jet water and flow to a slag storage device along a slag discharge main pipe (20) after the detection value of the slag discharge tank pressure sensor is equal to zero.
   USE - Method for cooling and slagging coal ash slag.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide is injected into the slag pipe instead of steam to control the slag discharge speed and cool the slag once. The amount of steam used and the input cost of steam manufacturing equipment and raw coal are reduced. The carbon dioxide enters the fluidized bed gasifier to suppress the reaction of carbon and oxygen in the fluidized bed gasifier to produce carbon dioxide. The reaction of carbon and water vapor is promoted. The cooling water at the liquid level in the slag pipe continuously evaporates into steam and the steam performs secondary treatment on the slag and the steam enters the fluidized bed gasifier through the slag pipe for utilization, which reduces the external steam supply by 5% and reduces the cost of steam input.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the system for cooling and slagging coal ash slag.
   Slag collecting tank (2)
   Slag discharge tank (3)
   First slag discharge valve (14)
   Pressure relief valve (15)
   Slag discharge level sensor (17)
   Water injection valve (18)
   Second slag discharge valve (19)
   Slag discharge main pipe (20)
   Slag flushing valve (23)
   Controller (25)

Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.)

PN KR2020081787-A; KR2271002-B1
TI Air conditioning system comprises a main control unit which is installed in the room A of the building and controls the electric variable air volume ventilation diffuse, and the heat exchanger installed on the duct path.
AU CHUN J K
   PARK S
   HEON H K
AE VE TECH CO LTD (VETE-Non-standard)
   VALUE ENG IND CO LTD (VALU-Non-standard)
GA 202063364B
AB    NOVELTY - Air conditioning system using an oxygen generator and a data base of an electric variable air volume (VAV) ventilation diffuser comprises a main control unit (100) which is installed in the room A of the building and controls the electric VAV ventilation diffuser (102) and the heat exchanger (104) installed on the duct path. The sensors (112,114,116,118) and motor (120) according to the control signal of the diffuser control unit (110) and installed inside the room A of the building at a predetermined interval from the main control unit. The electric VAV ventilation diffuser for controlling is installed on the duct path connected to each room (A), in accordance with the control signal of the heat exchanger control unit (124), desiccator (126), various dampers (130,134,138,142) and fans (144,146) of the total heat exchanger.
   USE - Air conditioning system.
   ADVANTAGE - The air conditioning system has the effect of collecting and sharing the humidity data and carbon dioxide concentration.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a air conditioning system control method, which involves:
   (A) detecting the concentration of compounds (VOCs), temperature, humidity, and carbon dioxide;
   (B) comparing the carbon dioxide concentration to a preset air quality reference value in the main control unit;
   (C) using ventilation diffuser for air inflow through the first filter, desiccator, and air supply by feeding the outside air into the heat exchanger through the outside air supply port, and to the passage of the room; and
   (D) filtering and purifying the inside and outside air by the first and second filters, and the inside and outside air is heat-exchanged by the desiccator, and the inside air is discharged while the temperature is lowered, and heating the outside air by the heat of the bet.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic view of air conditioning system using an oxygen generator and a data base of an electric variable air volume ventilation diffuser.
   Main control unit (100)
   VAV ventilation diffuser (102)
   Heat exchanger (104)
   Diffuser control unit (110)
   Sensors (112,114,116,118)
   Motor (120)
   Heat exchanger control unit (124)
   Desiccator (126)
   Various dampers (130,134,138,142)
   Fans (144,146)
