PT P
PN WO2020092368-A1; US2021025328-A1; US11002192-B2; KR2021083317-A; CN113195689-A; US2021239048-A1; EP3874010-A1; IN202117021870-A; EP3874010-A4; US11473504-B2
TI Processing carbonaceous feedstock involves            producing pressurized oxygen in electrolysis unit,            providing pressurized oxygen to combustion turbine, and            providing liquid fuel to combustion turbine.
AU BOWEN M D
   KELLY R G
AE ARB PYROLYSIS LLC (ARBP-Non-standard)
   BOWEN M D (BOWE-Individual)
   KELLY R G (KELL-Individual)
   ARB PYROLYSIS LLC (ARBP-Non-standard)
   ARB PYROLYSIS LLC (ARBP-Non-standard)
   ARB PYROLYSIS LLC (ARBP-Non-standard)
GA 202037623C
AB    NOVELTY - Processing carbonaceous feedstock involves                producing pressurized oxygen in an electrolysis                unit, providing pressurized oxygen to a combustion                turbine, and providing a liquid fuel to the                combustion turbine such that the liquid fuel mixes                with the pressurized oxygen and combusts within the                combustion turbine.
   USE - Method for processing carbonaceous                feedstock.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to process carbonaceous                feedstock, which has significant increases in the                capacity and efficiency of electrolysis systems                producing pure oxygen and hydrogen at high                pressures.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a system, which comprises pyrolysis                reactor configured to receive feedstock and produce                char and a liquid fuel, a gasifier configured to                receive the char and produce carbon monoxide and                activated carbon, an electrolysis unit configured                to convert water to oxygen and hydrogen, and                combustion turbine configured to receive the liquid                fuel produced by the pyrolysis reactor and to                receive the oxygen produced by the electrolysis                unit and to produce electrical power for use by the                electrolysis unit;
   (2) a gasifier, which comprises body defining                a gasification region, inlets to the gasification                region of the body, where the inlets are vertically                spaced along the body, where the inlets is                configured for passage of carbon dioxide and oxygen                into the gasification region of the body;
   (3) a method for introducing a char into a                gasifier, the gasifier having a gasification                region, which involves injecting carbon dioxide and                oxygen into the gasification region of the gasifier                at multiple locations in the gasification region                such that the carbon dioxide and the oxygen react                with the char to form carbon monoxide, and                withdrawing the carbon monoxide from the gasifier;                and
   (4) a method for providing a pyrolysis                reactor, an electrolysis unit, a combustion                turbine, and a gasifier, introducing a feedstock                into the pyrolysis reactor, producing a liquid fuel                and a char with the pyrolysis reactor, which                involves introducing water to the electrolysis                unit, producing oxygen and hydrogen with the                electrolysis unit, ntroducing the liquid fuel                produced with the pyrolysis reactor to the                combustion turbine, providing the oxygen produced                by the electrolysis unit to the combustion turbine,                producing electrical power with the combustion                turbine, supplying the electrical power produced by                the combustion turbine to the electrolysis unit,                introducing the char to the gasifier, and producing                carbon monoxide with the gasifier.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The liquid fuel is                  produced in a pyrolysis reactor, steam is                  produced using a boiler and supplied the steam to                  the combustion turbine to control a temperature                  of combustion of the liquid fuel, pre-heated feed                  water for the boiler using exhaust from the                  combustion turbine to heat the feed water, and                  condensed liquid from the exhaust of the                  combustion turbine to pre-heat the feed water for                  the boiler, where the pre-heated feed water is                  converted to steam by the boiler, and where the                  steam is provided to the combustion turbine. The                  electrical power is produced with an electric                  generator of the combustion turbine and providing                  the electrical power to the electrolysis unit.                  The pressurized oxygen produced by the                  electrolysis unit is at a pressure of 400 psi. A                  gaseous fuel is produced with the pyrolysis                  reactor and providing the gaseous fuel to a                  boiler, produces heated exhaust with the boiler                  and drying the feedstock with the heated exhaust                  produced by the boiler prior to introducing the                  feedstock into the pyrolysis reactor, and                  pre-heated feed water for the boiler using                  exhaust from the combustion turbine. The liquid                  is condensed from the exhaust of the combustion                  turbine to pre-heat the feed water for the                  boiler, where the pre-heated feed water is                  converted to steam by the boiler, and where the                  steam is provided to the combustion turbine. The                  feedstock is dried before introducing the                  feedstock into the pyrolysis reactor, introduced                  second feedstock into a second pyrolysis reactor                  and producing a second char using the second                  pyrolysis reactor, produced second liquid fuel                  and a second gaseous fuel using the pyrolysis                  reactor, injected oxygen produced by the                  electrolysis unit into a gasification region of                  the gasifier, and carbon dioxide into a                  gasification region of the gasifier. The oxygen                  and carbon dioxideis heated before injecting the                  oxygen and the carbon dioxide into the                  gasification region. The additional electrical                  power is produced using a steam turbine and                  provided additional electrical power to the                  electrolysis unit. The oxygen produced by the                  electrolysis unit is at a pressure of 400 psi.                  Preferred Components: The system comprises a                  boiler, where the boiler is configured to receive                  a gaseous fuel produced by the pyrolysis reactor.                  The boiler is configured to produce steam that is                  supplied to the combustion turbine to control a                  temperature of combustion within the combustion                  turbine. A combined cycle unit configured to heat                  feed water for the boiler, where the combined                  cycle unit is configured to use exhaust from the                  combustion turbine to heat the feed water. The                  combined cycle unit is further configured to                  condense liquid from the exhaust of the                  combustion turbine to pre-heat the feed water for                  the boiler, where the boiler is configured to                  convert the pre-heated feed water to steam for                  use by the combustion turbine. A steam turbine                  configured to receive steam from the boiler and                  to produce additional electrical power for use by                  the electrolysis unit. A a second pyrolysis                  reactor configured to receive a second feedstock                  and produce a second char. The withdrawing                  activated carbon comprises withdrawing activated                  carbon that has a surface area of between about                  600 m2/gram and about 1100 m2/gram. The char is                  heated to a temperature of 300 degrees F before                  introducing the char into the gasifier, injected                  carbon dioxide into a cooling region of the                  gasifier that is vertically below the                  gasification region, injected carbon dioxide and                  oxygen by injecting carbon dioxide and oxygen at                  a first location and at a first flow rate, and                  injecting carbon dioxide and oxygen at a second                  location and at a second flow rate, where the                  first location is below the second location and                  the first flow rate is greater than the second                  flow rate. The gasification region is maintained                  in an isothermal state by controlling the amount                  of carbon dioxide and oxygen injected into the                  gasification region. The carbon dioxide and                  oxygen is injected between 1.0 pounds of carbon                  dioxide and oxygen total per pound of char                  introduced to the gasifier and about 2.0 pounds                  of carbon dioxide and oxygen total per pound of                  char introduced to the gasifier. The amount of                  oxygen injected is between about 0.25 pounds of                  oxygen per pound of char introduced to the                  gasifier and about 0.75 pounds of oxygen per                  pound of char introduced to the gasifier. The                  ratio of carbon dioxide to oxygen injected in the                  gasification region is between about 2 : 1 and                  about 4 : 1. The body further defines a preheat                  region vertically above the gasification region                  such that char introduced to the body is heated                  before entering the gasification region. The                  preheat region is between about 10 feet and about                  20 feet deep.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN110357703-A
TI Preparing slow-release organic fertilizer comprises e.g. mixing sodium alginate, organic bentonite, PEG600, sodium citrate and deionized water to obtain a mixed solution and obtaining an organic fertilizer emulsion.
AU XU Y
AE XU Y (XUYY-Individual)
GA 201988834C
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing slow-release organic fertilizer comprises (i) mixing sodium alginate, organic bentonite, PEG600, sodium citrate and deionized water to obtain a mixed solution, (ii) obtaining an organic fertilizer emulsion, (iii) obtaining microcapsule-coated organic fertilizer particles, (iv) placing the organic fertilizer granules in an ice bath, adding biologically active agent solution, carrying out ultrasonic-assisted mechanical agitation for mixing, lyophilizing and taking out to obtain a slow-release organic fertilizer intermediate and (v) mixing 10-20 pts. wt. cellulose, 0.5-3.5 pts. wt. PEG 6000, 15-30 pts. wt. ethanol and 100 pts. wt. deionized water uniformly to obtain a coating liquid, spraying the coating liquid uniformly on the surface of the slow-release organic fertilizer intermediate particles by a high-pressure spray gun and drying.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing slow-release organic fertilizer.
   ADVANTAGE - The fertilizer improves soil quality and crop quality, has uniform particle size distribution, bioactive agent distribution and synergistic effect and is environment-friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing slow-release organic fertilizer comprises (i) mixing sodium alginate, organic bentonite, PEG600, sodium citrate and deionized water at 40-60 degrees C to obtain a mixed solution, (ii) placing the mixed solution and the organic fertilizer in an autoclave, introducing carbon dioxide, heating to a supercritical state under pressure, stirring and mixing the mixed solution uniformly under the action of supercritical carbon dioxide to form an organic fertilizer emulsion, (iii) reducing the outlet operating pressure of the autoclave and ejecting the organic fertilizer emulsion obtained in step (ii) together with the supercritical carbon dioxide from the outlet nozzle to obtain microcapsule-coated organic fertilizer particles, (iv) placing the organic fertilizer granules in an ice bath, adding biologically active agent solution corresponding to 1-12% by weight of the total mass of the organic fertilizer granules, carrying out ultrasonic-assisted mechanical agitation for mixing, lyophilizing and taking out to obtain a slow-release organic fertilizer intermediate and (v) mixing 10-20 pts. wt. cellulose, 0.5-3.5 pts. wt. PEG 6000, 15-30 pts. wt. ethanol and 100 pts. wt. deionized water uniformly to obtain a coating liquid, spraying the coating liquid uniformly on the surface of the slow-release organic fertilizer intermediate particles by a high-pressure spray gun and drying. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for the slow-release organic fertilizer prepared by using the above mention method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the mass ratio of sodium alginate, organic bentonite, PEG 600, sodium citrate and deionized water is 30-50:10-15:3-8:1-7:50-100. In the step (iii), the particle size of the organic fertilizer particles is 0.5-3 mm. In the step (iv), the biologically active agent solution comprises humic acid, biologically active enzyme, and deionized water in a mass ratio of 1-5:4-15:100. Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the raw material of the organic fertilizer includes agricultural waste, livestock waste, and river sludge, where the organic fertilizer is fermented and rotten by an aerobic composting process. In the step (ii), the temperature of the supercritical state is 38-42 degrees C and the pressure is 8-10 MPa. In the step (ii), the adding amount of the organic fertilizer is equivalent to 5-10% of the total mass of the mixed solution. In the step (iv), the temperature of the ice bath is 0-5 degrees C, the ultrasonic operation in the ultrasonic wave power is 100-200 W and the stirring speed of the mechanical stirring is 500-800 revolutions/minute. In the step (v), the coating liquid is sprayed on the surface of the slow release organic fertilizer intermediate particles to increase the surface weight of the particles by 5-10%.
   ACTIVITY - Fertilizer.

TI Method for controlling cultivation of Hericium            erinaceus in greenhouse, involves mixing water and            culture material, sterilizing, inoculating strain and            culture material into bags, culturing in greenhouse,            spraying water, and harvesting.
AU YU B
   PANG X
   XIE C
   ZHU Q
   HU S
   FAN J
   XIE Z
   XU J
   BAI W
AE ZHENJIANG GUMANYUAN ECOLOGICAL AGRIC CO (ZHEN-Non-standard)
GA 2018A3729X
AB    NOVELTY - Method for controlling cultivation of Hericium                erinaceus in greenhouse, involves adding water to a                culture material, stirring, stacking, sterilizing,                inoculating strain and the culture material into                bags, with inoculation amount of 10-15%, culturing                in a greenhouse at 6-33 degrees C until the                mycelium in the bag is full, maintaining the                surface temperature of 12-24 degrees C, carbon                dioxide content of 0.1% or less, and surface light                of 200~400 lux, spraying water to maintain indoor                air relative humidity of 85-90%, harvesting when                the color of H. erinaceus is white, terminating                spraying of water, ventilating for 3-4 days, and                repeating above process for next batch of harvest.                The culture material comprises 10-60 parts mass                wood chips, 20-70 parts mass bagasse, 5-15 parts                mass Chinese herbal medicine mixture, 3-5 parts                mass dried cow dung, 1-2 parts mass potassium                dihydrogen phosphate and 0.1-0.3 part mass organic                acid.
   USE - Method for controlling cultivation of Hericium                erinaceus in greenhouse.
   ADVANTAGE - The H. erinaceus is cultivated with high                quality and yield. The cultivated H. erinaceus is                rich in nutrition, and has fresh and delicious                taste.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for controlling cultivation of Hericium                erinaceus in greenhouse, involves adding water to a                culture material, stirring, stacking, sterilizing,                inoculating strain and the culture material into                bags, with inoculation amount of 10-15%, culturing                in a greenhouse at 6-33 degrees C until the                mycelium in the bag is full, maintaining the                surface temperature of 12-24 degrees C, carbon                dioxide content of 0.1% or less, and surface light                of 200~400 lux, spraying water to maintain indoor                air relative humidity of 85-90%, harvesting when                the color of H. erinaceus is white, terminating                spraying of water, ventilating for 3-4 days, and                repeating above process for next batch of harvest.                When the H. erinaceus surface is stinging and the                spores are not bombarded, the surface of H.                erinaceus is cleaned after harvesting. The culture                material comprises 10-60 parts mass wood chips,                20-70 parts mass bagasse, 5-15 parts mass Chinese                herbal medicine mixture, 3-5 parts mass dried cow                dung, 1-2 parts mass potassium dihydrogen phosphate                and 0.1-0.3 part mass organic acid. The relative                humidity of air is 55-65%. The content of carbon                dioxide in the air is 0.3-1%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The Chinese herbal                  medicine mixture comprises 5-10 parts mass                  Rehmannia, 5-10 parts mass Astragalus, 2-5 parts                  mass Codonopsis, 5-8 parts mass Angelica, 10-15                  parts mass hawthorn, 10-15 parts mass medlar, 3-8                  parts mass mulberry, 10-20 parts mass jujube and                  2-3 parts mass Atractylodes.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The organic acid is                  citric acid, acetic acid and/or malic acid.

PT P
PN CN108383142-A; CN108383142-B
TI Method for recycling alumina, involves reacting            low-temperature alkaline smelting aluminum ash, fine            aluminum ash slag and mixed smelting agent composed of            sodium hydroxide, sodium nitrate, carbon dioxide,            roasting and calcining.
AU DU J
   MA Y
   WEN Y
   ZHANG M
   HE K
AE MEP SOUTH CHINA ENVIRONMENTAL SCI INST (HNES-C)
   SOUTH CHINA ENVIRONMENTAL SCI INST (HNES-C)
GA 201865647S
AB    NOVELTY - The method for recycling alumina, involves (1)                grinding aluminum ash slag and sieving, cleaning,                and drying to obtain aluminum ash slag powder, (2)                mixing low-temperature alkaline smelting aluminum                ash, fine aluminum ash slag and mixed smelting                agent composed of sodium hydroxide and sodium                nitrate, mixing material and smelting agent, and                smelting, (3) carrying out water leaching, removing                leaching solution, cooling smelted product, mixing                smelting material and water at mass ratio of 1:5,                stirring, controlling leaching temperature at 40-65                degrees C, stirring, leaching and filtering, (4)                carrying out carbon analysis of aluminum hydroxide                precipitated in leachate filtrate, continuously                introducing carbon dioxide and filtering to obtain                aluminum hydroxide precipitation and weak acid                solution, (5) carrying out high temperature                roasting of aluminum hydroxide and calcining.
   USE - Method for recycling alumina.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables recycling alumina ensuring                higher reaction efficiency and reduced reaction                time by simple process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The method for recycling alumina, involves (1)                grinding aluminum ash slag and sieving to particle                size greater than or equal to 150 mesh, cleaning                using ionized water, and drying to obtain aluminum                ash slag powder with particle size less than or                equal to 150 mesh, the volume ratio of aluminum ash                to be washed and cleaning solution is 1:2, washing                time for 1.5 hours at 20 degrees C, drying at 50-80                degrees C for 2 hours and screening less than or                equal to 150 mesh, (2) mixing low-temperature                alkaline smelting aluminum ash, fine aluminum ash                slag and mixed smelting agent composed of sodium                hydroxide and sodium nitrate at mass ratio of 1:1,                mixing material and smelting agent at mass ratio of                1:(0.8-3), and smelting at 260-500 degrees C for                45-100 minutes, (3) carrying out water leaching,                removing leaching solution, cooling smelted                product, mixing smelting material and water at mass                ratio of 1:5, stirring, controlling leaching                temperature at 40-65 degrees C, stirring for 30-45                minutes, leaching for 45-60 minutes and filtering,                (4) carrying out carbon analysis of aluminum                hydroxide precipitated in leachate filtrate,                continuously introducing carbon dioxide and                filtering to obtain aluminum hydroxide                precipitation and weak acid solution, (5) carrying                out high temperature roasting of aluminum hydroxide                at 600-950 degrees C for 0.5-3.5 hours and                subjecting precipitate to high temperature                calcination.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: In step (1), the                  cleaning temperature is controlled at 20 degrees                  C for 1.5 hours.
DC E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.)
MC E34-C01

PN CN114160209-A
TI Preparing copper-zinc/metal-organic framework catalyst useful for carbon dioxide catalytic hydrogenation, comprises grinding mixture of water, zinc oxide and 2,5-dihydroxyterephthalic acid, adding alcoholic solution of copper(II) hydroxide, grinding, washing brown-red powder, drying and grinding.
AU SHI H
   WANG X
   SU H
   LI Y
   WEI Z
   CHEN H
AE CHINA SHIPBUILDING IND CORP 719TH RES (CSHI-C)
GA 2022415591
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing copper-zinc/metal-organic framework (MOF) catalyst comprises (i) grinding a mixture of water, zinc oxide and 2,5-dihydroxyterephthalic acid in an equimolar amount with zinc oxide in a ball mill at a grinding frequency of 30-40 Hz, (ii) after grinding the mixture for 100-120 minutes, adding alcoholic solution of copper(II) hydroxide containing half the molar amount of zinc oxide into the mixture, and grinding for 100-120 minutes, (iii) after grinding, washing brown-red powder formed with alcohol solution and drying overnight, and (iv) grinding material again for 100-120 minutes.
   USE - The copper-zinc/MOF catalyst is useful for carbon dioxide catalytic hydrogenation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method adopts the grinding method, and successively loads zinc and copper on the MOF to obtain the catalyst, the catalytic activity can make the reaction conversion rate of carbon dioxide reach 93% at 140degrees Celsius and room pressure, the yield of the product methanol is as high as 56%, which reduces the requirement of reaction conditions, and solves the technical problem that the catalytic activity of the existing catalyst can only be exerted under high temperature and high pressure, adopts the grinding method and utilizes large-scale grinding machine to realize industrial production, achieves kilogram-level production, and conveniently meets the requirements of large-scale realization of carbon dioxide catalytic hydrogenation to prepare methanol in the future.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Components: The material of the tank body of the grinding ball mill is a polymethyl methacrylate tank, and the grinding ball is 5.4 g tungsten carbide ball. In the step (i), based on 3 mmol of zinc oxide, and the amount of water added is 300 mul. In the step (ii), based on 3 mmol zinc oxide, the amount of copper(II) hydroxide added is 1.5 mmol, and the alcoholic solution of copper(II) hydroxide is 500 mul. In the steps (ii) and (iii), the alcohol solution used is the same alcohol solution, and the alcohol solution is more than 80% methanol or ethanol.Preferred Method: In the step (iii), the formed brown-red powder is washed with an alcohol solution for 3 times. In the step (ii), time of grindings for 2 times is 100 minutes. In the step (iv), the time of grinding is 100 minutes.

TI Method for operating plant for production of cement clinker, involves obtaining oxygen-enriched recirculation gas, and reacting hydrogen with carbon dioxide from certain portion of heat exchanger exhaust gases, to produce methane and water.
AU MERSMANN M
AE KHD HUMBOLDT WEDAG GMBH (KLOH-C)
GA 202213374J
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves introducing oxygen-enriched recirculation gas into a burner for heating a rotary kiln. The rotary kiln exhaust gases are introduced into the calciner, to deacidify the raw meal. The carbon dioxide (CO2) is deposited. The carbon dioxide-enriched gas stream is conducted from the calciner into the heat exchanger. A first part of the heat exchanger exhaust gases, is recycled into the cooler. The portion of the exhaust air from the cooler, is introduced into the rotary kiln. The oxygen-enriched recirculation gas by mixing recirculation gas with oxygen (O2) from a decomposition of water (H2O) in oxygen and hydrogen (H2), is obtained, where the hydrogen with carbon dioxide from a second portion of the heat exchanger exhaust gases, is reacted to produce methane (CH4) and water.
   USE - Method for operating plant for production of cement clinker.
   ADVANTAGE - The energy sources use electricity as an energy carrier or hydrogen as an energy carrier. The energy sources can be integrated into the process for the production of cement clinker and made usable. The clinker chiller is separated in the middle by a gas separator. On the colder side of the clinker cooler, fresh air flows into the clinker cooler, which immediately leaves the clinker cooler as exhaust air after the clinker has cooled.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a plant for the production of cement clinker.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the circuit diagram for the method for operating plant for production of cement clinker.

PN WO2022010874-A1
TI Method for scrubbing acid gas e.g. hydrogen sulfide from gas mixture, involves contacting gas mixture with aqueous solution comprising amine having higher binding selectivity for hydrogen sulfide than that for carbon dioxide under kinetically-controlled contacting conditions, heating, and separating.
AU PEREIRA C S
   SEAGRAVES J P
   NORTHROP P S
   SISKIN M
   INGRAM T
   VORBERG G
   ERNST M
   SIEDER G K
AE EXXONMOBIL RES & ENG CO (ESSO-C)
   BASF AG (BADI-C)
GA 2022075117
AB    NOVELTY - Method for scrubbing acid gas involves (i) contacting a gas mixture (102) comprising hydrogen sulfide with an aqueous solution comprising an amine having a higher binding selectivity for hydrogen sulfide than that for carbon dioxide under kinetically-controlled contacting conditions (a), which undergoes liquid phase separation above a critical solution temperature in an absorber tower (104) under conditions (a), (ii) forming a reaction product (110) of hydrogen sulfide and amine in aqueous solution, (iii) heating aqueous solution at a temperature which is more than or equal to critical solution temperature to form a light phase comprising amine and a heavy phase comprising water, (iv) separating light phase from heavy phase, (v) processing one of light phase or heavy phase to remove some hydrogen sulfide, and (vi) recycling portion(s) of light phase to the tower, after separating light phase from heavy phase. Majority of the reaction product is present in light phase or heavy phase.
   USE - Method useful for scrubbing acid gas e.g. hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide from gas mixture.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple, economical and efficient scrubbing of acid gas e.g. hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide from gas mixture with reduced energy consumption.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows an amine-based system for acid gas scrubbing.
   Gas mixture (102)
   Absorber tower (104)
   Reaction product of hydrogen sulfide and amine (110)
   Regeneration tower (122)
   Amine-based system (200)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The amine comprises sterically hindered secondary amine comprising at least one tertiary alkyl group bound to a nitrogen atom of the amine, and/or tertiary amine. The amine comprises secondary or tertiary amine chosen from compounds of formulae (I)-(VII). The gas mixture further comprises carbon dioxide, and a reaction product of carbon dioxide and amine is formed in the aqueous solution. The reaction product of carbon dioxide and amine is present in the same phase or a different phase than the reaction product of hydrogen sulfide and amine. Binding selectivity for hydrogen sulfide over carbon dioxide is more than or equal to 8, which is the ratio of molar ratio of hydrogen sulfide to carbon dioxide in the aqueous solution with respect to molar ratio of hydrogen sulfide to carbon dioxide in the gas mixture. The aqueous solution comprises 15-70 wt.% amine. The aqueous solution further comprises triethylene glycol monomethyl ether, sulfolane, phosphoric acid and/or sulfuric acid. Preferred Property: The amine has a critical solution temperature of 50-140 degrees C. Preferred Process: The reaction product of hydrogen sulfide and amine is present in the heavy phase, and the light phase is recycled to the absorber tower without being regenerated, and the heavy phase is processed in a regeneration tower (122) to remove some hydrogen sulfide. The reaction product of hydrogen sulfide and amine is present in the light phase, and the light phase is processed in a regeneration tower to remove some hydrogen sulfide before recycling the light phase to the absorber tower. Majority of the reaction product of carbon dioxide and amine is present in the heavy phase. The process further involves separating the light phase from the heavy phase by density liquid separation, processing the heavy phase to remove some carbon dioxide, and returning at least one portion of the heavy phase to the absorber tower after removing some hydrogen sulfide. The light phase and the heavy phase are returned to the absorber tower together in a combined stream, or the light phase and the heavy phase are returned to the absorber tower in separate streams. The gas mixture and the aqueous solution are contacted under counter-current conditions in the absorber tower. The conditions (a) include a residence time of 2 minutes or less in the absorber tower, and a temperature of 30-80 degrees C. One of the light phase or the heavy phase is processed in the regeneration tower at a temperature of 110-150 degrees C.

PN CN113667136-A; CN113667136-B
TI Aluminum-metal organic framework (MOFs) material            used as adsorbent for efficient separation of            acetylene/carbon dioxide, is frame material with            one-dimensional network structure, and has dicarboxylic            acid organic ligand.
AU QIAN G
   LU F
   LI B
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 2021D7349G
AB    NOVELTY - Aluminum-metal organic framework (MOFs)                material is a frame material with a one-dimensional                network structure, and has formula of Al(OH)X,                where X represents a dicarboxylic acid organic                ligand, and the aluminum-MOFs material can be used                as an adsorbent for efficient separation of                acetylene/carbon dioxide.
   USE - The aluminum-MOFs material is used as an                adsorbent for efficient separation of                acetylene/carbon dioxide, and used for application                of gas adsorption and separation.
   ADVANTAGE - The aluminum-MOFs material has high stability                and its application in the gas adsorption and                separation. The MOFs can be used as adsorbents to                achieve high-efficiency separation of                acetylene/carbon dioxide. The preparation method                improves the overall reaction yield and enables the                product to have high crystallinity and uniform                morphology by adjusting the ratio between the                reactants and the solvent, the reaction time and                the reaction temperature in the synthesis process.                The microporous aluminum-MOFs have excellent                chemical stability and structural versatility, and                the cheap cost and easy large-scale synthesis of                aluminum have greatly promoted the industrial                application of this series of materials in the                application of gas adsorption and separation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                preparation method of the aluminum-MOFs material,                involving (a) mixing the dicarboxylic acid organic                ligand, the adjusting agent and the solvent in                proportion, performing ultrasonication to obtain a                clear solution, adding soluble metal salt or metal                salt solution to the solution, ultrasonicating to                obtain a uniform turbid liquid, using an oven or a                magnetic stirrer to react, filtering under suction                after reacting, and drying to obtain aluminum-MOFs                material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The soluble metal salt                  is a soluble aluminum salt. The adjusting agent                  includes one or more of formic acid, acetic acid,                  trifluoroacetic acid, hydrochloric acid,                  hydrofluoric acid, sodium hydroxide, and                  potassium hydroxide, where the use of the                  adjusting agent should be reasonably chosen                  according to the properties of the dicarboxylic                  acid ligand itself, so as to deprotonate the                  dicarboxylic acid ligand without breaking its                  overall structure, and the m-aromatic                  dicarboxylic acid organic ligand is adjusted with                  acid, and the five-membered heterocyclic                  dicarboxylic acid organic ligand is adjusted with                  alkali. The soluble aluminum salt includes one or                  more of aluminum chloride, aluminum sulfate,                  basic aluminum acetate, aluminum nitrate and                  sodium metaaluminate.Preferred Method: During the suction                  filtration, it is necessary to use one of                  deionized water, N,N-dimethylformamide and                  acetonitrile to wash three times, and then use of                  one of the anhydrous methanol, acetone, anhydrous                  ethanol, dichloromethane to washing three times.                  The drying treatment needs to adopt the solvent                  exchange method to exchange the homogeneous                  crystal material obtained after suction                  filtration with one of high-purity methanol,                  high-purity ethanol, and high-purity acetone for                  multiple times, where the interval between each                  time is at least 3hours, and performing vacuum                  drying at room temperature and 50-120degrees                  Celsius for 12 hours, respectively, to obtain the                  dried MOFs material.Preferred Conditions: The ultrasonic                  temperature is 25-50degrees Celsius, and the                  ultrasonic time is 5-120 minutes. The oven                  reaction temperature is 85-150degrees Celsius.                  The oven reaction time is 12-96 hours. The                  stirring temperature of the magnetic stirrer is                  85-150degrees Celsius. The stirring speed of the                  agitator is 200-500 rpm. The reaction time of the                  stirrer is 4-96 hours. The molar ratio of the                  dicarboxylic acid organic ligand to the metal                  salt is 1:(1-3). The ratio of the dicarboxylic                  acid organic ligand, adjusting agent and solvent                  is 1 mmol:(0-25ml):(3-30 ml).Preferred Properties: The size of the                  one-dimensional pore canal of network structure                  is 1-3. In the ethyne/carbon dioxide (50/50)                  mixed gas experiment at 298 K, 1 bar total                  pressure and 2 ml minute-1 total flow, the                  acetylene adsorption of the aluminum-MOFs                  material is greater than 3 mmol g-1, and the                  acetylene/carbon dioxide separation ratio is                  greater than 3.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The dicarboxylic acid                  organic ligand is an m-aromatic ring dicarboxylic                  acid organic ligand or a five-membered                  heterocyclic dicarboxylic acid organic ligand.                  The dicarboxylic acid organic ligands are                  m-aromatic ring dicarboxylic acid organic ligands                  and five-membered heterocyclic dicarboxylic acid                  organic ligands, including isophthalic acid,                  5-aminoisophthalic acid, 5-hydroxyisophthalic                  acid, pyridine-3,5-dicarboxylic acid,                  pyridine-2,4-dicarboxylic acid,                  pyrazine-2,6-dicarboxylic acid,                  pyrimidine-4,6-dicarboxylic acid,                  1,3,5-triazine-2,4-dicarboxylic acid,                  2,5-furandicarboxylic acid,                  2,5-thiophenedicarboxylic acid,                  3,5-pyrazoledicarboxylic acid,                  2,4-furandicarboxylic acid,                  2,4-thiophenedicarboxylic acid, 2,4-pyrrole                  dicarboxylic acid, 3,5-isoxazole dicarboxylic                  acid, 3,5-isothiazole dicarboxylic acid and                  2,5-pyrrole dicarboxylic acid. The solvent                  includes one or more of deionized water, ethanol,                  methanol, acetonitrile, N,N-dimethylformamide,                  N,N-dimethylacetamide, N,N-diethylacetamide,                  N-methylpyrrolidone and dimethylsulfoxide. The                  adjusting agents and solvents were all                  commercially available analytical grade                  reagents.

PT P
PN CN112239223-A
TI Preparing rare earth oxide powder with large surface area, by adding alkali solution to rare earth chloride solution, adjusting pH, passing carbon dioxide gas for carbonization, performing carbonization, filtering, washing and calcining.
AU XIAO Y
   HE Q
   RAO M
   LAI A
   CHAI Z
AE UNIV JIANGXI SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYJL-C)
GA 202109813N
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing rare earth oxide powder with large surface area, involves (i) adding an alkali solution to rare earth chloride solution, adjusting the pH of the rare earth solution to 8-9, where the concentration of the rare earth chloride solution is 0.05-0.50 mol/l, and the rare earth element comprises samarium, europium, gadolinium, terbium, dysprosium, holmium, erbium, thulium, ytterbium, lutetium, scandium and/or yttrium, (ii) passing carbon dioxide gas to the rare earth hydroxide slurry for carbonization reaction, controlling the flow rate of carbon dioxide at 0.05-3 l/minute, performing carbonization reaction at 5-60 degrees C, when the system pH is stable at 4.8-5.2 for 5-120 minutes, stopping the carbonization reaction, filtering and washing to obtain a rare earth precipitation product, and (iii) calcining the rare earth precipitation product at 450-650 degrees C for 0.5-12 hours to obtain the rare earth oxide with a specific surface area greater than 60 m2/g.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing rare earth oxide powder with large surface area.
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively utilizes the solubility product of rare earth hydroxide and rare earth carbonate and homogeneous carbonization of carbon dioxide, and controls the morphology and structure of the rare earth precipitation product through conditions. The rare earth oxide powder with large surface area has low cost and high efficiency.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The rare earth chloride solution used in step (i) is an yttrium chloride solution. The alkaline solution used in step (i) comprises ammonia, sodium hydroxide and/or potassium hydroxide. The rare earth precipitation product obtained in step (ii) has a core-shell structure, the inner core is rare earth hydroxide, and the outer shell is rare earth carbonate. Preferred Method: The step (i) involves precipitating the alkali solution and the rare earth chloride solution in a co-current feeding mode, controlling the temperature of the precipitation process at 40-80 degrees C and the co-current precipitation pH at 5-7, after the feeding of the rare earth chloride solution is completed, and continuously using alkaline solution to adjust the final pH of the precipitation system to 8-9. The method involves adding citric acid, ascorbic acid, acetic acid and/or malonic acid, and polyethylene glycol with a molecular weight of 600-10000 to the rare earth chloride solution in step (i). Preferred Property: The particle size D50 of the rare earth oxide obtained in step (iii) is greater than 5 mu m.

The controller is connected with a voice prompt module that broadcasts window opening or closing voice prompt.
   USE - Flat sliding window opening degree intelligent reminding system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system monitors the opening degree of the sliding window, the indoor temperature, carbon dioxide concentration, PM2.5 concentration and realizing effect of window opening behavior on indoor air quality, human heat comfort and building energy consumption to improve flat sliding window opening monitoring efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method for realizing intelligent reminding control of sliding window opening degree by a flat sliding window opening degree intelligent reminding system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of a flat sliding window opening degree intelligent reminding system. (Drawing includes non-English language text).

PN FR3083459-A1; WO2020007768-A1; FR3083459-B1
TI Surface modification of inorganic microparticles used for preparing composite material, involves functionalizing microparticles using coupling agent, contacting with ethylenically unsaturated monomer, and recovering obtained microparticles.
AU PONCELET O
   BABLET J
   CHARIERE R
   GETTO D
   LIRAUT G
   OLIVIER P
   JACQUELINE B
   ROMAIN C
   DANIEL G
   GERARD L
AE COMMISSARIAT ENERGIE ATOMIQUE (COMS-C)
   RENAULT SAS (RENA-C)
   COMMISSARIAT ENERGIE ATOMIQUE (COMS-C)
   RENAULT SAS (RENA-C)
GA 202003841X
AB    NOVELTY - Surface modification of inorganic microparticles involves (i) arranging inorganic microparticles comprising reactive hydroxyl groups on the surface, (ii) functionalizing the microparticles on the surface by covalent grafting in supercritical carbon dioxide medium using a coupling agent which is soluble in supercritical carbon dioxide and comprising at least one group capable of interacting with the hydroxyl group to form a bond covalent and at least one group with ethylenic unsaturation(s) which is polymerizable by the radical route, (iii) contacting the obtained surface functionalized microparticles in supercritical carbon dioxide medium with at least one ethylenically unsaturated monomer(s) which is soluble in supercritical carbon dioxide and polymerizable by the radical route under conditions suitable for radical polymerization of the monomer, and (iv) recovering the inorganic microparticles coated with a polymeric layer on its surface.
   USE - Surface modification of inorganic microparticles used as fillers for preparing composite material (all claimed). Uses include but are not limited to aeronautical, aerospace, automotive, rail, and marine applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple and economical surface modification of inorganic microparticles with reduced generation of by-products. The inorganic microparticles provide composite material having improved mechanical properties, preferably elongation at break and/or tensile stress.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) inorganic microparticles, which have an average size of 1 mu m or more and coated with the polymeric layer on its surface;
   (2) use of surface modified inorganic microparticles as fillers for preparing a composite material comprising a polymer matrix of polyolefin(s), preferably polyethylene and/or polypropylene; and
   (3) composite material, which comprises the polymer matrix which are dispersed in the surface modified inorganic microparticles.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The inorganic microparticles are silica microparticles, preferably hollow silica microparticles. Preferred Property: The inorganic microparticles preferably have average size of 10-30 mu m.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The inorganic microparticles comprising reactive hydroxyl groups on its surface are obtained after performing a surface dehydration treatment in supercritical carbon dioxide medium. The covalent grafting step involves forming a urethane or ester-type covalent bond, preferably forming urethane-type covalent bond by reacting hydroxyl group at the surface of the inorganic microparticles with an isocyanate group carried by the coupling agent. The covalent grafting of the coupling agent on the surface of the inorganic microparticles is carried out at a temperature of 32-100 degrees C, preferably 50-85 degrees C and a pressure of 80-350 degrees C, preferably 200-300 bar. The radical polymerization step is in step (iii) is initiated in the presence of a free radical initiator, preferably chosen from azo-type initiators and peroxo-type initiators. The radical polymerization reaction in step (iii) is carried out in supercritical carbon dioxide at a temperature of 70-85 degrees C, preferably 80 degrees C and a pressure of 200-300 bar, preferably 300 bar. The steps (i)-(iii) are carried out within the same reactor, without removing the inorganic microparticles from the reactor. The particles are recovered in step (iv) after one or more washing steps in supercritical carbon dioxide, and then depressurization of the reactor. Preferred Composition: The coupling agent is a (meth)acrylate- or allyl group-containing compound of formula: F'-Ei-G. The ethylenically unsaturated group(s) of the coupling agent is chosen from acrylate, methacrylate, allyl and vinyl groups, preferably (meth)acrylate group or allyl groups. The radical polymerizable monomer comprises at least two ethylenically unsaturated groups, preferably di(meth)acrylate compounds. The monomers used in step (iii) are methacrylate group-containing compound of formula: of formula (II): G1-G2-E2. The monomers used in step (iii) comprises a polyalkoxylated chain, preferably ethylene glycol oligomer-type polyethoxylated chain having a weight average molecular weight of 6000 g/mol or less, preferably 200-6000 g/mol. The monomer(s) are chosen from poly(ethylene glycol) di(meth)acrylate, preferably mixture of diethylene glycol di(meth)acrylate and oligo(ethylene glycol) di(meth)acrylate. The azo-type initiator is at least one chosen from 1-azobisdimethylvaleronitrile, 1-azobisisobutyronitrile and azomethylbutyronitrile. The peroxo-type initiator is chosen from di-t-butyl peroxide, t-butylcumyl peroxide, t-butyl peroxypivalate, lauryl peroxide, cumene hydroperoxide, ethylhexyl peroxydicarbonate, diisopropyl peroxydicarbonate, 4-(t-butylperoxyperoxycarbonyl)-3-hexyl-6-7-(t-butylperoxycarbonyl)heptylcyclohexene, cumene hydroperoxide, t-butyl peroxyneodecanoate, t-butyl hydroperoxide and benzoyl peroxide.F' = group capable of reacting with the hydroxyl group on the surface of the inorganic microparticles to form a covalent bond, preferably isocyanato;G = ethylenically unsaturated group(s), preferably (meth)acrylate or allyl;Ei = organic spacer group, preferably ethylene;G1,G2 = groups comprising ethylenic unsaturation(s), preferably methacrylate;andE2 = organic group, preferably polyalkoxylated chain.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The composite material has a loading rate of inorganic microparticles of 5-60 vol.%, preferably 30-50 vol.%.

PN CN110586108-A
TI Catalyst useful for preparing methanol by carbon            dioxide hydrogenation gasoline to produce low-carbon            olefin, comprises gamma-aluminum trioxide as a carrier,            where the carrier is doped with iron, cobalt, and            sodium.
AU DAI C
   ZHAO X
   SHI X
   HU B
AE UNIV NORTHWEST (UYXB-C)
GA 2019A76999
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst comprises gamma -aluminum trioxide as                a carrier, where the carrier is doped with 8.3 wt.%                iron, 1.7 wt.% cobalt, and 1.8-7.4 wt.%                sodium.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for preparing methanol                by carbon dioxide hydrogenation gasoline to produce                low-carbon olefin (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: (i) adopts doping cobalt in                iron-based catalysts to reduce the selectivity of                by-product carbon monoxide, can promotes                iron-cobalt bimetal by sodium, which has a high                selectivity of low-carbon olefins and gasoline                fraction hydrocarbons, so that the Fe-Co-Na/ gamma                -Al2O3 catalyst is catalyzes the hydrogenation of                carbon dioxide to produce gasoline clean oils,                increases the olefin ratio of low-carbon                hydrocarbons, and successfully suppresses the                selectivity of the undesired products methane and                carbon monoxide; (ii) adopts Fe-Co-Na/ gamma -Al2O3                catalyst, which has a high conversion rate of CO2;                and (iii) adopts Fe-Co-Na// gamma -Al2O3 catalyst                to catalyze the carbon dioxide hydrogenation                reaction with a large space velocity of CO2.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing the catalyst, comprising (i) adding                soluble iron salts, soluble cobalt salt and                disodium EDTA to deionized water, and performing                ultrasonic to form a uniform complex solution                without precipitation; (ii) adding gamma -aluminum                trioxide ( gamma -Al2O3) into the above complex                solution and keep stirring, mixing gamma -Al2O3                with the complexing solution; and (iii) placing the                stirred solution into the oven and drying it                overnight, grinding and calcining the solid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the                  soluble iron salt is iron(iii) nitrate                  nonahydrate. In the step (i), the soluble cobalt                  salt is cobalt nitrate hexahydrate. Preferred                  Method: In the step (i), the ultrasonic is                  performed under a heating condition at 50 degrees                  C. In the step (i), the temperature of the oven                  is 80 degrees C. In the step (iii), the baking                  temperature is 500-600 degrees C, and the baking                  time is 4-6 hours.

PN CN110479235-A; CN110479235-B
TI Indium oxide catalyst used in fixed bed for            hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to produce methanol, is            hexagonal indium oxide with nano-multilevel            structure.
AU GAO P
   DANG S
   SUN Y
AE SHANGHAI ADVANCED RES INST CHINESE ACAD (CAAR-C)
GA 2019A0504R
AB    NOVELTY - Indium oxide catalyst is a hexagonal indium                oxide with a nano-multilevel structure.
   USE - Indium oxide catalyst used in a fixed bed for                hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to produce methanol                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The indium oxide catalyst has the advantages                of excellent catalytic performance, high                reactivity, high selectivity of target products,                good reaction stability, and low catalyst                deactivation rate, and carbon dioxide conversion                rate is as high as 29.3%, and methanol selectivity                up to 98.6%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for                following:
   (1) a method for preparing an indium oxide                catalyst, which involves dissolving the metal                indium salt in the first solvent to prepare a metal                indium salt solution;dissolving the precipitant in                a second solvent to prepare a precipitant                solution;precipitant solution is added dropwise to                the metal indium salt solution and stirred to form                a precipitation mother liquor;placing precipitation                mother liquor in a hydrothermal synthesis reaction                kettle, and placing hydrothermal synthesis reaction                kettle in an oven to age the precipitation mother                liquor and centrifuging aged mother liquor, drying                and roasting in order to obtain the indium oxide                catalyst; and
   (2) using indium oxide catalyst, which                involves using indium oxide catalyst in a fixed bed                for the reaction of carbon dioxide hydrogenation to                produce methanol, activating indium oxide catalyst                and the conditions for the activation are an inert                gas atmosphere, raising gradient temperature to                200-400 degrees C for 1-3 hours, where inert gas                includes one or more of argon, nitrogen and                helium.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The nano-multilevel                  structure comprises a flower-like structure                  formed by nanowires. The metal indium salt                  includes one or two of indium nitrate and indium                  chloride. The first solvent includes a mixed                  solution of absolute ethanol and deionized water.                  The second solvent includes a mixed solution of                  absolute ethanol and deionized water. Preferred                  Compositions: The concentration of the metal                  indium salt solution is between 0.1-1 mole per                  litre. The concentration of the precipitation                  mother liquor is between 1-3 mole per liter.                  Preferred Conditions: The stirring time is                  between 0.5-9 hours. The aging temperature is                  between 80-160 degrees C, and the aging time is                  12-22 hours. The aged precipitation mother liquor                  is centrifuged with deionized water until the pH                  of the precipitation mother liquor is between 6                  and 8.

PN WO2019211381-A1; CA3097937-A1; DK202070743-A; IN202047051695-A; AU2019263657-A1; KR2021005710-A; BR112020022175-A2; EP3787774-A1; US2021114892-A1; CN112912163-A; JP2021522159-W
TI Preparing ikaite as unit for storage/reduction of            carbon dioxide, involves contacting alkaline aqueous            solution which comprises carbonate and bicarbonate            ions, with a aqueous solution, and which comprises            calcium ion.
AU TRAMPE E
AE CLIMATE SOLUTIONS APS (CLIM-Non-standard)
   CLIMATE SOLUTIONS APS (CLIM-Non-standard)
   CLIMATE SOLUTIONS APS (CLIM-Non-standard)
   CLIMATE SOLUTIONS APS (CLIM-Non-standard)
GA 201993816B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing ikaite (CaC03 6H20) involves                contacting alkaline aqueous solution which                comprises carbonate and bicarbonate ions, with a                aqueous solution, which comprises calcium ion at a                temperature not exceeding 15 degrees C, where                contact between the alkaline aqueous solution and                the aqueous solution takes place at a permeable or                porous surface, through which the alkaline aqueous                solution is fed to the aqueous solution or through                which the aqueous solution is fed to the alkaline                aqueous solution at a flow rate facilitating                formation of ikaite crystals.
   USE - Method for preparing ikaite as unit for                storage/reduction of carbon dioxide                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare ikaite that is                fast and cost efficient.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a method for storing/reducing of carbon                dioxide, which involves preparing alkaline aqueous                solution by addition of carbon dioxide to alkaline                aqueous medium, subsequently preparing ikaite or                preparing calcite and subsequently storing the                ikaite and/or carbonate forms produced; and
   (2) a system for preparation of ikaite, which                comprises one compartment for holding an alkaline                aqueous solution, which comprises carbonate and                bicarbonate, where one compartment is used for                holding a aqueous solution, which comprises calcium                ion, one permeable or porous structure for                separating the alkaline aqueous solution and the                aqueous solution, permeable or porous structure                allowing feeding through one surface of the                structure of the alkaline aqueous solution to the                aqueous solution or aqueous solution to the                alkaline aqueous solution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: The pH of alkaline                  aqueous solution is 8-12. The pH of aqueous                  solution is more than 7.0. The preparation is                  carried out at 13 degrees C. The flow rate is                  increased after ikaite crystals is nucleated.                  Preferred Components: The carbonate and                  bicarbonate ions of the alkaline aqueous solution                  primarily are in the form of sodium carbonate and                  sodium bicarbonate. The alkaline aqueous solution                  comprises a surplus of bicarbonate ions relative                  to carbonate ions. The aqueous solution comprises                  an excess of calcium ion relative to carbonate                  and bicarbonate in the alkaline aqueous solution.                  The aqueous solution is natural seawater.                  Preferred Process: The ikaite crystalizes on of                  the permeable or porous surface. The gaseous                  carbon dioxide is supplied to a carbonate                  solution, preferably to a sodium or potassium                  carbonate solution. The method is carried out at                  sea at a depth where the ambient temperature does                  not exceed 15 degrees C. The ikaite is                  subsequently converted to calcite or other                  carbonate forms.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.); ; J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); J06 (Storing or distributing gases or liquids - including gas holders, vessels for gases, decantation and vaporisation of gases, pipelines and pipe systems, but excluding those for hydrocarbon gases or liquids and laying of pipelines (F17).)

PT P
PN CN110354631-A; CN110354631-B
TI Preparing ionic liquid-activated carbon composite            adhesive useful for absorbing carbon dioxide, comprises            e.g. mixing 1-alkyl-3-vinylimidazolium ionic liquid            halide with pure water, adding polyamine fluoroborate            ionic liquid and heating.
AU CHEN H
   LI K
   WANG X
   ZHAO B
AE UNIV SHANDONG SCI & TECHNOLOGY (SDST-C)
GA 201988899J
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing ionic liquid-activated carbon                composite adhesive comprises mixing 1 mol                1-alkyl-3-vinylimidazolium ionic liquid halide                (RVIMX) with certain amount of pure water and                activated carbon, where mass ratio between pure                water and activated carbon is 3:1, mechanically                aging, sonicating, heating at 80 degrees C for 6-18                hours, then heating at 95 degrees C for 12-36 hours                to obtain mixed black viscose, (ii) transferring                the above adhesive into sealed reaction kettle,                adding 0.20-0.5 mol polyamine fluoroborate ionic                liquid (MuABF4), then adding 0.5 g phase transfer                catalyst to seal the reaction vessel, passing                nitrogen or argon gas at 0.5 MPa, magnetically                stirring, heating at 120-180 degrees C for 8-48                hours, and (iii) cooling to room temperature to                obtain black flowable adhesive body as final                product.
   USE - The adhesive is useful for absorbing carbon                dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The adhesive: has stable chemical adsorption,                fluidity and water and air stability. The method                has fast adsorption rate, simple desorption and is                safe and environmentally friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) ionic liquid-activated carbon composite                adhesive obtained by above method; and
   (2) use method comprising placing the                composite adhesive product in carbon dioxide                absorption device for absorption, transferring                composite adhesive product into the desorption tank                through the valve, heating at 60-95 degrees C for                0.5-3 hours, collecting carbon dioxide, desorbing                and reusing the ionic liquid-activated carbon                composite adhesive.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The                  1-alkyl-3-vinylimidazolium ionic liquid halide                  (RVIMX) is of formula (I). The polyamine                  fluoroborate ionic liquid (MuABF4) is ionic                  liquid in which the cation is diethylenetriamine                  or triethylenetetramine and the anion is                  bis(2-aminoethyl)ammonium-tetrafluoroborate or                  N1,N2-bis(2-aminoethyl)ethane-1,2-diaminium-tetrafluoroborate.                  The activated carbon is the activated carbon                  powder particles of greater than or equal to 300                  mesh; phase transfer catalyst is                  octadecyltrimethylammonium chloride or                  tetrabutylammonium bromide; and in step (i), the                  rate of mechanical agitation is 500                  revolutions/minute. The activated carbon is the                  coconut shell activated carbon of 500-2000 mesh.                  The molar ratio between                  1-alkyl-3-vinylimidazolium ionic liquid halide                  (RVIMX) and polyamine fluoroborate ionic liquid                  (MuABF4) is 1:0.5.R = 1-8C alkyl, preferably ethyl, butyl,                  hexyl or octyl; andX = Cl or Br.

TI Performing indoor biological aeration and carbon reduction method comprises controlling the growth rate of microorganisms in the photobioreactor by adjusting the water flow rate, gas flow rate, light intensity.
AU ZHOU J
   ZHOU Y
   CHOW K F
   CHOW Y

   WOOFAA SMART TECH JIANGMEN CO LTD (WOOF-Non-standard)
GA 201989805F
AB    NOVELTY - Performing indoor biological aeration and carbon reduction method comprises controlling the growth rate of microorganisms in the photobioreactor by adjusting the water flow rate, gas flow rate, light intensity and running time of the photobioreactor according to the concentration of carbon dioxide in the air in the indoor field of the photobioreactor.
   USE - Method for performing indoor biological aeration and carbon reduction method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to perform indoor biological aeration and carbon reduction method, which replaces the traditional fresh air external circulation ventilation system to solve the indoor carbon dioxide accumulation problem, and solves the indoor and outdoor temperature difference from the root cause.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an indoor biological aeration and carbon reduction system, which comprises a photobioreactor, a functional device for cultivating microorganisms to produce photosynthesis, realizing oxygenation and carbon reduction, and an air sensor for detecting a concentration of carbon dioxide in the air of the indoor field where the photobioreactor is located, and a controller electrically connected to the photobioreactor and the air sensor, respectively, the controller according to the concentration of carbon dioxide sent by the air sensor A signal is used to control the growth rate of microorganisms within the photobioreactor by adjusting the water flow rate of the water pump in the photobioreactor, the gas flow rate of the air pump, and the light intensity of the light source and their respective operating times.

AU LI S
   NIE Y
   YANG T
   LI C
   SI Y
   CHEN Z
   DENG J
   ZHENG G
   YANG C
AE LUZHOU HONGDA ORGANIC CHEM PLANT (LUZH-Non-standard)
GA 2019358174
AB    NOVELTY - Producing 2,5-dimethyl-2,5-hexanediol comprises carrying out catalytic hydrogenation reaction of acetylene and acetone as main raw materials in the presence of benzene as solvent, and potassium hydroxide as catalyst, carrying out alkyne reaction, and neutralizing the material with potassium hydroxide under carbon dioxide atmosphere to obtain final product.
   USE - The method is useful for producing 2,5-dimethyl-2,5-hexanediol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes carbon dioxide instead of hydrogen chloride to neutralize potassium hydroxide; has no special requirements for production equipment, no additional corrosion to the equipment, no need to change equipment frequently, and high implicit negative costs; is environmentally friendly; and utilizes the potassium carbonate produced after the reaction as by-product.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for 2,5-dimethyl-2,5-hexanediol producing device comprising alkyne reaction device, a neutralization reaction device, a carbon dioxide storage tank, a potassium carbonate salt receiving groove, the alkaline water receiving groove, potassium carbonate drying and recycling device and a benzene solution oil phase pump, where bottom of the alkyne reaction device connecting top of the neutralizing reaction device through pipeline, the carbon dioxide storage tank connected to bottom of the neutralizing reaction device through the pipeline, the potassium fluotitanate salt receiving groove and alkali water receiving slot also connected with bottom of the neutralization reaction device through the pipeline, the benzene solution oil phase pump connected through the pipeline with the neutralization reaction device bottom, the potassium fluotitanate salt receiving groove connected with potassium carbonate drying and recycling device, the pipeline is provided with a valve.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide enters from the bottom of the material. The carbon dioxide is introduced so that the pH of the benzene phase reaches 6-7. Preferred Method: The method comprises discharging the product of the neutralization reaction from the bottom of the neutralization reaction vessel and drying for recovery.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide storage tank is further provided with a carbon dioxide pressure reducing device and a carbon dioxide pressure reducing device. The potassium carbonate drying recycling device is further provided with a draught fan.

PN CN108795317-A
TI Method of modifying nano calcium carbonate for silicone sealant, involves adding surface treatment agent to calcium carbonate slurry, surface-treating, adding sodium stearate, obtaining modified slurry and pressure-filtering.
AU WANG Z
   WANG P
AE WUHU TANGHUA BUILDING MATERIALS TECHNOLO (WUHU-Non-standard)
GA 201893296L
AB    NOVELTY - Method of modifying nano calcium carbonate involves adding acrylic emulsion and sodium carboxymethyl cellulose to mixed slurry formed of chitosan, turning on emulsification disperser, subjecting mixed slurry to cyclic emulsification dispersion, introducing carbon dioxide gas and mixed slurry into emulsification disperser when the average particle diameter (D50) is 0.6 mu m or less, emulsifying, dispersing, subjecting mixed slurry to carbonization tower, circulating mixed slurry between carbonization tower and emulsification disperser for secondary carbonization and emulsification, carbonizing to pH of 7-7.5, terminating introduction of carbon dioxide gas, obtaining calcium carbonate slurry, heating to 60-80 degrees C, adding surface treatment agent to calcium carbonate slurry, performing surface treatment, stirring for 20-30 minutes, adding sodium stearate, stirring for 30-50 minutes, obtaining modified calcium carbonate slurry, subjecting to pressure filtration, drying and pulverizing.
   USE - Method of modifying nano calcium carbonate for silicone sealant.
   ADVANTAGE - The method efficiently provides modified nano calcium carbonate having excellent reinforcing performance, tensile property, heat resistance and water resistance, long storage period and stable product performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The mixed slurry is obtained by adding chitosan to saturated calcium hydroxide slurry, placing in carbonization tower, agitating, pumping treated calcium hydroxide slurry into emulsification disperser, simultaneously mixing carbon dioxide gas and calcium hydroxide slurry, reacting into emulsification disperser, emulsifying, dispersing, continuously subjecting mixed slurry to carbonization tower, continuously circulating calcium hydroxide slurry between carbonization tower and emulsification disperser for carbonization and emulsification dispersion, carbonizing to the pH of 8-10 and terminating the passage of carbon dioxide gas. The surface treatment agent is composed of oleic acid and soybean oil.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The addition amount of chitosan is 5-10 wt.% with respect to content of saturated calcium hydroxide slurry. The total amount of acrylic emulsion and sodium carboxymethylcellulose added is 0.6-1.8% with respect to mixed slurry in carbonization column. The addition amount of compounding surface treatment agent is 1.5-3.5 wt.% with respect to weight of calcium carbonate slurry. The addition amount of sodium stearate is 1-2 wt.% with respect to content of calcium carbonate pulp.

GA 201851560V

The light phase in first splitter is sent into upper portion of absorption tower at low temperature through first absorbent pipeline (7). An inter-stage cooler (6) is set on the first absorbent pipeline and the low temperature absorption tower is provided with a second splitter (5). The light phase in the second splitter sends the light phase into upper portion of high temperature absorption tower through second absorbent pipeline (8).
   USE - Flue gas carbon dioxide absorption device based on two-phase absorbent.

PN US2022042741-A1
TI Method for compressing gas, involves condensing            fourth compressed gas into liquified gas, and            collecting liquefied gas, and cooling and compressing            third gas at third compressor to obtain fourth            compressed gas.
AU BRIGGS T
   RICHARDSON R
AE BELL ENG INC (BELL-Non-standard)
GA 202222198M
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves compressing a gas i.e.                carbon dioxide, at a first stage compressor to                create a first compressed gas. The first gas is                cooled. The compressed gas is compressed at a                second stage compressor. The second gas is                condensed into a second compressed gas that is                cooled. The second gas at a third stage compressor                is compressed to produce a third compressed gas.                The third gas at the third compressor is cooled and                compressed to obtain a fourth compressed gas, which                is condensed to produce liquefied gas. A gas line                that delivers the gas into the gas line.
   USE - Method for compressing gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables compressing the gas at the                first stage compressor to create the compressed gas                in an effective manner. The method allows the gas                to be compressed at the second stage compressor and                the third stage compressor in an efficient manner,                so that the liquefied gas can be delivered into the                gas line in a cost effective manner, thus                preventing the gas from backflowing into the inlet                piping, and hence increasing the gas volume with                the rising liquid level resulting in an increase in                pressure inside the vessel, and increasing the                density of the gas. The liquid is pumped into and                out of the vessel to draw in carbon dioxide,                compress and displace the compressed vapor into the                discharge header, thus improving the efficiency of                the process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:

PN WO2021234191-A1
TI Method for capturing carbon dioxide in gas, involves introducing stream of air in second reactor and source of nutrients reduced by nitrogen such that solid biomass within resulting liquid in second reactor is generated.
AU COLZI LOPES A
   ESTRADA PEREZ J M
AE TROVANT TECHNOLOGY SL (TROV-Non-standard)
GA 2021D5253J
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves introducing an alkaline solution (4) in a first reactor (1) and bubbling the gas (3) so that carbon dioxide is absorbed in the form of carbonates in the alkaline solution. A first liquid (6) comprising solubilized carbonates is formed. A clean gas stream (5) is formed in an upper portion of the first reactor. The first liquid is fed to a second reactor (2) which comprises chemoautotrophic nitrifying microorganisms (7). A stream of air (9) in the second reactor and a source of nutrients (8) reduced by nitrogen are introduced, so that the chemoautotrophic nitrifying microorganisms use the carbonates solubilized from the first liquid for growth to generate solid biomass within a resulting liquid (11) in the second reactor.
   USE - Method for capturing carbon dioxide in gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The resulting liquid after the biomass generation is recirculated to the first reactor acting as an alkaline solution, thus improving the overall resource efficiency of the process. The portion of the biomass generated in the second reactor in the anaerobic digestion is reused to produce the starting biogas, improving the overall efficiency of both processes in synergy.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the method for capturing carbon dioxide in the gas.
   First reactor (1)
   Second reactor (2)
   Gas (3)
   Alkaline solution (4)
   Clean gas stream (5)
   First liquid (6)
   Chemoautotrophic nitrifying microorganisms (7)
   Source of nutrients (8)
   Stream of air (9)
   Resulting liquid (11)

PN CN113500053-A
TI Method for preparing high-purity polypropylene, involves transferring dispersion liquid into supercritical device, injecting carbon dioxide into supercritical device, stirring for 3-6h under supercritical condition, naturally cooling to room temperature, releasing pressure, and separating system.
AU ZHANG P
   FU H
   LI G
   WANG J
AE SINOPEC ZHONGYUAN PETROCHEMICAL CORP LTD (SINO-Non-standard)
GA 2021C0438G
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves dispersing the polypropylene to be treated into ethanol to obtain dispersion liquid. The dispersion liquid is transferred into a supercritical device, carbon dioxide is injected into the supercritical device, stirred for 3-6h under the supercritical condition, naturally cooled to room temperature, and pressure is released. The system is separated after the supercritical treatment, washed and dried to obtain the high-purity polypropylene. The dosage ratio of the raw material polypropylene to be treated and the ethanol is (50-150) g to (100-200) mL. The temperature is 60-100 deg.C, and the pressure is 15-20 Mpa. The volume portion of the ethanol is 50-90 v%. The separation mode is filtration. The washing is carried out for several times by alternately adopting absolute ethyl alcohol and water.
   USE - Method for preparing high-purity polypropylene by supercritical carbon dioxide. Uses include but are not limited to electronic appliance, medicine, food and textile.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and easy, environment-friendly and pollution-free, and has important application prospect in the field of capacitor and battery. The method directly processes commercialized polypropylene granules, using supercritical carbon dioxide as medium to realize polyethylene glycol deliming, which is more efficient than directly using chemical solvent washing, which can effectively reduce the use of washing time and harmful solvent in the whole PP purification process.
