PN WO2021204618-A1
TI Low-temperature stable isocyanate prepolymer obtained by reacting isocyanate-reactive component comprising polyether carbonate polyol with isocyanate component comprising methylene diphenyl diisocyanate to produce foam, adhesive or sealant.
AU MARBACH J
   KARAFILIDIS C
   MARAZITA M
   SCHUMACHER M
   ARNTZ H
AE COVESTRO DEUT AG (CVST-C)
GA 2021B6597L
AB    NOVELTY - Low-temperature stable isocyanate (NCO) prepolymer obtained from the reaction of (A) an isocyanate-reactive component comprising at least one polyether carbonate polyol obtained from the reaction of a starter molecule with carbon dioxide and an alkylene oxide including ethylene oxide and/or propylene oxide with (B) an isocyanate component comprising methylene diphenyl diisocyanate containing at least 95 wt.% 4,4'-methylene diphenyl diisocyanate. The NCO prepolymer has an NCO content of 9-18 wt.%. The polyether carbonate polyol contains 5-25 wt.% carbon dioxide. The polyether carbonate polyol has monomer units originating from the carbon dioxide and from the alkylene oxide, and in each case at least 50% of the monomer units originating from the carbon dioxide and from the alkylene oxide are statistically distributed in the polyether carbonate polyol.
   USE - The prepolymer is useful for producing polyurethane elastomer, foam, adhesive or sealant (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The prepolymer has increased reactivity compared to other methylene diphenyl diisocyanate isomers even at low temperatures; and does not require heating step or storage at higher temperatures.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) producing low-temperature-stable NCO prepolymer, comprising (i) providing an isocyanate-reactive component (A) comprising at least one polyether carbonate polyol and isocyanate component (B) comprising methylene diphenyl diisocyanate, (ii) mixing the components (A) and (B) in order to obtain a mixture, (iii) allowing the obtained mixture to react in order to obtain the low-temperature-stable NCO prepolymer; and
   (2) polyurethane elastomer, foam, adhesive or sealant obtained from the reaction of a low-temperature-stable NCO prepolymer with an isocyanate-reactive compound.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The isocyanate component comprises methylene diphenyl diisocyanate containing at least 95 wt.% 4,4'-methylenediphenyl diisocyanate. The (A) isocyanate-reactive component contains at least 60 wt.%, preferably at least 85 wt.% at least one polyether carbonate polyol based on the entire (A) component reactive towards isocyanate. The component reactive towards isocyanate comprises at least one polyether carbonate polyol. The polyether carbonate polyol has an hydroxy number of 24-250 mg potassium hydroxide/g; and carbon dioxide content of 7-22 wt.%, preferably 9-21 wt.%. The polyether carbonate polyol has two isocyanate-reactive groups. In each case at least 75 wt.%, preferably at least 95% of the monomer units originating from the carbon dioxide and from the alkylene oxide are statistically distributed in the polyether carbonate polyol. The isocyanate-reactive compound is water from an environment of the cold-stable NCO prepolymer, preferably the environment being a gas atmosphere surrounding the cold-stable NCO prepolymer or a substrate , on which the low-temperature-stable NCO prepolymer is located.
   EXAMPLE - None given.

TI Preparation of electrochemically self-doped            titanium dioxide nanotube-based material used for            gas-phase photoelectric catalytic reduction of carbon            dioxide by preparing self-doped titanium dioxide            nanotube, and reacting with metal precursor.
AU ZHANG Y
   PAN H
   SUN M
   WANG X
AE UNIV HUAZHONG SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYHZ-C)
GA 2021812821
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of electrochemically self-doped                titanium dioxide nanotube-based material comprises                using the titanium dioxide nanotube as working                electrode in the three-electrode system, applying                reduction voltage to electrochemically reduce                titanium dioxide to titanium ion (Ti3+), and                introducing Ti3+ and oxygen vacancy defects in                titanium dioxide nanotube to obtain self-doped                titanium dioxide nanotube with Ti3+ doped in the                titanium dioxide lattice; and using self-doped                titanium dioxide nanotube as working electrode in                the three-electrode system, putting metal precursor                in the electrolyte solution, reacting with                self-doped titanium dioxide nanotube under the                action of square wave pulse voltage to form a                covalent bond with Ti3+, and reducing to simple                metal and depositing on the wall of titanium                dioxide nanotube.
   USE - The method is for preparation of                electrochemically self-doped titanium dioxide                nanotube-based material used for gas-phase                photoelectric catalytic reduction of carbon dioxide                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The material exhibits higher                photo-electron-hole separation efficiency and                catalytic activity, overcomes the traditional                liquid-phase photo-electric catalysis needs to add                electrolyte, low carbon dioxide solubility, product                is difficult to separate, and the generated product                is gaseous alkane hydrocarbon and oxygen mixture,                easy to separate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                application of the electrochemically self-doped                titanium nanotube-based material comprising                applying electric potential and light source to                electrochemically self-doped titanium                nanotube-based material, and subjecting to                gas-phase photoelectric catalytic reduction                reaction with carbon dioxide to form alkanes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Components: The metal precursor is                  gold salt, platinum salt, silver salt or copper                  salt. The gold salt is chloroauric acid,                  potassium chloroauric acid or sodium chloroauric                  acid. The platinum salt is chloroplatinic acid,                  potassium chloroplatinate or sodium                  chloroplatinate. The silver salt is silver                  nitrate, silver chlorate or silver sulfate. The                  copper salt is copper sulfate, copper chloride or                  copper nitrate.

PT P
PN CN112597651-A; CN112597651-B
TI Carbon dioxide background field concentration inverting method based on orbital carbon observation satellite-2 data and weather research and forecasting-stochastic time inverted lagrangian transport model involves obtaining footprint field.
AU PEI Z
   HAN G
   SHI T
   QIU R
AE UNIV WUHAN (UYWU-C)
GA 2021361691
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining meteorological data as an input of a weather research and forecasting (WRF) model. A footprint field of pressure weighting is obtained corresponding to an observation value based on the model. A prior total carbon flux is obtained. A Bayesian estimation is utilized to obtain better posterior total carbon flux. A simulated observation value is obtained according to the posterior total carbon flux and the footprint field. A condition that difference between the simulated observation value and the actual observation value is utilized in accordance with normal distribution of mean value.
   USE - The method is useful for inverting carbon dioxide background field concentration based on orbital carbon observation satellite-2 data and weather research and forecasting-stochastic time inverted lagrangian transport model.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: solves the background field concentration based on the OCO2 observation value; has high accuracy and low consumption; and provides more accurate data for research on issues e.g., urban-scale carbon cycle.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a carbon dioxide background field concentration inverting system based on orbital carbon observation satellite-2 data and weather research and forecasting-stochastic time inverted lagrangian transport model.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating the carbon dioxide background field concentration inverting method based on orbital carbon observation satellite-2 data and weather research and forecasting-stochastic time inverted lagrangian transport model (Drawing includes non-English language text).
DC T01 (Digital Computers)

PT P
PN CN112337455-A
TI Preparing noble metal loaded two-dimensional nanosheet photocatalyst by reacting two-dimensional nanomaterial and solvent, adding another solvent and noble metal salt, reacting noble metal salt and ultra-thin nanomaterial, and cooling.
AU LI J
   ZHANG Y
   QIAN Z
   DING T
   QIAN Y
   GAO X
   CHENG Y
   HUANG X
   PAN W
AE UNIV SHENZHEN (UYSZ-C)
GA 202117736Q
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation method of noble metal loaded two-dimensional nanosheet photocatalyst, involves (a) placing the two-dimensional nanomaterial and the first organic solvent in a high-temperature and high-pressure reactor (I), passing in carbon dioxide and increasing the temperature and pressure to a supercritical state, and contacting and reacting for 0.5-6 hours to obtain the exfoliated ultra-thin nanomaterial, where the temperature of the supercritical state of carbon dioxide is 33-88 degrees C, and the pressure is 7.5-26.5MPa, and (b) adding the second organic solvent and the noble metal salt to the reactor (I) of step (a), raising the temperature and pressurizing to the supercritical state of the second organic solvent, reacting the noble metal salt and the ultra-thin nanomaterial for 25-120 minutes, terminating the reaction, and cooling to obtain the photocatalyst. The mass ratio of the noble metal element contained in the noble metal salt to the ultra-thin nanomaterial is 1:(10-100).
   USE - Preparation method of noble metal loaded two-dimensional nanosheet photocatalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes the strong diffusion, high expansion and organic carrying characteristics of supercritical carbon dioxide to realize rapid stripping of target materials and obtain ultra-thin nano materials. The outstanding dispersibility and reducibility of supercritical organics are used to obtain highly dispersed noble metal supported catalysts. The method has a wide range of application, simple operation process, recyclable carbon dioxide and organic solvents, lower process cost, and reduced environmental pollution risk.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (a) further includes recovering carbon dioxide and the first organic solvent after reacting. The step (b) further includes recovering the second organic solvent after reacting. Preferred Conditions: When the second organic solvent is chosen from methanol, the temperature in the supercritical state of methanol is 240-285 degrees C, and the pressure is 8.2-14.5 MPa. When the second organic solvent is chosen from ethanol, the temperature in the supercritical state of ethanol is 245-285 degrees C, and the pressure is 6.5-14.5 MPa. Preferred Components: The two-dimensional nanomaterial is chosen from graphene, layered metal hydroxide, layered metal oxide, transition metal carbon/nitride, graphite phase nitrogen carbide, boron nitride, boron carbon nitrogen, layered sulfide, transition metal double halides or their mixtures. The noble metal salt is chosen from chloroauric acid, sodium tetrachloroaurate, potassium tetrachloroaurate, platinum tetrachloride, chloroplatinic acid, sodium chloroplatinate, silver nitrate, silver sulfate, silver acetate, palladium dichloride, potassium tetrachloropalladate, sodium tetrachloropalladate, potassium hexachloropalladate, sodium hexachloropalladate, ruthenium trichloride, ammonium chlororuthenate, ruthenium nitrosyl nitrate or their mixtures.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The first organic solvent is chosen from alcohols, amines, ethers, lipids, aromatic hydrocarbons, sulfoxides or their mixtures, preferably chosen from methanol, ethanol, n-propanol, isopropanol, n-butanol, dimethyl sulfoxide or their mixtures. The second organic solvent is chosen from methanol or ethanol.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); P35 (Life-saving, fire-fighting (A62).)
MC J04-E04C; J04-F02; P35-A03G
IP A62D-101/26; A62D-101/28; A62D-003/17; B01J-021/18; B01J-023/42; B01J-023/46; B01J-023/50; B01J-023/52; B01J-027/04; B01J-027/22; B01J-027/24; B82Y-030/00; B82Y-040/00; C01B-013/14; C01B-017/20; C01B-021/06; C01B-021/064; C01B-021/082; C01B-032/194; C01B-032/90; C01B-009/00

PT P
PN CN111895632-A; CN111895632-B
TI Passive household fresh air fan controlling method, involves adjusting water temperature value of air conditioner of target room, and controlling temperature of room within preset temperature range according to water temperature value.
AU WANG P
   WANG Z
   WANG J
   ZHANG L
AE HEBEI DIYE INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HEBE-Non-standard)
   BEIJING LVFENG SHENGYE ENERGY-SAVING TEC (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 2020B28629
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves monitoring whether carbon dioxide concentration of a target room of a passive house is greater than a first preset carbon dioxide concentration threshold value. A fresh air fan is controlled to start when the carbon dioxide concentration of the target room is greater than the first preset carbon dioxide concentration threshold value. A ventilation opening of the target room is controlled to open. Judgment is made to monitor whether current temperature of the target room is within preset temperature range. An air conditioner of the target room is controlled to start when the current temperature of the target room is not within the preset temperature range. A water temperature value of the air conditioner of the target room is adjusted according to heat exchange efficiency of a heat exchange module. Temperature of the target room is gradually controlled within the preset temperature range according to the water temperature value.
   USE - Passive household fresh air fan controlling method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables achieving better energy-saving effect and ensuring environmental protection.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a passive household fresh air fan controlling system;
   (2) a terminal device comprises a processor and a memory for storing a computer program executed by the processor to carry out a passive household fresh air fan controlling method; and
   (3) a computer-readable storage medium storing a computer program executed by processors to implement a passive household fresh air fan controlling method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram of a passive household fresh air fan controlling method. (Drawing includes non-English language text).

PT P
PN IN201811046955-A; IN406569-B
TI Developing hydrogen exfoliation graphene based            photocatalyst involves e.g. synthesizing hydrogen            exfoliation graphene from graphite oxide and mixing            different weight of hydrogen exfoliation graphene with            titanium oxide and copper sulfate.
AU CHAKRABORTTY S
   BISWAJIT R
   RUJ B
AE COUNCIL SCI & IND RES INDIA (COUI-C)
GA 202057546W
AB    NOVELTY - Developing hydrogen exfoliation graphene based                photocatalyst, comprises (a) synthesizing hydrogen                exfoliation graphene from graphite oxide through a                gas purging method at 200 degrees C temperature,                (b) mixing of different weight (0.25-1.25 g) of                hydrogen exfoliation graphene with titanium oxide                and copper sulfate to produce hydrogen exfoliation                graphene coupled copper loaded titanium oxide based                photo catalyst, and (c) producing methanol by the                reaction of carbon dioxide and photocatalyst using                operating conditions of variation of catalyst dose                (1-10 g/l), temperature (25-50 degrees C), reaction                time (30-240 minute), and carbon dioxide flow rate                (1-4 l/m), pH (3-8).
   USE - The process is useful for developing hydrogen                exfoliation graphene based photocatalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The process: minimize the difficulties arises                for low surface area, low catalytic activity of the                developed heterogeneous catalyst for maximum                catalytic conversion of the feed; developed                nano-composite material that produce methanol with                a high yield which could be solve the energy and                environmental crisis by recycling carbon dioxide to                fuels; provides hydrogen exfoliation graphene used                as coupled material of copper loaded titanium oxide                that enhance the electron capture and transport                properties which substantially increase the photo                induced charge transfer and extend the life time of                photo generated charge carriers; provides                economically improved heterogeneous photocatalyst                with improved photo-catalytic conversion                efficiency; provides a unique ratio for the                preparation of photocatalyst; provides an improved                method which is enriched the yield and productivity                of solar fuels, like methanol, ethanol etc. through                improved hydrogen exfoliation graphene based novel                photocatalyst; provides high conversion of carbon                dioxide to methanol; and is                economical/commercial.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The graphite oxide is                  mixed with hydrogen gas in a quartz muffle                  furnace at 200 degrees C for 2-3 minutes and                  produced hydrogen exfoliation graphene. The                  hydrogen exfoliation graphene coupled copper                  loaded titanium oxide is synthesized through                  sonicated sol-gel method. The hydrogen                  exfoliation graphene, titanium oxide and copper                  sulfate are mixed with a weight ratio of 1:2:3.                  The gel form of hydrogen exfoliation graphene                  coupled copper loaded titanium oxide is dried at                  85 degrees C for 12 hours. The dried particles                  are placed in a muffle furnace for the heat                  treatment at 500 degrees C for 2 hours. The                  carbon dioxide is reacting with photocatalyst to                  produce methanol in an annular type UV light                  enabled quartz photoreactor under the                  experimental conditions of 250 Watt UV                  illumination, 10 g/l of catalyst dose, 3 l/m flow                  rate of carbon dioxide, 50 degrees C temperature,                  3 hours reaction time and 3 pH.
   EXAMPLE - Graphene oxide material was prepared by                employed modified Hammer's method for highly                chemical oxidation. Next, hydrogen exfoliation                graphene was prepared through fast reduction method                by purging of ultrapure hydrogen gas on the fine,                fully dried graphene oxide material. Hydrogen gas                was purged on the graphene oxide material in the                argon atmosphere in a quartz type muffle furnace.                The experimentation was done with varying reaction                temperature at a fixed flow rate of hydrogen gas                for 2 minutes.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PT P
PN CN111185234-A
TI Acetic acid catalytic oxidation supported catalyst useful for catalytically oxidizing acetic acid into carbon dioxide and water.
AU LI X
   WANG Y
   LIANG Y
   CUI G
   HAN S
AE BEIJING MACHINERY EQUIP RES INST (CAES-C)
GA 202046603L
AB    NOVELTY - Acetic acid catalytic oxidation supported catalyst, is claimed. The acetic acid catalytic oxidation supported catalyst is heterogeneous copper-zirconium-cerium ternary non-noble metal catalyst, the heterogeneous copper-zirconium-cerium ternary non-noble metal catalyst is metal-organic framework supported catalyst, the metal-organic skeleton is zirconium-based metal-organic framework or copper-based metal-organic framework, the supporting component is metal nitrate, the products of acetic acid catalytic oxidation is carbon dioxide and water.
   USE - The acetic acid catalytic oxidation supported catalyst is useful for catalytically oxidizing acetic acid into carbon dioxide and water.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: can catalytically oxidize acetic acid into carbon dioxide and water, and has more than 93% removal rate of acetic acid.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing acetic acid catalytic oxidation supported catalyst, comprising (i) measuring metal salt precursor and organic ligand based on ratio and adding the solvent, dispersing and dissolving by ultrasound at room temperature to obtain mixed liquid, (ii) adding mixed liquid to reaction device for hydrothermal reaction to obtain reaction product, (iii) centrifuging, washing, and drying reaction product to obtain metal-organic framework, (iv) dissolving metal nitrate in deionized water, adding metal-organic framework, and continuously stirring to obtain stirred mixture, and (v) drying stirred mixture and calcining to obtain metal-organic framework-supported catalyst; and
   (2) processing catalytic oxidation of acetic acid, comprising (1) tableting or granulating metal-organic framework-supported catalyst and placing in reaction vessel, (2) injecting gas and acetic acid solution into reaction vessel, (3) heating reaction vessel and pressurizing it to start catalytic oxidation reaction, and (4) after completing reaction, measuring acetic acid content after reaction to calculate acetic acid conversion removal rate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The zirconium-based metal-organic framework has UIO-66 topology, the copper-based metal organic framework has MOF-74 topology. The metal loading of metal-organic framework-supported catalyst is 0.5-20.0 wt.%. Preferred Method: In the step (i), the ultrasonic dispersion time is 10-45 minutes, the organic ligand is terephthalic acid decorated with terephthalic acid, amine group or sulfonic acid group. In the step (ii), the hydrothermal reaction temperature is 150-350 degrees C. In the step (iii), the drying time is 12-24 hours, and the drying temperature is 80-120 degrees C. In the step (iii), the heating temperature is 450-750 degrees C, and the time is 3-6 hours. In the step (2), the flow rate of gas is 50-300 ml/hours, and the flow rate of the acetic acid solution is 1 to 100 ml/hours, in the step (3), the reaction vessel is heated to 150-250 degrees C, and the reaction pressure is 0.5-8 MPa.

AU XUE F

   ZHANG Y
   HUANG R
AE UNIV SHAOXING (UNSX-C)
GA 2020245063
AB    NOVELTY - The directional cracking device comprises an                outer casing, a water storage pipe, a liquid                storage pipe and a heating pipe sequentially                sleeved from the outside to the inside. The lengths                of the water storage pipe, the liquid storage pipe                and the heating pipe are all the same. The heating                tube is filled with heating material and a                detonating cord is arranged along the axial                direction. The water is injected into the water storage tube.                The energy dissipation slit is opened symmetrically                in the axial direction on the wall of the outer                sleeve.
   USE - Directional cracking device based on carbon                dioxide phase change expansion.
   ADVANTAGE - The directional cracking device is dust-free                and has a good energy-gathering cracking effect,                and is particularly suitable for smooth blasting.                The device adopts bilateral directional                energy-gathering cracking to avoid the problem that                the fracturing tube will fly out and hurt people                after blasting. The use of detonating index                blasting heating material solves the limitation                that the traditional fracturing tube cannot achieve                deep hole blasting, and achieves the simultaneous                initiation of fracturing tubes of different                depths.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                of directional cracking device based on carbon                dioxide phase change expansion.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a cross-sectional view of                the directional cracking device.

PT P
PN WO2020046064-A1; KR2020026159-A; KR2020026160-A; US2021220809-A1
TI Composite catalyst used in composition for reducing carbon dioxide, comprises organic-inorganic porous component comprising metal oxide clusters, and light-condensing organic material as linkers, and molecular reduction catalyst.
AU SON H
   KANG S O
   CHOI S
   KIM C H
   SON H J
   KANG S
   CHOI S H
   HUN K C
AE UNIV KOREA RES & BUSINESS FOUND (UYKR-C)
   UNIV KOREA RES & BUSINESS FOUND (UYKR-C)
GA 202019440N
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide reduction composite catalyst comprises an organic-inorganic porous component (100) comprising metal oxide clusters (110), and a light-condensing organic material as linkers (120) between the metal oxide clusters, which absorb visible light to form excitons and move the excitons through energy transfer between the linkers to transfer electrons of the excitons to a molecular reduction catalyst (130) and molecular reduction catalyst combined with the organic-inorganic porous component.
   USE - Composite catalyst is used in catalyst composition (claimed) for reducing carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The composite catalyst has improved catalytic activity and durability, and high carbon monoxide conversion efficiency. The linker absorbs visible light to form excitons, and transfers the excitons through energy transfer between the linkers, thereby transferring electrons of the excitons to the molecular reduction catalyst. The linkers are arranged at regular intervals through the metal oxide cluster, and the excitons can be easily moved along the linker, thereby reducing energy loss of the excitons, such that the amount of electrons delivered to the molecular reduction catalyst can be maximized.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) catalyst composition, which comprises the composite catalyst and a sacrificial electron donor; and
   (2) manufacture of carbon dioxide reduction composite catalyst, which involves preparing the organic-inorganic porous component, and dispersing the porous component in a solution comprising molecular reduction catalyst.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the composite catalyst.
   Organic-inorganic porous component (100)
   Metal oxide cluster (110)
   Linker (120)
   Molecular reduction catalyst (130)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The porous component further comprises a ceramic shell comprising metal oxide and/or metal sulfide, surrounding the surface, and the electrons of the exciton formed in the linker pass through the metal oxide cluster coated with the ceramic shell, and are delivered to the molecular reduction catalyst. The metal oxide is titanium oxide, zinc oxide, tin oxide, aluminum oxide, iron oxide, zirconium oxide, and/or hafnium oxide. The metal sulfide is zinc sulfide, cadmium sulfide, and/or tin sulfide. The molecular reduction catalyst comprises metal element(s) comprising rhenium, rubidium, manganese, cobalt, nickel, iron and/or iridium, and a carboxyl group, a phosphoric acid group and/or cyano group-containing group (CNCOON). The metal oxide cluster is a zirconium oxide (Zr6O8) cluster. The usage amount of molecular reduction catalyst is 0.01% or more with respect to amount of the porous component. Preferred Process: The process further involves coating the ceramic shell on the surface of the organic-inorganic porous component, by atomic layer deposition. The coating of ceramic shell involves providing titanium isopropoxide as a metal precursor in a chamber, providing water as an oxygen precursor in the chamber, reacting the oxygen precursor with the metal precursor bound to the surface of the organic-inorganic porous component, and repeating the above processes 15-25 times.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The linker comprises a metal porphyrin derivative, a squaraine derivative, and/or a metal complex compound. The sacrificial electron donor is 1,3-dimethyl-2-phenyl-1,3-dihydrobenzimidazole and/or triethanolamine. The catalyst composition further comprises 2,2,2-trifluoroethanol, additive(s), and water.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

AB    NOVELTY - The method involves adjusting rotation speed of a rotary rotor of a collection concentrator (320) by a gear ratio of the rotary rotor in a range of 10-20 RPH. Concentration of carbon dioxide is controlled by adjusting rotation speed of a blowing fan (310). A solenoid valve (285) is controlled to supply the condensed water to a spray line (285L) and a spray device to selectively spray the condensed water to an air filter (230f) of the condenser by pumping to prevent freezing during winter season when dew point temperature of outside air is lower than set temperature.
   USE - Method for controlling a carbon dioxide self-concentrated air conditioner in an energy-saving plant factory.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables achieving a structure suitable for constructing a duct plant air conditioning system, reducing power required for the compressor in environment in summer or winter by the structure providing energy efficiency while providing a low-cost structure with a generator.

TI Methane gas production facility for producing            methane gas from methane hydrate stratum formed in            sea-bottom, has gas-liquid separation unit that            separates methane gas from suctioned water.
AU OKATSU K
AE JGC CORP (JAGA-C)
   JGC CORP (JAGA-C)
   JGC CORP (JAGA-C)
   JGC CORP (JAGA-C)
GA 201954045C
AB    NOVELTY - The methane gas production facility has a                first horizontal well (22) that is provided along a                methane hydrate layer (MHL). An injection water                supply unit (34) supplies injection water which is                obtained by dispersing carbon dioxide gas in water                to the first horizontal well. A second horizontal                well (21) is provided along an area in which                methane released from the methane hydrate by way of                carbon dioxide replacement rises. A pressure                reduction and suction unit reduces pressure by                pumping water in the second horizontal well and                suctions the water that contains methane. A                gas-liquid separation unit (31) separates the                methane gas from the suctioned water.
   USE - Methane gas production facility for producing                methane gas from methane hydrate stratum formed in                sea-bottom and frozen ground zone.
   ADVANTAGE - The methane gas is released from methane                hydrate by substituted action with the carbon                dioxide in the press injection water pressingly                injected by the horizontal well side of the                downward side while advancing the generation of                methane gas by decomposition of methane hydrate.                Hence methane gas is efficiently produced according                to the effect of both pressure reduction and the                substitution of carbon dioxide over the large                region in which the horizontal well is                provided.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a methane                gas production method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                methane gas production facility.
   Horizontal well (21,22)
   Gas-liquid separation unit (31)
   Injection water supply unit (34)
   Vertical well unit (210)
   Methane hydrate layer (MHL)

PT P
PN CN109647402-A; CN109647402-B
TI High-efficient catalyst useful for deoxidation of 1-butene into 1,3-butadiene by carbon dioxide oxidation, comprises a carrier and an active component including a complex oxide of metallic iron and a doping element.
AU YAN B
   GAO Y
   WANG B
   JIANG T
AE UNIV TIANJIN SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYTC-C)
GA 201939471N
AB    NOVELTY - High-efficient catalyst comprises a carrier and an active component, including a complex oxide of metallic iron and a doping element. The loading of iron element is 5-15 wt.% to the weight of catalyst. The molar ratio of iron to doping element is 9:(1-10). The preparation of high-efficient catalyst comprises: (i) uniformly dispersing the carrier, the soluble ferric salt and the soluble salt of the doping element in deionized water, heating to remove solvent water and to obtaining a powder sample; and (ii) drying the powder sample, subjecting the powder sample to thermal insulation roasting from 20-25 degrees C to 500-600 degrees C for 3-8 hours with a heating rate of 1-5 degrees C/minute, and naturally cooling to room temperature.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for deoxidation of 1-butene into 1,3-butadiene by carbon dioxide oxidation (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst is simple to prepare, and has improved activity and stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing the high-efficient catalyst; and
   (2) use of catalyst for dehydrogenating 1-butene into 1,3-butadiene via carbon dioxide oxidation, comprising reacting 1-butene raw material and carbon dioxide in a ratio of 1:(2-13), preferably 1:(7-9) in presence of 0.1-0.2 g catalyst at 500-700 degrees C (preferably 550-650 degrees C) under normal pressure (i.e. standard atmospheric pressure), where the mass space velocity based on 1-butene is 1.5-7.5/hour.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The iron element is supported in a dosage of 10-15 wt.% based on the weight of the catalyst. The doping element is cobalt, chromium, copper, manganese, nickel, vanadium, zinc or molybdenum. The carrier is gamma -alumina, molecular sieve or zeolite. The molar ratio of iron element to single doping element is 9:(1-10). The molar ratio of iron, vanadium and second doping element is 9:1:1. The molar ratio of iron, vanadium and chromium is 9:1:(1-5). The molar ratio of iron, vanadium, chromium and molybdenum is 9:(1-0.5):(1-0.5):(1-0.5). Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the thermal insulation roasting is carried out at 550-600 degrees C for 3-5 hours with a heating rate of 3-5 degrees C/minute in a muffle furnace under air atmosphere. In the step (i), selecting an eggplant-shaped flask as reaction vessel, stirring and dispersing the raw materials at 180-220 rotations/minute for 180-240 minutes to achieve uniform dispersion, removing solvent water using a rotary evaporator at 60-80 degrees C for 40-60 minutes to obtain powder sample, drying the sample powder at 100-120 degrees C, grinding in a mortar and calcining.

PN WO2019073866-A1; JP2021035654-A
TI Carbon dioxide separation-and-recovery method involves cooling gas which is discharged in cooling step and pressurizing gas at cooling process.
AU TAKASHI S
   TOMOKO S
   SUZUKI T
   SASAKI T
AE HITACHI LTD (HITA-C)
   HITACHI LTD (HITA-C)
GA 2019364104
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves discharging the exhaust gas present in the carbon dioxide (CO2) adsorption tower after the adsorption step. The adsorbed CO2 is de-absorbed by heating the adsorbent. The adsorbent is cooled by introducing a gas containing CO2 at a temperature lower than the temperature in the desorption step. The gas discharged in the cooling step is cooled and pressurized at the cooling process. The gas containing CO2 at a temperature lower than desorption step is introduced into the cooling step is an exhaust gas supplied to the CO2 adsorption step. The CO2 adsorption, purge, CO2 desorption and cooling steps are performed in each adsorption column. The gas discharged in the cooling step is cooled and pressurized, and the CO2 concentration is detected while returning to the adsorption step.
   USE - Carbon dioxide separation-and-recovery method.
   ADVANTAGE - The high CO2 recovery and the high CO2 purity can be achieved by low energy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The CO2 concentration is detected at an outlet of the adsorption column. The CO2 adsorbent is an oxide containing Ce. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a CO2 adsorption system, has a purge system for discharging the exhaust gas present in a CO2 adsorption column. A desorption system desorbs adsorbed CO2 by heating the adsorbent. A cooling system cools the adsorbent by introducing a gas containing CO2 at a temperature lower than the temperature in the desorption system. The CO2 concentration is detected while cooling and boosting the gas discharged by the cooling system and returning it to the adsorption system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the carbon dioxide separation-and-recovery method. (Drawing includes non-English language text)

PN CN109593246-A
TI Producing high foaming expanded polyethylene pearl cotton involves taking expandable polyethylene, carbon dioxide foaming agent, anti-shrinkage agent, monoglyceride, talcum powder, silica aerogel, composite foaming agent and filler.
AU CHEN C
AE ANHUI RONGMAO PLASTIC PACKAGING MATERIAL (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 201935212D
AB    NOVELTY - Producing high foaming expanded polyethylene (EPE) pearl cotton involves taking 70-80 pts. wt. expandable polyethylene, 2-4 pts. wt. carbon dioxide foaming agent, 3-5 pts. wt. anti-shrinkage agent, 0.6-0.8 pts. wt. monoglyceride, 1-2 pts. wt. talcum powder, 4-6 pts. wt. silica aerogel, 4-10 pts. wt. composite foaming agent and 15-20 pts. wt. filler. The expandable polyethylene is mixed in a mixer for 3-5 minutes, then talc powder, silica aerogel and filler are added to continue mixing for 16-18 minutes to obtain a mixture. The mixture obtained is poured into a high-speed mixer, and an anti-shrinkage agent, a monoglyceride and a composite foaming agent are added, and the mixture is stirred at a low speed for 12-14 minutes at 70-80 degrees C. The mixture is then stirred at high temperature of 130-150 degrees C for 5-7 minutes, pass carbon dioxide foaming agent during high-speed stirring process, and extrude through the extruder when the carbon dioxide foaming agent is mixed and cool the molding.
   USE - Method for producing high foaming expanded polyethylene pearl cotton.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to produce high foaming expanded polyethylene pearl cotton, which has good elasticity, thermal stability and mechanical properties, and improves the tensile and tear strength of pearl cotton, and has low cost and good flame retardancy, higher bubble density, the foamed cells are more stable, the process is simple, and the mass production is convenient.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: The composite blowing agent comprises azodicarbonamide, zinc oxide and silicon dioxide, and the ratio ratio between azodicarbonamide, zinc oxide and silicon dioxide is 10:1:2. The filler comprises bentonite, clay, asbestos fiber and light calcium carbonate. The mixing temperature is 50-60 degrees C. The low-speed stirring rate is 400-500 revolutions per minute, and the high-speed stirring rate is 1300-1500 revolutions per minute. The pressure of the carbon dioxide blowing agent is 1000-2000 kilopascal.

GA 2019089570
AB    NOVELTY - A dual electric field device has a double electric field of corona discharge, where first electric field is alternating corona discharge electric field, positive corona discharge electric field or other that provides sufficient energy to oxidize various gas molecules into electric field source such as atoms, ions and radical, and second electric field is negative corona discharge electric field.
   USE - Dual electric field device is used for preparing organic or inorganic compound, preparing hydrogen and purifying cleaning gas containing carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, sulfides and/or nitrides (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The dual electric field device implement strong reduction and reforming to generate target product.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) use of dual electric field device in preparation of organic or inorganic compound. The apparatus converts gas containing carbon source into an organic compound or an inorganic compound. The carbon source-containing gas is main methane-containing energy gas or gas mixture essentially comprising carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide. The gas containing carbon source is coalbed methane, shale gas, biogas, flue gas, coke oven gas, refinery waste gas, automobile or internal combustion engine exhaust, water gas, syngas or natural gas;
   (2) use of dual electric field device in preparation of hydrogen. The dual electric field device reduces carbon dioxide and water vapor to produce ethanol. The ethanol is then decomposed into carbon monoxide and hydrogen, and carbon monoxide and hydrogen are separated; and
   (3) use of dual electric field device for purifying cleaning gas. The apparatus is used for purifying cleaning, decarbonization, sulfur removal and/or denitrification of exhaust gases containing carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, sulfides and/or nitrides.

GA 2018A5041C
AB    NOVELTY - The compressor has a dust collector (6) whose side is fixedly connected with a lubricator (5). An interior part of the dust collector is provided with a dust sensor. A side of the dust sensor is formed with a dust inlet that is provided with a fan. A lower part of the fan is provided with two side dust collecting chambers, where the dust collecting chambers are respectively provided with a driving motor. An upper end of the lubricator is provided with an oil injector pressing block. The lubricator is provided with an oil storage chamber. A lower end of the oil injector pressing block is fixedly connected with a spring, where an inner side of the oil storage chamber is provided with a piston rod.
   USE - Carbon dioxide compressor.
   ADVANTAGE - The driving motor drives the fan so as to collect the dust into the dust collecting chamber, and prevent the dust from affecting the service life of the compressor when the dust enters into the compressor for improving the efficiency for compressing carbon dioxide. The compressor is provided with a gas pressure sensor for monitoring the pressure in the compressor so as to prevent excessive pressure in the compressor, and prolong the service life of the compressor without abrasion.

AB    NOVELTY - A system comprises an absorber to receive a lean solvent system and a gaseous stream, contact the lean solvent system with the gaseous stream and produce a rich solvent system stream, a stripper to receive the rich solvent system stream, a cross-exchanger in fluid communication with a rich solvent system outlet on the absorber and a rich solvent system inlet on the stripper, a splitter to separate the rich solvent system stream into portion (A) and portion (B), to direct portion (A) directly to the stripper and portion (B) through the cross-exchanger, a reboiler in fluid communication with a lower portion of the stripper, to generate a vapor stream from the rich solvent system and pass the vapor stream back to the stripper and a condenser in fluid communication with a vapor outlet of the stripper. The gaseous stream comprises carbon dioxide.
   USE - System for capturing carbon dioxide from gas mixture.
   ADVANTAGE - The heat rate of the carbon dioxide capture process can be reduced by modifying the chemical solvents and solvent systems through the addition of physical solvents having high dielectric constants. The nonaqueous physical solvent additive can reduce the energy associated with the vaporization of steam and can be far less volatile than the chemical solvent and also reduces their loss during the process. The use of high dielectric solvents can minimize the hindrance and can also facilitate the ionization if the dielectric constant is sufficiently high (for example, higher than water).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The lean solvent system comprises 20-50 wt.% chemical solvent, and water nonaqueous physical solvent. The molar ratio of the physical solvent to chemical solvent is 0.01-0.7. The nonaqueous physical solvent has a dielectric constant greater than 7, and comprises ethylene carbonate, propylene carbonate, N-methyl-2-pyrrolidone, sulfolane, ethylene glycol, 1,3-propylene glycol, diethylene glycol, triethylene glycol, sulfolane, N-methyl-2-pyrrolidone and/or isobutylene glycol. The chemical solvent comprises alkanolamine comprising monoethanolamine (MEA), 2-amino-2-methyl-1 propanol (AMP), diglycolamine (DGA), piperazine, n-methylpiperazine and/or ammonia and an alkaline carbonate.

PN CN108467000-A; CN108467000-B
TI Method of filling beer with nitrogen instead of carbon dioxide, involves emptying air in beer can completely, and then sending beer can to nitrogen filling station and subjecing to two times of nitrogen foaming.
AU CHEN W
AE YANJING BEER YULIN CO LTD (YANJ-Non-standard)
GA 2018700793
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves emptying, and cleaning and replacing the wine filling machine (1), capping machine (2), sake conveying pipeline and gas conveying pipeline with a dry temperature of 99.95% or more and a pressure of 0.5 Mpa or more gas. The sake in sake tank is transferred to a buffer tank of the wine filling machine for use, under closed conditions. The filling machine is started, the beer can is sprayed three times at three injection points of the bottle screw, the air in beer can is completely drained, and then beer can is sent to the nitrogen filling station. The nitrogen flushing time in filling is not less than 400ms. After the filling of the beer can is subjected to two times of nitrogen foaming, and the liquid surface bubbles in beer removed to reduce oxygen content in the beer. After the foaming is completed, the beer can filled with beer enters the roll-sealing and capping machine to perform capping operation under the protection of nitrogen to obtain finished canned beer.
   USE - Method of filling beer with nitrogen instead of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The quality of the beer finished product is ensured. The problem that carbon dioxide in the beer plant is unbalanced in production and needs to be used for supplementing carbon dioxide is solved.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view illustrating the position of the nitrogen injection point of beer filling.
   Wine filling machine (1)
   Capping machine (2)

PT P
PN CN108063274-A; CN108063274-B
TI Method for preparing sacrificial fuel cell, involves utilizing titanium oxide/indium tin oxide electrode and platinum-copper oxide electrode adding sodium sulfate solution followed by introducing oxygen and carbon dioxide between bridges.
AU HUANG M
   LI H
AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA NORMAL (USCN-C)
GA 201843844T
AB    NOVELTY - A sacrificial fuel cell preparing method involves utilizing titanium oxide/indium tin oxide electrode as an anode and platinum-copper oxide electrode as a cathode, and adding a sodium sulfate solution having a concentration of 0.1 mol/L as electrolyte in the anode tank. Sodium sulfide and sodium sulfite are utilized as fuel followed by adding 0.1 mol/L sodium carbonate and 0.1 mol/L sodium hydrogen carbonate to the cathode cell, and introducing oxygen and carbon dioxide between bridges connected with saturated potassium chloride salt and constructing under UV light.
   USE - Method for preparing sacrificial fuel cell.
   ADVANTAGE - The fuel cell can consume self-generated electric energy, and convert carbon dioxide into organic fuel, and can be prepared in simple, environmentally friendly and safe manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A sacrificial fuel cell preparing method involves utilizing titanium oxide/indium tin oxide electrode as an anode and platinum-copper oxide electrode as a cathode, and adding a sodium sulfate solution having a concentration of 0.1 mol/L as electrolyte in the anode tank. Sodium sulfide and sodium sulfite at a concentration of 0.001-4 mol/l and a pH of 8-14 are utilized as fuel followed by adding 0.1 mol/L sodium carbonate and 0.1 mol/L sodium hydrogen carbonate to the cathode cell, and introducing oxygen and carbon dioxide between bridges connected with saturated potassium chloride salt and constructing under UV light to obtain sacrificial fuel cell. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a sacrificial fuel cell prepared by the method.

PN CN114000929-A
TI Mixed cycle power generation system of carbon dioxide in liquid state and supercritical state, has high temperature regenerator whose second outlet is communicated with inlet of power generation device through heat source, where heat source further heats supercritical carbon dioxide (sCO2).
AU MI X
   ZHANG X
   YU M
AE ZHEJIANG GAOSHENG SOLAR THERMAL ELECTRIC (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 2022259109
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a storage tank (2) whose outlet is communicated with a supercritical state generator (4) through a liquid pump (3). The liquid carbon dioxide in the storage tank is compressed by the liquid pump, and high-pressure liquid carbon dioxide is output. The liquid carbon dioxide is heated to supercritical carbon dioxide (sCO2) by a supercritical state generator. The first outlet of the supercritical state generator is connected with the second inlet of a low temperature regenerator (6) and the first inlet of a bypass heater (5) through the first branch and the second branch respectively. The sCO2 passing through the bypass heater and the sCO2 passing through the low temperature regenerator are combined in the pipeline leading to the second inlet of a high temperature regenerator (7). The second outlet of the high temperature regenerator is communicated with the inlet of a power generation device (9) through a heat source (8), and the heat source further heats sCO2.
   USE - Mixed cycle power generation system of carbon dioxide in liquid state and supercritical state. Can also be used for liquid and super-critical carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The mixed cycle power generation system of liquid and supercritical carbon dioxide solves the problem of high energy consumption and low efficiency of the compressor at the same time, using semi-closed sCO2 circulating system, decoupling the key device, and increasing the stability of the system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a generating method using mixed cycle power generation system of liquid and supercritical carbon dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a structure schematic diagram of the mixed cycle power generation system of liquid and supercritical carbon dioxide.
   Cooler (1)
   Storage tank (2)
   Liquid pump (3)
   Supercritical state generator (4)
   Bypass heater (5)
   Low-temperature heat regenerator (6)
   High temperature regenerator (7)
   High temperature heat source (8)
   Generating device (9)
   Circulating working substance condenser (10)
   Compression heat pump circulation device (11)
   Compressor (1101)
   Refrigerant storage tank (1102)
   Expansion valve (1103)

PN CN112858136-A; CN112858136-B
TI Quantitative evaluation of pore structure of shale            organic matter involves testing carbon dioxide and            nitrogen adsorption curves of shale sample and burned            samples, and explaining curves of before and after            combustion of shale sample.
AU XIAO Z
   GUO X
   WANG K
AE UNIV CHINA GEOSCIENCES WUHAN (UYCI-C)
GA 202162134H
AB    NOVELTY - Quantitative evaluation of pore structure of                shale organic matter involves obtaining (S1) shale                samples and pre-processing shale samples, using                (S2) full automatic specific surface and pore size                distribution analyzer for testing carbon dioxide                and nitrogen adsorption curves of the shale sample,                placing (S3) tested sample of carbon dioxide and                nitrogen adsorption curve in muffle furnace for                burning until the organic matter is completely                burned, testing (S4) carbon dioxide and nitrogen                adsorption of the burned samples using the analyzer                to obtain carbon dioxide and nitrogen adsorption                curves of the shale sample after burning,                explaining (S5) carbon dioxide and nitrogen                adsorption curves before and after the combustion                of the shale sample, obtaining pore structure                parameter of the shale sample before and after                combustion, and subtracting (S6) pore structure                parameters of the shale sample after combustion                from the pore structure parameters of the shale                sample before combustion.
   USE - Quantitative evaluation of pore structure of                shale organic matter.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables overcoming defect of field                emission scanning electron microscope technology                which cannot accurately characterize relative                contribution of organic pores less than 10 nm. The                method enables evaluating pore structure of the                shale organic matter in a simple, accurate and                reliable manner.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart of the                quantitative evaluation method of shale organic                matter pore structure. (Drawing includes                non-English language text).
   Obtaining shale sample and pre-processing                shale sample (S1)
   Using full automatic specific surface and                pore size distribution analyzer for testing carbon                dioxide and nitrogen adsorption curves of shale                sample (S2)
   Placing tested sample of carbon dioxide and                nitrogen adsorption curve in muffle furnace for                burning until the organic matter is completely                burned (S3)
   Testing carbon dioxide and nitrogen                adsorption of burned samples using the analyzer to                obtain carbon dioxide and nitrogen adsorption                curves of the shale sample after burning                (S4)
   Explaining carbon dioxide and nitrogen                adsorption curves before and after the combustion                of the shale sample (S5)
   Subtracting pore structure parameters of the                shale sample after combustion from the pore                structure parameters of the shale sample before                combustion (S6)

PN CN111790412-A; TW202037586-A
TI Method for reducing carbon dioxide to produce            carbon compounds involves using reduction reaction            device, mixing photocatalyst and liquid solution,            passing carbon dioxide gas, irradiating under light            source, and obtaining carbon compound.
AU CHEN J
   CHEN C C
   CHIANG C L
   DAI Y M
   LIU F Y
   TSO H F
AE CHEN J (CHEN-Individual)
   HONG KUEN IND CO LTD (HONG-Non-standard)
   UNIV TAICHUNG NAT EDUCATION (UYTA-Non-standard)
   CHEN C C (CHEN-Individual)
GA 2020A4727L
AB    NOVELTY - Method for reducing carbon dioxide to produce                carbon compounds involves using reduction reaction                device, mixing photocatalyst and liquid solution in                reactor and vibrating uniformly to form a mixed                solution, performing a ventilation, passing carbon                dioxide gas into mixed solution from gas storage                device (g1), making the carbon dioxide gas                saturated and dissolving in the mixed solution to                form a saturated solution, irradiating the                saturated solution under the light source, and                obtaining carbon compound. The photocatalyst                comprises bismuth oxide (I), metal-bismuth oxide                (II), lithium-metal oxide (III), sodium-metal oxide                (IV), and potassium-metal oxide (V). The reduction                reaction device comprises a reactor, a light source                and a gas storage device (g1). The light source and                the gas storage device (g1) are both connected to                the reactor. The gas storage device (g1) is used to                store a carbon dioxide gas.
   USE - Method for reducing carbon dioxide to produce                carbon compounds.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for reducing carbon dioxide to produce                carbon compounds involves using reduction reaction                device, mixing photocatalyst and liquid solution in                reactor and vibrating uniformly to form a mixed                solution, performing a ventilation, passing carbon                dioxide gas into mixed solution from gas storage                device (g1), making the carbon dioxide gas                saturated and dissolving in the mixed solution to                form a saturated solution, irradiating the                saturated solution under the light source, and                obtaining carbon compound. The photocatalyst                comprises bismuth oxide of formula: BiOX (I),                metal-bismuth oxide of formula: MBiO2X (II),                lithium-metal oxide of formula: LiaY'bOc (III),                sodium-metal oxide of formula: NaaY'bOc (IV), and                potassium-metal oxide of formula: KaY'bOc (V). The                reduction reaction device comprises a reactor, a                light source and a gas storage device (g1). The                light source and the gas storage device (g1) are                both connected to the reactor. The gas storage                device (g1) is used to store a carbon dioxide                gas.
   M = lead, calcium, strontium, barium, copper                or iron;
   X = fluoro, chloro, bromo or iodo;
   Y' = molybdenum, antimony, iron, cobalt,                nickel, zirconium, silicon, aluminum or tin;
   a = 0.1-8;
   b = 1-6;and
   c = 1-10.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The photocatalyst                  further comprises a complex of a compound (I) and                  a compound (II), a complex of two compounds                  (III), a complex of two compounds (IV), or a                  complex of two compounds (V). The photocatalyst                  further comprises a compound (I) or compound (II)                  and a carbon nanomaterial. The carbon nano                  material is graphene oxide or graphitic                  carbonitride. Preferred Process: The method                  further involves using detection device for                  detection. Preferred Condition: The light source                  is visible light, UV light or sunlight. The                  reaction is performed for 30 seconds to 6 hours.                  The reduction reaction device further comprises                  gas storage device (g2) which is connected to the                  reactor. The gas storage device (g2) is used to                  store a helium gas. The detection device                  connected with the reactor to measure the output                  of the carbon compound e.g. methane or methanol.                  The detection device is a gas                  chromatograph.

   KITAMURA H
   IWASA K
   HODOTSUKA M
   UDATSU M
   IWAASA K

   UTATSU M
AE TOSHIBA KK (TOKE-C)
   TOSHIBA ENERGY SYSTEMS & SOLUTIONS CORP (TOKE-C)
   TOSHIBA KK (TOKE-C)
   TOSHIBA ENERGY SYSTEMS & SOLUTIONS CORP (TOKE-C)
   TOSHIBA ENERGY SYSTEMS & SOLUTIONS CORP (TOKE-C)
   TOSHIBA KK (TOKE-C)

   TOSHIBA ENERGY SYSTEMS KK (TOKE-C)
   TOSHIBA ENERGY SYSTEMS & SOLUTIONS CORP (TOKE-C)
   TOSHIBA ENERGY SYSTEMS KK (TOKE-C)
GA 201937276C
AB    NOVELTY - The carbon dioxide capturing system (1) has an absorption tower (20) that absorbs carbon dioxide in exhaust gas (3). A regeneration tower separates the carbon dioxide from the absorption liquid (5) through the absorption tower. A re-boiler (42) supplies heat from heated steam (6) to the absorption liquid through a regeneration tower (30), and condenses the heated steam to generate downstream side condensed water (10). An upstream side line (51) supplies the heated steam to the re-boiler. A downstream side line (52) discharges the downstream side condensed water from the re-boiler. A branched line (63) is connected between the upstream side and the downstream side line. An upstream side cooler (64) that cools and condenses the heated steam from the branched line to generate an upstream side condensed water (9). A physical quantity measurement device measures a physical quantity of an absorption liquid component in the upstream side and the downstream side condensed water.
   USE - Carbon dioxide capturing system for recovering carbon dioxide in plant exhaust gas to be discharged from thermal power plant, steel plant and waste disposal factory.
   ADVANTAGE - The error due to disturbance is suppressed, and possible to detect leak of the absorption liquid component in the re-boiler with high accuracy. The steam is contained in the downstream side condensed water to be supplied to the downstream side measurement instrument is prevented, and is possible to improve measurement accuracy by the downstream side measurement instrument. The number of measurement instruments for measuring a physical quantity of the absorption liquid component is reduced, and thus the system configuration is simplified.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method of a carbon dioxide capturing system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawings show a schematic diagrams a fundamental configuration of the carbon dioxide capturing system and a heated steam supply and discharge system in the carbon dioxide capturing system.
   Carbon dioxide capturing system (1)
   Exhaust gas (3)
   Absorption liquid (5)
   Heated steam (6)
   Upstream side condensed water (9)
   Downstream side condensed water (10)
   Absorption tower (20)
   Regeneration tower (30)
   Re-boiler (42)
   Upstream side line (51)
   Downstream side line (52)
   Branched line (63)
   Upstream side cooler (64)

   EXAMPLE - After making the inside of the reaction vessel                nitrogen gas atmosphere, 121 mg (0.323 mmol)                2-{[(2-{[(8-hydroxyquinolin-2-yl)methyl]amino}ethyl)amino]methyl}quinolin-8-ol,                163 mg (0.710 mmol)                methyl-6-bromomethyl-2-pyridinecarboxylate, 163 mg                (1.94 mmol) sodium hydrogen carbonate and 16.1 mL                acetonitrile were added, and the mixture was heated                to 80&#176; C and stirred for 12 hours while                refluxing, cooled to room temperature and the                acetonitrile was concentrated to give crude                product. This crude product was purified by gel                filtration column chromatography using chloroform                as the developing phase, concentrated and dried                under reduced pressure to obtain                6-{[(2-{[(6-carboxypyridin-2-yl)methyl][(8-hydroxyquinolin-2-yl)methyl]amino}ethyl)[(8-hydroxyquinolin-2-yl)methyl]amino]methyl}pyridine-2-carboxylic                acid. 79.0 mg (0.117 mmol) methyl                6-({[(8-hydroxyquinolin-2-yl)methyl](2-{[(8-hydroxyquinolin-2-yl)methyl]({[6-(methoxycarbonyl)pyridin-2-yl]methyl})amino}ethyl)amino}methyl)pyridine-2-carboxylate                and 0.78 mL of 6N hydrochloric acid were added to                reaction vessel, and the mixture was heated to                100&#176; C and stirred for 4 hours while                refluxing. After the reaction solution was cooled                to room temperature, the deposited yellow                precipitate was separated by filtration and dried                under reduced pressure to obtain 95%                6-{[(2-{[(6-carboxypyridin-2-yl)methyl][(8-hydroxyquinolin-2-yl)methyl]amino}ethyl)[(8-hydroxyquinolin-2-yl)methyl]amino]methyl}pyridine-2-carboxylic                acid.

PN CN114920260-A
TI Preparation of nanometer H-beta molecular sieve,            involves placing solid mixture in supercritical            crystallization reaction kettle, introducing carbon            dioxide gas, heating for crystallization reaction, and            cooling room temperature.
AU SHAO R
   CUI M
   DING J
AE YANCHENG INST TECHNOLOGY (YANH-C)
GA 2022B0385T
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves mixing and grinding a                silicon source, an aluminum source and a template                agent in a grinding machine to obtain a solid                mixture. The molar ratio of the template agent to                the template agent is 1. The solid mixture is                placed in a supercritical crystallization reaction                kettle, carbon dioxide gas is introduced, heat for                crystallization reaction, and cooled to room                temperature after the crystallization reaction is                finished to obtain a crystallization reaction                product. The crystallization reaction product is                washed with deionized water until the pH value of                the filtrate is 7.0, and then dried and roasted to                obtain the nano H-Beta molecular sieve.
   USE - Preparation method of nanometre H-Beta                molecular sieve used for hydrogen cracking                petroleum refining and petroleum chemical                process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides nanometer H-Beta molecular                sieve with small crystal grain size, uniform                distribution, rich channel, large specific surface                area, large total acid amount and high yield. The                method uses N-octyl-N-butyl ammonium dodecyl acid                as template agent, without adding inorganic alkali,                without ion exchange and secondary washing, drying,                roasting to obtain H-beta molecular sieves.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a graphical representation                of the X ray diffraction pattern of nano H-Beta                molecular sieve. (Drawing includes non-English                language text)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - The aluminum source is one of aluminum                  isopropoxide or sodium metaaluminate. The                  template agent is ammonium                  N-octanoyl-N-butyldodecylate.

PN CN114887878-A
TI Method for improving fragrance retention time of            fragranced product by agilawood essential oil comprises            peeling agilawood, crushing, sieving, ultrasonically            processing, precipitating, filtering, adding ethanol,            mixing, inputting supercritical carbon dioxide fluid,            heating, condensing and cooling.
AU DENG J
   ZHANG J
   DAI S
AE YAOQI HAINAN COMMERCE & TRADE CO LTD (YAOQ-Non-standard)
GA 2022B1204M
AB    NOVELTY - Method for improving fragrance retention time                of a fragranced product by agilawood essential oil                comprises (a) firstly taking a section of                agilawood, peeling, crushing by a crusher, sieving                by screening device, obtaining agilawood powder and                agilawood slag with a particle size of 50 meshes,                (b) adding agilawood slag obtained in step (a) in                water, mixing, ultrasonically processing,                precipitating, filtering, obtaining oily matter,                adding the agilawood powder and oily matter in a                reaction kettle, (c) adding ethanol into the                reaction kettle, heating, stirring, mixing,                cooling, filtering again to obtain agilawood                mixture, adding to a extraction kettle and (d)                inputting supercritical carbon dioxide fluid in the                extraction kettle, heating under reduced pressure,                separating, extracting agilawood solution,                condensing, cooling and preparing agilawood                essential oil.
   USE - The method is useful for improving fragrance                retention time of fragranced product by agilawood                essential oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method obtains high-quality agilawood                essential oil; reduce waste of resources; makes the                agilawood essential oil more stable and pure;                extends the fragrance time of the product; makes                the screening more comprehensive; enhance the                filtering effect; makes the powder and slag not                easy to aggregate together; reduces blockage and                improves the screening efficiency.

PT P
PN CN114589850-A
TI Preparing fully biodegradable industrial packaging            material comprises e.g. obtaining carbon transgenic            corn starch/carbon dioxide synthetic starch, lime            water, nanocellulose, sodium alginate, filling            auxiliary agent, toughening agent, auxiliary agent and            radix puerariae fiber, heating, and mixing.
AU LUO X
   XIAO Y
AE SANYECAO BIODEGRADABLE NEW MATERIAL TECH (SANY-Non-standard)
GA 202287374L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing fully biodegradable industrial                packaging material comprises (i) obtaining carbon                transgenic corn starch/carbon dioxide synthetic                starch, lime water, nanocellulose, sodium alginate,                filling auxiliary agent, toughening agent,                auxiliary agent and radix puerariae fiber, heating,                mixing and stirring to obtain fully biodegradable                raw materials; (ii) dividing the fully                biodegradable raw materials into equal weights, and                loading and conveying the raw materials with a                carrier to obtain the raw materials with the                carrier; (iii) clamping raw materials from carrier,                and hot-pressing raw material to obtain the formed                blank part; (iv) performing incense and glue                spraying treatment on the formed blank part to                obtain a glue-sprayed blank part; (v) performing                water outlet processing on the glue-sprayed blank                part to obtain a water outlet treated product; and                (vi) drying the product treated by water outlet to                obtain a fully biodegradable product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing fully                biodegradable industrial packaging material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: used raw materials are simple to                obtain, economical and easy to recycle; and can                reduces the cost of production, reduces the degree                of pollution to environment, reduces the waste                caused by defective products, improves quality of                products, and produces high qualified rate product.                The packaging material: has high quality.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (i) comprises                  (ia) weighing 2-3 kg carbon transgenic corn                  starch/carbon dioxide synthetic starch and                  0.5-0.8 kg nanocellulose, 0.1-0.2 kg sodium                  alginate, adding into 2-3 l deionized water,                  stirring and mixing to obtain turbid liquid,                  adding 3-5 ml lime water with a mass of 10-15                  wt.% to every 100mL of turbid liquid, stirring                  and mix for 30-40 minutes at rotating speed of                  200-300 revolutions/minute to obtain a turbid                  liquid mixture; (ib) adding the turbid liquid                  mixture into pot, boiling at 110-120&#176;C,                  stirring at 200-300 revolutions/minute, boiling                  until it boils, scrapping the slurry floating on                  the surface 3-5 mm, adding it in another                  container, continuously cooking at                  110-120&#176;C, scrapping out the slurry                  floating on the surface 3-5 mm again, adding it                  into the same container, then repeatedly cooking                  until it is completely boiled to obtain the                  mixture starch slurry, which is ready for use;                  (ic) weighing 100-200 g radix puerariae and                  adding it into water to wash, after washing,                  adding it into juicer to extract juice, and                  mixing filling auxiliary agent to obtain Pueraria                  lobata fiber turbid liquid; and (id) adding 30-50                  ml radix puerariae fiber turbid liquid to every                  100 g starch slurry, adding auxiliary agent and                  toughening agent, allowing to stand for 12-24                  hours, keeping stirring, increasing temperature                  by layer polymerization at 5&#176;C/minites,                  heating process adopts the hydrothermal heating                  process, until the temperature rises to                  68-73&#176;C, continuously stirring the                  reaction for 2-3 hours, heat preservation and                  curing for 5-6 hours, after obtaining the cured                  product, adding the obtained cured product into                  an oven and drying to a moisture content of                  40-60% to obtain a fully biodegradable raw                  material. The step (iii) comprises (iiia)                  obtaining a mold that matches the shape of the                  product, and placing the raw material on the                  mold; (iiib) applying pressure and heating the                  raw materials in the mold; (iiic) after heating                  and pressing the raw material for preset period                  of time, taking out the raw material from the                  mold to obtain the formed blank part. The step                  (iv) comprises (iva) transferring the formed                  blanks part to the incense channel for incense,                  and obtaining products with different fruit                  flavors as needed, and placing them in a                  semi-closed container; and (ivb) nozzle extends                  into the formed blank part, spraying glue on the                  inner surface of the formed blank part, and                  allowing to stand for air-drying. The step (v)                  comprises (va) obtaining a water outlet mold, and                  providing cutter on the mold near the edge of the                  product; (vb) placing the glue-sprayed blank part                  into the sprue mold, and by punching the mold,                  cutter cutting off the nozzle on the edge of the                  glue-sprayed blank part; and (vc) taking out the                  blank part from the water outlet mold, and                  obtaining the blank piece after the water outlet.                  The method further comprises (vii) sterilizing                  the fully biodegradable product. The auxiliary                  agent is zinc stearate, and the filler auxiliary                  agent is bamboo powder and sodium bicarbonate. In                  the step (iv), the waterproof glue of fully                  biodegradable polylactic acid (PLA), polyvinyl                  acetate (PVA) or PBS is sprayed.
