TI Production of dimethyl ether used as substitute of combustible gas, involves forming feedstock using synthesis gas, carrying out catalytic conversion to form product stream, removing methanol/water to form gas mixture, and condensing.
AU PESCHEL A
AE LINDE GMBH (LINM-C)
   LINDE GMBH (LINM-C)
GA 2022310436
AB    NOVELTY - Production of dimethyl ether involves forming a feedstock using shifted/unshifted synthesis gas, carrying out catalytic conversion to form a product stream, separating the stream, at least partially removing methanol and water from the product stream to form a gas mixture, partially condensing the gas mixture at pressure (P1) by cooling from temperature (A) to temperature (B), washing the gas portion (a) of gas mixture remaining gaseous at the temperature (B) in an absorption column with a reflux containing dimethyl ether which is formed predominantly from cooling condensed portion of gas mixture, at least partially converting the gas portion (b) of gas mixture not scrubbed out in the absorption column into the feedstock and producing a dimethyl ether product using the portion of gas mixture formed.
   USE - Production of dimethyl ether used as substitute of combustible gas e.g. LPG and fuels such as diesel.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces dimethyl ether with high yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) plant for separating the gas mixture to form dimethyl ether, which comprises dimethyl ether, carbon dioxide and at least one component having boiling point lower than the carbon dioxide; and
   (2) plant for production of dimethyl ether, which comprises at least one rector and a separator plant.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method involves cooling the gas mixture at the temperature (B) of 1-10 degrees C, over a number of intermediate temperature levels, separating a number of condensates, washing the gas portion (a) of gas mixture remaining gaseous at the temperature (B) in an absorption column to form a top product and bottom product with a reflux containing dimethyl ether which is formed using dimethyl ether-carbon dioxide distillation column at the pressure level (P2) below the pressure (P1), partially condensing the top gas and recycling the gas to the top region of absorption column, operating the distillation column to form an overhead gas containing carbon dioxide at the top and dimethyl ether-enriched liquid at the bottom used as dimethyl ether-containing reflux, withdrawing at least a portion of the bottom liquid from distillation column to form a dimethyl ether product containing 98.5 mol% dimethyl ether. The method involves discharging the gas portion (b) of gas mixture not transferred to feed stock as purge stream, at least partially washing the stream with liquid carbon dioxide provided from distillation column to form a wash stream and at least partially recycling the wash stream to the dimethyl ether-carbon dioxide distillation column. Preferred Composition: The dimethyl ether-containing reflux contains 0.1 wt.% carbon dioxide. Preferred Properties: The temperature (A) is 20-40 degrees C, and pressure (P1) is 30-80 bar. The synthesis gas is transferred through the absorption column with gas portion (b).

The concentration                of the gammadeltaT cells is more than 90% of the                total number of cells, and the CD56+gammadeltaT                cells with high CD56 expression account for more                than 75% of the total number of gammadeltaT                cells.

   (2) culture medium based on T009 culture                medium comprising 5% fetal bovine serum (FBS),                10-200 ng/ml interleukin (IL)15 and 10-1000 ng/ml                pVC (L-ascorbic acid-2-phosphate); and
   (3) method for preparing the high-purity                CD56+gammadeltaT cell, involves preparing                peripheral blood mononuclear cells (PBMCs) from the                blood using lymphocyte separating liquid, using                sorting antibody and magnetic bead to prepare T                cell with purity of more than 90% from PBMC, adding                T cell into culture medium, and placing in carbon                dioxide culture incubator at 37degreesC and 5%                carbon dioxide for 2-3 weeks.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The culture medium                  further comprises (a) 10-200 ng/ml interleukin                  (IL)21, (b) 10-200 ng/ml IL21 and 100-100 IU IL2,                  and (c) 10-200 ng/ml IL4 and 100-1000 IU IL2. The                  sorting antibody is gammadelta T cell receptor                  antibody.Preferred Method: The method involves                  counting every 1-3 days, supplementing liquid,                  and controlling cell concentration at 5x105-5x106                  cells/ml.

   LU B

AE UNIV NANCHANG HANGKONG (UYNH-C)
GA 2022001069
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves processing groove of joint                portion of medium-titanium high-strength steel                plate to be welded, cleaning surface of the groove                and removing rust before welding, selecting welding                wire, and using direct current power supply                positive connection method, and using nitrogen,                argon gas and carbon dioxide mixed gas as                protective gas for welding. The flow ratio of                nitrogen, argon gas and carbon dioxide in the                protective gas is 1:(8-12):(20-25). The welding                wire comprises 0.08-0.099 wt.% carbon, 0.15-0.20                wt.% silicon, 0.25-0.45 wt.% chromium, 1.50-1.99                wt.% manganese, 0.130-0.199 wt.% titanium,                0.001-0.0019 wt.% nitrogen, and balance iron and                unavoidable impurity.
   USE - The method is used for gas shielded welding of                thin-gauge titanium-high-strength steel used in                engineering application field, and used in                manufacturing enterprises.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can obviously thin the crystal                grain of the welding joint, improve the strength of                the weld joint, impact toughness, plasticity, and                ensure the comprehensive physical property of the                joint meet the production requirement. It can                obviously reduce the welding cost, has good                economic benefit, and is suitable for                popularization and application.

TI Method for economically recycling lithium by using waste lithium iron phosphate material acid method, involves adding saturated lithium carbonate in filtrate, filtering, and recovering carbonate by carbon dioxide sedimentation in filter liquid.
AU YANG Q
   SHENG B
   FU Y
   LIU H
AE UNIV FUZHOU (UFZU-C)
GA 2021D7084B
AB    NOVELTY - Recycling lithium by using waste lithium iron phosphate material acid method comprises (i) mixing waste lithium-iron phosphate powder, concentrated sulfuric acid and water into a slurry, (ii) introducing air into the slurry and aerating and stirring, (iii) stopping aeration, adding hydrogen peroxide, and continuously stirring, and (iv) filtering the slurry, separating the iron phosphate and polyvinylidene fluoride, adding calcium carbonate to adjust the pH, adding lime to adjust pH, filtering, adding saturated lithium carbonate into the filter solution, and filtering, and (v) settling with carbon dioxide, filtering and washing, and drying to obtain lithium carbonate.
   USE - The method is useful for economically recycling of lithium by acid method of waste lithium iron phosphate material, which is useful in lithium ion power automobile.
   ADVANTAGE - The process is simple and efficient and strong pertinence. It is suitable for recycling lithium from waste lithium iron phosphate anode material of large scale industrialization. It uses simple and environment-friendly method of aeration to replace most of hydrogen peroxide, greatly reduces the use of peroxide and acid, reduces the recycling cost, simple and more environment friendly, suitable for large-scale industrial production, and provides high-purity lithium carbonate.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a waste iron phosphate lithium material acid economic recovery method of lithium process. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The particle size of the waste lithium iron phosphate powder is 60-150 mesh, which contains 30-35 wt.% iron, 3.5-4.5 wt.% lithium, 18-23 wt.% phosphorus and trace impurities such as sodium, magnesium, nickel, cobalt, and manganese. The mol ratio of hydrogen peroxide to lithium is 0.1-0.5: 1.

PN CN113621359-A; CN113621359-B
TI Preparing complex oil displacement agent useful in oil reservoir displacement for enhancing oil recovery comprises mixing supercritical carbon dioxide and surfactant, where surfactant prepared by polymerizing first reactant and second reactant under initiator.
AU MA L
   GUO X
   HOU Y
   WANG L
   SONG X
   CUI S
AE SHANDONG DESHI CHEM CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   DESHI ENERGY TECHNOLOGY GROUP CO LTD (DESH-Non-standard)
GA 2021D1903V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a complex oil displacement agent comprises mixing supercritical carbon dioxide and a surfactant in a weight ratio of (100-1000):1, where the surfactant is prepared by carrying out the reaction between a first monomer and formaldehyde in formic acid to obtain a first reactant, carrying out the reaction between the second monomer and acid chloride to obtain a second reactant, and polymerizing the first reactant and the second reactant under the initiator to obtain the surfactant, where the first monomer comprises 3-phenyl-4-penten-1-amine, 4-penten-1-amine, 4-methyl-pentene-1-amine and/or 2-amino-4-pentenoic acid, and the second monomer comprising 1-butene, 1-pentene, 1-hexene and/or cyclopentene.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing complex oil displacement agent, which is useful in oil reservoir displacement for enhancing oil recovery (all claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   a complex oil displacement agent prepared by the above mentioned method; and
   use of complex oil displacement agent in oil reservoir displacement, comprising injecting complex oil displacement agent into the oil reservoir at an injection rate of 5-20 ml/minute at 40-80degreesC under the 7-18 MPa.
   (1)
   (2)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The molar ratio of the formic acid, formaldehyde and the first monomer is (20-80): (10-40): (0.8-1.2). The weight ratio of the second monomer to the acid chloride is (2-12): (5-17). The weight ratio of the first reactant and the second reactant is (8-14): (11-17). The first monomer comprises 4-penten-1-amine. The second monomer comprises 1-pentene. The acid chloride comprises acetyl chloride, propionyl chloride, butyryl chloride, and/or valeryl chloride. Preferred Method: The first reactant is prepared by adding formic acid, formaldehyde and the first monomer to the first solvent under inert gas, and refluxing at 90-100degreesC for 18-24 hours, where the first solvent comprises dimethylformamide and/or water. The second reactant is prepared by adding the catalyst and the second monomer to the dichloromethane under inert gas, adding the acid chloride at 0-5degreesC, and carrying out the reaction at 10-45degreesC for 1.5-3 hours, where the catalyst comprises aluminium chloride, iron(III) chloride and/or zinc chloride. The surfactant is prepared by adding the first reactant, the second reactant, and the initiator to the second solvent under an inert gas, and carrying out the reaction 50-90degreesC for 1-5 hours, where the second solvent comprises hexane, benzene, toluene, xylene, ether and/or acetone, the initiator comprising azo, persulfuric acid and/or peroxy initiators, and the mass fraction of the first reactant in the second solvent is 20-40 wt.%. The complex oil displacement agent is prepared by uniformly mixing carbon dioxide and surfactant at 40-80degreesC under 7-18 MPa.

PN CN113546629-A
TI Preparing highly dispersed metal catalyst used in            reforming hydrogen production, by mixing template agent            with ethanol, adding nitric acid and aluminum            isopropoxide, drying, calcining, adding soluble metal            salt solution to obtain suspension, and calcining.
AU MEI Y
   LUO Y
   ZENG R
   MAO J
   YAN J
   ZHANG Y
   LIU X
   HE D
AE UNIV KUNMING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UKST-C)
GA 2021C3029C
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing highly dispersed metal catalyst                involves: mixing Pluronic P123 (RTM: Symmetric                triblock copolymer comprising polyethylene oxide                and polypropylene oxide) template agent with                ethanol, adding nitric acid and aluminum                isopropoxide, and drying to obtain a mixture;                calcining (A) the mixture to obtain aluminum oxide                rich in unsaturated penta-coordinated trivalent                aluminum ion; mixing the obtained aluminum oxide                with ethanol, and adding a soluble metal salt                solution to obtain a suspension, where the metal                element in the soluble metal salt solution is                nickel, cobalt or iron; solid-liquid separating the                suspension to obtain a precipitate; dispersing the                precipitate in water, adjusting the pH to 9-11, and                centrifuging to obtain a solid product; and                calcining (B) the solid product, and                reducing.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing highly                dispersed metal catalyst used in reforming of                methane and carbon dioxide to produce hydrogen (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the dispersibility of                active metals, and can reach methane and carbon                dioxide conversion rate of more than 35% at                600degrees Celsius. The hydrogen/carbon monoxide                ratio is stable at 0.8. The penta-coordinated                structure has improved specific surface area. The                catalyst can be used in different catalytic                reaction systems.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The calcination step                  (A) is carried out by heating at a heating rate                  of 1-10degrees Celsius/minute, preferably                  1degrees Celsius/minute from room temperature to                  300-800degrees Celsius, preferably 500degrees                  Celsius and heat-preserving for 2-6 hours,                  preferably 4 hours in an air atmosphere with a                  flow rate of 10-50 mL/minute, preferably 20                  mL/minute. The calcination step (B) is carried                  out by heating at a heating rate of 1-10degrees                  Celsius/minute, preferably 10degrees                  Celsius/minute from room temperature to                  300-800degrees Celsius, preferably 500degrees                  Celsius, and heat-preserving for 2-6 hours,                  preferably 4 hours.Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of                  template agent, ethanol, nitric acid and aluminum                  isopropoxide is (1-5) g:(10-100) mL:(0-10)                  mL:(2-10) g, preferably 2 g:40 mL:3.2 mL:4.08 g.                  The concentration of the nitric acid is 67 %mass.                  The mass ratio of the aluminum oxide rich in                  unsaturated penta-coordinated trivalent aluminum                  ion to the soluble metal salt in the soluble                  metal salt solution is (1-5) g:(0.1-2) g.Preferred Components: The soluble metal salt                  is nitrate.

GA 202194748J
AB    NOVELTY - Granule comprises 500-800 pts. wt. radix isatidis extract, and 10-40 pts. wt. adhesive.
   USE - Used as granule.
   ADVANTAGE - The granule utilizes adhesive, which can improve the internal cohesive force of the particles, and the powder condition is greatly improved without affecting food, and can extract the medicinal ingredients efficiently, and extracts the radix isatidis and licorice simultaneously, so that the medicinal ingredients can be fully mixed.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing granule, comprising (i) cutting radix isatidis and licorice into slices, placing in a medicine tank, adding water to decocting twice, combining the decoction liquid, and keeping the medicine residue; (ii) concentrating decoction liquid; (iii) placing the medicine residue in a supercritical extraction kettle for supercritical extraction, where the extraction temperature is 70-80 degrees C, and the extraction pressure is 50-80 MPa, the flow rate of carbon dioxide is 40-65 g/minutes, and adding a cosolvent with a weight ratio of 5-8% into carbon dioxide for extraction for 1-6 hours, where the temperature of the separation kettle is 50-65 degrees C, the pressure of the separation kettle is 40-50 MPa, and the separation time is 0.5-1 hour; (iv) combining the liquid medicine obtained in step (ii) and step (iii), adding a adhesive, and stirring at 50-60 degrees C; (v) drying and granulating; and (vi) packaging the finished product.

Preferred Method: The radix isatidis composite extract is obtained by extracting radix isatidis and licorice together. In the step (i), the decocting temperature is 95-100 degrees C, and the decocting time is 1.5-2 hours. Preferred Components: The weight ratio of the radix isatidis and licorice is 50:1. The adhesive is starch slurry, sodium carboxymethyl cellulose, and gelatin solution. The cosolvent is ethanol.

TI Preparation of solid oxide fuel cell anode material used in catalytic dry reforming of methane and carbon dioxide to produce hydrogen, involves dispersing copper, nickel and lanthanum strontium cobalt manganite powder in aqueous solvent.
AU MA W
   YU J
   WANG B
   QIAO C
   LV G
   WAN X
   LI S
AE UNIV KUNMING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UKST-C)
GA 202188207B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of solid oxide fuel cell anode material involves uniformly dispersing copper source, nickel source and lanthanum strontium cobalt manganite (LSCM) powder (I), in aqueous solvent, adding alkali until the reaction without blue flocculent precipitate is generated, obtaining intermediate product, heating intermediate product at 600-700 degrees C for 0.5-3 hours to obtain precursor, under hydrogen atmosphere, and keeping the precursor at 600-700 degrees C for 0.5-3 hours.
   USE - Preparation of solid oxide fuel cell anode material in catalytic dry reforming of methane and carbon dioxide to produce hydrogen (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparing the solid oxide fuel cell anode material in a simple, safe and cost-effective manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of solid oxide fuel cell anode material involves uniformly dispersing copper source, nickel source and LSCM powder of formula: LaxSr1-xCaxMn1-yO3-d (I), where x is 0.2-0.8 and y is 0.4-0.6, in aqueous solvent, adding alkali until the reaction without blue flocculent precipitate is generated, obtaining intermediate product, heating intermediate product at 600-700 degrees C for 0.5-3 hours to obtain precursor, under hydrogen atmosphere, and keeping the precursor at 600-700 degrees C for 0.5-3 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The LSCM powder is prepared by weighing strontium nitrate, lanthanum nitrate, chromium nitrate and manganese nitrate, in the molar ratio of 1:1-4:1-3:1-3, adding water to stir and dissolve until the solution is homogeneous, adding citric acid, stirring, and heating and boiling to generate self-combustion, obtaining combustion product, collecting the combustion product, and calcining. Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of total mass of copper source and nickel source to LSCM powder is 1:1-2. The molar ratio of total element of lanthanum to strontium and the molar ratio of total element of lanthanum and chromium to manganese is 1:1-3. Preferred Components: The copper source is copper nitrate. The nickel source is nickel nitrate. The alkali is sodium hydroxide or potassium hydroxide.

PN WO2021102589-A1; CA3117760-A1; CA3117760-C; US2022098571-A1; CN114829597-A; EP4065705-A1
TI Biodegradable foam used as automotive cushioning,            comprises polyester-based polyurethane foam and            soil-dwelling carbon-digesting bacteria or mixture            comprising soil dwelling carbon-digesting bacteria            embedded in carrier compound.
AU ROBINSON J J
   ASHDOWN N B
   WHITTON G A
AE EVOCO LTD (EVOC-Non-standard)
   EVOCO LTD (EVOC-Non-standard)
   AIWOKE CO LTD (AIWO-Non-standard)
   EVOCO LTD (EVOC-Non-standard)
GA 202159808D
AB    NOVELTY - Biodegradable foam comprises (a)                polyester-based polyurethane foam and (b) a                soil-dwelling carbon-digesting bacteria or a                mixture comprising a soil dwelling carbon-digesting                bacteria embedded in a carrier compound, where the                bacteria or mixture is substantially homogenously                dispersed throughout the polyester-based                polyurethane foam, and the bacteria-containing                polyester polyurethane foam exhibits biodegradation                rates higher than a polyester-based polyurethane                foam absent the soil-dwelling carbon-digesting                bacteria.
   USE - The biodegradable foam is useful in commercial                applications, such as footwear, automotive                cushioning, mattresses and commercial                products.
   ADVANTAGE - The biodegradable foam has excellent physical                or mechanical properties of foam during its                intended product life, and exhibits tunable                biodegradation rates.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for producing a biodegradable polyurethane foam,                involving (a) mixing polyester polyol with                catalysts, water and soil-dwelling carbon-digesting                bacteria to produce a mixture and (b) mixing                isocyanate into the mixture to induce a chemical                reaction between the isocyanate and the water to                produce carbon dioxide gas, and simultaneously                inducing a chemical reaction between the isocyanate                and the polyester polyol to produce a polyester                based polyurethane polymer with carbon dioxide                bubbles trapped within to produce the biodegradable                polyurethane foam, where the biodegradable                polyurethane foam exhibits tunable biodegradation                rates, which are higher than a polyester-based                polyurethane foam absent the bacteria.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The soil-dwelling                  carbon-digesting bacteria comprises at least one                  strain of Bacillus, such as B.subtilus,                  B.pumilus, B.licheniformis, B.megaterium,                  B.cerus, B.alvei, B.coagulans, B.simplex,                  B.brevis and B.amyloliquefaciens. The carrier                  compound is chosen from calcium carbonate, sodium                  bicarbonate and zeolite. The soil-dwelling                  carbon-digesting bacteria is in pure form or                  embedded in a carrier compound. The isocyanate is                  chosen from 4,4'-methylene diphenyl diisocyanate,                  2,4-methylene diphenyl diisocyanate,                  2,2'-methylene diphenyl diisocyanate, 2,4-toluene                  diisocyanate, 2,6-toluene diisocyanate, polymeric                  methylene diphenyl diisocyanate, and                  carbodiimide-modified methylene diphenyl                  diisocyanate. The method further involves adding                  one or more catalysts to the mixture, catalyzing                  the catalyst the reaction between isocyanate and                  water and simultaneously between isocyanate and                  polyester polyol. The catalyst is a tertiary                  amine catalyst, preferably triethylenediamine,                  N-methylmorpholine, N-methylimidazole,                  bis(dimethylaminopropyl)amine,                  dimethylaminoethoxyethanol,                  bis-(2-diemthylaminoethyl)-ether,                  dimethylaminopropylurea,                  N-dimethylaminopropyl-N-(2-hydroxyethyl)-N-methylamine,                  and                  N-dimethylaminoethyl-N-(2-hydroxyethyl)-N-methylamine.                  The catalyst is chosen from dibutyltin dilaurate,                  tin(II) 2-ethylhexanoate bismuth neodecanoate,                  potassium octoate, potassium acetate, zinc                  carboxylates, and nickel carboxylates. Preferred                  Composition: The soil-dwelling carbon-digesting                  bacteria is present in a carrier compound or in a                  pure form, and in an amount of 0.001-100 wt.%.                  The soil-dwelling carbon-digesting bacteria                  embedded in a carrier compound is present in the                  polyester-based polyurethane foam in an amount of                  0.0001-25 wt.%, preferably 0.01-0.5 wt.% of the                  total foam. Preferred Properties: The                  biodegradable foam has a tensile modulus of 5-100                  kg/cm2, a density of 5-1000 kg/m3, an elongation                  at break of 15-700%, a percent modern carbon of                  0-100%, a modulus of less than or equal to 50                  kg/cm2 or 2-100 kg/cm2, an isocyanate index of                  70-200, an average isocyanate functionality of                  2-6, a polyester polyol molecular weight of                  500-5000 g/mol, a polyester polyol average                  hydroxyl functionality of 1.1-6, and a polyester                  polyol hydroxyl number of 20-300 mg potassium                  hydroxide/g polyol. Preferred Method: The method                  further involves mixing surfactant, plasticizer                  and chain extender into the mixture. The method                  further involves mixing flame retardant,                  anti-oxidant, cell opener, emulsifier, hardening                  agent, non-functional filler, cross-linking                  agent, dye, pigment, or other hydroxy or amine                  functionalized material into the mixture.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN112225205-A; CN112225205-B
TI Preparing graphene by a high negative pressure            method involves physically exfoliating graphite powder            in an instant high negative pressure environment under            the protection of nitrogen, carbon dioxide or inert            gas.
AU ZHANG J
   HU J
   MAO H
AE NINGBO YIDE NEW MATERIAL CO LTD (NING-Non-standard)
GA 2021105829
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing graphene by a high negative pressure                method involves physically exfoliating graphite                powder in an instant high negative pressure                environment under the protection of nitrogen,                carbon dioxide or inert gas. The instantaneous high                negative pressure environment refers to an                environment where the vacuum degree reaches -0.090                to -0.099 megapascal (MPa) from normal pressure                within 0.1-1.0 second. The yield of graphene is                greater than 98%, and the number of graphene layers                is 1-10.
   USE - Method for preparing a graphene by a high                negative pressure method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple to operate, effectively                reduces production costs, has short process cycle,                and suitable for large-scale continuous                production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing a graphene/polyethylene terephthalate                (PET)composite material, using the graphene, which                involves:
   adding terephthalic acid, ethylene glycol and                sodium acetate to the graphene dispersion;
   incubating, stirring at 250&#176; C for                1-3 hours, heating to 285&#176; C and                vacuumizing, the reaction proceeds until the system                no longer exotherms;
   preparing the graphene/PET composite material                by water-cooling and pelletizing; and
   preparing the graphene dispersion is prepared                by dispersing graphene in ethylene glycol and                performing ultrasonic dispersion treatment for 30                minutes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method for preparing                  graphene by a high negative pressure method                  includes dispersing graphite powder in an organic                  solvent to form a dispersion with a concentration                  of 0.5-2 mg/mL ultrasonically mixing,                  centrifuging, and vacuum drying to obtain                  pretreated graphite powder. The nitrogen or inert                  gas is introduced into the pretreated graphite                  powder to normal pressure in a vacuum                  environment, and mixed fully to obtain a mixture                  of graphite powder and nitrogen or inert gas. The                  mixture of graphite powder and nitrogen or inert                  gas are blasted off under an instantaneous high                  negative pressure environment to obtain graphene.                  Preferred Component: The inert gas is neon,                  helium, argon, xenon or krypton. The organic                  solvent includes N-methylpyrrolidone,                  N-vinylpyrrolidone, N-dodecylpyrrolidone,                  N,N-dimethylformamide, N,N-dimethylacetamide, 1,                  2-Dichlorobenzene, cyclohexanone, cyclopentanone,                  acetone, ethanol, benzylamine, propylene                  carbonate, butyrolactone, n-propanol,                  isopropanol, chloroform, chloroform, 1,3-dimethyl                  phenyl-2-imidazolinone, pentafluorobenzonitrile,                  pentafluoropyrimidine, and tetrahydrofuran.                  Preferred Composition: The weight ratio of                  terephthalic acid, rights ethylene glycol, sodium                  acetate and graphene dispersion is                  100:20-60:0.2:0.1-20. The weight ratio of                  graphene to ethylene glycol in the graphene                  dispersion is 0.1-5:100. Preferred Conditions:                  The instantaneous high negative pressure                  environment is created by a dual-cylinder linkage                  device. The dual-cylinder linkage device includes                  two cylinders, each of which is equipped with a                  piston, and the two pistons are connected by a                  synchronous lifting mechanism. The construction                  process includes detonate gasoline in one                  cylinder, drive two pistons to move, in order to                  create a momentary high negative pressure                  environment in another cylinder. The gasoline is                  atomized gasoline, the gasoline content in the                  cylinder is 2.0-8.0 g, and the cylinder volume is                  0.02-0.08 m3. The ultrasonic frequency of the                  ultrasonic oscillation is 20-40 kilohertz (KHz),                  the power is 1-5 kilowatt (KW), the ultrasonic                  oscillation time is 10-100 minutes, and the                  temperature is 10-80&#176; C. The centrifugal                  separation speed is 8000-30000 rpm, and the                  centrifugation time is 10-60 minutes. The vacuum                  degree of vacuum drying is -0.01 to -0.098 MPa,                  and the temperature is 0-150&#176; C. The                  vacuum degree of the vacuum environment is -0.01                  to -0.098 MPa. The volume ratio of the pretreated                  graphite powder to the cylinder is 1/20-1/10, and                  the mixing time is 10-90 minutes. The ultrasonic                  frequency of ultrasonic dispersion is 20-30                  kilohertz (KHz), the power is 1-5KW, the time of                  ultrasonic dispersion is 30-60 minutes, and the                  temperature is 10-60&#176; C. The stirring                  rate is 50-200 rpm.

PT P
PN CN112125898-A; CN112125898-B
TI Manufacture of topramezone involves preparing 3-(3-chloro-6-methanesulfonyl-2-methylphenyl)-4,5-dihydro-1,2-oxazole by oxidizing 3-(3-chloro-2-methyl-6-(methylsulfanyl)phenyl)-4,5-dihydro-1,2-oxazole with oxidizing agent.
AU JI L
   ZHOU W
   TAO Y
   SHENG Y
   CHEN J
AE JIANGSU SEVENCONTINENT GREEN CHEM CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
   JIANGSU QIZHOU GREEN TECHNOLOGY RES INST CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
   JIANGSU QIZHOU GREEN TECHNOLOGY RES INST (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202104656F
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of topramezone involves oxidizing 3-(3-chloro-2-methyl-6-(methylsulfanyl)phenyl)-4,5-dihydro-1,2-oxazole (I) with an oxidizing agent under the action of a catalyst to obtain 3-(3-chloro-6-methanesulfonyl-2-methylphenyl)-4,5-dihydro-1,2-oxazole (II), reacting the compound (II) with magnesium and carbon dioxide to obtain 3-(4,5-dihydro-1,2-oxazol-3-yl)-4-methanesulfonyl-2-methylbenzoic acid (III) and condensing the compound (III) and 1-methyl-5-hydroxypyrazole.
   USE - Manufacture of topramezone.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables economical manufacture of topramezone with excellent purity and yield and with fewer by-products, low wastewater treatment difficulty and without toxic side effects in the production process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Manufacture of topramezone involves oxidizing 3-(3-chloro-2-methyl-6-(methylsulfanyl)phenyl)-4,5-dihydro-1,2-oxazole of formula (I) with an oxidizing agent under the action of a catalyst to obtain 3-(3-chloro-6-methanesulfonyl-2-methylphenyl)-4,5-dihydro-1,2-oxazole of formula (II), reacting the compound (II) with magnesium and carbon dioxide to obtain 3-(4,5-dihydro-1,2-oxazol-3-yl)-4-methanesulfonyl-2-methylbenzoic acid of formula (III) and condensing the compound (III) and 1-methyl-5-hydroxypyrazole.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst is ferric chloride and/or sodium tungstate. The oxidizing agent is hydrogen peroxide with concentration of 30-40 wt.%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Processes: The compound (I) is prepared by performing cyclization reaction of (E)-N-((3,6-dichloro-2-methylphenyl)methylidene)hydroxylamine of formula (IV) with thionyl chloride and ethylene to obtain compound to obtain 3-(3,6-dichloro-2-methylphenyl)-4,5-dihydro-1,2-oxazole of formula (V). The compound (IV) is prepared by performing substitution reaction of 1,2-dimethylbenzene of formula (VI) with chlorine or N-chlorosuccinimide (NCS) to obtain 1,4-dichloro-2,3-dimethylbenzene of formula (VII). The manufacture of topramezone involves adding compound (VI) to the solvent, stirring and cooling to 0 degrees C, adding chlorine gas or NCS, controlling the temperature at 0-10 degrees C, and obtaining compound (VII) after post-processing, adding compound (VII) to the solvent, adding catalyst, vacuumizing under nitrogen protection, dripping tert-butyl nitrite, and heating up to 60-80 degrees C under nitrogen protection, maintaining the temperature for the reaction until the reaction is complete, and obtaining the compound (IV) after post-processing, adding compound (IV) to the solvent, replacing the air with ethylene gas, dripping thionyl chloride at a temperature of 20-30 degrees C, maintaining the temperature for the reaction until the reaction is complete, and obtain the compound (V) after post-processing, adding compound (V) and sodium methyl mercaptan into a solvent, and reacting at 70-80 degrees C until the reaction is complete under the protection of an inert gas, and obtain compound (I) after post-treatment, adding compound (I) to the solvent, adding catalyst, lowering the temperature to below 5 degrees C, dripping hydrogen peroxide, raising the temperature to 45-55 degrees C after the dripping, maintaining the temperature for the reaction until the reaction is complete, and obtain compound (II) after post-processing, adding compound (II) to the solvent, lowering the temperature to -2 degrees C, adding magnesium powder, and controlling the temperature to less than 0 degrees C, after adding the magnesium powder, refluxing and reacting until the reaction is complete, after post-processing, and obtaining compound (III), adding compound (III) to the thionyl chloride, heating to reflux, maintaining the temperature for the reaction until the reaction is complete, and removing the thionyl chloride, adding dichloroethane, triethylamine, 1-methyl 5-hydroxypyrazole at 20-30 degrees C, heating to reflux, maintaining the temperature for the reaction until the reaction is complete, and post-processing. Preferred Components: The molar ratio of the compound (I), the oxidizing agent and the catalyst is 1:2-5:0.05-0.5. The molar ratio of the compound (IV) and the dichloroethane is 1:1.5-5. The molar ratio of compound (V) and sodium methyl mercaptan is 1:1-3. The catalyst is copper acetate and/or N-hydroxysuccinimide. The molar ratio of the compound (II) and the magnesium is 1:1-10. The molar ratio of the compound (II) and the carbon dioxide is 1:10-50.

PN CN211878705-U
TI Forest fire-proof early warning device, has first solar panel whose left side is provided with carbon dioxide sensor, where left side of top part of second solar panel is provided with humidity sensor and right side of top part is provided with smoke sensor.
AU HAN H
AE HAN H (HANH-Individual)
GA 2020B2482G
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a forest fire-proof early warning device, comprising a first solar panel, the left and right two sides of the bottom of the first solar panel are respectively provided with a connecting rod; the bottom of the connecting rod is provided with a second solar panel; the left side of the bottom of the first solar panel is provided with a temperature sensor; the left side of the bottom of the first solar panel is provided with a carbon dioxide sensor; the left side of the top of the second solar panel is provided with a humidity sensor; the right side of the top of the second solar panel is provided with a smoke sensor; the beneficial effects of the utility model are as follows: The device uses clean energy to drive, which is environment-friendly and sustainable, at the same time, using intelligent device to detect; it is practical and efficient, which greatly saves the cost of forest protection; it is good for finding and processing the fire in time; it is suitable for large scale popularization and use.

PT P
PN CN111875577-A; CN111875577-B
TI Preparing (R)-propylene carbonate, involves adding e.g. propylene oxide and catalytic promoter, passing air for activation, adding catalytic promoter, cooling, introducing carbon dioxide, reacting and distilling reaction liquid.
AU WU D
   ZHAO N
   ZHENG D
   ZHANG F
AE HUNAN YETOP PHARM TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HUNA-Non-standard)
GA 2020B19758
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing (R)-propylene carbonate, involves (a) adding propylene oxide, (S)-salen-cobalt(II) catalyst and catalytic promoter (a) into a pressure-resistant reaction device, and passing air for activation for 1-6 hours to obtain a mixed solution, (b) reducing the temperature of the mixed solution to 0-5 degrees C, adding catalytic promoter (b), cooling to -15 degrees C to -5 degrees C, introducing carbon dioxide until the internal pressure of the pressure-resistant reaction device is 0.4-0.6 MPa and reacting for 20-30 hours to obtain a reaction liquid, and (c) distilling the reaction liquid under normal pressure to obtain S-propylene oxide, stopping distilling and filtering, and carrying out high vacuum distillation of the mother liquor obtained by filtration to obtain (R)-propylene carbonate, where the mass ratio of propylene oxide, S-salen-cobalt(II) catalyst, catalytic promoter (a) and catalytic promoter (b) is 500:(6-8):(2-4):(2-4).
   USE - The method is useful for preparing (R)-propylene carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple to operate, ensures controllable reaction process, recycles and applies the reaction catalyst and the catalytic promoter, ensures no three wastes emissions in the whole reaction process and excellent quality, adopts a one-pot production method with low production cost, and is suitable for industrial production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The steps (a) and (b) are carried out under stirring conditions of 100-1200 rpm. The step (c) involves recovering (S)-salen-cobalt(II) catalyst, catalytic promoter (a) and catalytic promoter (b) by filtration, and directly using in preparation of next batch of (R)-propylene carbonate. Preferred Components: The catalytic promoter (a) comprises an inorganic iodide, preferably elemental iodine, potassium iodide, sodium iodide or magnesium iodide. The catalytic promoter comprises organic ammonium, preferably 4-dimethylaminopyridine, 1,8-diazabicyclo(5.4.0)undec-7-ene, N,N-diethylaniline, N,N-dimethylaniline, N,N-dimethylethanolamine and/or N,N-diethyl ethanolamine.
DC E13 (Heterocyclics.)
MC E07-A04; E11-A01; E11-F02B; E11-W; N05-B; N06-G; N07-D02; N07-F07

PT P
PN CN111718767-A
TI Preparing yttrium-based oil-soluble high-temperature vanadium inhibitor by mixing sulfonic acid, base oil, solvent and yttrium oxide, carrying out acid-base reaction, adding aqueous ammonia, passing carbon dioxide, reacting and filtering.
AU LI H
   MA Q
   LI X
AE JILIN JURUN ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (JILI-Non-standard)
GA 2020989715
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing yttrium-based oil-soluble high-temperature vanadium inhibitor involves (i) mixing 50-90 pts. wt. sulfonic acid, 10-30 pts. wt. base oil, 200-1000 pts. wt. solvent, 5-20 pts. wt. methanol and 30-50 pts. wt. yttrium oxide, and carrying out acid-base reaction for more than 15 minutes, and (ii) adding 5-20 pts. wt. water and 5-20 pts. wt. aqueous ammonia to the obtained product, passing in carbon dioxide for reaction for 20-60 minutes, adding 5-20 pts. wt. water and 5-20 pts. wt. aqueous ammonia again, continuing to react for 30-60 minutes, and filtering to obtain the product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing yttrium-based oil-soluble high-temperature vanadium inhibitor (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The inhibitor is mainly composed of metal yttrium compound and suitable for situations 1200 degrees C, has excellent high-temperature vanadium inhibitory ability and excellent oxidation resistance, does not contain magnesium compound, and avoids the problems of gelation and surface skinning caused by magnesium vanadium inhibitor.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a yttrium-based oil-soluble high-temperature vanadium inhibitor, where the yttrium content of the yttrium-based oil-soluble high-temperature vanadium inhibitor is 5-20 mg/kg.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (i), the sulfonic acid is chosen from petroleum sulfonic acid, synthetic sulfonic acid, alkylbenzene sulfonic acid and/or alkylnaphthalene sulfonic acid. In step (i), the base oil is mineral oil with kinematic viscosity of 10-200 mm2/second at 40 degrees C. In step (i), the solvent is chosen from xylene, 9C heavy aromatic, octane, petroleum ether, kerosene, diesel and petrol. Preferred Conditions: In step (i), the reaction is carried out at 20-80 degrees C for 10-60 minutes. In step (ii), the feed rate of carbon dioxide is 100-160 l/minute/kg sulfonic acid. In step (ii), the reaction is a carbonation reaction at 20-80 degrees C. Preferred Composition: The yttrium content of the yttrium-based oil-soluble high-temperature vanadium inhibitor is 10-20 mg/kg.

Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).)

PT P
PN WO2020175426-A1; JP2021502234-X; JP2022132373-A; JP2022141963-A
TI Low-temperature hydrocarbon reforming system            comprises first reactor that produces hydrogen and            carbon oxide by reacting oxidized oxygen carrier with            hydrocarbon-based material and second reactor that            reacts with oxidizing agent.
AU TADAFUMI A
   TAKIO N
   AJIRI M
   NOGUCHI T
AE UNIV TOHOKU (TOHO-C)
   SUPER NANO DESIGN CO LTD (SUPE-Non-standard)
   UNIV TOHOKU (TOHO-C)
   SUPER NANO DESIGN CO LTD (SUPE-Non-standard)
GA 202085503P
AB    NOVELTY - Low-temperature hydrocarbon reforming system                comprises first reactor that produces hydrogen and                carbon oxide by reacting oxidized oxygen carrier                with hydrocarbon-based material at 600 degrees C or                lower, and second reactor that reacts the reduced                oxygen carrier with oxidizing agent to produce the                oxidized oxygen carrier.
   USE - Low-temperature hydrocarbon reforming                system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system utilizes the energy of                low-temperature waste heat as the energy for the                reforming reaction from the hydrocarbon material to                hydrogen, is suitable for mass treatment of                hydrocarbon materials, realizes steam reforming of                hydrocarbon-based material, recovers high-purity                hydrogen and mixed gas of carbon monoxide, carbon                dioxide and hydrogen, increases the reaction rate                as much as possible even in a low temperature                state, improves efficiency of oxygen transfer of                the oxygen carrier, saves energy and reduces carbon                dioxide emissions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for producing the hydrogen and/or syngas at low                temperature involving performing the first reaction                of the oxidized oxygen carrier with the                hydrocarbon-based material at 600 degrees C or                lower to produce hydrogen and carbon oxide, and                reacting the reduced oxygen carrier with the                oxidizing agent, and performing second reaction                step of producing the oxidized oxygen                carriers.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method involves                  providing reactors filled with oxygen carriers,                  configuring the reactors to be capable of                  introducing a hydrocarbon-based material or                  oxidizing agent, introducing the hydrocarbon into                  the reactor in which the oxygen carrier is in an                  oxidized state, oxidizing the hydrocarbon-based                  material at 600 degrees C or lower, introducing                  the oxidizing agent into the reactor in which the                  oxygen carrier is in a reduced state, and                  reducing the oxidizing agent, again introducing                  the material using reactor, which can be switched                  between the hydrocarbon-based material and the                  oxidizing agent depending on the state of the                  oxygen carrier filled in each of the reactors.                  The method involves supporting the reactor and/or                  metal cocatalyst on the oxygen carrier coexisting                  inside the oxygen carrier and metal cocatalyst.                  The shape of the oxygen carrier has the most                  active exposed surface. Preferred Components: The                  oxidizing agent comprises water, carbon dioxide,                  oxygen and/or air. The oxygen carrier comprises                  cerium oxide, chromium-doped cerium oxide, iron                  oxide-zirconia complex oxide, iron oxide, indium                  oxide, yttrium-stabilized zirconium oxide,                  scandium oxide doped zirconium oxide, scandium                  oxide, lanthanum oxide gallium, lanthanum                  strontium manganite, gadolinium-doped cerium                  oxide, molybdenum oxide, manganese oxide,                  lanthanum strontium cobalt ferrite, lanthanum                  strontium ferrite (LSF), tricobalt tetroxide,                  cobalt (II) oxide, vanadium oxide and/or ceria                  zirconia solid solutions. Preferred Composition:                  The average particle size of the primary                  particles of the oxygen carrier is 1 mu m or                  less.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

AU DING Y
   LV F
   DING X
   YANG W
   LIN Q
AE FUJIAN ANDAFU NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO (FUJI-Non-standard)
GA 202051872J
AB    NOVELTY - A foam composite material comprises 300-370 pts. wt. polylactic acid, 130-155 pts. wt. polyether polyurethane, 40-48 pts. wt. silicone modified polyurethane, 20-27 pts. wt. hydroxy silicone oil, 14-18 pts. wt. maleic anhydride grafted polyethylene, 15-25 pts. wt. glass fiber, 7-10 pts. wt. nucleating agent, 6-8 pts. wt. chain extender, and 5-8 pts. wt. crosslinking agent.
   USE - Foam composite material.
   ADVANTAGE - The foam composite material is degradable, environmentally-friendly and lightweight, and has excellent tensile strength and resilience performance, low density, high foaming ratio, moderate and uniform cell size, and excellent elasticity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of foam composite material, which involves mixing polylactic acid, polyether polyurethane, silicone modified polyurethane, hydroxy silicone oil, maleic anhydride grafted polyethylene and chain extender, passing to internal mixer, mixing under nitrogen environment at 135-140 degrees C for 70-90 minutes, adding glass fiber, nucleating agent and crosslinking agent at 125-130 degrees C, mixing and blending to obtain mixed blend, placing mixed blend in sealed pressure-resistant mold, heating mold rapidly to 138-145 degrees C, passing supercritical carbon dioxide fluid into mold to control pressure at 15-18 MPa, and releasing pressure when carbon dioxide gas reaches saturation in mixed blend.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The nucleating agent is a mixture of nucleating agent TMC-328 (N,N',N''-tricyclohexyl-1,3,5- benzenetricarboxamide) and zinc acrylate in the mass ratio of 1:(0.35-0.42). Preferred Component: The crosslinking agent is an organic peroxide crosslinking agent. The organic peroxide crosslinking agent is di-tert-butyl hydroperoxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The foam composite material comprises 335 pts. wt. polylactic acid, 143 pts. wt. polyether polyurethane, 44 pts. wt. silicone modified polyurethane, 23.5 pts. wt. hydroxy silicone oil, 16 pts. wt. maleic anhydride grafted polyethylene, 20 pts. wt. glass fiber, 8.8 pts. wt. nucleating agent, 7 pts. wt. chain extender, and 7 pts. wt. crosslinking agent. The chain extender is a mixture of polyurethane chain extender 4,4'-methylene bis(3-chloro-2,6-diethylaniline) and 4-hydroxyethyloxyethyl-1-hydroxyethylbenzene diether in mass ratio of 1:(1.65-1.85). Preferred Process: The silicone modified polyurethane is a silicone-modified polyurethane elastomer synthesized by prepolymer method using 2,4-toluene diisocyanate, tetrahydrofuran propylene oxide copolyether polyol, 3,3'-dichloro-4,4'-diaminodiphenylmethane and silicone diol as raw materials.

PN CN111116968-A
TI Foaming polymer solution for efficiently preparing high expansion rate foam materials comprises e.g. configuring polymer solution, stirring, cooling solution, adding diphenylmethane diisocyanate, and quickly reacting diisocyanate with water.
AU WANG L
   LIU C
AE UNIV TIANJIN POLYTECHNIC (UNTP-C)
GA 202041386B
AB    NOVELTY - Foaming polymer solution for efficiently preparing high expansion rate foam materials comprises (i) configuring polymer solution with a specific mass fraction, stirring under constant temperature magnetic or mechanical until it is completely dissolved, cooling the solution, adding a specific mass fraction of diphenylmethane diisocyanate (MDI), and stirring to obtain polymer/MDI mixed solution, (ii) taking specific amount of polymer/MDI solution into a semi-closed mold, where the volume ratio of solution and mold is 3: 5, injecting water at a specific rate, quickly reacting diisocyanate with water to generate a large amount of carbon dioxide to form a porous foam structure, reacting and removing the foam, and (iii) placing the porous material into an ultrasonic cleaner to clean for 1 hour, then placing in water for a specific period of time, removing unreacted diisocyanate and residual solvent, and placing in a vacuum drying oven at 80 degrees C for drying.
   USE - The method is useful for foaming polymer solution for efficiently preparing high expansion rate foam materials.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: quickly generates a large amount of carbon dioxide to achieve high expansion ratio foaming; has universal applicability; is economical, simple and high efficiency; and has a wide range of application prospects in the fields of industrial construction, aerospace, high-speed trains.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic solvent system is a solvent e.g. dimethylformamide (DMF), dimethylacetamide (DMAC), dichloromethane or chloroform that can be miscible with diisocyanate without reaction. The diisocyanate comprises diphenylmethane diisocyanate (MDI), toluene diisocyanate (TDI) or isophorone diisocyanate (IPDI), and the mass fraction of diisocyanate 10 wt.%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The polymer solution comprises high-performance resins e.g. aromatic polyamides, aromatic polyesters and polyaryl sulfones, and other aromatic polymers, commercial resins e.g. polyvinylidene fluoride (PVDF) and polyacrylonitrile (PAN), and degradable resins e.g. poly epsilon -caprolactone (PCL), polylactic acid (PLA), polyhydroxyalkyl esters (PHA), polyhydroxybutyrate (PHB) and polybutylene succinate-co-terephthalate (PBST), where the mass fraction of polymer solution is 10-20 wt.%. The water is distilled water, and the injection speed is 10 ml/minute.

PT P
PN CN110787200-A; CN110787200-B
TI Extracting microwave-assisted ligustici wallichii effective substances involves crushing dried rhizomes of ligustici wallichii, sieving, and adding 20-25 times the weight of extract of ligustici wallichii powder to obtain mixed solution A.
AU CHEN C
   DU L
   YU H
   XU Q
   HUANG R
   LIU Y
   ZHANG Q
AE UNIV CHENGDU NORMAL (UCDN-C)
   UNIV CHENGDU NORMAL (UCDN-C)
GA 202015500A
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting microwave-assisted ligustici wallichii effective substances involves crushing dried rhizomes of ligustici wallichii, sieving, and adding 20-25 times the weight of extract of ligustici wallichii powder to obtain mixed solution A. The mixed solution A is subjected to gradient microwave extraction, and extraction is completed to obtain microwave extraction solution B. The microwave extraction solution B added to carbon dioxide subcritical extraction device for extraction, control carbon dioxide (CO2) flow rate at 30-35 L/h, and then analyze to obtain extracts. The chromatographic silica gel column is installed, the diameter-height ratio is 1: 0.5-1: 3, and the extract is dissolved with methanol. The 1-2 column volumes of solvent A added for elution to obtain eluent I, and discard. The solvent B was eluted with 4-7 column volumes of solvent B to obtain eluent II. The eluent II was recovered under reduced pressure at 35-40 degrees C to obtain effective product of ligustici wallichii.
   USE - Method for extracting microwave-assisted ligustici wallichii effective substances.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to extract microwave-assisted ligustici wallichii effective substances has low extraction solvent toxicity, simple process, high product yield, low cost, and suitable for industrial production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Component: The solvent A is one or more mixtures of petroleum ether, n-hexane, n-heptane, and ethyl acetate. The solvent B is one or more mixtures of ethyl acetate, dichloromethane, and chloroform. Preferred Composition: The extractant in the composition consists of bile chloride and ethanol with a molar ratio of 1: 2-1: 4. The process of supercritical CO2 extraction includes polyglycerol fatty acid ester is used as an additive, and the amount is 0.2-0.3% of the volume of the extraction device. The process of supercritical CO2 extraction includes 0.1-1 wt.% vitamin C in the volume of the extraction device is also added as a stabilizer. Preferred Conditions: The medium microwave extraction is divided into two stages in the first stage, the mixed liquid A is subjected to microwave extraction at a temperature of 40-50 degrees C, a microwave frequency of 2000-2200 megahertz (MHz), and a power of 150-180 watt for 8-10 minutes. The second stage includes the extraction temperature, time and power are the same as in the first stage, and the extraction frequency is 2800-3000 MHz. The extraction pressure is 25-30 megapascal (MPa), and the extraction temperature is 40-55 degrees C. The analytical pressure is 6-9 MPa, and the analytical temperature is 25-38 degrees C. The linear velocity of the elution solvent in 0.2-8 cm/min-1. The method includes during the supercritical CO2 extraction process, an entrainer is continuously pumped, and the amount of the entrainer is 6-10% of the mass of the extract B. The entrainer includes acetone, ethanol, and butyl acrylate, and the addition ratio is 1: 3-4: 1 by weight.

PT P
PN CN109280936-A; CN109280936-B
TI Copper oxide electrode preparation involves using            copper nitrate as copper source, pyromellitic acid and            triethylenediamine as double ligands, and precursor            synthesized by hydrothermal one-step method calcined at            high temperature.
AU LU J
   YANG M
   ZHI W
   SHAN S
   WANG Z
   WANG H
AE UNIV EAST CHINA NORMAL (UYEN-C)
GA 201915862L
AB    NOVELTY - Copper (I) oxide electrode preparation                involves using copper (III) nitrate as copper                source, pyromellitic acid and triethylenediamine as                double ligands, and precursor synthesized by                hydrothermal one-step method calcined at high                temperature into copper (I) oxide electrode                material. The copper nitrate trihydrate is mixed                with N,N-dimethylformamide, pyromellitic acid and                triethylenediamine hexahydrate in a volume ratio of                1:50-60:0.27-6.70:0.14-3.55 at 100-140&#176; C.                The reaction is carried out for 6-96 hours, where                the reaction solution is washed with ethanol and                dried to obtain a precursor.
   USE - Method for preparing copper (I) oxide                electrode.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares the copper (I) oxide                electrode in a simple, cost-effective and                eco-friendly manner with excellent selectivity in                electrocatalytic carbon dioxide synthesis alcohol,                high yield and effectively utilized carbon                dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The prepared precursor is calcined at                300-700&#176; C for 1-12 hours to obtain copper                (I) oxide electrode material. The obtained copper                (I) oxide electrode material is mixed with                Nafion(RTM: a sulfonated tetrafluoroethylene based                fluoropolymer-copolymer) 117 and deionized water in                a weight-to-volume ratio of 1:4-8:8-16 with                dispersing uniformly coated on both sides of the                carbon paper, and then dried to make the modified                electrode.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The copper (I) oxide                  modified electrode is used as a cathode of a                  two-chamber electrolytic cell and a platinum                  plate anode for electroreduction reaction of an                  alcohol compound synthesized by carbon                  dioxide.

PN US2019024540-A1; KR2019010038-A; CN109281719-A; KR2026327-B1; US10605124-B2; CN109281719-B
TI Hybrid power generating system for used in liquefied natural gas treatment system, comprises a power generating system using supercritical carbon dioxide configured to use the supercritical as a working fluid.
AU CHA S H
   CHE S
AE DOOSAN HEAVY IND & CONSTR CO LTD (DOOS-C)
   DOOSAN HEAVY IND & CONSTR CO LTD (DOOS-C)
   DOOSAN HEAVY IND & CONSTR CO LTD (DOOS-C)
GA 201907758G
AB    NOVELTY - The hybrid power generating system comprises a power generating system using supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2) configured to use the supercritical CO2 as a working fluid. A liquefied natural gas (LNG) treatment system to vaporize LNG, where the working fluid is cooled in the power generating system using supercritical CO2 and the LNG treatment system and is re-circulated to the power generating system using supercritical CO2. The power generating system using supercritical CO2 has a compressor (100) equipped to compress the working fluid. The heat exchanger (300) supply heat from an outside heat source to heat a portion of the working fluid passing through the compressor. The turbine (400) is driven by the working fluid.
   USE - Hybrid power generating system for used in liquefied natural gas (LNG) treatment system.
   ADVANTAGE - The hybrid power generating system improves the waste heat recovery efficiency of the power generating system using supercritical CO2 by utilizing the working fluid of the power generating system. The whole efficiency of the LNG treatment system is being improved by reducing the power consumption of the seawater pump in the LNG treatment system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of switchover mode start state after driving of the power generating system using supercritical CO2.
   Compressor (100)
   Heat exchanger (300)
   Turbine (400)
   Star-up Cooler (500)
   Second Control Valve (700)
DC Q51 (Internal combustion engines, reciprocating engines, rotary engines (F01, F02B,D,F,G,M,N,P))

TI Graphitic carbon nitride sensor for sensing an            analyte, such as oxygen or carbon dioxide, has sensing            medium which comprises uncondensed graphitic carbon            nitride disposed upon functionalized graphitic            material.
AU ELLIS J E
   STAR A
AE UNIV PITTSBURGH COMMONWEALTH SYSTEM HIGH (UPIT-C)
GA 201900451W
AB    NOVELTY - The sensor (10) includes a second electrode                (D) spaced from a first electrode (S). A sensing                medium is disposed on a substrate (30) between the                first electrode and the second electrode. The                sensing medium comprises a functionalized graphitic                material (20) and an uncondensed graphitic carbon                nitride (24) disposed upon the functionalized                graphitic material. An energy application system                applies electromagnetic energy to the sensing                medium to increase the conductance of the sensing                medium. A measurement system measures a variable                relating to the conductance of the sensing medium                which is dependent upon the presence of an analyte                to be detected.
   USE - Graphitic carbon nitride sensor for sensing an                analyte, such as oxygen or carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - In the case of holey reduced graphene oxide                (HGO), the band gap and/or electronic structure of                the composite material may be adjusted through                control of the hole size and frequency of the HGO,                thus providing a mechanism to detect the presence                of a number of different analytes.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                method of sensing an analyte.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing is a schematic diagram                illustrating a sensor which is operable as a                field-effect transistor.
   Sensor (10)
   Functionalized graphitic material (20)
   Graphitic carbon nitride (24)
   Substrate (30)
   Drain electrode (D)
   Source electrode (S)

PN CN109796035-A; CN109796035-B
TI Preparing ultrafine calcium carbonate comprises e.g. driving high-purity carbon dioxide into the reaction tank using gas compressor, cutting off the pipeline valve and heating reaction tank by infrared heating device.
AU YAN Z
AE YAN Z (YANZ-Individual)
   HUNAN JINJIAN NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO (HUNA-Non-standard)
GA 2019474424
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing ultrafine calcium carbonate comprises e.g. (i) driving high-purity carbon dioxide into the reaction tank using gas compressor, when the high-purity carbon dioxide pressure in the reaction tank reaches to 0.3 MPa, cutting off the pipeline valve, stopping burning carbon dioxide gas, heating reaction tank by infrared heating device, heating at rate of 5-10 degrees C/minutes, stopping heating and insulating when the temperature in the tank reaches to 80-95 degrees C, (ii) taking refined calcium hydroxide cooked pulp with 5-8% concentration in the storage tank and electrically heating at 80-90 degrees C, pressurizing calcium hydroxide mature slurry to 0.8 MPa by centrifugal pump and driving into the reaction tank through spray head and controlling the spraying time for 1-1.2 hours, taking high-purity carbon dioxide gas and reacting rapidly with calcium hydroxide mature slurry to obtain calcium carbonate and water, and reacting calcium carbonate, carbon dioxide and water.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing ultrafine calcium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple; has high production efficiency, and stable product quality; is safe, environmentally friendly and non-polluting; and improves product stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing ultrafine calcium carbonate comprises (i) driving high-purity carbon dioxide into the reaction tank using gas compressor, when the high-purity carbon dioxide pressure in the reaction tank reaches to 0.3 MPa, cutting off the pipeline valve, stopping burning carbon dioxide gas, heating reaction tank by infrared heating device, heating at rate of 5-10 degrees C/minutes, stopping heating and insulating when the temperature in the tank reaches to 80-95 degrees C, (ii) taking refined calcium hydroxide cooked pulp with 5-8% concentration in the storage tank and electrically heating at 80-90 degrees C, pressurizing calcium hydroxide mature slurry to 0.8 MPa by centrifugal pump and driving into the reaction tank through spray head and controlling the spraying time for 1-1.2 hours, taking high-purity carbon dioxide gas and reacting rapidly with calcium hydroxide mature slurry to obtain calcium carbonate and water, in which using small amount of carbon dioxide, reacting calcium carbonate, carbon dioxide and water to obtain calcium bicarbonate under the condition of excess carbon dioxide gas in the reaction tank, in which using excess amount of carbon dioxide, spraying, closing the calcium hydroxide mature slurry feed valve, opening the reaction tank discharge valve, and conveying the reaction mixture into the buffer tank, (iii) equipping bottom of the buffer tank with rotating knife row, heating buffer tank by infrared heating device to temperature of 280-350 degrees C, conveying the calcium bicarbonate into the buffer tank, heating and decomposing into calcium carbonate, carbon dioxide, water at high temperature, when the water in the buffer tank generates water vapor at high temperature of the internal temperature of the buffer tank at 280-350 degrees C, opening mixture in a buffer tank for 2-2.5 hours and opening the rotary knife row and the rotating laser, cutting and boiling calcium carbonate particles at high speed by rotating the knife row, cutting boiling calcium carbonate particles at high speed by rotating laser, cutting for 5-6 hours, and where the particle size of the calcium carbonate particles is 30-80 nm, (iv) opening the induced draft fan and opening the buffer tank discharge valve, passing mixture in the buffer tank through a cyclone and bag filter, when the electrostatic precipitator collects ultra-fine calcium carbonate products, heating cyclone dust collector, bag filter, and electrostatic precipitator by infrared heating device, ensuring the cyclone dust collector insulation layer and bag filter insulation layer, where the temperature in the electrostatic precipitator insulation layer is greater than 270 degrees C, taking water vapor and carbon dioxide into the water tank by the induced draft fan, when the water vapor turns into liquid water when it is cold, using liquid water as cooked slurry with calcium hydroxide, purifying the carbon dioxide gas and using as raw material in a carbon dioxide gas tank.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The arrangement of the teeth of the rotary cutter row is as follows outer wall of the rotating knife row is uniformly distributed with 6 teeth, 2 teeth are uniformly distributed at an angle of 30 degrees from the horizontal direction, 2 teeth are uniformly distributed at 75 degrees from the horizontal direction and 2 teeth in parallel with the horizontal direction. A rotating laser is mounted on the rotating blade, and the laser head of the rotating laser is arranged as follows uniformly 6 laser heads, 2 laser heads are uniformly distributed at an angle of 30 degrees from the horizontal direction, 2 laser heads are at an angle of 60 degrees from the horizontal direction, uniformly distributed two laser heads in parallel with the horizontal direction. The teeth of the rotary cutter are arranged as follows 6 teeth are uniformly distributed and at an angle of 60 degrees to the horizontal. The teeth of the rotary cutter are arranged as follows 4 teeth are uniformly distributed and at an angle of 60 degrees to the horizontal. A rotating laser is mounted on the rotating blade row. The laser head of the rotating laser is arranged as follows 4 laser heads are evenly distributed and at an angle of 60 degrees to the horizontal direction. In the step (iii), the buffer tank pressure is controlling to 0.15-0.2 MPa. Preferred Components: The buffer tank includes a tank body, a buffer tank feed port, buffer tank discharge port, laser head, cutter teeth, rotary cutter drive motor, motor and laser assembly, rotating laser and rotating blade. The upper part of the can body is provided with a buffer tank inlet. A buffer tank discharge port is provided on the lower side. A rotating blade is arranged at the bottom of the can body. The rotary cutter row is driven to rotate by rotating the cutter drive motor. A multiple of teeth are arranged outside the rotating blade row. The rotating blade is provided with a rotating laser. A multiple of laser heads are arranged on the top of the rotating laser. The bottom is driven by the motor and the laser assembly, cutting and boiling calcium carbonate particles at high speed by rotating the knife row. The boiling calcium carbonate particles are cut at a high speed by a rotating laser. The calcium carbonate particles are cut to a particle size of between 30 and 80 nm. The outer wall of the rotating blade is uniformly distributed with 6 teeth, 2 teeth are uniformly distributed at an angle of 30 degrees from the horizontal direction, 2 teeth are uniformly distributed at 75 degrees from the horizontal direction and two teeth are provided in parallel with the horizontal direction. The laser head of the rotating laser is arranged as follows outer wall of the rotating laser is uniformly distributed with 6 laser heads, 2 laser heads are uniformly distributed at an angle of 30 degrees from the horizontal direction, 2 laser heads are uniformly distributed at an angle of 60 degrees from the horizontal direction and two laser heads are uniformly distributed in parallel with the horizontal direction.

TI Processing carbon dioxide geological storage            enhanced gas extraction induced risk test method            comprises e.g. sampling from the outburst coal seams in            the coal mine area, testing, sampling and adding water            to the coal particles.
AU ZHANG C
   WANG E
   FENG X
   SHEN R
   LI Z
AE UNIV CHINA MINING & TECHNOLOGY BEIJING (UYMB-C)
GA 2019840858
AB    NOVELTY - Processing carbon dioxide geological storage                enhanced gas extraction induced risk test method                comprises (ia) sampling from the outburst coal                seams in the coal mine area, testing and conducting                on-site investigations, sampling the coal seams                collected on site, determining the relevant                parameters of the outburst coal seam, determining                the stress in the test protocol, water injection                rate and test parameters e.g. fracturing position,                (ib) using crushing sieve, collecting coal sampling                sieving coal with 0-1 mm grain diameter, using                crushed sieve, dividing the collected coal block                into sieve particles of 0-1 mm particle size,                adding water to the coal particles, and(ic) loading                6 wt.% water content coal sample into the                visualization box, where the visualization box                includes a box cavity and a box cover, sealing ring                is arranged between the box cavity and the cover                plate and fixed by sealing bolt, where the right                side of the visualization box has protruding                opening.
   USE - The method is useful for processing carbon                dioxide geological storage enhanced gas extraction                induced risk test method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple; has good detection                performance; can simulate the geological storage of                carbon dioxide in coal seams and strengthen the gas                drainage process under large-scale                conditions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Processing carbon dioxide geological storage                enhanced gas extraction induced risk test method                comprises (ia) sampling from the outburst coal                seams in the coal mine area, testing and conducting                on-site investigations, sampling the coal seams                collected on site, determining the relevant                parameters of the outburst coal seam, determining                the stress in the test protocol, water injection                rate and test parameters e.g. fracturing position,                (ib) using crushing sieve, collecting coal sampling                sieving coal with 0-1 mm grain diameter, using                crushed sieve, dividing the collected coal block                into sieve particles of 0-1 mm particle size,                adding water to the coal particles until 6 wt.%                water content coal sample, (ic) loading 6 wt.%                water content coal sample cover plate and                fixed by a sealing bolt, where the right side of                the visualization box has a protruding opening,                providing bottom of the visualization box with a                tank inflation port at the bottom and the outside,                disposing multiple of transparent loading plates on                the top of the visualization box and on the left                side wall and the front side wall of the                visualization box, where the transparent loading                plate on the top of the visualization box has six                serial numbers of X1-X6, the transparent loading                plate on the front side wall of the visualization                box has six serial numbers of Y1-Y6, transparent                loading plate on the left side wall of the                visualization box is Z1, providing each transparent                loading plate with loading piston, connecting the                loading piston with the pressurized cylinder                through the wall of the tank, there are multiple                steps in the uniform step on the back of the                visualization box, arranging external joint on the                mounting hole of the visualization box body,                arranging gasket between the external joint and the                wall of the visualization box body, providing                pressure sensor in the mounting hole as needed,                temperature sensor or multi-function drilling,                where multi-functional drilling includes a sealing                section and a venting section, and the                multifunctional drilling hole has total length of                0.5 m, length of the sealing section is 0.15 m, and the pipe wall, setting the serial number for the                multi-function drilling to A1-A6, using mounting                hole opened on the back of the visualization box to                the 6% of the visualization box and water rate coal                sample buried in air pressure sensor in the                visualization box, and finally using loading                piston, driving the transparent loading plate,                molding the 6 wt.% moisture content coal sample                under the condition of 20 MPa for 1 hour, and then                pressing the transparent loading plates, and                pressurizing the coal sample.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further                  comprises (id) installing instant protruding door                  in the protruding opening on the right side of                  the visualization box, where the momentary                  protruding door includes a rupture disc holder,                  rupture disc holder has mounting flange at one                  end, rupture disc holder is internally provided                  with a primary rupture disc and a secondary                  rupture disc which are connected in series,                  bursting pressure of the primary rupture disc is                  0.4/MPa, bursting pressure of the secondary                  rupture disc is 0.6/MPa, providing rupture disc                  holder between the primary rupture disc and the                  secondary rupture disc with a protruding door                  inflation port, inflating the primary rupture                  disc and
