rupture disc, and instantly punching the                  protruding mouth, and paper simulates the                  protruding conditions induced by the violent                  factors e.g. uncovering of coal, setting the air                  pressure sensor in the mounting hole, inserting                  the air pressure sensor into the sensor mounting                  hole on the outside of the visualization box by a                  gasket, connecting end of the air pressure sensor                  to the data acquisition and control system via a                  data line, arranging the polyurethane tube                  extending inside the coal sample inside the                  sensor mounting hole, testing the gas pressure at                  different positions by adjusting the position of                  the open end of the polyurethane tube, disposing                  the polyurethane pneumatic joint between the                  polyurethane tube and the wall of the                  visualization box body, setting the temperature                  sensor in the mounting hole, connecting data line                  to the tail of the temperature sensor, fixing in                  the sensor mounting hole of the visualization box                  through a conversion joint and a gasket,                  embedding the temperature sensor and the data                  line of the head in the position of the coal                  sample where temperature or stress needs and                  detecting, setting up multi-function drilling                  holes in the mounting holes, providing                  multifunctional drilling tail with an external                  joint on the visualization box, arranging the                  gasket between and the                  multifunctional drilling hole has total length of                  0.5 m, length of the sealing section is 0.15 m,                  and the length of the gas-permeable section is                  0.35 m, where ventilating section wall has                  venting hole distributed in the circumferential                  direction and the radial direction, and the vent applying triaxial stress                  loading operation to the coal sample to apply                  stress to the coal sample, subjecting the six                  transparent injection plates with the serial                  number X1-X6 on the top of the coal sample to                  stress of 6 MPa, and applying the six transparent                  plates with the serial number Y1-Y6 with stress                  of 8 MPa, left side of a transparent loading                  plate with the serial number Z1 applies stress of                  10 MPa, (iib) startting the vacuum pump, port at the bottom of the tank, dividing into                  four stages for ensuring that the coal sample                  adsorption equilibrium pressure is 0.74 MPa,                  first stage is inflated for 12 hours and the                  inflation pressure is 0.3 MPa, closing the                  cylinder, and stabilizing for 6 hours, inflating                  the second stage for 12 hours under pressure of                  0.6 MPa, then closing the cylinder, stabilizing                  for 6 hours, inflating the third stage for 12                  hours under pressure of 0.74 MPa, closing the                  cylinder and stabilizing for 6 hours, inflating                  the fourth stage for 6 hours, inflating and                  stabilizing at pressure of 0.74 MPa, and then                  closing the cylinder, total time is about 60                  hours, and recording the gas volume by the flow                  meter I32 during the inflation process, inflating                  the high-pressure gas cylinder to the protruding                  door inflation port between the primary rupture                  disc and the secondary rupture disc of the pulverized coal thrown from the                  protruding mouth and weighing, (iva) repeating                  steps (ic)-to (iic), using the plunger pump,                  injecting high-pressure PT P
PN CN114395816-A
TI Preparing ultra-low elongation and oil-free polypropylene staple fiber for anti-epidemic mask, comprises depositing mixture of aniline and carbon dioxide on modified polypropylene fibers under light condition.
AU SHAN Z
   HE H
   ZHANG S
AE JIANGSU ZHONGSHI FIBRE CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202263092W
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing ultra-low elongation and oil-free polypropylene staple fiber comprises depositing a mixture of aniline and carbon dioxide on modified polypropylene fibers under light conditions.
   USE - Preparation method of ultra-low elongation and oil-free polypropylene staple fiber for anti-epidemic mask
   ADVANTAGE - The ultra-low elongation oil-free polypropylene short fiber has low elongation and good waterproof and oleophobic and antibacterial property.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The modified polypropylene fiber is obtained by mixing trihydrazinobenzene, p-bromophenol and 3-epoxyethyl-3-bromopropene. The preparation method specifically comprises (i) mixing 3-epoxyethyl-3-bromopropene, lauroyl peroxide and ether to prepare 3-epoxyethyl-3-bromopropene solution, mixing sodium sulfite and ethanol, adjusting pH with sulfuric acid to prepare sodium bisulfite solution, refluxing mixed solution, potassium hydroxide solution, tetrabutylammonium bromide and tetrahydrofuran mixed oil bath, adding 3-epoxyethyl-3-bromopropene solution dropwise, extracting with dichloromethane and water, and rotary evaporating to prepare to obtain spinning solution, adding the spinning solution into the spinning box, using a screw extruder to spin, and cooling and solidifying by side blowing to prepare the modified polypropylene fiber, and (ii) mixing high temperature and high pressure gasification of aniline with carbon dioxide gas to prepare a mixed gas, adding modified polypropylene fibers into a closed container, introducing mixed gas, increasing pressure and temperature, and depositing fiber under light conditions, where the illuminance of the light is 550-750 lx, preferably by (a) adding the spinning solution into a spinning box at 189-190degrees Celsius, and at 230-260degrees Celsius and a spinning speed of 600-800 m/minute, spinning using a screw extruder equipped with a spinneret with a diameter of 0.2-0.3 mm, and cooling and solidifying by side blowing at 14-20degrees Celsius, and humidity of 25-30% and wind speed of 0.8-1.5 m/second for 30-40 minutes to prepare modified polypropylene fiber, and (b) adding the modified polypropylene fiber into a closed container, vacuumizing at 10-20 Pa for 30-40 minutes, introducing a mixture of 8-9 times the mass of the modified polypropylene fiber at 30-50 ml/minute, pressurizing to 2.4-2.6 MPa at 30-40 Pa/minute, heating to 138-142 at 2-4degrees Celsius/minute, keeping in light for 13-15 hours, taking out, washing 1-3 times with deionized water, adding in a 50-60degrees Celsius oven for 1.3-1.7 hours, cutting into 3-4 cm short fibers with a cutting machine to prepare ultra-low elongation oil-free polypropylene short fiber. The preparation method of spinning solution comprises: mixing the mixed solution, 10-12 wt.% potassium hydroxide solution, tetrabutylammonium bromide and tetrahydrofuran in a mass ratio of 1:0.9:0.9:49-1:1:0.11:50, placing in 70-72degrees Celsius oil bath, stirring and refluxing at 1200-1300 revolutions/minute for 25-35 minutes, adding 3-epoxyethyl-3-bromopropene solution with 2-3 times the mass of the mixture dropwise at 40-60 drops/minute, continuously refluxing for 9.8-10.2 hours, using dichloromethane and water for extraction for 2-4 times in turn, performing rotary steam at 10-20 Pa and 50-60degrees Celsius, and 1200-1400 revolutions/minute for 1-2 hours. The preparation method of the 3-epoxyethyl 3-bromopropene solution comprises at 24-26degrees Celsius, mixing 3-epoxyethyl-3-bromopropene, lauroyl peroxide and diethyl ether in a mass ratio of 1:0.4:10-1:0.6:12, and stirring at 1200-1300 revolutions/minute for 30-40 minutes. The preparation method of the mixed solution comprises mixing trihydrazinobenzene and ethanol in a mass ratio of 1:15-1:16, adding in an oil bath at 35-45degrees Celsius, and stirring and refluxing at 1200-1300 revolutions/minute for 1-2 hours, and heating to 130-132degrees Celsius at 2-4degrees Celsius, subsequently, adding sodium bisulfite solution with a mass of 0.8-1.2 times the mass of trihydrazinobenzene dropwise at 80-100 drops/minute, and stirring at 1200-1300 revolutions/minute for 15-25 minutes, adding p-bromophenol with 3-4 times the mass of trihydrazinobenzene dropwise at 80-100 drops/minute, continuously stirring and refluxing for 23-25 hours, adding 10-12 wt.% potassium hydroxide solution dropwise at 40-60 drops/minute to adjust the pH to 6.8-7.2, subsequently, at 10-20 Pa and 35-45degrees Celsius, rotary-steaming at 1200-1400 revolutions/minute for 30-40 minutes to prepare a mixed solution. The preparation method of the sodium bisulfite solution comprises at 24-26degrees Celsius, mixing sodium sulfite and ethanol in a mass ratio of 1:4-1:6, and stirring at 1200-1300 revolutions/minute for 30-40 minutes, subsequently adding 10-12 wt.% sulfuric acid solution dropwise at 40-60 drops/minute to adjust the pH to 3-4, and continuously stirring for 30-40 minutes. The preparation method of the mixed gas comprises: adding aniline into a reaction kettle at 185-187degrees Celsius and 0.3-0.5 MPa for 3-4 hours, introducing carbon dioxide with 2.2-2.4 times the mass of the aniline at 3-5 m/second to prepare a mixed gas.
DC A17 (Polymers of unsubstituted aliphatic monoolefins; including polyethylene.); A32 (Polymer fabrication - such as moulding, extrusion, forming, laminating, spinning.); A83 (Clothing, footwear.); ; F06 (Chemical-type treatment of textiles (D06B, L, M, P, Q).)

   USE - A carbonated aerosol skin external preparation.
   ADVANTAGE - The urea-containing carbonated aerosol skin external preparation has less ammonia odor from the agent after discharge, is easy to spread on the affected area, and has excellent moisturizing properties even when stored at high temperature.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Compositions: Amount of component (B) in the aerosol stock solution is 0.01-5 mass%. Component (B) is xanthan gum, hydroxypropylmethyl cellulose, hydroxypropylmethylcellulose stearoxy ether, acrylic acid/(meth)acrylic acid alkyl copolymer, and/or carboxyvinyl polymer. Component (B) contains water-soluble polymers selected from hydroxypropylmethyl cellulose and/or 2-methacryloyloxyethyl phosphorylcholine/butyl methacrylate copolymer. The carbonated aerosol skin external preparation is discharged in the form of a foam. Preferred Properties: Molecular weight of component (B) is greater than or equal to 10000. Component (B) is a water-soluble polymer having a viscosity of greater than or equal to 5 mPa.s at 20 degrees C in a 2 mass% aqueous solution of component (B). The pH immediately after discharge is less than or equal to 7.

AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of bismuth based nano materials                comprises preparing nano flake bismuth based                material by electrochemical reduction from nanorod                like bismuth sulfide. The nano rod like bismuth                sulfide is bismuth (III)                tris(diethyldithiocarbamate) is prepared by                hydrothermal treatment.
   USE - The preparation of bismuth based nano                materials is used as catalyst for electrocatalytic                carbon dioxide reduction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method solves the problem of low                concentration of oxygen active component in sub                surface solvent and has good carbon dioxide                electrocatalytic reduction efficiency.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The hydrothermal treatment                  is at 120-160 degrees C for 6-24 hours. The                  bismuth (III) tris(diethyldithiocarbamate) is                  prepared by mixing adding 33 ml 0.1 mol/l bismuth                  source precursor solution to 100 ml 0.1 mol/l                  sodium diethyldithiocarbamate solution. The                  electrochemical reduction comprises dispersing                  nano rod like bismuth sulfide and 5 wt.%                  perfluorosulfonic acid PTFE copolymer solution in                  ethanol, performing ultrasonic treatment, using 3                  M potassium chloride silver/silver chloride as                  reference electrode and platinum electrode e.g.                  platinum mesh as counter electrode and performing                  in-situ electrochemical reduction. Preferred                  Components: The amount of bismuth (III)                  tris(diethyldithiocarbamate) and deionized water                  in the hydrothermal treatment is 4-10 g/100 ml.                  The sulfur source precursor is sodium                  diethyldithiocarbamate. The molar ratio of                  bismuth source precursor to sulfur source                  precursor is 1:1-1:5, preferably 1:3. The                  concentration of bismuth source precursor is 0.1                  mol/l. The concentration of sulfur source                  precursor is 0.1 mol/l. The solvent in solution                  of bismuth source precursor and sulfur source                  precursor is water and/or methanol. The dosage of                  nano rod like bismuth sulfide and 5 wt.%                  perfluorosulfonic acid PTFE copolymer solution is                  0.1-2 mg/ mu L. The amount of nano rod like                  bismuth sulfide to ethanol is 1-50 mg/ml.

PN CN111362722-A
TI Regulating gradient mineralization of cement-based            materials comprises adding composite bacteria            suspension into the cement-based material and            maintaining it in the presence of carbon dioxide.
AU QIAN C
   CHEN Y
   WANG X
   XUE B
AE UNIV SOUTHEAST (UYSE-C)
GA 202063647H
AB    NOVELTY - Regulating gradient mineralization of                cement-based materials comprises adding composite                bacteria suspension into the cement-based material                and maintaining it in the presence of carbon                dioxide, where composite bacterial liquid                suspension is prepared by mixing Bacillus                mucilaginosus colloidal powder and photosynthetic                bacterial powder into water.
   USE - The method is useful for regulating gradient                mineralization of cement-based materials.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The water-cement mass                  ratio of the cement-based material is 0.4-0.6:1.                  The amount of composite bacterial suspension                  accounts for 0.3-0.5% the amount of the                  cement-based material. The content of the spores                  and photosynthetic bacterial powder of the                  Bacillus mucilaginosus is 4-8 g total bacterial                  powder per 500 ml of deionized water. The                  Bacillus mucilaginosus is Bacillus mucilaginosus                  having preservation number CCTCC No: M 2012406;                  the photosynthetic bacteria are photosynthetic                  bacteria having preservation number CCTCC No: M                  2012224. The cement-based material is Portland                  cement, elements are expressed in the form of                  oxides, ratio of element mass content: aluminum                  oxide and magnesium oxide is 3.6-4.3; silica and                  magnesium oxide is 10-12; sulfur trioxide and                  magnesium oxide is 2.0-2.5; calcium oxide and                  magnesium oxide is 25-26.5; potassium oxide and                  magnesium oxide is 0.3-0.6; sodium oxide and                  magnesium oxide is 0-0.1; and iron oxide and                  magnesium oxide is 1.5-2.0. Preferred Method: The                  composite bacterial liquid suspension is prepared                  by dissolving the powdered Bacillus mucilaginosus                  spores and photosynthetic bacterial powder at a                  mass ratio of 0.5-2.5:1 in deionized water,                  shaking, and culturing to obtain composite                  bacterial suspension having concentration of 109                  to 1010 CFU/ml. The culturing is carried out                  under the conditions of pH 9-11 and temperature                  of 20-30 degrees C for 20-30 hours. The method                  further includes centrifuging the composite                  bacterial suspension at a speed of 4000-7000                  revolutions/minute for 8-15 minutes, and taking                  the supernatant. The curing is carried out in the                  presence of carbon dioxide at a carbon dioxide                  concentration of 60.51-80.68 mol/l for 7-9 days                  at 18-22 degrees C.

AB    NOVELTY - Producing highly dispersible carbon black, comprises (a) heat treating carbon black under alkaline, carbon dioxide or steam atmosphere, and (b) oxidatively reforming the heat treated carbon black under oxidizing gas atmosphere.
   USE - The method is useful for producing highly dispersible carbon black.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves active area into which functional groups are attached to surface of carbon black, and prevents dispersibility of oxidatively modified carbon black.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alkaline atmosphere is solution or gas atmosphere including sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, ammonia and/or ammonium hydroxide. The rate of change of d-spacing by X-ray diffraction of carbon black after heat treatment is less than 1.5%, and the rate of change of lateral size is less than 20%. The oxidizing gas is a gas containing oxygen. The ratio of the functional group containing oxygen in the surface of the carbon black increases through the oxidation reforming. The functional group containing oxygen includes aldehyde, ketone, carboxy and/or hydroxy group. Preferred Method: The heat treatment is carried out for 5-30 minutes at 800-1000 degrees C such that the reduction rate of the particle size of the carbon black is less than 5% and the rate of change of oil absorption number of carbon black is less than 10%.

   ADVANTAGE - The refined peony seed oil: has inherent odor of peony seed oil, no peculiar smell, no solvent residue, low acid value and excellent stability.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The extraction time is 30-120 minutes. The extracting exhibits supercritical carbon dioxide bringing free fatty acids, and the extraction further includes separating the supercritical carbon dioxide carrying the free fatty acid, the separation temperature is 30-60 degrees C, the separation pressure is 5-20 MPa. The deacidification purification comprises carrying out deacidification purification in a supercritical carbon dioxide extraction apparatus, and introducing carbon dioxide gas into the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction apparatus to generate supercritical carbon dioxide, and the introducing speed carbon dioxide gas is 2-20 l/hour. The method comprises continuously introducing peony seed crude oil into the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device for extraction, after the supercritical carbon dioxide is produced, where the peony seed crude oil has a feed rate of 2-15 ml/minute. The peony seed crude oil is obtained by peony seed under low temperature pressing. The method further comprises degassing the extracted peony seed oil after the extracting. Preferred Components: The purity of the carbon dioxide gas is greater than or equal to 99.99%.

TI Preparation of nano graphene involves mixing graphite powder with water, refining to obtain slurry, extracting slurry with water or liquid carbon dioxide as extractant, filtering to obtain filtrate, and drying.
AU ZENG J
   CENG J
AE ZENG J (ZENG-Individual)
   CENG J (CENG-Individual)
GA 201870472M
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of nano graphene involves mixing graphite powder with water, refining to obtain a slurry, extracting the slurry with water or liquid carbon dioxide as an extractant, filtering to obtain a filtrate, and drying.
   USE - Preparation of nano graphene (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is easy, economical and suitable for large-scale industrial production, and rapidly produces high quality nano graphene material with high yield and less energy consumption.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: Before extracting, the method involves adding the mixture comprising extractant and slurry, with surface modifier, and solvent comprising polar solvent or non-polar solvent for dissolving the surface modifier. The extraction is one or more of supercritical extraction, microwave extraction, ultrasonic extraction, ultra-high pressure extraction, and nanoabrasive extraction. Preferred Conditions: The supercritical extraction is carried out at a pressure of 25-80 MPa, power of 5-25 kW, temperature of 0-40 degrees C (excluding 0 degrees C) and flow rate of 1000-2000 L/hour, or pressure of 30-76 MPa, power of 9-20 kW, temperature of 0-30 degrees C (excluding 0 degrees C) and flow rate of 1100-1800 L/hour, or pressure of 37-55 MPa, power of 13-19 kW, temperature of 10-26 degrees C (excluding 10 degrees C) and flow rate of 1200-1700 L/hour, or pressure of 41-49 MPa, power of 15-20 kW, temperature of 12-17 degrees C and flow rate of 1400-1500 L/hour. The microwave extraction is carried out at a frequency of 300-915 MHz, power of 5-25 kW, and temperature of 0-40 degrees C (excluding 0 degrees C), or frequency of 370-900 MHz, power of 5-20 kW and temperature of 10-30 degrees C (excluding 10 degrees C), or frequency of 426-875 MHz, power of 8-17 kW and temperature of 7-23 degrees C (excluding 7 degrees C), or frequency of 300-500 MHz, power of 5-25 kW and temperature of 9-18 degrees C (excluding 9 degrees C). The ultrasonic extraction is carried out at a frequency of 20-50 kHz, power of 5-25 kW and temperature of 0-40 degrees C (excluding 0 degrees C), or frequency of 20-40 kHz, power of 5-20 kW and temperature of 9-36 degrees C (excluding 9 degrees C), or frequency of 25-42 kHz, power of 9-10 kW and temperature of 15-30 degrees C (excluding 15 degrees C), or frequency of 30-43 kHz, power of 15-22 kW and temperature of 7-20 degrees C (excluding 7 degrees C). The ultra-high pressure extraction is carried out at a pressure of 250-380 MPa, power of 5-25 kW, temperature of 0-40 degrees C (excluding 0 degrees C) and flow rate of 1000-2000 L/hour, or pressure of 250-350 MPa, power of 5-20 kW, temperature of 11-40 degrees C (excluding 11 degrees C) and flow rate of 1200-1800 L/hour, or pressure of 270-300 MPa, power of 10-23 kW power, temperature of 15-30 degrees C (excluding 15 degrees C) and flow rate of 1300-1600 L/hour, or pressure of 300-380 MPa, power of 5-13 kW, temperature of 20-28 degrees C (excluding 20 degrees C) and flow rate of 1000-1500 L/hour. The nano-abrasive extraction is carried out at a pressure of 50-80 MPa, power of 5-25 kW, temperature of 0-40 degrees C (excluding 0 degrees C) and flow rate of 1000-5000 L/hour, or pressure of 50-70 MPa pressure, power of 16-25 kW, temperature of 16-30 degrees C (excluding 16 degrees C) and flow rate of 1700-4000 L/hour, or pressure of 70-80 MPa, power of 5-15 kW, temperature of 10-29 degrees C (excluding 10 degrees C) and flow rate of 1000-3000 L/hour, or pressure of 50-77 MPa, power of 8-20 kW, temperature of 9-19 degrees C (excluding 9 degrees C) and flow rate of 2000-3000 L/hour. Preferred Components: The surface modifier comprises silane coupling agent, titanate coupling agent, aluminate coupling agent, and/or stearic acid. The surface modifier for modifying graphene, comprises sulfonating agent, carboxylating agent, grafting agent, acetylation agent, and/or silylation agent. The filtering is carried out using filter membrane comprising ultrafiltration membrane and/or nanofiltration membrane, having a pore diameter of 1-100 nm. Preferred Properties: The graphite powder after refining, has a particle diameter of 5-30 mu m, 9-27 mu m, 12-20 mu m, or 16-19 mu m.

PN CN108892745-A; CN108892745-B
TI New polyvinyl acetate segment-containing synthetic            phospholipid used for producing liposome.
AU JIAO Z
   SONG J
   CHENG J
   HAN S
   WANG W
   ZHA X
AE UNIV SOUTHEAST (UYSE-C)
GA 201897168X
AB    NOVELTY - A polyvinyl acetate segment-containing                synthetic phospholipid (I), is new.
   USE - New polyvinyl acetate segment-containing                synthetic phospholipid is used for producing                liposome (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The polyvinyl acetate segment-containing                synthetic phospholipid (I) has super-supercritical                carbon dioxide property. The synthetic phospholipid                (I) is prepared by a simple method using                supercritical carbon dioxide under mild reaction                conditions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A polyvinyl acetate segment-containing                synthetic phospholipid of formula (I), is                new.
   PVAc = polyvinyl acetate segment;
   R1 = OH or O-;and
   R2 = hydrophilic structure.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                preparation of synthetic phospholipid (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The chain transfer                  catalyst is a carboxy-terminated dithioester                  compound. The free radical initiator is an azo                  initiator or a peroxide initiator. The molar                  ratio of the vinyl acetate monomer, chain                  transfer catalyst and radical initiator is                  1:(0.005-0.3):(0.0005-0.03). The reaction solvent                  is an alcohol solvent, an ether solvent, a ketone                  solvent, an ester solvent and/or an aromatic                  hydrocarbon solvent in any ratio. The base is an                  organic amine base or a pyridine base. The                  condensing agent is a carbodiimide-type                  condensing agent. The molar ratio of carboxyl                  group-polymerizable segment of carboxyl group,                  phosphatidyl group, base and condensing agent is                  1:(0.5-1.0):(0.5-2.0):(0.5-2.0). The                  precipitating agent is an organic ether.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preparation (claimed): The synthetic                  phospholipid (I) is obtained by (i) reacting                  vinyl acetate, a chain transfer catalyst and a                  free radical initiator to obtain a vinyl acetate                  polymerized segment of a terminal carboxyl group,                  and (ii) condensing the vinyl acetate polymerized                  segment of a terminal carboxyl group with a                  phosphatidyl group to obtain a polyvinyl                  acetate-modified phospholipid as synthetic                  phospholipid (I). Preferred Process: The step (i)                  is carried out at a reaction temperature of                  40-100 degrees C for a reaction time of 2-12                  hours. The step (ii) is carried out in a reaction                  system comprising a base, a condensing agent and                  a reaction solvent chosen from dichloromethane,                  chloroform, diethylformamide, tetrahydrofuran and                  N,N-dimethylformamide at a reaction temperature                  of 5-50 degrees C for a reaction time of 1-48                  hours. The method involves separating and                  purifying polyvinyl acetate modified phospholipid                  by mixing the reaction product obtained from step                  (ii) with acetone, methanol and/or diethyl ether,                  drying the obtained mixture to obtain a crude                  product, dissolving the crude product in a                  mixture of dichloromethane or chloroform,                  removing the insoluble material by filtration,                  adding a precipitating agent to the filtrate                  under stirring, filtering the precipitate, and                  drying obtain a final product. Preferred                  Composition: The polyvinyl acetate is a linear or                  branched structure having a number average                  molecular weight of 300-10000. The synthetic                  phospholipid (I) is preferably chosen from                  compounds of formulae (1a)-(1c).
   Preferred Definitions:
   hydrophilic structure = group chosen from                groups of formulae (i)-(iv);and
   n = 5-300.

PT P
PN CN108069827-A; CN108069827-B
TI Preparing 1,4-butynediol comprises treating copper-bismuth composite oxides with carbon dioxide, reacting, adding acetylene and adding aqueous formaldehyde solution.
AU ZHANG Y
   QIAO K
   DUAN R
   BAO H
   FU Q
   WANG L
   HUO W
   ZHANG B
AE SINOPEC FUSHUN RES INST PETROLEUM & PETR (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC FUSHUN PETROLEUM & PETROCHEM (SNPC-C)
GA 2018438847
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing 1,4-butynediol comprises treating copper-bismuth composite oxides with carbon dioxide as catalyst, reacting at 80-150 degrees C, at 0.2-2 MPa, adding acetylene at 40-200 ml/minute and adding 4-25% aqueous formaldehyde solution, where the mass ratio of the catalyst to the aqueous formaldehyde solution is 1:3-1:10.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing 1,4-butynediol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has high yield.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The reaction is carried out at 90-120 degrees C, at 0.5-1 MPa, the flow of acetylene is 80-150 ml/minute, the aqueous formaldehyde solution concentration is 9-15%, the amount of catalyst used same as the mass ratio of aqueous formaldehyde solution is 1:5-1:7. The carbon dioxide treatment is divided into two stages, the first stage is processed at 10-45 degrees C for 1-4 hours, the flow rate of carbon dioxide is 1-8 ml/minute, the second stage is purged with nitrogen and the flow rate of nitrogen is 1-8 ml/ minute, at 0-30 degrees C for 1-5 hours. The preparation of the copper- bismuth composite oxide catalyst comprises (i) preparing acidic solution containing copper salts and bismuth salts; (ii) preparing precipitant solution; (iii) adding bottom water into a reaction vessel and heating; (iv) adding the acidic solution of step (i) and the precipitant solution of step (ii) into the reaction vessel, introducing carbon dioxide gas from the bottom of the reactor and reacting; (v) stopping the reaction when the remaining acidic solution is 4:5-9:10 and aging; (vi) aging, passing gas carbon dioxide from the bottom of the reactor, adding remaining acidic solution and adding deionized water into the reaction system and adding 0.5-1.5 times of acidic solution and alkaline solution; (vii) increasing the flow of water into the reaction system and making the flow rate is 2-4 times the total flow of the acidic solution and alkaline solution; (viii) curing, washing and filtering; and (ix) adding deionized water containing potassium nitrate and sodium hexametaphosphate into the filter cake, stirring uniformly and spray drying. In the step (iii), the bottom water is added into the reactor, and the amount is 0.15-0.4 times the volume of the acid solution and the temperature is increased to the reaction temperature of 30-80 degrees C. In the step (iv), the acidic solution and alkaline solution are added in parallel to the reaction vessel, the pH of the reaction is 5-8, the reaction temperature is 30-80 degrees C, the bottom of the reactor is fed with carbon dioxide gas, and carbon dioxide is diluted with nitrogen, the volume concentration of carbon dioxide is 20-60%, and the flow rate is 150-1000 ml/minute. In the step (v), the aging is carried out at 30-80 degrees C for 10 to 70 minutes and the aging pH is 5-8. In the step (vi), the pH of the reaction is 5-8, and the reaction temperature is 30-80 degrees C and the volume of carbon dioxide introduced into the reactor is 20-60%, and the flow rate is 150-1000 ml/minute. In the step (viii), the curing is carried out for 0.5-4.0 hours. In the step (ix), the deionized water containing potassium nitrate and sodium hexametaphosphate is added into the filter cake and the mixture is stirred and spray drying is performed to obtain copper ruthenium catalyst. Preferred Components: The aqueous formaldehyde solution contains 0.5-3.5% diethylene glycol. The content of copper oxide is 30-80 wt.% and the content of bismuth oxide is 1-10 wt.% according to the copper-bismuth composite oxide catalyst. In the step (i), the copper salt includes copper sulfate, copper nitrate, copper acetate or copper chloride, the molar concentration of copper salt in acidic solution is 0.6-3 mol/l, the bismuth salt includes strontium nitrate, strontium sulfate, or strontium acetate, the molar concentration of strontium salt in the acidic solution is 0.01-0.05 mol/l, and the pH value of the acidic solution is 0-2. In the step (ii), the precipitant includes sodium carbonate, sodium hydroxide, potassium carbonate, potassium hydroxide, ammonia, and sodium bicarbonate and the molar concentration of the precipitant is 0.1-3 mol/l. The amount of potassium nitrate added is 1-20% of the mass of water in the slurry, the dry basis of the slurry is 15-45% and the sodium hexametaphosphate is added in an amount of 5-25% by weight of the catalyst. The catalyst has copper oxide content of 3-80 wt.% based on the weight of the catalyst, the content of cerium oxide is 1-10 wt.% and the particle size of the catalyst is 93% above 10-30 mu m.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN CN113832495-A
TI Preparing multi-level structure composite fuel            electrode used for solid oxide electrolytic cell,            involves preparing fuel electrode support using fuel            electrode powder and binder, phase converting,            calcining, preparing electrolyte membrane on surface of            electrode support, reducing, and acid-treating.
AU TU B
   QI H
AE UNIV SHANDONG SCI & TECHNOLOGY (SDST-C)
GA 202208404F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing multi-level structure composite fuel                electrode involves: (a) designing and synthesizing                a B-site excess doped basic perovskite oxide (I),                which is used as a fuel electrode powder; (b)                mixing the fuel electrode powder with an organic                solvent, a binder and a dispersant to form a                slurry, preparing a fuel electrode support by                casting method and using the slurry, placing in a                curing agent for phase conversion, calcining at                950-1050degrees Celsius for 2-5 hours, and then                preparing an electrolyte membrane and a porous                oxygen electrode on the surface of the fuel                electrode support; (c) reducing one side of the                fuel electrode treated in step (b); and (d)                acid-treating one side of the fuel electrode                treated in step (c).
   USE - The method is useful for preparing multi-level                structure composite fuel electrode used for solid                oxide electrolysis cell for high temperature                electrolysis of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can be carried out industrially on                a large scale, and provides multi-level structure                composite fuel electrode, which has high diffusion,                adsorption and dissociation performance of carbon                dioxide and carbon monoxide, high internal                transport activity of ions and other charged                species, and improved electrocatalytic carbon                dioxide reduction activity and carbon deposition                resistance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing multi-level structure composite fuel                electrode involves: (a) designing and synthesizing                a B-site excess doped basic perovskite oxide of                formula: A'B'xB''yO3-delta, which is used as a fuel                electrode powder, where x is 0.60-0.40, y is                0.45-0.65, A' is magnesium, calcium, strontium, or                barium, B' is nickel, tin and/or copper, and B'' is                manganese, iron, cobalt, zinc, titanium, yttrium,                zirconium, niobium and/or molybdenum; (b) mixing                the fuel electrode powder with an organic solvent,                a binder and a dispersant to form a slurry,                preparing a fuel electrode support by casting                method and using the slurry, placing in a curing                agent for phase conversion, calcining at                950-1050degrees Celsius for 2-5 hours, and then                preparing an electrolyte membrane and a porous                oxygen electrode on the surface of the fuel                electrode support; (c) reducing one side of the                fuel electrode treated in step (b); and (d)                acid-treating one side of the fuel electrode                treated in step (c).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (a) involves                  mixing a salt solution (A), a salt solution (B')                  and a salt solution (B'') with at least one of                  glycine solution, citric acid solution, ammonium                  citrate solution and EDTA, heating and stirring                  until gelatinous, then igniting to obtain powder,                  treating at high temperature of 1000-1200degrees                  Celsius for 2-5 hours to obtain fuel electrode                  powder. The acid treatment is carried out by                  using a mixture of 1-40 vol.% carbon dioxide and                  nitrogen.Preferred Components: The electrolyte                  membrane is yttria-stabilized zirconia, scandium                  oxide-doped zirconia, or lanthanum strontium                  magnesium gallium oxide. The porous oxygen                  electrode is zirconium-doped barium cobalt                  ferrite, zirconium-doped                  barium-strontium-cobalt-iron, or yttrium-doped                  barium-strontium-cobalt-iron.Preferred Property: The thickness of the                  fuel electrode support obtained after calcination                  is 550-800 mm.Preferred Composition: The fuel electrode                  slurry comprises 69.13-77.67 pts. wt. fuel                  electrode powder, 18.9-25.8 pts. wt. organic                  solvent, 2.78-4.02 pts. wt. binder, and 0.65-1.05                  pts. wt. dispersant.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic solvent is                  1-methyl-2-pyrrolidone. The curing agent is                  water, or a mixed solution of ethanol and water.                  When the mixed solution is selected, the volume                  fraction of ethanol is not greater than 20                  vol.%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The binder is                  poly(oxy-1,4-phenylenesulfonyl-1,4-phenylene).                  The dispersant is polyvinylpyrrolidone.

PT P
PN WO2021228877-A1; EP3942012-A1
TI Device for preparing and dispensing reconstituted beer, comprises first vessel containing liquid concentrate for preparing beer, second vessel containing alcoholic liquid, source of water, and source of pressurized carbon dioxide.
AU BROUWER E R
   BEKKERS A C
   BAKKERS A C
AE HEINEKEN SUPPLY CHAIN BV (HETB-C)
   HEINEKEN SUPPLY CHAIN BV (HETB-C)
GA 2021D0716V
AB    NOVELTY - The device (10) comprises a first vessel that contains a liquid concentrate for preparing beer, second vessel contains an alcoholic liquid, a reconstitution unit (100) and dispensing unit (50). The reconstituted beer comprising water from the source of water, carbon dioxide from the source of pressurized carbon dioxide, liquid concentrate from the first vessel and alcoholic liquid from the second vessel, in reconstitution unit and to dispense the reconstituted beer from the dispensing unit. The liquid concentrate for preparing beer has an ethanol content of 0-1% ABV. The alcoholic liquid contains 12-100 wt.% ethanol and 0-88 wt.% water, ethanol and water together constituting 80-100 wt.% of the alcoholic liquid. The alcoholic liquid contains 50-2,000 mg/L of hop acids selected from iso-alpha acids, hydrogenated iso-alpha acids, and hulupones. The liquid concentrate has a free amino nitrogen (FAN) content, 250-3,000 pg/L of riboflavin and 10-100 g/L of maltotetraose.
   USE - Device for preparing and dispensing reconstituted beer, for e.g. carbonated beverages using domestic appliance such as Sodastream (RTM: appliances produce carbonated beverages by carbonating water and mixing the carbonated water with a flavored syrup).
   ADVANTAGE - The device produces a clear, non-hazy reconstituted beer in no more than a few seconds. The use of an essentially alcohol-free concentrate offers the advantage that the stability of the concentrate is substantially improved as reactions between ethanol and components e.g. acids are avoided. In addition, precipitation of components that are poorly soluble in ethanol/water mixtures e.g. proteins is prevented. Due to the virtual absence of ethanol, it has a relatively high surface tension, which reduces unwanted foaming during the filling of the beer concentrate into containers or reservoirs. Precipitation of poorly water-soluble hop acids is prevented by dissolving these hop acids in the alcoholic liquid. The alcoholic liquid can suitably be combined with the liquid concentrate to produce a high quality reconstituted beer having the typical hop derived.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method of preparing and dispensing reconstituted beer.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the device for preparing reconstituted beer in which liquid concentrate and alcoholic liquid are provided by single serve capsule.
   Device for preparing reconstituted beer (10)
   Housing (11)
   Power supply (20)
   Dispensing unit (50)
   Reconstitution unit (100)

PN CN113082945-A
TI Preparation of ionic solvent phase change absorption system for capturing carbon dioxide, involves mixing organic amine solution and amino acid, adding deionized water until solution becomes clear, heating, and removing unreacted reactants.
AU ZHOU C
   YU G
   CHEN X
AE UNIV BEIJING CHEM TECHNOLOGY (UYBC-C)
GA 202179925P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of an ionic solvent phase change absorption system involves mixing equimolar amount of organic amine solution and amino acid, adding deionized water in the mixed solution until the solution becomes clear, introducing the clear solution into a three-necked flask, placing the flask in a constant temperature stirring water bath, opening condensing water pipe and starting an electromagnetic stirring device, introducing nitrogen atmosphere, heating the mixture at 25 degrees C, magnetically stirring at 300 rpm, reacting for 8 hours to ensure complete ion exchange, obtaining an ionic liquid, using vacuum rotary evaporator at 70 degrees C for rotary evaporation to remove unreacted reactants and partial water, placing the ionic liquid after rotary evaporation in a vacuum drying box for drying at 70 degrees C for 24 hours to obtain a target ionic liquid triethylene tetramine (TETA) glycine, adding a small amount of deionized water to dissolve the target ionic liquid in tertiary amine solution.
   USE - Preparation of ionic solvent phase change absorption system for capturing carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and efficient. The absorption system has excellent absorption performance at high temperature and reduced regeneration energy consumption, and replaces existing alcohol amine solution to capture carbon dioxide in a certain degree.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of an ionic solvent phase change absorption system involves mixing equimolar amount of organic amine solution and amino acid, adding deionized water in the mixed solution until the solution becomes clear, introducing the clear solution into a three-necked flask, placing the flask in a constant temperature stirring water bath, opening condensing water pipe and starting an electromagnetic stirring device, introducing nitrogen atmosphere, heating the mixture at 25 degrees C, magnetically stirring at 300 rpm, reacting for 8 hours to ensure complete ion exchange, obtaining an ionic liquid, using vacuum rotary evaporator at 70 degrees C for rotary evaporation to remove unreacted reactants and partial water, placing the ionic liquid after rotary evaporation in a vacuum drying box for drying at 70 degrees C for 24 hours to obtain a target ionic liquid triethylene tetramine (TETA) glycine, adding a small amount of deionized water to dissolve the target ionic liquid in tertiary amine solution, and forming ionic solvent phase change absorption system. The absorption system ensures high absorption capacity and absorption rate, and reduces regeneration energy consumption.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The nitrogen atmosphere contains nitrogen or inert gas such as argon.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic amine solution contains propylene diamine, diethylene triamine, triethylene tetramine, tetraethylene pentamine, pentaethylene hexamine and other organic amine solution containing more amino groups. The amino acid contains lysine, glycine, arginine, serine, alanine and other amino acids whose properties do not change before and after the absorption reaction.

PN CN112852078-A; CN112852078-B
TI Preparing polyvinylidene fluoride-based piezoelectric foam part based on selective laser sintering, comprises e.g. preparing polyvinylidene fluoride powder or polyvinylidene fluoride/inorganic filler mixed powder.
AU CHEN N
   YANG C
   WANG Q
   LIU P
   ZHANG C
   HUA Z
AE UNIV SICHUAN (USCU-C)
GA 2021617612
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing polyvinylidene fluoride-based piezoelectric foam part based on selective laser sintering, comprises (1) preparing polyvinylidene fluoride powder or polyvinylidene fluoride/inorganic filler mixed powder suitable for selective laser sintering; (2) uniformly mixing prepared polyvinylidene fluoride powder or polyvinylidene fluoride/inorganic filler mixed powder obtained in the step (1) and flow auxiliary agent, and sintering parts by selective laser to obtain the laser sintered part; (3) preparing the laser sintered parts obtained in step (2) by supercritical carbon dioxide autoclave foaming process to obtain polyvinylidene fluoride-based foam part; and (4) polarizing polyvinylidene fluoride-based foam part obtained in step (3) at temperature of 80-100 degrees C to obtain polyvinylidene fluoride-based piezoelectric foam part.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing polyvinylidene fluoride-based piezoelectric foam part based on selective laser sintering.
   ADVANTAGE - The foam part: has compressive modulus of 1.44 MPa and maximum piezoelectric output of 20.9 V when inorganic fillers are added.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing polyvinylidene fluoride-based piezoelectric foam part based on selective laser sintering, comprises (1) preparing polyvinylidene fluoride powder or polyvinylidene fluoride/inorganic filler mixed powder suitable for selective laser sintering; (2) uniformly mixing 100 pts. wt. prepared polyvinylidene fluoride powder or polyvinylidene fluoride/inorganic filler mixed powder obtained in the step (1) and 0.1-0.5 pts. wt. flow auxiliary agent, and sintering parts by selective laser to obtain the laser sintered part; where the process parameters of selective laser sintering includes preheating temperature is 150-160 degrees C, laser scanning speed is 8000-9600 mm/second, laser scanning power is 10-40 W, laser scanning times is 1-2 times, laser scanning distance is 0.1-0.3 mm, and powder layer thickness is 0.1-0.3 mm; (3) preparing the laser sintered parts obtained in step (2) by supercritical carbon dioxide autoclave foaming process to obtain polyvinylidene fluoride-based foam part; where the process parameters of the supercritical carbon dioxide autoclave foaming process includes the foaming process pressure is 7-16 MPa, the foaming temperature is 150-170 degrees C, and pressure maintaining time is 30-60 minutes; and (4) polarizing polyvinylidene fluoride-based foam part obtained in step (3) at temperature of 80-100 degrees C to obtain polyvinylidene fluoride-based piezoelectric foam part. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for the polyvinylidene fluoride-based piezoelectric foam parts is prepared by above mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (1), the inorganic filler comprises barium titanate, PZT, zinc oxide, and/or boron nitride. In the step (1), the inorganic filler is barium titanate, and addition amount of the inorganic filler is 3-20 wt.% of the total amount. In the step (2), the flow auxiliary agent is nano silicon dioxide or talc. In the step (2), the thickness of the solid structure of the laser sintered part is controlled to be 2-2.5 mm. Preferred Method: In the step (3), the foaming process pressure is 10-16 MPa, and the foaming temperature is 160-170 degrees C. In the step (3), process parameters of supercritical carbon dioxide autoclave foaming process includes pressure during the foaming process is 7-16 MPa, preferably 10-16MPa, foaming temperature is 150-170 degrees C, preferably 160-167 degrees C, the pressure-maintaining time is 30-60 minutes, preferably 30-45 minutes, and pressure-maintaining temperature is 35-45 degrees C, preferably 35-45 degrees C.

PT P
PN CN112028101-A
TI Preparing dispersible calcium carbonate comprises e. g. adding calcium oxide into water to carry out dry digestion reaction, classifying airflow to remove slag to obtain calcium hydroxide powder.
AU CAI M
   XUAN S
   FANG G
   WU Q
AE JIANDE HUAMING TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020C43039
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing dispersible calcium carbonate comprises (i) adding calcium oxide into water to carry out dry digestion reaction, and classifying airflow to remove slag to obtain calcium hydroxide powder; (ii) performing low temperature pulping and impurity removal, adding metered calcium hydroxide powder into cold water to prepare certain concentration of calcium hydroxide green slurry, sieving to remove impurities, and obtaining refined low-temperature calcium hydroxide slurry; (iii) adding the refined low-temperature calcium hydroxide slurry in the carbonization reactor, passing purified kiln gas or carbon dioxide-containing gas for low-temperature carbonization reaction, completing the carbonization reaction, and obtaining calcium carbonate slurry containing calcium carbonate seeds with a smaller particle size; (iv) performing high temperature pulping and impurity removal by adding metered calcium hydroxide powder into hot water, and preparing into calcium hydroxide raw slurry.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing dispersible calcium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method does not affected by climate, and has controllable granularity, good dispersion and is suitable for multi-variety production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing dispersible calcium carbonate comprises (i) adding calcium oxide into water to carry out dry digestion reaction, and classifying airflow to remove slag to obtain calcium hydroxide powder; (ii) performing low temperature pulping and impurity removal, adding metered calcium hydroxide powder into cold water to prepare certain concentration of calcium hydroxide green slurry, sieving to remove impurities, and obtaining refined low-temperature calcium hydroxide slurry; (iii) adding the refined low-temperature calcium hydroxide slurry in the carbonization reactor, passing purified kiln gas or carbon dioxide-containing gas for low-temperature carbonization reaction, completing the carbonization reaction, and obtaining calcium carbonate slurry containing calcium carbonate seeds with a smaller particle size; (iv) performing high temperature pulping and impurity removal by adding metered calcium hydroxide powder into hot water, preparing into certain concentration of calcium hydroxide raw slurry, sieving to remove impurities to obtain refined high-temperature calcium hydroxide slurry; (v) adding the refined high-temperature calcium hydroxide slurry into the reaction kettle of the calcium carbonate slurry containing calcium carbonate seed crystals obtained in step (iii), passing purified kiln gas or carbon dioxide-containing gas for high-temperature carbonization reaction, completing the carbonization reaction, and obtaining precipitated calcium carbonate slurry.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the mass ratio of the calcium oxide to water is 1:0.5-0.8. In the step (ii), the mass of the calcium hydroxide powder accounts for 10-35% of the total calcium hydroxide powder, the concentration of the refined low-temperature calcium hydroxide slurry is 0.6-2.3 mol/l. In the steps (iii) and (v), the volume concentration of carbon dioxide in the kiln gas or carbon dioxide-containing gas is 20-99%, and the aeration rate is 10-30 m3/(m3/hour). In the step (iv), the concentration of the refined high-temperature calcium hydroxide slurry is 0.6-2.3 mol/l. The specific surface area of the dispersible calcium carbonate is 5.5-9 m2/g, and oil absorption value 37-50 g DOP/100g. Preferred Method: In the step (i), the temperature of the water is 17-80 degrees C. In the step (ii), the temperature of the cold water is 14-30 degrees C. In the step (iv), the temperature of the hot water is 30-60 degrees C.

PT P
PN CN111394745-A; WO2021190402-A1
TI Lithium hydroxide preparation involves adjusting            pH value of lithium-containing low-magnesium brine to            precipitate calcium and magnesium by using inorganic            bases to obtain lithium-containing brine for removing            calcium and magnesium.
AU LIU L
   LIN J
   ZHANG R
AE EDING SHANGHAI INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY CO (EDIN-Non-standard)
   YIDING SHANGHAI INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY (YIDI-Non-standard)
GA 202066674A
AB    NOVELTY - Lithium hydroxide preparation involves                adjusting pH value of lithium-containing                low-magnesium brine to 10 to precipitate calcium                and magnesium by using inorganic bases to obtain                lithium-containing brine for removing calcium and                magnesium. An organic extractant is used to extract                the lithium in the lithium-containing brine from                which calcium and magnesium are removed to obtain a                raffinate containing sodium and potassium and a                lithium-containing organic phase. The phase is                stripped to obtain a lithium salt solution. The                inorganic acid solution is a mixture of carbon                dioxide and water, hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid                or nitric acid. The lithium salt solution is                purified by performing electrolysis to obtain a                lithium hydroxide solution. The inorganic acid                solution is sulfuric acid or nitric acid, where the                electrolysis is bipolar membrane                electrodialysis.
   USE - Method for preparing lithium hydroxide from                lithium-containing low-magnesium brine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares the lithium hydroxide from                lithium-containing low-magnesium brine in a simple,                cost-effective and eco-friendly manner with high                lithium hydroxide product yield, high purity, high                material recycling rate, and can continuously                prepare lithium hydroxide, which is particularly                suitable for remote locations, high raw material                transportation costs, photovoltaic power generation                and storage lithium.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Lithium hydroxide preparation involves                adjusting pH value of lithium-containing                low-magnesium brine to 10 to precipitate calcium                and magnesium by using inorganic bases to obtain                lithium-containing brine for removing calcium and                magnesium. An organic extractant is used to extract                the lithium in the lithium-containing brine from                which calcium and magnesium are removed to obtain a                raffinate containing sodium and potassium and a                lithium-containing organic phase. The phase is                stripped to obtain a lithium salt solution. The                inorganic acid solution is a mixture of carbon                dioxide and water, hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid                or nitric acid. The lithium salt solution is                purified by performing electrolysis to obtain a                lithium hydroxide solution. The inorganic acid                solution is sulfuric acid or nitric acid, where the                electrolysis is bipolar membrane electrodialysis.                The inorganic acid solution is a mixture of carbon                dioxide and water or hydrochloric acid. The                electrolysis is bipolar membrane electrodialysis or                ion membrane electrolysis. The lithium hydroxide                solution is concentrated and crystallized in                sequence to obtain lithium hydroxide and lithium                hydroxide lithium precipitation mother                liquor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The lithium-containing                  low-magnesium brine comprises lithium-containing                  low-magnesium salt lake brine, a                  lithium-containing low-magnesium desorption                  solution prepared after removing impurities from                  salt lake brine by adsorption and concentrating                  lithium, lithium-containing low-magnesium                  concentrate obtained by reverse osmosis of                  lithium-containing low-magnesium desorption                  solution, lithium-containing low-magnesium                  electrodialysis concentrate obtained by                  monovalent ion selective electrodialysis                  treatment of salt lake brine, and salt lake brine                  obtained by nanofiltration magnesium removal                  treatment of lithium-containing low-magnesium                  nanofiltration solution, high-magnesium brine and                  sodium carbonate or sodium bicarbonate to remove                  magnesium, lithium-containing low-magnesium                  brine, underground brine, lithium precipitation                  mother liquor, lithium battery waste leachate and                  lithium ore leachate. The inorganic base                  comprises lithium hydroxide, sodium hydroxide,                  sodium carbonate, calcium hydroxide, potassium                  hydroxide and ammonia. Preferred Conditions: The                  purification comprises successively performing                  plate and frame filtration, multi-media                  filtration, ultrafiltration and resin adsorption                  on the lithium salt solution. The electrolysis is                  ion-exchange membrane electrolysis, where the                  ion-exchange membrane electrolysis is carried out                  in an ion-exchange membrane electrolyzer. The                  electrolytic cell includes an anode chamber, a                  cation exchange membrane and a cathode chamber                  arranged in sequence. The lithium hydroxide                  precipitating mother liquor is reused to adjust                  the pH of the lithium-containing low-magnesium                  brine.

PN CN110339790-A
TI Spherical amino-modified silica aerogel used for            adsorption of carbon dioxide gas, comprises silicon            oxide as main network structure, and amino group on            surface, where aerogel has specified average particle            diameter and bulk density.
AU KONG Y
   JIANG X
   ZHAO Z
   ZHANG J
   SHEN X
AE UNIV NANJING TECHNOLOGY (UNTY-C)
GA 2019886570
AB    NOVELTY - Spherical amino-modified silica aerogel                comprises silicon oxide as a main network                structure, and an amino group on the surface, where                the aerogel has a spherical shape particles having                an average particle diameter of 1-5 mm, a bulk                density of 0.1-0.4 g/cm3, an apparent density of                0.08-0.25 g/cm3, a specific surface area of 10-450                m2/g and a formaldehyde adsorption rate of                2.01-3.66 mmol/g when the formaldehyde                concentration is 10 parts per million (ppm).
   USE - The aerogel is useful for selective adsorption                of gases included carbon dioxide and                formaldehyde.
   ADVANTAGE - The aerogel is prepared by simple method and                suitable for large-scale production, and has carbon                dioxide adsorption rate of 5.56 mmol/g and                formaldehyde adsorption rate of 3.66 mmol/g.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing the spherical amino-modified silica                aerogel involving (a) mixing and stirring soluble                glass and deionized water in volume ratio of                1:(5.3-5.8) at 10-35 degrees C for 1-5 minutes,                adding acid to adjust the pH value and stirring for                1-20 minutes to obtain a sol, (b) dripping the sol                into an oil at 50-80 degrees C to carry out a gel                reaction to obtain a spherical silica wet gel, (c)                washing the spherical silica wet gel with deionized                water and aging in an organic solvent, (d)                preparing an amino-modified liquid by mixing silane                coupling agent and ethanol at 25-35 degrees C,                stirring in a water bath at 45-60 degrees C for                0.5-1 hour, cooling the solution, adding deionized                water, stirring for 0.5-1 hour to obtain an amino                modification liquid, where the molar ratio of the                silane coupling agent, deionized water and ethanol                in the amino modification liquid is                1:(3-5):(12-24), (e) subjecting the spherical                silica wet gel to surface modification in an amino                modification liquid at 20-70 degrees C for 1-9 days                to obtain a spherical wet coagulation gel, and (f)                subjecting the spherical amino-modified silica wet                gel to a solvent-substituted solution and drying to                obtain the product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The modulus of sodium                  silicate in the soluble glass is 3-3.3 and a mass                  fraction of 30-40%. Preferred Method: The step                  (a) involves adding acid to adjust to a pH of 5-6                  or 9-10. The step (b) involves washing the                  spherical silica wet gel with deionized water at                  30-80 degrees C for 2-6 hours and aging in the                  organic solvent at 40-60 degrees C for 1-3 days.                  The step of drying is carried out using                  atmospheric drying, vacuum drying, freeze drying                  or supercritical drying.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The oil comprises a                  hydroxy silicone oil, a vegetable oil or a                  dimethyl silicone oil. The organic solvent                  comprises methanol, ethanol, propylene glycol or                  n-hexane. The silane coupling agent comprises                  3-aminopropyltriethoxysilane,                  3-aminopropyltrimethoxysilane or                  4-aminobutyltriethoxysilane. The                  solvent-substituted solution comprises water,                  methanol or ethanol.

PN CN109797136-A
TI Separating and culturing method of human fat            mesenchymal stem cells involves collecting fat,            removing impurities in fat, digesting by mixed enzyme,            collecting, centrifuging, subculturing, digesting and            freezing.
AU TAN K
   YAO Q
AE HUNAN NANHUA AMSPRING BIOTECHNOLOGY CO (HUNA-Non-standard)
GA 201950589F
AB    NOVELTY - Separating and culturing method of human fat                mesenchymal stem cells involves collecting fat,                removing impurities in fat, digesting by mixed                enzyme, collecting, centrifuging, subculturing,                digesting and freezing; collecting 2-100 ml human                fat, placing into sterile storage transport liquid,                sterilizing, cleaning, disinfecting, washing fat                tissue, dispersing fat mass and collecting fat                tissue to obtain cell precipitate; and carrying out                serum-free culturing by cracking erythrocyte lysis                buffer, centrifuging to obtain white cell                precipitate, inoculating and culturing into carbon                dioxide incubator with constant temperature and                humidity for 24-40 hours, observing 20-40% long                fusiform adherent cell to form, digesting by TrypLE                express (RTM: trypsin reagent), subculturing                obtained cells by serum-free system, reaching more                than 90% fusion of cell culture digesting by TrypLE                express (RTM: trypsin reagent) and freezing.
   USE - Method for separating and culturing human fat                mesenchymal stem cells.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has low cost, less used reagent                material, simplified operation flow, reduced                contamination risk, reduced cytotoxicity and                shortened culture period.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Separating and culturing method of human fat                mesenchymal stem cells comprises collecting fat,                removing impurities in fat, digesting by mixed                enzyme, collecting, centrifuging, subculturing,                digesting and freezing; collecting 2-100 ml human                fat, placing into sterile storage transport liquid,                sterilizing, cleaning, disinfecting, washing fat                tissue, placing into centrifugal tube, cutting                tissue with ophthalmic scissors, adding 4-10 times                physiological saline, centrifuging under 1500-2000                revolutions/minute speed for 5 minutes, sucking                upper-layer fat mass into centrifugal tube, adding                physiological saline, washing, centrifuging again,                digesting with mixed enzyme by adding obtained fat                tissue into mixed enzyme, dispersing fat mass by                beating using 2 ml pipettor, placing into carbon                dioxide incubator with constant temperature and                humidity, digesting for 10-30 minutes, and                collecting fat tissue obtained in centrifugation to                obtain cell precipitate; and carrying out                serum-free culturing by cracking erythrocyte lysis                buffer, centrifuging to obtain white cell                precipitate, re-suspending cell by serum-free                culture system, and inoculating and culturing into                carbon dioxide incubator with constant temperature                and humidity for 24-40 hours, observing 20-40% long                fusiform adherent cell to form, changing liquid,                culturing into the third or fourth day when cells                reach more than 90% fusion, digesting by TrypLE                Express (RTM: trypsin reagent), subculturing                obtained cells by serum-free system, reaching more                than 90% fusion of cell culture digesting by TrypLE                Express (RTM: trypsin reagent) and freezing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The human fat is                  abdominal fat, thigh fat or liposuction fatty                  suspension. Preferred Method: The collecting of                  human fat comprises storing fat into sterile                  storage transport liquid at 4-20 degrees C for                  12-24 hours; disinfecting and washing fat adipose                  tissue with 75% alcohol and soaking for 2-3                  seconds, adding 1-5 times physiological salt                  water, washing, placing into centrifuge tube                  using hemostatic forceps, adding 4-10 times                  physiological saline into centrifuge tube, mixing                  and centrifuging for 2-3 times; enzyme digesting                  into basic culture medium by adding 0.05-0.25%                  collagenase type-I, 0.05-0.25% collagenase                  type-IV and 5-10% TrypLE Express (RTM: trypsin                  reagent); inoculating inoculated culture into                  more than 5-25 mu g/cm2 fiber connection protein                  and spreading for 30 minutes; fat digesting for                  10-30 minutes, repeatedly blowing fat mass with 2                  ml liquid or 1 ml liquid transferring gun and                  continuously digesting; culturing for 24 hours to                  form cell adherent wall with visible oil droplets                  of upper layer of culture medium and small amount                  of impurities, changing liquid and washing with                  10 ml Dulbeccos phosphate-buffered saline and                  removing impurity; and digesting, adding                  physiological salt water to dilute after                  digestion and freezing.

PN CN109015996-A
TI Method for improving straw-woven grass tensile strength, involves removing impurities of thatch raw materials, followed by cleaning, classifying, drying, soaking in treatment liquid, introducing carbon dioxide fluid medium and treating.
AU PAN C
AE ANHUI JIAMEI CRAFTS CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 2018A53038
AB    NOVELTY - A straw-woven grass tensile strength improving method involves removing impurities of thatch raw materials, cleaning and classifying, and drying in a flowing air of temperature 45-55 degrees C and a relative humidity of 62-64% and drying until the water content is less than 30%. The dried thatch is soaked in the treatment liquid, followed by introducing a carbon dioxide fluid medium having a temperature of 140-160 degrees C in a closed container to maintain the pressure in the closed container of 6.5-8.5MPa, after 3-4 hours of treatment, restoring the atmospheric pressure and treating the resulting thatch under the conditions of a temperature of 55-65 degrees C and a pressure of 2.4-2.6 MPa for 3-4 hours.
   USE - Method for improving straw-woven grass tensile strength.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables improving the straw-woven grass tensile strength with effectively improved the performance and the knitting quality of the grass and better mildew-proof effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A straw-woven grass tensile strength improving method involves removing impurities of thatch raw materials, cleaning and classifying, and drying in a flowing air of temperature 45-55 degrees C and a relative humidity of 62-64% and drying until the water content is less than 30%. The dried thatch is soaked with the treatment liquid, followed by introducing a carbon dioxide fluid medium having a temperature of 140-160 degrees C in a closed container to maintain the pressure in the closed container of 6.5-8.5 MPa, after 3-4 hours of treatment, restoring the atmospheric pressure and treating the resulting thatch under the conditions of a temperature of 55-65 degrees C and a pressure of 2.4-2.6MPa for 3-4 hours, where the treatment liquid comprises 14-18 pts. wt. silicone resin containing silanol, 6-8 pts. wt. tetraphenylsilane, 1-3 pts. wt. 3-(2,4-dihydroxy)phenylpropionic acid, 3-6 pts. wt. N-cyclohexyl-2-benzothiazole sulfenic acid amide, 1-3 pts. wt. sodium dodecylbenzenesulfonate, 4-8 pts. wt. composite filler, 5-7 pts. wt. gamma -(2,3 propylene oxide) propyltrimethoxysilane, 4-6 pts. wt. hydroxycarboxylic acid, 12-18 pts. wt. ethanol and 40-50 pts. wt. water.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The hydroxycarboxylic acid is selected from glycolic acid, 2-hydracrylic acid, 2-hydroxy-1,2,3-hexane acid, o-hydroxyl benzoic acid and 2,3-dihydroxy-succinic acid.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); F09 (Paper-making production of cellulose, chemical treatment of wood - including chipboard and fibre-board (D21).); P63 (Working, preserving wood (B27).)

TI Production of methanol used as chemical substance raw material, involves supplying source gas comprising carbon dioxide and hydrogen to separation membrane reactor, and advancing shift reaction of source gas to methanol.

   NUMAGUCHI R
   SHIGAKI N
   WASHIMI I
   MOGI Y
   HARAOKA T
AE RES INST INNOVATIVE TECHNOLOGY EARTH (REIN-Non-standard)
   JFE STEEL CORP (JFES-C)
   RES INST INNOVATIVE TECHNOLOGY EARTH (REIN-Non-standard)
   JFE STEEL CORP (JFES-C)
GA 2018053520
AB    NOVELTY - Production of methanol involves supplying a source gas comprising carbon dioxide and hydrogen to a separation membrane reactor (10) having a space (11) at the impervious side, in which a catalyst is arranged, a water vapor separation membrane (13a), and a space (12) at the permeation side, advancing the shift reaction of source gas to methanol by the action of the catalyst, condensing methanol, separating methanol and the unconverted gas, and circulating the unconverted gas through the space (11).
   USE - Production of methanol used as chemical substance raw material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables production of methanol having high purity with high removal efficiency of reaction heat and methanol conversion ratio, by simple process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Production of methanol involves supplying a source gas comprising carbon dioxide and hydrogen to a separation membrane reactor having a space (11) at the impervious side, in which a catalyst is arranged, a water vapor separation membrane, and a space (12) at the permeation side, advancing the shift reaction of source gas to methanol by the action of the catalyst, passing the sweep gas through the space (12), removing a reaction heat generated by the shift reaction, flowing water vapor which is a by-product of the shift reaction permeating through the water vapor separation membrane out of the space (12), cooling the impervious fluid containing methanol produced by the shift reaction and a unconverted gas, condensing methanol, separating methanol and the unconverted gas, and circulating the unconverted gas through the space (11). An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for methanol production apparatus (1), which consists of the separation membrane reactor, a source gas supply unit (21), a sweep gas supply unit (22), an accommodation unit (31) for condensing methanol, an accommodation unit (32) for flowing water vapor which is the by-product of the shift reaction permeating through the water vapor separation membrane out of the space (12), and a circulation unit (40).
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic block diagram of methanol production apparatus.
   Methanol production apparatus (1)
   Separation membrane reactor (10)
   Spaces (11,12)
   Water vapor separation membrane (13a)
   Cooling medium flow path (14g)
   Source gas supply unit (21)
   Sweep gas supply unit (22)
   Accommodation units (31,32)
   Circulation unit (40)
   Cooling medium supply unit (50)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Component: The methanol production apparatus further consists of a cooling medium flow path (14g) adjacent to the space (11) and a cooling medium supply unit (50) for supplying the cooling medium to the cooling medium flow path.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The sweep gas is nitrogen and/or air. The water vapor separation membrane is an inorganic membrane having water vapor/methanol separation coefficient of 230 or more and water vapor transmission rate of 1x 10-7 mol/(second.Pa.m2) or more. The source gas contains a byproduct gas of an iron mill, in which hydrogen is added, or an off gas produced during purification of hydrogen by pressure fluctuation adsorption method. The byproduct gas of iron mill is a blast furnace gas or a converter gas. The water vapor separation membrane is supported by the surface of the porous base material. Preferred Property: The ratio of apparent volume of catalyst with respect to apparent volume of water vapor separation membrane and porous base material is 0.43-25.

AU CHOI J
   HE X
   LIU J
   LI T
AE CHINESE ACAD SCI MICROELECTRONICS INST (CAMI-C)
   ZHENXIN BEIJING SEMICONDUCTOR CO LTD (ZHEN-Non-standard)

GA 2020C46324
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a vaporizing device for vaporizing liquid carbon dioxide stored in a storage tank. A metal ion detector and an automatic control valve are arranged on a supply pipe. The metal ion detector detects metal ion in the supply pipe. The automatic control valve is electrically connected with the metal ion detector. The automatic control valve closes the supply pipe according to the metal ion detected by the metal ion detector. The metal ion detector comprises a metal ion sensing unit. A main body controls carbon dioxide of the supply pipe in supercritical state through an air pressure control unit and a temperature control unit.
   USE - Carbon dioxide supply system for a semiconductor cleaning system (Claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The system closes the supply pipe through the automatic control valve according to the metal ion in the supply pipe so as to prevent carbon dioxide containing metal ion from being supplied to a factory.
