PN CN111781165-A
TI Method for simultaneously analyzing content of water and carbon dioxide in natural gas via laser detection, involves obtaining concentration of water and carbon dioxide in natural gas according to linear function equation.
AU HU X
   XIANG L
AE SHENZHEN MIZI TECH DEV CO LTD (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 2020A3352M
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves establishing an independent variable group of water. A predetermined concentration of water gas is passed into a detection pool. An absorption spectrum of the water and carbon dioxide is recorded in preset band. Spectral intensity corresponding to each wavelength is determined. Dependent variable group of each group of mixed gas is determined corresponding to mixture of water and carbon dioxide in proportion. Influence coefficients are obtained through multiple linear regression algorithms. Concentration of water and carbon dioxide in the natural gas is obtained according to influence coefficient and linear function equation.
   USE - Method for simultaneously analyzing content of water and carbon dioxide in natural gas via laser detection.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables utilizing traditional laser absorption spectroscopy technology to select absorption band without interference, avoiding usage of using multiple analytical instruments to occupy a larger area so as to reduce investment and maintenance costs of analytical instruments.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a graph representing a method for simultaneously analyzing content of hydrogen and carbon dioxide in natural gas via laser detection. (Drawing includes non-English language text).

PN US2020140271-A1
TI Co-producing carbon dioxide containing stream and syngas stream involves e.g. introducing first high-pressure hydrocarbon containing stream and first high-pressure oxygen containing stream into syngas generator.
AU GUILLARD A
   ROESCH A
AE AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
GA 2020374628
AB    NOVELTY - Co-producing a carbon dioxide containing stream and a syngas stream, comprises introducing a first high-pressure hydrocarbon containing stream and a first high-pressure oxygen containing stream into a syngas generator (103), thus producing a high-pressure syngas stream, introducing a second high-pressure hydrocarbon containing stream and a second high-pressure oxygen containing stream into an oxy-combustion device (109), thus producing a high-pressure carbon dioxide containing stream, and introducing the high-pressure carbon dioxide containing stream into a work expander (111), thus generating work and a carbon dioxide containing stream.
   USE - The method is useful for co-producing a carbon dioxide containing stream and a syngas stream.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces overall operating and capital expenditures and reduces the direct and indirect carbon dioxide and criteria pollutants e.g. sulfur oxide, nitrogen oxides, carbon monoxide, volatile organic compound and particles.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a schematic view for integration of an air separation unit, a partial oxidation syngas unit, and an oxy-combustion unit.
   Syngas generator (103)
   Product reactor (105)
   Oxy-combustion device (109)
   Work expander (111)
   Air separation unit (114)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The high-pressure syngas stream is suitable for producing a downstream product without further syngas compression. The carbon dioxide containing stream is suitable for downstream separation. A high-pressure nitrogen stream is combined with the high-pressure syngas stream, thus producing an ammonia reactor feed stream, which is then introduced into the ammonia reactor. The ammonia reactor feed stream is further compressed in a feed compressor. The first high-pressure oxygen stream, the second high-pressure oxygen stream, and the high-pressure nitrogen containing stream are produced in the same air separation unit (114). The first high-pressure oxygen stream, the high-pressure nitrogen stream, and the second high-pressure oxygen stream are produced by vaporizing a first high-pressure liquid oxygen stream, a high-pressure liquid nitrogen stream, and a second high-pressure liquid oxygen stream in a main heat exchanger. The first high-pressure oxygen stream and the first high-pressure nitrogen stream are produced by vaporizing a first high-pressure liquid oxygen stream and a first high-pressure liquid nitrogen stream in a first heat exchanger, and the second high-pressure oxygen stream and a second high-pressure nitrogen stream are produced by vaporizing a first high-pressure liquid oxygen stream and a second high-pressure liquid nitrogen stream in a second heat exchanger. The high-pressure nitrogen stream is not subjected to further nitrogen compression. The high-pressure syngas stream has a pressure of greater than or equal to 50 barg, preferably greater than or equal to 100 barg. The high-pressure hydrocarbon containing stream is at the same pressure as required by the syngas generator. Preferred Components: The downstream product is: methanol produced in a methanol reactor; or ammonia produced in an ammonia reactor. The syngas generator comprises: a partial oxidation reactor; and an autothermal reactor.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

AU WU L
   GUO Q
   LI X
   XIA S
AE TECH INST PHYSICS & CHEM CHINESE ACAD (CAPC-C)
GA 201978584L
AB    NOVELTY - An inorganic semiconductor photocatalytic system comprises inorganic semiconductor nanocrystalline photocatalyst, solvent, carbon dioxide, organic substance and light. The inorganic semiconductor nanocrystalline photocatalyst comprises (II-B)-(VI-A), (III-A)-(V-A), (I-B)-(III-A)-(VI-A) group elements and a perovskite type quantum dots. The organic substance is an alcohol organic substance or an amine organic substance.
   USE - Inorganic semiconductor photocatalytic system for converting carbon dioxide and organic substance (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The inorganic semiconductor photocatalytic system simultaneously converts carbon dioxide and organic matter under illumination conditions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for converting carbon dioxide and an organic substance in an inorganic semiconductor photocatalytic system which involves mixing an inorganic semiconductor nanocrystalline photocatalyst and water to obtain an inorganic semiconductor photocatalyst solution, mixing the inorganic semiconductor photocatalyst solution and the alcohol to obtain a mixed solution (A), precipitating, separating the precipitate, mixing precipitate and the solvent to obtain mixed solution (B), mixing mixed solution (B) and organic substance to obtain a reaction liquid, adding inorganic base and sealing, introducing carbon dioxide gas and irradiating with light source to obtain a product by reduction reaction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The inorganic semiconductor nanocrystalline photocatalyst is cadmium sulfide, cadmium selenide, zinc sulfate, zinc selenide, cadmium selenide-cadmium sulfide, indium phosphite, copper indium sulfide or cesium lead tribromide (CsPbBr3). The inorganic semiconductor nanocrystalline photocatalyst comprises ligand. The ligand is trioctylphosphine oxide (TOPO), trioctylphosphine (TOP) amine, thio or alkane compound. The inorganic semiconductor nanocrystalline photocatalyst is a quantum dots, a nanorods, a nanowires or nanosheets. The inorganic base is sodium carbonate and/or cesium carbonate. Preferred Composition: The concentration of the inorganic semiconductor nanocrystalline photocatalyst in the inorganic semiconductor photocatalyst solution is 4x 10-5 to 3.2x 10-4 mol/L. The volume ratio of the inorganic semiconductor photocatalyst solution and the alcohol is 1:1-1:2. The concentration of the precipitate in the mixed solution (B) is 4x 10-6 to 3.2x 10-5 mol/L. The concentration of the organic matter in the reaction liquid is 0.01-0.2 mol/L. The concentration of the inorganic base in the reaction liquid is 0-0.2 mol/L.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The solvent is any one of water, acetonitrile or aqueous solution acetonitrile, tetrahydrofuran or aqueous solution of tetrahydrofuran, N,N-dimethylformamide or aqueous solution of N,N-dimethylformamide and 2-methyltetrahydrofuran or aqueous 2-methyltetrahydrofuran solution. The alcohol organic substance is 1-phenylethanol, 1-(4-methylphenyl)ethanol or 1-(4-methoxyphenyl)ethanol. The alcohol organic compound is of formula (I). The amine organic compound is benzylamine derivative of formula (II).R1-R6 = H, methyl, ethyl, methoxy, ethoxy or phenyl.

PT P
PN CN110091499-A
TI PTFE-based three dimensional printing method, involves mixing molten PTFE and supercritical carbon dioxide gas in extruder, and absorbing carbon dioxide by molecular chain of PTFE polymer, followed by realizing continuous extrusion of PTFE.
AU QU G
   PAN K
   LI J
   TANG X
   NING P
AE UNIV KUNMING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UKST-C)
GA 201969882K
AB    NOVELTY - A PTFE-based three dimensional printing method involves mixing a molten PTFE and a supercritical carbon dioxide gas in an extruder, so that the mass percentage of supercritical carbon dioxide in the PTFE is 4-10%, absorbing carbon dioxide by the molecular chain of PTFE polymer, reducing molecular chain entanglement and increasing free volume, while carbon dioxide acts as a lubricant between molecules, which reduces the viscosity of the melt. The glass transition temperature of PTFE swelled by supercritical carbon dioxide is lowered by 40-60 degrees C, and the pressure inside the extruder rises under the action of swelling, when the self-sealing effect is achieved, a pressure difference is formed at both ends of the container, and continuous extrusion of PTFE is realized to complete three dimensional printing.
   USE - PTFE-based three dimensional printing method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables printing PTFE-based three dimensional with reduced glass transition temperature of the polymer, and reduced the damage of decomposition in the PTFE thermoforming process, and better effect of realizing three dimensional printing molding of the PTFE.

PN CN109211800-A
TI Method of measuring concentration of carbon dioxide in sulfur hexafluoride gas, involves obtaining refractive index change value using polarized light phase of exiting light and linearly polarized light phase of emitted light.
AU LI G
   WANG Y
   ZHANG H
   ZHANG J
   ZHANG L
   GUAN Y
   WANG H
   CHI J
AE STATE GRID SICHUAN ELECTRIC POWER CO ELE (SGCC-C)
   STATE GRID CORP CHINA (SGCC-C)
GA 201907964R
AB    NOVELTY - Method of measuring concentration of carbon dioxide in sulfur hexafluoride gas, involves polarizing laser with wavelength of 632.8 nm, incident to measurement ambient air, recording linearly polarized light phase (A1) of exiting light, polarizing laser with wavelength of 632.8 nm which is incident on to-be-detected gas, recording linearly polarized light phase (A2) of emitted light, obtaining to-be-detected gas which is mixed gas of sulfur hexafluoride and carbon dioxide, obtaining refractive index change value using linearly polarized light phase (A1) of exiting light and linearly polarized light phase (A2) of emitted light. Refractive index change value is a change in refractive index of laser passing through gas to be detected in 632.8 nm band with respect to refractive index by measuring ambient air. According to relation of pre-calibrated carbon dioxide concentration change value and refractive index change value, concentration of carbon dioxide in gas to be detected is obtained.
   USE - Method of measuring concentration of carbon dioxide in sulfur hexafluoride gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method efficiently and accurately measures concentration of carbon dioxide in sulfur hexafluoride gas.

TI Synthesizing oxazinone with carbon dioxide involves mixing substance with strong alkali, adding nonproton dipolar solvent, reacting in carbon dioxide atmosphere, adding acid and substance e.g. estolide, reacting, and purifying.
AU ZHANG Z
   WU D
   SUN J
AE UNIV CHENGDU (UYUD-C)
   UNIV CHENGDU (UYUD-C)
GA 2018667252
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing oxazinone (I) with carbon dioxide involves mixing substance (II) with strong alkali, adding nonproton dipolar solvent, reacting in carbon dioxide atmosphere at - 10-120 degrees C, adding acid and substance A, reacting at - 10-120 degrees C, and purifying. The substance A is estolide or dehydration agent. The dehydration agent comprises acyl chloride, molecular sieve and/or non-metal oxide.
   USE - Method for synthesizing oxazinone with carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has good yield and mild and easy to control reaction condition, avoids the use of highly toxic carbon monoxide, phosgene or triphosgene, and does not use heavy metal salt catalyst and oxidant.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Synthesizing oxazinone of formula (I) with carbon dioxide involves mixing substance of formula (II) with strong alkali, adding nonproton dipolar solvent, reacting in carbon dioxide atmosphere at - 10-120 degrees C, adding acid and substance A, reacting at - 10-120 degrees C, and purifying. The substance A is estolide or dehydration agent. The dehydration agent comprises acyl chloride, molecular sieve and/or non-metal oxide.
   R1 and R2=alkyl, substituted alkyl and substituted aryl.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The strong alkali comprises tert-butoxide salt, methanol salt, ethanol salt, carbonate, phosphate, potassium hydroxide, sodium hydroxide and organic alkali. The tert-butoxide salt comprises sodium tert-butoxide, potassium tert-butoxide and lithium tert-butoxide, preferably potassium tert-butoxide. The molar ratio of strong alkali, acid and anhydride is 1:1:1.5. Preferred Method: Reaction is done at 60 degrees C for 16 hours and 60 degrees C for 6 hours. Purifying is done by silica gel flash chromatography with eluent petroleum ether and ethyl acetate mixed solvent at volume ratio of 10:1.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solvent includes dimethyl sulfoxide, N,N-dimethylformamide, N,N-dimethylacetamide, N-methylpyrrolidone, acetone, acetonitrile, diethylene glycol dimethyl ether, 1,4-dioxane, tetrahydrofuran, 2-methylhydrofuran, methylene dichloride, 1,2 dichloroethane and ethylene glycol dimethyl ether, preferably N,N-dimethylformamide. The molar ratio of tert-butoxide salt to substance (II) is greater than 1:1, preferably 3:1. The acid is 4-toluenesulfonic acid. The acid anhydride is acetic anhydride.
   Preferred Definitions:
   R1 and R2=Ph.

PN CN114605030-A
TI Performing resource utilization of carbon sink            oxygen-releasing type aquaculture sewage involves            performing two treatment stages, where aerobic            denitrifying bacteria and algae sewage front-end            treatment system cultures biogas slurry to meet            requirements of microalgae cultivation.
AU CAO L
   GAO Z
AE JIANGSU ZAOHUI BIOTECHNOLOGY RES INST CO (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202288530R
AB    NOVELTY - Performing resource utilization of carbon sink                oxygen-releasing type aquaculture sewage involves                performing two treatment stages, where an aerobic                denitrifying bacteria and algae sewage front-end                treatment system cultures biogas slurry to meet the                requirements of microalgae cultivation, and a                microalgae production system cultivates microalgae                with the treated biogas slurry and fixing carbon                dioxide.
   USE - Method for performing resource utilization of                carbon sink oxygen-releasing type aquaculture                sewage.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to perform resource                utilization of carbon sink oxygen-releasing type                aquaculture sewage, which processes and utilizes                the biogas slurry, without the need to dilute the                biogas slurry, and the treated farm manure does not                discharge any liquid, does not require to specially                discharge and treat the remaining liquid of the                manure in the farm, which can realize the reduction                and concentration of the sewage, and at the same                time realize the biodegradability of the biogas                slurry, without causing secondary pollution,                effluent of the aerobic denitrifying bacteria and                algae sewage front-end treatment system can fully                play a role in the microalgae production system,                and biogas can be used as a heat source, and                recover biogas combustion tail gas as carbon                dioxide source, so as to realize the efficient                production of microalgae.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Process: The treatment stage of                  the aerobic denitrifying bacteria and algae                  sewage front-end treatment system involves                  subjecting farm manure to solid-liquid separation                  through the solid-liquid separation equipment.                  The separated waste liquid is adjusted and the pH                  reaches 6.5-7.5, and then it is introduced into                  the anaerobic fermentation tank for anaerobic                  fermentation to produce biogas. The biogas slurry                  produced by anaerobic fermentation is taken and                  the supernatant is introduced into the biogas                  slurry storage tank. The chemical oxygen demand                  in the biogas slurry in the biogas slurry storage                  tank is controlled below 3000mg/L, and the                  concentration of suspended solids is controlled                  below 1000mg/L. The biogas slurry is processed in                  the bacteria-algae symbiosis treatment equipment                  to form a culture solution that meets the                  microalgae cultivation conditions. The culture                  solution is imported into the microalgae                  production equipment, and the biogas is                  transported to the biogas utilization system                  through pipelines. The biogas is used to generate                  electricity, and the waste heat and tail gas                  carbon dioxide are introduced into the microalgae                  production equipment to cultivate and produce the                  microalgae. The algal liquid produced by                  microalgae production equipment is introduced                  into harvesting equipment. The algal sludge is                  formed after separation by the harvesting                  equipment and enters the drying equipment for                  drying and powdering. After separation, the                  remaining tail water is re-introduced into the                  bacteria-algae symbiosis treatment equipment.                  Preferred Conditions: The pH of the separated                  sewage is adjusted to 6.5-7.5 with an acid                  solution, and the acid solution is any one of HCl                  solution and sulfuric acid solution. The bacteria                  and algae symbiosis treatment equipment includes                  aerobic denitrifying bacteria and algae membrane                  group for fully degrading biogas slurry,                  biological filter bed for adsorbing residual                  trace elements and organic suspended solids, an                  aeration tank for pretreatment and circulating                  water distribution, and a spray pipe for spraying                  biogas slurry on the aerobic denitrifying                  bacteria algal membrane group. The spray pipe is                  arranged above the aerobic denitrifying bacteria                  algal membrane group. The aeration tank is                  connected to the aeration tank through a pipeline                  and a water distribution pump, and the aerobic                  denitrifying bacteria algal membrane group, the                  biological filter bed and the aeration tank are                  connected in series from top to bottom. The                  method of processing involves entering the biogas                  slurry into the aeration tank for nitrification,                  and converting the nitrogen and ammonia                  pollutants in the biogas slurry into non-volatile                  oxidative nitrate nitrogen through a pretreatment                  nitrification reaction, inoculating aerobic                  denitrifying bacteria and microalgae on the                  aerobic denitrifying bacteria algal membrane                  group in advance. The aerated biogas slurry is                  evenly sprayed on the aerobic denitrifying                  bacteria algal membrane group through the                  distribution pump and the spray pipe for aerobic                  denitrification treatment. The biogas slurry is                  fully degraded and converted into N2, CO2 and                  water by the aerobic denitrifying bacteria and                  microalgae of the aerobic denitrifying bacteria                  algal membrane group. The water molecules in the                  biogas slurry are physically vaporized and                  evaporated to the atmosphere through the aerobic                  denitrifying bacteria algal membrane group. The                  respiration of aerobic denitrifying bacteria and                  microalgae also releases the water molecules they                  produce into the atmosphere. Portion of nitrate                  nitrogen, phosphorus and potassium in biogas                  slurry are metabolized and utilized by aerobic                  denitrifying bacteria and microalgae of aerobic                  denitrifying bacteria and algal membrane group.                  Portion of the trace elements and suspended                  solids are filtered and adsorbed by the                  biological filter bed and through the aerobic                  denitrifying bacteria and microalgae of the                  biological filter bed for further metabolism and                  utilization, the remaining biogas slurry after                  the treatment enters the aeration tank again. The                  biogas slurry is repeatedly cycled in the                  bacteria-algae symbiosis treatment equipment and                  finally a culture solution that meets the                  microalgae culture conditions is formed in the                  aeration tank. The microalgae production                  equipment is an open photobioreactor or a closed                  photobioreactor. The open photobioreactor or the                  closed photobioreactor is provided with a                  stirring device and/or aeration device to keep                  the algal liquid in a suspended state. The                  microalgae is selected from at least one of algal                  species with strong stress resistance such as                  Chlorella, Spirulina, and Chlamydomonas. The                  algal seed is inoculated into the culture                  solution, and the algal solution is obtained by                  culturing and producing in the microalgae                  production equipment. The recovery equipment is                  any one of a sedimentation device, a filtering                  device or a centrifugal device. The mass                  concentration of the algal liquid produced by the                  microalgae production equipment is 0.8-1.5 g/L.                  The separated waste liquid is adjusted to pH                  6.5-7.5 by acid solution, and the acid solution                  is HCl solution or H2SO4 solution. Both the                  aerobic denitrifying bacteria and algae sewage                  front-end treatment system and the microalgae                  production system can discharge portion of the                  water molecules in the biogas slurry to the                  atmosphere through natural evaporation or an                  auxiliary evaporator and is used to balance the                  water volume of biogas slurry in the aerobic                  denitrifying bacteria and algae sewage front-end                  treatment system and microalgae production                  system.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.); Q42 (Hydraulic engineering, soil shifting and sewerage (E02, 3))

PN WO2022108440-A1; WO2022108440-A8
TI Regenerating carbon and reactivating catalyst comprises preparing catalyst and distributing prepared catalyst, activating catalyst, injecting additional carbon monoxide and forming reduced catalyst, injecting steam to split reduced catalyst to regenerate carbon in second carbon production.
AU YARMO M A
   KASSIM M
   YUSOP M R
   WAN ISAHAK W N R
   ROSLI M E
   MOHAMMED HISAM M W
   ABU TAHARI M N
   SAMIDIN S
AE UNIV KEBANGSAAN MALAYSIA UKM (UYKE-Non-standard)
GA 202273080G
AB    NOVELTY - Method for regenerating carbon and reactivating a catalyst in a catalysis reaction, involves (a) preparing the catalyst and distributing the prepared catalyst to at least one reactor, (b) activating the catalyst with carbon monoxide at 300-900 degrees C to produce an activated catalyst, (c) injecting additional carbon monoxide for at least 1 hour for a first carbon production and forming a reduced catalyst simultaneously, and (d) injecting steam to split the reduced catalyst to regenerate carbon in a second carbon production, where the first carbon production, the catalyst converts carbon monoxide into carbon and carbon dioxide and in the second carbon production, the reduced catalyst interacts with the steam to form a metal oxide, hydrogen and carbon and the hydrogen is used to regenerate carbon and reactivate the catalyst for a continuous cycle of carbon formation in the reactor.
   USE - The method is used for regenerating carbon and reactivating a catalyst in a catalysis reaction. The method is used to conversion of carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide via thermal catalytic reaction.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves carbon production with better quality and effectiveness, provides simple and carbon growth parameters, does not require huge amount of energy to produce carbon and easily produces carbon monoxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst is a metal oxide-based catalyst or an impregnated catalyst, where the impregnated catalyst is a combination of a metal oxide and a promoter. The metal oxide-based catalyst is iron oxide or nickel oxide. The promoter is nitrate salts chosen from cobalt, chromium, zinc, molybdenum, zirconium, manganese, cerium, magnesium, nickel, iron, calcium and barium. The carbon collected is in a form of carbon amorphous and/or carbon nanotubes. The reduced catalyst is a metal carbide. Preferred Method: An optimum temperature to produce the reduced catalyst is 500 degrees C. The flow rate of the carbon monoxide in steps (b) and (c) is 5-50 ml/minute. The steam is injected using a pulse chemisorption water vapor at a dose of 5-40 and each dose consists of at least 0.23 cm3 of water vapor. Preferred Composition: The flow rate of the carbon monoxide is optimized at 10-20 ml/minute for iron oxide-based catalyst and nickel oxide-based catalyst, respectively.
   EXAMPLE - The example section describes about the catalyst preparation that does not support the claims.

TI Manufacturing catalyst support for platinum-group            fuel cell, comprises preparing acetylene black support,            and heat-treating acetylene-based support in carbon            dioxide atmosphere to form pores having average            diameter of preset range.

   KANG H M
AE VINATECH CO LTD (VINA-Non-standard)

PN CN215214806-U
TI Carbon dioxide high pressure stop valve with adjustable sealing performance, has damping mechanism that is located between upper bottom plate and lower bottom plate.
AU LI L
AE CHENGDU HAICHEN GAS CO LTD (CHEN-Non-standard)
GA 202204401T
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a carbon dioxide high pressure stop valve with adjustable sealing performance, comprising a valve body, a valve cover, a stop hole, a valve clack, a valve rod and a rotating wheel, the two ends of the valve respectively is provided with a water inlet and a water outlet, further comprising: an upper bottom plate, the upper bottom plate is located on the lower end face of the valve rod; the lower bottom plate, the lower bottom plate is located on the upper end face of the valve clack. In the utility model, rotating the rotating wheel, the valve rod controls the lower movement of the valve clack, when the valve clack and the stop hole contact, continuously rotating the rotating wheel, the distance between the upper bottom plate and the lower bottom plate is reduced by the spring; at this time, the valve clack is pressed by the pressure from the damping mechanism continuously with the stop hole; the pressure of the damping mechanism to the valve clack is directly given to the pressure of the valve rod; the pressure of the valve clack is gradually changed, so that the valve clack is less abrasion caused by good sealing performance, so the service life of the valve clack is high.

Purified air                is mixed with external oxygen and compressed to                obtain compressed mixed gas. Compressed mixed gas                is made to react by using an electric arc. Nitric                oxide gas is obtained. Temperature of nitric oxide                gas is reduced to perform oxidation reaction with                added oxygen to obtain nitrogen oxide. Nitric oxide                is reacted with water to generate nitric acid,                where acidic gas comprises carbon dioxide hydrogen                sulfide carbon dioxide. PT P
PN CN113603126-A; CN113603126-B
TI Preparation of calcium oxide involves performing            reduction treatment to industrial by-product gypsum,            and subjecting obtained calcium sulfide to oxidation            treatment under carbon dioxide atmosphere.
AU FAN C
   HE H
   HAO L
   LI S
AE INST PROCESS ENG CHINESE ACAD SCI (CAGC-C)
GA 2021D1123B
AB    NOVELTY - The method for preparing calcium oxide by                using industrial by-product gypsum, involves (a)                subjecting to reduction treatment to obtain calcium                sulfide, and (b) subjecting to oxidation treatment                under carbon dioxide atmosphere. The industrial                by-product gypsum includes combination of                phosphogypsum, desulfurized gypsum, titanium                gypsum, mirabilite gypsum, fluorogypsum, salt                gypsum or lemon gypsum. The industrial byproduct                gypsum is reduced and decomposed in the reducing                atmosphere to obtain the calcium sulfide. The                reducing atmosphere comprises combination of carbon                monoxide, sulfur, hydrogen, hydrogen sulfide or                methane.
   USE - Method for preparing calcium oxide by using                industrial by-product gypsum, used in solid waste                resource utilization technology application.
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively reduces the initial                reaction temperature, improves the decomposition                rate and conversion rate of the raw material,                reduces the system energy consumption and operation                cost, and improves the economic efficiency of the                process. The method has no secondary pollutant                discharge in the process of preparing calcium                oxide. The gas-solid product obtained by the method                is easy to be separated, and the solid product with                high purity can be obtained. The industrial                by-product gypsum is converted into calcium oxide                product by reduction and oxidation treatment.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic flow diagram                illustrating the process for preparing calcium                oxide by using industrial by-product gypsum.                (Drawing includes non-English language text)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The obtained calcium                  sulfide is oxidized under carbon dioxide                  atmosphere to obtain solid product calcium oxide                  and gaseous products carbon monoxide and sulfur                  dioxide. The carbon dioxide atmosphere includes                  carbon dioxide or a mixed gas of carbon dioxide                  and oxygen. The preparation process includes gas                  separation and recovery treatment, preferably,                  the gas separation and recovery treatment                  includes separating the obtained gas product to                  obtain independent carbon monoxide and sulfur                  dioxide, the carbon monoxide is reused for                  reduction treatment, and the sulfur products. The                  reduction treatment is performed by reductively                  decomposing the industrial by-product gypsum in a                  reducing atmosphere, and reacting at greater than                  500degreesC to obtain calcium sulfide. The                  oxidation treatment is performed by oxidizing                  obtained calcium sulfide in carbon dioxide                  atmosphere, heating at above 900degreesC for                  30-150 minutes to obtain solid product calcium                  oxide and gaseous products carbon monoxide and                  sulfur dioxide. Gas separation and recovery                  treatment is performed by separating the obtained                  gas product to obtain independent carbon monoxide                  and sulfur dioxide, the carbon monoxide is reused                  for reduction treatment, and the sulfur dioxide                  is used to prepare sulfur-containing                  products.Preferred Conditions: The temperature of the                  reduction treatment is not lower than                  500degreesC, the reducing atmosphere is carbon                  monoxide, and the reaction temperature is not                  lower than 750degreesC, preferably                  900-1100degreesC, preferably, the reducing                  atmosphere is carbon monoxide, the reaction time                  is not less than 30 minutes, preferably 30-120                  minutes, preferably, the reducing atmosphere is                  sulfur, the reaction temperature is not less than                  600degreesC, preferably 750-900degreesC,                  preferably, the reducing atmosphere is sulfur,                  the reaction time is not less than 90 minutes,                  preferably 90-150 minutes, preferably, the                  reducing atmosphere is hydrogen, the reaction                  temperature is not less than 500degreesC,                  preferably 750-900degreesC, preferably, the                  reducing atmosphere is hydrogen, and the reaction                  time is not less than 20 minutes, preferably                  20-80 minutes, preferably, the reducing                  atmosphere is hydrogen sulfide, the reaction                  temperature is not lower than 800degreesC,                  preferably 900-1100degreesC, preferably, the                  reducing atmosphere is hydrogen sulfide, the                  reaction time is greater than 10 minutes,                  preferably 10-60 minutes, preferably the reducing                  atmosphere is methane, the reaction temperature                  is greater than 700degreesC, preferably                  810-900degreesC, preferably the reducing                  atmosphere is methane, the reaction time is                  greater than 30 minutes, preferably 30-90                  minutes. The temperature of the oxidation                  treatment is above 900degreesC, preferably                  950-1100degreesC, optionally, the reaction time                  of the oxidation treatment is 30-150                  minutes.
DC E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).)

PN FR3109097-A3; FR3109097-B3
TI Biogas production installation comprises digester fed by biomass and desorption reactor fed by air allowing desorption of at least part of carbon dioxide contained in at least part of biomass and means for introducing biomass.
AU CARDON G
   GREGORY R
   TRUEBA A
   VALENTIN S
   GUILLAUME C
   RAPHAELLE G
   ANTONIO T
   SOLENE V
AE AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
GA 2021B7027F
AB    NOVELTY - Biogas production installation comprises a digester fed by biomass and a desorption reactor fed by air allowing the desorption of at least part of the carbon dioxide (CO2) contained in at least part P of the biomass, and means for introducing biomass in the form of droplets into the desorption reactor, and means for introducing air in the form of bubbles into the desorption reactor.
   USE - Used as biogas production installation.
   ADVANTAGE - The biogas production installation produces biogas by the degradation of organic matter in the absence of oxygen (anaerobic digestion) of sewage plant sludge, manure/slurry, agricultural residues, food waste; biogas is a powerful greenhouse gas and is a significant source of renewable energy in a context of the scarcity of fossil fuels; can supply a natural gas network, filling station for vehicles, and can also be liquefied to be stored in the form of liquid natural gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for producing biogas using installation, comprising (a) introducing biomass into the digester, (b) extracting at least a part P of the biomass of the digester, (c) introducing P part of the biomass in the form of a droplets in the desorption reactor, and (d) introducing air in the form of bubbles into the reactor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Method: The method further comprises (e) recovering the P part of the biomass depleted in CO2 of an air flow enriched in CO2; (f) introducing the P part of the CO2-depleted biomass into the digester; (g) performing anaerobic digestion of biomass in the digester; and (h) recovering biogas enriched in methane, where in step (d) air introduced in the form of bubbles into the desorption reactor is air enriched with oxygen, preferably air containing at least 22% oxygen.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The means for introducing air makes it possible to form bubbles with a diameter of less than 10 mm. The means for introducing biomass in the form of droplets into the desorption reactor comprises a spray column. The desorption reactor comprises at least one contactor, preferably a contactor with bulk packing. The installation comprises a means allowing the extraction of at least a part P of the biomass included in the digester, means of recovering biomass depleted in CO2 at the outlet of the desorption reactor and a means allowing the introduction of recovered biomass in the digester.

   KUBOTA H
   SHIGEIZUMI K
AE FUJITA KK (FUKG-C)
GA 2021B2772K
AB    NOVELTY - The system (1) has a processing device (10) that causes a carbonation reaction between an alkaline solid reaction product and a gas containing carbon dioxide. A measuring device (60) measures the carbon dioxide concentration of the gas after the carbonation reaction. A solenoid valve (V1) adjusts the amount of gas supplied from the supply source to the processing device. A control device (90) controls the solenoid valve so as to change the amount of gas supplied from the supply source based on the information acquired from the measuring device. A suction pump draws in the gas after the carbonation reaction inside the processing device and supplies the gas to the processing apparatus again.
   USE - Carbonation monitoring system for carbonating alkaline solid reaction material e.g. incineration ash such as cinders, and dust collecting ash and flying ash such as soot and dust floating in incinerator waste gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbonation monitoring system can make appropriate aggregate supply of the gas used for a carbonation reaction.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (1), the                  specific surface area of the unslaked lime added                  is less than 0.006 m2/g, and the content of                  calcium oxide in the unslaked lime is greater                  than 85%.

TI Method for detecting and tracking fluid leakage in            fault based on optical fiber bragg grating sensing,            involves calculating real-time phase state and leakage            rate of carbon dioxide by analyzing pressure,            temperature and strain data.
AU LI Q
   XU L
   TAN Y
   LI X
AE INST ROCK & SOIL MECHANICS CHINESE ACAD (CRSM-C)
GA 2021A2407K
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves cleaning residual oil                stain on a processed cylindrical rock sample by                alcohol and drying by a drying box until weight                remains unchanged. Dry density, height, diameter                and volume parameters are measured. A section of                the cylindrical rock sample is obtained by brazil                cleaving or sawing process. An optical fiber                grating sensor is placed on a broken layer by using                a fine walking knife. A seepage rate testing module                is fixed in a seepage measuring panel downstream                hole pressure inlet pipeline. Real-time phase state                and leakage rate of carbon dioxide are calculated                by analyzing pressure, temperature and strain                data.
   USE - Method for detecting and tracking fluid                leakage in fault based on an optical fiber bragg                grating sensing.
   ADVANTAGE - Method realizes convenient installation,                provides better durability, stable system and                long-time real-time online monitoring temperature                of fault fluid leakage, and pressure and fault                three-dimensional space-time strain                condition.

PT P
PN CN112761599-A; CN112761599-B
TI Method for enhancing oil recovery efficiency based            on self-generated carbon dioxide (CO2), involves            transporting separated lean amine solution into ground            CO2 capture device, and capturing CO2 again to obtain            rich amine solution.
AU WANG S
   GUO P
   WANG Z
   LIU H
   HU Y
   GU Z
   HU S
AE UNIV SOUTHWEST PETROLEUM (USWP-C)
GA 202154153A
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves preparing the                self-generating system in the ground liquid mixing                tank. The self-generating system 5-35 wt.% CO2                capture product in amine solution, 0-3 wt.%                anti-precipitation protective agent, and 62-95 wt.%                brine. The CO2 capture product of the amine                solution is a rich amine solution obtained after                the amine solution passes the ground CO2 capture                device to capture CO2. The prepared self-generating                system is injected into oil reservoirs above 70                degrees C. Conventional oil production operations                are carried out to separate crude oil and lean                amine solution from the produced mixture after the                reaction of the self-generating system is completed                and the fluid in the reservoir is balanced. The                separated lean amine solution is transported into                the ground CO2 capture device, and CO2 is captured                again to obtain the rich amine solution.
   USE - Method for enhancing oil recovery efficiency                based on self-generated carbon dioxide (CO2)                captured by CO2.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables improving the recovery rate                of crude oil in a simple and cost-effective                manner.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                method for enhancing oil recovery efficiency based                on self-generated CO2 captured by CO2. (Drawing                includes non-English language text)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - The amine solution is monoethanolamine,                  diethanolamine, methyldiethanolamine,                  aminomethylpropanol, diethylenediamine. The                  anti-precipitation protective agent is                  ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid, sodium                  polyacrylate, maleic acid-acrylic acid copolymer,                  hydrolyzed polymaleic anhydride, and acrylic                  acid-2-acrylamide-2-methylpropanesulfonic acid                  copolymer.
DC H01 (Obtaining crude oil and natural gas - including exploration, drilling, well completion, production and treatment. General off-shore platform and drilling technology is included together with the treatment of tar sands and oil shales (C10G, E21B).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); Q49 (Mining (E21))

PN WO2021074947-A1; JP2021552001-X; EP4047208-A1; JP7138807-B2; EP4047208-A4
TI Scroll compressor mounted in e.g. refrigerator has            fixed and oscillation base plates whose distance            becomes greater gradually as outer circumferential            section of fixed and oscillation spiral structures            approaches central section.
AU KOYAMA S
   HIRAMI T
   IMANISHI T
AE MITSUBISHI ELECTRIC CORP (MITQ-C)
   MITSUBISHI ELECTRIC CORP (MITQ-C)
   MITSUBISHI ELECTRIC CORP (MITQ-C)
GA 202141697R
AB    NOVELTY - The compressor has fixed scroll (1) in which a                fixed base plate (1c) and a fixed spiral structure                formed on the fixed base plate. An oscillation                scroll (2) has an oscillation base plate (2c) and                an oscillation spiral structure formed on the                oscillation base plate. The oscillation base plate                is arranged so as to face the fixed base plate, and                a compression chamber is formed by combining the                oscillation spiral structure with the fixed spiral                structure. The scroll compressor has a structure in                which the distance between the fixed base plate and                the oscillation base plate gradually becomes                greater, as the outer circumferential section of                each of the fixed spiral structure and the                oscillation spiral structure approaches the central                section.
   USE - Scroll compressor mounted in refrigerator, air                conditioner, and water heater.
   ADVANTAGE - The coolant leak between compression chambers                is reduced and the performance decrement of                compressor is suppressed, even if it is a case                where the mixed refrigerant containing carbon                dioxide, or carbon dioxide is used as refrigerant.                The amount of refrigerant leakage is reduced, and                the compressor efficiency is improved.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a sectional view of the                compression mechanism of the scroll                compressor.
   Fixed scroll (1)
   Discharge port (1a)
   Fixed base plate (1c)
   Oscillation scroll (2)
   Oscillation base plate (2c)

PN RU2744669-C1
TI Maxillofacial surgery includes, cryoexposure to scar tissue with gripping of adjoining areas of intact tissues. Laser exposure is carried out on frozen tissues with light scalpel of carbon dioxide laser.
AU ANDREEVA V V
   EGOROV V I
   SERGEEV I V
   VASILEV V S
AE VLADIMIRSKY MOSC REGIONAL RES CLINICAL (VLAD-Non-standard)
GA 202138585H
AB    NOVELTY - Invention refers to medicine, namely to maxillofacial, plastic, reconstructive and general surgery. One time cryoexposure to scar tissue is performed with cooling rate of tip 220C/min and temperature on tip working portion 180C, exposition 1520 s. After that, without waiting for thawing, laser exposure is performed. Herewith, cryoexposure to scar tissue is performed with gripping of adjoining areas of intact tissues with width of 0.51 mm. Then laser exposure is carried out on frozen tissues with light scalpel of carbon dioxide laser, with wavelength of 10.6 mcm in continuous mode with power of 36 W till their complete removal. After the operation, a restorative therapy is performed on a remote cicatrix till the wound is healed. As a restorative therapy, it is suggested to perform Ceroxin gel application 23 times or to insert into the cicatrix of the scar an injection of plasma enriched with growth factors, volume 0.1 ml to depth of 0.5 cm, pointwise at 0.51.0 cm from each other friend.
   USE - Medicine.
   ADVANTAGE - Method enables timely adequate treatment of the given head and neck pathology, provides early rehabilitation and a good aesthetic and cosmetic result, reduces the number of complications, reduces the length of rehabilitation, wherein the operation can be performed on an out-patient basis.

PN CN112490356-A; CN112490356-B
TI Preparing graphene-doped fully flexible            magneto-electric heterojunction comprises e.g. using            carbon dioxide infrared laser carbonization flexible            substrate to form graphene film on surface of flexible            substrate and dispersing polymer powder.
AU WEN D
   CHEN X
   LUO D
   LI R
   CUI W
   DI K
   LIU Y
AE UNIV CHONGQING POSTS & TELECOM (UCPT-C)
GA 2021301481
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing graphene-doped fully flexible                magneto-electric heterojunction comprises using                carbon dioxide infrared laser carbonization                flexible substrate, to form graphene film on the                surface of the flexible substrate, dispersing 70/30                or 80/20 mol% of P (vinylidene fluoride-TrFE)                polymer powder in the dimethyl formamide to obtain                the polyvinylidene fluoride solution, dropping the                polyvinylidene fluoride solution to the surface of                the graphene film, pre-baking the obtained P                (vinylidene fluoride-TrFE) /graphene/PI sample,                annealing at 135 degrees C to form beta phase P                (vinylidene fluoride-TrFE), using metal cobalt iron                boron as target material, using magnetron                sputtering to deposit on the surface of the P                (vinylidene fluoride-TrFE) ferroelectric layer,                forming a metal layer and taking tantalum metal as                target material, using magnetron sputtering method                to deposit on the surface of the cobalt iron boron                metal layer, forming protective layer. TI Cleaning of physical vapor deposition chamber            component for fabrication of integrated circuit,            involves directing jet of pressurized fluid at surface            of chamber component, directing pressurized carbon            dioxide, removing contaminants, drying, heating,            purging, surface conditioning and drying.

   LIU S
   BHAT S
   XIAO W
AE APPLIED MATERIALS INC (APMA-C)
   APPLIED MATERIALS INC (APMA-C)
GA 2021164709
AB    NOVELTY - Cleaning of physical vapor deposition (PVD)                chamber (100) component involves directing a jet of                pressurized fluid at a surface of the PVD chamber                component, directing pressurized carbon dioxide at                the surface of the PVD chamber component, placing                the PVD chamber component in a liquid and producing                ultrasonic waves in the liquid to further remove                contaminants from the surface of the PVD chamber                component, removing the PVD chamber components from                the liquid and drying the surface of the PVD                chamber component using a plasma, heating the                chamber component to a peak temperature of                50degrees Celsius, placing the PVD chamber                component in a process chamber, reducing the                pressure below atmospheric pressure and purging the                process chamber with a gas, surface conditioning                the surface of chamber component, and drying by                directing a gas on the surface of chamber                component.

GA 202104868U
AB    NOVELTY - Micro-nano bubble water, obtained by                generating micro-nano bubbles in pure water with                mixed gas. Where the mixed gas comprises nitrogen,                argon and carbon dioxide.
   USE - Used as micro-nano bubble water.
   ADVANTAGE - The microbubble water keeps food fresh at room                temperature, prolongs fresh keeping time of food,                has long storage time, realizes commercialization,                is directly used without preparing the bubble water                on site, has low production cost, and it                considerable social value and economic                benefits.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing micro-nano bubble water, comprising (i)                adding pure water into the first container, where                the first container is provided with first stage                bubble generator, and the second container is                provided with second stage bubble generator, (ii)                injecting the mixed gas into container, opening the                first stage bubble generator, using the first stage                bubble generator for forming external electric                field in the aqueous solution, preparing aqueous                solution and mixed gas into aqueous solution by the                first stage bubble generator, (iii) inputting the                water-gas solution from the first container into                the second container, opening the second-stage                bubble generator, using second-stage bubble                generator for forming external electric field in                the water-gas solution, and using the second stage                bubble generator for preparing the aqueous solution                into micro-nano-bubble water, and (iv) filling                micro-nano bubble water.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mixed gas further                  comprises carbon monoxide, oxygen and/or ozone.                  The mixed gas comprises 1-100 PPM nitrogen, 1-100                  PPM argon, 1-100 PPM carbon dioxide, 1-100 PPM                  carbon monoxide, 1-100 PPM oxygen, and 1-100 PPM                  ozone. The micro-nano bubble water further                  comprises sulfur dioxide micro-nano bubbles.                  Preferred Method: The preparation of sulfur                  dioxide micro-nano-bubble in pure water is as                  follows: heating pure water to 70-80 degrees C,                  pouring into vacuum container, reducing the                  pressure in the vacuum container to 2                  kilopascals, injecting sulfur dioxide micro-nano                  bubble into the pure water, cooling the pure                  water carried with sulfur dioxide micro                  nano-bubble to room temperature.

AE AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
GA 202053852Y
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves vaporizing a cryogenic liquid having as its main component oxygen, nitrogen or argon originating from a source by heat exchange with ambient air in the atmospheric vaporizer in a normal operation. The cryogenic liquid is no longer sent from the source to the atmospheric vaporizer but, instead, sending a gas to the atmospheric vaporizer at a temperature of at least 0 degrees C in a reheating phase. The gas originates from a cryogenic distillation air separation unit. The gas sent to the atmospheric vaporizer in the reheating phase is a stream of air purified of water and of carbon dioxide taken from the air supply of the cryogenic distillation air separation unit.
   USE - Method for reheating atmospheric vaporizer using gas originating from cryogenic air separation unit.
   ADVANTAGE - The reheating process is more economical and easier to implement, which do not require any dedicated reheater installation. The design and the dimensioning of the atmospheric vaporizer guarantee the supply of the gas for a predefined period before the bulk formation of ice.

Y-axis linear modules are set                on both sides of the first gantry. Two Y-axis                linear modules are equipped with Z-axis modules.                One of the Z-axis modules is provided with a laser                lens for cutting and a carbon dioxide cleaning                structure is provided beside the laser lens. The                laser lens and the carbon dioxide cleaning                structure are distributed along the Y-axis                direction. The laser lens is connected with a                picosecond UV laser. The other Z-axis module is                provided with a tear film structure and an                automated optical inspection detection                camera.
   USE - Used as flexible organic light emitting diode                display panel laser cutting device with cleaning                function.
   ADVANTAGE - The device performs shape cutting, chamfer                angle cutting, tearing and lead scribing                work.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation                of the flexible organic light emitting diode                display panel laser cutting device for with                cleaning function.

TI Cyclone for separation of gas-liquid mixture, used            for refrigerant accumulator in vehicle air conditioning            system, has inlet which is arranged coaxially with axis            of cyclone and opens directly into inlet chamber of            body of cyclone.

AE HANON SYSTEMS (HACL-C)
GA 202012710Q
AB    NOVELTY - The cyclone has a body which is provided with                an inlet chamber (2). The stationary vane (22) in a                shape of a helix ensures rotation of the gas-liquid                mixture in the outlet chamber (3). The inlet is                arranged coaxially with an axis of the cyclone and                opens directly into the inlet chamber of the body                of the cyclone. The outlet from the stationary vane                is oriented perpendicular to the axis of the                cyclone and tangentially to a diameter of the inlet                chamber of the cyclone. The stationary vane is flat                at an outlet end. The cyclone separates gaseous                carbon dioxide from liquid carbon dioxide and                liquid oil in refrigerant accumulator or                accumulator with the internal heat exchanger in the                vehicle air conditioning system.
   USE - Cyclone for separation of gas-liquid mixture,                used for refrigerant accumulator or accumulator                (all claimed) with internal heat exchanger in                vehicle air conditioning system.

PN CN110697805-A; CN110697805-B
TI River algae separation and biological fertilizer treatment method, involves completing mechanically broken algae, and completing preparation process of biological fertilizer by drying heater.
AU CAI K
   ZHANG C
   WANG K
   LI J
   WANG X
   ZHAO S
   XU C
   FAN X
   SHEN Y
   LI Y
AE HANGZHOU JINYI CONSTR GROUP CO LTD (HANG-Non-standard)
GA 2020084287
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves rubbing and washing an algae salvaged from the river away with salt to achieve the rehydration stage. The algae are placed in an algae separation furnace (3) after the rehydration stage is completed, and a differential pressure cell blasting process is used on the algae in the algae separation furnace. The supercritical carbon dioxide and water vapor are co-injected and pressurized to complete the heating and pressurizing stage. The pressure-retaining process is performed to complete the cooking stage, and algae is separated and pre-processed by using the blasting force generated by the high-pressure supercritical carbon dioxide and flashed water vapor during the momentary pressure relief. The algae are mechanically broken through milling assembly. The mechanically broken algae is completed, and the preparation process of biological fertilizer is completed by drying the heater.
   USE - River algae separation and biological fertilizer treatment method.
   ADVANTAGE - The characteristics of large processing capacity, high economic value, green environmental protection and strong sustainability. The problem of river algae's ecological damage to water is solved. The recyclability of algae is improved and the accumulation of algae waste is eased.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic view of the differential pressure cell blasting furnace. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Pressure relief pipe (1)
   Pneumatic ball valve (2)
   Algae separation furnace (3)
   Pressure gauge (4)
   Intake pressure pipe (5)

   Heart rate monitor (106)

The three or more sources                  of treatment modalities are programmed into a                  processor to optimize treatment of the subject.                  The complete treatment of the subject is provided                  in half the time of a treatment in an ozone/heat                  device. The apparatus further                  comprises an ozone degradator that degrades the                  ozone into before exiting the enclosure. The                  enclosure comprises two overlapping PEMF fields                  formed within the enclosure to provide the                  subject with at least partially amplified                  magnetic fields. Preferred Subject: The subject                  is a human.

PT P
PN CN110407241-A; CN110407241-B
TI Preparing high-activity calcium oxide by crushing phosphogypsum slag, microwave calcining, filtering, and mixing with aqueous ammonia, adding dihydroxy glycine to mixed solution, introducing carbon dioxide gas to mixed solution and cooling.
AU WEI M
   LV W
AE GUANGXI XIAYANG ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (GUAN-Non-standard)
   DUAN CHUNXU NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO (DUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201994309P
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing high-activity calcium oxide involves (i) crushing the phosphogypsum slag through 200-300 mesh, and carrying out microwave calcination to obtain a solid (I), (ii) washing for 2-3 times using deionized water, filtering to remove the filtrate, and mixing with aqueous ammonia in a mass ratio of 1:(3-8) to obtain a mixed solution, adding dihydroxy glycine to the mixed solution, where the ratio of the added dihydroxyethyl glycine to the mixed liquid product is (1-3):10, continuously introducing carbon dioxide gas to the mixed solution, adjusting the pH value of the mixed solution to 9-10, reacting for 2-4 hours at 30-40 degrees C and the stirring rotating speed of 500-600 rpm, and carrying out suction filtration to obtain a solid (II) and carrying out microwave calcination on the solid (II) obtained in the step (ii), and cooling to room temperature to obtain the high-activity calcium oxide.
   USE - Method for preparing high-activity calcium oxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method solves the problem that the easy introduction of impurities in the production of calcium oxide by the phosphogypsum raw material affects the purity of the calcium oxide, and obtains high active calcium oxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The microwave calcination in step (i) is first heated at 200-250 degrees C at a temperature increase rate of 15 degrees C/minutes to preheat the phosphogypsum slag for 15-30 minutes, followed by a temperature increase of 15 degrees C/minutes and the phosphogypsum residue is calcined at 400-600 degrees C for 30-60 minutes, cooling to room temperature at a temperature decreasing rate of 10 degrees C/minutes to obtain a solid (I). The microwave calcination in the step (iii) heating at 200-250 degrees C at a heating speed of 15 degrees C/minutes, preheating the solid (II) for 15-30 minutes, heating at 450-500 degrees C for 15-30 minutes at a temperature rising speed of 15 degrees C/minutes, heating at 600-800 degrees C at a temperature rising speed of 15 degrees C/minutes, calcining the solid (II) for 1-3 hours, and cooling to room temperature at a cooling speed of 10 degrees C/minutes to obtain high-activity calcium oxide. Preferred Composition: The aqueous ammonia in step (i) is aqueous ammonia with the mass fraction of 25-35%.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.)

AU LATIMER E
   COPELAND C
   FIEDLER M
   LAMONT D
AE XDI HOLDINGS LLC (XDIH-Non-standard)
GA 201944809L
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves vaporizing water by direct contact of the water with combustion products to produce a resulting fluid includes the steam and a non-condensable gas. The fluid is condensed to provide a mixture of the non-condensable gas and water condensate. A gas phase with the non-condensable gas is separated from a liquid phase with the condensate (128). The steam is regenerated by reducing pressure and then heating the condensate in a heat exchanger (116). The steam is injected into a formation for recovery of the hydrocarbons.
   USE - Method for recovering hydrocarbons with steam.
   ADVANTAGE - The separator losses to steam is minimized by including them in the optimization of cooler and the losses to water droplets are minimized by good separator design. The carbon dioxide exits from an overhead of the first separator and is exhausted or conveyed to a sequestration site distinct from the formation into which the steam is desired for injection with limited or no carbon dioxide co-injection.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system for recovering hydrocarbons with steam.

PN CN109440145-A; CN109440145-B
TI Preparation of graphene/copper composite            conductive material, involves exposing organic polymer            film polyimide to carbon dioxide infrared laser            irradiation, carbonizing surface of polyimide to form            porous graphene, obtaining porous graphene.
AU JU B
   CHEN J
   LI N
AE SUZHOU TANSU JIDIAN NEW MATERIAL CO LTD (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 201924511D
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation of graphene/copper composite                conductive material involves (1) exposing the                organic polymer film polyimide to carbon dioxide                infrared laser irradiation, carbonizing surface of                the polyimide to form porous graphene, the portion                not affected by the laser acts as a substrate to                support the graphene, obtaining porous graphene on                an organic substrate, (2) placing organic substrate                and graphene together in a plating solution, using                the graphene as a working electrode and a platinum                sheet as an auxiliary electrode, performing                electrochemical copper plating using a saturated                mercury sulfate electrode as a reference electrode,                preparing a graphene/copper composite material on                an organic substrate, taking out and vacuum drying                the graphene/copper composite material and the                organic substrate, stripping the graphene/copper                composite material from the organic substrate, and                (3) pressing the graphene/copper composite into a                press, and performing sintering treatment.
   USE - Preparation of graphene/copper composite                conductive material (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The graphene/copper composite conductive                material exhibits excellent mechanical properties                and electrical properties due to the uniform                distribution of graphene in the copper matrix. The                preparation method is simple in operation, and is                easy to obtain raw materials. The preparation                method meets different application requirements,                especially in the field of copper cable, so it is                easy to promote.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The plating solution                  comprises 50-100 g/l of copper sulfate                  pentahydrate, 180-220 g/l of sulfuric acid,                  40-100 mg/l of chloride ion, and 1-10000 mg/l of                  additives. Preferred Properties: The thickness of                  the organic polymer film is 50-1000 mu m, and the                  thickness of the porous graphene formed by laser                  irradiation on the surface of the film is 25-500                  mu m. Preferred Conditions: The plating                  temperature is 20-30 degrees C, and the plating                  time is 5-60 minutes. The sintering temperature                  is 400-1000 degrees C, and the sintering time is                  1-6 hours. The electroplating is carried out by a                  constant current method having a current density                  of 0.01-0.1 A/cm2 and a voltage range of -1 to 1                  V. The graphene/copper composite material is                  compression molded by a press having a pressure                  of 10-500 MPa at the time of pressing. The laser                  has a wavelength of 9.3-10.6 mu m, a laser power                  of 1-5 W, a pulse frequency of 1-50 kHz, and a                  laser scanning speed of 200-2000 mm/second. One                  surface of the organic polymer film is exposed to                  laser irradiation.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The additive is                  quaternary ammonium salt, polyether, and/or                  sodium polydithiopropane sulfonate.

PN CN109161525-A
TI Promoting proliferation of human bone marrow mesenchymal stem cells, comprises e.g. collecting fresh bone marrow, adding heparin sodium, and normal saline after, centrifuging, and washing obtained intermediate mononuclear cell layer.
AU ZHAO L
   CHEN M
AE HUAIAN KANGYILAI STEM CELLS BIOTECHNOLOG (HUAI-Non-standard)
GA 201906436W
AB    NOVELTY - Promoting proliferation of human bone marrow mesenchymal stem cells, comprises collecting fresh bone marrow, adding heparin sodium for anticoagulation, adding same amount of normal saline after, subjecting Percoll cell separation solution to density gradient centrifugation, washing obtained intermediate mononuclear cell layer with low-sugar DMEM medium and inoculating into petri dish, after adding the complete medium, culturing in 5% carbon dioxide incubator for 24 hours, changing the solution and discarding the cells that are not attached, after every 2-3 days, change the liquid, wait until the cells grow to 90% confluence, digesting with trypsin and subculturing, taking passage cells, digesting to obtain cell suspension, inoculating into culture flask, culturing in complete medium at 37 degrees C under 5% carbon dioxide for 24 hours, replacing with complete medium containing medicine to continue the culture, where the complete medium containing medicine contain isoprene.
   USE - The method is useful for promoting proliferation of human bone marrow mesenchymal stem cells.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses alpha -cedarene, beta -cedarene and isoprene, which promotes proliferation of human bone marrow mesenchymal stem cells, without affecting its multi-directional differentiation ability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for use of isoprene in preparation of medium/medicine for promoting proliferation of human bone marrow mesenchymal stem cells.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The complete medium is DMEM/F12 medium containing 10% FBS.

PT P
PN US2018339326-A1; US10369604-B2
TI Decontaminating soil and/or ground water containing halogenated organic contaminants and metal compound by treating with alkaline aqueous solution, reacting chelated metal with peroxide, and contacting contaminants with hydroxide ions.
AU LUNDY W L
AE LUNDY W L (LUND-Individual)
GA 201894705R
AB    NOVELTY - Soil and/or ground water containing halogenated organic contaminants and a metal compound are decontaminated by treating soil and/or ground water with alkaline aqueous solution having pH of 7-10 and comprising alkaline buffering agent, peroxide, and chelating agent, for chelating agent to chelate the metal of metal compound; (b) reacting chelated metal with peroxide to catalytically convert peroxide to reducing agent comprising hydroxide ions and oxidizing agents comprising hydroxyl radicals; (c) contacting halogenated organic contaminants in soil and/or ground water with hydroxide ions to convert and reduce halogenated organic contaminants through reduction reaction to environmentally safe, non-toxic compounds, where reduction reaction is nucleophilic substitution, and reduction reaction produces carbon dioxide; and (d) penetrating soil and/or ground water with carbon dioxide to diffuse alkaline aqueous solution into soil and/or ground water and generate additional reaction.
   USE - Decontamination of soil and/or ground water containing halogenated organic contaminants and metal compound.
   ADVANTAGE - The hydroxyl ion converts the halogenated organic compound into environmentally safe, non-toxic compounds. Gases that are produced from this reaction, and from the oxidation of organic compounds, are used to diffuse solution into the soil to chelate calcium and magnesium, causing the sodium to be dislodged from the soil and reduce sodicity in the soil.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Compositions: Concentration of peroxide is 0.6-4.5 mol/L. Concentration of chelating agents is 0.03-1.5 mol/L. Buffering agent comprises diammonium in a concentration of 0.2-2 lbs/gallon. Preferred Condition: Temperature of soil and/or ground water rises no more than 10 degrees F. Preferred Processes: Soil and/or ground water are treated with effective amount of alkaline aqueous solution by spraying or injecting the alkaline aqueous solution over or into the soil and/or ground water to be treated, and injecting the solution into areas exhibiting higher concentrations of contaminants. Alkaline aqueous solution is sprayed onto a floor and walls of an excavated area, in an amount of 0.25-2 gal/ft2. Alkaline aqueous solution is injected at a rate of 3-10 gallons per minute. Soil and/or ground water are treated with effective amount of alkaline aqueous solution by spraying or injecting the alkaline aqueous solution over or into the surface to be treated; determining areas not yet reached by the reagent; and spraying or injecting the solution into the areas not yet reached by the reagent. Areas not yet reached by the reagent are determined by observing the evolution of gasses at the surface; and identifying the areas not yet reached by the reagent as those areas known to be contaminated but did not evolve gasses at the surface.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); P43 (Sorting, cleaning, waste disposal (B06, B07, B08, B09).)
MC D04-A01K2; D04-A01P1; D04-B06E; P43-J
