PN CN108751590-A
TI Comprehensive treatment for waste gas and waste            water for production of wood raw material activated            carbon, by adding phosphate to wastewater, inoculating            Chlorella sorokiniana CS-01 cultivar into wastewater,            and performing light culture.
AU LIU B
   ZENG X
   QI G
   CHEN B
   WANG X
   ZENG L
AE CHONGQING FEIYANG ENVIRONMENTAL TECHNOLOGY CO              LTD (CHON-Non-standard)
GA 201891096E
AB    NOVELTY - Comprehensive treatment for waste gas and                waste water for production of wood raw material                activated carbon, involves: (a) adding 0.1-5% (w/v)                phosphate to wood activated carbon production                wastewater, and using ammonia water to adjust the                pH value of the wastewater to 5.0-8.0 to prepare a                microalgae for organic carbon source culture                system; (b) inoculating Chlorella sorokiniana CS-01                cultivar into the wastewater, and performing light                culture at a certain temperature; (c) subjecting                activated carbon production waste gas to dust                removal, combustion, cooling, and tempering                treatment, and then introducing into the culture                system, and the inorganic carbon source carbon                dioxide is supplemented to the cultivating                microalgae culture system; and (d) after a period                of cultivation, separation of microalgae, and                discharging waste water into the municipal sewage                pipe network for subsequent treatment.
   USE - The method is useful for comprehensive                treatment for waste gas and waste water for                production of wood raw material activated carbon                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and environment-friendly,                and has low energy consumption and low cost. The                carbon dioxide production in waste carbon                production is reduced by 90%. The utilization rate                is not greater than 70%, which has good                environmental and social benefits.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The waste water and                  the exhaust gas in step (a) are derived from a                  wood raw material activated carbon production                  process. Preferred Conditions: The culture                  temperature in step (b) is 20-35 degrees C. The                  exhaust gas cooling temperature is 20-35 degrees                  C, gas conditioning treatment to adjust the                  oxygen. The proportion of carbon is 0.5-10%                  (v/v). The cooling temperature of the exhaust gas                  in step (c) is 20-35 degrees C, and the method                  involves adjusting the ratio of oxygen to carbon                  dioxide to 0.5-10 vol.% The microalgae culturing                  time in step (d) is 10-20 days.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The phosphate in the                  step (a) is selected from phosphoric acid,                  potassium hydrogen phosphate, potassium                  dihydrogen phosphate, ammonium dihydrogen                  phosphate, and diammonium hydrogen                  phosphate.

PN CN108659689-A
TI Preparing sorbitol-based non-isocyanate            polyurethane coating involves adding sorbitol-based            epoxy resin to high-pressure reaction vessel, adding            catalyst, and conducting reaction by introducing carbon            dioxide at suitable pressure.
AU QU J
   WU Z
   CHEN R
AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UYSC-C)
   CARPOLY CHEM GROUP CO LTD (CARP-Non-standard)
GA 201883617Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a sorbitol-based non-isocyanate                polyurethane coating involves adding a                sorbitol-based epoxy resin to a high-pressure                reaction vessel, adding a catalyst, and conducting                a reaction by introducing a carbon dioxide at a                pressure of 1.0-4.0 MPa, and reacting at                temperature of 60-150 degrees C for 4-30 hours to                obtain sorbitol-based cyclic carbonate solution.                The catalyst is added in an amount of 0.5-3.0% by                mass of the sorbitol-based epoxy resin, the                catalyst is tetrabutylammonium bromide, lithium                chloride, lithium bromide and tetrabutylammonium                chloride.
   USE - Method for preparing a sorbitol-based                non-isocyanate polyurethane coating.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare a sorbitol-based                non-isocyanate polyurethane coating, which has good                biological degradability high hardness, excellent                impact resistance and chemical resistance,and it is                expected to become an environment-friendly coating                instead of excellent property oftraditional                polyurethane.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The sorbitol cyclic carbonate and diamine                curing agent having diamine curing agent is                ethylene diamine, butanediamine,                hexamethylenediamine, 1,8-octanediamine, sunflower                diamine, 1,12-dodecanediamine, isophorone diamine,                p-phenylenediamine, polyethyleneimine, and                polyether diamine, or polyamine curing agent,                solvent and antifoaming agent are uniformly stirred                at room temperature to form sorbitol-based                non-isocyanate polyurethane coating, spraying,                baking and curing into a film to obtain the desired                product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: The catalyst is added,                  the addition of the sorbitol-based epoxy compound                  to a 0-100% simethylformamide solvent is also                  included. The molar ratio of the primary amine                  curing agent or the polyamine curing agent to the                  carbonate of the primary amine ammonia and the                  sorbitan cyclic carbonate is from 0.9-1.2:1. The                  antifoaming agent is BYK-R605, TEGO Airex 900 and                  TEGO Airex 962, and the antifoaming agent is                  added in an amount of 0.5-1% by mass of the raw                  material. The amount added is 30-100% of the mass                  of the sorbitol cyclic carbonate. The mixture of                  compounds having an epoxy group average                  functionality of 4. The baking curing film                  forming temperature is 80-150 degrees C. The                  sorbitol-based cyclic carbonate solution is a                  pale yellow transparent viscous liquid having a                  storage stability of more than 1 year and a                  viscosity of 9-50 mPa s. The coating film formed                  by the sorbitol-based non-isocyanate polyurethane                  coating has a hardness of 3B-4H, an impact                  resistance of greater than or equal to 50 cm, and                  an adhesion force of 0. The flexibility is 1 mm                  in diameter and the gloss is greater than 88%.                  Preferred Components: The solvent is                  1-ethyl-2-pyrrolidone, dimethyl sulfoxide,                  dimethylformamide, 1-methyl-2-pyrrolidone and                  acetonitrile. The antifoaming agent is BYK-R605,                  TEGO Airex 900 and TEGO Airex 962. The sorbitol                  epoxy resin is obtained by reacting a hexahydric                  alcohol with epichlorohydrin and contains a                  monofunctional, difunctional, trifunctional,                  tetrafunctional, pentafunctional and                  hexafunctional sorbitol epoxy.

TI Gas-based shaft furnace direct reduced iron cooling system comprises cooling gas manufacturing device, cooling gas conveying device, cooling lock bucket, and direct reduction iron storage bin.
AU CHU M
   LI F
   ZHOU Y
   WANG G
   TANG Y
   LIU Z
   WANG J
   LI S
   WANG Z
   LIU Y
   TANG J
AE UNIV NORTHEASTERN (UYDB-C)
   LIAONING HUAXIN IRON & STEEL GENERIC TEC (LIAO-Non-standard)
GA 201871460Q
AB    NOVELTY - Gas-based shaft furnace direct reduced iron cooling system comprises a cooling gas manufacturing device, a cooling gas conveying device, a cooling lock bucket (6), and a direct reduction iron (DRI) storage bin (7), where one end of the cooling gas conveying device is connected to the cooling gas manufacturing device. The other end is connected with the cooling lock, where the discharge opening of the cooling lock bucket is connected to the DRI storage bin. PN CN107694606-A
TI Rhenium-modified covalent triazine pyridine catalyst, is obtained by e.g. taking 2,6-cyano-pyridine as monomer to prepare covalent triazine pyridine frame material, and supporting rhenium containing compound.
AU XU R
   WANG X
   HUANG Y
   CAO R
AE FUJIAN MATERIAL STRUCTURE INST (CAFU-C)
GA 201815533N
AB    NOVELTY - Rhenium-modified covalent triazine pyridine catalyst, is claimed. The catalyst is obtained by taking 2,6-cyano-pyridine as monomer to prepare covalent triazine pyridine frame material using iron heating method, by post-modification method supporting rhenium containing compound on covalent triazine pyridine frame to obtain the catalyst, where the rhenium-containing compound is rhenium (I) pentacarbonyl chloride.
   USE - The rhenium-modified covalent triazine pyridine catalyst is useful for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: has both wide-area light absorption (absorbed edge exceeds 800 nm) and high carbon dioxide adsorption; and coordination of the two catalysts enables the catalyst to exhibit high photocatalytic efficacy; suitable for solid-gas system; solves the problem of poor circulation compared with other catalysts containing antimony.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing rhenium-modified covalent triazine pyridine catalyst, comprising adding 50-200 mg covalent triazine pyridine frame material and 100-400 mg rhenium (I) pentacarbonyl chloride into 50-100 ml methanol solution, under 75-90 degrees C environment, heating the mixture to reflux for 1-3 days, centrifuging at 10,000 revolutions/minute for 3-10 minutes, washing successively with methanol, ethanol and acetone for 1-5 times to obtain rhenium modified covalent triazine pyridine catalyst.

   WEBB S S

GA 201937378L
AB    NOVELTY - A flowable composition comprises titanium dioxide nanoparticles, calcium carbonate nanoparticles, a surfactant(s), and water.
   USE - A flowable composition used for reducing light-sensitive foliar chloroplast movement in a plant leaf (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composition enhances carbon dioxide trapping from the air while increasing oxygen in the atmosphere and diluting concentration of greenhouse gasses.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Component: The titanium dioxide nanoparticles have a size of 10-500 nm. The calcium carbonate nanoparticles have a size of 200 nm to 3 mu m. The surfactant is lecithin, ether carboxylate, alkyl ethoxylate, or silicone. The composition comprises 32.7 wt.% potable water, 0.02 wt.% calcium hypochlorite, 0.5 wt.% Dispersogen PCE (RTM: dispersing agent for inorganic pigments), 1 wt.% Esperse 366 (RTM: natural oil based dispersant), 4 wt.% RDI-S (RTM: Titanium dioxide), 3.5 wt.% Vansil W-50 (RTM: calcium metaxilicate), 54.5 wt.% calcium carbonate, 0.04 wt.% clove oil, 0.27 wt. PMX (RTM: Silicone fluid), 0.15 wt.% Mergal K10N (RTM: water-based preservative), and 0.1 wt.% Mergal 186 (RTM: water-soluble liquid preservative).
   EXAMPLE - A flowable composition was prepared using 31.567 wt.% potable water, 0.014 wt.% calcium hypochlorite, 2.85 wt.% lecithin, 0.4 wt.% sodium carbonate, 61.5 wt.% calcium carbonate, 2.560 wt.% denatured ethyl alcohol, 0.166 wt.% clove oil, 0.135 wt.% Vanzan (RTM: xanthan gum), and 0.808 wt.% magenta cartridge.

TI Insecticidal composition comprises Tanacetum cinerariifolium extract and Agastache rugosa extract.
AU CHAE E S
AE CHAE E S (CHAE-Individual)
GA 2018471649
AB    NOVELTY - Insecticidal composition comprises Tanacetum cinerariifolium extract and Agastache rugosa extract.
   USE - The composition is useful for killing insect (claimed), preferably agricultural insects and hygiene insects.
   ADVANTAGE - The composition is environmentally friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for killing insect, comprising (a) preparing an aqueous solution containing Tanacetum cinerariifolium extract and Agastache rugosa extract, (b) contacting the aqueous solution with carbon dioxide gas sublimated in dry ice to produce a mixed gas, and (c) treating the mixed gas to pests.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The extracts are obtained by extracting Tanacetum cinerariifolium and Agastache rugosa with solvent including water and/or 1-4C lower alcohol. The insecticide composition comprises Tanacetum cinerariifolium extract and Agastache rugosa extract in weight ratio of 1:10-30. The composition is formulated in form of suspension, suspo-emulsion, wettable powder, granular concentrate, microemulsions, wetting agent, granule, powder, emulsion, spraying agent, softener, capsule and/or gel. The composition further comprises smoothing agent which is dispersed by using a carbon dioxide gas as a carrier.
   ACTIVITY - Insecticide. Test details are described but no results given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

AE HENAN ACAD SCI (HENA-Non-standard)
   HENAN CHEM IND RES INST CO LTD (HENA-Non-standard)
GA 202232109H
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a detection analysis device outer shell (1), photo-catalytic reactor (2), carbon dioxide, and water sampler. A water sampler movably clamped in a top aligning clamping groove and an inserting groove. The water simulator is provided with a top alignment clamping block and a square sampling cylinder (402). A side wall of the square sample cylinder is provided on a side wall with a reset L-shaped blocking plate. A carbon dioxide concentration sensor and a waterproof steam barrier net are fixed on a bottom surface of a carbon dioxide detector main body (3).
   USE - Analysis device for detecting total organic carbon (TOC) content in water used for evaluating water quality organic pollution. Can also be used for detecting total organic carbon content in air.
   ADVANTAGE - The analysis device has good detection effect, and is simple, convenient to use, and ensures the accurate data and satisfies the use requirement. The carbon dioxide detector main body is convenient for dismounting, convenient for periodic maintenance, and two sides of the outer shell of the detecting and analyzing device are provided with carbon dioxide detection main body, so that the measured data can form contrast. The water to be detected enters the inner cavity of the reaction chamber, and the UV pipeline generated by the ultraviolet lamp tube works to irradiate the photo-catalytic sheet, so as to react the water in reaction chamber. The organic matter is catalyzed to generate carbon dioxide gas, to measure the concentration of the carbon-dioxide gas in real time by carbon-dioxide detector to realize the detection and analysis of the total organic carbon content.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a sectional view of the analysis device for detecting total organic carbon content in water.

AB    NOVELTY - Use of 1.2-amino phenylboronic acid is claimed                in detecting carbon dioxide content.
   USE - 1.2-amino phenylboronic acid used in detecting                carbon dioxide content, where carbon dioxide is a                raw material of photosynthesis; proper amount of                carbon dioxide content in the atmosphere and the                high carbon dioxide concentration can inhibit the                respiration of the plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide content detection method is                simple and convenient, fast, low cost and has                actual application value.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for detecting 2-amino phenylboronic acid in                detecting carbon dioxide content, involving: adding                the 2-amino phenylboronic acid into the distilled                water, performing ultrasonic oscillation, then                taking out the sample from the ultrasonic                oscillator, mixing uniformly to obtain 2-amino                phenylboronic acid mother liquor;
   adding the 2-amino phenylboronic acid mother                liquor into the distilled water to obtain the                2-amino phenylboronic acid aqueous solution;
   performing fluorescence spectrum test to the                2-amino phenylboronic acid aqueous solution, where                the fluorescence intensity is marked as I0;
   introducing carbondioxide and nitrogen into                the 2-amino phenyl boric acid water solution,                respectively the 2-amino phenyl boric acid solution                after carbon dioxide gas treatment and                Ccarbondioxide-nitrogen gas treatment, and                respectively fluorescence spectrum test to                investigate the fluorescence spectrum properties of                the 2-amino phenylboronic acid after the action of                carbondioxide and nitrogen, where the                carbondioxide/nitrogen mixed gas respectively into                the 2-amino phenylboronic acid aqueous                solution;
   ubjecting sample to fluorescence spectrum                test, where the fluorescence intensity is marked as                It;
   taking the relative fluorescence intensity                (It/I0) as the ordinate, the time is the abscissa                plot;
   obtaining the time needed by the fluorescence                intensity reaching the balance value;
   ubjecting sample to fluorescence spectrum                test; the fluorescence intensity is marked as                I;
   taking the content of the carbon dioxide as                the abscissa;
   drawing the standard curve; introducing the                carbondioxide/nitrogen mixed gas into the 2-amino                phenylboronic acid aqueous solution;
   measuring the fluorescence spectrum of the                solution, substituting -lgI/I0 value into the                standard curve to obtain the calculation value of                the carbondioxide by comparing the calculated value                of carbondioxide and verifying the                reliability.

The                  gas flow rate of carbon dioxide is set to be                  10-90 mL per minute, the ventilation time is set                  to be 2-120 seconds, nitrogen gas flow rate is                  set to 10-90 mL per minute and the ventilation                  time is set to 1-60 minutes. The total flow speed                  of the gas is set to be 10-90 mL per minute and                  the ventilation time is set to be 0-200 seconds.                  The relative fluorescence intensity is the                  relative fluorescence intensity with the fixed                  wavelength of 350-410 nanometer. The ventilation                  time is set to be 30-120 seconds. The above                  fluorescence spectrum test conditions includes                  the temperature is 5-40degrees Celsius, the                  excitation wavelength is 280-320 nanometer, the                  excitation slit is 2.5-5 nanometer, the emitting                  slit is 2.5-10 nanometer.

PN CN109534287-A
TI Producing ammonia synthesis gas involves sending            blast furnace gas to first pressure swing adsorption            process to remove carbon dioxide, and which is called            decarbonation blast furnace gas.
AU DA N
   ZHONG J
   YANG H
AE YANG H (YANG-Individual)
GA 201931760V
AB    NOVELTY - Producing ammonia synthesis gas involves                sending the blast furnace gas to the first pressure                swing adsorption process to remove carbon dioxide,                which is called decarbonation blast furnace gas.                The concentration of carbon dioxide is less than 3                wt.%. The concentrated carbon dioxide gas having                the carbon dioxide gas concentration 93.5 wt.% or                more is sent to other processes as chemical raw                materials. The second pressure swing adsorption                process separated and purified the carbon dioxide                in the carbon dioxide removal blast furnace gas.                The copper molecular sieve purified the carbon                monoxide, which is called blast furnace                purification gas, where the concentration of carbon                dioxide is less than equal to 10 ppm and the blast                furnace purification gas is used as the synthetic                ammonia raw material gas, and at the same time, the                concentration of carbon monoxide reached 65 wt.% or                more. Tthe concentrated carbon monoxide gas is sent                to the coke oven gas cabinet.
   USE - Method for producing ammonia synthesis                gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The concentrated carbon monoxide gas is sent                to the coke oven gas cabinet. The concentrated                carbon monoxide gas separated from the coke oven                gas mixed blast furnace gas is desulfurized,                compressed, converted and transformed, and is                called desulfurization shift gas. The third                pressure swing adsorption process removed carbon                dioxide in the shift gas to obtain decarbonization                shift gas to obtain concentrated carbon dioxide,                when the concentration reacheds 3 wt.% or less, the                concentrated carbon dioxide gas with a                concentration of carbon dioxide gas of 93.5 wt.% or                more is sent to other processes as a chemical raw                material, and the decarburization shift gas is sent                to the fourth pressure swing adsorption process to                separate methane. The mixed concentration of                methane and CnHm is higher than 75 wt.%. The                methane-rich gas is sent to other processes as a                chemical raw material, and at the same time, the                methane-depleted gas having a methane concentration                of less than 3 wt.% is obtained. The                methane-depleted gas is continued to sent to the                fifth pressure swing adsorption purification                methane. The 5A molecular sieve purified the carbon                monoxide to obtain the decarbonation gas, and the                carbon monoxide gas. The concentration of carbon                dioxide in the medium is less than equal to 10 ppm                and the concentration of methane is inevitably                lower than 0.1 wt.%, which is used as ammonia                synthesis gas II. The desorption process obtains                carbon monoxide-rich and methane-gas-fired                combustion systems. The two ammonia synthesis gas                are mixed into ammonia synthesis total, raw                material gas, by adjusting the first pressure swing                adsorption blast furnace gas volume, where the                molar ratioof the synthesis hydrogen and nitrogen                is 3:1.

PN CN109438244-A; CN109438244-B
TI Producing glycerol carbonate by photothermal reaction of glycerol and carbon dioxide comprises e.g. introducing visible light source radiation as an energy source, and modifying the composite semiconductor zinc tungstate-zinc oxide.
AU HE D
   LIU J
AE UNIV TSINGHUA (UYQI-C)
GA 201925396D
AB    NOVELTY - Producing glycerol carbonate by photothermal reaction of glycerol and carbon dioxide comprises introducing visible light source radiation as an energy source into a reaction system for producing glycerol carbonate from glycerol and carbon dioxide, modifying the composite semiconductor zinc tungstate-zinc oxide by nano-silver with local surface plasmon effect to obtain gold/zinc tungstate-zinc oxide, using Au/ZnWO4-ZnO as a catalyst, using glycerol and carbon dioxide as raw materials, using organic solvents under mild warming and pressure and visible radiation conditions, catalyzing the direct synthesis of glycerol carbonate from glycerol and carbon dioxide, the method comprises adding a certain amount of Au/ZnWO4-ZnO catalyst, glycerol and an organic solvent to the high-pressure reaction vessel with the light source radiation window, sealing the reactor, replacing the air in the reactor with carbon dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for producing glycerol carbonate by photothermal reaction of glycerol and carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the yield of the target product glycerol carbonate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing glycerol carbonate by photothermal reaction of glycerol and carbon dioxide comprises introducing visible light source radiation as an energy source into a reaction system for producing glycerol carbonate from glycerol and carbon dioxide, modifying the composite semiconductor zinc tungstate-zinc oxide by nano-silver with local surface plasmon effect to obtain silver/ zinc tungstate-zinc oxide, using Au/ZnWO4-ZnO as a catalyst, using glycerol and carbon dioxide as raw materials, using organic solvents under mild warming and pressure and visible radiation conditions, catalyzing the direct synthesis of glycerol carbonate from glycerol and carbon dioxide, the method comprises (i) adding a certain amount of Au/ZnWO4-ZnO catalyst, glycerol and an organic solvent to the high-pressure reaction vessel with the light source radiation window, (ii) sealing the reactor, replacing the air in the reactor with carbon dioxide, charging carbon dioxide into the kettle to a certain pressure, and placing the reactor in a heating furnace and heating to the reaction temperature, (iii) turning on the xenon lamp at the same time when the target reaction temperature is reached, adjusting the xenon lamp power and UV filter, the light source illuminates the reaction liquid in the reaction vessel through a light transmission window, maintaining a constant stirring speed and allow the reaction to proceed under light conditions for a certain period of time, cooling the reaction kettle in an ice water bath, discharging the residual high pressure gas in the reaction vessel to normal pressure, opening the kettle, adding certain amount of internal standard, mixing uniformly, the solid-liquid mixture in the kettle is taken out, centrifugal separation, and using the liquid phase quantitatively analyzed using gas chromatography.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The source of visible light radiation into the reaction of glycerol and carbon dioxide. The light source radiation power used is 250 W. The used catalyst is Au/ZnWO4-ZnO. The mass percentage of gold on the Au/ZnWO4-ZnO photocatalyst is 2%. The organic solvent is N, N-dimethylformamide. The pressurizing condition is 5-8 MPa. The heating condition is 140-150 degrees C. The reaction time is 6-12 hours.

TI Aqueous polyurethane gloss oil resin useful for            coating a paper product package comprises carbon            dioxide copolymer polyols, diisocyanate, chain extender            crosslinking agent, hydrophilic chain extenders,            organometallic catalyst, deionized water, salt-forming            agent, and solvent.
AU LEI H
   LIN J
   WANG G
AE HUBEI WEIQING MACROMOLECULE TECHNOLOGY (HUBE-Non-standard)
GA 202272773R
AB    NOVELTY - An aqueous polyurethane gloss oil resin                comprises 200-400 pts. wt. carbon dioxide copolymer                polyol, 100-300 pts. wt. diisocyanate, 5-30 pts.                wt. chain extension cross-linking agent, 10-30 pts.                wt. hydrophilic chain extender, 0.01-1 pts. wt.                organometallic catalyst, 400-700 pts. wt. deionized                water, 10-30 pts. wt. salt-forming agent, and                61-160 pts. wt. solvent.
   USE - The aqueous polyurethane gloss oil resin is                useful for coating a paper product package, gold                and silver cards and leather.
   ADVANTAGE - The emulsion film exhibits higher hardness and                high gloss after film formation; and has short                reaction time, low reaction temperature, and                simplified reaction process. The aqueous                polyurethane resin has excellent adhesion, no                sticking back at room temperature, good scratch                resistance, high hardness, high brightness, ad                emulsion film exhibits fullness. The film has                certain toughness, high solid content, low                viscosity and good stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                method for a method for preparing aqueous                polyurethane gloss oil resin comprising (1) adding                200-400 pts. wt. carbon dioxide copolymer polyol                into the reaction kettle, and dehydrating at                100degrees Celsius for 1 hour to obtain anhydrous                carbon dioxide copolymer polyol, and (2) adding                anhydrous carbon dioxide copolymer polyol to the                reaction kettle, heating up to 70degrees Celsius,                adding 5-30 pts. wt. chain extension crosslinking                agent and 10-50 pts. wt. solvent, stirring for 10                minutes, adding 100-200 pts. wt. diisocyanate,                stirring for 10 minutes, adding 0.01-1 pts. wt.                organometallic catalyst, heating to 80-85degrees                Celsius, stirring for 10 minutes, adding 1-10 pts.                wt. chain-extending crosslinking agent, 10-30 pts.                wt. hydrophilic chain-extending cross-linking agent                and 50-100 pts. wt. solvent, stirring for 3 hours,                cooling to 70degrees Celsius, adding 10-30 pts. wt.                salt-forming agent and 1-10 pts. wt. solvent,                stirring for 10 minutes, then adding 400-700 pts.                wt. deionized water while stirring at high speed,                and stirring for 30 minutes, cooling to room                temperature, and filtering through a 200-mesh                filter to obtain a water-based polyurethane varnish                resin.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The aqueous                  polyurethane varnish resin comprises 200 pts. wt.                  carbon dioxide copolymer polyol 130 pts. wt.                  diisocyanate, 14 pts. wt. chain extension                  cross-linking agent, 20 pts. wt. hydrophilic                  chain extender, 0.01 pts. wt. organometallic                  catalyst, 550 pts. wt. deionized water, 15 pts.                  wt. salt-forming agent, 65 pts. wt. solvent,                  where the carbon dioxide copolymer polyol is                  polyester diol. The carbon dioxide copolymer                  polyol has a molecular weight of 2000 g/mol and a                  hydroxy functionality of 2.Preferred Components: The salt-forming agent                  is diethanolamine, triethylamine and/or                  dimethylethanolamine, preferably triethylamine.                  The hydrophilic chain extender is 1,4                  butanediol-2-sodium sulfonate,                  dimethylolpropionic acid, dimethylolbutyric acid                  and/or 1,2-propanediol-3-sodium sulfonate,                  preferably dimethylolpropionic acid. The chain                  extension crosslinking agent is diethylene                  glycol, dipropylene glycol, ethylene glycol,                  butanediol, 1,6-hexanediol, 1,3-propanediol,                  di-n-butylamine, ethylenediamine,                  1,2-propanediol, 1,4-hydroxymethylcyclohexane,                  trimethylhexamethylenediamine and/or                  tetraethylenepentamine, preferably ethylene                  glycol. The molecular weight of the carbon                  dioxide copolymer polyol is 1000-4000 g/mol. The                  hydroxy functionality of the carbon dioxide                  copolymer polyol is 2-4. The diisocyanate is                  isophorone diisocyanate, toluene diisocyanate,                  diphenylmethane diisocyanate, liquefied                  diphenylmethane diisocyanate, hexamethylene                  diisocyanate and/or hydrogenated diphenylmethane                  diisocyanate, preferably diphenylmethane                  diisocyanate. The solvent is dimethylacetamide,                  N-methylpyrrolidone and/or acetone, preferably                  dimethylacetamide. The organometallic catalyst is                  stannous isooctate, stannous octoate, dibutyltin                  dilaurate and/or organic bismuth, preferably                  dibutyltin dilaurate.

PN DE102020119337-A1; DE102020119337-A9
TI Device for production of plants and fungi, has            first control unit that outputs signal for operation of            first unit, so that it transports            carbon-dioxide-enriched air from mushroom cultivation            facility into plant cultivation facility.
AU JANY N
AE JANY N (JANY-Individual)
GA 202216313E
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a first control unit (9) that                receives and processes the measurement data from                the carbon-dioxide and oxygen sensors (14,15,24,25)                and sends signals to the first unit for                transporting gases (16). The first control unit                sends a signal to operate the at least outputs a                first unit so that it transports oxygen-enriched                air from the plant cultivation facility into the                mushroom cultivation facility. The first control                unit is set up in such a way that if the                carbon-dioxide concentration falls below a                predetermined level or the oxygen concentration                exceeds a predetermined level in the atmosphere in                a second room (12) in a plant breeding facility                (1), it outputs a signal for the operation of the                at least one first unit, so that this transports                carbon-dioxide-enriched air from a mushroom                cultivation facility (2) into the plant cultivation                facility.
   USE - Device for production of plants and                fungi.
   ADVANTAGE - The growth conditions are monitored and                adapted to the growth requirements, while at the                same time a cycle is to be created in order to                optimally further utilize the metabolic products                from plant and fungus cultivation. Due to the low                density, the oxygen can advantageously flow from                below through the growth area of the mushrooms and                thus comes into contact with as much of the surface                of the mushroom fruit bodies as possible. The                lignin-rich biomass is pasteurized or sterilized                before it is used as a substrate in the mushroom                cultivation system, in order to avoid unwanted                germs or microorganisms being introduced into the                mushroom cultivation area. The aqueous solution                rich in macromolecules can thus be reused and does                not have to be arranged of at high cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a method for cultivating biomass;                and
   (2) an apparatus for cultivating                biomass.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                device.
   Plant breeding facility (1)
   Mushroom cultivation facility (2)
   First control unit (9)
   Second room (12)
   Carbon-dioxide and oxygen sensors                (14,15,24,25)
   Gases (16)

PN WO2022003114-A1
TI Electrochemically reducing carbon dioxide into formic acid in gas phase recirculation system, comprises introducing gas or liquid comprising source of hydrogen into anode compartment of reactor cell, and introducing gas comprising carbon dioxide.
AU MARTENS J
   RONGE J
   THIJS B
AE UNIV KATHOLIEKE LEUVEN (UYLN-C)
GA 202202643G
AB    NOVELTY - Electrochemically reducing carbon dioxide (CO2) into formic acid (HCOOH) in a gas phase recirculation system, involves: (a) introducing a gas or liquid comprising a source of H into the anode compartment of a reactor cell, and introducing a gas comprising CO2 in the cathode compartment of the reactor cell, where the cathode compartment of the reactor cell comprises an electrocatalyst, thus reducing CO2 to HCOOH, with generating hydrogen (H2) and carbon monoxide (CO) as byproducts, (b) removing HCOOH from the system, (c) removing portion of the gas obtained after removal of HCOOH in step (b) from the system via an adjustable regulator valve set at a pressure which is lower than the reactor pressure, and (d) supplementing the remaining portion of the gas in the system obtained after step (c) with carbon dioxide, and introducing the carbon dioxide supplemented gas to the reactor cell.
   USE - The method is useful for electrochemically reducing CO2 into formic acid in gas phase recirculation system (claimed); and for producing formic acid useful as feedstock in the production of chemical building blocks, synthetic fuels and building materials, and as fuel cells in fuel cells or indirectly in hydrogen fuel cells, as a chemical product, as antibacterial agent, or as syngas equivalent for production of chemicals.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient production of formic acid with high conversion efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a continuous closed loop gas phase recirculation system for electrochemically reducing CO2 into formic acid, by the method above, comprising arranged along the direction of the movement of the gas: a reaction cell comprising an electrocatalyst in the cathode compartment of the reaction cell, connected via the anode compartment to an inlet for a gas comprising a source of H and connected via the cathode compartment to an inlet for a gas comprising CO2, a separator for collecting formic acid, an outlet for condensed formic acid, and an adjustable regulator valve connected to an outlet set at a pressure which is lower than the reactor pressure.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Method: The method further involves transferring the gas comprising the products obtained in step (a) to a separator removing HCOOH from the system. The flow and temperature of the gas are adjusted such that -75% of the formic acid content in the gas is condensed. Preferably, greater than or equal to 25 cycles are performed. Preferably, less than or equal to 25% of the gas is released via the regulator valve. The gas released from the regulatory valve is reintroduced into the reactor cell as a source of H. Preferred Components: The source of hydrogen (H) is selected from H2 gas, a gas comprising H2, ammonia (NH3) gas, a gas comprising NH3, liquid water or water vapor. The cathode catalyst shows activity towards CO2 reduction and selectivity towards formic acid production, and is selected from a single metal, a bimetallic electrocatalyst or a trimetallic electrocatalyst. One of the metals is p- and d-block metal catalyst such as tin (Sn), copper (Cu), palladium (Pd), lead (Pb), bismuth (Bi) or silver (Ag). Preferred Conditions: CO2 reduction in step (a) is performed at a pressure of 1-100 bar (g), or 100-500 bar. The CO2 reduction in step (a) is performed at 0-100 degrees C. The CO2 inlet pressure in step (a) is 1-500 bar(g).
DC D22 (Sterilising, bandages, dressing and skin protection agents - including sterilising agents (other than for food), sutures, plaster casts, bioactive prostheses, contact lenses, diapers, animal litter, timber, preservatives, disinfectants, bactericidal detergents, deodorants, insect repellent compounds, moth proofers, sheep dip (A61L).); E17 (Other aliphatics.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).); X16 (Electrochemical Storage)

PT P
PN CN113464230-A; CN215333025-U
TI Supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2) cycle power generation system, has back pressure adjusting unit comprising discharging tank connected with air storage tank by main main pressure regulating valve using automatic adjusting performance of valve.
AU YAO M
   MENG Y
   GAO J
   WU G
   XIN Z
   LI X
   ZHANG L
   LIN Y
   LI H
   SONG X
AE XIAN THERMAL POWER RES INST CO LTD (CHHN-C)
GA 2021B7796Y
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a working medium supplementing unit that is provided with a liquid carbon dioxide storage tank (1). A CO2 working medium of the liquid CO2 storage tank enters a gasifier (3) after being pressurized by a booster pump (2). A main circulating working unit is provided with an inlet tank (5). A working medium with low temperature and high pressure enters a boiler (9) after heating by a two-stage heat regenerator to continuously absorb heat. A discharging tank (12) is connected with an air storage tank by a main main pressure regulating valve (13). A back pressure adjusting unit (14) stabilizes pressure in the discharge tank at a certain value by using automatic adjusting performance of the main pressure regulation valve.
   USE - Supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2) cycle power generation system with adjustable back pressure.
   ADVANTAGE - The back pressure unit is provided with three groups of pressure regulating valve, which can satisfy the supercritical carbon dioxide circulating machine set before starting the working substance filling, CO2 turbine rotating speed, with load variable working condition full working condition operation period of the adjusting and control. When the machine set is operated in high load stage, the set back pressure can be controlled at a stable value by a voltage stabilizing adjusting valve which is more beneficial to the adjustment of the rotating speed. At the same time, by adjusting the back pressure, the exhaust tank pressure is stable at a certain boundary value which can avoid the system caused by excessive circulation resistance caused by high exhaust pressure and further limit the capacity of large load, reducing the operation risk, avoiding occurrence of tripping accident.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - AN INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a control method of supercritical CO2 cycle power generation system with adjustable back pressure.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow schematic structure of a supercritical CO2 cycle power generation system.
   Liquid carbon dioxide storage tank (1)
   Booster pump (2)
   Gasifier (3)
   Inlet tank (5)
   Boiler (9)
   Discharging tank (12)
   Main main pressure regulating valve (13)
   Back pressure adjusting unit (14)
DC Q51 (Internal combustion engines, reciprocating engines, rotary engines (F01, F02B,D,F,G,M,N,P)); Q52 (Reaction engines: external combustion; gas turbines; rockets (F01D, F02C, F02K, F02M)); X11 (Power Generation and High Power Machines)
MC Q51-C; Q52-A; X11-A10A

PT P
PN CN110122885-A; CN110122885-B
TI Protein powder comprises granulated sugar, soybean protein powder, oligosaccharide fructose, galactooligosaccharides, lemon juice, vitamin and trace element.
AU GAO J
   GAO Y
AE NANTONG SUN-GREEN BIO-TECH CO LTD (NANT-Non-standard)
   NANTONG SUN-GREEN BIO-TECH CO LTD (NANT-Non-standard)
GA 2019728674
AB    NOVELTY - Protein powder comprises granulated sugar, soybean protein powder, oligosaccharide fructose, galactooligosaccharides, lemon juice, vitamin and trace element, where the protein powder is prepared by (a) uniformly mixing the above-mentioned materials, (b) pouring the mixed material into sealed stirring tank, slowly heating and vacuuming, (c) carrying out high-pressure aeration stirring mixing, injecting high-pressure neutral carbon dioxide gas, heating, stropping stirring and stopping heating, (d) slowly adjusting the high air pressure in step (c) and expanding carbon dioxide dissolved, (e) carrying out high-pressure stewing, cooling solidifying the sugar substance, cooling the sealed stirring tank and reducing the temperature, (f) taking out the coarse powder, taking out the cured and cracked protein powder block and crushing the protein powder block into particles, and (g) bagging the crushed protein powder.
   USE - Protein powder.
   ADVANTAGE - The protein powder has improved adjustability and stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Protein powder comprises 10-20 pts. wt. granulated sugar, 80-90 pts. wt. soybean protein powder, 0.1-1 pt. wt. oligosaccharide fructose, 0.5-2 pts. wt. galactooligosaccharides, 1-5 pts. wt. lemon juice, vitamin and trace element, where the protein powder is prepared by (a) uniform mixing the above-mentioned materials into an intensive mixer, (b) pouring the mixed material into a sealed stirring tank, slowly heating at 140-160 degrees C and continuously vacuuming in the heating process, (c) carrying out high-pressure aeration stirring mixing, injecting high-pressure neutral carbon dioxide gas to the sealed stirring tank, heating at 50-60 degrees C in the process of brewing carbon dioxide gas, stropping stirring for 20-30 minutes and stopping heating, (d) slowly adjusting the high air pressure in step (c) and expanding carbon dioxide dissolved in the middle of the syrup in the process of reducing the air pressure to generate bubbles, (e) carrying out high-pressure stewing, cooling solidifying the sugar substance, maintaining the pressure in step (d), circulating and cooling the sealed stirring tank and reducing the temperature to 20-25 degrees C for 10-30 minutes, (f) taking out the coarse powder, opening the sealed stirring tank, taking out the cured and cracked protein powder block and crushing the protein powder block into particles with the size of 100-10 mesh, and (g) weighing and bagging the crushed protein powder.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The pressure in step (c) is carried out at 35-40 atm. The pressure in step (d) is carried out at 25-30 atm. Preferred Composition: The vitamin comprises 30-100 mg vitamin A, 0.32-2.2 mg vitamin B2, 0.25-2.8 mg vitamin B6, 50-400 mg vitamin C, 15-500 mg vitamin D and 0.6-3.2 mg vitamin E in each 100 g of protein powder. The trace element comprises 50-200 mg calcium, 10-200 mg iron, 0.5-5 mg zinc and 0.5-5.5 mg selenium in each 100 g of protein powder.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)
MC D03-F06; D03-H01G1; D03-H01T2C; D03-P

PT P
PN CN110075265-A
TI Preparing Justicia gendarussa essential oil comprises cleaning Justicia gendarussa, washing, cutting, heat-pumping, drying, crushing, performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction and molecular distillation purification and packaging.
AU PAN L
   WANG J
   BAI X
AE HAINAN BOGUCAO BIOLOGICAL ENG CO LTD (HAIN-Non-standard)
GA 2019703080
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing Justicia gendarussa essential oil comprises (1) selecting clean and Justicia gendarussa and cleaning for 6-10 minutes; (2) washing Justicia gendarussa, controlling moisture content, uniformly cutting Justicia gendarussa by cutting machine with length of cutting section of 2-5 cm; (3) heat-pumping Justicia gendarussa using heat pump low-temperature dryer and drying for 6-8 hours; (4) controlling working temperature of crusher and crushing dried Justicia gendarussa; (5) performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of Justicia gendarussa to obtain extract-A; (6) performing molecular distillation purification of extract-A to obtain extract-B; (7) performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of rosemary to obtain oil-C; (8) adding extract-B into 0.05-0.2 vol.% oil-C, emulsifying, homogenizing and filtering to obtain filtrate-D; (9) adding ginger oil; and (10) filtering E, pasteurizing and packaging.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing Justicia gendarussa essential oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The Justicia gendarussa essential oil has high medicinal ingredient concentration, good therapeutic effect and is convenient to use and carry.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing Justicia gendarussa essential oil comprises (1) selecting clean and Justicia gendarussa and cleaning for 6-10 minutes; (2) washing Justicia gendarussa, controlling moisture content, uniformly cutting Justicia gendarussa by cutting machine with length of cutting section of 2-5 cm; (3) heat-pumping Justicia gendarussa using heat pump low-temperature dryer and drying for 6-8 hours; (4) controlling working temperature of crusher at 10-35 degrees C and crushing dried Justicia gendarussa to 50-60 meshes; (5) performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of Justicia gendarussa to obtain extract-A; (6) performing molecular distillation purification of extract-A to obtain extract-B; (7) performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of rosemary to obtain oil-C; (8) adding extract-B into 0.05-0.2 vol.% oil-C, emulsifying and homogenizing and filtering to obtain filtrate-D; (9) adding 0.02-0.25 vol.% ginger oil into filtrate-D to obtain filtrate-E; and (10) filtering E to obtain filtrate-F, pasteurizing and packaging filtrate-F.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (5), performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction is on Justicia gendarussa at 45-55 degrees C with pressure of 25-35 Mpa and flow rate of 18-22 l for 45-60 minutes. In the step (6), performing molecular distillation purification on of Justicia gendarussa for 15-20 minutes. In the step (7), performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction on rosemary at 50-65 degrees C with pressure of 30-45 Mpa, flow rate of 20-24 l for 50-70 minutes. In the step (8), emulsifying and homogenizing using homogenizer for 50-65 minutes and filtering using double-vacuum filter. In the step (10), filtering and pasteurizing filtrate-E by two-phase centrifuge and sterilizing for 25-35 minutes.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.)

PT P
PN CN108938682-A; CN108938682-B
TI Extraction method of cedaric acid and cedarol, involves crushing and sieving dried Sabina squamata, adding entrainer containing ethanol and formic acid solution, soaking, extracting, collecting supercritical extract, drying and storing.
AU ZHENG J
   OU W
   WAN C
AE HUAIAN ANLAI BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HUAI-Non-standard)
GA 2018A14521
AB    NOVELTY - The supercritical carbon dioxide extraction method of cedaric acid and cedarol involves (a) crushing and sieving dried Sabina squamata to obtain S.squamata powder, (b) weighing 100 g S.squamata powder in an extraction tank, adding 100 mL entrainer containing ethanol and 15% formic acid aqueous solution in a volume ratio of 95:5, and soaking, where the extraction temperature is 45 degrees C, the extraction pressure is 30 MPa, the extraction method is performed by adopting pre-dip-static extraction and dynamic extraction by adding the raw materials to entrainer for pre-leaching, performing static extraction for 1 hour under the set extraction temperature and pressure conditions, dynamically extracting for 2 hours, adding entrainer through an entrainer pump after starting dynamic extraction, where the total amount of the entrainer is 300 mL, and (e) collecting supercritical extract, drying and storing.
   USE - Supercritical carbon dioxide extraction method of cedaric acid and cedarol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of cedaric acid and cedarol with improved extraction rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The supercritical carbon dioxide extraction method of cedaric acid and cedarol involves (a) crushing and sieving dried Sabina squamata to obtain S.squamata powder, (b) weighing 100 g S.squamata powder in an extraction tank, adding 100 mL entrainer containing ethanol and 15% formic acid aqueous solution in a volume ratio of 95:5, and soaking at room temperature for 10 hours, where the extraction temperature is 45 degrees C, the extraction pressure is 30 MPa, the carbon dioxide flow rate is 12 L/h, the separation kettle temperature is 78 degrees C and the pressure is 6.2 MPa, the extraction method is performed by adopting pre-dip-static extraction and dynamic extraction by adding the raw materials to entrainer for pre-leaching, performing static extraction for 1 hour under the set extraction temperature and pressure conditions, dynamically extracting for 2 hours, adding entrainer through an entrainer pump after starting dynamic extraction, where the total amount of the entrainer is 300 mL, and (e) collecting supercritical extract, drying and storing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Property: The step (a) involves crushing and sieving by a 50-mesh sieve.

PN CN108689804-A
TI Preparing cedar wood alcohol, comprises e.g. crushing impurity removed moso bamboo leaf, sieving, placing in supercritical carbon dioxide extraction kettle, extracting using absolute ethanol, dissolving in ethyl acetate, and concentrating.
AU GUO Q
AE GUO Q (GUOQ-Individual)
GA 201885074F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing cedar wood alcohol, comprises (a) crushing impurity removed moso bamboo leaf, and sieving with 40-mesh sieve, (b) placing the sieved bamboo leaves in supercritical carbon dioxide extraction kettle and extracting using absolute ethanol at 30-60 degrees C under pressure of 10-35 MPa and carbon dioxide flow rate of 1-5 ml/g crude drug/min for 60-150 minutes, and recovering solvent under reduced pressure, (c) dissolving obtained oil in ethyl acetate, separating ethyl acetate layer by using AB-8 type, H-103 type, D-101 type, DA-201 type, and DS-401 type macroporous adsorption resin column, eluting with 60-90% different concentrations of aqueous ethanol solution, and collecting, and (d) concentrating, separating by a silica gel column, eluting using chloroform-methanol-underwater layer solution as eluent, collecting, adding activated carbon, filtering, concentrating filtrate, and performing recrystallization by adding ethanol aqueous solution.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing cedar wood alcohol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides cedar wood alcohol having high purity, and is easy to realize industrial amplification.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing cedar wood alcohol, comprises (a) crushing impurity removed moso bamboo leaf, and sieving with 40-mesh sieve, (b) placing the sieved bamboo leaves in supercritical carbon dioxide extraction kettle and extracting using absolute ethanol at 30-60 degrees C under pressure of 10-35 MPa and carbon dioxide flow rate of 1-5 (preferably 0.2-2) ml/g crude drug/min for 60-150 minutes, and recovering solvent under reduced pressure, (c) dissolving obtained oil in ethyl acetate, separating ethyl acetate layer by using AB-8 type, H-103 type, D-101 type, DA-201 type, and DS-401 type macroporous adsorption resin column, eluting with 60-90% different concentrations of aqueous ethanol solution, and collecting 3-8 column volumes of eluent, and (d) concentrating the eluent, separating the eluted concentrate by a silica gel column, eluting using chloroform-methanol-underwater layer solution as an eluent, collecting the eluent, adding activated carbon to decolorize, filtering, concentrating filtrate, and performing recrystallization by adding ethanol aqueous solution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The extraction is carried out at 50 degrees C under pressure of 20 MPa, carbon dioxide flow rate of 2.2 ml/gx minute, and absolute ethanol dioxide flow rate of 0.8ml/min/g crude for 100 minutes. Preferred Components: The chloroform, methanol and water are in weight ratio of 5:2:1. The ethanol aqueous solution comprises 50-90 vol.% ethanol.

PT P
PN KR2022082569-A
TI Method for controlling ventilation device using air monitor, involves determining air volume of ventilation device according to carbon dioxide concentration measured by air monitor when time elapses after change in indoor illuminance value is detected.
AU PARK J Y
   KIM K D
AE KYUNGDONG NAVIEN CO LTD (KYNG-C)
GA 2022813728
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves determining (S110) whether a user is present in a room by detecting an indoor illuminance value and carbon dioxide concentration measured by an air monitor and determining an air volume of a ventilation device according to the presence of the user. The air volume of the ventilation device for a time is determined (S120) when a user is present in the room and a change in the indoor illuminance value is detected in the user access determination step. The air volume of the ventilation device is determined (S130) according to the carbon dioxide concentration measured by the air monitor when the time elapses after a change in the indoor illuminance value is detected in the occupant sleep entry step.
   USE - Method for controlling ventilation device (claimed) using air monitor.
   ADVANTAGE - The air quality is maintained comfortably by adjusting the air volume of the ventilation device in response to the presence or sleeping state of the user. The user's experience is improved by improving the quality of user's sleep.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a ventilation device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating a method for controlling ventilation device using air monitor. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Step for determining whether user is in room by detecting indoor illuminance value and carbon dioxide concentration measured by air monitor, and determining air volume of ventilation device according to presence of user (S110)
   Step for determining air volume of ventilation device for a time when user is present in room and change in indoor illuminance value is detected in user access determination step (S120)
   Step for determining air volume of ventilation device according to carbon dioxide concentration measured by air monitor when time elapses after change in indoor illuminance value is detected in occupant sleep entry step (S130)
   Step for returning user access determination step when change in current measurement value of indoor illuminance value is detected to be higher than previous measurement value within time from entering occupant deep sleep phase (S140)

The times of said step-down stage and the                step-up stage are the same. The recycling gas of                the x-th recycling gas boosting stage is derived                from the recycling gas discharged from other groups                of maintaining kettles during the x-th step-down                stage process, where x is less than or equal to n,                n and x are positive integers, M is a positive                integer greater than or equal to 2.

The stable                operation of carbon dioxide curing building                material product can be achieved. The effective                utilization rate of the curing kettle can be                increased. The tail gas is absorbed by the                absorption system, the content of the carbon                dioxide in the exhaust gas is reduced. The carbon                emission is reduced and the CO2 gas is efficiently                used. The automatic cutting and cutting of each                group of curing kettle through PLC or DCS                programmed design method is realized, so as to                realize flexible adjustment and fault cutting of                the cured kettle.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system                for programming carbon dioxide maintenance building                material product.

TI Liquid carbon dioxide production device comprises pre-cooling system input with raw material gas and refrigerant, rectifying system comprising rectifying tower provided with rectifying tower re-boiler and multi-stage rectifying tower top condensing and separating system.
AU HE D
AE CHONGQING CHUAN MAO CHEM TECHNOLOGY CO (CHON-Non-standard)
GA 202242016D
AB    NOVELTY - A device comprises a rectifying system and a super-cooling system. The pre-cooling system is input with a raw material gas and a refrigerant. The raw material gas is pre-cooled and separated by the refrigerant. The rectifying system comprises a rectifying tower (8) and a multi-stage rectifying tower top condensing and separating system. The rectifying tower is provided with a rectifying tower re-boiler (7). The multi-stage rectifying tower top condensing and separating system for condensing the gas discharged from the rectifying tower and gas-liquid separating. The raw material gas after separating out the liquid component through the precooling system is input to the middle part of the rectifying tower after passing through the dryer (6). The gas phase refrigerant generated by heat transfer of the super-cooling system flows to the low-pressure refrigerant compressor. The liquid dioxide after cooling is flowed to the liquid dioxide storage tank.
   USE - Production device for producing carbon dioxide liquid used as oil displacement agent, injecting oil layer, reducing crude oil viscosity, reducing residual oil saturation and improving crude oil recovery rate.
   ADVANTAGE - The production device for preparing liquid carbon dioxide with energy comprehensive utilization is more reasonable, the refrigeration system efficiency is higher, and the energy consumption is lower. The refrigerant evaporation temperature can be controlled by the gas phase control valve, so as to control the pre-cooling temperature of the raw material gas, effectively avoiding the condition that the pre cooling temperature of gas is too low or too low, the precipitation of water is frozen and the device or the pipeline is damaged. The regenerative energy consumption of the drying tower is saved. The service life of drying adsorbent is prolonged.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for production method of carbon dioxide liquid.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a structural schematic diagram of the liquid carbon dioxide production device (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   Dryer (6)
   Rectifying tower re-boiler (7)
   Rectifying tower (8)

PN CN113955021-A
TI Vacuum pipe system for conveying liquid cargo in            liquid cargo storage ship i.e. transport fluid cargo            ship, has insulating layer covered on outer side of            outer pipe, where insulating layer prevents outer pipe            from heat absorbing in external environment.
AU ZHOU X
   ZHANG R
   ZHANG D
AE JIANGNAN SHIPYARD GROUP CO LTD (CHSB-C)
GA 202219284R
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a baffle plate (11) fixed on                two ends of an outer pipe (10). An inner pipe (12)                is fixed in the outer pipe along axial direction of                the outer pipe. A liquid cargo (3) is arranged in                the inner pipe. The inner pipe, the baffle and the                outer pipe are combined into a sealing cavity (13),                where the sealing cavity is filled with carbon                dioxide gas, and the liquid cargo boiling point is                lower than carbon dioxide boiling point, and the                sealing cavity air pressure value is less than a                standard atmospheric pressure value. A heat                insulating layer is covered on an outer side of the                outer pipe, where the insulating layer prevents the                outer pipe from heat absorbing in external                environment.
   USE - Vacuum pipe system for conveying liquid cargo                in a liquid cargo storage ship (claimed) i.e. a                transport fluid cargo ship.
   ADVANTAGE - The system solves problems of low speed and                inconvenient use of an existing device for forming                vacuum. The system allows the carbon dioxide gas to                directly condense into solid carbon dioxide in                temperature and air pressure environment, so that                the sealing cavity air pressure value can be                quickly reduced, and the vacuum degree of the                sealed cavity can be increased to a predetermined                pressure value, thus improving speed of a sealed                cavity forming a vacuum, which is more convenient                than a common vacuumizing method. The carbon                dioxide is condensed into solid state by the                gaseous state, thus avoiding problem of                liquefaction when using other gases and gathering                and condensing at a bottom of the seal cavity. The                insulating layer prevents the outer pipe from                absorbing heat from the external environment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                method for manufactruing a vacuum pipe.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a side cross-sectional view                of a vacuum pipe system of a vacuum pipe                system.

   Liquid cargo (3)

   Heat insulating layer (14)

PN CN113088046-A; CN113088046-B; WO2022205791-A1
TI Graphene modified polyethylene terephthalate film            material, comprises graphite, ethylene glycol as raw            materials, and modified graphene prepared by ball            milling in supercritical carbon dioxide            atmosphere.
AU WU D
   CHEN D
   WU T
AE UNIV FUJIAN NORMAL QUANGANG PETROCHEMICA (UYFJ-C)
   UNIV FUJIAN NORMAL QUANGANG PETROCHEMICA (UYFJ-C)
GA 202180275T
AB    NOVELTY - Graphene modified polyethylene terephthalate                (PET) film material comprises graphite and ethylene                glycol as raw materials, modified graphene prepared                by ball milling in supercritical carbon dioxide                atmosphere, and then modified graphene is combined                with additional terephthalic acid, ethylene glycol                is synthesized by in-situ polymerization to obtain                graphene-modified PET composite pellets, and                graphene-modified PET film material is obtained                through multilayer stretch extrusion system.
   USE - Graphene modified polyethylene terephthalate                film material.
   ADVANTAGE - The graphene-modified polyethylene                terephthalate film material helps in improving                barrier effect of graphene in PET matrix, and                improves overall barrier performance of                graphene-modified PET film material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing graphene-modified PET film material,                which involves:
   (A) adding 1 pts. wt. graphite, more than 10                pts. wt. ethylene glycol, and appropriate amount of                catalyst into ball milling tank, then sealing tank                with flange screws, and flushing tank with                inflatable device to remove carbon dioxide gas from                air in tank, then flushing another carbon dioxide                gas into tank until pressure reaches 7.3-15                MegaPascal, and closing valve, at same time                additional carbon dioxide provided in supercritical                state; obtaining modified graphene by ball milling                at 32-100 degrees C, and rotating speed of 300-600                rotation/minute for 24-72 hours;
   (B) adding modified graphene solution                infour-necked bottle, heating to 200-265 degrees C                under nitrogen protection to react, and refluxing                for 1-2 hours to get first solution; taking another                appropriate amount of terephthalic acid, and                ethylene glycol, then heating up to 200 degrees C                under protection of nitrogen to dissolve all                terephthalic acid to obtain second solution; adding                second solution to first solution, continuing                reaction for 2-4 hours, heating up to 270-290                degrees C, and proceeding with polycondensation                reaction under vacuum conditions, where reaction                completed after 2-4 hours, and protecting under                nitrogen; discharging, solidifying, and pelletizing                to obtain graphene-modified PET composite pellets;                and
   (C) passing graphene-modified PET composite                material pellets through multilayer stretch                extrusion system to obtain graphene-modified PET                film material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The graphite is one or                  more of flake graphite, expanded graphite, block                  graphite, and amorphous graphite. The catalyst is                  one or two of antimony acetate (Sb(OAc)3), cobalt                  acetate (Co(OAc)2), antimony oxide (Sb2O3), and                  phosphoric acid (H3PO4).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The mass fraction of                  modified graphene in graphene-modified PET                  composite material pellets is 0.1-1.0 wt.%. The                  mass ratio of ethylene glycol/graphene is greater                  than 10. The molar ratio of ethylene glycol to                  monomer of terephthalic acid is 1.05-1.3.

(including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).)

PN KR2209381-B1
TI Portable device for detecting and analyzing atmospheric environment of packaging container for distributing agricultural products, has sensor module measuring concentration of oxygen and carbon dioxide by inhaling atmosphere of container.
AU HAN M U
   KANG S Y
AE ALLSENSING CO LTD (ALLS-Non-standard)
   TRITONNET CO LTD (TRIT-Non-standard)
GA 2021130973
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a housing formed as an enclosure and provided with a display unit, an input button and a suction tube. The suction tube is provided with a probe that is penetrated into an inner side of a packaging container. A sensor module is provided in an inner side of the housing to measure concentration of oxygen and carbon dioxide by inhaling atmosphere in the inner side of the container by the probe and the suction tube by driving a pump by the input of the input button. The sensor module comprises a main body (300) including a pump mounting portion that is mounted on a side surface of the pump. A sensor mounting groove (370) is inserted with an oxygen sensor (600) and a carbon dioxide sensor (700). An inlet hole (331) is penetrated from an upper portion of the body to the sensor mounting groove. An exhaust hole (341) is penetrated from a top side of the main body to the sensor mounting groove. A communication groove (350) is formed between the inlet hole and the exhaust hole.
   USE - Portable device for detecting and analyzing atmospheric environment of a packaging container for distributing agricultural products.
   ADVANTAGE - The device can insert the probe into the packaging container to inhale the atmosphere in the inner side of the packaging container so as to retain the inhaled atmosphere in the communication groove to prevent oxygen and carbon dioxide. The device can measure and analyze concentration in quick and stable manner so as to easily and accurately measure the atmospheric environment of the packaging container without time and space constraints during storage or distribution, thus managing food in the inner side of the packaging container in a fresh state.

PT P
PN DE102020114334-A1; US2020386678-A1; JP2020201097-A
TI Exhaust gas analyzer for analyzing exhaust gas emitted by internal combustion engine of test vehicle, has oxygen concentration calculating unit to calculate oxygen concentration in gas from carbon dioxide concentration.
AU MORI Y
   YABUSHITA H
AE HORIBA LTD (HORB-C)
   HORIBA LTD (HORB-C)
   HORIBA LTD (HORB-C)
GA 2020C31135
AB    NOVELTY - The exhaust gas analyzer (100) has an infrared light source for irradiating exhaust gas with infrared light. A photodetector detects infrared light after passing through the exhaust gas. A carbon dioxide concentration calculating unit calculates carbon dioxide concentration in the exhaust gas based on a detection signal obtained by the photodetector. An oxygen concentration calculating unit calculates oxygen concentration in exhaust gas from the carbon dioxide concentration using a combustion reaction equation for fuel combustion and an exhaust gas recirculation (EGR) rate in an exhaust gas recirculation system (EGR) or a value related to the EGR rate. The oxygen concentration calculating unit calculates the oxygen concentration in the exhaust gas using a conversion coefficient obtained from a ratio of an oxygen coefficient and a carbon dioxide coefficient in the combustion reaction equation.
   USE - Exhaust gas analyzer for analyzing exhaust gas emitted by internal combustion engine of test vehicle.
   ADVANTAGE - The concentration of oxygen that does not absorb infrared light is measured without using an oxygen meter separately, since the oxygen concentration calculating unit determines the oxygen concentration in the exhaust gas using the combustion reaction equation for fuel combustion and the EGR rate calculated in the exhaust gas recirculation system or the value associated with the EGR rate. Thus, the exhaust gas analyzer is downsized to achieve cost reduction and improved portability. An accurate calculation of the oxygen concentration is realized because not only the combustion reaction equation for fuel combustion, but also the EGR rate in the exhaust gas recirculation system of the internal combustion engine or the value associated with the EGR rate is used.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) an exhaust gas analysis method for analyzing exhaust gas emitted from the internal combustion engine;
   (2) a program for use in the exhaust gas analyzer; and
   (3) an engine learning apparatus for use in irradiating exhaust gas emitted from the internal combustion engine.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view illustrating an exhaust measurement system.
   Exhaust gas analyzer (100)
   Exhaust gas measurement system (200)
   Roller test bench (300)
   Gas sampling device (400)
   Exhaust gas recirculation system (EGR)

PT P
PN KR2016923-B1
TI Gas insulated switchgear using carbon dioxide mixed gas has first and third switch units that are set symmetrically about second switch unit and flow guide which is arranged so that mixed gas ejecting direction is set in opposite direction.
AU KIM J G
AE SEONDO ELECTRIC CO LTD (SEON-Non-standard)
GA 201978271P
AB    NOVELTY - The switchgear in which the ejection direction of the mixed gas is switched to the second outlet so that the mixed gas is discharged to the tank, when the power is cut off in a second switch unit (202). A second flow guide (90b) is configured to guide the flow of the mixed gas so that a cover (12) is not directly affected. The three switch units (201-203) are arranged in a straight line, when viewed from the front of a tank (10). The first switch unit and the third switch unit are arranged symmetrically about the second switch unit, when viewed from the front or side of the tank. The flow guides (90a,90b) are arranged so that the ejecting direction of the mixed gas is in the opposite direction, when viewed from the front of the tank. The first angle formed by the first and the second flow guides is not less than 15 degrees and not more than 30 degree.
   USE - Gas insulated switchgear using carbon dioxide mixed gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The global warming index is significantly lowered by replacing the gas medium with carbon dioxide in the existing sulfur hexafluoride gas. The insulation performance comparable to sulfur hexafluoride gas is realized by charging carbon dioxide at high pressure of 8 bar or more. The volume of the mixed gas with high heat instantly is increased when the arc is generated, and the arc is prevented from transitioning to the tank and generating ground fault when the mixed gas moves and ejects. The interference of the arc airflow between one switch unit and another switch unit is prevented or the influence of each other is minimized, in the switchgear through which the three-phase current is flowed. The generated spark discharge is eliminated and insulated when the power is cut off.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view showing the gas insulated switchgear using carbon dioxide mixed gas.
   Tank (10)
   Cover (12)
   Drive unit (14)
   Flow guides (90a,90b)
   Switch units (201-203)
