PT P
PN CN110152678-A; CN110152678-B
TI Preparing nano-copper-ytterbium alloy catalyst for electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to methane, comprises e.g. introducing inert gas into reaction vessel containing solvent, and dissolving metal salt and polyvinylpyrrolidone.
AU WANG Z
AE INNER MONGOLIA YUANCI NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (INNE-Non-standard)
   INNER MONGOLIA YUANCI NEW MATERIAL TECHN (INNE-Non-standard)
GA 2019761481
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nano-copper-ytterbium alloy catalyst for electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to methane, comprises e.g. (i) continuously introducing inert gas into reaction vessel (1) containing solvent, (ii) turning on magnetic stirrer in the above reaction system, and heating to control the reaction temperature at 200-400 degrees C, (iii) adding solvent as described above into a reaction vessel (2), dissolving metal salt and polyvinylpyrrolidone (K30) in the above solvent, (iv) adding the substance of the reaction vessel (2) into vessel (1) under stirring conditions, controlling the reaction temperature at 200-400 degrees C, where the reaction time is 5-20 minutes, (v) rapidly cooling the material in the reaction vessel (1), centrifuging, filtering the obtained solid, washing many times with deionized water and absolute ethanol, and vacuum drying at room temperature to obtain final product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing nano-copper-ytterbium alloy catalyst for electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to methane.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has simple process, low cost, and is safe and reliable. The copper-based alloy catalyst: improves electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide performance, and improves reduction selectivity of methane; provides some reference for the rational design of electrocatalyst.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing nano-copper-ytterbium alloy catalyst for electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to methane, comprises (i) continuously introducing inert gas into reaction vessel (1) containing solvent, where the solvent compound containing alcoholic hydroxy group, (ii) turning on magnetic stirrer in the above reaction system, and heating to control the reaction temperature at 200-400 degrees C, (iii) adding solvent as described above into a reaction vessel (2), dissolving metal salt and polyvinylpyrrolidone (K30) in the above solvent, the metal salt is copper nitrate, copper acetate, cerium nitrate and/or cerium acetate, (iv) adding the substance of the reaction vessel (2) into vessel (1) under stirring conditions, controlling the reaction temperature at 200-400 degrees C, the reaction time is 5-20 minutes, (v) rapidly cooling the material in the reaction vessel (1), centrifuging, filtering the obtained solid, washing many times with deionized water and absolute ethanol, and vacuum drying at room temperature to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solvent containing alcoholic hydroxy group in the step (i) is diethylene glycol or triethylene glycol. The amount of solvent in the step (i) is 10-30 ml. The input ratio of metal salt in the step (iii) is different, and the ratio between copper salt and cerium salt is 1:1, 4:1 or 9:1, respectively. The molar concentration of polyvinylpyrrolidone (K30) in the step (iii) is 0.5, 1, or 1.5 mol/l, respectively. Preferred Method: The heating to the highest temperature point in the step (ii) is 230, 250, 280 and 300 degrees C, respectively. The reaction time in the step (iv) is 5, 10, 15 or 20 minutes, respectively.

A combustion apparatus is provided in the supply flow path for guiding the combustion exhaust gas having passed through the liquid to the downstream side of the liquid storage tank. The liquid storage tank has a shielding portion (31) that is arranged at a position overlapping the discharge port (33) of the combustion exhaust gas in the liquid storage tank as viewed vertically from above the liquid surface of the liquid and from the vertical direction. The shielding portion has an inclined surface (31A) which is opposed to the discharge port and inclined with respect to the horizontal direction. The through-hole is not formed in the inclined surface.
   USE - Carbon dioxide application apparatus for supplying carbon dioxide contained in combustion exhaust gas into greenhouse.

The plate is arranged at the portion of an inner surface of the liquid storage tank. PN KR2019007989-A; KR1959132-B1
TI Performance and profitability evaluation method for separating membrane system, involves injecting mixing gas with carbon dioxide and hydrocarbon gas in separating membrane system, where cell is provided with separation film.
AU HWANG S
   KWON S J
AE UNIV INHA RES & BUSINESS FOUND (INHI-C)
GA 201911114T
AB    NOVELTY - The performance and profitability evaluation method involves injecting (S100) the mixing gas with the carbon dioxide and the hydrocarbon gas in a separating membrane system, where a cell is provided with a separation film. The speed of the component for passing the separation film from the mixing gas is calculated (S200), where the material balance is calculated (S300) according to a cell sequence, which is used for showing the gas flow of both sides of the separation film. The carbon dioxide removal ratio is provided for calculating the viscosity of the mixing gas, where the permeate side pressure drop for passing the separation film is calculated (S400). The performance of the separating membrane system is calculated, where the profitability of the separating membrane system is calculated. The area ratio of multiple separation films is diversified, where the profitability of the separating membrane system is calculated (S700).
   USE - Performance and profitability evaluation method for a separating membrane system (Claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The mixing gas is injected with the carbon dioxide and the hydrocarbon gas in a separating membrane system, where a cell is provided with a separation film, and hence ensures analyzing the optimal condition in an effective manner and distinguishes the optimal configuration according to the economical efficiency and the separation performance objective.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a separating membrane system with a separation film composite unit.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart of a performance and profitability evaluation method for a separating membrane system. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   Injecting the mixing gas with the carbon dioxide and the hydrocarbon gas in a separating membrane system (S100)
   Calculating the speed of a component for passing a separation film from the mixing gas (S200)
   Calculating the material balance according to a cell sequence used in order to show the gas flow of both sides of a separation film (S300)
   Calculating the permeate side pressure drop for passing a separation film (S400)
   Calculating the viscosity of the mixing gas (S500)
   Calculating the profitability of a separating membrane system (S700)

AB    NOVELTY - The automatic drip irrigation device includes a water storage tank (1), a water valve assembly (2), a drip tube (3), a mounting column (4), and a gas supply assembly (5). The water valve assembly includes a water valve housing (21), a water valve spool (22), a return spring (23), a first air bag (24), and a heat receiving casing (25). The gas supply assembly includes a concentrating casing (51), a support frame (52), a carbon ring (53), a driving assembly (54), a gas delivery pipe (55), and a gas storage chamber (56). The right side of gas pipe is located on right side of inner side of carbon ring. The gas storage chamber is a square casing. The gas storage chamber is fixed to upper right side of mounting column. The gas storage chamber is wrapped outside drip pipe. The storage through hole is formed in middle portion and a right side of lower side of gas chamber. A through hole on right side of gas storage chamber communicates with gas pipe.
   USE - Automatic drip irrigation device for selectively supplying carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The device is convenient to use, and saves resources. The device can adaptively adjust the drip rate according to the intensity of sunshine. The device can supply plant carbon dioxide with sufficient sunlight and irrigation water to facilitate plant growth. The drip water flow of device can be fully mixed with the carbon dioxide generated by the gas supply component when passing through the drip tube, thus bringing more carbon dioxide to the plant, which is better to the photosynthesis of the plant. The device can operate automatically without power resources, saves energy and environmental protection.

TI Patient monitoring device e.g. capnography device            monitors concentration or partial pressure of carbon            dioxide (C02), has arterial blood oxygen saturation            (Sp02) index which is indicative of patient well-being            from Sp02.
AU ORR J A
   BREWER L M
   GUNNESON P B
AE KONINK PHILIPS NV (PHIG-C)
   KONINK PHILIPS NV (PHIG-C)
   KONINK PHILIPS NV (PHIG-C)
   KONINK PHILIPS NV (PHIG-C)
GA 2018566016
AB    NOVELTY - The patient monitoring device has a capnograph                device (10), a pulse oximeter (70) and an                electronic processor (84) programmed to generate a                capnography index (50) indicative of patient                well-being from a capnogram measured by the                capnograph device. An arterial blood oxygen                saturation (Sp02) index (90) is generated which is                indicative of patient well-being from Sp02 (72)                measured by the pulse oximeter. A patient safety                index (92) is computed from the capnography index                and the Sp02 index. Several clinical warnings                determined in portion on the patient safety index                are computed. A display component (82) is                configured to display one of the computed several                clinical warnings. The capnography index is                generated from information derived from the                capnogram including a concentration or partial                pressure of carbon dioxide and respiratory rate                information.
   USE - Patient monitoring device e.g. pulse oximeter,                capnography device monitors concentration or                partial pressure of carbon dioxide (C02) in                respiratory gases.
   ADVANTAGE - The capnograph device is provided, whose                output more effectively assesses patient                respiratory health. More accurate respiratory                system status information is provided from                capnogram data. The recreational well-being illness                (RWI) designed to help medical personnel evaluate                the overall respiratory well-being of the patient.                The capnography monitoring is provided with output                that is more readily comprehended and acted upon by                medical personnel. The uncertainty and confusion                associated with capnometry and arterial blood                oxygen saturation monitoring are reduced, by                combining the capnography and arterial blood oxygen                saturation data.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a method for monitoring concentration of                carbon dioxide (C02); and
   (2) a non-transitory readable medium storing                program for monitoring concentration of carbon                dioxide (C02).
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                patient monitor that provides the patient safety                index (PSI) generated from the respiratory                well-being index (RWI) generated by the capnography                device and a blood oxygenation level generated by                the pulse oximeter.
   Capnograph device (10)
   Capnography index (50)
   Pulse oximeter (70)
   Arterial blood oxygen saturation (72)
   Display component (82)
   Electronic processor (84)
   Arterial blood oxygen saturation index                (90)
   Patient safety index (92)

PN CN107988133-A
TI Domestication cultivation of hydrogen autotrophic denitrification bacteria involves inoculating sludge into a reaction container filled with sodium nitrate aqueous, introducing hydrogen and carbon dioxide mixed gas, and shake culturing.
AU LI H
   HAN Y
   JIANG M
   SUN J
   JIANG L
   LIN H
   ZENG H
   LI X
   DONG K
   MO L
AE UNIV GUILIN TECHNOLOGY (UYGI-C)
GA 201839382V
AB    NOVELTY - Domestication cultivation of hydrogen autotrophic denitrification bacteria comprises standing anaerobic sludge in sewage treatment plant denitrification tank to form solid-liquid critical surface, and taking 0-1 cm solid-liquid critical surface as sludge; inoculating the sludge into a reaction container filled with sodium nitrate aqueous solution as domestication culture medium to obtain a reaction liquid; introducing hydrogen and carbon dioxide mixed gas to exhaust the dissolved oxygen in the reaction liquid, and shake culturing to obtain a mixed solution; inoculating mixed solution with 10% inoculum size into another domestication culture medium containing 0.1-0.5 mg/L bromate anion and 0.1-0.6 mg/L perchlorate anion, introducing 0.04-0.05 MPa hydrogen and carbon dioxide mixed gas, and shake culturing at constant temperature to obtain culture liquid; and inoculating culture liquid into the same domestication culture medium in fourth step, and repeatedly culturing for 4-6 times.
   USE - Method for domestication cultivating hydrogen autotrophic denitrification bacteria used for simultaneously degrading bromate anion and perchlorate anion in water (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is efficient and clean.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Domestication cultivation of hydrogen autotrophic denitrification bacteria comprises standing anaerobic sludge in sewage treatment plant denitrification tank to form solid-liquid critical surface, and taking 0-1 cm solid-liquid critical surface as sludge; inoculating the sludge into a reaction container filled with sodium nitrate aqueous solution as domestication culture medium to obtain a reaction liquid; introducing hydrogen and carbon dioxide mixed gas to exhaust the dissolved oxygen in the reaction liquid, and shake culturing to obtain a mixed solution; inoculating mixed solution with 10% inoculum size into another domestication culture medium containing 0.1-0.5 mg/L bromate anion and 0.1-0.6 mg/L perchlorate anion, introducing 0.04-0.05 MPa hydrogen and carbon dioxide mixed gas, and shake culturing at constant temperature to obtain culture liquid; and inoculating culture liquid with 10% inoculum size into the same domestication culture medium in fourth step, and repeatedly culturing for 4-6 times.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: Shake culture is done at 30 plus minus 1 degrees C with 160 revolutions/minute speed.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).)

PT P
PN CN112178672-A
TI Coupled variable temperature adsorption carbon trapping power station system, has flue, carbon capture adsorption tower, power generation turbine system, working medium compression system and product gas recycling system.
AU WANG C
   JIN L
   ZHOU L
   GAO X
   CHE D
AE UNIV XIAN JIAOTONG (UYXJ-C)
GA 202107378U
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a coal burning boiler main body, a flue, a carbon capture adsorption tower, a power generation turbine system, a working medium compression system and a product gas recycling system. The carbon capture adsorption tower is provided with the coal burning boiler main body and the flue, and an adsorbent. The power generation turbine system and the working medium compression system are attached with each other for burning to heat supercritical carbon dioxide. The product gas recycling system is utilized for compressing high purity carbon dioxide.
   USE - The power station system is used for trapping carbon by coupling temperature change adsorption.
   ADVANTAGE - The system fully uses low-grade heat, improves whole energy utilization rate, effectively relieves problem of over-high smoke temperature, and greatly reduces discharge of carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The system comprises a coal burning boiler main body, a flue, a carbon capture adsorption tower, a working medium compression system, a power generation turbine system, and a product gas recycling system. The carbon capture adsorption tower comprises an adsorbent, the coal burning boiler main body and the flue. The carbon capture adsorption tower, the power generation turbine system and the working medium compression system are respectively connected, for burning to heat supercritical carbon dioxide, and generating flue gas containing carbon dioxide, and discharging the flue gas through the flue. The flue uses the flue gas of middle temperature section to pre-heat the secondary air needed by combustion of the coal burning boiler. At the same time, the flue gas of the middle temperature section is provided to the carbon capture adsorption tower, as the first desorption heat source of the carbon capture adsorption tower. The power generation turbine system takes the supercritical carbon dioxide as working substance to generate power. The pressure of the working medium supercritical carbon dioxide after power generation is reduced to be working medium exhaust gas, and the working medium exhaust gas is sent to the working medium compression system. The working medium compression system compresses the working medium exhaust gas, to generate high pressure working medium supercritical carbon dioxide, heat the high pressure working medium supercritical carbon dioxide, and then send into the coal burning boiler main body. The carbon capture adsorption tower receives the flue gas discharged from the flue, trapping carbon dioxide in the flue gas by physical temperature change adsorption method. The carbon capture adsorption tower uses part of the working medium exhaust gas at the high temperature side outlet of the high temperature regenerator in the working medium compression system as the second desorption heat source. The carbon capture adsorption tower uses the first desorption heat source and the second desorption heat source, heating the adsorbent desorption to obtain high purity carbon dioxide, and sending the high purity carbon dioxide into the product gas recycling system. The product gas recycling system is used for compressing the high purity carbon dioxide, cooling and sending to the subsequent processing device, at the same time, the main compressor in the working medium compression system extracting part of the compression, and cooling the high-purity carbon dioxide to its own inlet, so as to prevent the main compressor in generating two-phase state. Part of the working medium supercritical carbon dioxide generated by the main compressor heats the product gas by waste heat of compressed high-purity carbon dioxide in the system. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for trapping carbon by coupling temperature change adsorption.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing is a flow diagram of the coupling variable temperature adsorption carbon capture power station system (Drawing includes non-English language text).

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); Q73 (Combustion equipment/processes (F23)); X11 (Power Generation and High Power Machines)

TI Combined recovery of phosphate and nitrogen from            sewage sludge, optionally biological waste, involves            optionally disintegrating sewage sludge, drying,            condensing ammonia-rich vapors, alkalizing, treating            sewage sludge ash, separating acid-insoluble component,            recycling and purifying.
AU LEBEK M
AE REMONDIS AQUA GMBH & CO KG (REMO-Non-standard)
GA 2022A6583J
AB    NOVELTY - Combined recovery of phosphate and nitrogen                from sewage sludge involves (1A1) optionally                disintegrating sewage sludge, (1A2) drying the                sewage sludge, resulting in vapors rich in ammonia                and dried sewage sludge, (1B1) optionally                condensing the ammonia-rich vapors from the step                (1A2), (1B2) optionally alkalizing the condensed                ammonia-rich vapors from the step (1B1) with                release of ammonia, (1D2) incinerating the dried                sewage sludge from step (1A2) to sewage sludge ash,                (1D3) treating the sewage sludge ash, (1D5)                optionally recycling at least portion of the                phosphoric acid-containing liquid from the step                (1D4) for use in the step (1D3), (1D6) purifying                the phosphoric acid-containing liquid, (1D7)                optionally concentrating at least portion of the                purified phosphoric acid-containing liquid from the                step (1D6) so that phosphoric acid is recovered and                separated, and (1E) converting the condensed                ammonia-rich vapors.
   USE - Combined recovery of phosphate and nitrogen                from sewage sludge, optionally biological waste                (claimed) in the form of aqueous liquid phase,                liquid, jelly and fermentation residue from biogas                plant. Uses include but are not limited to run-off                water (rainwater), liquid discharged and collected                from wastewater treatment, storage and disposal                facility, domestic waste water from toilets (faecal                or black water), sanitary facilities, kitchens and                washing machines (washing or gray water), companies                that discharge into the public sewage system                (commercial or industrial waste water), heated                water from cooling systems and wastewater treatment                plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is economical, enables combined                recovery of phosphate and nitrogen from sewage                sludge, optionally biological waste with excellent                recovery effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Combined recovery of phosphate and nitrogen                from sewage sludge involves (1A1) optionally                disintegrating sewage sludge, (1A2) drying the                sewage sludge, resulting in vapors rich in ammonia                and dried sewage sludge, (1B1) optionally                condensing the ammonia-rich vapors from the step                (1A2), (1B2) optionally alkalizing the condensed                ammonia-rich vapors from the step (1B1) with                release of ammonia, and expulsion of the ammonia                with heating and/or by applying a reduced pressure                and/or with the aid of a air or steam flow (ammonia                stripping 1), (1D2) incinerating the dried sewage                sludge from step (1A2) to sewage sludge ash, (1D3)                treating the sewage sludge ash from the step (1D2)                with phosphoric acid, (1D4) separating the                acid-insoluble component of the treated sewage                sludge ash from the step (1D3) so that an                acid-insoluble component and a filtrate or                supernatant in the form of a phosphoric                acid-containing liquid are produced, (1D5)                optionally recycling at least portion of the                phosphoric acid-containing liquid from the step                (1D4) for use in the step (1D3), (1D6) purifying                the phosphoric acid-containing liquid from the step                (1D4) by adding sulfuric acid to the phosphoric                acid-containing liquid from the step (1D4) so that                a calcium sulfate precipitate is recovered and                separated, and/or by applying by ion exchange or                liquid-liquid extraction, resulting in a purified                phosphoric acid-containing liquid, (1D7) optionally                concentrating at least portion of the purified                phosphoric acid-containing liquid from the step                (1D6) so that phosphoric acid is recovered and                separated, and (1E) converting the condensed                ammonia-rich vapors from the step (1B1) and/or the                ammonia obtained in the step (1B2) with phosphoric                acid in the form of the liquid containing                phosphoric acid from the step (1D4), the purified                liquid containing phosphoric acid from the step                (1D6) and/or the phosphoric acid from the step                (1D7) to obtain ammonium phosphate compound and                optionally separating off the obtained ammonium                phosphate compound. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is                included for combined recovery of phosphate and                nitrogen from sewage sludge and biological waste,                which involves (2A1) optionally disintegrating                sewage sludge, (2A2) drying the sewage sludge,                resulting in ammonia-rich vapors and dried sewage                sludge, (2B1) optionally condensing the                ammonia-rich vapors from the step (2A2), (2B2)                optionally alkalizing the condensed ammonia-rich                vapors from the step (2B1) with release of ammonia,                and driving out the ammonia with heating and/or by                applying the vacuum and/or with the aid of an air                or steam stream (ammonia stripping 2), (2C1)                optionally separating the solids of the biological                waste from the liquid phase, (2C2) introducing                carbon dioxide gas under increased pressure or                supercritical carbon dioxide into the liquid phase                of the biological waste in order to dissolve                particulate-bond phosphates, (2C3) reducing the                carbon-carbon content in the liquid phase from step                (2C2) by acidifying the liquid phase and driving                off dissolved carbon dioxide and/or carbon dioxide                bond as carbonate, (2C4) alkalizing the liquid                phase from the step (2C3) with the release of                ammonia and expulsion of the ammonia with heating                and/or by applying a vacuum and/or with the aid of                an air or steam stream (ammonia stripping 3), (2C5)                performing precipitation and separation of calcium                phosphate from the liquid phase from the step                (2C4), (2D1) admixing the precipitated and                separated calcium phosphate from the step (2C5) to                the dried sewage sludge from the step (2A), (2D2)                incinerating the mixture of precipitated and                separated calcium phosphate from the step (2C4) and                dried sewage sludge from the step (2A) to sewage                sludge ash, (2D3) treating the sludge ash from the                step (2D2) with phosphoric acid, (2D4) separating                the acid-insoluble portion of the treated sewage                sludge ash from the step (2D3) to produce an                acid-insoluble portion and a filtrate or                supernatant in the form of a phosphoric                acid-containing liquid, (2D5) optionally recycling                at least portion of the phosphoric acid-containing                liquid from step (2D4) for used in the step (2D3),                (2D6) purifying the phosphoric acid-containing                liquid from the step (2D4) by adding sulfuric acid                to the phosphoric acid-containing liquid from the                step (2D4) so that a calcium sulfate precipitate is                recovered and separated, and/or by applying ion                exchange or Liquid-liquid extraction, resulting in                a purified liquid containing phosphoric acid, (2D7)                optionally concentrating at least portion of the                purified phosphoric acid-containing liquid from                step (2D6) so that phosphoric acid is recovered and                separated, (2E) reacting the condensed ammonia-rich                vapors from the step (2B1), and/or the ammonia                obtained in the step (2B2) and the ammonia obtained                in the step (2C4) with phosphoric acid in the form                of the phosphoric acid-containing liquid from the                step (2D4), the purified phosphoric acid-containing                liquid from step (2D6) and/or the phosphoric acid                from step (2D7) to obtain ammonium phosphate                compound, and optionally separating off the                obtained ammonium phosphate compound.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The method is                  performed by using at least 10%, preferably                  40-60% phosphoric acid-containing liquid.                  Preferred Process: In the step (1A1) or (2A1),                  the disintegration of the sewage sludge is                  carried out before the step (1A2) or (2A2), by                  mechanical treatment, for example using a ball                  mill, by treatment using ultrasound and/or at                  elevated pressure and temperature. In the step                  (1B2) or (2B2), the alkalizing of the condensed                  ammonia-rich vapors from the step (1B1) or (2B1)                  is carried out by addition of sodium hydroxide,                  optionally with further addition of calcium oxide                  or calcium hydroxide(lime milk), and/or using air                  or steam jet. In the step (1D7) or (2D7),                  concentrating at least portion of the purified                  phosphoric acid-containing liquid from the step                  (2D6) so that phosphoric acid is recovered and                  separated is carried out, preferably by                  evaporation. In the step (1D6) or (2D6), the                  purification of the phosphoric acid-containing                  liquid from the step (1D4) or (2D4) by adding                  sulfuric acid and purifying by using ion exchange                  or liquid-liquid extraction, preferably ion                  exchange. In the step (1E), the ammonia obtained                  in the step (1B2) is reacted with phosphoric acid                  or in the (2E) the ammonia obtained in the step                  (2B2) and in the step (2C4) is reacted with                  phosphoric acid. In the step (2C1), separating                  the solids of the biological waste from the                  liquid phase carried out before the step (2C2),                  preferably by centrifugation.

PT P
PN CN114887078-A
TI Preparation of magnetic metal organic framework            medicine carrying system based on supercritical dipping            by dissolving drug molecule in supercritical carbon            dioxide to carry drug into metal organic framework            pores of ferrosoferric oxide loaded metal organic            framework composite material.
AU GU N
   LI N
   WANG Q
   JIAO Z
AE UNIV SOUTHEAST (UYSE-C)
GA 2022A7807G
AB    NOVELTY - A method for preparing magnetic metal organic                framework (MOF) medicine carrying system based on                supercritical dipping comprises forming                supercritical carbon dioxide in a reaction kettle                under the condition of temperature and pressure,                dissolving the drug molecule in the supercritical                carbon dioxide and the supercritical carbon dioxide                carries the drug molecules into the MOF pores of                ferrosoferric oxide loaded MOF composite material                to obtain the product.
   USE - The method is used for preparing magnetic                metal organic framework medicine carrying system                based on supercritical dipping.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses supercritical carbon dioxide                as the drug-carrying medium which has low viscosity                and high diffusivity hence the drug molecules are                carried easily and diffuses into the pores of the                MOF effectively; the supercritical carbon dioxide                enlarges the pores of the MOF hence ensures high                drug loading compared with the traditional drug                loading technology using organic solvent; the                organic solvent needs to be removed by subsequent                purification steps, while supercritical carbon                dioxide can easily be removed by decompression to                obtain the MOF drug loading system. The method is                economical and environmentally friendly and has no                residual problem hence does not require a                purification step, and at the same time, all the                drugs soluble in supercritical carbon dioxide can                be loaded into the MOF material and the loading                capacity is high.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method specifically                  comprises (S1) putting the ferrosoferric oxide                  loaded MOF composite material and the drug                  molecule in a dialysis bag into a reaction                  kettle, introducing supercritical carbon dioxide                  into the reaction kettle, discharging the air and                  (S2) continuously introducing supercritical                  carbon dioxide into the reaction kettle, heating,                  when the temperature and pressure in the reaction                  kettle reaches a predetermined value, starting                  the stirring device, slowly releasing pressure                  after reaction to obtain the product, where the                  temperature in the reaction kettle is maintained                  at 40-60&#8451;, the pressure is maintained                  at 16-25 MPa and the reaction is carried out for                  3-6 hours, and the pressure release rate is                  maintained at 1-4 MPa/minute. The preparation                  method of the ferrosoferric oxide loaded MOF                  composite material comprises dispersing the                  ferrosoferric oxide nanoparticles into a mixed                  solution of 1,3,5-benzenetricarboxylic acid and                  ferric chloride hexahydrate aqueous solution,                  ultrasonically dispersing the ferrosoferric oxide                  uniformly, carrying out hydrothermal reaction at                  60-80&#8451; for 20-24 hours, carrying out                  magnetic separation to separate the reaction                  product after completing the reaction, washing                  using deionized water and absolute ethyl alcohol,                  after washing, carrying out magnetic separation                  to collect the product and vacuum drying to                  obtain the ferrosoferric oxide loaded MOF                  composite material.Preferred Composition: The solid-liquid                  ratio of the ferrosoferric oxide nanoparticles                  and the mixed solution is (0.8-1.0) g:(50-70) mL                  and the concentration of                  1,3,5-benzenetricarboxylic acid and ferric                  chloride hexahydrate aqueous solution in the                  mixed solution is 0.10-0.12 mol/L. In step (S1),                  the mass ratio of the ferrosoferric oxide loaded                  MOF composite material and the drug molecule is                  1:1-2. In step (S2), the ratio of the volume of                  supercritical carbon dioxide and ferrosoferric                  oxide loaded MOF composite material is 10                  mL:(10-30) mg.
DC B07 (General - tablets, dispensers, catheters (excluding drainage and angioplasty), encapsulation etc, but not systems for administration of blood or saline or IV feeding etc.)
MC B11-C05; B05-C04; B05-A03A2; B05-C07; B10-C02; B05-C08

PT P
PN CN114591284-A
TI Preparing cyclic carbonate in carbon dioxide based            on zinc-metal-organic framework catalyst, comprises            e.g. mixing and catalyzing cycloaddition reaction of            carbon dioxide and styrene oxide in presence of            catalyst, separating by centrifugation and            recycling.
AU WANG S
   DING F
   SUN Y
   GUO J
   YOU L
AE UNIV SHENYANG CHEM TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (UYHG-C)
GA 202279894X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing cyclic carbonate in carbon dioxide                based on zinc-metal-organic framework catalyst,                comprises taking catalyst composed of                zinc-metal-organic framework and                1,3-bis(3,5-dimethylisophthalate)-1H                benzimidazolium bromide, mixing and catalyzing                cycloaddition reaction of carbon dioxide and                styrene oxide at 70-100&#8451; for 24 hours                when the carbon dioxide is in the spherical                pressure and there is no solvent to obtain cyclic                carbonate, separating by centrifugation and then                recycling for multiple times, where the catalyst is                1 mol.% zinc-metal-organic framework + 1 mol.%                1,3-bis(3,5-dimethylisophthalate)-1H                benzimidazolium bromide and the above-mentioned 1                mol.% zinc-metal-organic framework + 1 mol.%                1,3-bis(3,5-dimethylisophthalate)-1H                benzimidazolium bromide, which efficiently                catalyzes the cycloaddition of carbon dioxide and                styrene oxide after the reaction.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing cyclic                carbonate in carbon dioxide based on                zinc-metal-organic framework catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes catalyst, which catalyze                the cycloaddition reaction of carbon dioxide and                styrene oxide under mild conditions to prepare                cyclic carbonate, which the yield reaches greater                than 98% and the yield is greater than 90% after                multiple cycles; utilizes catalyst, which is easy                to separate and reuse for multiple times, catalytic                yield is not significantly reduced and has high                potential for industrial practical                application.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The zinc-metal-organic                  framework in the catalyst is prepared by adding                  0.03 mmol H4L1+Cl-, 0.10 mmol zinc nitrate                  hexahydrate, 3 ml N,N-dimethylformamide and 1 ml                  distilled water into a 20 ml glass bottle,                  adjusting pH value to 4-6 with dilute nitric                  acid, maintaining in ultrasonic for 10 minutes to                  make uniformly dispersed, reacting at                  90-110&#8451; for 48-72 hours in a sealed                  state, cooling to room temperature naturally,                  separating the solvent to obtain quartz-based                  white crystal zinc-metal-organic framework                  (C27H41N3O12Zn), maintaining in a vacuum oven at                  100&#8451; for 24 hours to completely dry,                  where the zinc-metal-organic framework is:                  C27H41N3O12Zn of formula (I). The preparation of                  1,3-bis(3,5-dimethylisophthalate)-1H                  benzimidazolium bromide in catalyst comprises                  adding 10 mmol, 1.90 gtrimethylsilylbenzimidazole                  and 21 mmol, 6.03 g dimethyl                  4-bromomethylisophthalate to 20 ml acetonitrile,                  refluxing at 80-100 &#176;C for 12-24 hours,                  removing the solvent by filtration and washing                  with tetrahydrofuran to obtain white                  1,3-bis(3,5-dimethylisophthalate)-1H                  benzimidazolium bromide. The preparation of                  H4L1+Cl-is prepared by adding 2.70 mmol, 1.50 g                  1,3-bis(dimethyl 3,5-isophthalate)-1H imidazolium                  bromide, 18.7 mmol, 0.75 sodium hydroxide into 50                  ml distilled water to a round-bottomed flask,                  refluxing at 100-110&#8451; for 4-5 hours,                  cooling to room temperature, filtering and                  acidifying with hydrochloric acid to obtain white                  product. The preparation of                  1,3-bis(3,5-dimethylisophthalate)-1H imidazolium                  bromide comprises adding 21 mmol, 6.03 g dimethyl                  4-bromomethylisophthalate, 10 mmol, 1.46 ml                  N-(trimethylsilyl)imidazole to 40 ml                  acetonitrile, refluxing under argon for 12 hours,                  allowing to stand, washing with tetrahydrofuran                  to obtain white precipitate                  1,3-bis(3,5-dimethylisophthalate)-1H imidazolium                  bromide.                                                                                                                              
DC E13 (Heterocyclics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

TI Gas turbine facility for use in power generation plant, has fluid introduction pipe introducing fluid at lower temperature than temperature of carbon dioxide flowing through carbon dioxide circulation pipe into circulation pipe.
AU ONO T
   TASHIMA T
   IWAI S
   TAJIMA T
AE TOSHIBA ENERGY SYSTEMS & SOLUTIONS CORP (TOKE-C)
   TOSHIBA ENERGY SYSTEMS & SOLUTIONS CORP (TOKE-C)
   TOSHIBA ENERGY SYSTEMS & SOLUTIONS CORP (TOKE-C)
GA 2021E8135N
AB    NOVELTY - The facility has a combustion apparatus 70 which combusts fuel and an oxidant to produce a combustion gas to be introduced into a turbine 1. A carbon dioxide circulation pipe circulates carbon dioxide in a working fluid discharged from the turbine through the combustion apparatus as a cooling medium. A fluid introduction pipe introduces a fluid at a lower temperature than a temperature of carbon dioxide flowing through the circulation pipe. A heat exchanger is interposed on the pipe and heats the carbon dioxide to circulate through the apparatus, where both ends of the pipe are coupled to the pipe so as to sandwich the exchanger.
   USE - Gas turbine facility for use in power generation plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The cooling medium can be introduced into the combustion apparatus and the turbine under appropriate conditions, when the temperature of the working fluid at the turbine inlet rises more than the planned value, so that the damage of the turbine can be prevented. The efficiency of power generation plants can be increased in response to demands for reduction of carbon dioxide and resource saving. The temperature rise of circulating carbon dioxide can be avoided.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a sectional view of a turbine and a combustion apparatus.

   Packing casing (11)
   Inner casing (20)
   Initial-stage stator blade (42)
   Exhaust hood (60)

PN KR2021109324-A
TI Integrated carbon dioxide capture system comprises e.g. absorption tower for collecting carbon dioxide in target gas by generating amine carbonate by reacting with amine adsorbent, and first connection unit connected to absorption tower.
AU PARK Y
   JIN H S
AE GWANGJU INST SCI & TECHNOLOGY (GWAN-C)
GA 2021A3848V
AB    NOVELTY - The system has an absorption tower (1) in which the target gas is introduced and reacted with an amine absorbent to generate amine carbonate. A connection part (2) is connected to the absorption tower, and a desorption tower (3) is connected with the connection part. An alkali source injection unit (4) injects an alkaline source capable of desorbing carbon dioxide by reacting with the amine. A solid carbonate discharge unit (5) is linked to the desorption tower. A temperature control unit controls the temperature of the tower to control the properties of solid carbonates precipitated in the tower. The amine is selected from a compound selected from carbamate and bicarbonate, calcium chloride and magnesium chloride. An absorption tower collects carbon dioxide in target gas by generating amine carbonate by reacting with amine adsorbent.
   USE - Integrated carbon dioxide capture system.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide can be desorbed at a lower temperature while maintaining amine regeneration efficiency similar to that of the prior art, thereby reducing energy required for desorption. Since carbon dioxide is discharged in gaseous form, the separate conversion and transfer process of the product such as liquefaction is required.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of an integrated carbon dioxide capture system.
   Absorption tower (1)
   Connection part (2)
   Desorption tower (3)
   Alkali source injection unit (4)
   Solid carbonate discharge unit (5)

TI Air processing system, has air inlet flow passage provided with fresh air inlet section and indoor air supply section, and carbon dioxide removing section connected with indoor circulating section through energy exchanger to exchange heat.
AU CAO R

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The preparation of polyionic liquid BzIm comprises mixing halide, imidazole substitute and acetonitrile to hydrothermal tank, sealing hydrothermal tank at 60-120 degrees C for 24-96 hours, soaking solid product in water, filtering to obtain solid, adding aqueous sodium hydroxide solution, soaking, filtering to obtain solid, soaking solid in aqueous sodium hydroxide solution, washing filtered solid with water, mixing washed solid in 10 wt.% aqueous 2-hydroxypyridine solution for 4 hours and freeze drying filtered solid. The composite is prepared by dissolving 2.5 g ionic liquid in 2.5 g surfactant to obtain ionic liquid mixture, adding 20-30 g water to 20 g polyionic liquid, soaking for 30 minutes, adding ionic liquid mixture, performing ultrasonic treatment for 1 hour and freezing at - 20 degrees C for 4 hours. PT P
PN CN114862179-A
TI Performing modeling method for calculating carbon            sequestration in mulberry garden ecological system            involves calculating total biomass of burning mulberry            branches, calculating carbon residue and calculating            net carbon sequestration of felling mulberry branches            for fuel wood combustion.
AU DAI J
   HU Y
   ZHU Z
   CHU Z
   BIAN X
   XU J
AE UNIV SOOCHOW (USWZ-C)
GA 2022A6309K
AB    NOVELTY - Performing modeling method for calculating                carbon sequestration in mulberry garden ecological                system involves calculating total biomass of                burning mulberry branches by using planting density                and area of each mulberry tree, amount of cutting                mulberry per plant, and moisture content of                mulberry branches, and calculating the carbon                residue according to total biomass of burning                mulberry branches, and then calculating net carbon                sequestration of felling mulberry branches used for                fuelwood combustion after deducting non-carbon                dioxide greenhouse gases produced during combustion                and carbon offset from natural gas use realized by                fuelwood combustion. The carbon residue of mulberry                leaf composting is calculated according to total                biomass of felled mulberry leaves used for in-situ                composting of mulberry orchards and weighting                factor of the composted mulberry leaves, and then                carbon sequestration amount of mulberry leaf                compost is obtained.
   USE - Method for performing modeling method for                calculating carbon sequestration in mulberry garden                ecological system.
   ADVANTAGE - The method performs modeling method, which                separately models carbon loss and carbon offset of                seven cycle paths of photosynthetic carbon,                explores an accurate and reliable mulberry garden                carbon sequestration accounting model, and has                important significance for quantitative evaluation                of mulberry garden carbon sequestration.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Performing modeling method for calculating                carbon sequestration in mulberry garden ecological                system involves calculating total biomass of                burning mulberry branches by using planting density                and area of each mulberry tree, amount of cutting                mulberry per plant, and moisture content of                mulberry branches, and calculating the carbon                residue according to total biomass of burning                mulberry branches, and then calculating net carbon                sequestration of felling mulberry branches used for                fuelwood combustion after deducting non-carbon                dioxide greenhouse gases produced during combustion                and carbon offset from natural gas use realized by                fuelwood combustion. The carbon residue of mulberry                leaf composting is calculated according to total                biomass of felled mulberry leaves used for in-situ                composting of mulberry orchards and weighting                factor of the composted mulberry leaves, and then                carbon sequestration amount of mulberry leaf                compost is obtained. The initial net carbon content                of silk fibers are obtained by using yield of fresh                cocoons and silk fiber output rate of fresh                cocoons, where carbon sequestration amount of silk                fibers are calculated based on weight factor of                one-time release of stored carbon at end of service                life of silk fiber products, and carbon                sequestration amount of silkworm chrysalis is                calculated based on the carbon content in the                silkworm chrysalis. The total biomass of silkworm                sand and leftover leaves are used to calculate the                carbon sequestration after composting the silkworm                sand and leftover leaves, and biomass of mulberry                is used to calculate the carbon sequestration of                mulberry, and then carbon sequestration of                underground biomass is calculated by using ratio of                growth of underground biomass to growth of                above-ground biomass. The net carbon sequestration                of cutting mulberry branches are used for fuelwood                burning, mulberry leaf compost carbon                sequestration, silk fiber carbon sequestration,                silkworm chrysalis carbon sequestration, silkworm                sand and food waste. The carbon sequestration                amount after composting the leftover leaves, carbon                sequestration amount of mulberry fruit and carbon                sequestration amount of underground biomass are                combined and calculated to obtain final carbon                sequestration amount of mulberry garden.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The planting density                  and area of each mulberry tree product, amount of                  mulberry cutting per plant, and moisture content                  of the mulberry branches are used to calculate                  total biomass of burning mulberry branches, and                  carbon residue calculated according to the total                  biomass of burning mulberry branches is deducted                  during burning where non-carbon dioxide GHGs                  produced and carbon offsets for natural gas uses                  from fuel wood combustion. The calculated net                  carbon sequestration of mulberry branches used                  for fuel wood combustion includes tracking the                  biomass carbon used for fuel wood combustion,                  calculating the total biomass burning combustion,                  and then net carbon sequestration C'11 of biomass                  burning based on the total biomass burning mass                  mcombustion is calculated. The carbon emission                  Coffsetcombustion offset by fuel wood combustion                  is calculated by formula                  Coffsetcombustion=12mcombutionxCoximulberryxLHVmulberryxEFnGas/(LHVnGasx44)                  where Coximulberry is oxidation rate of biomass                  burning of mulberry branches, LHVmulberry is low                  calorific value of mulberry branches, EFnGas is                  carbon emission coefficient of natural gas, and                  LHVnGas is low calorific value of natural gas.                  The total biomass mass of composted mulberry                  leaves and weighting factor of composted mulberry                  leaves are used to obtain carbon residue of                  mulberry leaf compost based on carbon residue                  minus non-carbon dioxide greenhouse gas                  emissions. The carbon sequestration of mulberry                  leaf compost are calculated by calculating the                  total biomass mcompost of the compost based on                  composted mulberry leaves by total carbon residue                  minus non-carbon dioxide GHG emissions. The fresh                  cocoon yield is used to obtain the initial net                  carbon content of silk fibers based on the weight                  factor of the silk fibers to calculate carbon                  fixation amount of the silk fibers by calculating                  initial net carbon content C21 in the silk fiber                  by formula C21=mcocoonxbetax(1-wfiber)xCFfiber,                  where mcocoon is yield of fresh cocoons, beta is                  silk production rate of fresh cocoons, wfiber is                  moisture content of the silk fiber, and Cfiber is                  the carbon content in the silk fiber, and weight                  factor FWfiber of silk fiber in 100-year                  evaluation period is calculated by formula                  FWfiber=1-0.76t0/100, where t0 is raw silk. The                  carbon fixation amount C'21 of the silk fiber is                  calculated based on initial net carbon amount in                  silk fiber and weight factor FWfiber of silk                  fiber in 100-year evaluation period by formula                  C'21=C21x(1-FWfiber)=cocoonxbetax(1-wfiber)xCfiberx(0.76t0/100).                  The carbon sequestration amount C'22 of silkworm                  chrysalis is calculated by using the fresh cocoon                  yield mcocoon, and by formula                  C'22=mcocoonxalphax(1-wpupa)xCpupax(0.76tpupa/100)                  where alpha is percentage of silkworm chrysalis                  produced by fresh cocoons, wpupa is silkworm                  chrysalis sold as a by-product after reeling,                  Cpupa is carbon content in silkworm chrysalis,                  and tpupa is carbon fixation time. The silkworm                  sand is calculated by utilizing the total biomass                  of silkworm sand and leftover leaves, and carbon                  sequestration of leftover leaves are calculated                  by calculating the total biomass leftover of                  silkworm sand and leftover leaves. The silkworm                  sand and leftover leaves are composted and                  returned to the field, and total biomass of                  silkworm sand and leftover leaves are not weighed                  so the dry matter mass of silkworm cocoons are                  subtracted from the total mulberry leaf amount,                  and then life activity of silkworms are removed.                  The amount of mulberry leaves consumed is used to                  calculate the amount of carbon sequestration. The                  initial net carbon amount C23 in the silkworm                  sand and the leftover leaves is net carbon amount                  in total supplied leaves of mulberry orchard                  minus the carbon amount in the silk fiber, carbon                  amount in the silkworm chrysalis and carbon                  amount in the carbon dioxide breathed by the                  silkworm. The carbon sequestration amount of                  underground biomass is calculated by using the                  ratio of growth of underground biomass to growth                  of above-ground biomass.

PN DE102020126392-A1; WO2022074193-A1
TI Water dispenser of water dispenser arrangement for use in beverage dispensing system for dispensing hot drinks and cold carbonated drinks, has control device which regulates introduction of carbon dioxide through switching valve.
AU JOHANN J
   DEPAUL M
AE BWT AG (BWTA-Non-standard)
GA 2022503103
AB    NOVELTY - The water dispenser (1) has a heater (18) designed as boiler (7) which is provided to submit warm water. One metering valve is designed as switching valve (9a-9c) through which carbon dioxide (CO2) in the heaters is conductive. A cooled carbonator (8) is used for dispensing carbonated water. The cooled carbonator is connectable to the CO2 compressed gas tank (11) such that the partial flow of carbonized water in the heaters is conductive. The partial flow with a main stream for the heater is miscible in the volume of the partial flow of 2-90% bismuth from 5-50% of the total stream for heater. A control device (14) is configured for regulating the introduction of the CO2 through the switching valve such that the control takes place based on time and volume. The control is adjustable on the basis of a degree of hardness of the input water.
   USE - Water dispenser of water dispenser arrangement for dispensing hot and cold water, for use in beverage dispensing system for dispensing hot drinks such as tea or coffee and cold carbonated drinks (all claimed) such as carbonated sweetened drinks.
   ADVANTAGE - The formation of scale in the boiler of a beverage machine can be reduced without removing magnesium from the water and removing most of the calcium. The CO2 dissolved in water is introduced into the heater such that the heater allows for easier dosing through a single dosing valve. The water dispenser enables a particularly simple control such that the separate water meter is not required to measure the flow rate through the boiler, and can be determined indirectly through the hot drinks taken. The compressed gas tank and the two switching valves form a metering pump with very simple construction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The two switching valves are arranged in series, and are alternately controllable such that the water is conductive from the carbonator in the heater, or the heater with the CO2 compressed gas tank is connectable. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) an arrangement has a water dispenser and a cartridge (4) for water treatment such that the water is supplied to the water dispenser. The cartridge contains an ion exchange material loaded with magnesium and zinc. The ion exchange material is loaded with 30% or 50% of total capacity with magnesium or zinc. The ion exchange material is loaded with less than 60% or less than 20% of total capacity is loaded with hydrogen and sodium; and
   (2) a method for operating water dispenser, involves providing the water dispenser with a heater designed as boiler for heating the water. The CO2 is supplied to the heater. The pH value in the heater is adjusted to below 7.5, preferably to 6.0 to 7.5, and preferably to 6.5 to 7.0 by supplying CO2. The water in the heater is heated to 60 degree Celsius, preferably to 80 degree Celsius. The metering of the CO2 is taken place through the two switching valves designed as magnetic valves, and is clocked in opposite directions.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic view of the beverage dispensing system.
   Water dispenser (1)
   Cartridge (4)
   Boiler (7)
   Cooled carbonator (8)
   Switching valves (9a-9c)
   CO2 compressed gas tank (11)
   Control device (14)
   Heater (18)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - The cartridge is designed to emit the mineral selected from magnesium, silicon, lithium and zinc to the water.

PN BR102020019568-A2
TI Production of methanol involves mixing gaseous load(s) of reactants with recycle gas stream(s) to produce gaseous stream, phase-separating to produce stream of raw liquid methanol, recovering methanol with supersonic separators, compressing, and purifying.
AU DE MEDEIROS J L
   BRIGAGAO G V
   WIESBERG I L
   DE OLIVEIRA ARINELLI L
   DE QUEIROZ FERNANDES ARAUJ
AE UNIV RIO DE JANEIRO (UYRI-Non-standard)
GA 202254242T
AB    NOVELTY - Production of methanol from synthesis gas or carbon dioxide with hydrogen involves mixing at least one gaseous load of reactants with at least one recycle gas stream to produce a gaseous stream which constitutes the reaction mixture, synthesizing methanol in a gas phase and high pressure using at least one reactor containing a heterogeneous catalyst, cooling the effluent gas, phase-separating to produce a stream of raw liquid methanol, recovering methanol from the unconverted tail gas by a battery of one or more supersonic separators to produce a low-methanol gas stream to be recycled to the reactor, compressing the recycling gas to be mixed with the reactor gas, sending the streams of raw methanol to a purification system capable of adapting the methanol to the commercialization or use specifications, based on a set of distillation columns by at least two distillation columns (C1) and (C2) to separate a methanol-water stream to produce methanol at the top and water at the bottom.
   USE - Production of methanol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces methanol with high productivity and greater total profitability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Production of methanol from synthesis gas or carbon dioxide with hydrogen involves mixing at least one gaseous load of reactants with at least one recycle gas stream to produce a gaseous stream which constitutes the reaction mixture, adjusting the temperature of the reaction mixture for methanol synthesis according to a recommended temperature range for better performance of the catalyst used, through heat exchange with energy integration in the process or with utilities generated by the heat of the synthesis reaction itself, synthesizing methanol in a gas phase and high pressure using at least one reactor containing a heterogeneous catalyst, cooling the effluent gas from the reactor, or from the reactor system containing methanol to promote methanol condensation, phase-separating the cooled effluent from the reactor, or from the reactor system to produce a stream of raw liquid methanol and a stream of unconverted gas containing residual methanol vapor, recovering methanol from the unconverted tail gas by a battery of one or more supersonic separators in parallel, each with injection of liquid water at the point of entry of the separators in a molar ratio of water/methanol of 0-5 to produce a low-methanol gas stream to be recycled to the reactor and a liquid stream of crude methanol with water, compressing of the recycling gas to be mixed with the reactor gas charge in the case of a pressure difference between these streams, or compressing the mixture of these two gases before synthesis of methanol in the case the pressure of these streams are similar, sending the two streams of raw methanol to a purification system capable of adapting the methanol to the commercialization or use specifications, based on a set of distillation columns by at least two distillation columns (C1) and (C2) in series in which column (C1) is destined to elimination of gases dissolved in the raw methanol to generate an effluent gas at low pressure and a degassed methanol-water bottom, and a column (C2) destined to the separation of the methanol-water stream to produce methanol at the top and water at the bottom.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The methanol synthesis catalyst comprises 10-80 wt.% copper oxide, 0-60 wt.% chromium oxide, 0-85 wt.% zinc oxide, 0-60 wt.% alumina or silica, 0-35 wt.% zirconium oxide, and 0-30 wt.% magnesium, cerium and palladium oxides.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: In the production of methanol, division of the gaseous stream leaving the crude methanol separator vessel into two streams, each of which feeds the battery, both with liquid water injection at the point of entry in the molar ratio of water/methanol of 0-5, so that the supersonic separation system is divided into two batteries which operate independently with water injection of 0-5 mol/mol or more to recover methanol of a purge gas stream and to recover methanol from the stream to be recycled to the reactor. Preferred Conditions: The methanol synthesis temperature is 100-400 degrees C and a pressure is 10-400 bar. Preferred Components: The gaseous charge of reagents comprises hydrogen, carbon monoxide, methanol, water, dimethyl ether, nitrogen, oxygen, argon and/or carbon dioxide.

TI Impregnation modification treatment method of solid wood floor used for heating floor, involves compressing and shaping, coating release agent on surface of hot pressing steel plate, and placing soaked plate in pressing machine.
AU SHEN X
   SHE X
   OUYANG Q
   MO D
   ZHONG L
   YANG L
   LIU Y
AE GUANGDONG NATURE HOME TECHNOLOGY RES CO (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2022359442
AB    NOVELTY - Impregnation modification treatment method of solid wood floor involves: placing plate with water content of 5-10% in supercritical device, using supercritical fluid carbon dioxide and 10-40 vol.% polyethylene glycol as carrier, and setting temperature parameter at 35-55degrees Celsius for 30-100 minutes at pressure parameter of 9-18 MPa and flow speed of 15-40 L/hour; introducing modified liquid into liquid storage tank of dipping device, placing plate on material trolley, transmitting guide rail to dipping tank, dipping, heating dipping tank at 40-60degrees Celsius, pre-vacuum treating for 10-20 minutes at vacuum degree of -0.095 MPa, introducing pre-configured modified liquid from liquid storage tank and pressurizing and dipping treatment, vacuum processing for 30 minutes at vacuum degree of -0.095 MPa, extracting redundant modified liquid, and taking out plate.
   USE - The method is used for impregnation modification treating solid wood floor for heating floor.
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively improves the size stability of the wood, enhances the stability and heat conducting performance of the product, and improves the whole performance of plate density and strength. The method uses supercritical pre-treatment to improve the permeability of the plate, increasing post-impregnation modification effect, at the same time, using polyethylene glycol to increase the post-implantation modification effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Impregnation modification treatment method of solid wood floor involves: placing plate with water content of 5-10% in supercritical device, using supercritical fluid carbon dioxide and 10-40 vol.% polyethylene glycol as carrier, and setting temperature parameter at 35-55degrees Celsius for 30-100 minutes at pressure parameter of 9-18 MPa and flow speed of 15-40 L/hour; introducing modified liquid into liquid storage tank of dipping device, placing plate on material trolley, transmitting guide rail to dipping tank, dipping, heating dipping tank at 40-60degrees Celsius, pre-vacuum treating for 10-20 minutes at vacuum degree of -0.095 MPa, introducing pre-configured modified liquid from liquid storage tank and pressurizing and dipping treatment for 20-40 minutes at pressure of 1.0-1.5 MPa, discharging modified liquid, vacuum processing for 30 minutes at vacuum degree of -0.095 MPa, extracting redundant modified liquid, taking out plate, using high speed rotating device for drying plate surface modifier for 10-30 minutes at speed of 8000-12000 revolutions/minute, and putting in room temperature environment at 25-35degrees Celsius for 24 hours; and coating release agent on surface of hot pressing steel plate, placing soaked plate in pressing machine for compression treatment, setting compression rate of 10-20%, hot pressing at 90degrees Celsius for 10-20 minutes at pressure of 3 MPa/m2, slowly boosting, compressing and shaping, hot pressing to 120degrees Celsius for 50-90 minutes, placing plate stacking in dampening kiln for dampening treatment at at 40-55 degrees Celsius for 4-5 days at humidity of 70-90% until water content of plate is increased to 8%, and entering balancing room to balance and preserve for 15 days.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The modified liquid is prepared by adding 5-10 wt.% silane coupling agent, 3-8 wt.% graphene oxide and 4-7 wt.% tartaric acid in 75-88 wt.% aqueous closed isocyanate, adding while stirring and ultrasonically dispersing for 30 minutes.Preferred Components: The raw material of plate comprises Fraxinus mandschurica, Dalbergia cochinchinensis, Betula, white oak and red oak.

PN CN113416690-A
TI Constructing tissue-engineered skin with rapid vascularization comprises e.g. placing microcarriers loaded with angiogenesis-related factors in cell suspension for inoculation, and mixing with endothelial progenitor cell-microsphere complex.
AU MING L
   WANG Q
   WANG Z
   DONG L
AE SHAANXI ZHONGHONGKERUI REGENERATIVE MEDI (SHAA-Non-standard)
GA 2021B2451A
AB    NOVELTY - Constructing tissue-engineered skin with rapid vascularization comprises (i) configuring human umbilical cord-derived endothelial progenitor cells into a cell suspension, placing microcarriers loaded with angiogenesis-related factors in the suspension for inoculation, inoculating endothelial progenitor cells, incubating, subjecting cell-microcarrier complex to dynamic perfusion pulse culture; (ii) inoculating neonatal foreskin tissue-derived fibroblasts into the hydrogel, mixing with the endothelial progenitor cell-microsphere complex and endothelial progenitor cell-microtubule I complex, and spreading flat in culture container; (iii) seeding the adipose stem cells from human adipose tissue on hydrogel, mixing with endothelial progenitor cell-microsphere complex, endothelial progenitor cell-microtubule I complex and endothelial progenitor cell-microtubule II complex, and spreading flat, culturing, starting adipogenic induction culture; and (iv) combining layers of tissue and culturing.
   USE - The method is useful for constructing tissue-engineered skin with rapid vascularization.
   ADVANTAGE - The microcarrier is loaded with angiogenesis-related factors, which are slowly released during the process of tissue engineering skin culture, and accelerating the maturation of the capillary network.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Constructing tissue-engineered skin with rapid vascularization comprises (i) configuring human umbilical cord-derived endothelial progenitor cells into a cell suspension with a concentration of 0.5-5x 106 cells/ml, placing microcarriers loaded with angiogenesis-related factors in the suspension for inoculation, where microcarrier comprises one type of microsphere and two types of microtubes, both of which are hollow structures, the diameter of the microspheres is 20-50 mu m, and the microtubes are oriented nanofiber tubes and the first type has a diameter of 10-20 mu m and a length of 200-300 mu m, which is denoted as microtube I and the second type has a diameter of 30-50 mu m and a length of 50-100 mu m, and is denoted as microtube II, operating three types of microcarriers separately, inoculating endothelial progenitor cells, incubating in an incubator for 1-2 hours, separately subjecting each cell-microcarrier complex to dynamic perfusion pulse culture for 10-20 days to obtain endothelial progenitor cells-microspheres complex, endothelial progenitor cell-microtubule I complex and endothelial progenitor cell-microtubule II complex; (ii) inoculating neonatal foreskin tissue-derived fibroblasts into the hydrogel, uniformly mixing with the endothelial progenitor cell-microsphere complex and endothelial progenitor cell-microtubule I complex, and spreading flat in a culture container with a thickness of 0.5-3 mm, cultivating for 7-10 days under the condition of 37 degrees C and 5 vol.% carbon dioxide to obtain the dermis; (iii) seeding the adipose stem cells from human adipose tissue on the hydrogel, uniformly mixing with the endothelial progenitor cell-microsphere complex, endothelial progenitor cell-microtubule I complex and endothelial progenitor cell-microtubule II complex, and spreading flat, in a culture vessel with a thickness of 0.5-3 mm, culturing at 37 degrees C and 5 vol.% carbon dioxide for 1 day, starting adipogenic induction culture, and obtaining a fat layer after 7-10 days; and (iv) combining two layers of tissue and culturing for 10-20 days in the order of dermis layer on top and fat layer on the bottom. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for: a tissue engineered skin constructed by the above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the microcarrier is a degradable synthetic polymer material, e.g. polycaprolactone, polylactic acid glycolic acid copolymer, polyglycolide, polylactic acid and its copolymer, or natural polymer material e.g. collagen, elastin, hydrolyzed products of collagen in gelatin, silk fibroin, and/or chitin; and the angiogenesis-related factor includes vascular endothelial growth factor, platelet-derived growth factor, basic fibroblast growth factor, hepatocyte growth factor, and/or angiopoietin. In the (ii) and (iii), the hydrogel materials are all hyaluronic acid, sodium alginate, chitosan, cellulose, collagen, gelatin and/or DNA materials form a three-dimensional network structure, and the pore size of the three-dimensional network is less than 200 mu m. In the step (ii), in the culture container, the density of microtube I is 50-200/mm2, and the ratio of microspheres and microtubes I is 1:1. In the step (iii), in the culture container, the ratio of the number of microtubules I to microtube II is 5-10:1, the total density of the two is 50-200/mm2, and the ratio of the sum of the two types of microtubules and the number of microspheres is 1:1 . Preferred Method: In the step (i), the parameters of dynamic perfusion pulse culture are: flow rate 30-60 ml/minute, and pulse frequency 25-75 times/minute. In the step (ii), the fibroblast is seeded on the hydrogel at a density of 0.5-5x 106 cells/ml. In the step (iii), the adipose stem cell is seeded on the hydrogel at a density of 0.5-5x 106 cells/ml.

Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA.
