TI Carbonaceous material used as electrode material, has preset BET specific surface area measured according to nitrogen adsorption method, pore volume, powder electrical conductivity, surface functional group amount and hydrogen content.
AU HITOMI M
   MATSUSHITA M
   NISHINAMI H
   NISHITA M
   YAMADA T
AE KURARAY CO LTD (KURS-C)
GA 202173572G
AB    NOVELTY - A carbonaceous material has a BET specific surface area of 1750-2100 m2/g according to a nitrogen adsorption method, a pore volume of 0.76-0.91 cm3/g as calculated by a Horvath-Kawazoe (HK) method based on an adsorption isotherm according to the nitrogen adsorption method, a powder electrical conductivity of 13-22 S/cm, a surface functional group amount of 0.22-0.29 meq/g, a hydrogen content of 0.41 %mass or less, and a pore volume of 0.37-0.41 cm3/g as calculated by a non localized density functional theory (NLDFT) method based on an adsorption/desorption isotherm according to a carbon dioxide adsorption/desorption method. The pore volume (A) calculated by the HK method based on adsorption isotherm according to a water vapor adsorption method is 0.48-0.64 cm3/g. The pore volume (B) of the pores having diameter of 1.2 nm or less is 0.14-0.30 cm3/g as calculated by the HK method on the basis of the adsorption isotherm according to the water vapor adsorption method.
   USE - Carbonaceous material used as electrode material for electrical double layer capacitor (all claimed) for electric vehicle (EV) and hybrid vehicle (HV).
   ADVANTAGE - The material provides capacitor having excellent gas-evolution inhibitory effect during charging/discharging and high capacity retention rate for long time.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A carbonaceous material has a BET specific surface area of 1750-2100 m2/g according to a nitrogen adsorption method, a pore volume of 0.76-0.91 cm3/g as calculated by a Horvath-Kawazoe (HK) method based on an adsorption isotherm according to the nitrogen adsorption method, a powder electrical conductivity of 13-22 S/cm, a surface functional group amount of 0.22-0.29 meq/g, a hydrogen content of 0.41 %mass or less, and a pore volume of 0.37-0.41 cm3/g as calculated by a non localized density functional theory (NLDFT) method based on an adsorption/desorption isotherm according to a carbon dioxide adsorption/desorption method. The pore volume (A) calculated by the HK method based on adsorption isotherm according to a water vapor adsorption method is 0.48-0.64 cm3/g. The pore volume (B) of the pores having diameter of 1.2 nm or less is 0.14-0.30 cm3/g as calculated by the HK method on the basis of the adsorption isotherm according to the water vapor adsorption method. The ratio of the pore volume (B) and the pore volume (A) is 25-59%. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for manufacture of the carbonaceous material, which involves carbonizing the carbon precursor, alkali-cleaning the activated carbon obtained by activating in an alkaline solution, acid-cleaning the activated carbon after alkaline cleaning, and heat-treating at 1100-1300 degrees C.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a polarizing electrode.
   Electrode composition (1)
   Etching aluminum foil (3)
   Tab (4)
   Sealant (5)
   Polarizing electrode (6)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The pore volume of the pores having diameter of 4 nm or more of the carbonaceous material as measured by a Barrett-Joyner-Halenda (BJH) method is 0.07-0.18 cm3/g. Preferred Composition: The carbonaceous material contains 40 ppm or less alkali metal, and is derived from a carbon precursor derived from coconut shell.

PN CN112899071-A
TI Preparing five-kernel oral freeze-dried block by supercritical extraction involves taking raw peach kernels, almonds, wild jujube kernels, bunge cherry seed, tangerine peel, pulverizing and drying, passing sieve to obtain kernel powder.
AU ZHANG H
   WANG G
   ZOU X
   WANG L
   SUN Z
AE NINGBO TECH-INNO HEALTH IND CO LTD (NING-Non-standard)
GA 2021679901
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing five-kernel oral freeze-dried block by supercritical extraction involves taking 13-17 pts. wt. raw peach kernels, 13-17 pts. wt. almonds, 13-17 pts. wt. wild jujube kernels, 8-10 pts. wt. bunge cherry seed, 4-6 pts. wt. pine nuts, 13-17 pts. wt. tangerine peel, pulverizing and drying, passing 40-60 mesh sieve to obtain kernel powder, adding the five-core powder obtained in step one to the dynamic extraction unit, and extract the five-core oil in it by supercritical carbon dioxide, taking 18-19 wt.% kernel oil, 18-22 wt.% raw cocoa pulp, 45-48 wt.% sugar, 0.34-0.45 wt.% lecithin, 14-16 wt.% whole milk powder, mixing and refining through a two-roller device, then using ball mill for ball milling, controlling the operating temperature to be above 45 degrees C.
   USE - Method for preparing five-kernel oral freeze-dried block by supercritical extraction.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has the antioxidant effect of polyphenol flavonoids, increases the five-kernel extraction effect, extracts the polyphenol flavonoid substance in the raw material, so that the five-kernel oral freeze-dried block.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing five-kernel oral freeze-dried block by supercritical extraction involves taking 13-17 pts. wt. raw peach kernels, 13-17 pts. wt. almonds, 13-17 pts. wt. wild jujube kernels, 8-10 pts. wt. bunge cherry seed, 4-6 pts. wt. pine nuts, 13-17 pts. wt. tangerine peel, pulverizing and drying, passing 40-60 mesh sieve to obtain kernel powder, adding the five-core powder obtained in step one to the dynamic extraction unit, and extract the five-core oil in it by supercritical carbon dioxide, taking 18-19 wt.% kernel oil, 18-22 wt.% raw cocoa pulp, 45-48 wt.% sugar, 0.34-0.45 wt.% lecithin, 14-16 wt.% whole milk powder, mixing and refining through a two-roller device, then using ball mill for ball milling, controlling the operating temperature to be above 45 degrees C. The material is liquid, and then the material is introduced into the McIntyre refiner for re-refining to obtain chocolate material, added the chocolate material obtained to the chocolate mixing and kneaded machine through a roller mill for stirring and kneading, transformed from a flake to a slurry, and then to a liquid form, and then added an emulsifier to it to obtain chocolate liquid, blended the chocolate liquid obtained, controlled the blending operation temperature to be 26-50 degrees C, so that the polymorphic chocolate oil phase forms stable crystals to obtain chocolate crystals. The chocolate crystals obtained are molded, and the molded chocolate crystals are cooled through a cooling tunnel, the cooling sequence temperature in the cooling tunnel is controlled to be 15-18 degrees C, 10-12 degrees C, 15 degrees C to obtain brownies, wrapped the five brownies obtained with a freeze-dried matrix liquid, placed them in a mold, and placed at a temperature below -15 degrees C for freezing for more than 10 hours, and then freeze-dried, demolded to obtain product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Conditions: The method involves taking 15 pts. wt. peach kernels, 15 pts. wt. almonds, 15 pts. wt. wild jujube kernels, 9 pts. wt. bunge cherry seed, 5 pts. wt. pine nut kernels, 15 pts. wt. tangerine peel, grinding and drying, and passing through a 40-60 mesh sieve to obtain five kernel powder. The dynamic extraction unit performs supercritical carbon dioxide extraction to control the extraction kettle temperature is 32-48 degrees C, the temperature of the separation kettle is 40-60oC, the flow rate of carbon dioxide is 12-18 Kg/hour, the extraction pressure is 240-360 bar, and the extraction time is 120-180 minutes. The dynamic extraction unit performs supercritical carbon dioxide extraction to control the extraction kettle temperature is 40 degrees C, the temperature of the separation kettle is 50 degrees C, the flow rate of carbon dioxide is 15Kg/hour, the extraction pressure is 300 bar, and the extraction time is 150 minutes. The five-nut chocolate is wrapped in a freeze-dried matrix liquid and placed in a mold and freeze for 12 hours at a temperature of -20 degrees C, then freeze-dried, and controlled the starting temperature of freeze-drying to -45 degrees C for 24 hours, and the final holding temperature is 28 degrees C. Preferred Components: The composition of the freeze-dried matrix liquid comprises 70 wt.% collagen protein solution, 15 wt.% whey protein powder, 15 wt.% citrus fiber.

TI Preparing methane, by constructing two-chamber microbial electrochemical system, separating two chambers, adding anode and cathode, inoculating methanogens, and performing coordination of e.g. direct electron transfer.
AU LI M
   XI B
   WANG Y
   QI X
   XU P
   HOU J
   YE M
AE CHINESE RES ACAD ENVIRONMENTAL SCI (CRHK-C)
GA 202144764S
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing methane by co-processing biogas slurry and biogas based on microbial electrocatalysis, involves constructing two-chamber microbial electrochemical system, where the anode is made of carbon-based materials, the cathode is made of carbon-based materials or conductive ceramic materials, separating two chambers by a proton exchange membrane, adding anode with anodic nutrient solution and inoculated with sulfur oxidizing bacteria, adding cathode with cathodic nutrient solution, inoculating hydrogen-trophic methanogens, converting hydrogen sulfide in the crude biogas into polysulfides and sulfates to release electrons and protons, transferring polysulfide of electrons and protons produced by anodic biogas slurry treatment and hydrogen sulfide to the cathode, performing coordination of direct electron transfer and indirect electron transfer and converting the carbon dioxide in the crude biogas into methane at the cathode.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing methane (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing methane by co-processing biogas slurry and biogas based on microbial electrocatalysis, involves constructing two-chamber microbial electrochemical system, where the anode is made of carbon-based materials, the cathode is made of carbon-based materials or conductive ceramic materials, separating two chambers by a proton exchange membrane, adding anode with anodic nutrient solution and inoculated with sulfur oxidizing bacteria, adding cathode with cathodic nutrient solution, inoculating hydrogen-trophic methanogens, applying voltage at 0.2-1 V, temperature of 20-50 degrees C and pH of 6-8, changing liquid cycle for 1-4 days, when the maximum current and gas production of the system are maintained for more than 3 consecutive cycles stable, functional bioanode and biocathode are enriched successfully, constructing single-chamber microbial electrochemical system, placing the successfully enriched functional bioanode and biocathode in the single-chamber microbial electrochemical system, pumping the biogas slurry after anaerobic digestion, applying a voltage of 0.2-1 V, performing temperature second enrichment of electroactive microorganisms under the conditions of 20-50 degrees C and hydraulic retention time of 1-7 days, so that a composite bioelectrode with functional microorganisms as the mainstay and synergistic microorganisms as a supplement is formed on the electrode, where the air volume and chemical oxygen demand (COD) removal rate remain stable for more than 3 consecutive cycles, which is regarded as a successful startup, pumping biogas slurry to start the successful microbial electrochemical system, which degrades volatile fatty acids and refractory organics under the action of the anode electroactive microorganisms, returning the treated biogas slurry to anaerobic digestion at a ratio of 20%-80% system, passing crude biogas produced by anaerobic digestion into the microbial electrochemical system at a flow rate of 1-5 m3/(m3.day), converting hydrogen sulfide in the crude biogas into polysulfides and sulfates to release electrons and protons, transferring polysulfide of electrons and protons produced by anodic biogas slurry treatment and hydrogen sulfide to the cathode, performing coordination of direct electron transfer and indirect electron transfer by methanogenic microorganisms at the cathode under the action, and converting the carbon dioxide in the crude biogas into methane at the cathode.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon-based material is carbon brush or graphite felt. The conductive ceramic material is titanium suboxide. The sulfur oxidizing bacteria are Thiobacillus, desulfurization bacteria and Desulfomonas. The anode nutrient solution comprises 1 g/l sodium acetate, 5.62 g/l sodium dihydrogen phosphate, 6.16 g/l disodium hydrogen phosphate, 0.31 g/l ammonium chloride, 0.13 g/l potassium chloride, 1 ml/l trace element liquid and 1 ml/l mineral element liquid. The cathodic nutrient solution comprises 0.5 g/l sodium acetate, 1 g/l sodium bicarbonate, 5.62 g/l sodium dihydrogen phosphate, 6.16 g/l disodium hydrogen phosphate, 0.31 g/l ammonium chloride, 0.13 g/l potassium chloride, 1 ml/l trace element liquid and 1 ml/l mineral element liquid. The crude biogas is directly passed into the multi-stage microbial electrochemical system for biogas upgrading, or the lye is first absorbed and then added to the microbial electrochemical system, when the methane in the biogas when the content is more than 90%. The polysulfide is an intermediate product of hydrogen sulfide anodic oxidation, which is a redox shuttle mediator with dual functions of oxidation and reduction.

PN CN112604711-A
TI Preparing titanium silicate molecular sieve useful for photoelectric catalytic reduction of carbon dioxide or styrene, and hydrogen peroxide to catalyze benzaldehyde and styrene oxide synthesis, comprises e.g. adding hydrochloric acid.
AU WANG B
   XIAO Q
   JING H
   FENG H
   SHI L
   LIU Y
   XIA H
   WANG Q
AE UNIV LANZHOU TECHNOLOGY (UYLZ-C)
GA 2021384100
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing titanium silicate molecular sieve, comprises e.g. (i) dissolving cetyltrimethylammonium bromide (CTABr) in deionized water, sonicating for 15 minutes at 313 K, obtaining a homogeneous clear solution, adding hydrochloric acid, dissolving the tetrabutyl titanate (TBOT) solution in isopropanol, dripping to the above solution under mechanical stirring, cooling to 273 K for 30 minutes, under vigorous stirring, slowly adding ethyl orthosilicate into the above solution slowly, after 5 minutes, stopping the stirring, crystallizing under static conditions for 6 hours, filtering the above solution with suction, washing with acetone, placing an oven at 100 degrees C, taking out to a mortar after 4 hours, grinding to a uniform powder, placing in a muffle furnace, setting to 873 K for 2 hours, and calcining for 4 hours to obtain Ti-SBA-1 product, (iii) adding the titanium-silica molecular sieve Ti-SBA-1 to the metal salt solution, and (iii) adding the titanium-silica molecular sieve Ti-SBA-1.
   USE - The molecular sieve is useful for photoelectric catalytic reduction of carbon dioxide or styrene, and hydrogen peroxide to catalyze benzaldehyde and styrene oxide synthesis (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The molecular sieve: has mesopores, large specific surface area, and strong adsorption performance; and relieves the pressure of shortage of petroleum resources to a certain extent.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing titanium silicate molecular sieve, comprises (i) dissolving cetyltrimethylammonium bromide (CTABr) in deionized water, sonicating for 15 minutes at 313 K, obtaining a homogeneous clear solution, adding hydrochloric acid, dissolving the tetrabutyl titanate (TBOT) solution in isopropanol, dripping to the above solution under mechanical stirring, cooling to 273 K for 30 minutes, under vigorous stirring, slowly adding ethyl orthosilicate into the above solution slowly, after 5 minutes, stopping the stirring, crystallizing under static conditions for 6 hours, filtering the above solution with suction, washing with acetone, placing an oven at 100 degrees C, taking out to a mortar after 4 hours, grinding to a uniform powder, placing in a muffle furnace, setting to 873 K for 2 hours, and calcining for 4 hours to obtain Ti-SBA-1 product, (iii) adding the titanium-silica molecular sieve Ti-SBA-1 to the metal salt solution, ultrasonicating for 30 minutes to make the metal ions uniformly dispersed in the Ti-SBA-1, suction-filtering the solution, placing the obtained solid in an oven for drying treatment, transferring to a muffle furnace, and calcining to obtain a metal-doped titanium silicon molecular sieve material, and (iii) taking the molecular sieve, adding to the sodium hydroxide solution to activate for 6 hours, then filtering the solution, placing in an oven to dry, transferring to a container with anhydrous ethanol solution, oil bath, when the temperature rises to 70 degrees C, while adding 3-aminopropyl triethoxysilane dropwise, taking out after 6 hours, washing with ethanol, placing into the oven, drying for 12-18 hours, taking out, adding absolute ethanol and salicylaldehyde to the above solution, placing in an oil bath for an oil bath, setting the temperature to 80 degrees C and refluxing for 6 hours, washing with propanol, placing in an oven, and drying for 12-18 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the molar ratio of each raw material component of TEOS, CTABr, TBOT, HCI and water is 1:0.13:0.2:20:250. In the step (i), the concentration of the hydrochloric acid solution is 37%. In the step (ii), the metal element doping is palladium doping or gold doping. In the step (iii), the mass ratio of each raw material component of sodium hydroxide, Ti SBA 1, ethanol, 3 aminopropyltriethoxysilane and salicylaldehyde is 0.48:1:39.45:2.37:4.68. The novel titanium-silicon molecular sieve has a three-dimensional cubic structure, the mesopore size is 2-3 nm, and the specific surface area is greater than or equal to 500 m2/g. Preferred Method: In the step (ii), when palladium is doped, the palladium source is palladium chloride solution, the drying temperature in the oven is 80 degrees C, the drying time is 12-18 hours, during calcination, the temperature is set to 500 degrees C for 2 hours, and the calcination time is 2 hours. In the step (ii), when gold is doped, the gold source is a chloroauric acid solution, before suction filtration, the pH value of the solution needs to be adjusted to 9-10 with ammonia water, the drying temperature of the oven is 80 degrees C, the drying time is 12-18 hours, during calcination, the temperature is increased to 600 degrees C by 2-hour program, and the calcination time is 2 hours.

PN CN112574412-A
TI Polyimide used in membrane for e.g. gas separation, is prepared by polymerization of diamino triptycene and tetracarboxylic dianhydride, or polymerization of diamino triptycene derivative and tetracarboxylic dianhydride.
AU CHENG B
   LI K
   YU D
   MA X
   ZHU Z
   LI Q
   ZHU W
   LI Z
AE UNIV TIANJIN POLYTECHNIC (UNTP-C)
GA 202137347X
AB    NOVELTY - A polyimide is prepared by polymerization of diamino triptycene and tetracarboxylic dianhydride, or polymerization of diamino triptycene derivative and tetracarboxylic dianhydride. The polymerization degree of the copolymer is 1-10000. The diamino triptycene or its derivatives (I). The tetracarboxylic dianhydride contains 4,4'-biphenyltetracarboxylic dianhydride, bicyclo(2.2.2)oct-7-ene 2,3,5,6-tetracarboxylic dianhydride, ethylenediaminetetraacetic dianhydride, 1,2,3,4-cyclobutanetetracarboxylic dianhydride, 3,4,9,10-perylenetetracarboxylic dianhydride, 3,3',4,4'-benzophenonetetracarboxylic dianhydride, pyromellitic dianhydride, 1,2,4,5-cyclohexanetetracarboxylic dianhydride, naphthalene-1,4,5,8-tetracarboxylic dianhydride, 1,2,4,5-cyclohexanetetracarboxylic dianhydride, 1,6,7,12-tetrachloroperylene-3,4,9,10-tetracarboxylic dianhydride, 1,2,3,4-cyclobutanetetracarboxylic dianhydride, 1,6,7,12-tetrachlorotetraaryltetracarboxylic dianhydride, and 3,4,9,10-tetracarboxylic dianhydride.
   USE - Polyimide used in membrane e.g. homogeneous membrane or heterogeneous membrane for gas separation (claimed) to effectively separate methane from gas mixture of methane and carbon dioxide in biogas separation applications, separate nitrogen and oxygen from air, remove carbon dioxide in purification process of natural gas, and recovery hydrogen in synthetic ammonia application.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A polyimide is prepared by polymerization of diamino triptycene and tetracarboxylic dianhydride, or polymerization of diamino triptycene derivative and tetracarboxylic dianhydride. The polymerization degree of the copolymer is 1-10000. The diamino triptycene or its derivatives is of formula (I). The tetracarboxylic dianhydride is chosen from one or more of 4,4'-biphenyltetracarboxylic dianhydride, bicyclo(2.2.2)oct-7-ene 2,3,5,6-tetracarboxylic dianhydride, ethylenediaminetetraacetic dianhydride, 1,2,3,4-cyclobutanetetracarboxylic dianhydride, 3,4,9,10-perylenetetracarboxylic dianhydride, 3,3',4,4'-benzophenonetetracarboxylic dianhydride, pyromellitic dianhydride, 1,2,4,5-cyclohexanetetracarboxylic dianhydride, naphthalene-1,4,5,8-tetracarboxylic dianhydride, 1,2,4,5-cyclohexanetetracarboxylic dianhydride, 1,6,7,12-tetrachloroperylene-3,4,9,10-tetracarboxylic dianhydride, 1,2,3,4-cyclobutanetetracarboxylic dianhydride, 1,6,7,12-tetrachlorotetraaryltetracarboxylic dianhydride, 3-methylglutaric anhydride, meso-butane-1,2,3,4-tetracarboxylic dianhydride, 2-bromonaphthalene-1,4 ,5,8-tetracarboxylic dianhydride, 1,2,3,4-cyclopentanetetracarboxylic dianhydride, divinyltriaminopentaacetic dianhydride, 2,6-dibromonaphthalene-1,4,5,8-tetracarboxylic dianhydride, 1,6,2,3-dianhydride- beta -D-mannanose, 3,3',4,4'-diphenylsulfonyl tetracarboxylic dianhydride, dibromopyromellitic dianhydride, dicyclohexyl-3,4,3',4'-tetracarboxylic dianhydride, 1,2,3,4-tetramethyl-1,2,3,4-cyclobutanetetracarboxylic dianhydride, tricyclo(6.4.0.0(2,7))dodecane-1,8,2,7-tetracarboxylic dianhydride, divinyltriaminopentaacetic dianhydride, 3-methylglutaric anhydride, 3-(tert-butyldimethylsiloxy) glutaric anhydride, perylene-3,4,9,10-tetracarboxylic dianhydride, bisphenol A dianhydride, hexafluorodianhydride, 3,3,3',3'-tetramethyl-1,1'-spirobisindane-5,5'-diamino-6,6'-diol, N,N,N',N'-tetra(p-aminophenyl)p-phenylenediamine, spirofluorene dianhydride, 3,3',4,4'-benzophenone tetracarboxylic dianhydride and diethyl anthracene dianhydride.
   Ar' = phenyl or its derivative, or naphthalenyl or its derivative;and
   X = Cl, Br or I.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the polyimide, which involves dissolving equimolar diamino triptycene or its derivatives and tetracarboxylic dianhydride in m-cresol, and adding isoquinoline as a catalyst to the reaction solution, initiating copolymerization reaction, precipitating in ethanol, and performing Soxhlet extraction to remove the m-cresol mixed in the polymer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Condition: The copolymerization is performed at 80-180 degrees C for 1-24 hours.
DC A26 (Other condensation polymers including silicone polymers and polyimides (mineral silicates and similar materials would not usually appear in Section A).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. PT P
PN CN112079374-A
TI Preparing nano-active calcium carbonate, comprises e.g. taking dimer acid and chitosan, and aluminate, maleic acid and polyethylene glycol laurate, performing carbonization of calcium hydroxide, carrying out surface modification and drying.
AU WU X
AE ENERGY RES INST JIANGXI ACAD SCI (ENER-Non-standard)
GA 2020C78679
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nano-active calcium carbonate, comprises e.g. taking dimer acid and chitosan as crystal form control agents, and aluminate, maleic acid and polyethylene glycol laurate as composite modifier, performing carbonization of calcium hydroxide, carrying out surface modification and drying of calcium carbonate to obtain final product. The method specifically comprises (i) carbonizing calcium hydroxide slurry with mass concentration of 5-12 wt.%, sieving, aging, adding crystal form control agent, passing carbon dioxide, and controlling carbonization temperature, stirring speed, and (ii) passing calcium carbonate slurry of step (i), then removing impurities, adjusting the pH value of regulator, and performing wet surface modification treatment on calcium carbonate using composite modifier, and (iii) drying modified calcium carbonate slurry of step (ii), filtering and dehydrating, vacuum drying and crushing.
   USE - The nano-active calcium carbonate is useful for plastic film (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The calcium carbonate: has Brunauer-emmett-teller range of 35-55 m2/g, particle size of 20-50 nm, activation degree of more than 99%, and excellent mechanical properties; and reaches maximum filling amount in polyethylene plastic film of 31%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing nano-active calcium carbonate, comprises taking dimer acid and chitosan as crystal form control agents, and aluminate, maleic acid and polyethylene glycol laurate as composite modifier, performing carbonization of calcium hydroxide, carrying out surface modification and drying of calcium carbonate to obtain final product. The method specifically comprises (i) carbonizing calcium hydroxide slurry with mass concentration of 5-12 wt.%, sieving through 200-mesh sieve to remove impurities, aging for 12 hours, adding crystal form control agent, passing carbon dioxide with volume concentration of 20-40%, and controlling carbonization temperature at 12-18 degrees C, stirring speed of 90-120 revolutions/minute, when reaching pH value to 6.5-7, then it is the end point of carbonization to obtain calcium carbonate slurry, where the crystal form control agent is mixture of dimer acid and chitosan with mass ratio of 3:1-3, the dosage is 1-2.5 wt.% the mass of calcium hydroxide dry basis, and the dimer acid is 20-40 wt.% of dimer acid in ethanol, and (ii) passing calcium carbonate slurry of step (i) through 200-mesh sieve, then removing impurities, adjusting the pH value of regulator to 7-9, and performing wet surface modification treatment on calcium carbonate using composite modifier for 60-90 minutes at 75-95 degrees C, where dosage of composite modifier is 0.5-3 wt.% the mass of calcium carbonate dry basis, and the composite modifier is mixture of aluminate, maleic acid and polyethylene glycol laurate in a mass ratio of 3-5:1-2:0.5-1, and (iii) drying modified calcium carbonate slurry of step (ii), then filtering and dehydrating, vacuum drying and crushing to obtain nano-activated calcium carbonate with Brunauer-emmett-teller of 35-55 m2/g, particle size of 20-50 nm, and activation degree of more than 99%.
DC E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); A11 (Polysaccharides; natural rubber; other natural polymers (only a restricted range of (modified) natural polymers are included. Thus starch would be excluded, but chemically modified starch included).); A25 (Polyurethanes; polyethers.)
MC E31-U01; E34-D03A; A08-M01; A08-M01B; A08-M01C; A08-M10; A08-R; A10-E07C; A10-E09; A11-A03; A12-S06; A12-W14

PT P
PN CN112022750-A; CN112022750-B
TI Compound essential oil used for producing nanocapsules for anti-allergic and acne-removing cosmetics, comprises combination of lavender essential oil, watercress essential oil and aidicha essential oil in preset ratio.
AU GONG Y
   ZHANG M
   LI H
AE UNIV QINGDAO SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYQS-C)
GA 2020C42766
AB    NOVELTY - A compound essential oil comprises a combination of lavender essential oil, watercress essential oil and aidicha essential oil in a ratio of 5-6:3-4:5-6. The lavender essential oil is obtained by cutting fresh fragrant leaves into small pieces, placing in an extraction tank, turning on a pressure pump and delivering the deionized water to a pre-heater at an initial temperature of 100 degrees C, continuously programming the temperature to 150 degrees C at 2 degrees C/minute and delivering the hot deionized water to the extraction tank, cooling to normal temperature, adding 0.1 g sodium chloride, separating the organic layer and drying with anhydrous sodium sulfate, and using a rotary evaporator to remove the organic solvents under vacuum. The watercress essential oil is obtained by crushing leaves of watercress, sieving and performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, filtering the obtained extract, recovering ethanol using a rotary evaporator and evaporating to dryness.
   USE - Compound essential oil is used for producing nanocapsules for anti-allergic and acne-removing cosmetics e.g. anti-allergy and anti-acne moisturizing lotion or anti-acne cream (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The compound essential oil provides nanocapsules which are capable of maintaining the stability of the effective ingredients of essential oils, and inhibiting volatilization of essential oils, bacteria, and deposition of melanin and allergies. The compound essential oil provides anti-allergic and acne-removing cosmetics which are non-toxic and non-irritating.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A compound essential oil comprises a combination of lavender essential oil, watercress essential oil and aidicha essential oil in a ratio of 5-6:3-4:5-6. The lavender essential oil is obtained by cutting fresh fragrant leaves into small pieces, placing in an extraction tank, turning on a pressure pump and delivering the deionized water to a pre-heater at an initial temperature of 100 degrees C at a rate of 20 mL/minute, continuously programming the temperature to 150 degrees C at 2 degrees C/minute and delivering the hot deionized water to the extraction tank at a rate of 20 mL/minute, cooling to normal temperature, adding 0.1 g sodium chloride, separating the organic layer and drying with anhydrous sodium sulfate, and using a rotary evaporator to remove the organic solvents under vacuum. The watercress essential oil is obtained by crushing leaves of watercress, sieving and performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction at a pressure of 45 MPa and a temperature of 50 degrees C for 3.5 hours, filtering the obtained extract, recovering ethanol using a rotary evaporator and evaporating to dryness. The aidicha essential oil is obtained by crushing aidicha leaves, sieving, adding to a round bottom flask, adding distilled water, adjusting the pH to 5.0 with hydrochloric acid, adding 0.5% composite enzyme comprising cellulase and pectinase into the container, placing in a water bath at a constant temperature of 45 degrees C for 35 minutes, transferring to a microwave dynamic extraction equipment barrel, setting the microwave power to 850 W and microwave radiation for 40 minutes, and connecting the volatile oil separator to the microwave extraction equipment to collect the distilled essential oils. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for compound essential oil nanocapsules, which are made into nanocapsules comprising 25 wt.% compound essential oil by using a complex aggregation method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The ratio of lavender essential oil, watercress essential oil and aidicha essential oil is preferably 5:3:5.
   ACTIVITY - Antiallergic; Antiseborrheic; Dermatological.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D23 (Oils, fats and waxes - including fatty acids, essential oils, but excluding butter (substitutes) and montan wax (C11B, C).); D21 (Preparations for dental or toilet purposes - including filling alloys, compositions for dentures or dental impressions, anti-caries chewing gum, plaque disclosing compositions, toothpastes, cosmetics, shampoos, topical anti-sunburn compositions and toilet soaps (A61K).)
MC B04-A08G2; B04-A10; B04-A98; B04-B01C1; B14-G02A; B14-N17D; B14-R01; B14-S18; D08-B09A1A

PT P
PN US2020248902-A1; US11060723-B2
TI Making coal for coal burning facility to reduce            emissions of mercury or other harmful components            arising from combustion of coal, comprises transporting            treated coal having applied sorbent composition            comprising halogen to facility.
AU COMRIE D C
AE NOX II LTD (NOXI-Non-standard)
GA 202074952Q
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing coal for a coal burning facility to                reduce emissions of mercury or other harmful                components arising from combustion of coal in a                furnace of the facility, comprises transporting a                treated coal having an applied sorbent composition                comprising a halogen to the coal burning facility,                where the treated coal is delivered to and                combusted in the furnace to make energy, combustion                gas, and ash, the treated coal reduces a quantity                of mercury in the combustion gas as compared to                mercury present in a combustion gas when coal is                combusted without the applied sorbent                composition.
   USE - The methods are useful for preparing coal for                coal burning facility to reduce emissions of                mercury or other harmful components arising from                combustion of coal in a furnace of the facility                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The methods: reduce emissions of elemental and                oxidized mercury; increase the level of mercury,                arsenic, lead and/or chlorine in the coal ash;                decrease the levels of leachable heavy metals (e.g.                mercury) in the ash, preferably to levels below the                detectable limits; and make a highly cementitious                ash product; reduce the levels of mercury emitted                into the atmosphere upon burning of                mercury-containing fuels e.g. coal; reduce costs of                waste disposal from coal burning utilities and                reduce the cost of concrete products for needed                building projects; utilizes the cementitious ash in                building products as total or partial replacement                for portland cement results in reduced carbon                dioxide emissions that result from the manufacture                of portland cement; avoids carbon dioxide emissions                from calcining of limestone to make portland cement                and the burning of fossil fuels to provide the                energy needed to make portland cement, the sorbent                components that tend to increase the efficiency of                energy production from burning of coal, further                reducing greenhouse emissions from the burning of                fossil fuel to produce energy; reduces sulfur                and/or mercury emissions from coal burning                facilities to protect the environment and comply                with government regulations and treaty obligations;                and prevents release of mercury and other heavy                metals into the environment by leaching from solid                wastes e.g. coal ash produced by burning the                mercury containing coal; and prevents or reduces                mercury emissions from such facilities as                coal-burning utilities leads to a variety of                environmental benefits, including less air                pollution, less water pollution and less hazardous                waste production, with less resulting ground                contamination.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing coal for a coal burning facility to                reduce emissions of mercury or other harmful                components arising from combustion of coal in a                furnace of the facility, comprising adding a                sorbent composition comprising a halogen to coal to                form a treated coal, and transporting the treated                coal to the coal burning facility, where the                treated coal is delivered to and combusted in the                furnace to make energy, combustion gas, and ash,                and the treated coal reduces a quantity of mercury                in the combustion gas as compared to mercury                present in a combustion gas when coal is combusted                without the sorbent composition.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The transporting is via a                  rail car. Prior to the transporting, the                  composition is applied to the coal on a belt or                  conveyor. Prior to the transporting, the                  composition is applied to the coal prior to or                  during processing in a crusher. Prior to the                  transporting, processing the coal in a pug mill,                  where the composition is applied to the coal                  prior to or during the processing the coal in the                  pug mill. The composition is applied to the coal                  as a liquid comprising calcium bromide. The                  method further comprises measuring a                  concentration of mercury in the combustion gas                  and adjusting a rate of adding the composition                  comprising a halogen based on a measured                  concentration of mercury in the combustion gas.                  The composition further comprises a source of                  calcium and the method further comprises                  adjusting a rate of addition of the source of                  calcium in the applied sorbent composition based                  on a measured concentration of sulfur in the                  combustion gas. The composition comprises an                  aluminosilicate material and the method further                  comprises measuring an amount of acid leachable                  mercury in the ash and adjusting an addition rate                  of aluminosilicate in the composition based on                  the amount of acid leachable mercury in the ash.                  Adding the sorbent composition comprises at least                  one of (i) applying the sorbent composition to                  the coal on a conveyor or a belt, (ii) applying                  the sorbent composition prior to or during                  processing the coal in a crusher, or (iii)                  applying the sorbent composition to the coal                  prior to or during processing in a pug mill.                  Preferred Components: The halogen is bromine and                  the composition comprises calcium bromide. The                  halogen is iodine and the composition comprises a                  compound of sodium iodide, potassium iodide                  and/or calcium iodide. Preferred Composition: The                  composition further comprises alumina and silica                  and the composition comprises at least one of an                  aluminosilicate clay or cement kiln dust.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.); Q73 (Combustion equipment/processes (F23))
MC H09-A; H09-F01; Q73-A15A; Q73-T05B; Q73-T07

PT P
PN CN110810804-A
TI Preparing kelp biological salt by rinsing salt on surface of kelp with water, adding in steam explosion tank for steam explosion treatment, mixing kelp slag, performing ultrasonic treatment, spray-drying, extracting, and concentrating.
AU JU W
   SUN Y
   TANG X
   WANG S
   BI N
   QIU M
   WANG P
   PENG L
AE HOMEY GROUP CO LTD (HOME-Non-standard)
GA 202018595G
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing kelp biological salt involves (a) selecting fresh kelp from May to June, rinsing the salt on the surface of the kelp with water, (b) adding in a steam explosion tank for steam explosion treatment for 10-15 minutes, adding distilled water, (c) mixing the kelp slag and the sterile distilled water to obtain a mixed material, adding 2-5 wt.% compound bacteria to the mixed material, performing ultrasonic treatment, (d) mixing the supernatant liquid obtained in step (b) with the fermented centrifuge liquid obtained in step (c) to obtain a mixed liquid, adding 2-3 wt.% mixed enzyme to the mixed liquid to adjust the pH to 5-8, at a temperature of 40-55 degrees C, (e) spray-drying the enzymolysis solution to obtain kelp biological salt powder, (f) extracting the kelp biological salt powder with supercritical carbon dioxide to collect the remaining biological salts, and (g) concentrating the supernatant under low pressure and lyophilizing, to obtain kelp biological salt.
   USE - The method is used for preparing kelp biological salt.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is efficient, easy to control, and beneficial to large-scale production, solves the problems of low extraction rate and high production cost, reduces the shape of kelp by pulverization, increases the exposed area of cells, breaks cells by steam explosion, provides 96% of biological salt extraction rate, and the raw materials used in the invention are abundant and readily available.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing kelp biological salt involves (a) selecting fresh kelp from May to June, rinsing the salt on the surface of the kelp with water, controlling the moisture, drying with a grinder and homogenizing, (b) weighing the homogenized kelp raw material obtained in step (a), adding in a steam explosion tank for steam explosion treatment for 10-15 minutes, adding distilled water, stirring and rinsing for 5-10 minutes, centrifuging and collecting the supernatant, adding distilled water, and stirring the mixture and washing 2-3 times, centrifuging, collecting the kelp residue and the supernatant, and mixing the supernatants for later use, (c) weighing the kelp slag obtained in step (b), mixing the kelp slag and the sterile distilled water at a mass ratio of 1:5-10 to obtain a mixed material, adding 2-5 wt.% compound bacteria to the mixed material, and performing fermentation for 8-24 hours at 55 degrees C and a pH value of 6-7 and performing the ultrasonic treatment, and collecting fermentation centrifuge liquid, (d) mixing the supernatant liquid obtained in step (b) with the fermented centrifuge liquid obtained in step (c) to obtain a mixed liquid, adding 2-3 wt.% mixed enzyme to the mixed liquid to adjust the pH to 5-8, at a temperature of 40-55 degrees C, using a shaker for enzymatic hydrolysis for 5-8 hours to obtain an enzymatic solution, (e) spray-drying the enzymolysis solution to obtain kelp biological salt powder, (f) extracting the kelp biological salt powder with supercritical carbon dioxide to collect the remaining biological salts, adding 1-3 times biological salt to 60-80 wt.% ethanol solution, stirring uniformly, leaving for 3-5 hours, centrifuging, and taking the supernatant, and (g) concentrating the supernatant under low pressure and lyophilizing, to obtain kelp biological salt.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The step (b) comprises adding distilled water for stirring and washing, where the weight ratio of the kelp raw material to the distilled water after steam explosion treatment is 1:(3-5). Preferred Microorganisms: The compound bacteria in step (c) comprise lactic acid bacteria and effective microorganism (EM) bacteria in a mass ratio of 1:(1-1.5). Preferred Components: The compound enzyme in step (d) is pectinase, cellulase, and pepsin, where the mass ratio of pectinase, cellulase, and pepsin is (3-5):(0.5-1.5):(1-3). Preferred Conditions: The conditions of the steam explosion in step (b) are: the pressure of 1-3 MPa, and a pressure maintaining time of 10-20 seconds. The conditions of the ultrasonic treatment are: an ultrasonic frequency of 10-15 kHz, an ultrasonic power of 0.5-2 W/cm2, and an ultrasonic application period of 5-8 minutes/hour. The conditions for the spray drying are: an inlet temperature of 180-200 degrees C, and a nozzle rotation speed of 20-50 Hz, where the flow rate is 2-5 ml/second, outlet temperature 70-90 degrees C, and the wind speed is 1-2 m/seconds. The conditions of the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction treatment are: a pressure of 15-20 MPa, a temperature of 20-30 degrees C, flow rate of carbon dioxide of 10-15 kg/hour and time of 10-20 minutes.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); V06 (Electromechanical Transducers and Small Machines)
MC D03-H01T2A; D05-A02; D05-A04D; V06-V01N

PT P
PN CN110551311-A
TI Physically foaming thermoplastic polyurethane, comprises e.g. placing thermoplastic polyurethane in an oven, pouring thermoplastic elastomer, drying, stirring, discharging mixture and heating reaction kettle.
AU CHEN W
   LIN T
AE FUJIAN PUTIAN LIANSHENG SHOES CO LTD (FUJI-Non-standard)
GA 2019A7008M
AB    NOVELTY - Physically foaming thermoplastic polyurethane (TPU), comprises e.g. placing 80 kg thermoplastic polyurethane in an oven, and drying at 120 degrees C for 1.5 hours, pouring the dried TPU into a mixing bucket, pouring 5 kg thermoplastic elastomer (POE), 15 kg ethylene-vinyl acetate (EVA), 8 kg titanium dioxide, 6 kg zinc oxide and 3 kg stearic acid to form a mixture, stirring at 60 degrees C for time 30 minutes, and discharging the mixture, pouring the mixture into the barrel of the twin screw extruder, the temperature of the screw is 200-220 degrees C, the temperature of the last section of the barrel of the twin screw extruder is 140-160 degrees C, and die temperature is 210-230 degrees C, pelletizing the material extruded from the die of the twin-screw extruder in water, and the particle size is generally 40 particles/g or 70 particles/g, after drying the TPU granules in the water pelletizing process, leaving for 2 hours to evaporate and cool the heat and moisture on the surface of the TPU granules.
   USE - The method is useful for physically foaming thermoplastic polyurethane.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Physically foaming thermoplastic polyurethane (TPU), comprises (i) placing 80 kg thermoplastic polyurethane in an oven, and drying at 120 degrees C for 1.5 hours, (ii) pouring the dried TPU into a mixing bucket, pouring 5 kg thermoplastic elastomer (POE), 15 kg ethylene-vinyl acetate (EVA), 8 kg titanium dioxide, 6 kg zinc oxide and 3 kg stearic acid to form a mixture, stirring at 60 degrees C for time 30 minutes, and discharging the mixture, (iii) pouring the mixture into the barrel of the twin screw extruder, the temperature of the screw is 200-220 degrees C, the temperature of the last section of the barrel of the twin screw extruder is 140-160 degrees C, and die temperature is 210-230 degrees C, (iv) pelletizing the material extruded from the die of the twin-screw extruder in water, and the particle size is generally 40 particles/g or 70 particles/g, (v) after drying the TPU granules in the water pelletizing process, leaving for 2 hours to evaporate and cool the heat and moisture on the surface of the TPU granules, (vi) placing the TPU particles into a reaction kettle with a pressure of 150 Mpa or higher, pouring tap water, and closing the valve, (vii) opening the inlet valve of the reactor and passing in 50 kg carbon dioxide and 60 kg nitrogen, (v) after the gas turning off, closing the inlet valve of the reactor, and checking whether the inlet valve and the inlet valve of the reactor are leaking, (ix) checking the valves of the reaction kettle, turning on the stirring motor to stir the TPU particles for 1 hour, the reaction kettle is not heated to allow the TPU particles to fully penetrate with carbon dioxide and nitrogen, which is more conducive to the foaming of the TPU particles, (x) after stirring for 1 hour, heating the reaction kettle, the temperature of the thermal conductivity is 160-200 degrees C, and (xi) opening the discharge valve of the reactor when the temperature in the reaction kettle reaches the target temperature of the TPU particle foaming, and discharging for 3-4 seconds, drying the foamed TPU particles.
DC A32 (Polymer fabrication - such as moulding, extrusion, forming, laminating, spinning.); A17 (Polymers of unsubstituted aliphatic monoolefins; including polyethylene.); A25 (Polyurethanes; polyethers.); A94 (Semi-finished materials - fibres, films, foams.)
MC A04-G01B; A04-G07; A07-A04E; A08-A01; A08-B02; A08-B03; A08-E02; A08-M03B; A08-M09B; A11-A04; A11-B06B; A12-S02C; A12-S04A2; A12-S09A

PN CN110434431-A
TI High strength steel welding with precise quality control comprises determining welding parameters by testing, carrying out tensile test, selecting welding consumables, carrying out pre-welding preparation and post-welding treatment.
AU JIANG J
   PENG Z
AE UNIV GUANGZHOU (UNGZ-C)
GA 201996911C
AB    NOVELTY - High-strength steel welding with precise quality control comprises determining welding parameters by testing the test piece by thermal simulation test machine and heating to a predetermined preheating temperature, further heating the test piece by welding heat input, cooling to normal temperature, heating the test piece to proposed post-weld heat treatment temperature, preserving heat, cooling to normal temperature, carrying out tensile test, selecting welding consumables comprises welding with carbon dioxide protective welding by a solid welding wire, carrying out pre-welding preparation by opening V-shaped groove with no blunt edge, preheating the V-shaped groove, carrying out welding at 27-32 V welding voltage, 260-290 A welding current, 4.5-5.9 mm/s welding speed and 25 l/min shielding gas flow rate and carrying out post-welding treatment comprises heat treatment of welds of weldments and parts within 60 mm of both sides of the weld and cooling to normal temperature.
   USE - The method is useful for high-strength steel welding with precise quality control.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves high tensile steel welding quality.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - High-strength steel welding with precise quality control comprises determining the welding parameters by testing the test piece by thermal simulation test machine and heating the test piece to a predetermined preheating temperature at a heating rate of 10 degrees C /s and maintaining for 5 second, further heating the test piece by welding heat input, heating to peak temperature and allowing to stand for 1 second, cooling to normal temperature, heating the test piece to proposed post-weld heat treatment temperature at a rate of 200 degrees C/hour, preserving heat for 2 hour, cooling to a normal temperature at a cooling rate of 100 degrees C/hour, carrying out tensile test of the test piece, where the proposed welding heat input and the proposed post-weld heat treatment temperature qualifies welding parameters if the tensile strength of the test piece is less than or equal to 690 MPa, selecting welding consumables comprises welding with carbon dioxide protective welding by a solid welding wire with a wire diameter of 1.2 mm and a diffusing hydrogen content of greater than or equal to 5 ml/100 g, carrying out pre-welding preparation by opening V-shaped groove with no blunt edge by laser cutting the portion to be welded, preheating the V-shaped groove of the weldment at a temperature determined in the step of determining the welding parameter, carrying out welding, where the parameter value during the welding process comprises welding heat input determined in the step of determining the welding parameter, 27-32 V welding voltage, 260-290 A welding current, 4.5-5.9 mm/s welding speed and 25L/min shielding gas flow rate and carrying out post-welding treatment comprises heat treatment of welds of weldments and parts within 60 mm of both sides of the weld, where the heat treatment temperature is determined in the step of determining the welding parameters and heat treatment rate during the heat treatment is 200 degrees C/hours, maintaining the temperature for 2 hours after heating to the post-weld heat treatment temperature and cooling to normal temperature at a rate of 100 degrees C/hour.
DC M23 (Soldering; welding - including brazing, flame cutting and scarfing, cutting and welding rods, soldering and unsoldering apparatus and solder compositions (B23K).); X24 (Electric Welding)

PN CN110421658-A
TI Method for preparing sound insulation and heat preservation household wood board material, involves mixing aerogel wood and enzymatic concentrate, and injecting mixed billet into sheet mold, followed by drying and solidifying.
AU MA M
AE ZHEJIANG SENNUOCHEN CLOTHING CO LTD (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 201995585L
AB    NOVELTY - A sound insulation and heat preservation household wood board material preparing method involves mixing an aerogel wood and an enzymatic concentrate, and stirring in a blender to obtain a mixed billet, injecting the mixed billet into a sheet mold, coating the phenolic resin glue on the surface of the sheet parison with a rubberizing roller, and feeding the coated parison blank into a molding machine, drying and solidifying to obtain the finished product, where the aerogel wood is prepared by weighing a mountain jute wood and putting into the pulverizer to obtain the sassafras numb powder, putting the yam numb powder in a oven, and drying, mixing the dried hawthorn eucalyptus powder and the 1-propenyl-3-methylimidazolium ionic liquid into an oil bath, adding anhydrous ethanol to the cellulose ion mixture, depositing a cellulose gel block, and rinsing with anhydrous ethanol, drying the washed cellulose gel block in a supercritical carbon dioxide drying device to obtain aerogel wood.
   USE - Method for preparing a sound insulation and heat preservation household wood board material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparing a sound insulation and heat preservation household wood board material with better impact resistance and stability, excellent sound insulation effect, excellent thermal insulation effect and wide application prospect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A sound insulation and heat preservation household wood board material preparing method involves mixing an aerogel wood and an enzymatic concentrate, and stirring in a blender for 20-30 minutes to obtain a mixed billet, injecting the mixed billet into a sheet mold, and pre-pressing to obtain a sheet parison, coating the phenolic resin glue on the surface of the sheet parison with a rubberizing roller, and feeding the coated parison blank into a molding machine, drying and solidifying by hot pressing to obtain the finished product, where the aerogel wood is prepared by weighing a mountain jute wood and putting into the pulverizer for 1-2 hours to obtain the sassafras numb powder, putting the yam numb powder in a vacuum drying oven, and drying it to dryness to obtain dry sassafras numb powder, mixing the dried hawthorn eucalyptus powder and the 1-propenyl-3-methylimidazolium ionic liquid into an oil bath, and stirring and dissolving at an oil bath temperature of 80-90 degrees C to obtain cellulose ion mixture, adding an equal volume of anhydrous ethanol to the cellulose ion mixture, depositing a cellulose gel block, separating and obtaining a cellulose gel block by filtration, and rinsing with anhydrous ethanol for 3-5 times, drying the washed cellulose gel block in a supercritical carbon dioxide drying device to obtain aerogel wood, and the enzymatic hydrolysate is prepared by weighing the bamboo fiber and pulverized bamboo fiber material, mixing the bamboo fiber pulverized material with the pectinase and deionized water, and putting it into the enzymatic hydrolysis tank, putting the enzymatic hydrolysis tank into the incubator, heating at 30-40 degrees C for 6-8 hours, after the completion of the above enzymatic hydrolysis treatment t obtain an enzymatic hydrolyzate product, transferring the obtained enzymatic hydrolyzed product to a rotary evaporator, and subjecting the mixture to a rotary evaporation concentration treatment, and after the rotary evaporation concentration treatment is completed to obtain an enzymatic hydrolyzate.

PT P
PN CN110358794-A
TI Enhancing sludge carbon source conversion by            strengthening sulfate radicals based on interspecific            hydrogen transfer by preparing sludge, directly            domesticating and enriching sulfate-reducing bacteria,            and performing anaerobic fermentation.
AU ZHOU A
   LIU H
   YUE X
   ZHANG J
   ZHAO B
AE UNIV TAIYUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYTL-C)
GA 201988810C
AB    NOVELTY - Method for enhancing sludge carbon source                conversion by strengthening sulfate radicals based                on interspecific hydrogen transfer, involves (a)                preparing sludge sample, (b) enriching and                domesticating degrading fatty acid bacteria liquid                with acetic acid and carbon dioxide as end                products, using traditional continuous passage                enrichment, transferring to the same enrichment                medium at 10% inoculation every 3 days, adding                carbon sources with respect to 1000 g chemical                oxygen demand (COD) conversion, adjusting the pH,                incubating in an air bath shaker, transferring 10                plus minus 2 times, and obtaining concentrated                degraded fatty acid bacteria solution i.e.                sulfate-reducing bacteria (SRB) solution by                centrifugation, (c) adding sulfite to the sludge                for pretreating, adding the SRB solution into the                pretreated sludge, and adjusting the pH, and (d)                transferring the mixture to a reaction flask,                purging the reaction flask with nitrogen for 15                minutes, sealing and placing the bottle in shaker                and fermenting.
   USE - The method is useful for enhancing sludge                carbon source conversion by strengthening sulfate                radicals based on interspecific hydrogen                transfer.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for enhancing sludge carbon source                conversion by strengthening sulfate radicals based                on interspecific hydrogen transfer, involves (a)                preparing sludge sample by naturally maintaining                sludge under stirring condition at 4 degrees C for                24-30 hours, (b) enriching and domesticating                degrading fatty acid bacteria liquid with acetic                acid and carbon dioxide as end products under                anaerobic conditions using mixed propionic acid,                n/isobutyric acid and n/isovaleric acid as carbon                sources, and the remaining activated sludge from                the sewage treatment plant as a separation source,                where the ratio of the remaining activated sludge                to the medium is mixed in a volume ratio of 1:10,                using traditional continuous passage enrichment,                transferring to the same enrichment medium at 10%                inoculation every 3 days, where the content of each                component of the culture medium is 0.6875 g                ammonium sulfate and 10 ml trace elements, adding                carbon sources with respect to 1000 g chemical                oxygen demand (COD) conversion, adjusting the pH to                7-7.8 using 10% dilute sulfuric acid or 1 mol/l                sodium hydroxide, incubating at speed of 120                revolutions per minute (rpm) and a temperature of                33 degrees C in an air bath shaker, continuously                transferring 10 plus minus 2 times, and obtaining                concentrated degraded fatty acid bacteria solution                i.e. sulfate-reducing bacteria (SRB) solution by                centrifugation, (c) adding sulfite to the sludge                for pretreating, where the dosage is 56 mg Fe(VI)/g                TSS-32mg-S(IV)/g TSS, adding the SRB solution into                the pretreated sludge, where the ratio of the SRB                broth is volatile suspended solids (VSS) sludge:VSS                bacterium solution of 100:1, and adjusting the pH                to 7 plus minus 0.5, and (d) transferring the                mixture to a reaction flask, purging the reaction                flask with nitrogen for 15 minutes, sealing and                placing the bottle in an air bath shaker for                performing anaerobic fermentation at speed of                110-120 rpm and fermenting at 30-35 degrees C for                1-10 days.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Method: The SRB bacteria are                  domesticated by mixing with carbon sources. The                  sulfate radical is obtained by reacting potassium                  ferrate with sodium sulfite. The sulfuric acid                  radical is used to pretreat the sludge, and the                  product is used as an electron acceptor of the                  SRB bacteria. The method involves removing the                  impurities such as gravel having a larger                  particle size in the sludge using a 40 mesh                  sieve, and storing the obtained concentrated                  residual sludge in a refrigerator at 4 degrees                  C.

PT P
PN CN110028470-A; CN110028470-B
TI Preparing dehydroacetic acid and patchouli compounds, comprises e.g. reacting 4-hydroxy-2-pyrone substrate, monovalent copper salt catalyst and cesium carbonate, acidifying with hydrochloric acid, extracting, and post processing.
AU MA J
   WANG Z
   CHENG P
AE UNIV NANKAI (UNAN-C)
   UNIV NANKAI (UNAN-C)
GA 201965132G
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing dehydroacetic acid and patchouli compounds (V), comprises e.g. (i) adding 4-hydroxy-2-pyrone substrate (I), monovalent copper salt catalyst and cesium carbonate in a Schleck bottle, introducing carbon dioxide gas in Schleck bottle, then adding solvent anhydrous N,N-dimethylformamide, (ii) acidifying with hydrochloric acid, extracting and purifying to obtain pure intermediate 3-carboxy-4-hydroxy-2-pyrone compounds (II), (iii) adding (II), 2-(methylamino)pyridine and isopropanol to container, then adding solvent, reacting with 1-(3-dimethylaminopropyl)-3-ethylcarbodiimide hydrochloride, extracting and separating to obtain compounds (III), either (iv) adding (III) and sodium hydride to anhydrous tetrahydrofuran solution, reacting with methyl magnesium bromide and separating to obtain dehydroacetic acid or its similar compounds (IV), or (v) adding (III) and sodium hydride to anhydrous tetrahydrofuran solution, reacting with isoamyl magnesium bromide and separating to obtain (V).
   USE - The method is useful for preparing dehydroacetic acid and patchouli compounds.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: uses low toxicity and low cost raw material sources and less device equipments; is simple and suitable for industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing dehydroacetic acid and patchouli compounds of formula (V), comprises (i) adding 4-hydroxy-2-pyrone substrate of formula (I), monovalent copper salt catalyst and cesium carbonate in a Schleck bottle, vacuuming the Schleck bottle and introducing carbon dioxide gas in the Schleck bottle, then adding solvent anhydrous N,N-dimethylformamide to the Schleck bottle, and placing in 50-60 degrees C oil bath, (ii) acidifying with hydrochloric acid, then extracting, then passing through a silica gel column to obtain pure intermediate 3-carboxy-4-hydroxy-2-pyrone compounds of formula (II) having recovery yield of 40-56%, (iii) adding (II), 2-(methylamino)pyridine and isopropanol into a container, then adding solvent (toluene), stirring for 10-30 minutes, adding 1-(3-dimethylaminopropyl)-3-ethylcarbodiimide hydrochloride, reacting at 50 degrees C on oil bath for 16 hours, transferring to 100 ml pear-shaped flask, adding isopropanol and washing the flask, vacuum extracting and removing the solvent, and then separating by column chromatography to obtain isopropyl 4-hydroxy-2-oxo-2H-pyran-3-carboxylate compounds of formula (III), where the ratio between (II), 2-(methylamino)pyridine, isopropanol, 1-(3-dimethylaminopropyl)-3-ethylcarbodiimide hydrochloride and solvent is 1 mmol:1.2 mmol:3 mmol:1.2 mmol:30 ml, either (iv) adding (III) and sodium hydride to anhydrous tetrahydrofuran solution, then adding methyl magnesium bromide, reacting at room temperature for 2-3 hours to obtain crude product, and separating by column chromatography to obtain dehydroacetic acid or its similar compounds of formula (IV), where the ratio between (III), sodium hydride, methyl magnesium bromide and anhydrous tetrahydrofuran is 1 mmol:4 mmol:1.5 mmol:20 ml, or (v) adding (III) and sodium hydride to anhydrous tetrahydrofuran solution, then adding isoamyl magnesium bromide, reacting at room temperature for 2-3 hours to obtain crude product, and then separating by column chromatography to obtain (V), where the ratio between (III), sodium hydride, isoamyl magnesium bromide and anhydrous tetrahydrofuran is 1 mmol:4mmol:1.5 mmol:20 ml.
   R1 = H or methyl; and
   R2 = methyl, ethyl, propyl or isopropyl.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The monovalent copper salt catalyst is cuprous iodide.

TI Method for customizing compound fertilizer, involves analyzing soil and meteorology of crops and target areas, collecting crop basis big data of crops, and meteorological big data of target areas, and compound fertilizer big data.
AU WEI Z
   CAO Z
   WEN B
AE CHENGDU JINLUFU BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (CHEN-Non-standard)
GA 201958687N
AB    NOVELTY - A compound fertilizer customizing method involves analyzing soil and meteorology of crops and target areas, collecting crop basis big data of crops, soil big data and meteorological big data of target areas, and compound fertilizer big data, where crop base big data includes a crop base factor, a weight b corresponding to the crop base factor, an index factor B corresponding to the crop base factor. Big data processing by performing big data calculation on the collected crop basic big data, soil big data, meteorological big data and compound fertilizer big data, and calculating the required soil in the target area. The percentage value of element content in custom compound fertilizer is obtained by expression as given in the specification, and calculating custom compound fertilizer according to the mass percentage value of each element in the customized compound fertilizer, selecting the corresponding raw materials to complete the preparation of the customized compound fertilizer.
   USE - Method for customizing compound fertilizer.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A compound fertilizer customizing method involves analyzing soil and meteorology of crops and target areas, collecting crop basis big data of crops, soil big data and meteorological big data of target areas, and compound fertilizer big data, where crop base big data includes a crop base factor, a weight b corresponding to the crop base factor, an index factor B corresponding to the crop base factor, and the crop base factor is an element according to different growth stages of the crop, the value assigned by the demand, and soil big data includes a soil factor, a weight corresponding to the soil factor, an index factor corresponding to the soil factor, and the soil factor includes a soil texture factor, a soil structural factor, a soil elemental element factor, and a soil trace element factors, soil organic matter factors, soil microbial factors, soil moisture factors, soil air factors, soil temperature factors, soil pH factors, eteorological big data includes a meteorological impact factor, a weight corresponding to the meteorological impact factor, and an exponential factor corresponding to the meteorological impact factor, the meteorological impact factor includes an illumination factor, a humidity factor, a rainfall factor, and a temperature factor, temperature difference factor, carbon dioxide concentration factor, wind factor, evaporation factor, seasonal factor, the meteorological impact factor is based on the degree of influence of light, humidity, rainfall, temperature, temperature difference, carbon dioxide concentration, wind, evaporation or season, compound fertilizer big data includes a compound fertilizer composition factor, compound fertilizer component factor is based on nitrogen, phosphorus, potassium, sulfur, magnesium, calcium, silicon, boron, zinc, molybdenum, iron, manganese, copper, organic matter and biological bacteria are respectively assigned values, among which nitrogen, phosphorus and potassium are a large number of elements, and sulfur, magnesium, calcium and silicon are medium quantities. Big data processing by performing big data calculation on the collected crop basic big data, soil big data, meteorological big data and compound fertilizer big data, and calculating the required soil in the target area. The percentage value of element content in custom compound fertilizer is obtained by expression as given in the specification, and calculating custom compound fertilizer according to the mass percentage value of each element in the customized compound fertilizer, selecting the corresponding raw materials to complete the preparation of the customized compound fertilizer.

PN CN109675918-A; CN109675918-B
TI Method for performing in situ repair of heavy metal contaminated farmland, involves setting working cushion layer and plastic film on mellow soil followed by using rinsed filter tube and vacuum collecting filter tube and using green eluent.
AU JIANG G
   ZHOU G
   NA P
   WANG L
   CHEN X
   ZHANG C
AE BEIJING CHEM ENG & METALLURGY RES INST (CNNU-C)
GA 2019407804
AB    NOVELTY - An in situ repair of heavy metal contaminated farmland performing method involves setting working cushion layer and plastic film on mellow soil of farmland, passing rinsed filter tube and vacuum collecting filter tube through the plastic film, inserting the working cushion layer into the mellow soil, forming a pumping system, inserting the filter tube into the mellow soil to uniformly set small hole on outer surface, such that opening portion is wrapped in 3 layers with gauze, blocking smudge particles into the filter tube, making eluent to enter the filter tube through gauze, inserting the vacuum collecting filter tube into the mellow soil to uniformly set small hole on outer surface, such that opening portion is wrapped in 3 layers with gauze, blocking the smudge particles into the vacuum collecting filter tube, allowing the eluent to pass through the gauze into the vacuum collecting filter tube followed by utilizing green eluent.
   USE - Method for performing in situ repair of heavy metal contaminated farmland.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables performing in situ repair of heavy metal contaminated farmland in a simple and convenient manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An in situ repair of heavy metal contaminated farmland performing method involves setting working cushion layer and plastic film on mellow soil of farmland, passing rinsed filter tube and vacuum collecting filter tube through the plastic film, inserting the working cushion layer into the mellow soil, forming a pumping system, inserting the filter tube into the mellow soil to uniformly set small hole on outer surface, such that opening portion is wrapped in 3 layers with gauze, blocking smudge particles into the filter tube, making eluent to enter the filter tube through gauze, inserting the vacuum collecting filter tube into the mellow soil to uniformly set small hole on outer surface, such that opening portion is wrapped in 3 layers with gauze, blocking the smudge particles into the vacuum collecting filter tube, allowing the eluent to pass through the gauze into the vacuum collecting filter tube, adding 0.4-0.04mol/l EDTA and 0.2-0.02 mol/l citric acid in a volume ratio of 1:1 to obtain eluent, adding oxygen and carbon dioxide to the eluent using static mixer to obtain green eluent containing oxygen and carbon dioxide, adding 1-1.5 g/l ammonium bicarbonate to the green eluent, when calcium content in the soil is less than or equal to 0.5%, setting repair cycle (T) to control reaction time of the green eluent in the mellow soil (T is 5-10 days), such that well spacing is Tx x, if the green eluent has migration rate of x m/day in the mellow soil, making the green eluent to enter the mellow soil through the rinsed filter tube, performing oxidation of green eluent and mellow soil and complexation reaction, transferring heavy metals in the mellow soil to liquid phase, completing in situ repair of heavy metal contaminated farmland and forming eluent, where chemical component of citric acid is trimethyl 3-hydroxy-1,3,5-pentanetricarboxylate.
