TI Manufacturing binder containing hydratable            material for manufacturing building element by            providing starting material from raw materials            convertible into hydratable material, tempering and            cooling the hydratable material.
AU ZAJAC M
   SKOCEK J
   BOLTE G
   BEN HAHA M
   BEN H M
AE HEIDELBERGCEMENT AG (HEID-Non-standard)
   HEIDELBERGCEMENT AG (HEID-Non-standard)
   HCONNECT 2 GMBH (HCON-Non-standard)
GA 201932081B
AB    NOVELTY - A binder containing hydratable material is                manufactured by providing a starting material from                greater than or equal to 1 raw materials                convertible by tempering at 600-1200 degrees C into                the hydratable material where starting material has                calcium (Ca)/silicon (Si) molar ratio of 0.5-1.8                and contains less than or equal to 30 wt.% elements                than Ca and Si, tempering the starting material at                600-1200 degrees C for 1 minute-5 hours to provide                hydratable material containing not greater than 10                wt.% monocalcium silicate and 15 wt.% hydratable                phases in the form of lime and dicalcium silicate,                where greater than or equal to 50% of the                crystalline hydratable phases are dicalcium                silicate, residence time and tempering temperature                are adapted to obtain hydratable material by                converting not greater than 80 wt.% starting                material, and cooling the hydratable material to                provide the binder containing hydratable                material.
   USE - Manufacture of binder containing hydratable                material for manufacturing building element                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The building element has less dense shales of                hydrates-carbonates product and enables higher                carbonation and hydration degrees. The binder has                lower molar Ca/Si ratio than Portland cement (OPC),                resulting in less energy consumption and carbon                dioxide release for calcination of limestone.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                manufacturing a building element from a binder                containing hydratable material which involves                providing a starting material from greater than or                equal to 1 raw materials convertible by tempering                at 600-1200 degrees C into hydratable material,                where starting material has a Ca/Si molar ratio                0.5-1.8 and contains less than or equal to 30 wt.%                other elements than Ca and Si, tempering the                starting material at 600-1200 degrees C during a                residence time of 1 minute-5 hours to provide the                hydratable material containing not greater than 10                wt.% monocalcium silicate and greater than or equal                to 15 wt.% hydratable phases in the form of lime                and dicalcium silicate, where greater than or equal                to 50% hydratable phases are dicalcium silicate,                residence time and tempering temperature are                adapted to obtain hydratable material by converting                not greater than 80 wt.% starting material, cooling                the hydratable material to provide the binder                comprising the hydratable material, grinding the                binder to a fineness according to the determination                of the particle size distribution by laser                granulometry of less than or equal to 90 mu m,                preferably less than or equal to 60 mu m, where                Rosin Rammler Parameter (slope)n can vary from                0.6-1.4, preferably 0.7-1.2 if the binder is                coarser, mixing the binder with water and                optionally greater than or equal to 1 of aggregate,                additives, and mixtures to obtain a binder paste,                placing the binder paste, hydrating the binder                paste until the amount of bound water is greater                than or equal to 5% of hydraulic phases mass in dry                binder and carbonating the binder paste until the                amount of additionally bound carbon dioxide is                greater than or equal to 150% of the initially                bound water mass and/or strength exceeds 2 MPa                while still keeping the bound water greater than or                equal to 50% of the initially bound water.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: Method further includes                  grinding the binder to fineness according to                  determination of particle size distribution by                  laser granulometry of D90 less than or equal to                  90 mu m, preferably less than or equal to 60 mu                  m, where Rosin Rammler Parameter (slope)n can                  vary from 0.6-1.4, preferably 0.7-1.2. Placing                  includes filling the binder paste into a mold or                  formwork. Carbonation is carried out with gaseous                  carbon dioxide. The gaseous carbon dioxide is                  supplied with a pressure from atmospheric                  pressure to 6 bar, preferably 2-4 bar, with a                  carbon dioxide concentration of greater than or                  equal to 1 vol.%, preferably greater than or                  equal to 5 vol.%. Carbonation is carried out with                  carbon dioxide dissolved during the carbonation                  process in an aqueous alkaline solution.                  Carbonation is carried out with carbon dioxide                  dissolved during the carbonation process in an                  aqueous solution containing substances improving                  the dissolution of the calcium ions. Carbonation                  is carried out with carbon dioxide dissolved                  during the carbonation process in an aqueous                  solution containing substances improving the                  dissolution of the carbonate ions. Preferred                  Components: Hydratable material contains greater                  than or equal to 15 wt.% dicalcium silicate, less                  than or equal to 10 wt.% monocaclium silicate,                  and 20-80 wt.% raw materials and/or formed during                  transformation step such as quartz, tricarbon                  disulfide (C3S2), tricarbon monosulfide (C3S),                  tricalcium aluminate (C3A), tetracalcium                  aluminoferrite (C4AF), gehlenite, merwenite,                  altered hydrated phases, glassy and/or X-ray                  amorphous components, and other rock- and                  soil-forming minerals. Raw materials are concrete                  fines, overburden from quarries, low quality                  limestone, marl, and dolomite, where Portland                  cement raw material limestone, clay and/or                  bauxite to adjust Ca/Si ratio when needed.                  Building element is precast concrete portion or                  concrete good. Preferred Conditions: Tempering                  temperature is 800-1100 degrees C, preferably                  800-1000 degrees C. Residence time is 5 minutes-2                  hours, preferably 30 minutes-2 hours. The                  solution has initially a pH of 8-14 accounting                  for the activity index of hydroxide.

PT P
PN CN109593820-A
TI Detecting anticancer activity of Rhododendron molle extract useful for preparing drug for inhibiting colon cancer comprises detecting inhibitory activity, analyzing apoptosis and cycle arrest, calculating expression level of gene.
AU LUO X
   ZHANG J
   CHEN M
   DAI L
   LIU J
   ZONG L
AE UNIV JIANGXI NORMAL (UYJX-C)
GA 201934986P
AB    NOVELTY - Detecting anticancer activity of Rhododendron molle extract comprises (a) inoculating cancer cells, incubating, treating with different concentrations of the Rhododendron molle extract, incubating, detecting the proliferation inhibition effect, (b) sub-culturing of HT-29 cells, replacing the culture medium, co-culturing, pipetting out the medium, washing, fixing, staining and evaluating the effect, (c) replacing the medium with complete medium, adding control group, centrifuging the cells and detecting the apoptosis and cycle of the cells by flow cytometry and (d) inoculating HT-29 cells, incubating, treating extract, harvesting to extract total RNA, using reverse transcription system kit for reverse transcription of the RNA, quantitative analysis of the expression levels of Bax, Bcl-2, P21 and P53 using real-time fluorescent quantitative kit, performing normalization using the expression level of the beta -actin gene and calculating the expression level of the gene.
   USE - The extract is useful for preparing a drug for inhibiting colon cancer (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides technical support and scientific basis for developing medicines for treating cancer.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Detecting anticancer activity of Rhododendron molle extract comprises (a) inoculating many cancer cells to be detected in a 96-well plate, incubating in 5% carbon dioxide incubator for 24 hours in 37 degrees C, treating each cancer cell with different concentrations of the Rhododendron molle extract, continuously incubating for 48-72 hours, detecting the proliferation inhibition effect of Rhododendron molle extract on different cancer cell cells by 3-(4,5-Dimethylthiazol-2-yl)-2,5-Diphenyltetrazolium Bromide (MTT) method, (b) sub-culturing of the logarithmically growing colon cancer HT-29 cells at 37 degrees C for 24 hours, replacing the culture medium with medium containing different concentrations of Rhododendron molle extract, co-culturing in 5% carbon dioxide incubator for 13 days at 37 degrees C, pipetting out the medium, washing with phosphate-buffered saline (PBS), fixing with 4% para formaldehyde, staining with crystal violet and evaluating the effect of the extract of Rhododendron molle on the formation of HT-29 cells by the number of clone formation and the rate of colony formation, (c) replacing the medium with complete medium containing different concentrations of extract after the subculture of HT-29 cells in logarithmic growth phase for 24 hours, adding a control group with an equal amount of complete medium containing dimethyl sulfoxide (DMSO) for 24 hours, centrifuging the cells into a single cell suspension to collect the cells and detecting the apoptosis and cycle of the cells by flow cytometry and (d) inoculating HT-29 cells in logarithmic growth phase into 6-well plates, incubating overnight, treating with Rhododendron molle extract of different concentrations for 12 hours, harvesting the cells to extract total RNA with trizol, using reverse transcription system kit for reverse transcription of the RNA, quantitative analysis of the expression levels of Bax, Bcl-2, P21 and P53 using real-time fluorescent quantitative kit, performing normalization using the expression level of the beta -actin gene and calculating the expression level of the gene using 2- Delta Delta CT.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The concentration of Rhododendron molle extract in the step: (a) is 400, 200, 100, 50, 25 micrograms/ml, respectively and the detection wavelength of the tetrazolium salt colorimetric method is 570 nm; (b) is 400, 200, 100, 50, 25, 0 micrograms/ml in the medium; (c) is 400, 200, 100, 50, 25 micrograms/ml in the complete medium containing the different concentrations and (d) is 400, 200, 100, and 0 micrograms/ml respectively.
   ACTIVITY - Cytostatic.
DC B05 (Other organics - aromatics, aliphatic, organo-metallics, compounds whose substituents vary such that they would be classified in several of B01 - B05.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); T01 (Digital Computers)
MC B04-A08G2; B04-A10; B04-E01B; B04-E02B; B04-E02F; B04-E03B; B04-E03F; B04-H20C1; B04-N02; B05-C07; B07-D13; B07-F01; B11-C07B3; B11-C08F8; B12-K04E3; B12-K04F; B12-K04G2A; B14-H01E1; D05-H09; T01-J06A
IP A61K-036/45; A61P-035/00; C12Q-001/02

      WO2014092532-A1   MALAYSIAN PALM OIL BOARD (MPOB)   GUPTA S V, KHOSLA P, JI X, SAMBANTHAMURTHI R, TAN Y A

PT P
PN CN109503676-A; CN109503676-B
TI Preparing xylitol and mixed syrup from a xylose mother liquor, comprises taking mother liquor of xylose, adding calcium hydroxide solution into mother liquor of xylose, adjusting pH and transforming calcium hydroxide to calcium carbonate.
AU WANG X
   LIAO C
   ZHENG Y
   JIANG X
   LI Z
   ZHANG J
   ZHANG W
AE ZHEJIANG HUAKANG PHARM CO LTD (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
   JIAOZUO HUAKANG SUGAR ALCOHOL TECHNOLOGY (JIAO-Non-standard)
GA 201929204H
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing xylitol and mixed syrup from a xylose mother liquor, comprises taking the mother liquor of xylose, adding calcium hydroxide solution into the mother liquor of xylose, adjusting the pH to 8.5-9, passing carbon dioxide so as to saturate the pH to 6.6-7, and transforming calcium hydroxide to calcium carbonate by depositing the colloidal substance, and then filtering through diatomaceous earth to remove the deposited colloidal substance; then using cation exchange resin to remove part of the calcium ion; after pretreatment, passing the xylose mother liquor into chromatographic separation system, and separating the xylose component from other heterosaccharide components to obtain an extract containing the main component of xylose and raffinate sugar component.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing xylitol and mixed syrup from a xylose mother liquor.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing xylitol and mixed syrup from a xylose mother liquor, comprises (i) taking the mother liquor of xylose, adding calcium hydroxide solution into the mother liquor of xylose, adjusting the pH to 8.5-9, passing carbon dioxide so as to saturate the pH to 6.6-7, and transforming calcium hydroxide to calcium carbonate by depositing the colloidal substance, and then filtering through diatomaceous earth to remove the deposited colloidal substance; then using cation exchange resin to remove part of the calcium ion; (ii) after pretreatment, passing the xylose mother liquor into chromatographic separation system, and separating the xylose component from other heterosaccharide components after passing through the chromatographic column to obtain an extract containing the main component of xylose and raffinate sugar component; where the concentration of the dry matter in the extract is 15-25%, the content of xylose is 80% or more; the concentration of the dry matter of the raffinate is 8-20%, mainly mannose and glucose; (iii) decolorizing the extract by powdered activated carbon, and removing the impurities by filtration; (iv) decolorizing and filtering the xylose extract, and passing yang-yin series ion exchange system for separation and purification; (v) vacuum-concentrating the extracted extract after separation and purification until dry matter mass concentration is 75-85%; where the chromatographic raffinate is vacuum concentrated to a dry matter mass percentage concentration of 65-75% to obtain a finished mixed syrup, sugar group main component composed of mannose and glucose, where the mannose accounts for 30-32%, and glucose accounts for 18-22%; (vi) carrying out cooling crystallization of extract to control the crystallization yield in the range of 50-55%; (vii) centrifuging the crystallized material, and controlling the moisture content of the xylose to be less than or equal to 0.5%; (viii) using drinking water to dissolve the xylose, carrying out hydrogenation reaction in the hydrogenation reactor, and sedimenting the catalyst to remove the catalyst; and (ix) purifying the hydrogenation solution by a positive-negative tandem ion exchange system, and concentrating the purified material by vacuum evaporation to a dry matter mass percentage of 75-85%, and making the vacuum boiled sugar crystals to make xylitol crystals, precipitating, and finally centrifuging and drying to obtain xylitol crystals.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (i), the xylose mother liquor dry matter mass percentage concentration, i.e., sugar concentration is 50-60 wt.%; where, in dry matter, glucose content is 12-18 wt.%, xylose content is 40-50 wt.%, arabinose content is 17-23 wt.%, mannose content is 10-22 wt.%, and galactose content is 0-6 wt.%. In step (i), the cation exchange resin is D001 type styrene macroporous strong acid cation exchange resin, feed temperature is 55-65 degrees C, feed pH is 5-7, advanced material, feed flow rate is 0.1-0.5 l/minute, time is 10-50 minutes, the purified water flow rate is 0.1-0.5 l/minute for 10-50 minutes, and the pH of the discharge was controlled at 1.5-6. The chromatographic separation system is SSMB sequential simulated moving bed chromatography separation system. The chromatographic material is Novartis's Applexion XA resin. The resin has different binding force to xylose and heterosaccharide, and the feed is continuously pushed. Preferred Method: The chromatographic separation unit is then separated by elution of the eluent, where the feed temperature is 50-65 degrees C, the feed pH is 1.5-6, the feed liquid feed amount is 100-150 ml/minute, and the amount of eluent purified water passed is 100-200 ml/minute after 10-20 hours, where xylose and sugar are extracted separately. In step (iii), the added mass of the powdered activated carbon to the liquid quantity is 1-3, and maintained at 50-65 degrees C for 30-60 minutes. The cation resin is a D001 type styrene macroporous strong acid cation exchange resin. The anion resin is D301 type styrene macroporous weak base anion exchange resin, the feeding temperature is 50-65 degrees C, the feeding pH is 2-7, the material is first introduced, the feed flow rate is 0.1-0.5 l/minute, and the time is 10-50 minutes; then, the purified water is flowed at a flow rate of 0.1-0.5 l/minute for 10-50 minutes, and the conductivity of the discharged aqueous solution is controlled to be less than 100 mu s/cm. in step (v), the extract liquid is vacuum concentrated at vacuum -0.075 to -0.095 MPa, discharge temperature is 60-75 degrees C; chromatographic raffinate is vacuum concentrated at -0.075 to -0.095MPa, and discharge temperature is 60-75 degrees C. The step (vi) includes cooling to 35-45 degrees C, and the crystallization time is 2-10 hours. The step (viii) includes dissolving xylose in drinking water, the refractive index is controlled at 50-60%, pH is 5-7; the nickel catalyst with a mass percentage of 0.8-2% is added, the reaction temperature is controlled at 130-140 degrees C, the steam pressure is 0.4 MPa or more, and the pressure of the reaction kettle is controlled at 7-9.5 MPa. The reaction time is 60-120 minutes, and the conversion rate is 99% or more. The positive-negative tandem ion exchange system is D001 type styrene macroporous strong acid cation exchange resin, anion resin is D301 type styrene macroporous weak base anion exchange resin, feed temperature is 45-55 degrees C, first feed material flow rate is 0.1-0.5 l/minute, time is 10-50 minutes, then passing purified water at flow rate 0.1-0.5 l/minute, time is 10-50 minutes, and controlling discharge conductivity at less than or equal to 10 mu s/cm.
DC D17 (Sugar and starch industry (C07H, C13).); E13 (Heterocyclics.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)
MC D06-G; E10-A07B; E11-A02; E11-D02; A04-C; A10-E21; A11-C07; A12-L04A; A12-M03; A12-W11K; N02-C01; N06-C01

PN CN109486448-A
TI Preparation of modified petroleum resin reinforced ethylene-vinyl acetate hot melt adhesive, involves preparing modified aluminum oxide/silicon dioxide aerogel powder, dissolving in ethyl acetate, adding to petroleum resin and grinding.
AU JIN L
AE JIN L (JINL-Individual)
GA 201927141R
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation of modified 5C petroleum resin reinforced ethylene-vinyl acetate hot melt adhesive involves dissolving aluminum chloride hexahydrate in deionized water, dissolving ethyl orthosilicate in ethanol, mixing aluminum and silicon, stirring, adding 1,2-propylene oxide, forming gel, aging to obtain alcohol gel, drying, extracting carbon dioxide stream and drying to obtain aluminum oxide/silicon dioxide aerogel powder, adding to ethanol solution, adding silane coupling agent, reacting to obtain modified aluminum oxide/silicon dioxide aerogel powder, dissolving in ethyl acetate, adding to 5C petroleum resin, grinding, steaming to remove ethyl acetate to obtain mixed substance, sequentially adding ethylene-vinyl acetate resin, microcrystalline wax, 2,6-di-terbutyl-4-methyl phenol (antioxidant 264), and antioxidant SP to reaction kettle, adding to mixed substance, heating in molten state, adding dioctyl phthalate to adjust viscosity to obtain cooled yellow solid.
   USE - Preparation of modified 5C petroleum resin reinforced ethylene-vinyl acetate hot melt adhesive.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of modified 5C petroleum resin reinforced ethylene-vinyl acetate hot melt adhesive with high mechanical property and excellent heat-resistant aging effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The preparation of modified 5C petroleum resin reinforced ethylene-vinyl acetate hot melt adhesive, involves dissolving aluminum chloride hexahydrate in deionized water, dissolving ethyl orthosilicate in ethanol, mixing aluminum and silicon in molar ratio of (2-5):1, stirring for 30 minutes, placing in ice water bath, adding 1,2-propylene oxide, stirring for 30 minutes, forming a gel, aging at 50 degrees C for 12-24 hours, water washing and alcohol washing to obtain alcohol gel, placing alcohol gel in a supercritical aerogel drying device for low temperature drying, heating at supercritical temperature of 40-50 degrees C, increasing pressure to a supercritical pressure of 15-25 MPa, extracting carbon dioxide stream and drying by alcohol gel to obtain aluminum oxide/silicon dioxide aerogel powder, adding aluminum oxide/silicon dioxide aerogel powder to concentration of 80-95% ethanol solution, adjusting the pH of solution to 6.5, adding 20 wt.% silane coupling agent, heating the reaction in water bath to obtain modified aluminum oxide/silicon dioxide aerogel powder, dissolving modified aluminum oxide/silicon dioxide aerogel powder in ethyl acetate, adding to 5C petroleum resin, ultrasonically treating, grinding by colloid mill for 2 hours, steaming to remove ethyl acetate to obtain mixed substance, sequentially adding ethylene-vinyl acetate resin, microcrystalline wax, 2,6-di-terbutyl-4-methyl phenol (antioxidant 264), and antioxidant SP to reaction kettle, heating to melt, stirring vigorously, adding to mixed substance, heating and stirring in a molten state for period of time, adding dioctyl phthalate to adjust the viscosity to obtain cooled yellow solid. The raw material comprises 80-120 pts. wt. ethylene-vinyl acetate resin, 15-25 pts. wt. microcrystalline wax, 0.1-0.5 pts. wt. 2,6-di-terbutyl-4-methyl phenol (antioxidant 264), 0.2-0.4 pt. wt. antioxidant SP, 3-8 pts. wt. dioctyl o-dibenzoate, 20-35 pts. wt. 5C petroleum resin, 15-25 pts. wt. modified aluminum oxide/silicon dioxide aerogel powder, and 20-30 pts. wt. ethyl acetate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of aluminum and silicon is (2-3):1. The mass fraction of aluminum oxide/silicon dioxide aerogel powder is 15-25% of ethanol solution.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of silane coupling agent and aluminum oxide/silicon dioxide aerogel powder is 1:(5-10). The silane coupling agent is KH570 (RTM: ( gamma )-methacryloyloxypropyltrimethoxysilane) or A171 (RTM: Vinyltrimethoxysilane). Preferred Conditions: The supercritical temperature is 45 degrees C and the supercritical pressure is 20-25 MPa. The carbon dioxide stream extraction and drying time is 30-90 minutes.

PN CN109486890-A
TI Preparing fish protein mixed peptide from crude fish protein e.g. providing crude fish protein powder, stirring, mixing uniformly, inactivating enzyme, enzymatic hydrolysis by microwave and controlling pH during enzymatic hydrolysis.
AU ZHOU C
   HONG P
   LIU H
   DENG C
   YANG P
AE UNIV GUANGDONG OCEAN (UYOG-C)
GA 201927131S
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing fish protein mixed peptide from crude fish protein comprises e.g. taking crude fish protein powder, adding deionized water, stirring and mixing uniformly to obtain a slurry, adding phospholipase to the slurry, inactivating enzyme after the end of enzymatic hydrolysis, and removing the fat by supercritical carbon dioxide extraction to obtain a degreasing slurry, adding degreasing liquid to the degreasing slurry, stirring for 10-20 minutes, allowing to stand for 2-3 hours, and dialyzing to obtain retained liquid to obtain a mash slurry, adding a complex protease to the mash slurry, and assisting enzymatic hydrolysis by microwave to obtain a first enzymatic hydrolysate, placing first enzymatic hydrolyzate in a constant temperature oscillator at 35-45 degrees C to continue enzymatic hydrolysis for 3-4 hours, controlling pH to 6.5-7.5 during enzymatic hydrolysis, inactivating enzyme after the end of enzymatic hydrolysis, and cooling to room temperature.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing fish protein mixed peptide from crude fish protein.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves enzymolysis efficiency, enhances anti-oxidation effect of protein peptide, removes fishy smell, has good decoloring and degreasing effect, is simple, economical and fully utilizes resources and is suitable for large-scale production. The mixed peptide has low fat content, no odor and high sensory acceptance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing fish protein mixed peptide from crude fish protein comprises (i) taking an amount of crude fish protein powder, adding deionized water, stirring and mixing uniformly to obtain a slurry, (ii) adding phospholipase to the slurry, inactivating enzyme after the end of enzymatic hydrolysis, and removing the fat by supercritical carbon dioxide extraction to obtain a degreasing slurry, (iii) adding degreasing liquid to the degreasing slurry, stirring for 10-20 minutes, allowing to stand for 2-3 hours, and dialyzing to obtain retained liquid to obtain a mash slurry, (iv) adding a complex protease to the mash slurry, and assisting enzymatic hydrolysis by microwave to obtain a first enzymatic hydrolysate, (v) placing first enzymatic hydrolyzate in a constant temperature oscillator at 35-45 degrees C to continue enzymatic hydrolysis for 3-4 hours, controlling pH to 6.5-7.5 during enzymatic hydrolysis, inactivating enzyme after the end of enzymatic hydrolysis, and cooling to room temperature to obtain a second enzymatic solution, (vi) centrifuging second enzymatic solution in a centrifuge and removing precipitate to obtain a supernatant, where the centrifuge speed is 2000-3000 revolutions/minute, (vii) decolorizing supernatant to obtain a decolorizing solution, (viii) subjecting filtered filtrate to ultrafiltration separation, collecting components having a molecular weight in the range of 1000-3000 Da, where the ultrafiltration pressure is 0.5-1.0 MPa and performing at room temperature and (ix) collecting component by the ultrafiltration by vacuum freeze-drying to obtain a powder product i.e. obtaining fish protein mixed peptide powder, where the vacuum freeze drying time is 20-30 hours, temperature is 30-35 degrees C and the degree of vacuum is 100-300 Pa
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The preparation process of crude fish protein powder comprises: slaughtering live fish, removing internal organs, peeling, deboning, remove fat particles visible to the naked eye, grinding with a blender to obtain a fish slurry, degreasing fish slurry with an ethanol solution having a volume concentration of greater than or equal to 95%, and then air-drying, freeze-drying and pulverizing to obtain crude fish protein powder. In the step (i), the adding amount of deionized water is 2-3 times the weight of the crude fish protein powder. In the step (ii), the extraction pressure is 15-30 MPa. The carbon dioxide flow rate is 30-100 l/hour. The extraction temperature is 45-60 degrees C and the extraction time is 1-2 hours. In the step (iii), performing dialysis by semi-permeable membrane dialysis, and the molecular weight cut off of the semi-permeable membrane is 300-700 Da. In the step (iv), the complex protease comprises trypsin, chymotrypsin, neutral protease, alkaline protease, papain, flavor protease in the mass ratio of 2-3:1-2.5:1.1-2.1:0.8-1.5:0.9-1.4:1.1-1.5. During the enzymatic hydrolysis, controlling pH to 6-8, the temperature is 40-60 degrees C, and the enzymatic hydrolysis time is 6-10 minutes. In the step (v), inactivating enzyme in a 90-95 degrees C water bath for 10-15 minutes. In the step (vii), carrying out decoloring treatment by adding activated carbon to the supernatant, stirring at 70-90 degrees C for 0.5-2 hours, filtering, removing activated carbon to obtain a filtrate, where the adding amount of activated carbon is 1.0-2.5 wt.% of the supernatant. Preferred Components: In the step (ii), the phospholipase is a mixture of phospholipase A1, phospholipase A2, phospholipase B and phospholipase C in the weight ratio 1-1.6: 1.5-2: 0.5-0.9: 0.7-1.1. The adding amount of phospholipase is 0.5-1.2 wt.% of the slurry. The enzymatic conditions are: pH is 7-8, temperature is 45-55 degrees C, and enzymatic hydrolysis time 1.5-2 hours. In the step (iii), the components of mashing solution comprises: 60-90 pts. wt. ethanol, 20-30 pts. wt. acetic acid, 20-30 pts. wt. sodium chloride, 8-15 pts. wt. baking soda, 6-8 pts. wt. citric acid, 5-11 pts. wt. ginger juice, and 1-2 pts. wt. sodium phytate.

PN CN108889907-A; CN108889907-B
TI Casting of large-scale precision casting involves making polystyrene foam white mold consistent with casting entity, shell-type coating mold surface, drying, molding, forming cavity shell, shell-type packaging, and vacuum casting.
AU DAI W
   JIA J
   WANG K
AE HENAN SHENGDEWEI MACHINERY TECHNOLOGY CO (HENA-Non-standard)
GA 201897236P
AB    NOVELTY - Casting of large-scale precision casting involves making polystyrene foam white mold consistent with casting entity, coating surface of white mold with top layer coating and back layer coating, drying, cutting coating of shell coating at riser on white mold, inverting the coated white mold onto a heating furnace trolley, heating the furnace, liquefying polystyrene foam white mold, allowing to flow out from pouring riser to wet pan, taking out and recovering to form cavity shell, heating the furnace, vaporizing polystyrene liquid and other volatiles infiltrated into the coating, filtering gas overflow, decomposing into water and carbon dioxide, heat preserving, opening furnace door, pulling out the trolley, cooling, sealing riser with a plastic bag, continuing cooling to obtain cavity shell, placing the prepared shell in a sand box, filling dry sand, vibrating, sealing plastic film, connecting vacuum tube, maintaining negative pressure, pouring molten metal into shell, and casting.
   USE - Casting of large-scale precision casting.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is environmentally-friendly, and pollution-free, and provides high-grade, complex structure, precision casting without defects such as carbonization, pores, slag inclusions, and wrinkles.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Casting of large-scale precision casting involves making polystyrene foam white mold consistent with casting entity, coating surface of white mold with top layer coating and back layer coating, which involves applying top layer coating to a thickness of 0.2-0.8 mm, drying at 30-65 degrees C, humidity of 40-75%, for 2-3 hours, applying back layer coating on outside top layer coating when 70-80% top layer coating is dried, applying back layer coating 2-7 times to a thickness of 0.8-2 mm per coating, so that total thickness of the coating is 3-8 mm, drying every time after applying coat, at 60-75 degrees C, humidity of 40-60%, for 1-2 hours each time, drying shell type coating integrally, placing white mold in a drying chamber at 65-75 degrees C, 3-5 m/second wind speed, and 30-50% humidity, continuing drying for 4-8 hours, cutting coating of shell coating at riser on white mold, inverting the coated white mold onto a heating furnace trolley, heating the furnace to 200-360 degrees C at 200 degrees C/hour or less, heat preserving for 10-50 minutes, liquefying polystyrene foam white mold, allowing to flow out from pouring riser to wet pan, taking out and recovering to form cavity shell, heating the furnace to 450-850 degrees C, heat preserving for 5-40 minutes, decomposing and vaporizing polystyrene liquid and other volatiles infiltrated into the coating, filtering gas overflow using a de-dusting machine, decomposing into water and carbon dioxide, heat preserving, opening furnace door for 10 minutes, pulling out the trolley until shell temperature drops to below 500 degrees C, cooling naturally cooling to below 50 degrees C in air, sealing riser with a plastic bag, continuing cooling to room temperature to obtain cavity shell to be fabricated, placing the prepared shell in a sand box, filling dry sand, vibrating compactly, sealing plastic film, connecting vacuum tube, maintaining negative pressure in flask at 0.025-0.045 MPa, pouring molten metal into shell, casting, separating and removing riser, and obtaining casting with polystyrene foam white mold.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The top layer coating comprises 5-10 pts. wt. bauxite, 6-8 pts. wt. zircon powder, 60-75 pts. wt. corundum, 2-5 pts. wt. carboxymethyl cellulose, 1-2 pts. wt. phenolic resin, 3-6 pts. wt. water glass, 1-2 pts. wt. silica sol, 3-6 pts. wt. phosphate, 0.1-0.2 pt. wt. surfactant comprising fatty alcohol polyoxyethylene ether, alkylphenol, and/or ethylene oxide condensate, and 0.01-0.02 pt. wt. n-octanol. The back layer coating comprises 40-45 pts. wt. alumina bauxite, 6-8 pts. wt. quartz powder, 15-25 pts. wt. corundum, 6-8 pts. wt. diatomaceous earth, 0.5-2 pts. wt. cryolite, 2-4 pts. wt. carboxymethyl cellulose , 1-3 pts. wt. phenolic resin, 4-8 pts. wt. water glass, 3-6 pts. wt. phosphate, 0.1-0.2 pt. wt. surfactant, 0.5-3 pts. wt. lignin, and 0.01-0.02 pt. wt. defoamer comprising n-butanol, n-pentanol, and/or n-octanol.

GA 2018919065
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nitrogen heterocyclic carbene gold porous organic polymer i.e. (1,3-bis((4-methylphenyl)methyl)-2,3-dihydro-1H-imidazol-2-yl)(chloro)gold compound (I) comprises taking alkenyl functionalized 1,3-bis(p-vinylbenzyl)imidazolecarbene chloride gold i.e. (1,3-bis((4-ethenylphenyl)methyl)-2,3-dihydro-1H-imidazol-2-yl)(chloro)gold (II), divinylbenzene (III), azobisisobutyronitrile and N,N-dimethylformamide in a mass to volume ratio of 0.27:0.06-2.60:0.10-0.94:2-g one half 0 ml, and carrying out prepolymerization reaction at room temperature for 0.25-1 hour, then carrying out polymerization reaction at 70-100 degrees C, after the reaction is completed, filtering, then washing the filtrate three times with dimethylformamide and acetone, and drying under vacuum to obtain (I), where the polymerization time is 12-30 hours.
   USE - (I) is useful as catalyst for preparing 2-oxazolidinone compound.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has simple process, short synthesis cycle, low toxicity and excellent repeatability, and (I) has adjustable specific surface area and pore volume, high catalytic activity and easy separation and recyclability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing nitrogen heterocyclic carbene gold porous organic polymer i.e. (1,3-bis((4-methylphenyl)methyl)-2,3-dihydro-1H-imidazol-2-yl)(chloro)gold compound of formula (I) comprises taking alkenyl functionalized 1,3-bis(p-vinylbenzyl)imidazolecarbene chloride gold i.e. (1,3-bis((4-ethenylphenyl)methyl)-2,3-dihydro-1H-imidazol-2-yl)(chloro)gold of formula (II), divinylbenzene of formula (III), azobisisobutyronitrile and N,N-dimethylformamide in a mass to volume ratio of 0.27:0.06-2.60:0.10-0.94:2-g one half 0 ml, and carrying out prepolymerization reaction at room temperature for 0.25-1 hour, then carrying out polymerization reaction at 70-100 degrees C, after the reaction is completed, filtering, then washing the filtrate three times with dimethylformamide and acetone, and drying under vacuum to obtain (I), where the polymerization time is 12-30 hours.
   R1 and R2 = alkyl or aryl.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for (I) as a catalyst for preparing 2-oxazolidinone compound of formula (IV) by cyclization reaction of carbon dioxide with propargylamine compound of formula (V), comprising carrying out cyclization reaction by dissolving (I) and propargylamine compound of formula (V) in a methanol solvent, then carrying out polar synthesis reaction of 2-oxazolidinone compound of formula (IV) by introducing 0.1-1 MPa carbon dioxide at a reaction temperature of 25-80 degrees C and a reaction time of 12-48 hours, filtering off catalyst and washing with anhydrous diethyl ether for many times, combining organic phases and drying over anhydrous sodium sulfate, filtering, concentrating and separating by silica gel column chromatography to obtain final product, where the filtered catalyst can be repeatedly used after washing and drying, and the mass ratio between (I), (V) and methanol is 0.01-1:0.1-0.5:1-10 g/ml, and the propargylamine compound is N-benzyl-2-butynylamine, N-methyl-2-butynylamine, N-methyl propargylamine, N-isopropyl propargylamine, N-cyclohexyl propargylamine, N-tert-butyl propargylamine, N-isopropyl-2-butynylamine, N-cyclohexyl-2-butynylamine or N-tert-butyl-2-butynylamine.

PN CN108794803-A
TI Preparing supramolecular intercalation structure for photoaging resistant materials, involves weighing nitrate or chloride salt of M2 and M3 in deionized water with carbon dioxide to prepare mixed salt solution.
AU LI D
   ZHAO M
   TANG P
   FENG Y
   MA R
AE UNIV BEIJING CHEM TECHNOLOGY (UYBC-C)
GA 2018927186
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a supramolecular intercalation structure involves weighing the nitrate or chloride salt of M2+ and M3+ in deionized water with carbon dioxide to prepare a mixed salt solution, where the molar ratio of M2+ to M3+ is 2-4, and the molar concentration of M3+ is 0.05-0.8 mol/L. The mixed salt solution M2+, M3+, an alkaline solution having a molar concentration of 0.05-1 mol/L is prepared, where the molar amount of the base is 2-2.4 times the sum of the molar amounts of M2+ and M3+, and then the ultraviolet absorbent is added to the alkali solution to form a mixed solution. The ratio of the ultraviolet absorber to the M3+ molar amount is 1-3. The mixed solution and the above mixed salt solution are all mixed under nitrogen protection and stirring to form a precipitate, and crystallized at 60-110 degrees C for 1-8 hours, filtered, and washed the precipitate to get filtrate.
   USE - Method for preparing a supramolecular intercalation structure for photoaging resistant materials.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare a supramolecular intercalation structure, which has reduced amount of the expensive hindered amine light stabilizer, greatly improves utility rate of the hindered amine light stabilizer, reduces preparation cost of the hindered amine light stabilizer and simple operation, mild reaction conditions, easy to control the molar ratio of the interlayer hindered amine light stabilizer and the ultraviolet absorber, and is convenient for industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The filtrate is centrifuged for 3-6 times to obtain an ultraviolet absorber intercalated layered double hydroxides (LDH) wet cake. The M2+ is magnesium ions, zinc ions, nickel ions, calcium ions, ferrous ion, copper ion, and M3+ is aluminum ion, cobalt ions, titanium(III) ion, ferrous ion, chromic ions, the base is sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide or ammonia. The ultraviolet absorber is sodium p-aminobenzoate, sodium p-methoxycinnamate, sodium salicylate, sodium 2-hydroxy-4-methoxybenzophenone-5-sulfonate, sodium 3-carboxylate, sodium cinnamate, sodium benzotriazole-4-hydroxy-benzenesulfonate, and sodium 2-phenylbenzimidazole-5-sulfonate. The obtained ultraviolet absorbent is intercalated with LDH wet cake by using water to prepare a slurry having a solid content of 10-100 g/L, and then a hindered amine light stabilizer is added, where the hindered amine light stabilizer is added in the slurry. The M3 + molar amount of 0.3-1 times, then stirred at 20-100 degrees C for 3-12 hours, cooling, filtration, washing the filter cake 4-9 times, drying at 60-100 degrees C for 12-24 hours to get super molecular intercalation structure anti-photoaging material. The super molecular intercalation structure anti-photoaging material is expressed as hindered amine light stabilizer (HALS)/ultraviolet absorber (UAB)/LDH, and the utilization rate of hindered amine light stabilizer is greater than 90%. The hindered amine light stabilizer is a tetramethylpiperidine compound, which is represented by (I), where R1 is H or CH3, R2 is O-R3-COONa, N-R3-COONa, O-R3-SO3Na or N-R3-SO3Na, where R3 is (CH2)k(C6H4 )m(C3N3H4)n, k is an integer between 0-8, m, n is 0 or 1..
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Component: The M2+ is magnesium ions and zinc ions, and M3+ is aluminum ions.
DC A94 (Semi-finished materials - fibres, films, foams.)
MC A08-A03

PT P
PN WO2018204061-A1
TI Treating and stabilizing brown stock from pulping stage in pulp mill prior to transfer to delignification or bleaching stage, comprises e.g. reacting/treating brown stock pulp with treatment solution of alkali in alcohol/water co-solvent.
AU MARCOCCIA B
   ATALLA R S
   PANDE H
   ATALLA R H
AE CELLULOSE SCI INT INC (CELL-Non-standard)
   DOMTAR PAPER CO LLC (DOMT-Non-standard)
GA 201887818G
AB    NOVELTY - Method (m1) of treating and stabilizing a brown stock from a pulping stage in a pulp mill prior to transfer to a delignification or bleaching stage, comprises reacting/treating a brown stock pulp with a treatment solution of an alkali in an alcohol/water co-solvent to yield a treated brown stock pulp slurry in a post-treatment solution, reducing the alkalinity of the post-treatment solution through dilution with displacement or with an acidifying agent or both to yield a neutralized post-treatment solution having the treated brown stock pulp, separating the treated brown stock and any precipitated alkali salt, resulting from the use of acidifying agents, from the neutralized post-treatment solution, and subsequently separating any precipitated salt from the brown stock pulp by pulp washing.
   USE - The method is useful for treating and stabilizing the brown stock from the pulping stage in the pulp mill prior to transfer to the delignification or bleaching stage.
   ADVANTAGE - The method treats brown stock from the pulp mill, thus improving its delignification and/or bleaching response, and is economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) a method (m2) of processing a brown stock comprising (i) treating a brown stock with a treatment solution of sodium hydroxide in an ethanol/water co-solvent to produce a composition having a treated brown stock in a post-treatment solution, (ii) adjusting the pH of the post-treatment solution with carbon dioxide to produce a neutralized post-treatment solution including the treated brown stock, where the neutralized post-treatment solution has a pH of 8-10, and the carbon dioxide reacts with the sodium hydroxide dissolved in the post-treatment solution to precipitate sodium carbonate/bicarbonate, (iii) separating the treated brown stock and the precipitated sodium carbonate/bicarbonate from the neutralized post-treatment solution, (iv) separating the treated brown stock from the precipitated sodium carbonate/bicarbonate salt, (v) recaustization the sodium carbonate/bicarbonate to yield recovered sodium hydroxide, and (vii) recovering the ethanol from the neutralized post-treatment solution;
   (2) a system for treating brown stock to improve its delignification and/or bleaching response, comprising a treatment stage, a post-treatment stage and a recycling/recovery stage, where the treatment stage comprises reacting a brown stock with a treatment solution of sodium hydroxide in an ethanol/water co-solvent to produce a composition including a treated brown stock in a post-treatment solution, the post-treatment stage comprises treating the post-treatment solution with an acidifying agent to produce a neutralized post-treatment solution including the treated brown stock, and the acidifying agent neutralizes the alkali and precipitating any alkali salt, the recycling/recovery stage comprises separating the treated brown stock and any precipitated salt from the neutralized post-treatment solution, separating the alkali salt from the treated brown stock, recovering the alkali by recaustizing the alkali salt to yield recovered alkali, and recovering the ethanol from the neutralized post-treatment solution; and
   (3) a brown stock produced by the system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The system further comprises a delignification and/or bleaching stage including delignifying and/or bleaching the treated brown stock.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The method (m1) further comprises: recovering the alkali by recaustizing the alkali salt to yield recovered alkali; recycling the recovered alkali to form treatment solution; distilling the neutralized post-treatment solution to recover the alcohol, and recycling the recovered alcohol to form treatment solution; and delignifying and/or bleaching the treated brown stock pulp. The method (m2) further comprises recycling the recovered sodium hydroxide to form treatment solution, and recycling the recovered ethanol to form treatment solution. Preferred Components: The alkali is an alkali metal hydroxide and the alkali salt is an alkali metal salt. The alkali metal hydroxide is sodium hydroxide and the alkali metal salt is sodium carbonate/sodium bicarbonate. The sodium carbonate/sodium bicarbonate is recaustized by treatment with quicklime. The acidifying agent is carbon dioxide. The post-treatment solution has a pH of 8-10 at the end of neutralization.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. PT P
PN CN108728404-A; CN108728404-B
TI Culturing in vitro fertilized bovine embryo, by culturing cumulus-oocyte complexes in mature medium, transferring to fertilization medium, adding semen preparation culture solution and culturing in embryo culture medium.
AU WANG X
   GUO J
   WANG N
   HAO S
   ZHAO M
   MA Y
   GUO C
AE TIANJIN BOYU LIMU TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (TIAN-Non-standard)
   TIANJIN ZHONGKE BOYA BIOLOGY BREEDING (TIAN-Non-standard)
   TIANJIN LIMU DINGFENG BIOTECHNOLOGY CO (TIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201890117N
AB    NOVELTY - Culturing in vitro fertilized bovine embryo, by (a) taking ovary from slaughterhouse, extracting follicles, obtaining cumulus-oocyte complexes (COCs) containing cumulus cells, collecting live cattle, extracting COCs containing at least three layers of cumulus cells, inoculating in a mature medium containing (4-(2-hydroxyethyl)-1-piperazineethanesulfonic acid), collecting ex vivo or living body COCs in oocyte mature culture medium, transferring to new mature medium and culturing, (b) washing mature COCs with fertilization culture solution, transferring to fertilization medium, freezing semen with liquid nitrogen, thawing, aseptically cutting, injecting into centrifuge tube containing semen preparation medium, centrifuging, adding semen preparation culture solution to above centrifuge tube, resuspending, adding sperm suspension and culturing, and (c) inoculating in embryo culture medium, culturing, transferring to frozen liquid and loading embryo.
   USE - The method is useful for culturing in vitro fertilized bovine embryo.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for culturing in vitro fertilized bovine embryo, involves (a) taking ovary from slaughterhouse in thermos bucket with physiological saline, at 31-33 degrees C, transferring to laboratory within 3 hours, extracting 2-8 mm follicles on surface, collecting precipitate, obtaining cumulus-oocyte complexes (COCs) containing at least 3 layers of cumulus cells under a stereomicroscope, washing for 2 times with egg washing solution, removing excess impurities, collecting live cattle, extracting COCs containing at least three layers of cumulus cells using stereomicroscope, inoculating in a mature medium containing (4-(2-hydroxyethyl)-1-piperazineethanesulfonic acid) (HEPES), at 38.8 degrees C, transferring to laboratory within 3 hours, collecting ex vivo or living body COCs in oocyte mature culture medium, washing once with nutrient solution, transferring to new mature medium, and culturing at 38.8 degrees C and 5.5-6.5% carbon dioxide for 22-24 hours, (b) washing mature COCs with fertilization culture solution once, transferring to fertilization medium, placing in incubator, freezing with liquid nitrogen, thawing in water bath at 37 degrees C, aseptically cutting, injecting semen into 15 ml centrifuge tube containing semen preparation medium, centrifuging at 328 Xg for 2 times for 5 minutes for each time, centrifuging the mixture, removing supernatant, adding 300 mu l semen preparation culture solution to above centrifuge tube, resuspending, taking appropriate sperm suspension for sperm count, adding sperm suspension to fertilized culture droplet containing oocytes, placing in incubator, incubating for 16-20 hours, and culturing at 38.8 degrees C, saturated humidity and 5.5-6.5% carbon dioxide, and (c) removing granulosa cells around embryo with detached needle, inoculating in embryo culture medium, recording as first day of embryo culture, culturing at 38.8 degrees C, 6% O2, 88% N2 and saturated humidity, recording the cleavage rate on third day, and blastocyst rate on seventh day, calculating blastocyst hatching rate on ninth day (percentage of hatching blastocysts divided by the number of blastocysts), assessing quality, washing for 3 times with preservation solution, transferring to frozen liquid after equilibration for 10 minutes in equilibration solution, loading embryo according to 5-stage liquid filling method, marking, placing in desuperheater, dropping to -35 degrees C at a rate of 0.5 degrees C/minute, removing embryonic tubules and storing in liquid nitrogen for cryopreservation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The physiological saline is physiological saline comprising 400 IU/ml penicillin and 400 mu g/ml streptomycin. The egg washing solution is BY base culture solution comprising 3 mg/ml bovine serum albumin. The mature culture solution is BY basal medium added with 100 ml/l fetal bovine serum (FBS), 10 mu g/ml follicle-stimulating hormone (FSH), 10 mu g/ml luteinizing hormone (LH), 1 mu g/ml E2 and 20 ng/ml epidermal growth factor (EGF). The HEPES has concentration of 10-20 mmol/l, preferably 15 mmol/l. The fertilization culture solution comprises 112 mM sodium chloride, 4.02 mM potassium chloride, 2.25 mM calcium chloride dihydrate, 0.52 mM magnesium chloride hexahydrate, 0.83 mM potassium dihydrogen phosphate, 37.0 mM sodium bicarbonate, 1.25 mM sodium pyruvate, 10 mu g/ml heparin, 4 mg/ml bovine serum albumin (BSA), 100 U/ml penicillin and 100 mu g/ml an aqueous solution of streptomycin. The semen preparation culture solution comprises 112 mM sodium chloride, 4.02 mM potassium chloride, 2.25 mM calcium chloride dihydrate, 0.52 mM magnesium chloride hexahydrate, 0.83 mM potassium dihydrogen phosphate, 37 mM sodium bicarbonate, 1.25 mM sodium pyruvate, 10 mu g/ml heparin, 4 mg/ml BSA, 10 mM caffeine, 100 U/ml penicillin, and 100 mu g/ml an aqueous solution of streptomycin. The embryo culture solution comprises 109.5 mM sodium chloride, 3.1 mM potassium chloride, 26.2 mM sodium bicarbonate, 0.8 mM magnesium chloride hexahydrate, 1.19 mM potassium dihydrogen phosphate, 0.4 mM sodium pyruvate, 1.5 mM glucose, 5 mM calcium galactate, 2.5 volume/volume% FBS, 1 mM L-glutamine, 2 volume/volume% essential amino acids, 1 volume/volume% non-essential amino acids, 3 mM glutathione, 0.04 sodium citrate and 0.02 weight/volume% an aqueous solution of maltose. The essential amino acid comprises 6.32 g L-arginine hydrochloride, 1.564 g L-cystine dihydrochloride, 2.1 g L-histidine hydrochloride monohydrate, 2.625 g L-isoleucine, 2.62 g L-leucine, 3.625 g L-lysine hydrochloride, 0.755 g L-methionine, 1.65 g L-phenylalanine, 2.38 g L-threonine, 0.51 g L-tryptophan, 1.8 g L-tyrosine and 2.34 g L-valine. The non-essential amino acid comprises 0.89 g L-alanine, 1.5 g L-asparagine monohydrate, 1.33 g L-aspartic acid, 1.47 g L-glutamic acid, 0.75 g glycine, 1.15 g L-valine and 1.05 g L-serine. The BY base culture solution is an aqueous solution comprising 200 mg/l calcium chloride, 0.72 mg/l ferric nitrate nonahydrate, 400 mg/l potassium chloride, 97.7 mg/l magnesium sulfate, 6800 mg/l sodium chloride, 140 mg/l sodium dihydrogen phosphate monohydrate, 2200 mg/l sodium bicarbonate, 50 mg/l sodium acetate, 25 mg/l L-alanine, 70 mg/l L-arginine hydrochloride, 30 mg/l L-aspartic acid, 0.11 mg/l L-cysteine hydrochloride monohydrate, 26 mg/l L-cystine dihydrochloride, 75 mg/l L-glutamic acid, 50 mg/l glycine, 21.88 mg/l L-histidine hydrochloride monohydrate, 10 mg/l L-hydroxyproline, 20 mg/l L-isoleucine, 60 mg/l L-leucine, 70 mg/l L-lysine hydrochloride, 15 mg/l L-methionine, 25 mg/l L-phenylalanine, 40 mg/l L-valine, 25 mg/l L-serine, 30 mg/l L-threonine, 10 mg/l L-tryptophan, 57.66 mg/l L-tyrosine disodium dihydrate, 25 mg/l L-valine, 0.05 mg/l ascorbic acid, 0.01 mg/l alpha -D-tocopheryl phosphate, 0.01 mg/l biotin, 0.1 mg/l calcitonin, 0.01 mg/l D-calcium pantothenate, 0.5 mg/l choline chloride, 0.01 mg/l folic acid, 0.05 mg/l inositol, 0.019 mg/l menadione sodium bisulfite trihydrate, 0.025 mg/l niacin, 0.025 mg/l nicotinamide, 0.05 mg/l P-aminobenzoic acid, 0.05 mg/l pyridoxine hydrochloride, 0.01 mg/l riboflavin, 0.01 mg/l thiamine hydrochloride, 0.14 mg/l vitamin A acetate, 10 mg/l adenine sulfate, 0.2 mg/l adenine, 1 mg/l disodium adenosine triphosphate, 0.2 mg/l cholesterol, 0.5 mg/l 2-deoxy-D-ribose, 1000 mg/l D-glucose, 0.05 mg/l glutathione, 0.3 mg/l guanine hydrochloride, 0.354 mg/l or 0.34 mg/l sodium hypoxanthine, 0.5 mg/l ribose, 0.3 mg/l thymosin, 805 mg/l Tween (RTM: Polysorbate), 0.3 mg/l uracil, and 10 mg/l phenol red. The BY base culture solution further comprises 0.2-0.3 mg/l, preferably 0.25 mg/l sodium selenite and/or 0.05-0.1 mg/l, preferably 0.075 mg/l copper sulfate.

PN CN108192859-A
TI Cryopreserving, reviving and preparing mesenchymal stem cells of placental tissue by washing placental tissue, cutting placental lobule, refrigerating, freezing in liquid nitrogen, thawing, adding buffer and trypsin and subculturing cells.
AU WANG J
   XIONG H
   SONG C
   HU X
AE CHONGQING SIDEMU BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (CHON-Non-standard)
GA 201851416J
AB    NOVELTY - Method for cryopreserving, reviving and preparing mesenchymal stem cells of placental tissue involves washing the placental tissue with phosphate-buffered saline (PBS) buffer, cutting the placental lobule, adding the small piece of tissue to the pre-cooling protective agent, refrigerating, freezing in liquid nitrogen, removing the frozen tissue block from liquid nitrogen, thawing in a constant temperature water bath, placing the thawed tissue block in a clean centrifuge tube, adding PBS buffer, uniformly mixing, then centrifuging, removing the supernatant, washing the tissue block, adding type II collagenase and trypsin for digestion, collecting the cell solution by sieve filtration, centrifuging, resuspending the cell pellet in the mesenchymal stem cell culture medium, culturing in a carbon dioxide incubator, changing the medium, digesting the cells using trypsin and then subculturing the cells.
   USE - The method is useful for cryopreserving, reviving and preparing mesenchymal stem cells of placental tissue.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for cryopreserving, reviving and preparing mesenchymal stem cells of placental tissue involves (a) washing the placental tissue with phosphate-buffered saline (PBS) buffer, so that the residual blood on the surface of the placenta tissue is rinsed clean and no blood clot are present on the surface of the placenta, (b) cutting the placental lobule of the placental tissue obtained in the step (a) using tissue scissors and further crushing the tissue in a crusher into a small piece, (c) adding the small piece of tissue obtained in the step (b) to the pre-cooling protective agent in a volume ratio of 1:1, uniformly mixing and dispensing into several cryotubes, where the protective agent comprises vitrification solution and mesenchymal stem cell culture medium, (d) refrigerating the cryotube obtained in the step (c) at 4 degrees C for 0.5 hour, then refrigerating at -20 degrees C for 2 hours, continuing refrigerating at -80 degrees C for 1 day and finally freezing in -196 degrees C liquid nitrogen, (e) removing the frozen tissue block from liquid nitrogen when necessary, thawing in a constant temperature water bath, placing the thawed tissue block in a clean centrifuge tube, adding PBS buffer, uniformly mixing, then centrifuging and removing the supernatant, (f) washing the tissue block, adding 0.1% type II collagenase and 0.25% trypsin for digestion, collecting the cell solution by sieve filtration, then centrifuging and removing the supernatant, (g) resuspending the cell pellet obtained in the step (f) in the mesenchymal stem cell culture medium and culturing in a carbon dioxide incubator and (h) changing the medium once in every 3-4 days, continuing culturing until the cell fusion degree reaches greater than or equal to 70%, digesting the cells using trypsin and then subculturing the cells. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for establishing a database of placental stem cells, which involves cryopreserving the placental mesenchymal stem cells in liquid nitrogen, recording the relevant fetal information, performing cell biological characteristics and multi-directional differentiation potential identification, carrying out molecular genetic diagnosis of cells, saving all relevant data for the cell, establishing a database of placental stem cells and correlating with cryopreserved cells.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: In step (b), the size of the placental lobule is 5 cmx 5 cm and the size of the small piece is 3 mmx 3 mm. In step (c), the protective agent comprises vitrification solution and a mesenchymal stem cell culture medium in a volume ratio of 1:9, where the vitrification solution comprises 27-32 vol.%, preferably 30 vol.% glycerol, 12-16 vol.%, preferably 15 vol.% ethylene glycol, 12-16 vol.%, preferably 15 vol.% dimethyl sulfoxide and 0.3-0.5 M, preferably 0.4 M sucrose and the mesenchymal stem cell culture medium comprises 12-18 pts. wt., preferably 15 pts. wt. fetal bovine serum, 0.8-1 pt. wt., preferably 1 pt. wt. L-glutamine, 0.02-0.05 pt. wt., preferably 0.04 pt. wt. gentamicin and 80-90 pts. wt., preferably 85 pts. wt. Dulbecco's Modified Eagle Medium/Nutrient Mixture F-12 (DMEM-F12). In step (f), digesting for 20-30 minutes. The method further involves (i) detecting at least one of the following items for the placental mesenchymal stem cells: cell activity, cell contamination, genetic disease and human leukocyte antigen (HLA)-ABC/DR matching or (j) crypreserving the placental mesenchymal stem cells in liquid nitrogen.

PT P
PN CN108199107-A; CN108199107-B
TI Recovering ternary battery cathode material by plasma technology comprises connecting the two poles of the battery to the resistive load, generating plasma, oxidizing, purifying anode active material, and absorbing energy.
AU YIN H
AE YIN H (YINH-Individual)
GA 201851268D
AB    NOVELTY - Recovering ternary battery cathode material by plasma technology comprises (i) connecting the two poles of the battery to the resistive load through the wire, and measuring the open circuit voltage to control the discharge result, (ii) discharging the battery, stripping the battery metal casing, and taking out the anode electrode of the ternary battery, (iii) generating plasma, sweeping across the surface of the anode electrode sheet of the ternary battery, oxidizing the acetylene black conductive agent in the anode electrode material into carbon dioxide gas, and purifying anode active material, (iv) absorbing energy carried by plasma excited by the air, removing the surface of the anode electrode sheet, and recovering the anode electrode active material and the aluminum foil separately, and (v) absorbing gas by the lime slurry or the calcium salt solution to prevent the secondary pollution caused by the hydrogen fluoride gas generated by the decomposition of the organic binder.
   USE - The method is useful for recovering ternary battery cathode material by plasma technology.
   ADVANTAGE - The method realizes the resource utilization of the waste battery.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Recovering ternary battery cathode material by plasma technology comprises (i) connecting the two poles of the battery to the resistive load through the wire, and measuring the open circuit voltage to control the discharge result with a multimeter, (ii) discharging the battery, stripping the battery metal casing in a closed and dry environment (to prevent the hydrolysis of lithium hexafluorophosphate electrolyte to produce toxic gas hydrogen fluoride), and taking out the anode electrode of the ternary battery, (iii) generating plasma by current of 2200-2600 W, sweeping across the surface of the anode electrode sheet of the ternary battery at a speed of 80-110 mm/seconds, carrying a large amount of active particles (ions, electrons, excited atoms and free radicals) by the plasma are sufficient to decompose the organic binder (poly vinylydene fluoride and polytetrafluoroethylene) in the anode electrode material, oxidizing the acetylene black conductive agent in the anode electrode material into carbon dioxide gas, and purifying the anode active material, (iv) at the same time, absorbing the energy carried by the plasma excited by the air by the material covering the surface of the anode electrode, and absorbing a large amount of energy to cause the anode electrode material to be excited to form a plasma that is rapidly expanding to generate a shock wave, and the shock wave makes the anode electrode material, removing the surface of the anode electrode sheet, and recovering the anode electrode active material (lithium nickel-1/3cobalt-1/3manganese-1/3oxygen, lithium nickel oxide/4 cobalt oxide/4 manganese oxide/2oxygen2, and lithium nickel oxide/5 cobalt oxide/2 manganese oxide/3oxygen2 or lithium nickel oxide/8 cobalt oxide/1 manganese oxide/1 oxide2) and the aluminum foil separately, and (v) absorbing the gas generated in the above process by the lime slurry or the calcium salt solution to prevent the secondary pollution caused by the hydrogen fluoride gas generated by the decomposition of the organic binder.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The two-stage of the battery connected with the resistive load through the wire for discharge purpose. The open-circuit current measured by meter is to control discharge result. In the sealed environment dry battery metal shell is to prevent hydrolysis of the lithium hexafluorophosphate electrolyte to produce a toxic gas hydrogen fluoride. The striking plasma current power is 2200-2600 W. The plasma is generated by using air as excitation target. The plasma is swept across the surface of the anode electrode sheet of the ternary battery at a speed of 80-110 mm/seconds according to the thickness of the anode electrode material. The plasma can decompose the organic binder (poly vinylydene fluoride and polytetrafluoroethylene) in the cathode material, and oxidize the acetylene black conductive agent in the cathode material to carbon dioxide gas, and achieving the purpose of purifying the anode active material. The plasma excited by the air can excite the anode electrode material to form sharply expanding new plasma to generate a shock wave, and the shock wave causes the anode electrode material to be removed from the surface of the anode electrode sheet. The purpose of recovering the anode electrode active material (lithium nickel-1/3cobalt-1/3manganese-1/3oxygen, lithium nickel oxide/4 cobalt oxide/4 manganese oxide/2oxygen2, and lithium nickel oxide/5 cobalt oxide/2 manganese oxide/3oxygen2 or lithium nickel oxide/8 cobalt oxide/1 manganese oxide/1 oxide2) and the aluminum foil can be achieved. The gas generated in the process is absorbed by lime slurry or calcium salt solution to prevent secondary pollution caused by hydrogen fluoride gas generated by decomposition of organic binder.

TI Recycling polyethylene terephthalate involves e.g. pre-loading reactor with ratio of polyethylene terephthalate and deionized water, injecting carbon dioxide to reactor at injection pressure and heating ratio of terephthalate and the water.
AU ZHANG J
   LU W
   FAN M
AE UNIV WYOMING (UYWO-C)
   UNIV WASHINGTON STATE (UNWS-C)
GA 2021C5995C
AB    NOVELTY - Recycling polyethylene terephthalate (PETE), comprises: pre-loading a reactor with a ratio of a polyethylene terephthalate and a deionized (DI) water; injecting a carbon dioxide into the reactor at an injection pressure; heating the ratio of polyethylene terephthalate and a deionized water and carbon dioxide within the reactor to a reaction temperature and for a reaction time; reacting the polyethylene terephthalate with the deionized water and the carbon dioxide; converting the polyethylene terephthalate to a liquid product and a solid product in the reactor; and collecting the liquid product and the solid product from the reactor.
   USE - The method is useful for recycling polyethylene terephthalate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is advantage to develops high-efficiency, low-energy consumption and environmental-friendly recycling technology for PETE plastic waste; and is advantage is to provide de-linkage process that breaks the chemical bond in PETE plastic and leads to further hydrolysis and that can be extended to investigate operating conditions as well PETE plastics degradation mechanism.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (a) recycling polyethylene terephthalate (PETE) by depolymerizing PETE, comprising pre-loading a reactor with a weight ratio of a polyethylene terephthalate and a deionized (DI) water, injecting carbon dioxide into the reactor at an injection pressure, sealing the weight ratio of the polyethylene terephthalate and the deionized water and the carbon dioxide within the reactor, heating the weight ratio of the polyethylene terephthalate and the deionized water and the carbon dioxide within the reactor to a reaction temperature and reacting the weight ratio of the polyethylene terephthalate and the deionized water and the carbon dioxide within the reactor for a reaction duration; and
   (b) environmentally friendly method for recycling PETE, comprising pre-loading a supercritical high temperature reactor with a weight ratio of a polyethylene terephthalate and a deionized (DI) water in excess of 1:10: injecting carbon dioxide into the supercritical high temperature reactor at an injection pressure in excess of 500 psi, sealing the weight ratio of the polyethylene terephthalate and the deionized water and the carbon dioxide within the supercritical high temperature reactor, setting a reaction temperature of the supercritical high temperature reactor with a controller, where the reaction temperature is in excess of 160?oC, heating the weight ratio of the polyethylene terephthalate and the deionized water and the carbon dioxide within the supercritical high temperature reactor to the reaction temperature and reacting the weight ratio of the polyethylene terephthalate and the deionized water and the carbon dioxide within the reactor for a reaction duration in excess of 0.25 hour.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises collecting the carbon dioxide after the completion of the reaction from the supercritical high temperature reactor. The method further comprises collecting a solid product reactant from the supercritical high temperature reactor, where the solid product reactant comprises polyenes and benzoic acid crystals, and collecting a liquid product reactant from the supercritical high temperature reactor, where the liquid product reactant comprises ethylene glycol.Preferred Components: The solid product comprises terephthalic acid. The reaction temperature comprises 160?oC, or 240?oC. The reaction time comprises 0.25 hour, or 2 hours. The injection pressure comprises 700 psi. The solid product reactant comprises polyenes and benzoic acid crystals. The liquid product reactant comprises ethylene glycol. The reactor comprises a supercritical high temperature reactor. The method of claim 9, where the weight ratio of the polyethylene terephthalate and the deionized (DI) water comprises 1:10, 1:15. 1:20,1:25 or 1:30. The weight ratio of the polyethylene terephthalate and the deionized (DI) water is less than or equal to1:30. The reaction temperature is less than or equal to240?oC. The reaction time is less than or equal to2.0 hour.

AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide zero-emission production                process using solar energy to calcine limestone                comprises reflecting the solar energy on a                heat-collecting accumulator through the mirror                field, where the heat-collecting accumulator                focuses and stores the energy, heating the                collected kiln tail waste gas containing high                concentration carbon dioxide gas to 900-1200degrees                Celsius conveying to a decomposing furnace to                provide heat, calcining, decomposing limestone,                allowing the high-concentration carbon dioxide                waste gas produced by the decomposition of                limestone in the decomposition furnace entering a                pre-heater system to preheat the limestone,                returning to the heat-collecting accumulator,                cooling the decomposed calcium oxide to                80-100degrees Celsius through a cyclone cooler of                independently supplying cold air.

AB    NOVELTY - The air conditioning and ventilation system carbon dioxide indoor air collecting device (1) comprises a housing (11) with a room air supply (111), a room air discharge (112), carbon dioxide adsorber material. The first adsorption-desorption area and the second adsorption-desorption area are separated from one another by a volume partition and the volume partition can be moved in a lateral position. The volume partition in the area of the room air supply and the room air discharge has air guide flaps. The first and the second adsorption-desorption area has fixed adsorber volume with the adsorbent. The displaceable adsorber volume is provided with the adsorbent. The displaceable adsorber volume of the first adsorption-desorption area and one displaceable adsorber volume of the second adsorption-desorption area are arranged on opposite sides of the common volume partition.
   USE - Air conditioning or ventilation system-based carbon dioxide indoor air collection device.

TI Air-carbon dioxide combined cycle power generation system, has air compressor inter-stage cooler heat absorbing side is supercritical carbon dioxide flowing, and heat releasing side is low pressure compressor outlet air flowing.

   DU W

GA 2021D0208B
AB    NOVELTY - The system comprises a high-pressure air preheater (8), a medium pressure air reheater and a low pressure air reheater that are arranged in a boiler hearth (12). An outlet of a low-pressure turbine (7) is located in the boiler hear inlet. A supercritical carbon dioxide heat recovery cycle power generation system is provided with a high pressure carbon dioxide turbine (14) and a super critical carbon dioxide condenser (15) that are sequentially arranged at an outlet of the high pressure air turbine. An air compressor inter-stage cooler and a pipeline are connected with each device. An outlet side of a heat absorbing side of the air compressor is connected with a supercritical air outlet of an air compressor (2). A heat releasing side is connected to an outlet end of the low pressure compressor. A second generator (13) is coaxially connected with the supercritical oxygen turbine.
   USE - Air-carbon dioxide combined cycle power generation system for use in large-scale power plant stage application using air turbine to generate electricity. Can also be used in other fields other than power plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The system realizes cascade utilization of fuel combustion energy in the boiler, and improves the economical efficiency of cycle power generation of heat-engine plant. The system adopts the circulating working medium that is not water and water vapor, so as to increase a high-efficiency clean power generation mode selection of the water shortage area of our country.

GA 202141732V
AB    NOVELTY - The device includes a supply amount checking unit having a bubble check tube (110) formed in the form of a transparent tube, with liquid accommodated inside. The carbon dioxide is introduced to the bottom moves upward in the form of bubbles and is discharged to the outlet (111) at the top. The supply amount checking unit has a detection unit for detecting the number of bubbles moving along. A valve assembly consists of a valve housing having a supply passage formed inside so as to communicate with the lower end of the bubble check pipe, and an inlet (230) through which carbon dioxide flows into the supply passage. A needle valve (240) is provided in the supply channel and controls the amount of carbon dioxide flowing into the bubble check pipe. A motor (250) moves the needle valve in the left and right directions.
   USE - Carbon dioxide supply control device for use in fish tanks.
   ADVANTAGE - According to the device, it is possible to check the supply amount of carbon dioxide in real-time by detecting the number of bubbles moving along the bubble check pipe, and by adjusting the needle valve, it is possible to easily control the appropriate supply amount of carbon dioxide to be changed due to internal and external factors. The user can not only visually check the supply of carbon dioxide, but also control the supply amount as desired while checking the supply amount with an accurate value, and the pH value of the fish tank can also be checked.

TI Dehumidification system for greenhouse, has third fan that is configured to generate process airflow that flows through second portion of desiccant in order to provide dehumidification to process airflow.
AU OBRIEN T S
   GIALLOMBARDO R G
   TOMASINI C
AE THERMA-STOR LLC (THER-Non-standard)
   THERMA-STOR LLC (THER-Non-standard)
GA 201960265L
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a first fan that is configured to generate a carbon dioxide airflow through a primary heat exchanger (220) and a secondary heat exchanger (230). A burner (240) is configured to generate a flame into one end of the primary heat exchanger. The flame is configured to increase an amount of carbon dioxide within the carbon dioxide airflow (170). A second fan is configured to generate a reactivation airflow (150) that flows over a portion of the secondary heat exchanger, and a portion of the primary heat exchanger. The reactivation airflow enters the enclosure through the reactivation airflow inlet and exits the enclosure through the reactivation airflow outlet. A third fan is configured to generate a process airflow (140) that flows through a second portion of the desiccant in order to provide dehumidification to the process airflow. The process airflow enters the enclosure through the process airflow inlet and exiting the enclosure through the process airflow outlet.
   USE - Dehumidification system for greenhouses.
   ADVANTAGE - The desiccant absorbs water vapor from process airflow, thus dehumidifying greenhouse. The greenhouse is allowed to have increased carbon dioxide levels while maintaining desired humidity and temperature levels within the greenhouse. The desiccant dehumidifier and carbon dioxide generator provides carbon dioxide to greenhouse, while simultaneously providing dehumidification and cooling to greenhouse. The temperature, humidity, and levels of carbon dioxide within greenhouse is maintained to certain levels to provide essential conditions to support plant growth.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of a desiccant dehumidifier and carbon dioxide generator utilized by the greenhouse system.

   Reactivation airflow (150)
   Carbon dioxide airflow (170)
   Primary heat exchanger (220)
   Secondary heat exchanger (230)
   Burner (240)
