TI Nozzle assembly for apparatus for dry cleaning large-sized substrate, has exhausting part for ejecting solid carbon dioxide powder from exhaust end, emission tubes mounted on vent to spray solid carbon dioxide on object through spray hole.
AU JI H Y
AE YS TECH CO LTD (YSTE-Non-standard)
GA 201849830A
AB    NOVELTY - The assembly (100) has an exhaust unit (105) supplies with liquid carbon dioxide from an outer side through a first supply tube (110) extended along a first direction. A second supply tube (120) is arranged parallel to the first supply tube. A slit part (130) is formed between the first and second supply tubes to perform adiabatic expansion for carbon dioxide while delivering the carbon dioxide from the first supply tube to the second supply tube. An exhausting part (140) ejects solid carbon dioxide powder from an exhaust end. Emission tubes are mounted on a vent for controlling inner side diameter of the exhaust end and spraying the solid carbon dioxide on an object through a spray hole. A cover (160) s formed with a fluid path (165) such that flow of drying gas suppresses condensation of the exhaust unit.
   USE - Nozzle assembly for an apparatus for dry cleaning a large-sized substrate for flat panel display fabrication.
   ADVANTAGE - The emission tubes can be easily replaced according to amount of the solid carbon dioxide powder, and allows flow of the drying gas while being contact with an outer side of the exhaust unit, so that fogging generation of the exhaust unit can be suppressed. The emission tubes uniformly spray the solid carbon dioxide on the large-sized substrate, so that choose detergency can be provided to the large-sized substrate, and the large-sized substrate can be easily washed.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a cross-sectional view of a nozzle assembly.
   Nozzle assembly (100)
   Exhaust unit (105)
   Supply tubes (110, 120)
   Slit part (130)
   Exhausting part (140)
   Cover (160)
   Fluid path (165)

PN US2018043121-A1; WO2018029689-A1; CN109475716-A; EP3496794-A1; US11000660-B2; EP3496794-B1; CN109475716-B; CN113398415-A
TI Oxygen-capnography face mask for delivering oxygen to patient, has carbon dioxide conduit comprising distal end that is connected to naris conduit and is in fluid flow communication with interior space of naris conduit.
AU GOULITSKI K
   KERTSER M
AE ORIDION MEDICAL 1987 LTD (ORID-Non-standard)
   ORIDION MEDICAL 1987 LTD (ORID-Non-standard)
   ORIDION MEDICAL 1987 CO LTD (ORID-Non-standard)
   ORIDION MEDICAL 1987 LTD (ORID-Non-standard)
GA 201813588A
AB    NOVELTY - The mask (310) has a mask unit comprising a mask internal space, and configured to be laid over a face of a subject. A internal partition wall is positioned in the mask internal space and defines a subject respiratory space (SRS) and a subject oxygen reservoir (SOR) in the mask internal space, where the internal partition wall comprises a naris conduit (380) extending from a inner partition wall (330) into the SRS, and a carbon dioxide conduit (381) comprising a distal end that is connected to the naris conduit, and is in fluid flow communication with an interior space of the naris conduit.
   USE - Oxygen-capnography face mask for delivering oxygen to a patient, and for continuously monitoring carbon dioxide exhaled from the patient.
   ADVANTAGE - The mask configures the naris conduits such that they enable oxygen to flow from a SOR space to a SRS during inhalation while quickly expelling traces of carbon dioxide, and such that exhaled carbon dioxide quickly fills up the naris conduits during exhalation while expelling oxygen traces back to the SOR. The mask forms a SRS in the mask for preventing dilution of carbon dioxide during exhalation, thus realizing accurate measurement of carbon dioxide concentration. The mask prevents under-pressure condition in the SRS during inhalation and over-pressure condition in the SRS during exhalation, thus facilitates breathing when a patient is in breathing difficulties such as breathing in oxygen.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The inner partition wall is L-shaped structure.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of an oxygen-capnography face mask with an internal partition wall.
   Oxygen-capnography face mask (310)
   Inner partition wall (330)
   Carbon dioxide port (350)
   Naris conduit (380)
   Carbon dioxide conduit (381)

The main pipeline of the three-way pipe is connected with the bottom of the bioreactor, the carbon dioxide inlet branch pipe is provided with a gas flow controller (3) and a valve (4), the upper end of the bioreactor is further provided with a water inlet (5), the bottom side wall is provided with a liquid outlet (8). TI Combined exhaled air and environmental gas sensor apparatus for mobile respiratory equipment, comprises housing comprising port aperture, connector, ambient aperture and plenum, sensor positioned within plenum, and processor.
AU BRADKE B S
   EVERMAN B R
AE GMECI LLC (GMEC-Non-standard)
   SPOTLIGHT LABS (SPOT-Non-standard)
   GMECI LLC (GMEC-Non-standard)
GA 2021C9024E
AB    NOVELTY - Combined exhaled air and environmental gas sensor 108 apparatus 100 comprises: a housing 104 comprising a port aperture, a connector configured to attach port aperture to a respiratory exhaust port, an ambient aperture connecting to an exterior environment and a plenum comprising port aperture and an ambient aperture, where the plenum is configured to permit free flow of exhaled air from port aperture and environmental gas from exterior environment by way of ambient aperture; a sensor positioned within the plenum, where the sensor is configured to detect a carbon dioxide level of gas within the plenum and generate many sensor outputs indicating detected carbon dioxide level; and a processor communicatively connected to sensor, where the processor is configured to operate in a breath analysis mode and an environmental analysis mode, receive sensor outputs from sensor, match sensor outputs to mode parameter profile, and switch from breath analysis mode to environmental analysis mode.
   USE - Combined exhaled air and environmental gas sensor apparatus used for mobile respiratory equipment for pilots and firefighters.
   ADVANTAGE - The combined exhaled air and environmental gas sensor apparatus provides safety, efficient and accurate performance for detecting various kinds of information about wearer and environment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Combined exhaled air and environmental gas sensor apparatus comprises: a housing comprising a port aperture, a connector configured to attach the port aperture to a respiratory exhaust port, an ambient aperture connecting to an exterior environment and a plenum comprising port aperture and an ambient aperture, where the plenum is configured to permit free flow of exhaled air from the port aperture and environmental gas from the exterior environment by way of the ambient aperture; a sensor positioned within the plenum, where the sensor is configured to detect a carbon dioxide level of gas within the plenum and generate many sensor outputs indicating detected carbon dioxide level; and a processor communicatively connected to the sensor, where the processor includes a memory and is configured to operate in a breath analysis mode and an environmental analysis mode, receive the sensor outputs from the sensor, match the sensor outputs to a mode parameter profile, and switch from the breath analysis mode to the environmental analysis mode as a function of the mode parameter profile, the processor in the environmental analysis mode is configured to measure the carbon dioxide level of the environmental gas by the sensor, and the processor in the breath analysis mode is configured to measure the carbon dioxide level of the exhaled gas by the sensor.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows schematic view of a combined exhaled air and environmental gas sensor apparatus.
   Combined exhaled air and environmental gas sensor 100 apparatus (100)
   Housing (104)
   Exhaled air and environmental gas sensor (108)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ELECTRONICS - Preferred Components: The housing includes a sensor-bearing surface, which is attached to the sensor-bearing surface, and is located opposite the port aperture. The sensor is configured to detect the carbon dioxide level by detecting a level of a related compound and detecting the carbon dioxide level as a function of the level of the related compound, and total volatile organic compound level. The processor is further configured to detect a breath as a function of the received sensor outputs, to switch to breath analysis mode during the detected breath, to switch to environmental analysis mode after the detected breath, and is configured to calibrate the sensor to a calibration setting. The apparatus further comprises an environmental sensor located partially within the exterior environment, where the environmental sensor is configured to detect an environmental parameter and the processor is configured to select the calibration setting from many candidate calibration settings as a function of an environmental parameter, and an environmental sensor configured to detect an environmental parameter, where the processor is further configured to detect and output a user alert as a function of the detection. The environmental parameter includes a temperature, a pressure and humidity level. The processor is further configured to detect a user condition. The user condition includes hypocapnia and fatigue.Preferred Method: The process of switching from the breath analysis mode to the environmental analysis mode comprises determining a breath occurred as a function of the mode parameter profile, and switching from the breath analysis mode to the environmental analysis mode after a preconfigured amount of time from when the breath occurred.

PT P
PN US2022322626-A1
TI Growing algae in cultivation container involves            circulating, via cultivation container, in closed loop,            receiving signals indicative of carbon dioxide and            hydrogen sulfide, in gas mixture, extracting gas            mixture from cultivation container, adding first type            of gas to gas mixture.
AU BERZIN I
   BASHAN O
AE VAXA TECHNOLOGIES LTD (VAXA-Non-standard)
GA 2022C9295N
AB    NOVELTY - Growing algae in cultivation container                involves circulating, via the cultivation                container, in a closed loop, a first predetermined                amount of gas mixture having a first type of gas                and at least one second type of gas. The gas                mixture enters the container via one or more                entrance spargers and exit via at least one exit                pipe. The first type of gas contains carbon                dioxide. Signals indicative of the amounts of                carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide, in the gas                mixture is received. Second predetermined amount of                the gas mixture is extracted from the cultivation                container, when the signal indicates that the                amount of hydrogen sulfide rises above a first                predetermined level. amount of the first type of                gas is added to the gas mixture, equal to the                second predetermined amount.
   USE - Method for growing algae in cultivation                container.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables more precisely using                geothermal gas a source for carbon dioxide in algae                cultivation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an algae                cultivation system (100), which has a cultivation                container (110) having one or more entrance                spargers (112) for introducing a gas mixture into                the cultivation container and an exit pipe for                releasing the gas mixture from cultivation                container, a circulating system (120) for                circulating the gas mixture in closed loop form the                exit pipe back to the one or more entrance                spargers, at least one sensor (130) for detecting                changes in an amount of carbon dioxide and hydrogen                sulfide in the gas mixture; a first gas supply                system for supplying a first type of gas, where the                first type of gas contains carbon dioxide at a                known first amount; a second gas supply system for                supplying a second type of gas; and a controller                configured to control the first gas supply system                and the second gas supply system to supply to the                circulating system a first predetermined amount of                gas mixture comprising the first type of gas and                second type of gas; receive from the sensor a                signal indicative of the amount of hydrogen sulfide                in the gas mixture; and when the signal indicates                that the amount of hydrogen sulfide rises above a                first predetermined level, open a valve to extract                a second predetermined amount of the gas mixture                from the cultivation container; and control the                first gas supply system to supply to the                circulating system the first type of gas in an                amount equal to the second predetermined                amount.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of an                algae cultivation system.
   100Algae cultivation system
   110Cultivation container
   112Entrance spargers
   120Circulating system
   130Sensor
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Condition: The first type of gas                  further comprises a toxic gas at a known second                  amount. The first type of gas is a geothermal                  gas. The second type of gas contains                  nitrogen.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. MC B04-F08A; B11-A04A; B05-C05; B05-C04; B05-C03; D05-H08A; D05-H02; P13-E99

PT P
PN WO2022013336-A1
TI Use of porous filler for reducing gas permeability of elastomer composition used for article e.g., tubeless articles in which porous filler comprises a filler material selected from surface-reacted calcium carbonate.
AU WELKER M
   RENTSCH S
AE OMYA INT AG (OMYA-C)
GA 202216771T
AB    NOVELTY - Use of porous filler is claimed for reducing the gas permeability of elastomer composition comprising an elastomer. The porous filler comprises a filler material selected from surface-reacted calcium carbonate, and/or precipitated hydromagnesite, and the surface-reacted calcium carbonate is a reaction product of natural ground or precipitated calcium carbonate with carbon dioxide and greater than or equal to 1 hydronium ion donors in an aqueous medium, where the carbon dioxide is formed in situ by hydronium ion donor treatment and/or is supplied from an external source and mixtures.
   USE - Utilization of porous filler for reducing gas permeability of elastomer composition used for article e.g., tubeless articles, membranes, sealings, O-rings, gloves, pipes, cables, electrical connectors, oil hoses, balls and shoe soles (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The elastomer composition has a reduced gas permeability. The mechanical properties of the elastomer composition comprising the porous filler remain unaffected or are improved. The presence of a surface-treatment layer on the surface of the filler material improves the dispersion of porous filler within the elastomer composition and improves the chemical compatibility.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) preparation of an elastomer composition having a reduced gas permeability which involves (a) providing a crosslinkable polymer, (b) providing a porous filler comprising a filler material selected from surface-reacted calcium carbonate, and/or precipitated hydromagnesite, where the surface-reacted calcium carbonate is a reaction product of natural ground or precipitated calcium carbonate with carbon dioxide and greater than or equal to 1 hydronium ion donors in an aqueous medium, where the carbon dioxide is formed in situ by hydronium ion donor treatment and/or is supplied from an external source, (c) optionally providing a further filler, preferably selected from carbon black, silica, ground natural calcium carbonate, precipitated calcium carbonate, talc, clay, kaolin, and/or nanofillers, (d) mixing, in any order, the crosslinkable polymer of step (a), the porous filler of step (b) and optionally the second filler of step (c) to form a mixture, and (e) crosslinking the mixture obtained in step (d) to form an elastomer composition having a reduced gas permeability; and
   (2) elastomer composition having a reduced gas permeability formed from a composition comprising a crosslinkable polymer, a porous filler, and a further filler where where the surface-reacted calcium carbonate is a reaction product of natural ground or precipitated calcium carbonate with carbon dioxide and greater than or equal to 1 hydronium ion donors in an aqueous medium, where the carbon dioxide is formed in situ by hydronium ion donor treatment and/or is supplied from an external source.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Parameters: The filler material has a Brunauer Emmett Teller (BET) specific surface area of 20-200 m2/g, preferably 70-120 m2/g; and/or a volume median particle size d50 of 0.1-75 mu m, preferably 1.5-15 mu m; and/or a volume top cut particle size d98 of 0.2-150 mu m, preferably 3-30 mu m; and/or an intra-particle intruded specific pore volume of 0.1-3 cm3/g, preferably 0.6-1.8 cm3/g, determined by mercury porosimetry measurement. The porous filler further comprises a surface-treatment layer on greater than or equal to 1 portion of the surface of filler material, where the surface-treatment layer is formed by contacting the filler material with a surface-treatment composition in an amount of 0.07-9 mg/m2, preferably 0.11-3 mg/m2 filler material surface. Preferred Components: The porous filler further comprises a surface-treatment layer on greater than or equal to 1 portion of the surface of filler material, where the surface-treatment layer is formed by contacting the filler material with a surface-treatment composition in an amount of 0.07-9 mg/m2, preferably 0.11-3 mg/m2 of the filler material surface. The volume ratio of porous filler to further filler is 10:90-90:10, preferably 40:60-60:40.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The surface-treatment composition comprises greater than or equal to 1 unsaturated surface-treatment agent selected from mono- or di-substituted succinic anhydride containing compounds comprising unsaturated carbon moieties, mono- or di-substituted succinic acid containing compounds comprising unsaturated carbon moieties, mono- or di-substituted succinic acid salts containing compounds comprising unsaturated carbon moieties, unsaturated fatty acids, salts of unsaturated fatty acids, unsaturated esters of phosphoric acid, salts of unsaturated phosphoric acid esters, abietic acid, salts of abietic acid, trialkoxysilanes comprising unsaturated carbon moieties and mixtures, preferably selected from (a) sodium, potassium, calcium, magnesium, lithium, strontium, prim.-amine, sec.-amine, tert.-amine and/or ammonium salts, where the amine salts are linear or cyclic, of mono- or di-substituted succinic acids, where one or both acid groups can be in the salt form, preferably both acid groups are in the salt form; unsaturated fatty acids, preferably oleic acid and/or linoleic acid; unsaturated esters of phosphoric acid; abietic acid and/or mixtures, preferred are completely neutralized surface treatment agents; and/or (b) a maleic anhydride grafted polybutadiene homopolymer or a maleic anhydride grafted polybutadiene-styrene copolymer and/or an acid and/or salt, preferably a maleic anhydride grafted polybutadiene homopolymer having (i) a number average molecular weight Mn measured by gel permeation chromatography of 1000-20000 g/mol, preferably 2000-10000 g/mol measured according to EN ISO 16014-1:2019, and/or (ii) a number of anhydride groups per chain of 2-12, preferably 2-6, and/or (iii) an anhydride equivalent weight of 400-2200, preferably 550-1800, and/or (iv) an acid number of 10-300 meq KOH per g maleic anhydride grafted polybutadiene homopolymer, preferably 30-150 meq KOH/g, measured according to ASTM D974-14, and/or (v) a molar amount of 1,2-vinyl groups of 5-80 mol.%, preferably 15-40 mol.%, based on the total amount of unsaturated carbon moieties in the maleic anhydride grafted polybutadiene homopolymer, and/or an acid and/or salt. The surface-treatment composition comprises greater than or equal to 1 saturated surface-treatment agent selected from (I) a phosphoric acid ester blend of greater than or equal to 1 phosphoric acid mono-ester and/or salts and/or greater than or equal to 1 phosphoric acid di-ester and/or salts, and/or (II) greater than or equal to 1 saturated aliphatic linear or branched carboxylic acid and/or salts, preferably greater than or equal to 1 aliphatic carboxylic acid having a total amount of carbon atoms of 16-18 and/or a salt and/or (III) greater than or equal to 1 mono-substituted succinic anhydride consisting of succinic anhydride mono-substituted with a group selected from a linear, branched, aliphatic and cyclic group having a total amount of carbon atoms of 2-30 in the substituent and/or salts, and/or (IV) greater than or equal to 1 polydialkylsiloxane, preferably greater than or equal to 1 polydimethylsiloxane selected from dimethicone, polydiethylsiloxane, and/or polymethylphenylsiloxane, and/or (V) greater than or equal to 1 trialkoxysilane, preferably a sulfur-containing trialkoxysilane or an amino-containing trialkoxysilane and/or (VI) mixtures of materials (I)-(V). The elastomer composition comprises an elastomer formed from an elastomer precursor selected from natural or synthetic rubbers, such as ethylene-propylene rubber, ethylene-propylene-diene rubber, nitrile-butadiene rubber, acrylonitrile-butadiene rubber, butyl rubber, styrene-butadiene rubber, polyisoprene, hydrogenated nitrile-butadiene rubber, polychloroprene, isobutene-isoprene rubber, chloro-isobutene-isoprene rubber, brominated isobutylene-isoprene rubber, acrylic rubbers, butadiene rubbers, epichlorhydrin rubbers, silicone rubbers, fluorocarbon rubbers, polyurethane rubbers, polysulfide rubbers, and/or thermoplastic rubbers. The porous filler is contained in the elastomer composition in an amount of 5-175 parts per hundred (phr), preferably 30-150 phr, based on total weight of the elastomer precursor in the elastomer composition. The elastomer composition further comprises additives, such as pigments, dyes, waxes, lubricants, oxidative- and/or UV-stabilizers, plasticizers, crosslinking agents, crosslinking coagents, compatibilizers, silanes, antioxidants, processing aids, and/or further fillers. Preferred Parameters: The (a) Shore A hardness of elastomer composition is increased, preferably greater than or equal to 8%, compared to the same elastomer composition, where the porous filler is replaced by carbon black in an isovolumic amount, and/or (b) the air permeability is decreased by greater than or equal to 5%, preferably greater than or equal to 15%, compared to the same elastomer composition, where the porous filler is replaced by carbon black in an isovolumic amount, where the carbon black has a statistical thickness surface area (STSA) of 39 plus minus 5 m2/g, measured according to ASTM D 6556-19, and the Shore A hardness is measured according to NF ISO 7619-1:2010 and the air permeability is measured according to NF ISO 2782-1:2018. Preferred Process: Crosslinking step (e) is performed by (i) the addition of crosslinking agent and a crosslinking coagent, and subsequent thermal crosslinking at a temperature of greater than or equal to 100 degrees C, preferably greater than or equal to 180 degrees C, optionally in combination with compression molding at a pressure of greater than or equal to 100 bar, preferably greater than or equal to 200 bar, and/or (ii) curing by UV light radiation, electron-beam radiation, nuclear radiation, gamma -radiation, microwave radiation and/or ultrasonic radiation.

PN CN113893709-A
TI Separating ammonia carbon by ion liquid film comprises e.g. fixing ion liquid in membrane separator, ammonia and carbon dioxide gas from one side of membrane contact and ion liquid hydrogen bond of ammonia gas through ion liquid complexing action.
AU DONG H
   BAI Y
   ZENG S
   JIANG H
   ZHANG X
   LI T
   BAI L
AE HUIZHOU GREEN ENERGY & NEW MATERIALS RES (HUIZ-Non-standard)
   INST PROCESS ENG CHINESE ACAD SCI (CAGC-C)
GA 2022137132
AB    NOVELTY - Separating ammonia carbon by ion liquid film comprises fixing ion liquid film in membrane separator, passing ammonia and carbon dioxide gas from one side of membrane contact and ion liquid hydrogen bond of ammonia gas through ion liquid complexing action, passing the ammonia to other side of film, collecting in gas storage tank to ensure separation of the ammonia gas and carbon dioxide, where ion liquid film is composed of ion liquid 0-80 wt.% ion liquid, the polymer is polyimide, sulfonated polymer, and polyether block copolymer; and ion liquid film is obtained by adding polymer into ethanol, water, dichloromethane, tetrahydrofuran, N,N-dimethylformamide into mixed solvent, adding ion liquid stirring and performing ultrasonic treatment at least three times to obtain uniform ion-polymer liquid solution, placing casting solution in film-making mold, volatilizing solvent and vacuum drying; ionic liquid is 1H-imidazol-3-ium compound (I) and pyridinium compound (II).
   USE - The ionic liquid film is useful for ammonia and carbon dioxide separation system in production process of melamine, synthetic ammonia to urea, and amino acid (claimed)
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple process, high efficiency and is environmentally-friendly, easy amplification, and small device floor area. The ion liquid film has high ammonia permeability and ammonia-carbon separation selectivity, excellent thermal stability, and mechanical stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Separating ammonia carbon by ion liquid film comprises fixing ion liquid film in membrane separator, passing ammonia and carbon dioxide gas from one side of membrane contact and ion liquid hydrogen bond of ammonia gas through ion liquid complexing action, passing the ammonia to other side of the membrane, collecting in gas storage tank to ensure separation of the ammonia gas and the carbon dioxide, where ion liquid film is composed of ion liquid 0-80 wt.% ion liquid, the polymer is polyimide, sulfonated polymer, and polyether block copolymer; and ion liquid film is obtained by adding polymer into ethanol, water, dichloromethane, tetrahydrofuran, N,N-dimethylformamide into mixed solvent, adding ion liquid stirring and performing ultrasonic treatment at least three times to obtain uniform ion-polymer liquid solution, placing casting solution in film-making mold, volatilizing solvent and vacuum drying; ionic liquid is 1H-imidazol-3-ium compound of formula (I) and pyridinium compound of formula (II).
   R1 = CnH2n+1;
   n = 0-4;
   R2 = H, CH3 or C2H5;
   R3 = CmH2m+1 or CmH2mOH;
   m = 0-8;
   R1, R2 and R3 = CnH2n+1 or CnH2nOH.
   R4 = CmH2m+1; and

TI Polyurethane-based open-pit high-step slope            reinforcing material has white material including            polyol, carbon dioxide absorbent, catalyst, soluble            salt and surfactant, and black material including            isocyanate and polymerization inhibitor.
AU LI F
AE SHENHUA GROUP ZHUNGEER ENERGY CO LTD (SHGR-C)
GA 2021E7094X
AB    NOVELTY - A polyurethane-based outdoor high step slope                reinforcing material comprises white material and                black material. The white material comprises                polyol, diluent, carbon dioxide absorbent,                catalyst, soluble salt, surfactant, crosslinking                agent and chain extender. The black material                comprises isocyanate, diluent and polymerization                inhibitor. The polyol is polyether polyol or                polyester polyol. The diluent is ketone, aromatic                hydrocarbon, sulfone, ether, acetate and/or                phosphate. The carbon dioxide absorbent is alkaline                absorbent or adsorbent material with abundant                micropores. The catalyst is fatty amine, alicyclic                amine, aromatic amine, alcohol amine, ammonium                salt, and alkyl compound of lead, tin, titanium,                antimony, mercury, zinc, bismuth, zirconium,                aluminum and/or carboxylate. The soluble salt is                organic substance soluble salt. The isocyanate is                toluene diisocyanate, methylene diphenyl                diisocyanate, hexamethylene diisocyanate and/or                polymethylene polyphenyl isocyanate.
   USE - Polyurethane-based open-pit high-step slope                reinforcing material.
   ADVANTAGE - The material has good mechanical property and                permeability, detection bonding strength of 3-10                MPa, shear strength of 3-10 MPa, compressive                strength of 20-70 MPa, and diffusion radius of 1-5                m, is suitable for crack gap of more than 0.1 mm,                and solves the problems about fluidity, high cost                and water swelling of polyurethane reinforcing                material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A polyurethane-based outdoor high step slope                reinforcing material comprises white material and                black material. The white material comprises 60-84%                polyol, 4-5% diluent, 10-35% carbon dioxide                absorbent, 0.01-3% catalyst, 0.05-1% soluble salt,                0.01-2% surfactant, 0.01-1.5% crosslinking agent                and 0.01-3% chain extender. The black material                comprises 60-95% isocyanate, 4-38% diluent and                0.01-2% polymerization inhibitor. The polyol is                polyether polyol or polyester polyol. The diluent                is ketone, aromatic hydrocarbon, sulfone, ether,                acetate and/or phosphate. The carbon dioxide                absorbent is alkaline absorbent or adsorbent                material with abundant micropores. The catalyst is                fatty amine, alicyclic amine, aromatic amine,                alcohol amine, ammonium salt, and alkyl compound of                lead, tin, titanium, antimony, mercury, zinc,                bismuth, zirconium, aluminum and/or carboxylate.                The soluble salt is organic substance soluble salt.                The isocyanate is toluene diisocyanate, methylene                diphenyl diisocyanate, hexamethylene diisocyanate                and/or polymethylene polyphenyl isocyanate.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                preparation of polyurethane-based open-pit                high-step slope reinforcing material comprising                stirring polyol, diluent, surfactant, carbon                dioxide absorbent, catalyst, soluble salt,                crosslinking agent and chain extender for 2-5 hours                to obtain white material, stirring isocyanate,                polymerization inhibitor and diluent for 2-5 hours                to obtain black material, and mixing and grouting                white material and black material at volume ratio                of 1: 1.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The polyether polyol                  and polyester polyol has functionality of greater                  than or equal to3. PN CN113546627-A
TI Preparation of low-temperature carbon dioxide methanation catalyst involves mixing cobalt nitrate and aluminum nitrate, mixing sodium carbonate, aqueous ammonia and urea precipitation agent, mixing obtained solutions, and centrifuging.
AU MA Q
   ZHAO T
   GAO X
   LIU Z
   ZHANG J
AE UNIV NINGXIA (UNNX-C)
GA 2021C30351
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of low-temperature carbon dioxide methanation catalyst involves dissolving cobalt nitrate in deionized water, adding aluminum nitrate, stirring, dissolving sodium carbonate, aqueous ammonia and urea precipitation agent in deionized water, mixing obtained solutions, putting into a stainless steel reaction kettle lined with polytetrafluoroethylene, crystallizing mother liquid at 80-120degrees Celsius to obtain solid-liquid mixture, centrifuging, pouring, washing the solid-liquid mixture with deionized water for 3 times to remove excess precipitant to obtain precipitate, drying to obtain a layered hydrotalcite-like precursor, placing the precursor in muffle furnace, calcining in air atmosphere of 300-500degrees Celsius and cooling to room temperature, placing obtained catalyst in 10-99.9% hydrogen atmosphere for reduction at heating rate of 2-5degrees Celsius/minute, and obtaining Co-supported alumina catalyst which is used for low-temperature carbon dioxide methanation reaction.
   USE - Preparation of low-temperature carbon dioxide methanation catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalytic performance of the prepared methanation catalyst is obviously high than that of cobalt/aluminum oxide catalyst by traditional impregnation method. The preparation method is simple, the catalytic performance is stable, and the service life is long.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of low-temperature carbon dioxide methanation catalyst involves dissolving cobalt nitrate in deionized water, configuring magnetic stirrer to form a homogeneous solution of 0.5-3 mol/L at speed of 1000 rpm, adding aluminum nitrate, stirring for 0.5-2 hours at room temperature, configuring two mixed homogeneous solution with molar ratio of 1-5:1, dissolving sodium carbonate, aqueous ammonia and urea precipitation agent in deionized water at room temperature, configuring magnetic stirrer to form a homogeneous solution at speed of 1000 rpm and stirring to obtain second solution, slowly adding second solution to first solution, stirring during addition to keep the solution homogeneous, stirring at room temperature for 1 hour, putting into a stainless steel reaction kettle lined with polytetrafluoroethylene, crystallizing mother liquid at 80-120degrees Celsius to obtain solid-liquid mixture, centrifuging, pouring, and washing the solid-liquid mixture with deionized water 3 times to remove excess precipitant until the pH of the washing solution is 7-8 to obtain a precipitate, placing the precipitate in oven and drying overnight at 60-100degrees Celsius to obtain a layered hydrotalcite-like precursor, placing the precursor in muffle furnace, calcining in air atmosphere of 300-500degrees Celsius and cooling to room temperature, placing obtained catalyst in 10-99.9% hydrogen atmosphere for reduction at heating rate of 2-5degrees Celsius/minute, and obtaining co-supported alumina catalyst which is used for low-temperature carbon dioxide methanation reaction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The precipitation agent comprises 0.5-1 mol/L sodium carbonate solution, 0.5-1 mol/L ammonia or 1.0-1.5 mol/L urea.

PN CN111533149-A
TI Preparing light and highly dispersed calcium oxide comprises e.g. mixing quicklime and water, carrying out digestion treatment, adding crystal form control agent, stirring, performing ultrasonic dispersion treatment, heating and stirring.
AU GU Y
AE CHANGSHU HONGYU CALCIUM COMPOUND CO LTD (CHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202080726W
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing light and highly dispersed calcium oxide comprises e.g. (i) mixing the quicklime and water according to a certain quality ratio and carrying out digestion treatment to obtain sodium hydroxide solution; (ii) adding a certain amount of the first crystal form control agent into the calcium hydroxide solution prepared in step (i), stirring it evenly, and performing ultrasonic dispersion treatment; and (iii) adding the uniformly dispersed calcium hydroxide solution in step (ii) into the carbonization reactor, heating and stirring, passing the carbon dioxide mixed gas to the bottom of the calcium hydroxide solution at a certain flow rate to react to a weakly alkaline pH value, adding a certain amount of the second crystal form control agent to react to a neutral pH value under the condition of continuously feeding the carbon dioxide mixed gas and heating and stirring, stopping feeding of the carbon dioxide mixed gas, and completing the carbonization reaction.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing light and highly dispersed calcium oxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes two crystal form control agents to prepare spherical calcium oxide with a small and uniform particle size and produces calcium oxide in the form of spherical particles by preventing agglomeration.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing light and highly dispersed calcium oxide comprises (i) mixing the quicklime and water according to a certain quality ratio and carrying out digestion treatment to obtain sodium hydroxide solution; (ii) adding a certain amount of the first crystal form control agent into the calcium hydroxide solution prepared in step (i), stirring it evenly, and performing ultrasonic dispersion treatment; (iii) adding the uniformly dispersed calcium hydroxide solution in step (ii) into the carbonization reactor, heating and stirring, passing the carbon dioxide mixed gas to the bottom of the calcium hydroxide solution at a certain flow rate to react to a weakly alkaline pH value, adding a certain amount of the second crystal form control agent to react to a neutral pH value under the condition of continuously feeding the carbon dioxide mixed gas and heating and stirring, stopping feeding of the carbon dioxide mixed gas, and completing the carbonization reaction; (iv) filtering the mixture after the carbonization treatment in step (iii), taking the filter residue, washing it with water, and drying it to obtain light high-dispersion spherical calcium carbonate; and (v) adding the light high-dispersion spherical calcium carbonate prepared in step (iii) into a rotary kiln for temperature-maintaining calcination, cooling to below 70 degrees C, and grinding. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for light high-dispersion calcium oxide particles, prepared as specified above.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the mass ratio of the quicklime to water is 8.5-9:1. In the step (ii), the first crystal form control agent is sodium hexametaphosphate, and the added amount of the first crystal form control agent is 1.5-2.5 wt.% of the dry basis mass of the calcium hydroxide. In the step (iii), the second crystal form control agent is a sodium citrate solution with a mass concentration of 10-13% and the added amount of the second crystal form control agent is 1-1.5 wt.% of the dry basis mass of the calcium hydroxide. In the step (iii), the carbon dioxide mixed gas is a mixed gas of carbon dioxide and nitrogen, where the volume content of the carbon dioxide is 85-95 wt.% of the total volume of the mixed gas. In the step (iii), the weakly alkaline pH is 8.5-9.5. The calcium oxide is spherical or ellipsoid with a particle size of less than 45 mu m, and its calcium oxide content is higher than 97%. Preferred Method: In the step (iii), the heating temperature is 40-60 degrees C and the stirring rate is 50-500 revolutions/minute. In the step (v), the calcination process conditions are the calcination temperature is 1250-1300 degrees C and the time is 18-20 hours.

PT P
PN KR2092092-B1
TI Producing flame retardant wood comprises e.g. forming multiple holes on surface of wood, controlling water content, immersing primary dried wood in flame retardant composition, curing, and controlling moisture content by secondary drying.
AU CHOI K S
   GYUN S J
AE CHOI K S (CHOI-Individual)
   GYUN S J (GYUN-Individual)
GA 2020456445
AB    NOVELTY - Producing flame retardant wood comprises (a) preparing wood, (b) forming multiple holes having diameter of 1-50 nm and depth of 10-100 nm on surface of the wood, (c) controlling water content until it reaches less than or equal to 10% by primary drying the surface treated wood, (d) immersing the primary dried wood in flame retardant composition containing monoammonium phosphate and lithium compound-containing carbon dioxide adsorbent, (e) curing the immersed wood in the flame retardant composition for 12-36 hours, and (f) controlling the moisture content until reaches to less than or equal to 15% by secondary drying the cured wood. The flame retardant composition comprises 50-150 pts. wt. distilled water, 10-30 pts. wt. monoammonium phosphate, 1-25 pts. wt. ammonium sulfate, 5-20 pts. wt. ammonium oxalate monohydrate, 0.5-10 pts. wt. polyacrylate, 0.01-5 pts. wt. monoethanolamine, and 0.1-10 pts. wt. lithium compound-containing carbon dioxide adsorbent based on 100 pts. wt. dried wood.
   USE - The method is useful for producing flame retardant wood.
   ADVANTAGE - The method exhibits continuous flame retardant effect in event of fire, prevents emission of toxic gases due to occurrence of fire by absorbing and adsorbing toxic gases i.e. carbon dioxide, human damage, and combustion of houses and buildings, generates flame retardant effect from inside of wood by immersing flame retardant composition in wood, reduces fire risk than flame retardant wood produced by conventional coating method, is environmentally friendly, and does not affect strength of wood.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing flame retardant wood comprises (a) preparing wood, (b) forming multiple holes having diameter of 1-50 nm and depth of 10-100 nm on surface of the wood, (c) controlling water content until it reaches less than or equal to 10% by primary drying the surface treated wood, (d) immersing the primary dried wood in flame retardant composition containing monoammonium phosphate and lithium compound-containing carbon dioxide adsorbent, (e) curing the immersed wood in the flame retardant composition for 12-36 hours, and (f) controlling the moisture content until reaches to less than or equal to 15% by secondary drying the cured wood. The flame retardant composition comprises 50-150 pts. wt. distilled water, 10-30 pts. wt. monoammonium phosphate, 1-25 pts. wt. ammonium sulfate, 5-20 pts. wt. ammonium oxalate monohydrate, 0.5-10 pts. wt. polyacrylate, 0.01-5 pts. wt. monoethanolamine, and 0.1-10 pts. wt. lithium compound-containing carbon dioxide adsorbent based on 100 pts. wt. dried wood. The lithium compound-containing carbon dioxide adsorbent is obtained by dispersing or dissolving in lithium compound salt in organic solvent, mixing with magnesium oxide, and firing at 300-500 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (d) is carried out for 6-48 hours in pressure range of 0.5-5 Mpa. The step (e) is carried out at 25-70 degrees C.
   EXAMPLE - The oak tree was cut into width of 15 cm in, length of 45 cm, and thickness of 1 cm to obtain wood piece. The obtained wood piece was naturally dried until the water content reaches to 8%, and the dried wood was immersed in flame retardant composition comprised of 143 pts. wt. distilled water, 25 pts. wt. monoammonium phosphate, 17 pts. wt. ammonium sulfate, 15 pts. wt. ammonium oxalate monohydrate, 7.50 pts. wt. polyacrylate, and 2.5 pts. wt. monoethanolamine to the components under pressure of 1.5 Mpa for 24 hours. Immersed for 24 hours, the wood was dried, cured in warm room at 50 degrees C for 24 hours, and dried until the water content reaches to 10% to obtain flame retardant wood.

AB    NOVELTY - Fully transparent visual carbon dioxide dry ice supercritical laboratory pressure vessel comprises PC blind flange A (1), stainless steel flat flange ring (2), PC blind flange B (3), pressure gauge, automatic pressure relief valve, automatic temperature control resistance wire, glass fiber cloth sleeve. The PC blind flange A and PC blind flange B are provided with through holes A. The sides of the stainless steel flat flange ring are drilled with through holes B of M18. The stainless steel flat flange ring has a fully threaded through hole on one side. The automatic temperature control resistance wire is sleeved in the glass fiber cloth sleeve.
   USE - Fully transparent visual carbon dioxide dry ice supercritical laboratory pressure vessel.
   ADVANTAGE - The fully transparent visual carbon dioxide dry ice supercritical laboratory pressure vessel has characteristics of high impact resistance, safety, anti-fall, anti-crush.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing fully transparent visual carbon dioxide dry ice supercritical laboratory pressure vessel, which involves:
   (A) aligning center of PC blind flange B, stainless steel flat flange ring and oil-resistant non-oil gasket B, aligning through hole A of PC blind flange B, through hole B at lower end of stainless steel flat flange ring and through hole C of oil-resistant non-petroleum gasket B;
   (B) connecting through high-strength bolt connectors through through holes A, through holes B and through holes C, PC blind flange B and stainless steel flat flange ring;
   (C) connecting PC blind flange B and stainless steel flat flange ring, forming cavity inside the stainless steel flat flange ring, adding solid dry ice into cavity;
   (D) placing oil-resistant non-petroleum gasket A and PC blind flange A on upper end of stainless steel flat flange ring, aligning through hole C of oil-resistant non-petroleum gasket A, through hole A of PC blind flange A and upper through hole B of stainless steel flat flange ring;
   (E) using temperature control regulator to control operation of the automatic temperature control resistance wire to heat the solid dry ice in the cavity, heating range is 0-50 degrees C, air pressure inside the cavity reaches 7.38 megapascal; and
   (F) opening automatic pressure relief valve to automatically relieve pressure.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of fully transparent visual carbon dioxide dry ice supercritical laboratory pressure vessel.

   PC blind flange B (3)

TI Mobile cathode-based supercritical composite electroplating processing bit comprises reaction cell, carbon dioxide gas cylinder, high pressure pump and direct current power supply component.
AU GAO G
   FU X
   SU Z
AE GAO G (GAOG-Individual)
   GAO G (GAOG-Individual)
GA 2019916804
AB    NOVELTY - Mobile cathode-based supercritical composite electroplating processing bit comprises reaction cell (1), a carbon dioxide gas cylinder (2), a high pressure pump (3), and a direct current power supply component (4), and carbon dioxide gas cylinder. The outlet end is connected to one end of the high pressure pump through a hose, and the other end of the high pressure pump communicates with the bottom of the reaction tank through a hose. The reaction tank is provided with anode assembly driving mechanism (5), and an anode assembly (6) located inside the reaction tank is driven by the anode assembly driving mechanism. An electric heating assembly is equipped on the reaction tank (1). A transmission mechanism (8) is arranged axially along the anode assembly and the transmission mechanism is located at the anode assembly.
   USE - Mobile cathode-based supercritical composite electroplating processing bit.
   ADVANTAGE - The mobile cathode-based supercritical composite electroplating processing bit realizes that a group of anode conductive tubes is required for sets of processed drill bits in mass production, so that the production cost is reduced by a large number of high-efficiency production, promotes widespread use of electroplated drill bits, saves waste of electrical energy caused by the use of mechanical agitators for agitation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for using mobile cathode-based supercritical composite electroplating processing bit, which involves:
   (A) pre-electroless plating the substrate of the drill bit to be processed, and plating metal layer which is easy to be electrodeposited on the surface of the drill bit;
   (B) adding a binary system plating solution containing nano-hard particles and a composite additive into the reaction tank, introducing carbon dioxide gas into the reaction tank and controlling temperature inside of the reaction tank between 35-70 degrees C and the pressure is in the range of 8-20 Mega Pascals (MPa) to form a ternary electroplating system with a supercritical carbon dioxide emulsion as a carrier;
   (C) clamping end of the drill bit by the clamping assembly, and driving drill bit along the axis of the insulating disk body and the conductive disk body through the transmission assembly;
   (D) continuously moving in the direction, driving the anode assembly through the anode assembly driving mechanism to rotate back and forth around the drill bit to be processed; and
   (E) performing post-treatment to obtain a drill with nanocomposite coatings of desired performance.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows schematic view of mobile cathode-based supercritical composite electroplating processing bit.
   Reaction cell (1)
   Reaction tank (1)
   Carbon dioxide gas cylinder (2)
   High pressure pump (3)
   Direct current power supply component (4)
   Anode assembly driving mechanism (5)
   Anode assembly (6)
   Transmission mechanism (8)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The plating solution is a nickel salt solution containing nickel sulfate and nickel chloride, nano hard particles are diamond nanoparticles having a diameter of 30-60 nanometer. The composite additive is an additive composed of a dodecyl compound and an ether compound, the concentration of the nickel salt solution is 300-450 g / L, and concentration of the nano diamond particles is 1-20 g / L. The concentration of the composite additive is 0.1-2 g/L, and the plating solution further includes boric acid as an electrodeposition slow release agent at a concentration of 30-60 g/L, and the composite additive is composed of sodium lauryl sulfate and polyethylene. The concentration of sodium lauryl sulfate is 0.2 g/L, and the concentration of polyethylene glycol trimethyl decyl ether is 0.8 g/L. The metal in which the substrate of the drill bit to be subjected to the pre-electroless plating treatment in the pretreatment electrodeposited is pure copper or pure nickel.

PT P
PN CN109877182-A
TI Preparing ultra-long carbon dioxide cracking tube useful for blasting and in the field of carbon dioxide cracking tube processing technology comprises e.g. selecting alloy steel, heating, and forging by casting machine.
AU TANG Y
AE SHANDONG POLYTECHNIC (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019550158
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing ultra-long carbon dioxide cracking tube for blasting comprises (1) selecting 10-25% alloy steel as material, (ii) placing the resultant for heating in carbon furnace at 700-1200 degrees C, placing the heated alloy steel in cold water at 5- 10 degrees C for cooling, repeating the above steps, forging the processed alloy steel by casting machine, (iii) carring out pressing treatment, performing segmentation detection on both ends and the middle of each cracked tubes processed in each step (ii), and ensuring the hardness of the cracked tube, (iv) detecting the cracked tube in the step (iii), again placing in a carbon furnace at 700-1200 degrees C for heating, placing the heated cracked tube in a cold water at 10-20 degrees C for cooling, repeating the above step for 2-4 times to complete the final quenching step, and (v) plating the surface of the cracked tube which has been quenched in step (iv), respectively with hard chrome, plating the surface with hard chrome with thickness of 10-25 mu m.
   USE - The ultra-long carbon dioxide cracking tube is useful for blasting (claimed) and in the field of carbon dioxide cracking tube processing technology.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is economical, can reduce the production time, improves product hardness and leak tightness, and reduces cracking tube processing step.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing ultra-long carbon dioxide cracking tube for blasting comprises (1) selecting 10-25% alloy steel as material, (ii) placing the resultant for heating in carbon furnace at 700-1200 degrees C, placing the heated alloy steel in cold water at 5- 10 degrees C for cooling, repeating the above steps, forging the processed alloy steel by casting machine, cutting the forged alloy steel into length of 3.6-4.5 m, drilling the center of the cut alloy steel, where the diameter of the hole is 5-10 cm, and placing the drilled steel pipe for allowing to stand, (iii) carring out pressing treatment, performing segmentation detection on both ends and the middle of each cracked tubes processed in each step (ii), and ensuring the hardness of the cracked tube, (iv) detecting the cracked tube in the step (iii), again placing in a carbon furnace at 700-1200 degrees C for heating, placing the heated cracked tube in a cold water at 10-20 degrees C for cooling, repeating the above step for 2-4 times to complete the final quenching step, and (v) plating the surface of the cracked tube which has been quenched in step (iv), respectively with hard chrome, plating the surface with hard chrome with thickness of 10-25 mu m.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the material selection step, an alloy steel having an internal alloying element of 16% is selected as raw material. In the forging step, the selected alloy steel is placed in a carbon furnace at 750 degrees C for heating, the heated alloy steel is placed in cold water of 6 degrees for cooling, and the above steps are repeated three times, and the treatment is good, the alloy steel was forged by casting machine, and the forged alloy steel was cut into length of 4 m, and the center of the cut alloy steel was drilled, and the diameter of the hole was 7 cm. In the second forging step, the cracked tube detected in the step (iii) is again placed in a carbon furnace at 700 degrees C for heating, the heated cracked tube was placed inside a cold water of 10 degrees to be cooled, and the above steps were repeated twice to complete the forging. In the surface processing step, the surface of the cracked tube is plated with hard chromium thickness of 20 mu m.

PN CN108152498-A
TI Inactivation test for bovine BVDV, IBRV, PI3, BRSV inactivated vaccine comprises selecting appropriate bovine viral diarrhea virus, infectious bovine rhinotracheitis virus, placing in carbon dioxide incubator, shaking and absorbing.
AU WANG W
   SHI X
   LI Z
   MENG Q
   HUANG H
   LIU F
   JI Y
   WU H
AE CHINA WITTER JIANGSU BIOLOGICAL PHARM CO (CHWI-Non-standard)
GA 2018472537
AB    NOVELTY - Inactivation test for bovine BVDV, IBRV, PI3, BRSV inactivated vaccine comprises (a) selecting appropriate bovine viral diarrhea virus, infectious bovine rhinotracheitis virus, bovine parainfluenza type 3 virus or bovine respiratory syncytial virus in resuscitation culture in suitable cell lines, (b) selecting 2 monolayers that have grown into excellent cells, placing in carbon dioxide incubator, shaking, (c) after absorbing, adding cell maintaining liquid with a certain volume in the cell bottle, culturing, observing the cell lesion, harvesting the cell culture liquid, freezing and thawing, (d) taking cell culture liquid after freezing and thawing, adding nutrient maintenance solution into the cell spinner, placing the cell bottle device into the carbon dioxide culturing box continuously, culturing, observing the cell pathological changes, if there is no cell lesion, inactivated completely, (e) harvesting culture solution, carrying out fluorescent staining.
   USE - The method is useful for inactivation test for bovine BVDV, IBRV, PI3, BRSV inactivated vaccine.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Inactivation test for bovine BVDV, IBRV, PI3, BRSV inactivated vaccine comprises (a) selecting appropriate bovine viral diarrhea virus, infectious bovine rhinotracheitis virus, bovine parainfluenza type 3 virus or bovine respiratory syncytial virus in resuscitation culture in suitable cell lines, (b) selecting 2 monolayers that have grown into excellent cells, suitable volume of bottles from 80-90% density, discarding the cell culture fluid, inoculating 20-30 ml of the virus solution after inoculation into the cell spinner, placing in 5% carbon dioxide incubator at 37 degrees C, where the inactivated virus solution is adsorbed on the aforementioned suitable cells for 40-60 minutes, shaking 2-3 times during the period, (c) after absorbing, adding cell maintaining liquid with a certain volume in the cell bottle, where the cell bottle device continuously culturing for 3-4 days in the carbon dioxide, culturing in box at 37 degrees C, observing the cell lesion, for virus-free liquid cell pathological changes, harvesting the cell culture liquid, freezing and thawing for 2-3 times, (d) taking 10-15 ml cell culture liquid after freezing and thawing, inoculating the grown until density is 80-90% than 175 cm2 after adsorption on suitable cells after 40-60 minutes, adding nutrient maintenance solution into the cell spinner, placing the cell bottle device into the carbon dioxide culturing box continuously, culturing for 3 days at 37 degrees C, where the cell pathological changes of virus liquid, observing the cell pathological changes, if there is no cell lesion, inactivated completely, (e) for normally has no virus liquid of cell lesion, harvesting culture solution, carrying out fluorescent staining, if no specific fluorescence, completely inactivated, e.g. special fluorescent appears and inactivated completely.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: In the step (e) mode of the fluorescent dye comprises taking the virus culture medium with normal cytopathic effect is inoculated into a 96-well cell culture plate filled with monolayer MDBK cells, wells is 0.1 ml, positive virus control wells and normal cell control hole 4 hole, centering on carbon dioxide culture box at 37 degrees C to continuously culture for 24 hours, discarding the culture liquid, washing with phosphate-buffered saline, fixing with 0.1 ml 80% acetone and 20% methanol at -20 degrees C for 30 minutes, washing with phosphate-buffered saline, respectively adding concentration is 2.5 mu g/ml of bovine virus monoclonal fluorescent antibody 0.1 ml, placing in wet box at 37 degrees C for 40 minutes, washing with phosphate-buffered saline, placing under the fluorescence microscope, observing the result, positive virus control wells and normal cell control wells should be no fluorescence, as no specific fluorescence test hole, it shows a completely inactivated as special fluorescent appears and inactivation is not complete.

TI Flax subcritical carbon dioxide pretreatment emulsion prepared by mixing water, industrial castor oil, diglycerol fatty acid ester, gelatin, and polydimethylsiloxane.
AU ZHENG L
   ZHENG H
   GAO S
   ZHANG J
   WANG C
   WEI A
   ZHANG B
AE YIXING SUNSHINE LINEN TEXTILE CO LTD (YIXI-Non-standard)
   NINGXIA SUNSHINE LINEN TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (NING-Non-standard)
GA 202236435R
AB    NOVELTY - Flax subcritical carbon dioxide pretreatment emulsion prepared by mixing water, industrial castor oil, diglycerol fatty acid ester, gelatin, and polydimethylsiloxane in a mass ratio of 50-72:30-15.5:15-10:1.5-0.5:3.5-2, is claimed.
   USE - Used as flax subcritical carbon dioxide pretreatment emulsion.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: uses the pretreatment emulsion subcritical carbon dioxide process, the pretreatment time of flax fiber is shortened to less than 6 hours, the fiber moisture regain reaches 16-20%, after carding, the long hemp can be increased to more than 85%, and the short flax is 5-10%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for flax subcritical carbon dioxide pretreatment process, comprising dissolving subcritical carbon dioxide fluid in the pretreatment emulsion diffuses and penetrates into the flax fiber, where the amount of the pretreatment emulsion is 5-10% of the quality of the flax fiber, and the processing parameters are temperature 20-30degrees Celsius, pressure 8-12 Mpa, and processing time 3-6 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The flax subcritical carbon dioxide pretreatment process comprises (S1) adding water, diglycerol fatty acid ester and gelatin in sequence at room temperature, starting high-speed stirring for 5-10 minutes, where the stirring speed is 500-1000 revolutions/minute, then adding industrial castor oil and polydimethylsiloxane, and stirring for 30-60 minutes at stirring speed of 1000-2000 revolutions/minute to obtain a pre-treatment emulsion, (S2) placing the flax fiber on the pretreatment distributor, placing the pretreatment distributor in the pretreatment tank, loading the pretreatment emulsion prepared by step (S1) into the solvent tank, after the carbon dioxide in the carbon dioxide storage tank is cooled by the refrigerator, pressurizing by the high-pressure pump and heating by the preheater and then flowing into the rotary atomizer, pumping the pretreatment emulsion placed in the solvent tank into the rotary atomizer by the solvent pump, and the mixed fluid in the rotary atomizer flows into the pretreatment tank and diffuses and penetrates into the flax fiber, (S3) during pretreatment, turning on the circulating pump and the flow rate is 1-10 m3/hour, the subcritical carbon dioxide fluid dissolved with the pretreatment emulsion flows out from the pretreatment tank, under the action of the circulating pump, the subcritical carbon dioxide fluid flows through the preheater and the rotary atomizer again, and entering the pretreatment tank for circulating treatment, and (S4) after completing the subcritical pretreatment, the subcritical carbon dioxide fluid in the pretreatment tank flows into at least one separation kettles to separate carbon dioxide from the pretreatment emulsion, and then flowing into the carbon dioxide storage tank for medium reuse.Preferred Components: The interior of the pre-treatment tank in step (S2) is separated from bottom to top by partitions and is sequentially arranged into an expansion chamber, a circulation chamber and a treatment chamber, a distribution pipe is arranged on the side wall of the treatment chamber, the fluid inlet pipeline at the bottom end of the expansion chamber is connected to the outlet of the rotary atomizer, the periphery of the expansion chamber is communicated with the distribution pipe on the side wall of the treatment chamber through a communication pipe, a pre-treatment distributor for stacking flax is built in the treatment chamber, the pre-treatment distributor includes a porous distribution pipe, the bottom end of the porous distribution pipe is snap-fitted with the opening corresponding to the top of the circulation chamber and communicates with the circulation chamber, and a fluid outlet pipeline is arranged on the side wall of the circulation chamber to connect the circulation pump and the separation kettle. The distribution pipes are distributed in a serpentine shape, the distribution pipes are evenly opened, the openings are trumpet-shaped, the inner diameter is 2-5 mm, the inclination angle of the inner and outer diameters is 45degrees, and the spacing between the openings on the outside of the distribution pipe is 5-10 mm. The pretreatment distributor comprises at least one treatment distributor units. The treatment distributor unit is assembled from at least one porous distribution pipes and porous distribution plates. The bottom end of the lowermost porous distribution pipe is connected to the circulation chamber. The port of the porous distribution pipe is provided with a sealing and rotating positioning clamping structure.

TI Method for converting ecological value of bio-based process, involves calculating total weight of carbon dioxide uptake by multiplying standard weight of carbon dioxide uptake per season of growth per surface unit of land area.
AU ROORDA H W
AE GET2GREEN LOGISTICS BV (GETT-Non-standard)
GA 2020C8083M
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves calculating a total weight of carbon dioxide uptake by multiplying a standard weight of the carbon dioxide uptake per season of growth per surface unit of land area assigned for the growth of a crop by the total surface of the area. The economic value leads are added to a shift in the business model of organizations engaged in cultivation and production of hemp products.
   USE - Method for converting ecological value of bio-based process based on growth of industrial hemp or fiber hemp.

AB    NOVELTY - Prolonging fresh-keeping time of milk by using carbon dioxide micro-nano bubble comprises (i) performing sensory inspection, physical and chemical indicator, and microbiological inspection to inspect raw milk; (ii) filtering the milk under pressure of 50 kPa using milk filter paper; (iii) mixing the milk with carbon dioxide and passing into the micro-nano bubble device to form milk-CO2 micro-nano bubble mixing system; and (iv) storing the milk-carbon dioxide micro-nano bubble mixing system, pre-washing, sterilizing and obtaining sterilized milk storage tank.
   USE - The method is useful for prolonging fresh-keeping time of milk by using carbon dioxide micro-nano bubble.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes micro-nano bubbles with small bubble size, and realizes large solubility, high adsorption rate, slow rising speed in milk, and the antibacterial effect of carbon dioxide and solves the problems of microbial contamination during the traditional milk process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the pore diameter of the milk filter paper is 100-250 mm. In the step (iii), the carbon dioxide should be decompressed before mixed with milk to ensure that milk can enter the micro-nano bubble device smoothly. In the step (iv), the storage temperature of the milk storage tank is 0 degrees C, the milk storage tank has bearing pressure, so that carbon dioxide gas can be stored in the milk storage tank.

TI Preparing tetra-substituted vinyloxazolidine-2,4-dione from carbon dioxide comprises e.g. reacting propargyl amide, halogenated hydrocarbon, carbon dioxide, catalyst, copper salt and solvent, quenching, separating, purifying and drying.
AU SUN S
   ZHOU C
   CHENG J
   DONG Y
AE UNIV CHANGZHOU (UNCZ-C)
GA 2019A7592F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing tetra-substituted vinyloxazolidine-2,4-dione from carbon dioxide comprises (i) adding propargyl amide, halogenated hydrocarbon, carbon dioxide, catalyst, copper salt, alkali, and solvent in a reaction tube, and placing the reaction tube at ambient temperature for reaction, and (ii) terminating the reaction, quenching the reaction solution with saturated saline, extracting the organic phase in the system with ethyl acetate, separating the product and purifying by column chromatography, and then drying to obtain final product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing tetra-substituted vinyloxazolidine-2,4-dione from carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes carbon dioxide as carboxy source for first time to construct a tetra-substituted vinyloxazolidine-2,4-dione at ambient temperature.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for tetra-substituted vinyloxazolidine-2,4-dione of formula (II) prepared by above method.
   R1 = aryl or heterocyclic ring;
   R2 = aryl or alkyl; and
   R3 = aryl, heterocyclic or alkyl.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The inorganic base is potassium carbonate or potassium phosphate. The transition metal catalyst is tetrakis(triphenylphosphine)palladium(0) or bis(triphenylphosphine)palladium(II) dichloride. The copper salt is anhydrous copper(II) sulfate or copper(I) iodide. The halogenated hydrocarbon is brominated hydrocarbon or iodohydrocarbon. The amount of transition metal catalyst used is 0.05-0.10 equivalents, the amount of copper salt is 0.15-0.50 equivalent, the amount of alkali used is 2 equivalents, the amount of halogenated hydrocarbon is 1.2-2.0 equivalents, and the pressure of carbon dioxide is 0.1 MPa. Preferred Method: The reaction is stirred at room temperature for 16-24 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The propargyl amide is of formula (I). The base is inorganic base or organic base. The solvent is dimethylsulfoxide or N,N-dimethylformamide. The organic base is 1,5,7-triazabicyclo(4.4.0)dec-5-ene, 1,8-diazabicyclo(5.4.0)undec-7-ene or 7-methyl-1,5,7-triazabicyclo(4.4.0)dec-5-ene.R1 = aryl group or heterocyclic ring; andR2 = aryl group or alkyl group.

TI Sterilizing tissue, preferably acellular tissue matrix or intact tissue by packaging tissue in inner package, packaging inner package in outer package, treating tissue with super critical carbon dioxide and sterilant, and sealing.
AU QIU Q
   CONNOR J
AE LIFECELL CORP (LIFC-C)
GA 2019426774
AB    NOVELTY - Method for sterilizing tissue, involves packaging the tissue in an inner package before treatment with super critical carbon dioxide, where the inner package is permeable to super critical carbon dioxide and sterilant, packaging the inner package in an outer package before treatment with super critical carbon dioxide, where the outer package comprises (i) a first portion including a material i.e. permeable to super critical carbon dioxide and a sterilant, and (ii) a second portion i.e. impermeable to moisture, treating the tissue with super critical carbon dioxide and the sterilant to sterilize the tissue, and sealing the first portion of the outer package with the second portion of the outer package subsequent to sterilization such that the inner package is sealed within a moisture impermeable enclosure.
   USE - The method is useful for sterilizing tissue. The tissue is an acellular tissue matrix or an intact tissue (all claimed).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The inner package is permeable to super critical carbon dioxide, and sterilant. The first portion of the outer package is impermeable to bacteria. The sterilant comprises peracetic acid (PAA). The sterilant further comprises peroxide, where the peroxide is hydrogen peroxide. The concentration of PAA and hydrogen peroxide in the sterilant is 10-14% and 1-3%, respectively. The concentration of PAA and hydrogen peroxide in the chamber is 20-100 ppm and 1-10 ppm, respectively. Preferred Method: The method involves placing the tissue in a nitrogen atmosphere for 24 hours after treatment with super critical carbon dioxide and the sterilant. The method involves treating the tissue in a chamber, where the concentration of PAA and hydrogen peroxide in the chamber is 1-1000 ppm and 1-1000 ppm, respectively.

AE UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
GA 2019253646
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide capturing method coupled with                chemical absorption and biotransformation involves                (a) generating ammonium bicarbonate by absorbing                carbon dioxide by using aqueous ammonia as a                simulated absorption liquid, adding to the                microalgae culture medium and supplying as a                nutrient substance for the growth of microalgae and                (b) under ventilating conditions, introducing 5%                carbon dioxide and 95% nitrogen as simulation smoke                gas, introducing the mixture in a culture medium at                a rate of 20 ml/min and maintaining the pH to 7-8                on the basis of promoting the growth of                microalgae.
   USE - The method is useful for coupling chemical                absorption and biotransformation and treating waste                liquid absorbing carbon dioxide by treating aqueous                ammonia through microalgae.

PN CN108030744-A
TI White rhizoma Bletilla traditional Chinese medicine mask used for e.g. whitening skin, comprises film cloth, and fragrance composed of supercritical carbon dioxide-rhizoma Bletilla extract and supercritical carbon dioxide-rose extract.
AU YANG W
   YANG X
   ZHANG C
   SHI J
   JIANG C
   TIAN W
   JIANG J
AE SICHUAN KANGYANGJIAN BIOTECHNOLOGY CO (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 2018411331
AB    NOVELTY - A white rhizoma Bletilla traditional Chinese medicine mask comprises film cloth and fragrance. The fragrance comprises 10-30 wt.% supercritical carbon dioxide-rhizoma Bletilla extract and 70-90 wt.% supercritical carbon dioxide-rose extract.
   USE - White rhizoma Bletilla traditional Chinese medicine mask used for whitening skin, increasing skin nutrition and preventing wrinkles.
   ADVANTAGE - The pure white rhizoma Bletilla traditional Chinese medicine mask effectively maintains the active ingredients of medicinal plants without using organic solvents during production process, and does not produce side effects.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of white rhizoma Bletilla traditional Chinese medicine mask, which involves (1) mixing supercritical carbon dioxide-rhizoma Bletilla extract and supercritical carbon dioxide-rose extract to obtain fragrance, (2) injecting the fragrance into the packaging bag, placing the film cloth in the packaging bag, and sealing the product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Process: The crushed rhizoma Bletilla is passed through 40-mesh sieve.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The supercritical carbon dioxide-rhizoma Bletilla extract is obtained by crushing and sieving the rhizoma Bletilla, placing obtained powder in supercritical carbon dioxide extractor, using supercritical carbon dioxide as extraction medium, extracting rhizoma Bletilla with carrier concentration of 10-55% at extraction pressure of 20-40 MPa and extraction temperature of 15-40 degrees C for 60-180 minutes. The supercritical carbon dioxide-rose extract is obtained by using supercritical carbon dioxide as extraction medium and extracting rose powder at extraction pressure of 20-40 MPa and extraction temperature of 15-40 degrees C for 60-180 minutes.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The carrier is ethanol.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - TEXTILES AND PAPER - Preferred: The film cloth is selected from non-woven cloth, silk, cotton fiber, viscose fiber, biofiber, Tencel (RTM: lyocell fibers) or bamboo charcoal fiber.
   ACTIVITY - Dermatological.

AB    NOVELTY - Preparing calcium carbonate gel comprises (i)                preparing calcium salt solution, carbonate solution                and sodium tripolyphosphate solution, (ii) mixing                carbonate solution obtained in the step (i) and the                sodium tripolyphosphate solution to obtain a mixed                solution A, and reacting for a period of time under                specific conditions, and (iii) rapidly adding the                calcium salt solution obtained in the step (i) to                the mixed solution A obtained in the step (ii)                under the same experimental conditions,                continuously stirring and reacting for a period of                time, then carrying out vacuum filtration                separation, washing and drying to obtain stable                vaterite calcium carbonate gel, and where the molar                ratio of sodium tripolyphosphate and calcium salt                in the reaction solution is 5:100-12:100.

PT P
PN US2022189763-A1
TI Atomic layer deposition of ruthenium on substrate for forming interconnect lines for integrated circuits, involves exposing surface of substrate to chemical precursor, depositing chemical precursor on surface, and exposing to co-reactant.
AU KUMMEL A
   WANG V
   BREEDEN M
AE UNIV CALIFORNIA (REGC-C)
GA 202279793V
AB    NOVELTY - Atomic layer deposition (ALD) of ruthenium on a substrate comprises at least one cycle of: exposing a surface of a substrate to a chemical precursor comprising ruthenium; depositing the chemical precursor on the surface of the substrate; and exposing the precursor to a co-reactant, to provide ruthenium film on the substrate surface. The precursor comprises ruthenium(IHD)carbon dioxide or bis(ethylcyclopentadienyl)ruthenium(II), and the coreactant comprises oxygen. The ruthenium film has a resistivity less than 9.5 muOmegacm, and/or is prepared without a post-deposition annealing process.
   USE - The substrate is useful in ALD of ruthenium on a substrate used for fabricating a semiconductor device, e.g. integrated circuits that serve as liners for back end of line interconnects.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is capable of depositing atomic layer of ruthenium on the substrate with high selectivity and deposition conformality and without surface treatment and/or passivation; and provides films/layers having low resistivities at narrow pitches.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) an interconnect or a semiconductor device; and
   (2) a method for ALD of ruthenium on a substrate.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a schematic view of resistivity-oxygen dose relationship for ruthenium-atomic layer deposition.
DC U11 (Semiconductor Materials and Processes)
MC U11-A13
IP H01L-021/02; H01L-021/768; H01L-023/532

   ADVANTAGE - (I): has excellent inhibition effect and                corrosion inhibition effect with high corrosion                inhibition rate when the metal corrosion inhibitor                compound is used to inhibit the corrosion of carbon                dioxide to metals in the oilfield production                process and has simple preparation process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - N-methylnaphthalene-benzotriazole-based metal                corrosion inhibitor compound of formula (I) is                new.
   R = H or 1-4C alkyl.
